# Ramayana - Aranya Kanda

## Sarga 1

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

प्रविश्य तु महारण्यं दण्डकारण्यमात्मवान्। ददर्श रामो दुर्धर्षस्तापसाश्रममण्डलम्।।3.1.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

आत्मवान् selfpossessed, दुर्धर्षः invincible रामः Rama, महारण्यम् great forest, दण्डकारण्यम् Dandaka forest, प्रविश्य having entered, तापसाश्रममण्डलम् multitude of hermitages of the ascetics, ददर्श saw.

#### Explanation

The invincible and selfpossessed Rama entered the great forest of Dandaka and saw there a multitude of hermitages of the ascetics.

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

कुशचीरपरिक्षिप्तं ब्राह्म्या लक्ष्म्या समावृतम्। यथा प्रदीप्तं दुर्दर्शं गगने सूर्यमण्डलम्।।3.1.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कुशचीरपरिक्षिप्तम्  ceremonial robes(made) of kusa grass spread all over ब्राह्म्या of the brahmanas, लक्ष्म्या of auspicious appearance, समावृतम् surrounded, गगने in the sky, प्रदीप्तम् glowing, सूर्यमण्डलं यथा like the orb of the Sun, दुर्दर्शम् difficult to gaze.

#### Explanation

They (the hermitages) were strewn all over with kusa grass and robes by bark with auspicious sacrificial materials around and hence dazzling like the orb of the sun in the sky difficult to gaze.

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

शरण्यं सर्वभूतानां सुसम्मृष्टाजिरं सदा। मृगैर्बहुभिराकीर्णं पक्षिसङ्घैस्समावृतम्।।3.1.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सर्वभूतानाम् for all beings, शरण्यम्  shelter, सदा always, सुसम्मृष्टाजिरं the courtyard being wellcleaned, wetted and decorated, बहुभिः by many, मृगैः by antelopes, आकीर्णम् filled all over, पक्षिसङ्घै by flocks of birds, समावृतम् filled with.

#### Explanation

The courtyards (of the hermitages) were cleanswept, sprinkled with water and decorated. They provided shelter to all kinds of creatures and were filled all over with  flocks of birds and antelopes.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

पूजितं च प्रनृत्तं च नित्यमप्सरसां गणैः। विशालैरग्निशरणैः स्रुग्भाण्डैरजिनैः कुशैः।।3.1.4।। समिद्भिस्तोयकलशैः फलमूलैश्च शोभितम्। आरण्यैश्च महावृक्षैः पुण्यैस्स्वादुफलैर्वृतम्।।3.1.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अप्सरसाम्  by celestial damsels, गणैः by groups, पूजितं च glorified, प्रनृत्तं च and offered dances as service, विशालैः with vast, अग्निशरणैः with fire sanctuaries स्रुग्भाण्डैः with wooden ladles for offering ghee अजिनैः with deerskins, कुशैः with kusa grass, समिद्भि: with faggots (specially offered to fire in sacrificial ceremonies), तोयकलशैः pots filled with water, फलमूलैश्च with fruits and roots, शोभितम् adorned, आरण्यैः produced in forests, पुण्यैः sacred ones, स्वादुफलैः by sweet fruits, महावृक्षैः with huge trees, उपशोभितम् looking delightful all over.

#### Explanation

The hermitages looked glorified with dances by celestial damsels. They were  adorned with vast fire sanctuaries, ladles for sacrificial ceremonies, deerskins and kusa grass faggots for pots of water, roots and fruits. They looked delightful with huge sacred trees of the forest laden with sweet fruits.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

पूजितं च प्रनृत्तं च नित्यमप्सरसां गणैः। विशालैरग्निशरणैः स्रुग्भाण्डैरजिनैः कुशैः।।3.1.4।। समिद्भिस्तोयकलशैः फलमूलैश्च शोभितम्। आरण्यैश्च महावृक्षैः पुण्यैस्स्वादुफलैर्वृतम्।।3.1.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अप्सरसाम्  by celestial damsels, गणैः by groups, पूजितं च glorified, प्रनृत्तं च and offered dances as service, विशालैः with vast, अग्निशरणैः with fire sanctuaries स्रुग्भाण्डैः with wooden ladles for offering ghee अजिनैः with deerskins, कुशैः with kusa grass, समिद्भि: with faggots (specially offered to fire in sacrificial ceremonies), तोयकलशैः pots filled with water, फलमूलैश्च with fruits and roots, शोभितम् adorned, आरण्यैः produced in forests, पुण्यैः sacred ones, स्वादुफलैः by sweet fruits, महावृक्षैः with huge trees, उपशोभितम् looking delightful all over.

#### Explanation

The hermitages looked glorified with dances by celestial damsels. They were  adorned with vast fire sanctuaries, ladles for sacrificial ceremonies, deerskins and kusa grass faggots for pots of water, roots and fruits. They looked delightful with huge sacred trees of the forest laden with sweet fruits.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

बलिहोमार्चितं पुण्यं ब्रह्मघोषनिनादितम्। पुष्पैश्चान्यैः परिक्षिप्तं पद्मिन्या च सपद्मया।।3.1.6।। फलमूलाशनैर्दान्तैश्चीरकृष्णाजिनाम्बरैः। सूर्यवैश्वानराभैश्च पुराणैर्मुनिभिर्वुतम्।।3.1.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

बलिहोमार्चितम् worshipped with various offerings and sacrifices, पुण्यम् sacred, ब्रह्मघोषनिनादितम् reverberating with chanting of the Vedas,अन्यैः पुष्पैः by other flowers, परिक्षिप्तम् scattered here and there, पद्मिन्या by a lotuspond, सपद्मया filled with lotuses, फलमूलाशनैः by those living on fruits and roots, दान्तै: selfrestrained ones, चीरकृष्णाजिनाम्बरैः wearing bark garments and skins of black antelopes, सूर्यवैश्वानराभैश्च glowing like the Sun or the fire, पुराणैः by the aged, मुनिभिः by the sages, वृतम् surrounded by.

#### Explanation

The hermitages where various sacrificial offerings were made reverberated with Vedic chantings. Flowers were scattered all over. There was a lotuspond with lotuses. There were aged, selfrestrained sages glowing like the Sun or the fire, living on fruits and roots, clothed in bark and dark  deerskin.

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

बलिहोमार्चितं पुण्यं ब्रह्मघोषनिनादितम्। पुष्पैश्चान्यैः परिक्षिप्तं पद्मिन्या च सपद्मया।।3.1.6।। फलमूलाशनैर्दान्तैश्चीरकृष्णाजिनाम्बरैः। सूर्यवैश्वानराभैश्च पुराणैर्मुनिभिर्वुतम्।।3.1.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

बलिहोमार्चितम् worshipped with various offerings and sacrifices, पुण्यम् sacred, ब्रह्मघोषनिनादितम् reverberating with chanting of the Vedas,अन्यैः पुष्पैः by other flowers, परिक्षिप्तम् scattered here and there, पद्मिन्या by a lotuspond, सपद्मया filled with lotuses, फलमूलाशनैः by those living on fruits and roots, दान्तै: selfrestrained ones, चीरकृष्णाजिनाम्बरैः wearing bark garments and skins of black antelopes, सूर्यवैश्वानराभैश्च glowing like the Sun or the fire, पुराणैः by the aged, मुनिभिः by the sages, वृतम् surrounded by.

#### Explanation

The hermitages where various sacrificial offerings were made reverberated with Vedic chantings. Flowers were scattered all over. There was a lotuspond with lotuses. There were aged, selfrestrained sages glowing like the Sun or the fire, living on fruits and roots, clothed in bark and dark  deerskin.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

पुण्यैश्च नियताहारैः शोभितं परमर्षिभिः। तद्ब्रह्मभवनप्रख्यं ब्रह्मघोषनिनादितम्।।3.1.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पुण्यैः by holy people, नियताहारैः  with regulated diet, परमर्षिभिः by great sages, शोभितम् shining, ब्रह्मघोषनिनादितम् echoing with the chanting of the Vedas, ब्रह्मभवनप्रख्यम्  resembling the abode of Brahma, तत् that (hermitage).

#### Explanation

The hermitages echoing with  the sounds of Vedic recitations and resembling the very abode of Brahma were adorned with great, holy sages living on regulated diet.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

ब्रह्मविद्भिर्महाभागैर्ब्राह्मणैरुपशोभितम्। तद्दृष्ट्वा राघवः श्रीमांस्तापसाश्रममण्डलम्।।3.1.9।। अभ्यगच्छन्महातेजा विज्यं कृत्वा महद्धनुः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ब्रह्मविद्भि: by those knowing the Supreme Spirit, महाभागैः by the venerable ones, बाह्मणैः by brahmins, उपशोभितम् was adorned, तत् that, तापसाश्रममण्डलम् groups of hermitages of the sages, दृष्ट्वा after seeing, श्रीमान्  illustrious, महातेजाः a man of great lustre, राघवः Rama, महत् great, धनुः bow, विज्यम् with loosened string, कृत्वा doing, अभ्यगच्छत् went forward.

#### Explanation

The illustrious, effulgent Rama saw groups of hermitages adorned with venerable brahmins, with knowledge of the Supreme Spirit. On seeing them he loosened the string of his great bow and went forward.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

दिव्यज्ञानोपपन्नास्ते रामं दृष्ट्वा महर्षयः।।3.1.10।। अभ्यगच्छन्स्तदा प्रीता वैदेहीं च यशस्विनीम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

दिव्यज्ञानोपपन्नाः richly endowed with divine knowledge, ते महर्षयः those great sages, रामं Rama, यशस्विनीम् highly reputed, वैदेहीं च Sita too, दृष्ट्वा on seeing, प्रीताः pleased, तदा then, अभ्यगच्छन् went towards.

#### Explanation

On seeing Rama with illustrious Sita, the great sages, richly endowed with divine knowledge, went forward to greet him.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

ते तं सोममिवोद्यन्तं दृष्ट्वा वै धर्मचारिणम्।।3.1.11।। लक्ष्मणं चैव दृष्ट्वा तु वैदेहीं च यशस्विनीम्। मङ्गलानि प्रयुञ्जानाः प्रत्यगृह्णन्दृढव्रताः।।3.1.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

दृढव्रताः steadfast in vows, ते they, उद्यन्तम् rising, सोममिव like the Moon, धर्मचारिणम् follower of  righteous path, दृष्ट्वा तु on seeing, लक्ष्मणं चैव Lakshmana too, यशस्विनीम् famed, वैदेहीं च Sita also, दृष्ट्वा तु seeing, मङ्गलानि words of benediction, प्रयुञ्जानाः uttering, प्रत्यगृह्णन् went towards and received them.

#### Explanation

The sages, steadfast in their vows, saw Rama who was shining like the rising Moon, Lakshmana and the famed Sita went forward and received them with words of benediction.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

ते तं सोममिवोद्यन्तं दृष्ट्वा वै धर्मचारिणम्।।3.1.11।। लक्ष्मणं चैव दृष्ट्वा तु वैदेहीं च यशस्विनीम्। मङ्गलानि प्रयुञ्जानाः प्रत्यगृह्णन्दृढव्रताः।।3.1.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

दृढव्रताः steadfast in vows, ते they, उद्यन्तम् rising, सोममिव like the Moon, धर्मचारिणम् follower of  righteous path, दृष्ट्वा तु on seeing, लक्ष्मणं चैव Lakshmana too, यशस्विनीम् famed, वैदेहीं च Sita also, दृष्ट्वा तु seeing, मङ्गलानि words of benediction, प्रयुञ्जानाः uttering, प्रत्यगृह्णन् went towards and received them.

#### Explanation

The sages, steadfast in their vows, saw Rama who was shining like the rising Moon, Lakshmana and the famed Sita went forward and received them with words of benediction.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

रूपसंहननं लक्ष्मीं सौकुमार्यं सुवेषताम्। ददृशुर्विस्मिताकारा रामस्य वनवासिनः।।3.1.13।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वनवासिनः (sages) living in the woods, रामस्य Rama's, रूपसंहननम् beauty of the body, लक्ष्मीम् splendour, सौकुमार्यम् tenderness, सुवेषताम् will attired, विस्मिताकाराः wonderstruck sages, ददृशुः saw.

#### Explanation

Those sages living  in the forest were wonderstruck to see the handsome, graceful, delicate and welldressed Rama.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

वैदेहीं लक्ष्मणं रामं नेत्रैरनिमिषैरिव। आश्चर्यभूतान्ददृशुः सर्वे ते वनचारिणः।।3.1.14।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सर्वे all, ते वनचारिण: those forestrangers, आश्चर्यभूतान् creating wonder, वैदेहीम् Sita, लक्ष्मणं Lakshmana, चैव too, रामं Rama, अनिमिषैरिव unblinking, नेत्रै: with eyes, ददृशुः saw.

#### Explanation

All the foresters looked at the wonderful figures of Rama, Sita and Lakshmana with unblinking eyes.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

अत्रैनं हि महाभागा स्सर्वभूतहिते रताः। अतिथिं पर्णशालायां राघवं संन्यवेशयन्।।3.1.15।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सर्वभूतहिते in the welfare of all beings, रताः engaged, महाभागाः glorious, अत्र here, पर्णशालायाम् in a leafy hut, अतिथिम् guest, एनम् राघवम् this Rama, संन्यवेशयन् made him sit and rest.

#### Explanation

Ever engaged in the welfare of all beings, the sages made Rama, their guest, sit in their leafy hut.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

ततो रामस्य सत्कृत्य विधिना पावकोपमाः। आजह्रुस्ते महाभागाः सलिलं धर्मचारिणः।।3.1.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः thereafter, पावकोपमाः comparable to fire, महाभागाः great ones, ते they, धर्मचारिणः followers of righteousness, विधिना as per tradition, सत्कृत्य after rendering due hospitality, रामस्य to Rama, सलिलम् water, आजह्रुः offered.

#### Explanation

The great sages comparable to the firegod and followers of righteousness honoured Rama  according to tradition by offering water.

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

पुष्पं मूलं फलं सर्वमाश्रमं च महात्मनः। निवेदयित्वा धर्मज्ञास्ते ततः प्राञ्जलयोऽब्रुवन्।।3.1.17।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

धर्मज्ञाः knowers of dharma, ते they, तत: then, पुष्पम् flower, फलम् fruit, मूलं root, सर्वम् all, आश्रमं च and the hermitage, महात्मनः to the great self, निवेदयित्वा having offered, प्राञ्जलयः with folded palms, अब्रुवन् said.

#### Explanation

The sages who were knowers of dharma saluted the noble Rama, offered fruits, roots and flowers available  at the hermitage and with folded palms said thus:

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

धर्मपालो जनस्यास्य शरण्यस्त्वं महायशाः। पूजनीयश्च मान्यश्च राजा दण्डधरो गुरुः।।3.1.18।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राजा king, महायशा: of great fame, जनस्यास्य of the people, धर्मपालः protector of dharma, शरण्यश्च  refuge, पूजनीयश्च worthy of worship, मान्यश्च respectable, दण्डधरः chastiser of evildoers, गुरुः a respectable one.

#### Explanation

O king of great reputation, you are the refuge of the people, a protector of dharma, a chastiser of wrongdoers, a worshipful and respectable preceptor.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

इन्द्रस्येह चतुर्भागः प्रजा रक्षति राघव। राजा तस्माद्वरान्भोगान्रम्यान् भुङक्तेलोकनमस्कृतः।।3.1.19।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राघव O Rama, इन्द्रस्य Indra's, इह here, चतुर्भागः fourth part, प्रजाः people, रक्षति protects, तस्मात् therefore, राजा king, नमस्कृतः obeyed, वरान् best, भोगान् pleasures, भुङक्ते enjoys.

#### Explanation

O Rama, in this world a king is considered the fourth part of Indra as he protects the people. Therefore, he is respected and he enjoys the choicest pleasures of life.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

ते वयं भवता रक्ष्या भवद्विषयवासिनः। नगरस्थो वनस्थो वा त्वं नो राजा जनेश्वरः।।3.1.20।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

भवद्विषयवासि residents of your domain, ते to you, वयम् we, भवता by you, रक्ष्याः deserve to be protected, नगरस्थः residing in a city, वनस्थः वा or staying in the forest, जनेश्वर: Lord of the people, त्वम् you, राजा king.

#### Explanation

Since we are the residents of your territory we deserve to be protected by you. Whether you live in the city or in the forest, you are the lord of the people and our king.

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

न्यस्तदण्डा वयं राजञ्जितक्रोधा जितेन्द्रियाः। रक्षणीयास्त्वया शश्वदगर्भभूतास्तपोधनाः।।3.1.21।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राजन् O king, जितक्रोधाः men who have conquered anger, जितेन्द्रियाः selfcontrolled, तपोधनाः  asceties, वयम् we, न्यस्तदण्डाः cast aside the symbol of authority, गर्भभूताः like your children, त्वया by you, शश्वत् forever, रक्षणीयाः to be protected.

#### Explanation

O king, we have conquered our anger and have controlled our senses. We ascetics have cast aside all our authority. We are like your children and need to be protected by you forever

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

एवमुक्त्वा फलैर्मूलैः पुष्पैर्वन्यैश्च राघवम्। अन्यैश्च विविधाहारैः सलक्ष्मणमपूजयन्।।3.1.22।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

एवम् in that manner, उक्त्वा having said, सलक्ष्मणम् along with Lakshmana, राघवम् Rama, फलैः with fruits, मूलैः with roots, पुष्पैः with flowers, अन्यैश्च and by other, वन्यैः by forest produce, विविधाहारैः different kinds of food, अपूजयन् offered with reverence.

#### Explanation

Having said so, the ascetics offered with reverence fruits, roots, flowers and other forest  products to Rama and Lakshmana.

---

## Sarga 2

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

कृतातिथ्योऽथ रामस्तु सूर्यस्योदयनं प्रति। आम्नत्ऱ्य स मुनीन्सर्वान्वनमेवान्वगाहत।।3.2.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अथ then, कृतातिथ्यः on receiving hospitality, सः रामस्तु that Rama also, सूर्यस्योदयनं प्रति at the time of sunrise, सर्वान् all, मुनीन् sages, आम्नत्ऱ्य  took leave, वनमेव into the forest, अन्वगाहत entered deep.

#### Explanation

After receiving hospitality from the sages, Rama took of all of them at sunrise and entered deep into the forest.

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

नानामृगगणाकीर्णमृक्षशार्दूल सेवितम्। ध्वस्तवृक्षलतागुल्मं दुर्दर्शसलिलाशयम्।।3.2.2।। निष्कूजनानाशकुनिझिल्लिकागणनादितम्। लक्ष्मणानुगतो रामो वनमध्यं ददर्श ह।।3.2.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लक्ष्मणानुगतः followed by Lakshmana, रामः Rama, नानामृगगणाकीर्णम् filled with different kinds of animals, ऋक्षशार्दूलसेवितम् inhabited by bears and tigers, ध्वस्तवृक्षलतागुल्मम् a place where trees, creepers and bushes were crushed (due to frequent visit by demons), दुर्दश difficult to see, सलिलाशयम् waterreservoirs like wells, निष्कूजनानाशकुनि with the twitter of various birds, झिल्लिकागणनादितम् resounding with the chirping of crickets. वनमध्यम् midst of the forest, ददर्श ह witnessed.

#### Explanation

With Lakshmana following, Rama went through the forest, filled with herds of animals and inhabited by bears and tigers. It was a place where trees, creepers and bushes were crushed (by frequent visits of demons). It was difficult to locate a water source.  And it resounded with the chirping of various birds and insects (crickets).

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

नानामृगगणाकीर्णमृक्षशार्दूल सेवितम्। ध्वस्तवृक्षलतागुल्मं दुर्दर्शसलिलाशयम्।।3.2.2।। निष्कूजनानाशकुनिझिल्लिकागणनादितम्। लक्ष्मणानुगतो रामो वनमध्यं ददर्श ह।।3.2.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लक्ष्मणानुगतः followed by Lakshmana, रामः Rama, नानामृगगणाकीर्णम् filled with different kinds of animals, ऋक्षशार्दूलसेवितम् inhabited by bears and tigers, ध्वस्तवृक्षलतागुल्मम् a place where trees, creepers and bushes were crushed (due to frequent visit by demons), दुर्दश difficult to see, सलिलाशयम् waterreservoirs like wells, निष्कूजनानाशकुनि with the twitter of various birds, झिल्लिकागणनादितम् resounding with the chirping of crickets. वनमध्यम् midst of the forest, ददर्श ह witnessed.

#### Explanation

With Lakshmana following, Rama went through the forest, filled with herds of animals and inhabited by bears and tigers. It was a place where trees, creepers and bushes were crushed (by frequent visits of demons). It was difficult to locate a water source.  And it resounded with the chirping of various birds and insects (crickets).

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

सीतया सह काकुत्स्थस्तस्मिनघोरमृगायुते। ददर्श गिरिशृङ्गाभं पुरुषादं महास्वनम्।।3.2.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सीतया सह along with Sita, काकुत्स्थः Rama of the Kakutstha family, घोरमृगायुते infested with dreadful animals, तस्मिन् in that place, गिरिशृङ्गाभम् comparable to the peak of a mountain, महास्वनम्  heavy sound, पुरुषादम् a cannibal, ददर्श saw.

#### Explanation

While Rama along with Sita was wandering in the forest infested with  dreadful animals, he saw a roaring cannibal resembling the peak of a mountain.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

गम्भीराक्षं महावक्त्रं विकटं विषमोदरम्। बीभत्सं विषमं दीर्घं विकृतं घोरदर्शनम्।।3.2.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

गम्भीराक्षम् deepeyed, महावक्त्रम् with a large face, विकटम् of dreadful appearance, विषमोदरम् with a huge belly, बीभत्सम् loathsome, विषमम् crude, दीर्घम् tall, विकृतम् ugly, घोरदर्शनम् of frightful appearance.

#### Explanation

He was of dreadful appearance with deep eyes, a huge face and a big belly. He looked tall, ugly and frightening.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

वसानं चर्म वैयाघ्रं वसार्द्रं रुधिरोक्षितम्। त्रासनं सर्वभूतानां व्यादितास्यमिवान्तकम्।।3.2.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वैयाघ्रम् tiger's, चर्म skin, वसानम्  wore, वसार्द्रम्  wet with marrow, रुधिरोक्षितम् drenched with  blood, सर्वभूतानाम् of all beings, त्रासनम् causing terror, व्यादितास्यम् with wide open mouth, अन्तकम् इव like the god of death.

#### Explanation

Wearing a tigerskin, he resembled the god of death. With a wide, open mouth, wet with marrow and drenched with blood, he struck terror into  all beings.

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

त्रीन्सिम्हान्श्चतुरो व्याघ्रान्द्वौ वृकौ पृषतान्दश। सविषाणं वसादिग्धं गजस्य च शिरो महत्। अवसज्यायसे शूले विनदन्तं महास्वनम्।।3.2.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

त्रीन् three, सिंहान् lions, चतुरः four, व्याघ्रान् tigers, द्वौ two, वृकौ wolves, दश ten, पृषतान् deer, सविषाणम् along with the tusk of an elephant, वसादिग्धम् wet with the fat of flesh, महत् great, गजस्य शिरः elephant's head, आयसे on an iron, शूले spoke, अवसज्य having pierced, महास्वनम् shouting loudly, विनदन्तम् while he  produced.

#### Explanation

Roaring loudly, he carried an iron spit in which were pierced three lions, four tigers, two wolves, ten deer  and the huge head of an elephant with its tusks smeared with the fat of flesh.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

स रामं लक्ष्मणं चैव सीतां दृष्ट्वा च मैथिलीम्। अभ्यधावत्सुसङ्कृद्धः प्रजाः काल इवान्तकः।।3.2.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः that, रामम् Rama, लक्ष्मणं च and Lakshmana, मैथिलीम् princess of Mithila, सीतां च and to Sita, दृष्ट्वा seeing सुसङ्कृद्धः he was with anger, अन्तकः god of death, कालः Yama, प्रजाः इव like the people, अभ्यधावत् ran towards.

#### Explanation

Looking extremely furious, he ran towards Rama, Lakshmana and Sita like the god of death rushes towards the (dying) people.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

स कृत्वा भैरवं नादं चालयन्निव मेदिनीम्। अङ्केनादाय वैदेहीमपक्रम्य ततोऽब्रवीत्।।3.2.9।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, सः that demon, मेदिनीम् earth, चालयन्निव as if shaking, भैरवम् dreadful, नादम् sound, कृत्वा after producing, वैदेहीम् Sita, अङ्केन in his lap, आदाय taking, अपक्रम्य after moving aside, अब्रवीत् said.

#### Explanation

Making a dreadful sound, as if shaking the earth, he took Sita on his lap, stepped aside and said:

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

युवां जटाचीरधरौ सभार्यौ क्षीणजीवितौ। प्रविष्टौ दण्डकारण्यं शरचापासिधारिणौ।।3.2.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

जटाचीरधरौ both of you clad in bark and with matted hair, क्षीणजीवितौ both with shortened life, युवाम् both of you, सभार्यौ with your wife, शरचापासिधारिणौ holding bows, arrows and swords, दण्डकारण्यम् to Dandaka forest, प्रविष्टौ  entered into.

#### Explanation

You both clad in bark and wearing matted hair, holding bows, arrows and swords have entered into the Dandaka forest with your wife. Your life has been cut short indeed৷৷

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

कथं तापसयोर्वां च वासः प्रमदया सह। अधर्मचारिणौ पापौ कौ युवां मुनिदूषकौ।।3.2.11।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तापसयोः you wear attire of ascetics, वाम् you both, वासः stay, कथम् how is it possible, अधर्मचरिणौ both unrighteous people, मुनिदूषकौ both distorting ascetic discipline, पापौ sinners, युवाम् both , कौ who are you?

#### Explanation

How is it that you both, dressed in the robes of ascetics, live  with a woman? Leading an unrighteous and sinful life, who are you who have brought disgrace to the ascetic discipline ?

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

अहं वनमिदं दुर्गं विराधो नाम राक्षसः। चरामि सायुधो नित्यमृषिमांसानि भक्षयन्।।3.2.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अहम् I, विराधो नाम Viradha by name, राक्षसः demon, सायुधः ready with arms, नित्यम् always, ऋषिमांसानि flesh of ascetics, भक्षयन् while eating, दुर्गम्  dense, इदम् here, वनम् forest, चरामि I wander.

#### Explanation

I am a demon named Viradha. I move in this dense forest with my weapons eating the flesh of the sages.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

इयं नारी वरारोहा मम भार्या भविष्यति। युवयोः पापयोश्चाहं पास्यामि रुधिरं मृधे।।3.2.13।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वरारोहा a beautiful woman (with fine hips), इयं नारी this woman, मम my, भार्या wife, भविष्यति will be, अहम् I, मृधे in the fight, पापयोः of both of you sinners, युवयोः of you both, रुधिरम् blood, पास्यामि I shall drink.

#### Explanation

This  woman of fine hips shall be my wife. You sinners I shall drink  the blood of both of you in the fight.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

तस्यैवं ब्रुवतो दुष्टं विराधस्य दुरात्मनः। श्रुत्वा सगर्वितं वाक्यं सम्भ्रान्ता जनकात्मजा।।3.2.14।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

एवम् in that way, ब्रुवतः while he spoke, दुरात्मनः of that wicked, तस्य विराधस्य that Viradha's, सगर्वितम् boastful, जनकात्मजा daughter of Janaka, दुष्टम् cruel, वाक्यम् statements, श्रुत्वा after hearing, सम्भ्रान्ता  bewildered out of fear.

#### Explanation

Sita, daughter of Janaka was bewildered out of fear when she heard the cruel  and boastful words of that wicked Viradha.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

सीता प्रवेपतोद्वेगात् प्रावाते कदली यथा।।3.2.15।। तां दृष्ट्वा राघवः सीतां विराधाङ्कगतां शुभाम्। अब्रवील्लक्ष्मणं वाक्यं मुखेन परिशुष्यता।।3.2.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सीता Sita, उद्वेगात् due to anxiety, प्रवाते in strong wind, कदली यथा like a plantain tree, प्रवेपिता was shaken, राघवः Rama, विराधाङ्कगताम् in the lap of Viradha, शुभाम् Sita, the auspicious, ताम् her, दृष्ट्वा on seeing, परिशुष्यता with a parched(pale), मुखेन with face, लक्ष्मणम् to Lakshmana, वाक्यम् these words, अब्रवीत् said.

#### Explanation

Seeing the auspicious Sita in the lap of Viradha shaken out of anxiety, like a plantain tree in a strong wind, Rama with his throat parched said to Lakshmana:

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

सीता प्रवेपतोद्वेगात् प्रावाते कदली यथा।।3.2.15।। तां दृष्ट्वा राघवः सीतां विराधाङ्कगतां शुभाम्। अब्रवील्लक्ष्मणं वाक्यं मुखेन परिशुष्यता।।3.2.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सीता Sita, उद्वेगात् due to anxiety, प्रवाते in strong wind, कदली यथा like a plantain tree, प्रवेपिता was shaken, राघवः Rama, विराधाङ्कगताम् in the lap of Viradha, शुभाम् Sita, the auspicious, ताम् her, दृष्ट्वा on seeing, परिशुष्यता with a parched(pale), मुखेन with face, लक्ष्मणम् to Lakshmana, वाक्यम् these words, अब्रवीत् said.

#### Explanation

Seeing the auspicious Sita in the lap of Viradha shaken out of anxiety, like a plantain tree in a strong wind, Rama with his throat parched said to Lakshmana:

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

पश्य सौम्य नरेन्द्रस्य जनकस्यात्मसम्भवाम्। मम भार्यां शुभाचारां विराधाङ्के प्रवेशिताम्।।3.2.17।। अत्यन्तसुखसंमव़ृद्धां राजपुत्रीं यशस्विनीम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सौम्य O gentle one, नरेन्द्रस्य king's, जनकस्य Janaka's, आत्मसम्भवाम् daughter, born of his own self, मम भार्याम् my wife, शुभाचाराम् of good conduct, अत्यन्तसुखसंवृद्धाम् brought up in utmost comfort, यशस्विनीम् an illustrious one, विराधाङ्के  in the lap of Viradha, प्रवेशिताम् entered, राजपुत्रीम्  princess, पश्य  see.

#### Explanation

Gentle Lakshmana, see the illustrious daughter ofJanaka, a lady of good conduct,  brought up in utmost comfort caught in the lap of Viradha

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

यदभिप्रेतमस्मासु प्रियं वरवृतं च यत्।।3.2.18।। कैकेय्यास्तु सुसंवृत्तं क्षिप्रमद्यैव लक्ष्मण।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लक्ष्मण O Lakshmana, अस्मासु for us, यत् whatever, अभिप्रेतम् desired, यत् that, वरवृतं च  those boons desired, कैकेय्याः by Kaikeyi's, प्रियम् joy, अद्यैव to day itself, क्षिप्रम् now itself, सुसंवृतम् attained well.

#### Explanation

O Lakshmana whatever Kaikeyi desired through her boons has now come true so soon.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

या न तुष्यति राज्येन पुत्रार्थे दीर्घदर्शिनी।।3.2.19।। ययाहं सर्वभूतानां प्रियः प्रस्थापितो वनम्। अद्येदानीं सकामा सा या माता मध्यमा मम।।3.2.20।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

दीर्घदर्शिनी farsighted lady, या such a lady as she is, पुत्रार्थे for her son, राज्येन with kingdom, न तुष्यति not being contended, यया by whomsoever, सर्वभूतानाम् of all beings, प्रियः dear, अहम् I, वनम् forest, प्रस्थापितः is sent, या whoever, मम my, मध्यमा माता middle mother, सा she, अद्य today, सकामा has her desire fulfilled.

#### Explanation

Kaikeyi, my middle mother is farsighted indeed  Not content with the kingdom alone see sent me, beloved of all  beings, away to the forest. Let her cherished desire be fulfilled.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

या न तुष्यति राज्येन पुत्रार्थे दीर्घदर्शिनी।।3.2.19।। ययाहं सर्वभूतानां प्रियः प्रस्थापितो वनम्। अद्येदानीं सकामा सा या माता मध्यमा मम।।3.2.20।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

दीर्घदर्शिनी farsighted lady, या such a lady as she is, पुत्रार्थे for her son, राज्येन with kingdom, न तुष्यति not being contended, यया by whomsoever, सर्वभूतानाम् of all beings, प्रियः dear, अहम् I, वनम् forest, प्रस्थापितः is sent, या whoever, मम my, मध्यमा माता middle mother, सा she, अद्य today, सकामा has her desire fulfilled.

#### Explanation

Kaikeyi, my middle mother is farsighted indeed  Not content with the kingdom alone see sent me, beloved of all  beings, away to the forest. Let her cherished desire be fulfilled.

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

परस्पर्शात्तु वैदेह्याः न दुःखतरमस्तिमे। पितुर्विनाशात्सौमित्रे  स्वराज्यहरणात्तथा।।3.2.21।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सौमित्रे O Lakshmana, वैदेह्याः Sita's, परस्पर्शात् the touch of a man other man, पितुः father's, विनाशात् more than demise, तथा in the same way, स्वराज्यहरणात् more than the the loss of my kingdom, मे to me, दुःखतरम् more painful, नास्ति is not.

#### Explanation

O Lakshmana nothing is more sorrowful to me than the touch of Vaidehi by another. It is more painful to me than the death of my father or loss of my kingdom.

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

इति ब्रुवति काकुत्स्थे बाष्पशोकपरिप्लुते। अब्रवील्लक्ष्मणः क्रुद्धो रुद्धो नाग इव श्वसन्।।3.2.22।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

काकुत्स्थे when Rama of the Kakutstha family, इति this way, ब्रुवति while he spoke, लक्ष्मणः Lakshmana, बाष्पशोकपरिप्लुते full of tears of sorrow, क्रुद्धः angry, रुद्धः tied up, नागः इव like a serpent, श्वसन् hissing, अब्रवीत् said.

#### Explanation

As Rama was thus full of tears of sorrow, Lakshmana sighed heavily in anger like a hissing serpant in confinement. And said:

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

अनाथ इव भूतानां नाथस्त्वं वासवोपमः। मया प्रेष्येण काकुत्स्थ किमर्थं परितप्यसे।।3.2.23।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

काकुत्स्थ O Rama, भूतानाम् of all beings, नाथः lord, वासवोपमः comparable to Indra, त्वम् you, अनाथः an orphan, इव like, प्रेष्येण by a servant (at your service), मया by myself, किमर्थम् for what reason, परितप्यसे feeling sad.

#### Explanation

O Rama, you are, like Indra, lord of all beings. What makes you feel sad like an orphan when I am at your service like a servant?

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

शरेण निहतस्याद्य मया क्रुद्धेन रक्षसः। विराधस्य गतासोर्हि मही पास्यति शोणितम्।।3.2.24।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

क्रुद्धेन by an angry one, मया by me, अद्य now, शरेण by the arrow, निहतस्य killed, गतासोः of one who loses life, रक्षसः of this demon, विराधस्य Viradha's, शोणितम् blood, मही the earth, पास्यति will drink.

#### Explanation

The earth will drink the blood of Viradha with his life gone, slain by my angry arrow.

---

### Shloka 25

#### Shloka Text

राज्यकामे मम क्रोधो भरते यो बभूव ह। तं विराधे प्रमोक्ष्यामि वज्री वज्रमिवाचले।।3.2.25।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राज्यकामे  one who was desirous of kingdom, भरते on Bharata, मम my, यः whatever, क्रोध: anger, बभूव ह was felt, तम् that, वज्री Indra, वज्रम् thunderbolt, अचले इव at a mountain, विराधे on Viradha, प्रमोक्ष्यामि I will release.

#### Explanation

Like Indra who hits the mountain with his thunderbolt, I shall release on Viradha the anger I felt against Bharata for his passion for the kingdom.

---

### Shloka 26

#### Shloka Text

मम भुजबलवेगवेगितः पततु शरोऽस्य महान्महोरसि। व्यपनयतु तनोश्च जीवितं पततु ततस्समहीं विघूर्णितः।।3.2.26।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मम my, भुजबलवेगवेगितः by the strength of my arms, महान् great, शरः arrow, अस्य his, महोरसि in high speed, पततु may fall, तनोः from the body, जीवितम् life, व्यपनयतु separate, ततः then, विघूर्णितः  reeling round and round, महीम् earth, पततु may fall down.

#### Explanation

Spurred by the speed of my strong arms, my arrow will fall on his broad chest and separate his life from his body. Then his body reeling round and round will fall on the ground. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये अरण्यकाण्डे द्वितीयस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the second sarga of Aranyakanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 3

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

इत्युक्त्वा लक्ष्मणश्श्रीमान्विराधे प्रहसन्निव। कोभवान्वनमभ्येत्य चरिष्यसि यथासुखम्।।3.3.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

इति उकत्वा having said so, लक्ष्मण श्रीमान्  graceful Lakshmana, प्रहसन्निव  with a smile, विराधे at Viradha, कः भवान् who are you? वनमभ्येत्य having come to the forest, यथासुखम् at will, चरिष्यसि you are roaming about.

#### Explanation

Graceful Lakshmana asked  Viradha  with a smile, Who are you wandering in the forest at will ?.

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

अथोवाच पुनर्वाक्यं विराधः पूरयन्वनम्। पृच्छतो मम हि ब्रूतं कौ युवां क्व गमिष्यथः।।3.3.2.।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अथ then, विराथः Viradha, वनम् forest, पूरयन् filling with echoes, पुनः again, वाक्यम् words, उवाच said, पृच्छतः asked, मम me, ब्रूतम् whereabouts, युवाम् you both, कौ who are you, क्व where, गमिष्यथः going.

#### Explanation

Then Viradha with his voice filling the forest said, Instead of asking about me you both should speak out who  you are and where you are going

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

तमुवाच ततो रामो राक्षसं ज्वलिताननम्। पृच्छन्तं सुमहातेजा इक्ष्वाकुकुलमात्मनः।।3.3.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः thereafter, सुमहातेजाः highly effulgent, रामः Rama, पृच्छन्तम् when asked, ज्वलिताननम् burning countenance, तं राक्षसम् to that demon, आत्मनः his, इक्ष्वाकुकुलम् Ikshvaku family, उवाच described.

#### Explanation

Questioned by the demon, who bore a burning countenance, the highly effulgent Rama introduced his Ikshvaku family:

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

क्षत्रियौ वृत्तसम्पन्नौ विद्धि नौ वनगोचरौ। त्वां तु वेदितुमिच्छावः कस्त्वं चरसि दण्डकान्।।3.3.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

नौ both of us, वृत्तसम्पन्नौ endowed with good lineage, वनगोचरौ both moving in the forest, क्षत्रियौ two kshatriyas, विद्धि know, त्वां तु about you, वेदितुम् wish to know, इच्छावः we both wish, दण्डकान् in the Dandaka forest, चरसि you are going about, त्वम् you, कः tell.

#### Explanation

Know us to be both kshatriyas, endowed with a good lineage and yet moving in the forest. We both wish to know, who you are and why you are going about in Dandaka forest?

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

तमुवाच विराधस्तु रामं सत्यपराक्रमम्। हन्त वक्ष्यामि ते राजन्निबोध मम राघव।।3.3.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

विराधस्तु Viradha also, सत्यपराक्रमम् one with truth as his prowess, तं रामम् to that Rama, उवाच said, हन्त O, राजन् to king, राघव Rama, ते to you, वक्ष्यामि I will tell, मम myself, निबोध  know.

#### Explanation

Then Viradha said to Rama whose truth was his strength, I will tell you who I am. O, listen:

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

पुत्रः किल जयस्याहं माता मम शतह्रदा। विराध इति मामाहुः पृथिव्यां सर्वराक्षसाः।।3.3.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अहम् I am, जयस्य Jaya's, पुत्रः son, किल indeed, शतह्रदा Satahrada, मम my, माता mother,  पृथिव्याम् on this earth, सर्वराक्षसाः all the demons, माम् me, विराधः Viradha इति so, आहुः call me.

#### Explanation

I am indeed son to Jaya. My mother is Satahrada. Verily all the demons on this earth call me Viradha.

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

तपसा चाभिसम्प्राप्ता ब्रह्मणो हि प्रसादजा। शस्त्रेणावध्यता लोकेऽच्छेद्याभेद्यत्वमेव च।।3.3.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तपसा through penance, ब्रह्मणः from Brahma, प्रसादजा bestowed by his pleasure, लोके in this world, शस्त्रेण with any weapon, अवध्यता not to be killed, अभिसम्प्राप्ता has been acquired, अच्छेद्याभेद्यत्वमेव च  not to be cut or broken by any.

#### Explanation

By the grace of Lord Brahma I was bestowed with this prowess through my penance. In this world none can kill me with a weapon nor split nor cut me to pieces.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

उत्सृज्य प्रमदामेनामनपेक्षौ यथागतम्। त्वरमाणौ पलायेथां न वां जीवितमाददे।। 3.3.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

एनाम् this lady, प्रमदाम् woman, उत्सृज्य on giving up, अनपेक्षौ without claiming her, त्वरमाणौ quickly, यथागतम् to the place where you came from, पलायेथाम् both of you may run, वाम् to you both, जीवितम् life, न आददे I will not take away.

#### Explanation

Therefore, leave this lady and run away to the place you came from. I will not take away your life .

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

तं रामः प्रत्युवाचेदं कोपसंरक्तलोचनः। राक्षसं विकृताकारं विराधं पापचेतसम्।।3.3.9।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रामः Rama, कोपसंरक्तलोचनः with eyes reddened due to anger, विकृताकारम् with deformed appearance, पापचेतसम् with sinful intentions, तम् him, विराधं राक्षसम् to demon Viradha, प्रत्युवाच replied.

#### Explanation

With eyes reddened with anger Rama replied to the sinful, deformed Viradha:

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

क्षुद्र धिक्त्वां तु हीनार्थं मृत्युमन्वेषसे ध्रुवम्। रणे प्राप्स्यसे सन्तिष्ठ न मे जीवन् गमिष्यासि।।3.3.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

क्षुद्र O vile creature, हीनार्थम् mean, त्वाम् you, धिक् fie upon, ध्रुवम् surely, मृत्युम् death, अन्वेषसे you are seeking, सन्तिष्ठ stay, रणे in war, प्राप्स्यसे you will attain, जीवन् while being alive, मे me, न गमिष्यासि you will not go.

#### Explanation

O vile, mean creature  surely you are seeking death. Wait, you will meet it in the fight.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

ततः सज्यं धनुः कृत्वा रामस्सुनिशिताञ्छरान्। सुशीघ्रमभिसन्धाय राक्षसं निजघान ह।।3.3.11।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, रामः Rama, धनुः bow, सज्यम् strung, कृत्वा after setting, सुनिशितान् sharp, शरान् arrows, सुशीघ्रम् quickly, अभिसन्धाय after aiming, राक्षसम् rakshasa, निजघान hit,  ह indeed.

#### Explanation

Then Rama strung his bow immediately, shot the sharp arrows at Viradha and hit him verily.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

धनुषा ज्यागुणवता सप्त बाणान्मुमोच ह। रुक्मपुङ्खान्महावेगान्सुपर्णानिलतुल्यगान्।।3.3.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

धनुषा bow, ज्यागुणवता string tied, महावेगान् swift ones, सुपर्णानिलतुल्यगान् in speed comparable to Garuda and the windgod, रुक्मपुङ्खान् with bright feathers, सप्त बाणान् seven arrows, मुमोच ह verily released.

#### Explanation

From his bow  strung tight,  Rama shot seven swift, goldenfeathered arrows comparable to Garuda  and the Windgod in speed.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

ते शरीरं विराधस्य भित्त्वाबर्हिणवाससः। निपेतुश्शोणितादिग्धा धरण्यां पावकोपमाः।।3.3.13।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पावकोपमाः looking like fire, ते they, बर्हिणवाससः arrows tied with peacock feathers, विराधस्य Viradha's, शरीरम् body, भित्त्वा after piercing, शोणितादिग्धाः drenched in blood, धरण्याम् on the ground, निपेतुः fell.

#### Explanation

The arrows tied with peacockfeathers, looking like fire, pierced the body of Viradha which, drenched in blood, fell on the ground.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

स विद्धो न्यस्य वैदेहीं शूलमुद्यम्य राक्षसः। अभ्यद्रवत्सुसङ्कृद्धस्तदा रामं सलक्ष्मणम्।।3.3.14।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

विद्धः Pierced, सः he, राक्षसः that rakshasa, तदा then, वैदेहीम् Sita, न्यस्य  left her, शूलम् a spear, उद्यम्य after lifting, सुसङ्कृद्धः with intense anger, सलक्ष्मणम् with Lakshmana, रामम् towards Rama, अभ्यद्रवत् ran towards.

#### Explanation

Hit  by Rama and Lakshmana, the enraged  demon left Vaidehi, lifted a sharp spear   and ran towards them.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

स विनद्य महानादं शूलं शक्रध्वजोपमम्। प्रगृह्याशोभत तदा व्यात्तानन इवान्तकः।।3.3.15।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तदा then, सः that, महानादम् loud noise, विनद्य producing, शक्रध्वजोपमम् like Indra's banner, शूलम् spike, प्रगृह्य taking, व्यात्ताननः with wide open mouth, अन्तकः god of death, इव like, अशोभत shone.

#### Explanation

The demon who produced a great noise with his wide open mouth resembled the god of death. He lifted a sharp spike that looked like Indra's banner.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

अथ तौ भ्रातरौ दीप्तं शरवर्षं ववर्षतुः। विराधे राक्षसे तस्मिन् कालान्तकयमोपमे।।3.3.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अथ and then, तौ both, भ्रातरौ brothers, कालान्तकयमोपमे looking like Yama, the god of death, तस्मिन् at him, राक्षसे  at that  demon, विराधे on Viradha, दीप्तम् bright, शरवर्षम् rain of arrows, ववर्षतुः showered.

#### Explanation

And then both the brothers showered bright arrows on the demon Viradha who looked like Yama, the god of death.

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

स प्रहस्य महारौद्रः स्थित्वाजृम्भत राक्षसः। जृम्भमाणस्य ते बाणाः कायान्निष्पेतुराशुगाः।।3.3.17।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महारौद्रः extremely frightening, सः राक्षस: that rakshasa, प्रहस्य after laughing, स्थित्वा  stood there, अजृम्भत yawned, जृम्भमाणस्य as he was yawning, कायात् from the body, आशुगाः swiftmoving, ते बाणाः those arrows, निष्पेतुः fell down.

#### Explanation

The demon who was extremely frightening stood there, laughing and yawning.  As he yawned, all the swiftlyflying arrows fell out of his body.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

स्पर्शात्तु वरदानेन प्राणान्सम्रोध्य राक्षसः। विराधः शूलमुद्यम्य राघवावभ्यधावत।।3.3.18।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षसः demon, विराधः Viradha, वरदानेन by virtue of a boon, स्पर्शात् तु by touch as well, प्राणान् (Prana, Apana, Vyana, Udana, and Samana) vital breath, संरोध्य  controlled, शूलम्  spike, उद्यम्य on taking out, राघवौ Rama and Lakshmana, अभ्यधावत ran after.

#### Explanation

By virtue of a boon Viradha, the demon, controlled the vital breath of his body by a mere touch and, raising the spike, ran towards the two Raghava princes.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

तच्छूलं वज्रसङ्काशं गगने ज्वलनोपमम्। द्वाभ्यां शराभ्यां चिच्छेद रामः शस्त्रभृतांवरः।।3.3.19।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

शस्त्रभृताम् among the wielders of bows, वरः best, रामः Rama, ज्वलनोपमम् like burning fire, वज्रसङ्काशम् resembling thunderbolt, तत् that, शूलम्  spike, द्वाभ्याम् with two, शराभ्याम् arrows, गगने in the sky, चिच्छेद broke asunder.

#### Explanation

With two arrows, Rama, the best among wielders of weapons, broke down the spike in the sky burning like fire, and resembling the  thunderbolt of Indra.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

तद्रामविशिखच्छिन्नं शूलं तस्यकराद्भुवि। पपाताशनिना छिन्नं मेरोरिव शिलातलम्।।3.3.20।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रामविशिखैः by Rama's arrow, छिन्नम् broken, तत् शूलम् spike, भुवि on the ground, अपतत् fell, अशनिना by the thunder, छिन्नम् broken into pieces, मेरोः of mount Meru, शिलातलम् इव like a slab, पपात fell down.

#### Explanation

The spike, broken down by Rama's arrow, fell like a slab of mount Meru splintered by Indra's thunder.

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

तौ खड्गौ क्षिप्रमुद्यम्य कृष्णसर्पोपमौशुभौ। तूर्णमापेततुस्तस्य तदा प्रहरतां बलात्।।3.3.21।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तदा then, तौ both, उद्यतौ coming on, कृष्णसर्पोपमौ both looking like black serpents, शुभौ auspicious, ख़ड्गौ the two swords, क्षिप्रम् quickly, उद्यम्य raising, तूर्णम् at once, तस्य at him, आपेततुः fell upon, बलात् strongly, प्रहरताम् struck  तस्य him.

#### Explanation

Then both Rama and Lakshmana quickly raised their swords which looked like black serpents and at once fell upon the demon who was going to strike with great force.

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

स वध्यमानः सुभृशं भुजाभ्यां परिरभ्यतौ। अप्रकम्प्यौ नरव्याघ्रौ रौद्रः प्रस्थातुमैच्छत।।3.3.22।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वध्यमानः being struck, रौद्रः angry one, सः he, अप्रकम्प्यौ the unshakeable two, नरव्याघ्रौ two tigerlike men, तौ both, भुजाभ्याम् with his arms, सुभृशम् tightly, परिगृह्य holding, प्रस्थातुम् to set out, ऐच्छत intended.

#### Explanation

Struck down, the angry demon tightly caught hold of both of them under his arms and wanted to set out, carrying Rama and Lakshmana, the two unshakeable tigers among men.

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

तस्याभिप्रायमाज्ञाय रामो लक्ष्मणमब्रवीत्। वहत्वयमलं तावत्पथाऽनेन तु राक्षसः।।3.3.23।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तस्य his, अभिप्रायम् intention, आज्ञाय having understood, रामः Rama, लक्ष्मणम् to Lakshmana, अब्रवीत् said, अयं राक्षसः this demon, अनेन पथा by this path, अलम् no need to prohibit, वहतु तावत् let him carry us.

#### Explanation

Rama understood the intention of the demon and said to Lakshmana, Let him go his way, no need to stop him.

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

यथा चेच्छति सौमित्रे तथा वहतु राक्षसः। अयमेव हि नः पन्था येन याति निशाचरः।।3.3.24।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सौमित्रे O Lakshmana, राक्षसः the demon, यथा as, इच्छति wishes to go, तथा like that, वहतु let him carry, निशाचरः nightstalker येन by which ever, याति moves, अयमेव this alone, सः such, पन्थाः हि direction only.

#### Explanation

O Lakshmana, let the nightstalker go the way he wishes. That is, in fact, our path.

---

### Shloka 25

#### Shloka Text

स तु स्वबलवीर्येण समुत्क्षिप्य निशाचरः। बालाविव स्कन्धगतौ चकारातिबलोद्धतः।।3.3.25।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अतिबलोद्धतः one puffed with great strength, सः that Viradha, निशाचरः तु rakshasa on his part, स्वबलवीर्येण by his own strength, समुत्क्षिप्य after lifting, बालाविव like two children, स्कन्धगतौ on both his shoulders, चकार made.

#### Explanation

Puffed up with his own great strength, Viradha lifted them and placed them on his shoulders as though they were two children.

---

### Shloka 26

#### Shloka Text

तावारोप्य ततः स्कन्धं राघवौ रजनीचरः। विराधो विनदन्घोरं जगामाभिमुखो वनम्।।3.3.26।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, रजनीचरः the nightstalker, विराधः Viradha, तौ both राघवौ Rama and Lakshmana, स्कन्धम् on the shoulders, आरोप्य keeping, घोरम् dreadfuly, विनदन् while screaming, वनम् अभिमुखः towards the forest, जगाम proceeded.

#### Explanation

Then the nightstalker proceeded towards the forest, carrying both the scions of the Raghu race on his shouldersroaring.

---

## Sarga 4

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

ह्रियमाणौ तु तौ दृष्ट्वा वैदेही रामलक्ष्मणौ। उच्चैस्स्वरेण चुक्रोश प्रगृह्य सुमहाजाभुजौ।।3.4.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वैदेही Sita, प्रगृह्य  holding, ह्रियमाणौ the two being abducted, सुमहाभुजौ two huge arms, रामलक्ष्मणौ Rama and Lakshmana, दृष्ट्वा  seeing, उच्चै स्वरेण at the top of her voice, चुक्रोश shouted.

#### Explanation

Seeing those two mightyarmed Rama and Lakshmana being carried away by Viradha, Sita started crying loudly:

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

एष दाशरथी रामः सत्यवान् शीलवान् शुचिः। रक्षसा रौद्ररूपेण ह्रियते सहलक्ष्मणः।।3.4.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सत्यवान् truthful , शीलवान् of good conduct, शुचिः pure, एषः this person, दाशरथिः son of Dasaratha, रामः Rama, सहलक्ष्मणः  with Lakshmana, रौद्ररूपेण dreadful in appearance, रक्षसा by The demon, ह्रियते is whisked away.

#### Explanation

None

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

मामृका भक्षयिष्यन्ति शार्दूलाद्वीपिनस्तथा। मां हरोत्सृज काकुत्स्थौ नमस्ते राक्षसोत्तम।।3.4.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

माम् me, ऋका wild bears, तथा in the same way, शार्दूलाद्वीपिनः tigers and panthers, भक्षयिष्यन्ति will eat me, राक्षसोत्तम O best of demons, माम् me, हर take away, काकुत्स्थौ Rama and Lakshmana, उत्सृज spare them, ते to you, नमः I salute.

#### Explanation

The wild bears, tigers and panthers will eat me up here. O best among demons, pray take me and leave both the Kakutsthas.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

तस्यास्तद्वचनं श्रुत्वा वैदेह्या रामलक्ष्मणौ। वेगं प्रचक्रतुर्वीरौ वधे तस्य दुरात्मनः।।3.4.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वीरौ heroic, रामलक्ष्मणौ Rama and Lakshmana, तस्याः वैदेह्याः that Sita's, तत् वचनम् those words, श्रुत्वा on hearing, दुरात्मनः of the wicked one, तस्य his, वधे to kill, वेगम् haste, प्रचक्रतुः began.

#### Explanation

On hearing those words of  Sita, both the heroes, Rama and Lakshmana hastened to kill the wicked Viradha.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

तस्य रौद्रस्य सौमित्रिः सव्यं बाहुं बभञ्ज ह। रामस्तु दक्षिणं बाहुं तरसा तस्य रक्षसः।।3.4.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सौमित्रिः Lakshmana, रौद्रस्य of that dreadful demon तस्य his, सव्यं बाहुम् left shoulder, बभञ्ज ह broke down, रामः Rama, तु too, तस्य रक्षसः that demon's, दक्षिणम् right, बाहुम् shoulder, तरसा quickly.

#### Explanation

Lakshmana quickly broke the left and Rama the right shoulder of that dreadful demon.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

स भग्नबाहुस्सविग्नो निपपाताशु राक्षसः। धरण्यां मेघसङ्काशो वज्रभिन्न इवाचलः।।3.4.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

भग्नबाहुः broken shoulders, मेघ सङ्काशः resembling cloud, सः he, संविग्नः anxious, राक्षसः demon, निपपातः fell down, वज्रभिन्नः split by a thunderbolt, अचलः इव like a mountain, आशु fell at once, धरण्याम् on the ground.

#### Explanation

With both shoulders broken, the demon, looking like a cloud (huge and dark) fell at once on the ground like a mountain split by a thunderbolt.

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

मुष्टिभिर्जानुभिः पद्भिः सूदयन्तौ तु राक्षसम्। उद्यम्योद्यम्य चाप्येनं स्थण्डिले निष्पिपेषतुः।।3.4.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षसम् to the demon, मुष्टिभिः with fists, जानुभिः with knees, पद्भि: with feet, सूदयन्तौ striking, एनम् him, उद्यम्योद्यम्य by lifting him again and again, स्थण्डिले on the bare ground, निष्पिपेषतुः pulverized .

#### Explanation

Striking the demon with fists, knees and feet, both of them smit him  on the bare ground and pulverized him.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

स विद्धो बहुभिर्बाणैः खङ्गाभ्यां च परिक्षतः। निष्पिष्टो बहुधा भूमौ न ममार स राक्षसः।।3.4.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

बहुभिः by many, बाणैः by arrows, विद्धः pierced, खङ्गाभ्याम् with two swords, परिक्षतः  wounded, बहुधा in many ways, भूमौ on the ground, निष्पिष्टः pulverized, सः राक्षसः that demon, न ममार was not killed.

#### Explanation

Although pierced by arrows, and wounded by the two swords, and pulverized on the ground, the demon did not die.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

तं प्रेक्ष्य रामः सुभृशमवध्यमचलोपमम्। भयेष्वभयदश्श्रीमानिदं वचनमब्रवीत्।।3.4.9।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

भयेषु from fear, अभयदः guaranter of safety, (who promises deliverance from fear), श्रीमान् illustrious, रामः Rama, अचलोपमम् mountainlike, तम् him, सुभृशम् very much, अवध्यम् could not be killed, प्रेक्ष्य after seeing, इदम् these, वचनम् words, अब्रवीत् said.

#### Explanation

Seeing the mountainlike Viradha not dying though seriously wounded, illustrious Rama, guaranter of safety from fear said to Lakshmana:

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

तपसा पुरुषव्याघ्र राक्षसोऽयं न शक्यते। शस्त्रेण युधि निर्जेतुं राक्षसं निखनावहे।।3.4.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पुरुषव्याघ्र O tiger among men, तपसा by penance, अयं राक्षसः this demon, युधि in war, शस्त्रेण by weapons, निर्जेतुम् to conquer, न शक्यते is not possible, राक्षसम् this demon, निखनावहे shall bury him.

#### Explanation

O Lakshmana, best among men, this demon by virtue of his penance cannot be killed with any weapon in war. Therefore, let us bury him.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

कुञ्जरस्येव रौद्रस्य राक्षसस्यास्य लक्ष्मण। वनेऽस्मिन् सुमहच्छ्वभ्रं खन्यतां रौद्रवर्चसः।।3.4.11।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लक्ष्मण Lakshmana, रौद्रस्य of the dreadful one, अस्य रक्षसः of this demon, कुञ्जरस्येव like an elephant, रौद्रवर्चसः one who emits a fearful glow, अस्मिन् in this, वने forest, सुमहत् very great, श्वभ्रम् a pit, खन्यताम् dig.

#### Explanation

Dig a deep pit to bury this demon, dreadful like an elephant and emitting a fearful glow.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

इत्युक्त्वा लक्ष्मणं रामः प्रदरः खन्यतामिति। तस्थौ विराधमाक्रम्य कण्ठे पादेन वीर्यवान्।।3.4.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वीर्यवान् courageous one, रामः Rama, लक्ष्मणम् to Lakshmana, इति thus, प्रदरः crevice, खन्यताम् may be dug up, इति thus, उक्त्वा having said, विराधम् Viradha, पादेन with his foot, कण्ठे on his neck, आक्रम्य holding firmly there, तस्थौ stood.

#### Explanation

Courageous Rama standing there put his foot firmly on the neck of Viradha and said to Lakshmana,' Dig up a crevice'.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

तच्छ्रुत्वा राघवेणोक्तं राक्षसः प्रश्रितं वचः। इदं प्रोवाच काकुत्थ्सं विराधः पुरुषर्षभम्।।3.4.13।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षसः the demon, विराधः Viradha, राघवेण by Rama, उक्तम् instructed, तत् that, श्रुत्वा on hearing, पुरुषर्षभम् best among men, काकुत्थ्सम् to Lakshmana, इदम् thus, प्रश्रितम् humble, वचः words, प्रोवाच uttered.

#### Explanation

When the demon Viradha heard Rama saying so, he said these humble words to the scion of the Kakutsthas and the best of men :

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

हतोऽस्मि पुरुषव्याघ्र शक्रतुल्यबलेन वै। मया तु पूर्वं त्वं मोहान्न ज्ञातः पुरुषर्षभ।।3.4.14।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पुरुषव्याघ्र O tiger among men, शक्रतुल्यबलेन equal to Indra in prowess, हतः अस्मि I am killed, पुरुषर्षभ bull among men, मया by me, मोहात् due to delusion, त्वम् you, पूर्वम् earlier, न ज्ञातः was not realised.

#### Explanation

You have killed me, O Rama, a tiger among men. You are equal to Indra in prowess. O best of men, out of delusion I could not recognise you earlier.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

कौसल्यासुप्रजा राम तातस्त्वं विदितो मया। वैदेही च महाभागा लक्ष्मणश्च महायशाः।।3.4.15।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तात O dear one, त्वम् you, कौसल्यासुप्रजाः the good son of Kausalya, रामः Rama, विदितः are known now, महाभागा a great lady, वैदेही च Sita too, महायशाः a man of great fame, लक्ष्मणश्च and Lakshmana too.

#### Explanation

O dear Rama, I know now you are the blessed son of Kausalya, Sita is a noble lady and a glorious soul.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

अभिशापादहं घोरां प्रविष्टो राक्षसीं तनुम्। तुम्बुरुर्नाम गन्धर्वः शप्तो वैश्रवणेन ह।।3.4.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अहम् I, अभिशापात् by a curse, घोराम् this terrible, राक्षसीम् demoniac, तनुम् body, प्रविष्टः I  have entered, वैश्रवणेन by Kubera, शप्तः I am cursed, तुम्बुरुः नाम by name Tumburu, गन्धर्वः celestial musician.

#### Explanation

I am Tumburu by name,  a celestial singer of the gandharva clan. Cursed by Kubera, I got this dreadful body of a demon.

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

प्रसाद्यमानश्च मया सोऽब्रवीन्मां महायशाः। यदा दाशरथी रामस्त्वां वधिष्यति संयुगे। तदा प्रकृतिमापन्नो भवान्स्वर्गं गमिष्यति।।3.4.17।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मया by me, प्रसाद्यमानः while being appeased, महायशा: illustrious, सः he, माम् me, अब्रवीत् said, दाशरथिः son of Dasaratha, रामः Rama, यदा when, त्वाम् you, संयुगे at war, वधिष्यति will kill, तदा then, प्रकृतिम् your original form, आपन्नः you will get back, भवान् you, स्वर्गम् to heaven, गमिष्यति you will go.

#### Explanation

When I appealed to him, that lord of great fame (Kubera) said that I would get back my original form and ascend to heaven when Rama, son of Dasaratha, would kill me in a duel.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

अनुपस्थीयमानो मां स क्रुद्धो व्याजहार ह। इति वैश्रवणो राजा रम्भासक्तमुवाच ह।।3.4.18।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः that, अनुपस्थीयमानः not being attended to, क्रुद्धः angry, माम् me, व्याजहार said, राजा king, वैश्रवणः Kubera, रम्भासक्तम्  passionately attached to Rambha, इति like this, उवाच ह cursed.

#### Explanation

When king Kubera saw me absent (from duty) as I was infatuated with Rambha, he got angry and cursed me.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

तव प्रसादान्मुक्तोऽहमिहशापात्सुदारुणात्। भुवनं स्वं गमिष्यामि स्वस्ति वोऽस्तु परन्तप।।3.4.19।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तव your, प्रसादात् by grace blessing, सुदारुणात् from the dreadful form, अभिशापात् from the  curse, परन्तप scorcher of enemies अहम् I, मुक्तः am relieved, स्वम् my own, भुवनम् abode, गमिष्यामि I will go, वः to you both, स्वस्ति wish you well, अस्तु may be.

#### Explanation

O scorcher of enemies by your grace I am rid of the curse. Delivered from my dreadful form, I will now go to my world. May both of you fare well.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

इतो वसति धर्मात्मा शरभङ्गः प्रतापवान्।।3.4.20।। अध्यर्धयोजने तात महर्षिस्सूर्यसन्निभः। तं क्षिप्रमधिगच्छ त्वं स ते श्रेयोऽभिधास्यति।।3.4.21।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तात O dear, इतः from here, अध्यर्धयोजने one and half yojanas away, (a yojana  about 8 miles) धर्मात्मा a righteous self, प्रतापवान् of great power, सूर्यसन्निभः comparable to the Sun, शरभङ्गः Sarabhanga, महर्षिः sage, वसति lives, त्वम् you, क्षिप्रम् quickly, तम् him, अधिगच्छ  approach, सः he, ते to you, श्रेयः welfare, अभिधास्यति bestow on you.

#### Explanation

O dear, there lives Sarabhanga, a righteous sage, comparable to the Sun, at a distance of one and a half yojanas from here. Go to him at once. He will bless you with your wellbeing.

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

इतो वसति धर्मात्मा शरभङ्गः प्रतापवान्।।3.4.20।। अध्यर्धयोजने तात महर्षिस्सूर्यसन्निभः। तं क्षिप्रमधिगच्छ त्वं स ते श्रेयोऽभिधास्यति।।3.4.21।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तात O dear, इतः from here, अध्यर्धयोजने one and half yojanas away, (a yojana  about 8 miles) धर्मात्मा a righteous self, प्रतापवान् of great power, सूर्यसन्निभः comparable to the Sun, शरभङ्गः Sarabhanga, महर्षिः sage, वसति lives, त्वम् you, क्षिप्रम् quickly, तम् him, अधिगच्छ  approach, सः he, ते to you, श्रेयः welfare, अभिधास्यति bestow on you.

#### Explanation

O dear, there lives Sarabhanga, a righteous sage, comparable to the Sun, at a distance of one and a half yojanas from here. Go to him at once. He will bless you with your wellbeing.

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

अवटे चापि मां राम प्रक्षिप्य कुशली व्रज। रक्षसां गतसत्त्वानामेष धर्मस्ससनातनः। अवटे ये निधीयन्ते तेषां लोकास्सनातनाः।।3.4.22।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राम Rama, माम् me, अवटे in the pit, प्रक्षिप्य on burying, कुशली proper, व्रज you may go, गतसत्त्वाम्  who are dead, रक्षसाम् of the demons, एषः this one, सनातनः eternal, धर्मः justice, ये those, अवटे विनिक्षिप्यन्ते buried in a pit, तेषाम् to them, सनातनाः eternal, लोकाः in other worlds (heaven)

#### Explanation

Bury me in the pit, O Rama, and proceed happily. This is a great tradition for the dead demons. Those who are buried in the pit attain heaven.

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

एवमुक्त्वा तु काकुत्थ्सं विराधश्शरपीडितः। बभूव स्वर्गसम्प्राप्तो न्यस्तदेहो महाबलः।।3.4.23।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महाबलः mighty strong, विराधः Viradha, काकुत्थ्सम् to the scion of the Kakutstha race, एवम् in that way, उक्त्वा having said, शरपीडितः hit by the arrows, न्यस्तदेहः casting his body, स्वर्गसम्प्राप्तः बभूव attained heaven.

#### Explanation

Having said so to Rama, the mighty  Viradha, hit by the arrows left his body and attained heaven.

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

तच्छ्रुत्वा राघवो वाक्यं लक्ष्मणं व्यादिदेश ह। कुञ्जरस्येव रौद्रस्य राक्षसस्यास्य लक्ष्मण।।3.4.24।। वनेऽस्मिन् सुमहच्छ्वभ्रं खन्यतां रौदकर्मणः। इत्युक्त्वा लक्ष्मणं रामः प्रदरः खन्यतामिति। तस्थौ विराधमाक्रम्य कण्ठे पादेन वीर्यवान्।।3.4.25।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राघवः Rama, तत् वाक्यम् those words, श्रुत्वा heard, लक्ष्मणम् Lakshmana, व्यादिदेश ordered, अस्यराक्षसस्य for this demon, रौद्रस्य of the dreadful, कुञ्जरस्येव like an elephant वनेऽस्मिन् in this forest, रौद्रकर्मणः of dreadful acts, सुमहत् wide, श्वभ्रः pit, खन्यताम् dig, रामः Rama, लक्ष्मणम् Lakshmana, प्रदरः a pit, खन्यताम् dig, इति in this way, वीर्यवान् valiant, पादेन by his foot, कण्ठे on the neck, विराधम् to Viradha, आक्रम्य occupying, तस्थौ stood firmly.

#### Explanation

When Rama heard those words, he said, O Lakshmana dig for this elephantlike demon of dreadful acts, a big pit. Having said so the mighty Rama stood firmly stamping the neck of Viradha with his foot.

---

### Shloka 25

#### Shloka Text

तच्छ्रुत्वा राघवो वाक्यं लक्ष्मणं व्यादिदेश ह। कुञ्जरस्येव रौद्रस्य राक्षसस्यास्य लक्ष्मण।।3.4.24।। वनेऽस्मिन् सुमहच्छ्वभ्रं खन्यतां रौदकर्मणः। इत्युक्त्वा लक्ष्मणं रामः प्रदरः खन्यतामिति। तस्थौ विराधमाक्रम्य कण्ठे पादेन वीर्यवान्।।3.4.25।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राघवः Rama, तत् वाक्यम् those words, श्रुत्वा heard, लक्ष्मणम् Lakshmana, व्यादिदेश ordered, अस्यराक्षसस्य for this demon, रौद्रस्य of the dreadful, कुञ्जरस्येव like an elephant वनेऽस्मिन् in this forest, रौद्रकर्मणः of dreadful acts, सुमहत् wide, श्वभ्रः pit, खन्यताम् dig, रामः Rama, लक्ष्मणम् Lakshmana, प्रदरः a pit, खन्यताम् dig, इति in this way, वीर्यवान् valiant, पादेन by his foot, कण्ठे on the neck, विराधम् to Viradha, आक्रम्य occupying, तस्थौ stood firmly.

#### Explanation

When Rama heard those words, he said, O Lakshmana dig for this elephantlike demon of dreadful acts, a big pit. Having said so the mighty Rama stood firmly stamping the neck of Viradha with his foot.

---

### Shloka 26

#### Shloka Text

ततः खनित्रमादाय लक्ष्मणश्श्वभ्रमुत्तमम्। अखनत्पार्श्वतस्तस्य विराधस्य महात्मनः।।3.4.26।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः thereafter, लक्ष्मणः Lakshmana, खनित्रम् spade, आदाय fetched, महात्मनः of the great soul, तस्यविराधस्य that Viradha's, पार्श्वत: by his side, उत्तमम् sufficiently big, श्वभ्रम् pit, अखनत् dug.

#### Explanation

Then Lakshmana fetched a spade and dug a sufficiently big pit by the side of the great soul Viradha.

---

### Shloka 27

#### Shloka Text

तं मुक्तकण्ठंनिक्षिप्य शङ्कुकर्णं महास्वनम्। विराधं प्राक्षिपच्छ्वभ्रे नदन्तं भैरवस्वनम्।।3.4.27।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मुक्तकण्ठनिक्षिप्य released pressure on the neck, महास्वनम् with loud voice, शङ्कुकर्णम्   of pointed ears as an iron peg, भैरवस्वनम् fearful sound नदन्तम् making, तं विराधम् that Viradha, श्वभ्रे in the pit, प्राक्षिपत् dropped.

#### Explanation

Rama took his foot off Viradha's neck and dropped his body with pointed ears into the pit while Viradha was groaning fearfully৷৷

---

### Shloka 28

#### Shloka Text

तमाहवे दारुणमाशुविक्रमौ स्थिरावुभौ संयति रामलक्ष्मणौ। मुदान्वितौ चिक्षिपतुर्भयावहं नदन्तमुत्क्षिप्य बिले तु राक्षसम्।।3.4.28।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

आशुविक्रमौ both warriors of quick and brave action, संयति in this battle, स्थिरौ stable, रामलक्ष्मणौ Rama and Lakshmana, उभौ both, मुदा joy, अन्वितौ both endowed, आहवे in the war, दारुणम् dreadful, भयावहम् frightening, नदन्तम् was roaring तं राक्षसम् that demon, बलेन forcefully, उत्क्षिप्य  lifted, चिक्षिपतुः both of them  threw him down .

#### Explanation

Rama and Lakshmana, stable and firm, brave and quick, together lifted the body of Viradha who was fierce in battle and roaring and forcibly threw it into the pit with joy.

---

### Shloka 29

#### Shloka Text

अवध्यतां प्रेक्ष्य महासुरस्य तौ शितेन शस्त्रेण तदा नरर्षभौ। समर्थ्य चात्यर्थविशारदावुभौ बिले विराधस्य वधं प्रचक्रतुः।।3.4.29।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तदा then, नरर्षभौ both bulls among men, अत्यर्थविशारदौ very efficient, तौ उभौ both of them, शितेन with sharp, शस्त्रेण weapons, महासुरस्य of that mighty demon, तस्य विराधस्य of that Viradha, अवध्यताम् not to be killed in any other way, प्रेक्ष्य having seen, समर्थ्य after reflecting over the issue, बिले in hole, वधं killed, प्रचक्रतुः both undertook.

#### Explanation

The two princes, bulls among men, who were adept in archery saw that the great demon could not be killed by any other means. They reflected over the issue  and undertook the task of killing him with sharp weapons and buried him in a pit.

---

### Shloka 30

#### Shloka Text

स्वयं विराधेन हि मृत्युरात्मनः प्रसह्य रामेण यथार्थमीप्सितः। निवेदितः काननचारिणा स्वयं न मे वधः शस्त्रकृतो भवेदिति।।3.4.30।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

विराधेन by Viradha, स्वयम् himself, आत्मनः for him, मृत्युः death, यथार्थम् truly, प्रसह्य forcibly, रामेण by Rama, ईप्सितः wished to, काननचारिणा one by the forestranger, मे  for me, शस्त्र कृतः by the weapons, वधः kill, न भवेत् not possible for you, इति thus, स्वयम् he himself, निवेदितः was revealed.

#### Explanation

Rama deliberately wanted to kill Viradha with weapons. Viradha the forestranger himself revealed that it was not possible to kill him with weapons and asked Rama to bury him in a pit.

---

### Shloka 31

#### Shloka Text

तदेव रामेण निशम्य भाषितं कृता मतिस्तस्य बिलप्रवेशने। बिलं च रामेणातिबलेन रक्षसा प्रवेश्यमानेन वनं विनादितम्।।3.4.31।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तत् then, भाषितमेव words spoken, निशम्य hearing, रामेण by Rama, तस्य his, बिलप्रवेशने putting him in the pit, मतिः mind, कृता determined, बिलम् pit, प्रवेश्यमानेन while placing, अतिबलेन  mighty, रामेण by Rama, रक्षसा  by the demons, वनम् forest, विनादितम् echoed.

#### Explanation

While Rama, who had made up his mind after hearing him, was putting the mighty demon in the pit, the forest rang with his roar.

---

### Shloka 32

#### Shloka Text

प्रहृष्टरूपाविव रामलक्ष्मणौ विराधमुर्व्या प्रदरे निहत्य तौ। ननन्दतुर्वीतभयौ महावने शिलाभिरन्तर्दधतुश्च राक्षसम्।।3.4.32।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

प्रहृष्टरूपाविव both joyful, रामलक्ष्मणौ Rama and Lakshmana, तं विराधम् that Viradha, उर्व्याः earth's, प्रदरे in the crevice, निहत्य after killing, वीतभयौ both fearlessly, महावने in the great forest, ननन्दतुः both rejoiced, राक्षसम् to the demon, शिलाभिः with rocks, अन्तर्दधतुश्च  covered him.

#### Explanation

Rama and Lakshmana were happy to consign Viradha's body to the pit after killing him. They filled the pit with rocks in the great forest without fear.

---

## Sarga 5

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

हत्वा तु तं भीमबलं विराधं राक्षसं वने। ततस्सीतां परिष्वज्य समाश्वास्य च वीर्यवान्।।3.5.1।। अब्रवील्लक्ष्मणं रामो भ्रातरं दीप्ततेजसम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वीर्यवान् heroic, रामः Rama, भीमबलम् of terrific strength, राक्षसम् demon, तं विराधम् that Viradha, वने in the forest, हत्वा on killing, ततः thereafter, सीताम् Sita, परिष्वज्य embraced, समाश्वास्य च connsoled, दीप्ततेजसम् glowing like fire, भ्रातरम्  brother,  अब्रवीत् said.

#### Explanation

On killing Viradha, the demon of terrific strength, heroic Rama embraced Sita, consoled her and said to his brother Lakshmana, glowing like fire:

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

कष्टं वनमिदं दुर्गं न च स्म वनगोचराः।।3.5.2।। अभिगच्छामहे शीघ्रं शरभङ्गं तपोधनम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

इदम् this, दुर्गम् wild, वनम् forest, कष्टम् is difficult, वनगोचराः uracquainted, न च स्मः we are not, शीघ्रम् quickly, तपोधनम् to the sage, शरभङ्गम् Sarabhanga, अभिगच्छामहे we shall go.

#### Explanation

This wild forest with which we have not been acquainted earlier is difficult  to pass. (Hence) let us proceed quickly to sage Sarabhanga.

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

आश्रमं शरभङ्गस्य राघवोऽभिजगाम ह।।3.5.3।। तस्य देवप्रभावस्य तपसा भावितात्मनः। समीपे शरभङ्गस्य ददर्श महदद्भुतम्।।3.5.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राघवः Rama, शरभङ्गस्य Sarabhanga's, आश्रमम् hermitage, अभिजगाम ह reached, देवप्रभावस्य of divine grace, तपसा by penance, भावितात्मनः had realised his self, तस्य शरभङ्गस्य that Sarabhanga's, समीपे  nearby, महत् great, अद्भुतम् wonderful phenomenon, ददर्श saw.

#### Explanation

Rama went to the hermitage of Sarabhanga, a sage with divine grace who had realised the supreme (by his penance). Thus by the side of the sage he witnessed a huge wonder.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

आश्रमं शरभङ्गस्य राघवोऽभिजगाम ह।।3.5.3।। तस्य देवप्रभावस्य तपसा भावितात्मनः। समीपे शरभङ्गस्य ददर्श महदद्भुतम्।।3.5.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राघवः Rama, शरभङ्गस्य Sarabhanga's, आश्रमम् hermitage, अभिजगाम ह reached, देवप्रभावस्य of divine grace, तपसा by penance, भावितात्मनः had realised his self, तस्य शरभङ्गस्य that Sarabhanga's, समीपे  nearby, महत् great, अद्भुतम् wonderful phenomenon, ददर्श saw.

#### Explanation

Rama went to the hermitage of Sarabhanga, a sage with divine grace who had realised the supreme (by his penance). Thus by the side of the sage he witnessed a huge wonder.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

विभ्राजमानं वपुषा सूर्यवैश्वानरोपमम्। अवरुह्यरथोत्सङ्गादाकाशे विबुधानुगम।।3.5.5।। असंस्पृशन्तं वसुधां ददर्श विबुधेश्वरम्। सुप्रभाभरणं देवं विरजोऽम्बरधारिणम्।।3.5.6।। तद्विधैरेव बहुभिः पूज्यमानं महात्मभिः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वपुषा with his body, विभ्राजमानम् shining, सूर्यवैश्वानरोपमम् like the Sun or the fire, रथोत्सङ्गात् from the the lap of the chariot, अवरुह्य descending, आकाशे in the sky, विबुधानुगम followed by the gods, विबुधेस्वरम् lord of the gods (Indra), वसुधाम् earth, असंस्पृशन्तं without touching, सुप्रभाभरणम् adorned with shining ornaments, विरजोऽम्बरधारिणम् wearing pure white clothes (that never got soiled), तद्विधैरेव like him, बहुभिः many, महात्मभिः by great souls, पूज्यमानम्  worshipped, देवम् deity, विबुदेश्वरम् lord of the gods (Indra), ददर्श witnessed.

#### Explanation

Rama saw the lord of the gods (Indra) in the sky getting off the chariot, with his body shining brightly like the sun or the fire, followed by the gods, his feet untouched by the earth. Clad in spotless attire and adorned with shining ornaments, he was being worshipped by many greatsouls like him.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

विभ्राजमानं वपुषा सूर्यवैश्वानरोपमम्। अवरुह्यरथोत्सङ्गादाकाशे विबुधानुगम।।3.5.5।। असंस्पृशन्तं वसुधां ददर्श विबुधेश्वरम्। सुप्रभाभरणं देवं विरजोऽम्बरधारिणम्।।3.5.6।। तद्विधैरेव बहुभिः पूज्यमानं महात्मभिः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वपुषा with his body, विभ्राजमानम् shining, सूर्यवैश्वानरोपमम् like the Sun or the fire, रथोत्सङ्गात् from the the lap of the chariot, अवरुह्य descending, आकाशे in the sky, विबुधानुगम followed by the gods, विबुधेस्वरम् lord of the gods (Indra), वसुधाम् earth, असंस्पृशन्तं without touching, सुप्रभाभरणम् adorned with shining ornaments, विरजोऽम्बरधारिणम् wearing pure white clothes (that never got soiled), तद्विधैरेव like him, बहुभिः many, महात्मभिः by great souls, पूज्यमानम्  worshipped, देवम् deity, विबुदेश्वरम् lord of the gods (Indra), ददर्श witnessed.

#### Explanation

Rama saw the lord of the gods (Indra) in the sky getting off the chariot, with his body shining brightly like the sun or the fire, followed by the gods, his feet untouched by the earth. Clad in spotless attire and adorned with shining ornaments, he was being worshipped by many greatsouls like him.

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

हरिभिर्वाजिभिर्युक्तमन्तरिक्षगतं रथम्।।3.5.7।। ददर्शादूरतस्तस्य तरुणादित्यसन्निभम्। पाण्डुराभ्रघनप्रख्यं चन्द्रमण्डलसन्निभम्।।3.5.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तस्य from that place, अदूरतः not far from, हरितैः by tawnycoloured, वाजिभिः by horses, युक्तम् yoked with, तरुणादित्यसन्निभम् shining like the rising sun, पाण्डुराभ्रघनप्रख्यम् comparable to a palewhite cloud, चन्द्रमण्डलसन्निभम् shining like the lunar orb, अन्तरिक्षगतम् found in the sky, रथम् chariot, ददर्श witnessed.

#### Explanation

Not far from that place, Rama saw a chariot, yoked with tawnycoloured horses, shining like the morning  Sun. It looked like a palewhite cloud, resembling the lunar orb.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

हरिभिर्वाजिभिर्युक्तमन्तरिक्षगतं रथम्।।3.5.7।। ददर्शादूरतस्तस्य तरुणादित्यसन्निभम्। पाण्डुराभ्रघनप्रख्यं चन्द्रमण्डलसन्निभम्।।3.5.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तस्य from that place, अदूरतः not far from, हरितैः by tawnycoloured, वाजिभिः by horses, युक्तम् yoked with, तरुणादित्यसन्निभम् shining like the rising sun, पाण्डुराभ्रघनप्रख्यम् comparable to a palewhite cloud, चन्द्रमण्डलसन्निभम् shining like the lunar orb, अन्तरिक्षगतम् found in the sky, रथम् chariot, ददर्श witnessed.

#### Explanation

Not far from that place, Rama saw a chariot, yoked with tawnycoloured horses, shining like the morning  Sun. It looked like a palewhite cloud, resembling the lunar orb.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

अपश्यद्विमलं छत्रं चित्रमाल्योपशोभितम्। चामरव्यजने चाग्र्ये रुक्मदण्डे महाधने।।3.5.9।। गृहीते वरनारीभ्यां धूयमाने च मूर्धनि।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

विमलम् pure white, चित्रमाल्योपशोभितम् decked with variegated flower garlands, छत्रम् umbrella, अग्य्रे  in the forefront, रुक्मदण्डे golden staff, महाधने very costly, वरनारीभ्याम् by two excellent women, गृहीते held, मूर्धनि near the head, धूयमाने fanning, चामरव्यजने  'Chamara' made of yak's tail, अपश्यत् saw.

#### Explanation

Rama saw a pure white umbrella with a highly expensive golden staff, decorated with variegated flower garlands.While near his (Indra's) head stood a pair of exquisite ladies with fans made of yak's tail.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

गन्धर्वामरसिद्धाश्च बहवः परमर्षयः।।3.5.10।। अन्तरिक्षगतं देवं वाग्भिरग्य्राभिरीडिरे।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

गन्धर्वामरसिद्धाश्च celestial musicians, gods and siddhas, बहवः many, परमर्षयः great sages, अन्तरिक्षगतम् stationed in the sky, देवम् deity, अग्य्राभिः by choicest, वाग्भि: with words, ईडिरे praised.

#### Explanation

Many celestial singers of heaven, gods, great sages and siddhas sang with the choicest words the glory of this god stationed in the sky.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

सह सम्भाषमाणे तु शरभङ्गेन वासवे।।3.5.11।। दृष्ट्वा शतक्रतुं तत्र रामो लक्ष्मणमब्रवीत्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वासवे Indra, शरभङ्गेन with Sarabhanga, सह सम्भाषमाणे was conversing with, तत्र there, शतक्रतुम् Indra, दृष्ट्वा  seeing, रामः Rama, लक्ष्मणम् Lakshmana, अब्रवीत् said.

#### Explanation

While Indra was conversing with Sarabhanga, Rama saw him and said to Lakshmana.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

रामोऽथ रथमुद्दिश्य लक्ष्मणायप्रदर्शयन्।।3.5.12।। अर्चिष्मन्तं श्रिया जुष्टमद्भुतं पश्य लक्ष्मण। प्रतपन्तमिवादित्यमन्तरिक्षगतं रथम्।।3.5.13।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अथ then, रामः Rama, रथम् chariot, उद्दिश्य pointing at, अद्भुतम् wonderful one, भ्रातुः brother, प्रदर्शयन् while showing, लक्ष्मण Lakshmana, अर्चिष्मन्तम् effulgent, श्रिया with grace, जुष्टम् endowed with, अद्भुतम् wonderful, प्रतपन्तम् shining, आदित्यमिव like the Sun, अन्तरिक्षगतम् in the sky, रथम् chariot, पश्य see,

#### Explanation

Pointing at the chariot, Rama said, Lakshmana, see this wonderful, effulgent chariot endowed with grace is shining like the Sun in the sky.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

रामोऽथ रथमुद्दिश्य लक्ष्मणायप्रदर्शयन्।।3.5.12।। अर्चिष्मन्तं श्रिया जुष्टमद्भुतं पश्य लक्ष्मण। प्रतपन्तमिवादित्यमन्तरिक्षगतं रथम्।।3.5.13।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अथ then, रामः Rama, रथम् chariot, उद्दिश्य pointing at, अद्भुतम् wonderful one, भ्रातुः brother, प्रदर्शयन् while showing, लक्ष्मण Lakshmana, अर्चिष्मन्तम् effulgent, श्रिया with grace, जुष्टम् endowed with, अद्भुतम् wonderful, प्रतपन्तम् shining, आदित्यमिव like the Sun, अन्तरिक्षगतम् in the sky, रथम् chariot, पश्य see,

#### Explanation

Pointing at the chariot, Rama said, Lakshmana, see this wonderful, effulgent chariot endowed with grace is shining like the Sun in the sky.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

ये हयाः पुरुहूतस्य पुरा शक्रस्य नश्श्रुताः। अन्तरिक्षगता दिव्यास्त इमे हरयो ध्रुवम्।।3.5.14।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पुरुहूतस्य of Indra who is often invoked, शक्रस्य of Indra, ये those, हयाः horses, नः for us, पुरा earlier, श्रुताः heard, ते those, हरयः horses, अन्तरिक्षगताः stationed in the sky, दिव्याः wonderful, इमे we, ध्रुवम् surely.

#### Explanation

The horses of Indra, who is often invoked (during sacrificial rituals) and about whom  we have heard earlier, are now stationed in the sky.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

इमे च पुरुषव्याघ्रा ये तिष्ठ्न्त्यभितो रथम्। शतं शतं कुण्डलिनो युवानः खङ्गपाणयः।।3.5.15।। विस्तीर्णविपुलोरस्काः परिघायतबाहवः। शोणांशुवसनास्सर्वे व्याध्रा इव दुरासदाः।।3.5.16।। उरोदेशेषु सर्वेषां हारा ज्वलनसन्निभाः। रूपं बिभ्रति सौमित्रे पञ्चविंशतिवार्षिकम्।।3.5.17।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सौमित्रे O Lakshmana, पुरुषव्याघ्राः finest among men, दिशम् अभितः in each direction, ये those, शतं शतम् in hundreds , कुण्डलिनः wearing earrings, खङ्गपाणयः holding swords in their hands, विस्तीर्णविपुलोरस्काः broadchested, परिघायतबाहवः with iron beamlike strong arms, शोणांशुवसनाः clad in red clothes, युवानः youth, इमे सर्वे all of them, व्याघ्रा इव like tigers, दुरासदाः difficult to overpower, सर्वेषाम् all of them, उरोदेशेषु in their chest, ज्वलनसन्निभाः flaming like fire, हाराः necklaces, पञ्चविंशतिवार्षिकम् of twentyfive years of age, रूपम् appearance, बिभ्रति appear.

#### Explanation

O Lakshmana  around the chariot stand hundreds of finest young men of about twentyfive years. Clad in red robes, they are wearing earrings, and holding swords in their hands. They have broad chests and stout arms like iron beams. Like tigers, they are difficult to overpower. They have necklaces hanging on their chests blazing like fire.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

इमे च पुरुषव्याघ्रा ये तिष्ठ्न्त्यभितो रथम्। शतं शतं कुण्डलिनो युवानः खङ्गपाणयः।।3.5.15।। विस्तीर्णविपुलोरस्काः परिघायतबाहवः। शोणांशुवसनास्सर्वे व्याध्रा इव दुरासदाः।।3.5.16।। उरोदेशेषु सर्वेषां हारा ज्वलनसन्निभाः। रूपं बिभ्रति सौमित्रे पञ्चविंशतिवार्षिकम्।।3.5.17।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सौमित्रे O Lakshmana, पुरुषव्याघ्राः finest among men, दिशम् अभितः in each direction, ये those, शतं शतम् in hundreds , कुण्डलिनः wearing earrings, खङ्गपाणयः holding swords in their hands, विस्तीर्णविपुलोरस्काः broadchested, परिघायतबाहवः with iron beamlike strong arms, शोणांशुवसनाः clad in red clothes, युवानः youth, इमे सर्वे all of them, व्याघ्रा इव like tigers, दुरासदाः difficult to overpower, सर्वेषाम् all of them, उरोदेशेषु in their chest, ज्वलनसन्निभाः flaming like fire, हाराः necklaces, पञ्चविंशतिवार्षिकम् of twentyfive years of age, रूपम् appearance, बिभ्रति appear.

#### Explanation

O Lakshmana  around the chariot stand hundreds of finest young men of about twentyfive years. Clad in red robes, they are wearing earrings, and holding swords in their hands. They have broad chests and stout arms like iron beams. Like tigers, they are difficult to overpower. They have necklaces hanging on their chests blazing like fire.

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

इमे च पुरुषव्याघ्रा ये तिष्ठ्न्त्यभितो रथम्। शतं शतं कुण्डलिनो युवानः खङ्गपाणयः।।3.5.15।। विस्तीर्णविपुलोरस्काः परिघायतबाहवः। शोणांशुवसनास्सर्वे व्याध्रा इव दुरासदाः।।3.5.16।। उरोदेशेषु सर्वेषां हारा ज्वलनसन्निभाः। रूपं बिभ्रति सौमित्रे पञ्चविंशतिवार्षिकम्।।3.5.17।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सौमित्रे O Lakshmana, पुरुषव्याघ्राः finest among men, दिशम् अभितः in each direction, ये those, शतं शतम् in hundreds , कुण्डलिनः wearing earrings, खङ्गपाणयः holding swords in their hands, विस्तीर्णविपुलोरस्काः broadchested, परिघायतबाहवः with iron beamlike strong arms, शोणांशुवसनाः clad in red clothes, युवानः youth, इमे सर्वे all of them, व्याघ्रा इव like tigers, दुरासदाः difficult to overpower, सर्वेषाम् all of them, उरोदेशेषु in their chest, ज्वलनसन्निभाः flaming like fire, हाराः necklaces, पञ्चविंशतिवार्षिकम् of twentyfive years of age, रूपम् appearance, बिभ्रति appear.

#### Explanation

O Lakshmana  around the chariot stand hundreds of finest young men of about twentyfive years. Clad in red robes, they are wearing earrings, and holding swords in their hands. They have broad chests and stout arms like iron beams. Like tigers, they are difficult to overpower. They have necklaces hanging on their chests blazing like fire.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

एतद्दि किल देवानां वयो भवति नित्यदा। यथेमे पुरुषव्याघ्रा दृश्यन्ते प्रियदर्शनाः।।3.5.18।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation
