तौ both of them, दुर्वृत्ताम् woman of bad behaviour, तां च her also, हत्वा after slaying, अपावर्तितुम् get from there, अर्हथ you should, मम my, इयं भगिनी this sister, तेषाम् their, रुधिरम् blood, पास्यति will drink.

#### Explanation

I want you to get  both of them along with that notorious woman killed. This sister of mine will drink their blood.

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

मनोरथोऽयमिष्टोऽस्या भगिन्या मम राक्षसाः। शीघ्रं सम्पाद्यतां तौ च प्रमथ्य स्वेन तेजसा।।3.19.24।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षसाः O demons, स्वेन by your, तेजसा power, तौ both of them, शीघ्रम् quickly, प्रमथ्य after impounding, अस्याः her, मम भगिन्याः my sister's, इष्टः very dear, मनोरथः desire, शीघ्रम् swiftly, सम्पाद्यताम् be fulfiled.

#### Explanation

O demons quickly go and crush them with your power and fulfil my sister's sincere wish.

---

### Shloka 25

#### Shloka Text

इति प्रतिसमादिष्टा राक्षसास्ते चतुर्दश। तत्र जग्मुस्तया सार्धं घना वातेरिता यथा।।3.19.25।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

इति this way, प्रतिसमादिष्टाः having been ordered, ते they, चतुर्दश fourteen, राक्षसाः demons, तया सार्धम् together with Surpanakha, वातेरिताः as if blown by wind, घनाः यथा like clouds, तत्र there, जग्मुः went.

#### Explanation

Thus commanded, the fourteen demons along with Surpanakha rushed there like windblown clouds.

---

### Shloka 26

#### Shloka Text

ततस्तु ते तं समुदग्रतेजसं तथापिऽतीक्ष्णप्रदरा निशाचराः। न शेकुरेनं सहसा प्रमर्दितुं वनद्विपा दीप्तमिवाग्निमुत्थितम्।।3.19.26।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, तथा like that, तीक्ष्णप्रदराः strike with sharp weapons, ते निशाचराः those nightrangers, वनद्विपाः wild elephants, उत्थितम् risen up, दीप्तम् kindled, अग्निम् इव like fire, समुदग्रतेजसम् glowing in front, तम् him, एनम् this person, सहसा all at once, प्रमर्धितुम् to crush, न शेकुः here not able to.

#### Explanation

The nightrangers were unable to crush him(Rama) despite their sharp, glowing weapons  just as wild elephants cannot face all at once the rising flame of the forest fire. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये अरण्यकाण्डे एकोनविंशस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the nineteenth sarga of Aranyakanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 20

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

ततश्शूर्पणखा घोरा राघवाश्रममागता। राक्षसानाचचक्षे तौ भ्रातरौ सह सीतया।।3.20.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः thereafter, घोरा frightful, शूर्पणखा Surpanakha, राघवाश्रमम् Rama's hermitage, आगता came, राक्षसान् to the demons, सह सीतया with Sita, भ्रातरौ brothers, तौ both, आचचक्षे spoke about.

#### Explanation

The frightful Surpanakha came to the hermitage of Rama and showed both the brothers and Sita to the demons.

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

ते रामं पर्णशालायामुपविष्टं महाबलम्। ददृशुस्सीतया सार्धं वैदेह्या लक्ष्मणेन च।3.20.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ते they, वैदेह्याः with Vaidehi, सार्धम् together with, लक्ष्मणेन च and with Lakshmana, पर्णशालायाम् in the leafthatched cottage, उपविष्टम् seated, महाबलम् a man of great strength, रामम् Rama, ददृशुः saw.

#### Explanation

They saw mighty Rama together with Vaidehi and Lakshmana sitting in the leafthatched cottage.

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

तान्दृष्ट्वा राघवश्श्रीमानागतांस्तां च राक्षसीम्। अब्रवीद्भ्रातरं रामो लक्ष्मणं दीप्ततेजसम्।।3.20.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

श्रीमान् handsome, राघवः scion of the Raghu dynasty, रामः Rama, आगतान् those who arrived, तान् them, तां राक्षसीम्  to the demoness, दृष्ट्वा  seeing, दीप्ततेजसम्  blazing like fire, भ्रातरम् brother, लक्ष्मणम् to Lakshmana, अब्रवीत् said.

#### Explanation

Seeing the demons along with Surpanakha arrive, said the handsome Rama, scion of the Raghu dynasty to his brother Lakshmana, who was blazing like fire :

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

मुहूर्तं भव सौमित्रे सीतायाः प्रत्यनन्तरः। इमानस्या वधिष्यामि पदवीमागतानिह।।3.20.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सौमित्रे O Lakshmana, मुहूर्तम् for a while, सीतायाः to Sita, प्रत्यनन्तरः stand by, भव you may be, अस्याः her, पदवीम् in the way, इह here, आगतान् those who came in, इमान् these to, वधिष्यामि will kill.

#### Explanation

O Lakshmana  stand by Sita for a while. I shall kill these demons who have come here.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

वाक्यमेतत्ततश्श्रुत्वा रामस्य विदितात्मनः। तथेति लक्ष्मणो वाक्यं रामस्य प्रत्यपूजयत्।।3.20.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, लक्ष्मणः Lakshmana, विदितात्मनः knower of the self, रामस्य Rama's, एतत् this, वाक्यम् statement, श्रुत्वा after hearing, तथेति agreeing to, रामस्य  Rama's, वाक्यम् statement, प्रत्यपूजयत् in turn honoured.

#### Explanation

Lakshmana heard him and in turn honoured the word of Rama, knower of the self.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

राघवोऽपि महच्चापं चामीकरविभूषितम्। चकार सज्यं धर्मात्मा तानि रक्षांसि चाब्रवीत्।।3.20.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

धर्मात्मा righteous man, राघवोऽपि Rama too, चामीकरविभूषितम् decked with gold, महत् great, चापम् bow, सज्यम् stringed, चकार made, तानि those, रक्षांसि to the demons, अब्रवीत् च said.

#### Explanation

Righteous Rama too, lifted up his great bow decked with gold and fastened the string and then said to the demons:

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

पुत्रौ दशरथस्यावां भ्रातरौ रामलक्ष्मणौ। प्रविष्टौ सीतया सार्धं दुश्चरं दण्डकावनम्।।3.20.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

आवाम् we both are, दशरथस्य Dasaratha's, पुत्रौ two sons, भ्रातरौ two brothers, रामलक्ष्मणौ Rama and Lakshmana, सीतया सार्धम् along with Sita, दुश्चरम् inaccessible, दण्डकावनम् Dandaka forest, प्रविष्ठौ both have entered.

#### Explanation

We, Rama and Lakshmana, sons of Dasaratha, are brothers, who have entered the inaccessible Dandaka forest along with Sita.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

फलमूलाशनौ दान्तौ तापसौ धर्मचारिणौ। वसन्तौ दण्डकारण्ये किमर्थमुपहिंसथ।।3.20.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

फलमूलाशिनौ both survive on fruits and roots etc, दान्तौ both are selfrestrained, धर्मचारिणौ both follow the righteous path, दण्डकारण्ये in Dandaka forest, वसन्तौ living, तापसौ ascetics, किमर्थम् why, उपहिंसथ torment.

#### Explanation

Feeding on fruits and roots and following the righteous path, we are living in Dandaka forest like ascetics. Why are you torturing us?

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

युष्मान्पापात्मकान्हन्तुं विप्रकारान्महाहवे। ऋषीणां तु नियोगेन प्राप्तोऽहं सशरायुधः।।3.20.9।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अहम् I, पापात्मकान् sinners, विप्रकारान् offenders, युष्मान् you, आहवे in war, हन्तुम् to kill, सशरायुधः equipped with bows, arrows and weapons, ऋषीणाम् of ascetics, नियोगेन by their order, प्राप्तः came.

#### Explanation

I came here equipped with bows, arrows and weapons, asked by ascetics to kill in the battle sinners like you who are offending them.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

तिष्ठतैवात्र सन्तुष्टा नोपावर्तितुमर्हथ। यदि प्राणैरिहार्थो वा निवर्तध्वं निशाचराः।।3.20.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सन्तुष्टाः satisfied, निशाचराः demons, अत्रैव here only, तिष्ठतः you may stay, उपावर्तितुम् to return, न अर्हथ you should not, यदि वा or else, प्राणैः with life, अर्थः purpose, निवर्तध्वम् you may return.

#### Explanation

If you care for your life, you may return, or else, if you please you may remain (die) here, O demons.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

तस्य तद्वचनं श्रुत्वा राक्षसास्ते चतुर्दश। ऊचुर्वाचं  सुसंक्रुद्धा ब्रह्मघ्नाश्शूलपाणयः।।3.20.11।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तस्य his, तत् वचनम् those words, श्रुत्वा on hearing, ब्रह्मघ्नाः destroyers of brahmins, शूलपाणयः spears in hand, ते those, चतुर्दश राक्षसाः fourteen demons, सुसङ्क्रुद्धाः very angry, वाचम् words, ऊचुः spoke.

#### Explanation

At these words, the destroyers of brahmins, the fourteen demons were inflamed. They lifted their spears and spoke:

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

क्रोधमुत्पाद्य नो भर्तुः खरस्य सुमहात्मनः। त्वमेव हास्यसे प्राणानद्यास्माभिर्हतो युधि।।3.20.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

नः our, भर्तुः lord's, सुमहात्मनः of great, खरस्य Khara's, क्रोधम् wrath, उत्पाद्य causing, अद्य now, अस्माभिः by us, युधि in the fight, हतः killed, त्वमेव you alone, प्राणान् life, हास्यसे will give up.

#### Explanation

You have incited the wrath of Khara, our great master for which you will now pay with your life in the fight.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

का हि ते शक्तिरेकस्य बहूनां रणमूर्धनी। अस्माकमग्रतः स्थातुं किं पुनर्योद्दुमाहवे।।3.20.13।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

बहूनाम् by many, अस्माकम् of us, अग्रतः in front of, रणमूर्धनि at the head of the battle, स्थातुम् to stand, एकस्य of  one, ते to you, का शक्तिः where is the power, आहवे in war, योद्धुम् to fight, किं पुनः why again.

#### Explanation

How can you face us alone? We outnumber you? Leave alone fighting, you cannot even stand in front of us.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

एहि बाहुप्रयुक्तैर्नः परिघैश्शूलपट्टिसैः। प्राणांस्त्यक्षसि वीर्यं च धनुश्च करपीडितम्।।3.20.14।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

एहि come on, नः us, बाहुप्रयुक्तैः by those dicharged by our arms, परिघैः by daggers, शूलपट्टिशै spears with sharp, प्राणान् your life, वीर्यं च prowess, करपीडितम् held by hand, धनुश्च the bow aslo, त्यक्षसि will shun.

#### Explanation

Come on with daggers and sharpedged spears hurled, you will give up your life,your prowess as well as the bow held in your hand.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

इत्येवमुक्त्वा सङ्कृद्धा राक्षसास्ते चतुर्दश। चिक्षिपुस्तानि शूलानि राघवं प्रति दुर्जयम्।।3.20.15।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सङ्कृद्धाः mighty angry, ते those , राक्षसाः demons, इत्येवम् thus, उक्त्वा after saying, तानि शूलानि those spears, दुर्जयम् invincible, राघवं प्रति on Rama, चिक्षिपुः hurled.

#### Explanation

Saying these words, the fourteen infuriated demons hurled their spears at invincible Rama.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

तानि शूलानि काकुत्स्थस्समस्तानि चतुर्दश। तावद्भिरेव चिच्छेद शरैः काञ्चनभूषणैः।।3.20.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

काकुत्स्थ Rama, चतुर्दश fourteen, तानि शूलानि their spears, समस्तानि all of them, तावद्भि: by equal number, एव only, काञ्चनभूषणैः decorated with gold, शरैः with arrows, चिच्छेद cut to pieces.

#### Explanation

All the fourteen spears hurled turned into splinters by the same number of golden arrows released by Rama.

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

ततः पश्चान्महातेजा नाराचान्सूर्यसन्निभान्। जग्राह परमक्रुद्धश्चतुर्दश शिलाशितान्।।3.20.17।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, महातेजाः effulgent, परमक्रुद्धः  enraged, शिलाशितान् sharpened by stone, सूर्यसन्निभान् glowing like the Sun, चतुर्दश fourteen, नाराचान् arrows, जग्राह took.

#### Explanation

Then the effulgent Rama, mighty angry, took fourteen arrows sharpened by stones and glowing like the  Sun.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

गृहीत्वा धनुरायम्य लक्ष्यानुद्दिश्य राक्षसान्। मुमोच राघवो बाणान्वज्रानिव शतक्रतुः।।3.20.18।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राघवः Rama, धनुः bow, गृहीत्वा on taking, आयम्य after stretching, लक्ष्यान् the target, राक्षसान् the demons,  उद्दिश्य aiming at, शतक्रतुः like Indra, वज्रानिव like thunderbolt, बाणान् arrows, मुमोच released.

#### Explanation

Rama took the bow, stretched it and aimed at his target, the demons  and sent the arrows as Indra would release the thunderbolt.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

रुक्मपुङ्खाश्च विशिखा दीप्ता हेमविभूषिताः। ते भित्त्वा रक्षसां वेगाद्वक्षांसि रुधिराप्लुताः।3.20.19।। विनिष्पेतुस्तदा भूमौ न्यमज्जन्ताशनिस्वनाः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रुक्मपुङ्खाः feathered with gold, दीप्ताः blazing, हेमविभूषिताः decorated with gold, ते those, विशिखाः arrows, वेगात् speedily, रक्षसाम् the demons, वक्षांसि their chests, भित्त्वा piercing, रुधिराप्लुताः  drenched in blood, तदा then, विनिष्पेतुः fell down, अशनिस्वनाः creating sounds like thunder, भूमौ on earth, न्यमज्जन्त pierced into.

#### Explanation

Goldfeathered blazing arrows decorated with gold, released by Rama flew fast and pierced the chests of the demons. Drenched in blood they (the arrows) thundered (out of their chests) and  plunged into the earth.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

ते भिन्नहृदया भूमौ छिन्नमूला इव द्रुमाः।।3.20.20।। निपेतुश्शोणितार्द्राङ्गा विकृता विगतासवः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ते they, भिन्नहृदयाः  with pierced hearts, शोणितार्द्राङ्गाः bodies drenched in blood, विकृताः disfigured, विगतासवः with their life gone, छिन्नमूलाः  cut at the roots, द्रुमाः इव like trees, भूमौ on earth, निपेतुः fell down.

#### Explanation

With their hearts split, their bodies drenched in blood, disfigured and lifeless, the demons fell down on earth like trees cut  at the roots.

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

तान् दृष्ट्वा पतितान्भूमौ राक्षसी क्रोधमूर्छिता।।3.20.21।। परित्रस्ता पुनस्तत्र व्यसृजद्भैरवस्वनान्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

भूमौ on the ground, पतितान् fell down, तान् those, दृष्ट्वा seeing, राक्षसी the demoness,  (Surpanakha), क्रोधमूर्छिता  wild with anger, परित्रस्ता frightened, तत्र there, पुनः again, भैरवस्वनान् frihghtening sound, व्यसृजत् released.

#### Explanation

Seeing the demons fallen on the ground, the demoness wild with anger and fear roared.

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

उपगम्य खरं सा तु किञ्चित्संशुष्कशोणिता।।3.20.22।। पपात पुनरेवार्ता सनिर्यासेव सल्लकी।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

किञ्चित् slightly, संशुष्कशोणिता blood dried up, सनिर्यासा juice exuded, सल्लकी इव like a sallaki tree, सा she, तु but, आर्ता afflicted, खरम् to Khara, उपगम्य coming, पुनरेव again, पपात fell down.

#### Explanation

Her blood slightly dried up, she came back afflicted to Khara and fell down like a sallaki tree, its sap oozing.

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

पपात पुनरेवार्ता सनिर्यासेव सल्लकी ।।3.20.23 ।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

Missing

#### Explanation

Missing

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

भ्रातुस्समीपे शोकार्ता ससर्ज निनदं मुहुः।।3.20.24।। सस्वरं मुमुचे बाष्पं विषण्णवदना तदा।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तदा then, शोकार्ता afflicted with tears, भ्रातुः of the brother, समीपे presence, मुहुः again and again, निनदम् sound, ससर्ज released, विषण्णवदना with a sad face, सस्वरम् making loud noise, बाष्पम् tears, मुमुचे shed.

#### Explanation

Then desperately screaming and shedding tears with a sad face beside her brother, she roared again and again.

---

### Shloka 25

#### Shloka Text

निपातितान् दृश्य रणे तु राक्षसान् प्रधाविता शूर्पणखा पुनस्ततः। वधं च तेषां निखिलेन रक्षसां शशंस सर्वं भगिनी खरस्य सा।।3.20.25।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

शूर्पणखा Surpanakha, रणे in the fight, निपातितान् fallen, राक्षसान् demons, दृश्य seeing, पुनः ततः then, प्रधाविता she ran, भगिनी sister, रक्षसाम् of demons, वधम् about the death, सर्वम् all, निखिलेन in detail, खरस्य to Khara, शशंस told.

#### Explanation

When Surpanakha saw the demons fall dead on the battleground, she ran to Khara,her brother and told in detail about the death of the demons. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये अरण्यकाण्डे विंशस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the twentieth sarga of Aranyakanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 21

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

स पुनः पतितां दृष्ट्वा क्रोधाच्छूर्पणखां खरः। उवाच व्यक्तया वाचा तामनर्थार्थमागताम्।।3.21.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः खरः Khara, पुनः again, पतिताम्  fallen down, शूर्पणखाम् Surpanakha, दृष्ट्वा saw, अनर्थार्थम् unfortunate situation, आगताम्  who came, ताम् her, व्यक्तया in a clear, वाचा words, क्रोधात् angrily, उवाच spoke.

#### Explanation

Seeing Surpanakha collapse, Khara said in clear terms about the unfortunate situation she had created:

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

मया त्विदानीं शूरास्ते राक्षसा रुधिराशनाः। त्वत्प्रियार्थं विनिर्दिष्टाः किमर्थं रुद्यते पुनः।।3.21.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मया myself, त्वत्प्रियार्थम् for your pleasure, इदानीम् just now, रुधिराशनाः  who feed on blood, शूराः heroes, ते राक्षसाः those demons, विनिर्दिष्टाः commissioned, पुनः again, किमर्थम् for what reason, रुद्यते  crying.

#### Explanation

The valiant demons who feed on blood have been commissioned for your pleasure. Why are you crying again?

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

भक्ताश्चैवानुरक्ताश्च हिताश्च मम नित्यशः। हन्यमाना न हन्यन्ते न न कुर्युर्वचो मम।।3.21.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

भक्ताश्चैव  devoted, अनुरक्ताश्च  fond of me, नित्यशः always, मम to me, हिताश्च wellwishers, हन्यमानाः while they are being attacked, न हन्यन्ते are not killed , मम my, वचः words, न न कुर्युः never fail to carry out my orders.

#### Explanation

They are loyal and faithful to me. They are always my wellwishers. Even when attacked, they cannot be killed. They never fail to carry out my orders.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

किमेतच्छ्रोतुमिच्छामि कारणं यत्कृते पुनः। हा नाथेति विनर्दन्ती सर्पवल्लुठसि क्षितौ।।3.21.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

यत्कृते for what reason, हा नाथ Ha Lord, इति saying like that, विनर्दन्ती lamenting, पुनः again, सर्पवत् like a serpent, क्षितौ on the ground, लुठसि you are rolling, एतत् this, कारणम् reason, किम् why, श्रोतुम् to hear, इच्छामि I wish.

#### Explanation

I wish to know what makes you lament and roll on the ground like a serpent and hissing?

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

अनाथवद्विलपसि नाथे तु मयि संस्थिते। उत्तिष्ठोत्तिष्ठ माभैषीर्वैक्लव्यं त्यज्यतामिह।।3.21.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

नाथे as lord, मयि me, संस्थिते is present, अनाथवत् like an orphan, विलपसि you are weeping, उत्तिष्ठ you get up, मा भैषीः do not fear, इह now, वैक्लब्यम् worry, त्यज्यताम् may be given up.

#### Explanation

When I am here like a lord to protect you, why do you weep like an orphan? Get up. Give up your anxiety. Be not afraid.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

इत्येवमुक्ता दुर्धर्षा खरेण परिसान्त्विता। विमृज्य नयने सास्रे खरं भ्रातरमब्रवीत्।।3.21.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

इत्येवम् thus, उक्ता addressed, खरेण by Khara, परिसान्त्विता pacified, दुर्दर्षा in invincible woman, सास्रे tearful, नयने eyes, विमृज्य wiping, भ्रातरम् to brother, खरम् to Khara, अब्रवीत् said.

#### Explanation

Thus consoled by Khara, invincible Surpanakha wiped her tearful eyes and said to her brother:

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

अस्मीदानीमहं प्राप्ता हृतश्रवणनासिका। शोणितौघपरिक्लिन्ना त्वया च परिसान्त्विता।।3.21.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

हृतश्रवणनासिका with ears and nose sliced off, अहम् I, शोणितौघपरिक्लिन्ना drenched with flow of blood, इदानीम् now, प्राप्ता अस्मि myself having come, त्वया by you, परिसान्त्विता pacified, च also.

#### Explanation

My ears and nose sliced off, drenched with blood, I came to you and you pacified me.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

प्रेषिताश्च त्वया वीर राक्षसास्ते चतुर्दश। निहन्तुं राघवं क्रोधान्मत्प्रियार्थं सलक्षणम्।।3.21.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वीर O hero, त्वया by you, क्रोधात् out of anger, मत्प्रियार्थम् to please me, सलक्ष्मणम् along with Lakshmana, राघवम् Rama, निहन्तुम् to kill, ते those, चतुर्दश fourteen, राक्षसाः demons, प्रेषिताः were sent.

#### Explanation

O warrior  you had sent, out of anger, fourteen demons to put an end to Rama along with Lakshmana in order to please me.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

ते तु रामेण सामर्षाः शूलपट्टसपाणयः। समरे निहतास्सर्वे सायकैर्मर्मभेदिभिः।।3.21.9।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सामर्षाः those spirited ones, शूलपट्टसपाणयः with spears in hands, ते those, सर्वे all, रामेण by Rama, मर्मभेदिभिः hearts pierced, सायकैः by arrows, निहताः killed.

#### Explanation

The demons who went with spears in their hands were killed, their hearts pierced by  Rama's arrows.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

तान्दृष्ट्वा पतितान्भूमौ क्षणेनैव महाबलान्। रामस्य च महत्कर्म महांस्त्रासोऽभवन्मम।।3.21.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

क्षणेनैव in moments, भूमौ on earth, पतितान् fallen down, महाबलान् mighty ones, तान् them, रामस्य Rama's, महत् great, कर्म च action, दृष्ट्वा seeing, मम to me, महान् powerful, त्रासः fear, अभवत् befell.

#### Explanation

Seeing the powerful demons collapse on the ground in a moment's time through Rama's action, I was frightened.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

अहमस्मि समुद्विग्ना विषण्णा च निशाचर। शरणं त्वां पुनः प्राप्ता सर्वतोभयदर्शिनी।।3.21.11।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

निशाचर O demon , अहम् I, समुद्विग्ना scared, विषण्णा sad, च and, अस्मि am, सर्वतः all over, भयदर्शिनी  fear in sight, पुनः again, त्वाम् your, शरणं प्राप्ता sought refuge in you.

#### Explanation

O demon  I am scared and sad. To me everything looks frightening. I, therefore, seek refuge in you.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

विषादनक्राध्युषिते परित्रासोर्मिमालिनि। किं मां न त्रायसे मग्नां विपुले शोकसागरे।।3.21.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

विषादनक्राध्युषिते  by crocodiles of distress, परित्रासोर्मिमालिनि filled with rows of waves of fright, विपुले  vast , शोकसागरे  in sea of sorrow, मग्नाम् drowned, माम् me, किम् why, न त्रायसे you do not save?

#### Explanation

I am drowned in a vast sea of sorrow where crocodiles in the form of distress reside. It is full of rows of waves of fright. Why do you not save me?

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

एते च निहता भूमौ रामेण निशितैः शरैः। येऽपि मे पदवीं प्राप्ता राक्षसाः पिशिताशनाः।।3.21.13।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पिशिताशनाः  carnivorous, ये those, राक्षसाः demons, मे my, पदवीम् path, प्राप्ता came, एते च these, रामेण by Rama, भूमौ on the earth, निशितैः by sharp, शरैः arrows, निहताः were killed.

#### Explanation

The demons who live on flesh, who came following me fell down on earth and lay dead,  shot by the sharp arrows of Rama.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

मयि ते यद्यनुक्रोशो यदि रक्षस्सु तेषु च। रामेण यदि ते शक्तिस्तेजो वास्ति निशाचर।3.21.14।। दण्डकारण्यनिलयं जहि राक्षसकण्टकम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

निशाचर O nightranger, ते yours, मयि in me, तेषु among them, रक्षस्सु च on demons, अनुक्रोशः have compassion, अस्ति यदि if you have, ते to you, रामेण equal to Rama, शक्तिः power, तेजो वा or brilliance, अस्ति यदि if you have, दण्डकारण्यनिलयम् living inDandaka forest, राक्षसकण्टकम् enemy of demons, जहि kill.

#### Explanation

O demon  if you have compassion for me or for the demons, if you have the power and energy to face Rama, kill the enemy of the demons living in Dandaka forest.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

यदि रामं ममामित्रं न त्वमद्यवधिष्यसि।।3.21.15।। तवैव चाग्रतः प्राणांस्त्यक्षामि निरपत्रप।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मम my, अमित्रम् enemy, रामम् that Rama, त्वम् you, अद्य now, न वधिष्यसि यदि if you do not kill, निरपत्रप O shameless one, तव your, अग्रतः presence, प्राणान् life, त्यक्ष्यामि will give up.

#### Explanation

If now you do not kill that Rama, my enemy, O shameless one, I will give up my life in your very presence.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

बुद्ध्याहमनुपश्यामि न त्वं रामस्य संयुगे।।3.21.16।। स्थातुं प्रतिमुखे शक्तस्सबलश्च महात्मनः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

त्वम् you, सबलश्च with your army too, संयुगे in an encounter, महात्मनः of the great man, रामस्य Rama's, प्रतिमुखे face to face, स्थातुम् to stand, न शक्तः you are not capable, अहम् I, बुद्ध्या through my intellect, अनुपश्यामि I can foresee.

#### Explanation

Instinctively I can foresee that you and your army do not have the capability to encounter that great Rama in a fight.

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

शूरमानी न शूरस्त्वं मिथ्यारोपितविक्रमः।।3.21.17।। मानुषौ यो न शक्नोषि हन्तुं तौ रामलक्ष्मणौ।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

यः who, मानुषौ two humans, तौ रामलक्ष्मणौ those Rama and Lakshmana, हन्तुम् to kill, न शक्नोषि you are not able to, त्वम् you, शूरमानी calling yourself a hero, नशूरः त्वम् you are not a hero, मिथ्यारोपितविक्रमः your claim to be a hero is false.

#### Explanation

You call yourself a hero, yet are not able to kill two humans like Rama and Lakshmana. Your claim to heroism is false. You are not a hero at all.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

रामेण यदि ते शक्तिस्तेजो वास्ति निशाचर।।3.21.18।। दण्डकारण्यनिलयं जहि तं कुलपांसन।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कुलपांसन a disgrace to the family, निशाचर O demon , रामेण by Rama, ते your, शक्तिः power, तेजो वा or brilliance, अस्ति यदि if it is, दण्डकारण्यनिलयम् dwelling in Dandaka forest, तम् him, जहि kill.

#### Explanation

O demon  you are a disgrace to the family. If you have power or energy, kill that Rama dwelling in Dandaka forest.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

निस्सत्वस्याल्प वीर्यस्य वासस्ते कीदृशस्त्विह।।3.21.19।। अपयाहि जनस्थानातत्वरितस्सहबान्धवः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

निस्सत्वस्य of a man who has no strength, अल्पवीर्यस्य of less valiant one, ते your, इह here, वास: stay, कीदृशः what kind of, सहबान्धव: with your relations, त्वरितः quickly, जनस्थानात् from this Janasthana, अपयाहि go away.

#### Explanation

You have little strength and less heroism. You with your relations should immediately leave Janasthana. What kind of position do you have here?

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

रामतेजोभिभूतो हि त्वं क्षिप्रं विनशिष्यसि।3.21.20।। स हि तेजस्समायुक्तो रामो दशरथात्मजः। भ्राता चास्य महावीर्यो येन चास्मि विरूपिता।।3.21.21।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

त्वं you, रामतेजोभिभूतः let down by the brilliance of Rama, क्षिप्रं swiftly, हि indeed, विनशिष्यसि you will be surely destroyed, सः he, हि दशरथात्मजः son of Dasaratha, रामः Rama, तेजस्समायुक्तः endowed with brilliance, येन by whom, विरूपिता अस्मि I am disfigured, अस्य भ्राता च and his brother, महावीर्यः a very valiant man.

#### Explanation

Let down by the brilliance of Rama, son of Dasaratha, you will be soon destroyed. He is mighty and his brother who has disfigured me is very valiant.

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

रामतेजोभिभूतो हि त्वं क्षिप्रं विनशिष्यसि।3.21.20।। स हि तेजस्समायुक्तो रामो दशरथात्मजः। भ्राता चास्य महावीर्यो येन चास्मि विरूपिता।।3.21.21।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

त्वं you, रामतेजोभिभूतः let down by the brilliance of Rama, क्षिप्रं swiftly, हि indeed, विनशिष्यसि you will be surely destroyed, सः he, हि दशरथात्मजः son of Dasaratha, रामः Rama, तेजस्समायुक्तः endowed with brilliance, येन by whom, विरूपिता अस्मि I am disfigured, अस्य भ्राता च and his brother, महावीर्यः a very valiant man.

#### Explanation

Let down by the brilliance of Rama, son of Dasaratha, you will be soon destroyed. He is mighty and his brother who has disfigured me is very valiant.

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

एवं विलप्य बहुशो राक्षसी विततोदरी। कराभ्यामुदरं हत्वा रुरोद भृशदुःखिता।।3.21.22।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

विततोदरी  big bellied woman, राक्षसी demoness i, बहुशः in several ways, एवम् in that manner, विलप्य  weeping, भृशदुःखिता very sad, कराभ्याम् with both her hands, उदरम् belly (here, chest), हत्वा beating, रुरोद cried.

#### Explanation

Overcome by sorrow, the bigbellied demoness cried  in several ways and being very sad started beating her chest with both hands. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये अरण्यकाण्डे एकविंशस्सर्गः। Thus ends the twentyfirst sarga of Aranyakanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 22

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

एवमाधर्षितश्शूरश्शूर्पणख्या खरस्ततः। उवाच रक्षसां मध्ये खरः खरतरं वचः।।3.22.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, शूर्पणख्या by Surpanakha, एवम् in that way, रक्षसाम् of demons, मध्ये in the midst, आधर्षितः put to shame, शूरः hero, खरः Khara, खरतरम् harsher, वचः words, उवाच spoke.

#### Explanation

Ashamed and accused thus in the presence of the demons, the valiant Khara spoke to Surpanakha in harsh words:

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

तवावमानप्रभवः क्रोधोऽयमतुलो मम। न शक्यते धारयितुं लवणाम्भ इवोल्बणम्।।3.22.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तव your, अवमानप्रभवः arising out of insult, मम mine, अतुलः incomparable, अयं क्रोधः this anger, उल्बणम् excessive, लवणाम्भः salt water, इव like, धारयितुम् to bear, न शक्यते is not possible for me.

#### Explanation

Like the overslowing salt sea water, I am unable to contain this my excessive anger arising out of your insult.

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

न रामं गणये वीर्यान्मानुषं क्षीणजीवितम्। आत्मदुश्चरितैः प्राणान्हतोयोऽद्य विमोक्ष्यते।।3.22.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मानुषम् human being, क्षीणजीवितम् shortlived, रामम् Rama, वीर्यात् due to valour, न गणये I do not count, यः he who has, आत्मदुश्चरितैः by his own bad conduct, हतः be killed, अद्य now, प्राणान् life, विमोक्ष्यते will leave.

#### Explanation

Rama is a shortlived human. I do not care for his valour. His life span will be shortened (he will be killed) now for his own misdeeds.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

बाष्पस्संह्रियतामेष सम्भ्रमश्च विमुच्यताम्। अहं रामं सह भ्रात्रा नयामि यमसादनम्।।3.22.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

एषः this, बाष्पः tears, संह्रियाताम् may be controlled, सम्भ्रमश्च anxiety also, विमुच्यताम् give up, अहम् I, रामम् Rama, भ्रात्रा सह along with his brother, यमसादनम् to the abode of Yama, नयामि l will send.

#### Explanation

Restrain your tears and your anxieties I will send Rama along with his brother to the abode of Yama.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

परश्वथहतस्याद्य मन्दप्राणस्य संयुगे। रामस्य रुधिरं रक्तमुष्णं पास्यसि राक्षसि।।3.22.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षसि O demoness, अद्य now, संयुगे in the fight, परश्वथहतस्य of one killed by an axe, मन्दप्राणस्य whose life has gone slow, रामस्य Rama's, रक्तम् red, उष्णम् hot, रुधिरम् blood, पास्यसि you can drink.

#### Explanation

With his life ebbing out, killed by my axe in the fight, you will drink today, O demoness, Rama's red, hot blood.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

सा प्रहृष्टा वचश्श्रुत्वा खरस्य वदनाच्च्युतम्। प्रशशंस पुनर्मौर्ख्याद्भ्रातरं रक्षसां वरम्।।3.22.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सा she, खरस्य Khara's, वदनात् from the mouth, च्युतम् that came out, वचः the words, श्रुत्वा on hearing, प्रहृष्टा she was pleased, मौर्ख्यात् due to stupidity, भ्रातरम् to the brother, रक्षसाम् among demons, वरम् great, पुनः again, प्रशशंस she praised.

#### Explanation

On hearing the words that came out of Khara's mouth, she was pleased. And in stupidity began praising her brother once again as the best of demons.

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

तया परुषितः पूर्वं पुनरेव प्रशंसितः। अब्रवीद्दूषणं नाम खरस्सेनापतिं तदा।।3.22.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तया by her, पूर्वम् earlier, परुषितः been acused, पुनरेव again, प्रशंसितः was praised, खरः Khara, तदा then, दूषणं नाम Dooshana by name, सेनापतिम् army chief, अब्रवीत् said.

#### Explanation

Khara, accused by her earlier and admired again, said to Dusana, chief of army:

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

चतुर्दश सहस्राणि मम चित्तानुवर्तिनाम्। रक्षसां भीमवेगानां समरेष्वनिवर्तिनाम्।।3.22.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मम my, चित्तानुवर्तिनाम् acting according to will, भीमवेगानाम् of terrific speed, समरेषु in war, अनिवर्तिनाम् never turn back, रक्षसाम् of demons, चतुर्दश fourteen, सहस्राणि thousand.

#### Explanation

There are fourteen thousand demons at my command ready to act according to my will. They are warriors of terrific speed and will not retreat from the fight.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

नीलजीमूतवर्णानां घोराणां क्रूरकर्मणाम्। लोकहिंसाविहाराणां बलिनामुग्रतेजसाम्।।3.22.9।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

नीलजीमूतवर्णानाम् of the colour of the dark blue cloud, घोराणां of dreadful, कृ़रकर्मणाम् those who commit cruel acts लोकहिंसाविहाराणां those who roam about torturing people, बलिनाम् of the strong, उग्रतेजसाम् very powerful people.

#### Explanation

They are of the colour of the dark blue cloud, dreadful and merciless, strong and powerful fighters who roam about forturing people.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

तेषां शार्दूलदर्पाणां महास्यानां महौजसाम्। सर्वोद्योग मुदीर्णानां रक्षसां सौम्य कारय।।3.22.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सौम्यः handsome, शार्दूलदर्पाणां powerful like tigers, महास्यानां having large countenances, महौजसाम् mighty, उदीर्णानाम् of the arrogant, तेषां of all such, रक्षसां demons, सर्वोद्योगं कारय get ready.

#### Explanation

O handsome one, get them all, powerful like tigers, demons with large countenances, mighty and arragant, ready (for the fight).

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

उपस्थापय मे क्षिप्रं रथं सौम्य धनूंषि च। शरांश्चित्रांश्च खङ्गांश्च शक्तीश्च विविधाश्शिताः।।3.22.11।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सौम्य O good natured one, मे my, रथम् chariot, धनूंषि च bows too, शरान् arrows, चित्रान् wonderful, खङ्गांश्च swords, विविधाः of different kinds, शिताः sharp, शक्तीश्च missiles, क्षिप्रम् quickly, उपस्थापय get them ready.

#### Explanation

O goodnatured one, get my chariot, bows and arrows, wonderful swords of different kinds and sharp missiles ready quickly.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

अग्रे निर्यातुमिच्छामि पौलस्त्यानां महात्मनाम्। वधार्थं दुर्विनीतस्य रामस्य रणकोविदः।।3.22.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रणकोविदः expert in warfare, दुर्विनीतस्य of the immodest, रामस्य Rama's, वधार्थम् to kill, महात्मनाम् great self, पौलस्त्यानाम् those of Paulasthya family, अग्रे in front, निर्यातुम् to go, इच्छामि I desire.

#### Explanation

O expert in warfare, I wish to lead those great warriors of Paulasthya family, to kill that immodest Rama.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

इति तस्य ब्रुवाणस्य सूर्यवर्णं महारथम्। सदश्वैश्शबलैर्युक्तमाचचक्षेऽथ दूषणः।।3.22.13।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तस्य his (Khara's), इति this, ब्रुवाणस्य while (Khara was) telling, अथ at that time, दूषणः Dusana, सूर्यवर्णम् colour of the Sun, महारथम् huge chariot, शबलैः variegated colours, सदश्वैः good horses, युक्तम् fit, आचचक्षे indicated.

#### Explanation

While Khara was speaking thus, Dusana reported that a huge chariot of the colour of the Sun yoked with good horses of variegated hues stood ready.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

तं मेरुशिखराकारं तप्तकाञ्चनभूषणम्। हेमचक्रमसम्बाधं वैदूर्यमयकूबरम्।।3.22.14।। मत्स्यैः पुष्पैर्द्रुमैश्शैलैश्चन्द्रसूर्यैश्च काञ्चनैः। मङ्गलैः पक्षिसङ्घैश्च ताराभिरभिसंवृतम्।।3.22.15।। ध्वजनिस्त्रिंशसम्पन्नं किङ्किणीकविराजितम्। सदश्वयुक्तं सोऽमर्षादारुरोह खरो रथम्।।3.22.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः खरः that Khara, मेरुशिखराकारम् looking like the peak of the mount Meru, तप्तकाञ्चनभूषणम् decorated with best of gold, हेमचक्रम् having golden wheels, असम्बाधम् wellbuilt, वैदूर्यमयकूबरम् carriage pole studded with vaiduryas, काञ्चनैः gold, मत्स्यैः with fishes, पुष्पैः with flowers, द्रुमैः trees, शैलैः mountains, सूर्यैश्च Sun, मङ्गलैः with auspicious ones, पक्षिसङ्घैश्च flocks of birds, ताराभिः with stars, अभिसंवृतम् all surrounded by, ध्वजनिस्त्रिंशसम्पन्नम् equipped with flags and swords, किङ्किणीकविराजितम् with small bells, सदश्वयुक्तम् yoked to fine horses, रथम् chariot, अमर्षात् with impatience, अरुरोह  mounted.

#### Explanation

Then the impatient Khara mounted the chariot that looked like the peak of mount Meru. It was decorated with pure gold, had golden wheels, poles studded with vaidurya. The carriage of the chariot was engraved with golden figures of fishes, flowers, trees, the Sun, stars, flocks of auspicious birds, flags and swords. Shining with small bells, it was yoked to fine horses.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

तं मेरुशिखराकारं तप्तकाञ्चनभूषणम्। हेमचक्रमसम्बाधं वैदूर्यमयकूबरम्।।3.22.14।। मत्स्यैः पुष्पैर्द्रुमैश्शैलैश्चन्द्रसूर्यैश्च काञ्चनैः। मङ्गलैः पक्षिसङ्घैश्च ताराभिरभिसंवृतम्।।3.22.15।। ध्वजनिस्त्रिंशसम्पन्नं किङ्किणीकविराजितम्। सदश्वयुक्तं सोऽमर्षादारुरोह खरो रथम्।।3.22.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः खरः that Khara, मेरुशिखराकारम् looking like the peak of the mount Meru, तप्तकाञ्चनभूषणम् decorated with best of gold, हेमचक्रम् having golden wheels, असम्बाधम् wellbuilt, वैदूर्यमयकूबरम् carriage pole studded with vaiduryas, काञ्चनैः gold, मत्स्यैः with fishes, पुष्पैः with flowers, द्रुमैः trees, शैलैः mountains, सूर्यैश्च Sun, मङ्गलैः with auspicious ones, पक्षिसङ्घैश्च flocks of birds, ताराभिः with stars, अभिसंवृतम् all surrounded by, ध्वजनिस्त्रिंशसम्पन्नम् equipped with flags and swords, किङ्किणीकविराजितम् with small bells, सदश्वयुक्तम् yoked to fine horses, रथम् chariot, अमर्षात् with impatience, अरुरोह  mounted.

#### Explanation

Then the impatient Khara mounted the chariot that looked like the peak of mount Meru. It was decorated with pure gold, had golden wheels, poles studded with vaidurya. The carriage of the chariot was engraved with golden figures of fishes, flowers, trees, the Sun, stars, flocks of auspicious birds, flags and swords. Shining with small bells, it was yoked to fine horses.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

तं मेरुशिखराकारं तप्तकाञ्चनभूषणम्। हेमचक्रमसम्बाधं वैदूर्यमयकूबरम्।।3.22.14।। मत्स्यैः पुष्पैर्द्रुमैश्शैलैश्चन्द्रसूर्यैश्च काञ्चनैः। मङ्गलैः पक्षिसङ्घैश्च ताराभिरभिसंवृतम्।।3.22.15।। ध्वजनिस्त्रिंशसम्पन्नं किङ्किणीकविराजितम्। सदश्वयुक्तं सोऽमर्षादारुरोह खरो रथम्।।3.22.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः खरः that Khara, मेरुशिखराकारम् looking like the peak of the mount Meru, तप्तकाञ्चनभूषणम् decorated with best of gold, हेमचक्रम् having golden wheels, असम्बाधम् wellbuilt, वैदूर्यमयकूबरम् carriage pole studded with vaiduryas, काञ्चनैः gold, मत्स्यैः with fishes, पुष्पैः with flowers, द्रुमैः trees, शैलैः mountains, सूर्यैश्च Sun, मङ्गलैः with auspicious ones, पक्षिसङ्घैश्च flocks of birds, ताराभिः with stars, अभिसंवृतम् all surrounded by, ध्वजनिस्त्रिंशसम्पन्नम् equipped with flags and swords, किङ्किणीकविराजितम् with small bells, सदश्वयुक्तम् yoked to fine horses, रथम् chariot, अमर्षात् with impatience, अरुरोह  mounted.

#### Explanation

Then the impatient Khara mounted the chariot that looked like the peak of mount Meru. It was decorated with pure gold, had golden wheels, poles studded with vaidurya. The carriage of the chariot was engraved with golden figures of fishes, flowers, trees, the Sun, stars, flocks of auspicious birds, flags and swords. Shining with small bells, it was yoked to fine horses.

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

निशाम्य तु रथस्थं तं राक्षसा भीमविक्रमाः। तस्थुस्संपरिवार्यैनं दूषणं च महाबलम्।।3.22.17।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

भीमविक्रमाः warriors of terrific prowess, राक्षसाः demons, रथस्थम् seated on the chariot, तम् him, निशाम्य seeing, एनम् him, महाबलम् very strong one, दूषणं च Dusana too, सम्परिवार्य after surrounding, तस्थुः stood by.

#### Explanation

The demons of terrific prowess surounded Khara and Dusana and stood by, when they saw both of them mounting the chariot.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

खरस्तु तान्महेष्वासान्घोरवर्मायुधध्वजान्। निर्यातेत्यब्रवीद्दृष्ट्वा रथस्थस्सर्वराक्षसान्।।3.22.18।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रथस्थः from the chariot, खरस्तु Khara on his part, महेष्वासान् those holding great weapons, घोरवर्मायुधध्वजान् those wearing dreadful shields, bearing weapons and holding flags, तान् सर्वराक्षसान् all the demons, दृष्ट्वा after seeing, निर्यात march on, इति thus, अब्रवीत् ordered.

#### Explanation

Khara mounted the chariot, saw the demons holding great weapons, wearing dreadful shields and holding flags, and ordered them to march.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

ततस्तद् राक्षसं सैन्यं घोरचर्मायुधध्वजम्। निर्जगाम जनस्थानान्महानादं महाजवम्।।3.22.19।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, घोरवर्मायुधध्वजम् wearing dreadful shields, holding weapons and banners, तत् that, राक्षसं सैन्यम् army of demons, महानादम् a loud noise, महाजवम् in great speed, जनस्थानात् from Janasthana, निर्जगाम started.

#### Explanation

Then the army of the demons wearing dreadful shields, holding weapons and flags, and making a loud noise started in great speed from Janasthanam.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

मुद्गरैः पट्टिसैश्शूलैस्सुतीक्ष्णैश्च परश्वधैः। खङ्गैश्चक्रैश्च हस्तस्थैर्भ्राजमानैश्च तोमरैः।।3.22.20।। शक्तिभिः परिघैर्घोरैरतिमात्रैश्च कार्मुकैः। गदासिमुसलैर्वज्रैर्गृहीतैर्भीमदर्शनैः।।3.22.21।। राक्षसानां सुघोराणां सहस्राणि चतुर्दश। निर्यातानि जनस्थानात्खरचित्तानुवर्तिनाम्।।3.22.22।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

गृहीतैः took, हस्तस्थैः by those held in the hand, मुद्गरैः maces, पट्टिसैः piercing  weapons, शूलैः spears, सुतीक्ष्णैः sharp, परश्वधैः axes used in battle, खङ्गै swords, चक्रैश्च wheels, भ्राजमानैः shining splinters, तोमरैः iron clubs, शक्तिभिः powerful missiles, घोरैः dreadful, परिघैः iron bars, अतिमात्रैः of giant size, कार्मुकैः bows made of bamboo, गदासिमुसलैः pounding pestles, भीमदर्शनैः frightening to look at, वज्रैः weapons like thunderbolt, खरचित्तानुवर्तिनाम् of those acting according to the wishes of Khara, सुघोराणाम् very fierce, राक्षसानाम् of demons, चतुर्दय सहस्राणि fourteen thousand, जनन्थानात् from Janasthana, निर्यातानि departed.

#### Explanation

The fourteen thousand demons loyal to the wishes of Khara, took in their hands, maces, piercing weapons, spears, sharp axes used in battle, swords, wheels, splinters, iron clubs, powerful missiles, dreadful iron bars of giant size, bows made of bamboo, clubs and frightening weapons like thunderbolt (of Indra) and  departed from Janasthana.

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

मुद्गरैः पट्टिसैश्शूलैस्सुतीक्ष्णैश्च परश्वधैः। खङ्गैश्चक्रैश्च हस्तस्थैर्भ्राजमानैश्च तोमरैः।।3.22.20।। शक्तिभिः परिघैर्घोरैरतिमात्रैश्च कार्मुकैः। गदासिमुसलैर्वज्रैर्गृहीतैर्भीमदर्शनैः।।3.22.21।। राक्षसानां सुघोराणां सहस्राणि चतुर्दश। निर्यातानि जनस्थानात्खरचित्तानुवर्तिनाम्।।3.22.22।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

गृहीतैः took, हस्तस्थैः by those held in the hand, मुद्गरैः maces, पट्टिसैः piercing  weapons, शूलैः spears, सुतीक्ष्णैः sharp, परश्वधैः axes used in battle, खङ्गै swords, चक्रैश्च wheels, भ्राजमानैः shining splinters, तोमरैः iron clubs, शक्तिभिः powerful missiles, घोरैः dreadful, परिघैः iron bars, अतिमात्रैः of giant size, कार्मुकैः bows made of bamboo, गदासिमुसलैः pounding pestles, भीमदर्शनैः frightening to look at, वज्रैः weapons like thunderbolt, खरचित्तानुवर्तिनाम् of those acting according to the wishes of Khara, सुघोराणाम् very fierce, राक्षसानाम् of demons, चतुर्दय सहस्राणि fourteen thousand, जनन्थानात् from Janasthana, निर्यातानि departed.

#### Explanation

The fourteen thousand demons loyal to the wishes of Khara, took in their hands, maces, piercing weapons, spears, sharp axes used in battle, swords, wheels, splinters, iron clubs, powerful missiles, dreadful iron bars of giant size, bows made of bamboo, clubs and frightening weapons like thunderbolt (of Indra) and  departed from Janasthana.

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

मुद्गरैः पट्टिसैश्शूलैस्सुतीक्ष्णैश्च परश्वधैः। खङ्गैश्चक्रैश्च हस्तस्थैर्भ्राजमानैश्च तोमरैः।।3.22.20।। शक्तिभिः परिघैर्घोरैरतिमात्रैश्च कार्मुकैः। गदासिमुसलैर्वज्रैर्गृहीतैर्भीमदर्शनैः।।3.22.21।। राक्षसानां सुघोराणां सहस्राणि चतुर्दश। निर्यातानि जनस्थानात्खरचित्तानुवर्तिनाम्।।3.22.22।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

गृहीतैः took, हस्तस्थैः by those held in the hand, मुद्गरैः maces, पट्टिसैः piercing  weapons, शूलैः spears, सुतीक्ष्णैः sharp, परश्वधैः axes used in battle, खङ्गै swords, चक्रैश्च wheels, भ्राजमानैः shining splinters, तोमरैः iron clubs, शक्तिभिः powerful missiles, घोरैः dreadful, परिघैः iron bars, अतिमात्रैः of giant size, कार्मुकैः bows made of bamboo, गदासिमुसलैः pounding pestles, भीमदर्शनैः frightening to look at, वज्रैः weapons like thunderbolt, खरचित्तानुवर्तिनाम् of those acting according to the wishes of Khara, सुघोराणाम् very fierce, राक्षसानाम् of demons, चतुर्दय सहस्राणि fourteen thousand, जनन्थानात् from Janasthana, निर्यातानि departed.

#### Explanation

The fourteen thousand demons loyal to the wishes of Khara, took in their hands, maces, piercing weapons, spears, sharp axes used in battle, swords, wheels, splinters, iron clubs, powerful missiles, dreadful iron bars of giant size, bows made of bamboo, clubs and frightening weapons like thunderbolt (of Indra) and  departed from Janasthana.

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

तांस्त्वभिद्रवतो दृष्ट्वा राक्षसान् भीमविक्रमान्। खरस्यापि रथः किञ्चिज्जगाम तदनन्तरम्।।3.22.23।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अभिद्रवतः going speedily to attack, भीमविक्रमान् of frightening prowess, तान् राक्षसान् those demons, दृष्ट्वा after seeing, किञ्चित् a little, तदनन्तरम् behind, खरस्य Khara's, रथः chariot, अपि too, जगाम went.

#### Explanation

Khara's chariot moved a bit behind the army of demons of frightening prowess who rushed forward to attack (Rama).

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

ततस्ताञ्छबलानश्वास्तप्तकाञ्चनभूषितान्। खरस्य मतिमाज्ञाय सारथिस्समचोदयत्।।3.22.24।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः thereafter, सारथिः charioteer, तप्तकाञ्चनभूषितान् decorated with pure gold, शबलान्  colourful, तान् them, अश्वान् horses, खरस्य of Khara's, मतिम् mind, आज्ञाय knowing, समचोदयत् hastened.

#### Explanation

Knowing the mind of Khara, the charioteer hastened the horses of the colourful  chariot decorated with pure gold.

---

### Shloka 25

#### Shloka Text

स चोदितो रथश्शीघ्रं खरस्य रिपुघातिनः। शब्देनापूरयामास दिशश्च प्रदिशस्तदा।।3.22.25।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तदा then, रिपुघातिनः killers of enemy, खरस्य Khara's, शीघ्रम् speedily, चोदितः spurred, सः रथः that chariot, दिशश्च the four directions, प्रदिशश्च intermediate directions, शब्देन by the sound, आपूरयामास filled.

#### Explanation

Spurred by the charioteer, the chariot of Khara, the slayer of enemies, produced a rattle that filled all four quarters and the intermediate directions.

---

### Shloka 26

#### Shloka Text

प्रवृद्धमन्युस्तु खरः खरस्वनो रिपोर्वधार्थं त्वरितो यथान्तकः। अचूचुदत्सारथिमुन्नदन्घनं महाबलो मेघ इवाश्मवर्षवान्।।3.22.26।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

प्रवृद्धमन्युः whose anger increased, खरस्वनः with harsh tone, खरः Khara, रिपोः enemy's, वधार्थम् to kill, त्वरितः at quick pace, अन्तकः यथा like Yama, the god of death, अश्मवर्षवान् like hail stones, महाबलः powerful, मेघः इव like the cloud, घनम् heavy, उन्नदन् सारथिम् roaring charioteer, अचूचुदत् impelled him to drive.

#### Explanation

Khara in extreme anger hastened, like Yama, to slay his enemy. He roared loudly like a cloud raining hail stones and impelled his charioteer to speed up. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वालल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये अरण्यकाण्डे द्वाविंशस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the twentysecond sarga of Aranyakanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 23

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

तस्मिन्याते बले घोरमशिवं शोणितोदकम्। अभ्यवर्षन्महामेघस्तुमुलो गर्दभारुणः।।3.23.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

गर्दभारुणः dark like the colour of an ass, तुमुलः fierce, महामेघः huge cloud, तस्मिन् that army, याते marched, तत् there, घोरम् dreadful, बलम् army, अशिवम् inauspicious, शोणितोदकम् water red as blood, अभ्यवर्षत् rained.

#### Explanation

As the army marched from there (Janasthana) a huge dark cloud of the colour of an ass rained dreadful inauspicious water, red as blood.

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

निपेतुस्तुरगास्तस्य रथयुक्ता महाजवाः। समे पुष्पचिते देशे राजमार्गे यदृच्छया।।3.23.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रथयुक्ताः yoked to the chariot, महाजवाः of high speed, तस्य his, तुरगाः horses, राजमार्गे on the highway, पुष्पचिते समे sprinkled with flowers evenly, देशे place, यदृच्छया casually, निपेतुः stumbled.

#### Explanation

The swift horses yoked to the chariot stumbled casually while galloping on the plane highway sprinkled with flowers.

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

श्यामं रुधिरपर्यन्तं बभूव परिवेषणम्। अलातचक्रप्रतिमं परिगृह्य दिवाकरम्।।3.23.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

दिवाकरम् Sun, परिगृह्य took position, श्यामम् dark, रुधिरपर्यन्तम् red in the periphery, अलातचक्रप्रतिमम् moving firebrand creating a ring, परिवेषणम् surrounding, बभूव appeared,

#### Explanation

A dark ring, bloodred in colour appeared on the border of the Sun's orb, dark in the middle. This phenomenon of the Sun appeared like a ring of moving charcoal covering the Sun.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

ततो ध्वजमुपागम्य हेमदण्डं समुच्छ्रितम्। समाक्रम्य महाकायस्तस्थौ गृध्रस्सुदारुणः।।3.23.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, महाकायः huge, सुदारुणः frightening, गृध्रः vulture, समुच्छ्रितम् erected, ध्वजम् flag, उपागम्य flying up, हेमदण्डम् golden staff, समाक्रम्य after occupying, तस्थौ sat.

#### Explanation

A huge vulture, frightening to look at flew up and sat on top of the flag on the staff of gold.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

जनस्थानसमीपे तु समागम्य खरस्वनाः। विस्वरान्विविधांश्चक्रुर्मांसादा मृगपक्षिणः।।3.23.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

खरस्वनाः of harsh voice, मांसादाः flesheating, मृगपक्षिणः beasts and birds, जनस्थानसमीपे near Janasthana, समागम्य flocking, विविधान् different kinds of, विस्वरान् hoarse voice, चक्रुः created.

#### Explanation

Flesheating beasts and birds flocked at Janasthana producing a cacophony.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

व्याजह्रुश्च प्रदीप्तायां दिशि वै भैरवस्वनम्। अशिवं यातुधानानां शिवा घोरा महास्वनाः।।3.23.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

घोराः frightful, महास्वनाः ghastly howls, शिवाः jackals, प्रदीप्तायाम् in the illuminated, दिशि direction, भैरवस्वनम् terrible sound, यातुधानानाम् of demons, अशिवम् inauspicious, व्याजह्रुः indicated.

#### Explanation

The dreadful jackals making ghastly howls turning towards the illuminated direction and produced frightening sounds indicating inauspicious time for the demons.

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

प्रभिन्नगिरिसङ्काशास्तोयशोणितधारिणः। आकाशं तदनाकाशं चक्रुर्भीमा वलाहकाः।।3.23.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

प्रभिन्नगिरिसङ्काशाः those looking like shattered mountains, तोयशोणितधारिणः clouds bearing bloodlike water, भीमाः most frightening, वलाहकाः clouds, तत् that, आकाशम् sky, अनाकाशम् other than the sky, चक्रुः rendered.

#### Explanation

The clouds looked like shattered mountains pouring down bloodlike rain which filled the sky and made it appear frighteningly different.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

बभूव तिमिरं घोरमुद्धतं रोमहर्षणम्। दिशो वा विदिशो वापि न च व्यक्तं चकाशिरे।।3.23.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

घोरम् terrible, उद्धतम् arose, रोमहर्षणम् horripilation, तिमिरम् darkness, बभूव became, दिशो वा  whether quarters, विदिशो वापि or the intermediary, व्यक्तम् clearly, न चकाशिरे did not shine.

#### Explanation

A terrific darkness, spread all over, produced frightening horripilation. Even the four quarters and the intermediary zones were not discernible.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

क्षतजार्द्रसवर्णाभा सन्ध्याकालं विना बभौ। खरस्याभिमुखा नेदुस्तदा घोरमृगाः खगाः।।3.23.9।। कङ्कगोमायुगृध्राश्च चुक्रुशुर्भयशंसिनः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कालं विना before Sunset, क्षतजार्द्रसवर्णाभा glow like a fresh wound, सन्ध्या twilight, बभौ appeared, तदा then, घोरमृगाः dreadful animals, खगाः birds, खरस्य of Khara, अभिमुखाः directed towards, नेदुः produced sounds, कङ्कगोमायुगृध्राश्च kanka birds, jackals, and vultures, भयशंसिनः indicating danger, चक्रुशुः screeched.

#### Explanation

Even before Sunset the twilight looked like a fresh wound. Then wild animals, birds, kankas, jackals and vultures facing towards Khara screeched, indicating danger.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

नित्याशुभकरा युद्धे शिवा घोरनिदर्शनाः।।3.23.10।। नेदुर्बलस्याभिमुखं ज्वालोद्गारिभिराननैः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

युद्धे in war, नित्याशुभकराः always ominous, घोरनिदर्शनाः dreadful premonitions, शिवाः jackals, ज्वालोद्गारिभिः as if emitting fire, आननैः with their faces, बलस्य of the army, अभिमुखम् facing towards, नेदुः produced sounds.

#### Explanation

Bad omens that always bring inauspiciousness were a dreadful evidence of what is going to happen. Jackals howled, facing Khara's army with their mouths emitting fire.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

कबन्धः परिघाभासो दृश्यते भास्करान्तिके।।3.23.11।। जग्राह सूर्यं स्वर्भानुरपर्वणि महाग्रहः। प्रवाति मारुतश्शीघ्रं निष्प्रभोऽभूद्दिवाकरः।।3.23.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

भास्करान्तिके close to the Sun, परिघाभासः appearing like an iron beam, कबन्ध: Ketu, दृश्यते  seen, महाग्रहः a great planet, स्वर्भानुः Rahu, अपर्वणि  other than the new Moon, सूर्यम् Sun, जग्राह  eclipsed, मारुतः wind, शीघ्रम् swiftly, प्रवाति blew, दिवाकरः Sun, निष्प्रभः without lustre, अभूत् appeared.

#### Explanation

The planet Ketu appeared  like an iron beam near the Sun. Although it was not the fullmoon or newmoon day, Rahu eclipsed the Sun. Wind blew hard and the Sun was lustreless.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

कबन्धः परिघाभासो दृश्यते भास्करान्तिके।।3.23.11।। जग्राह सूर्यं स्वर्भानुरपर्वणि महाग्रहः। प्रवाति मारुतश्शीघ्रं निष्प्रभोऽभूद्दिवाकरः।।3.23.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

भास्करान्तिके close to the Sun, परिघाभासः appearing like an iron beam, कबन्ध: Ketu, दृश्यते  seen, महाग्रहः a great planet, स्वर्भानुः Rahu, अपर्वणि  other than the new Moon, सूर्यम् Sun, जग्राह  eclipsed, मारुतः wind, शीघ्रम् swiftly, प्रवाति blew, दिवाकरः Sun, निष्प्रभः without lustre, अभूत् appeared.

#### Explanation

The planet Ketu appeared  like an iron beam near the Sun. Although it was not the fullmoon or newmoon day, Rahu eclipsed the Sun. Wind blew hard and the Sun was lustreless.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

उत्पेतुश्च विना रात्रिं ताराः खद्योतसप्रभाः। संलीनमीनविहगा नलिन्यश्शुष्कपङ्कजाः।।3.23.13।। तस्मिन् क्षणे बभूवुश्च विना पुष्पफलैर्द्रुमाः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

खद्योतसम्प्रभाः glittering like fireflies, ताराः stars, विना without, रात्रिम् night, उत्पेतुः rose, संलीनमीनविहगाः fishes and birds disappear, नलिन्यः lotusponds, शुष्कपङ्कजाः have dried up lotuses, तस्मिन् in that, क्षणे moment, द्रुमाः trees, पुष्पफलैः flowers and fruits, विना without, बभूवुश्च became.

#### Explanation

The stars rose in the sky and glittered like fireflies even before night arrived. The fishes and the aquatic birds in the lotusponds hid themselves under water.  Lotuses dried up in tanks and the trees stood bereft of flowers and fruits.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

उद्धूतश्च विना वातं रेणुर्जलधरारुणः।।3.23.14।। वीचीकूचीति वाश्यन्त्यो बभूवुस्तत्र शारिकाः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

विना वातम् without wind, जलधरारुणः like a brownish cloud, रेणुः dust, उद्धूतः rose up, तत्र there, शारिकाः sarika bird, वीचीकूचीति thoughtlessly, वाश्यन्त्यः singing, बभूवुः been.

#### Explanation

Although there was no wind, dust rose like a brown cloud and the sarika birds sang (vichi kuchi) thoughtlessly.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

उल्काश्चापि सनिर्घाता निपेतुर्घोरदर्शनाः।।3.23.15।। प्रचचाल मही सर्वा सशैलवनकानना।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

घोरदर्शनाः dreadful in appearance, सनिर्घाताः thunderstorms, उल्काश्चापि meteors, निपेतुः dropped down, सशैलवनकानना mountains, groves and forests, सर्वाः all, मही earth, प्रचचाल trembled.

#### Explanation

Dreadful to watch were the thunderstorms and the meteors that dropped down from the sky. The earth trembled all over with mountains, groves and forests.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

खरस्य च रथस्थस्य नर्दमानस्य धीमतः।।3.23.16।। प्राकम्पत भुजस्सव्यस्स्वरश्चास्यावसज्जत।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रथस्थस्य stationed on the chariot, नर्दमानस्य  shouting, धीमतः intelligent , खरस्य of Khara, सव्यः left, भुजः shoulder, प्राकम्पत shook violently, अस्य his, स्वरश्च throat also, अवसज्जत throttled.

#### Explanation

While the intelligent and courageous Khara was seated on the chariot and shouting, his left shoulder shook violently and throat throttled.

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

सास्रा सम्पद्यते दृष्टिः पश्यमानस्य सर्वतः।।3.23.17।। ललाटे च रुजा जाता न च मोहान्यवर्तत।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सर्वतः around, पश्यमानस्य while watching, दृष्टिः sight, सास्रा filled with tears, सम्पद्यते became, ललाटे on the forehead, रुजा च  uneasiness, जाता had developed, मोहात् due to delusion, न not, च न्यवर्तत did not retreat.

#### Explanation

While watching all this, his eyes brimmed with tears. His forehead ached. Yet he was too deluded to beat a retreat.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

तान्समीक्षय महोत्पातानुत्थितान्रोमहर्षणान्।।3.23.18।। अब्रवीद्राक्षसान्सर्वान्प्रहसन्सखरस्तदा।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः खरः that Khara, तदा then, उत्थितान् occurring, रोमहर्षणान् horripilation, तान् those, महोत्पातान् most dreadful phenomena, समीक्ष्य after watching, प्रहसन् while laughing, सर्वान् all, राक्षसान् demous, अब्रवीत् said.

#### Explanation

Looking at these dreadful, horripilating occurrences Khara laughed and said to the demons:

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

महोत्पातानिमान्सर्वानुत्थितान्घोरदर्शनान्।।3.23.19।। न चिन्तयाम्यहं वीर्याद्बलवान्दुर्बलानिव।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

उत्थितान् produced, घोरदर्शनान् those terrific to see, इमान् these, महोत्पातान् the events boding calamity, सर्वान् all, अहम् I, वीर्यात् due to valour, बलवान् strong, दुर्बलानिव as he deals with the weak, न चिन्तयामि I do not take them seriously.

#### Explanation

Valiant and strong, I do not take seriously these terrifying omens boding calamity  just as a strong man does not care for the weak.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

तारा अपि शरैस्तीक्ष्णैः पातयामि नभस्स्थलात्।।3.23.20।। मृत्युं मरणधर्मेण सङ्क्रुद्धो योजयाम्यहम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अहम् I, ताराः stars, अपि even, तीक्ष्णैः with sharp, शरैः by arrows, नभस्स्थलात् from the sky, पातयामि I will dislodge, सङ्क्रुद्धः if angered, मृत्युम् god of death, मरणधर्मेण mortal nature, योजयामि  will cause.

#### Explanation

With my sharp arrows I can even bring down the stars from the sky. If angered, I can cause mortality to the god of death.

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

राघवं तं बलोत्सिक्तं भ्रातरं चास्य लक्ष्मणम्।।3.23.21।। अहत्वा सायकैस्तीक्ष्णैर्नोपावर्तितुमुत्सहे।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

बलोत्सिक्तम् man proud of his strength, तं राघवम् to that Rama, अस्य and his, भ्रातरम् brother, लक्ष्मणं च and Lakshmana, तीक्ष्णैः by sharp, सायकैः with arrows, अहत्वा without killing, उपावर्तितुम् to return, नोत्सहे I am not intending.

#### Explanation

I do not wish to return, without killing with my sharp arrows that Rama who is proud of his strength and his brother Lakshmana.

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

सकामा भगिनी मेऽस्तु पीत्वा तु रुधिरं तयोः।।3.23.22।। यन्निमित्तस्तु रामस्य लक्ष्मणस्य विपर्ययः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रामस्य Rama's, लक्ष्मणस्य Lakshmana's, विपर्ययः adversity, यन्निमित्तः for whose cause, मे भगिनी my sister, तयोः their, रुधिरम् blood, पीत्वा after drinking, सकामा her desire, अस्तु be fulfilled,

#### Explanation

Let my sister be satisfied by drinking the blood of both Rama and Lakshmana on whose account this adversity has come.

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

न क्वचित्प्राप्तपूर्वो मे संयुगेषु पराजयः।।3.23.23।। युष्माकमेतत्प्रत्यक्षं नानृतं कथयाम्यहम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मे for me, संयुगेषु in wars, क्वचित् ever, पराजयः defeat, न प्राप्तपूर्वः never faced, एतत् this, युष्माकम् all of you, प्रत्यक्षम् directly witnessed, अहम् I, अनृतम् untruth, न कथयामि I am not telling.

#### Explanation

I have never faced defeat in wars, and all of you have seen it with your eyes. I am not telling you a lie.

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

देवराजमपि क्रुद्धो मत्तैरावतयायिनम्।।3.23.24।। वज्रहस्तं रणे हन्यां किं पुनस्तौ कुमानुषौ।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

क्रुद्धः angered, मत्तैरावतयायिनम् riding an intoxicated Airavata, वज्रहस्तम् one who wields thunderbolt, देवराजमपि even the king of the gods, रणे in war, हन्याम् I will kill, तौ both of them, कुमानुषौ human beings, किं पुनः should I say again.

#### Explanation

When angry, I can kill even (Indra) the wielder of the thunderbolt, rider of the intoxicated Airavata and lord of the gods. What to speak of these two petty humans?

---

### Shloka 25

#### Shloka Text

सा तस्य गर्जितं श्रुत्वा राक्षसस्य महाचमूः।।3.23.25।। प्रहर्षमतुलं लेभे मृत्युपाशावपाशिता।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मृत्युपाशावपाशिता fallen into the trap of death, सा that, महाचमूः great army, तस्य राक्षसस्य of the demon, गर्जितम् roaring, श्रुत्वा on hearing, अतुलम् immeasureable, प्रहर्षम् joy, लेभे experienced.

#### Explanation

Fallen into the deathtrap, the great army of the demon, felt very happy on hearing Khara roaring.

---

### Shloka 26

#### Shloka Text

समीयुश्च महात्मानो युद्धदर्शनकाङ्क्षिणः।।3.23.26।। ऋषयो देवगन्दर्वास्सिद्धाश्च सहचारणैः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महात्मानः great souls, ऋषयः sages, देवगन्धर्वाः gods and gandharvas, सिद्धाश्च: siddhas (accomplished spiritually), चारणैः सह including celestial bards, युद्धदर्शनकाङ्क्षिणः desirous of seeing the war, समीयुः assembled.

#### Explanation

Great souls like sages, gods, gandharvas, and siddhas including celestial bards assembled to see the battle.

---

### Shloka 27

#### Shloka Text

समेत्य चोचुस्सहितास्तेऽन्योन्यं पुण्यकर्मणः। स्वस्ति गोब्राह्मणेभ्योऽस्तु लोकानां येऽभिसङ्गताः।।3.23.27।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पुण्यकर्मणः holy men, ते they, समेत्य collecting together, अन्योन्यम् to one another, सहिताः together, गोब्राह्मणेभ्यः for cows and brahmins, लोकानां of the people, सर्वशः all over, स्वस्ति अस्तु let it be auspicious, ऊचुः they spoke, ये who, अमिसङ्गताः associated

#### Explanation

Holy men assembled and uttered benedictions, saying, 'let there be wellbeing for the cows and brahmins associated with men of pious deeds in all the (three) worlds.

---

### Shloka 28

#### Shloka Text

जयतां राघवस्संख्ये पौलस्त्यान् रजनीचरान्।।3.23.28।। चक्रहस्तो यथा युद्धे सर्वानसुरपुङ्गवान्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

चक्रहस्तः wielder of the wheel (Visnu), युद्धे in war, सर्वान् all, असुरपङ्गवान् chiefs of demons, यथा like, राघवः Rama, पौलस्त्यान् all descendants of Paulastya, सङ्ख्ये in war, रजनीचरान्  nightrangers (demons), जयताम् let him win.

#### Explanation

'Let Rama conquer the descendants of Paulasthya in the war like Visnu, the wielder of the wheel had defeated great demons'.

---

### Shloka 29

#### Shloka Text

एतच्चान्यच्च बहुशो ब्रुवाणाः परमर्षयः।।3.23.29।। जातकौतूहलास्तत्र विमानस्थाश्च देवताः। ददृशुर्वाहिनीं तेषां राक्षसानां गतायुषाम्।।3.23.30।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

एतच्चान्यच्च these matters, बहुशः more and more, ब्रुवाणाः while talking, परमर्षयः great ascetics, तत्र there, विमानस्थाः occupants of heavenly chariots, देवताश्च gods too, जातकौतूहलाः with much eagerness, गतायुषाम् those of reduced life span, तेषाम् of those, राक्षसानाम् of demons, वाहिनीम् army, ददृशुः saw.

#### Explanation

The great ascetics were talking thus and more. The gods waiting eagerly on heavenly chariots saw the army of the demons whose life span was shortened (who were doomed to death).

---

### Shloka 30

#### Shloka Text

एतच्चान्यच्च बहुशो ब्रुवाणाः परमर्षयः।।3.23.29।। जातकौतूहलास्तत्र विमानस्थाश्च देवताः। ददृशुर्वाहिनीं तेषां राक्षसानां गतायुषाम्।।3.23.30।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

एतच्चान्यच्च these matters, बहुशः more and more, ब्रुवाणाः while talking, परमर्षयः great ascetics, तत्र there, विमानस्थाः occupants of heavenly chariots, देवताश्च gods too, जातकौतूहलाः with much eagerness, गतायुषाम् those of reduced life span, तेषाम् of those, राक्षसानाम् of demons, वाहिनीम् army, ददृशुः saw.

#### Explanation

The great ascetics were talking thus and more. The gods waiting eagerly on heavenly chariots saw the army of the demons whose life span was shortened (who were doomed to death).

---

### Shloka 31

#### Shloka Text

रथेन तु खरो वेगादुग्रसैन्यो विनिस्सृतः। तं दृष्ट्वा राक्षसं भूयो राक्षसाश्च विनिस्सृताः।।3.23.31।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

उग्रसैन्यः terrific army, खरः Khara, रथेन on the chariot, वेगात् speedily, विनिस्सृतः drove fast, तं राक्षसम् that demon also, दृष्ट्वा seeing, भूयः again, राक्षसाः demons, विनिस्सृताः marched.

#### Explanation

Seeing Khara, their chief on the chariot driving fast, the army of demons too marched forward.

---

### Shloka 32

#### Shloka Text

श्येनगामी पृथुग्रीवो यज्ञशत्रुर्विहङ्गमः। दुर्जयः करवीराक्षः परुषः कालकार्मुकः।।3.23.32।। मेघमाली महामाली सर्वास्यो रुधिराशनः। द्वादशैते महावीर्याः प्रतस्थुरभितः खरम्।।3.23.33।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

श्येनगामी moving like a hawk, पृथुग्रीवः Pruthugreeva (one with a big neck), यज्ञशत्रुः Yagnasatru (enemy of sacrifices), विहङ्गमः Vihangama (who knows how to move in the sky), दुर्जयः Durjaya (an invincible one), करवीराक्षः Karaviraksha (one whose eyes are like the oleander flower), परुषः Parusha (harsh), कालकार्मुकः Kalakarmuka (bow of death), मेघमाली Meghamali (clouds in a row), महामाली Mahamali (a long row), सर्पास्यः Sarpasya (serpentmouthed), रुधिराशनः Rudhirasana (one who feeds on blood), महावीर्याः Mahaveerya (mighty), एते all of them, द्वादश twelve, खरम् अभितः ahead of Khara, प्रतस्थुः marched.

#### Explanation

Surrounded by twelve valiant warriors like Syenagami, Pruthugreeva, Yagnasatru, Vihangama, Durjaya, Karaviraksha, Parusha, Kalakarmuka, Meghmali, Mahamali, Sarpasya and Rudhirasana, Khara proceeded.

---

### Shloka 33

#### Shloka Text

श्येनगामी पृथुग्रीवो यज्ञशत्रुर्विहङ्गमः। दुर्जयः करवीराक्षः परुषः कालकार्मुकः।।3.23.32।। मेघमाली महामाली सर्वास्यो रुधिराशनः। द्वादशैते महावीर्याः प्रतस्थुरभितः खरम्।।3.23.33।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

श्येनगामी moving like a hawk, पृथुग्रीवः Pruthugreeva (one with a big neck), यज्ञशत्रुः Yagnasatru (enemy of sacrifices), विहङ्गमः Vihangama (who knows how to move in the sky), दुर्जयः Durjaya (an invincible one), करवीराक्षः Karaviraksha (one whose eyes are like the oleander flower), परुषः Parusha (harsh), कालकार्मुकः Kalakarmuka (bow of death), मेघमाली Meghamali (clouds in a row), महामाली Mahamali (a long row), सर्पास्यः Sarpasya (serpentmouthed), रुधिराशनः Rudhirasana (one who feeds on blood), महावीर्याः Mahaveerya (mighty), एते all of them, द्वादश twelve, खरम् अभितः ahead of Khara, प्रतस्थुः marched.

#### Explanation

Surrounded by twelve valiant warriors like Syenagami, Pruthugreeva, Yagnasatru, Vihangama, Durjaya, Karaviraksha, Parusha, Kalakarmuka, Meghmali, Mahamali, Sarpasya and Rudhirasana, Khara proceeded.

---

### Shloka 34

#### Shloka Text

महाकपालिस्स्थूलाक्षः प्रमाथी त्रिशिरास्तथा। चत्वार एते सेनान्यो दूषणं पृष्ठतो ययुः।।3.23.34।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महाकपालिः Mahakapali (one with a big skull), स्थूलाक्षः Sthulaksha (bigeyed), प्रमाथी Pramathi (crusher of enemy), तथा similarly, त्रिशिराः Trisira (threeheaded one), एते all of them, चत्वारः four, सेनान्यः army men, दूषणं परितः around Dusana, ययुः walked.

#### Explanation

Similarly Dusana proceeded with four  Mahakapali, Sthulaksha, Pramathi and Trisira  around him.

---

### Shloka 35

#### Shloka Text

सा भीमवेगा समराभिकामा महाबला राक्षसवीरसेना। तौ राजपुत्रौ सहसाभ्युपेता माला ग्रहाणामिव चन्द्रसूर्यौ।।3.23.35।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

भीमवेगाः very fast, समराभिकामाः eager for war, महाबलाः mighty, सा that, राक्षसवीरसेना the heroic army of demons, सहसा at once, ग्रहाणाम् of planets, माला garland, चन्द्रसूर्यौ इव like Sun and Moon, तौ those, राजपुत्रौ king's sons, अभ्युपेता: attacked.

#### Explanation

The mighty, swiftfooted army of valiant demons surrounded the princes (Rama and Lakshmana) all on a sudden like the planets around the Sun and the Moon. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये अरण्यकाण्डे त्रयोविंशस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the twentythird sarga of Aranyakanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 24

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

आश्रमं प्रति याते तु खरे खरपराक्रमे। तानेवौत्पातिकान्रामस्सह भ्रात्रा ददर्श ह।।3.24.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

खरपराक्रमे endowed with malicious might, खरे when Khara, आश्रमं प्रति towards the hermitage, याते marched, रामः Rama, भ्रात्रा सह with his brother, औत्पातिकान् phenomena boding calamity, तानेव ददर्श ह he also saw.

#### Explanation

When Khara endowed with malicious might was marching towards the hermitage, Rama and his brother saw the same omens of impending calamity the demon had seen.

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

तानुत्पातान्महोघोरानुत्थितान्रोमहर्षणान्। प्रजानामहितान्दृष्ट्वा रामो लक्ष्मणमब्रवीत्।।3.24.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महाघोरान् most dreadful, रोमहर्षणान horripilation, प्रजानाम् to people, अहितान् harmful, तान् those, उत्पातान् calamitous, दृष्ट्वा seeing, रामः Rama, लक्ष्मणम् Lakshmana, अब्रवीत् said.

#### Explanation

When Rama saw the most dreadful, horripilating phenomena foreboding calamity, he said to Lakshmana:

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

इमान्पश्य महाबाहो सर्वभूतापहारिणः। समुत्थितान्महोत्पातान्संहर्तुं सर्वराक्षसान्।।3.24.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महाबाहो longarmed, सर्वभूतापहारिणः alldestructive, सर्वराक्षसान् all demons, संहर्तुम् to kill, समुत्थितान् present, इमान् these, महोत्पातान् great calamitous events, पश्य see.

#### Explanation

O longarmed Lakshmana see these calamitous phenomena that forebode mass destruction of men and demons.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

अमी रुधिरधारास्तु विसृजन्तः खरस्वनाः। व्योम्नि मेघा विवर्तन्ते परुषा गर्दभारुणाः।।3.24.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रुधिरधाराः flow of blood, विसृजन्तः showering, खरस्वनाः harsh sounds, परुषाः rough, गर्दभारुणाः brownishred like the colour of donkeys, अमी these, मेघाः clouds, व्योम्नि in the sky, विवर्तन्ते are moving about.

#### Explanation

These rumbling clouds, brownishred like the colour of a donkey,  are moving about in the sky showering streams of blood.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

सधूमाश्च शरास्सर्वे मम युद्धाभिनन्दिनः। रुक्मपृष्ठानि चापानि विचेष्टन्ते च लक्ष्मण।।3.24.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

युद्धाभिनन्दिनः welcome me to war, सर्वे all, मम शराः my arrows, सधूमाश्च smoking, लक्ष्मण Lakshmana, रुक्मपृष्ठानि coated with gold, चापानि च and bows, विचेष्टन्ते are trying.

#### Explanation

O Lakshmana all my arrows are smoking and this goldplated bow welcomes me to war.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

यादृशा इह कूजन्ति पक्षिणः वनचारिणः। अग्रतो नो भयं प्राप्तं संशयो जीवितस्य च।।3.24.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

इह here, वनचारिणः moving in the forest, पक्षिणः birds, यादृशाः in such way, कूजन्ति screeching, नः for us, अग्रतः in future, भयम् fear, प्राप्तम् is foreseen, जीवितस्य of life, संशयः च also doubtful.

#### Explanation

The way the forestbirds are screeching makes me foresee danger and makes survival doubtful.

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

सम्प्रहारस्तु सुमहान्भविष्यति न संशयः। अयमाख्याति मे बाहुस्स्फुरमाणो मुहुर्मुहुः।।3.24.7।। सन्निकर्षे तु न श्शूर जयं शत्रोः पराजयम्। सप्रभं च प्रसन्नं च तव वक्त्रं हि लक्ष्यते।।3.24.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सुमहान् very great, सम्प्रहारस्तु destruction of life, भविष्यति will take place, संशयः doubt, न no, शूर O brave one, मुहुः मुहुः again and again, स्फुरमाणः shivering, अयम् this, मे बाहुः my (right) shoulder, सन्निकर्षे in near future, नः our, जयम् success, शत्रोः enemy's, पराजयम् defeat, आख्याति indicating, तव your, वक्त्रम् face, सप्रभं च is bright, प्रसन्नं च cheerful too, लक्ष्यते हि appears indeed.

#### Explanation

A mass destruction of life is going to take place. There is no doubt about it. O brave Lakshmana my right shoulder trembles again and again, indicating the defeat of the enemy. Your beaming visage presages victory.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

सम्प्रहारस्तु सुमहान्भविष्यति न संशयः। अयमाख्याति मे बाहुस्स्फुरमाणो मुहुर्मुहुः।।3.24.7।। सन्निकर्षे तु न श्शूर जयं शत्रोः पराजयम्। सप्रभं च प्रसन्नं च तव वक्त्रं हि लक्ष्यते।।3.24.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सुमहान् very great, सम्प्रहारस्तु destruction of life, भविष्यति will take place, संशयः doubt, न no, शूर O brave one, मुहुः मुहुः again and again, स्फुरमाणः shivering, अयम् this, मे बाहुः my (right) shoulder, सन्निकर्षे in near future, नः our, जयम् success, शत्रोः enemy's, पराजयम् defeat, आख्याति indicating, तव your, वक्त्रम् face, सप्रभं च is bright, प्रसन्नं च cheerful too, लक्ष्यते हि appears indeed.

#### Explanation

A mass destruction of life is going to take place. There is no doubt about it. O brave Lakshmana my right shoulder trembles again and again, indicating the defeat of the enemy. Your beaming visage presages victory.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

उद्यतानां हि युद्धार्थं येषां भवति लक्ष्मण। निष्प्रभं वदनं तेषां भवत्यायुः परिक्षयः।।3.24.9।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लक्ष्मण Lakshmana, युद्धार्थम् for war, उद्यतानाम् of those making effort, येषाम् of those, वदनम् face, निष्प्रभम् pale, भवति becomes, तेषाम् their, आयुः life, परिक्षयः shortened, भवति become.

#### Explanation

O Lakshmana, while preparing for war, those whose faces look pale are, for sure, going to die.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

रक्षसां नर्दतां घोरश्श्रूयते च महाध्वनिः। आहतानां च भेरीणां राक्षसैः क्रूरकर्मभिः।।3.24.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

नर्दताम् of the roaring ones, रक्षसाम् of the demons, क्रूरकर्मभिः doing cruel deeds, राक्षसैः by the demons, आहतानाम् of the beaten (drums), भेरीणां च of kettle drums, घोरः dreadful, महाध्वनिः very loud sound, श्रूयते च is heard.

#### Explanation

A dreadful roar of demons and the loud sound of wardrums beaten by the wicked demons are heard.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

अनागतविधानं तु कर्तव्यं शुभमिच्छता। आपदं शङ्कमानेन पुरुषेण विपश्चिता।।3.24.11।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

आपदम् danger, शङ्कमानेन by a person apprehending, शुभम् good, इच्छता by one desirous of, विपश्चिता by a learned one, पुरुषेण by a man, अनागतविधानम् defence before the onset of danger, कर्तव्यं हि should be planned.

#### Explanation

A wise man who apprehends danger or one who desires his own good should plan his defence even before the onset of danger.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

तस्माद्गृहीत्वा वैदेहीं शरपाणिर्धनुर्धरः। गुहामाश्रय शैलस्य दुर्गां पादपसङ्कुलाम्।।3.24.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तस्मात् therefore, वैदेहीम् to Sita, गृहीत्वा taking, शरपाणिः with arrows in hand, धनुर्धरः wielding bow, शैलस्य mountains, दुर्गाम् inaccessible, पादपसङ्कुलाम् crowded with trees, गुहाम् cave, आश्रय take shelter.

#### Explanation

Therefore, take shelter along with Sita in the inaccessible mountain cave, overgrown with trees, ready with your bow and arrows.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

प्रतिकूलितुमिच्छामि न हि वाक्यमिदं त्वया। शापितो मम पादाभ्यां गम्यतां वत्स मा चिरम्।।3.24.13।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

इदं वाक्यम् these words, त्वया you, प्रतिकूलितुम् to oppose, न इच्छामि I do not desire, वत्स dear one, मम पादाभ्याम् my word of honour on my feet, शापितः असि you are bound, गम्यताम् go, चिरम् मा without delay.

#### Explanation

O dear, I do not like you to oppose this word of honour. You must touch my feet and go without delay.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

त्वं हि शूरश्च बलवान्हन्याह्येतान्न संशयः। स्वयं तु हन्तुमिच्छामि सर्वानेव निशाचरान्।।3.24.14।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

त्वम् you, शूरश्च valiant too, बलवान् strong man, एतान् these demons, हन्याः हि you can kill them, तु सर्वानेव all of them, निशाचरान् demons, स्वयम् myself, हन्तुम् to kill,  इच्छामि wish.

#### Explanation

You are valiant and strong. There is no doubt that you can kill all these demons. But I desire to kill them.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

एवमुक्तस्तु रामेण लक्ष्मणस्सह सीताया। शरानादाय चापं च गुहां दुर्गां समाश्रयत्।।3.24.15।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रामेण by Rama, एवम् in that way, उक्तः was bid, लक्ष्मणः Lakshmana, शरान् arrows, चापं च bow too, आदाय taking, सीतया सह with Sita, दुर्गाम् inaccessible, गुहाम् cave, समाश्रयत् took shelter.

#### Explanation

Thus ordered by Rama, Lakshmana took the arrows and the bow and accompanied by Sita took shelter in the inaccessible cave.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

तस्मिन्प्रविष्टे तु गुहां लक्ष्मणे सह सीतया। हन्त निर्युक्तमित्युक्त्वा रामः कवचमाविशत्।।3.24.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तस्मिन् when he, लक्ष्मणे Lakshmana, सीतया सह with Sita, गुहाम् into the cave, प्रविष्टे entered, रामः Rama, हन्त oh, निर्युक्तम् work is accomplished, इति this, उक्त्वा having spoken, कवचम् shield, आविशत् put on.

#### Explanation

With Lakshmana and Sita inside the cave, Rama heaved his shield, saying, Oh the work is accomplished.

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

स तेनाग्नि निकाशेन कवचेन विभूषितः। बभूव राम स्तिमिरे विधूमोऽग्निरिवोत्थितः।।3.24.17।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अग्निनिकाशेन blazing like fire, तेन by that, कवचेन by the shield, विभूषितः welldecorated, सः रामः that Rama, तिमिरे in darkness, उत्थितः arising, विधूमः smokeless, अग्निरिव like fire, बभूव looked.

#### Explanation

Adorned with the shield, Rama shone like smokeless fire rising in darkness.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

स चापमुद्यम्य महच्छरानादाय वीर्यवान्। बभूवावस्थितस्तत्र ज्यास्वनैः पूरयन्दिशः।।3.24.18।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वीर्यवान् valiant, सः he, महत् huge, चापम् bow, उद्यम्य lifting, ज्यास्वनैः by the twangs of the  bowstring, दिशः all directions, पूरयन् filling, तत्र there, अवस्थितः बभूव stayed.

#### Explanation

Valiant Rama lifted the huge bow and arrows and stood there, filling the directions with the twangs of his bow.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

ततो देवास्सगन्धर्वास्सिद्धाश्च सह चारणैः। समेयुश्च महात्मानो युद्धदर्शनकाङ्क्षिणः।।3.24.19।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः thereafter, सगन्धर्वाः along with the celestial muscians, देवाः gods, महात्मनः great souls, सिद्धाश्च siddhas, चारणैः with bards, सह along with, युद्धदर्शनकाङ्क्षिणः desiring to see the war, समेयुः assembled.

#### Explanation

Then the gods along with gandharvas, siddhas, and celestial bards assembled there, wishing to witness the war.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

ऋषयश्च महात्मानो लोके ब्रह्मर्षिसत्तमाः। समेत्य चोचुस्सहिता अन्योन्यं पुण्यकर्मणः।।3.24.20।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महात्मानः highsouled, ऋषयश्च ascetics, लोके in the world, ब्रह्मर्षिसत्तमाः brahmarsis, पुण्यकर्मणः pious, समेत्य assembling, अन्योन्यम् to one another, सहिताः collectively, ऊचुः said.

#### Explanation

Great ascetics of the world, distinguished brahmarsis, accomplishers of pious deeds assembled and said to one another:

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

स्वस्ति गोब्राह्मणेभ्योऽस्तु लोकानां येऽभिसङ्गताः। जयतां राघवो युद्धे पौलस्त्यान् रजनीचरान्।।3.24.21।। चक्रहस्तो यथा युद्धे सर्वानसुर पुङ्गवान्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

गोब्राह्मणेभ्यः to cows and brahmins, लोकानां of all the world, (सर्वशः by all means), स्वस्ति auspicious, अस्तु let it be, (ऊचुः spoke), चक्रहस्तः Visnu, युद्धे in war, सर्वान् all, असुरपङ्गवान् powerful demons, यथा likewise, राघवः Rama, सङ्ख्ये पौलस्त्यान् all members of the Paulastya race in  the battle, रजनीचरान् nightrangers (demons), जयताम् let him win.

#### Explanation
