'Let there be wellbeing for the cows, brahmins, and all the worlds. Let Rama, the scion of the Raghu race, conquer the Paulastyas (demons) in war like lord Visnu, wielder of the disc, who defeated the most powerful demons'.

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

एवमुक्त्वा पुनः प्रोचुरालोक्य च परस्परम्।।3.24.22।। चतुर्दश सहस्राणि रक्षसां भीमकर्मणाम्। एकश्च रामो धर्मात्मा कथं युद्धं भविष्यति।।3.24.23।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

एवम् in that way, उक्त्वा having spoken, आलोक्य च and after looking at, परस्परम् to one another, पुनः again, प्रोचुः spoke, भीमकर्मणाम् of those of terrific deeds, रक्षसाम् of demons, चतुर्दश fourteen, सहस्राणि thousands, धर्मात्मा righteous self, रामश्च Rama, एकः all alone, युद्धम् war, कथम् how, भविष्यति will it be?

#### Explanation

Thus looking at one another they discussed among themselves how there can be war between the righteous Rama fighting singlehanded on the one hand and the fourteen thousand demons, performers of terrific deeds, on the other.

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

एवमुक्त्वा पुनः प्रोचुरालोक्य च परस्परम्।।3.24.22।। चतुर्दश सहस्राणि रक्षसां भीमकर्मणाम्। एकश्च रामो धर्मात्मा कथं युद्धं भविष्यति।।3.24.23।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

एवम् in that way, उक्त्वा having spoken, आलोक्य च and after looking at, परस्परम् to one another, पुनः again, प्रोचुः spoke, भीमकर्मणाम् of those of terrific deeds, रक्षसाम् of demons, चतुर्दश fourteen, सहस्राणि thousands, धर्मात्मा righteous self, रामश्च Rama, एकः all alone, युद्धम् war, कथम् how, भविष्यति will it be?

#### Explanation

Thus looking at one another they discussed among themselves how there can be war between the righteous Rama fighting singlehanded on the one hand and the fourteen thousand demons, performers of terrific deeds, on the other.

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

इति राजर्षयस्सिद्धास्सगणाश्च द्विजर्षभाः। जातकौतूहलास्तस्थुर्विमानस्थाश्च देवताः।।3.24.24।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राजर्षयः rajarsis, सगणाः along with their groups, सिद्धाः siddhas, द्विजर्षभाः great brahmins, विमानस्थाः on the celestial chariots, देवताश्च gods also, इति this way, जातकौकूहलाः with curiosity born, तस्थुः waited.

#### Explanation

Rajarsis, siddhas, great brahmins with their clan and gods on the celestial chariots waited to see the war whetted with curiosity.

---

### Shloka 25

#### Shloka Text

आविष्टं तेजसा रामं सङ्ग्रामशिरसि स्थितम्। दृष्ट्वा सर्वाणि भूतानि भयाद्विव्यथिरे तदा।।3.24.25।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तदा then, सङ्ग्रामशिरसि on the war front, स्थितम् standing, तेजसा with heroic lustre, आविष्ठम् engrossed, रामम् Rama, दृष्ट्वा seeing, सर्वाणि all, भूतानि beings, भयात् out of fear, विव्यथिरे were alarmed.

#### Explanation

Seeing Rama standing on the war front, engrossed and radiating heroic lustre, all beings were alarmed.

---

### Shloka 26

#### Shloka Text

रूपमप्रतिमं तस्य रामस्याक्लिष्टकर्मणः। बभूव रूपं क्रुद्धस्य रुद्रस्येव पिनाकिनः।।3.24.26।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अक्लिष्टकर्मणः of one who is unwearied in action, तस्य his, रामस्य Rama's, अप्रतिमम् incomparable, रूपम् form, क्रुद्धस्य of an angry one, पिनाकिनः wielder of Pinaka, रुद्रस्य Rudra's, रूपमिव like the form, बभूव appeared.

#### Explanation

Rama, who is unwearied in action and who is of an incomparable (lovely) form, looked ferocious like the wielder of Pinaka (Lord Siva).

---

### Shloka 27

#### Shloka Text

ततो गम्भीरनिर्ह्रादं घोरवर्मायुधध्वजम्। अनीकं यातुधानानां समन्तात्प्रत्यदृश्यत।।3.24.27।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, गम्भीरनिर्ह्रादम् deep sound, घोरवर्मायुधध्वजम् having dreadful shields, weapons and banners, यातुधानानाम् of the demons, अनीकम् army, समन्तात् on all sides, प्रत्यदृश्यत appeared before.

#### Explanation

Then appeared on all sides the wellequipped army of demons raising deep sounds with dreadful shields, weapons and banners.

---

### Shloka 28

#### Shloka Text

सिंहनादं विसृजतामन्योन्यमभिगर्जताम्। चापानि विस्फ़ारयतां जृम्भतामप्यभीक्ष्णशः।।3.24.28।। विप्रघुष्टस्वनानां च दुन्धुभींश्चापि निघ्नताम्। तेषां सुतुमुलश्शब्दः पूरयामास तद्वनम्।।3.24.29।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सिंहनादम् roar of lion, विसृजताम् released, अन्योन्यम् at one another, अभिगर्जताम् shouting loudly, चापानि bows, विस्फ़ारयताम् stretching the bows, अभीक्ष्णशः constantly, जृम्भतामपि  yawning, विप्रघुष्टस्वनानां च  proclaiming loudly, दुन्धुभींश्च अपि even drums, निघ्नताम् beating, तेषाम् of them, सुतुमुलः tumultuous, शब्दः sound, तद्वनम् that forest, पूरयामास filled.

#### Explanation

The forest was filled with tumultuous sounds of the warriors roaring like lions,  shouting at one another, stretching the bowstrings constantly, yawning, proclaiming loudly and beating the drums.

---

### Shloka 29

#### Shloka Text

सिंहनादं विसृजतामन्योन्यमभिगर्जताम्। चापानि विस्फ़ारयतां जृम्भतामप्यभीक्ष्णशः।।3.24.28।। विप्रघुष्टस्वनानां च दुन्धुभींश्चापि निघ्नताम्। तेषां सुतुमुलश्शब्दः पूरयामास तद्वनम्।।3.24.29।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सिंहनादम् roar of lion, विसृजताम् released, अन्योन्यम् at one another, अभिगर्जताम् shouting loudly, चापानि bows, विस्फ़ारयताम् stretching the bows, अभीक्ष्णशः constantly, जृम्भतामपि  yawning, विप्रघुष्टस्वनानां च  proclaiming loudly, दुन्धुभींश्च अपि even drums, निघ्नताम् beating, तेषाम् of them, सुतुमुलः tumultuous, शब्दः sound, तद्वनम् that forest, पूरयामास filled.

#### Explanation

The forest was filled with tumultuous sounds of the warriors roaring like lions,  shouting at one another, stretching the bowstrings constantly, yawning, proclaiming loudly and beating the drums.

---

### Shloka 30

#### Shloka Text

तेन शब्देन वित्रस्ताश्वापदा वनचारिणः। दुद्रुवुर्यत्र निश्शब्दं पृष्ठतो न व्यलोकयन्।।3.24.30।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तेन शब्देन by that sound, वित्रस्ताः alarmed, वनचारिणः roaming the forest, श्वापदाः animals, यत्र whereever, निश्शब्दम् silence, दुद्रुवुः ran, पृष्ठतः at the back, न व्यलोकयन् did not look back.

#### Explanation

The ferocious animals of the forest got frightened and ran away to silent spots without looking back.

---

### Shloka 31

#### Shloka Text

तत्त्वनीकं महाघोरं रामं समुपसर्पत। धृतनानाप्रहरणं गम्भीरं सागरोपमम्।।3.24.31।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महाघोरम् extremely frightening, धृतनानाप्रहरणम् equipped with different kinds of missiles, गम्भीरम् deep, सागरोपमम् like the sea, तत् अनीकम् that army, रामम् to Rama, समुपसर्पत came near.

#### Explanation

The fierce army, deep like the sea, equipped with various missiles approached Rama.

---

### Shloka 32

#### Shloka Text

रामोऽपि चारयंश्चक्षुस्सर्वतो रणपण्डितः। ददर्श खरसैन्यं तद्युद्धाभिमुखमुत्थितम्।।3.24.32।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रणपण्डितः wellversed in warfare, रामोऽपि even Rama, चक्षुः his eyes, सर्वतः on all sides, चारयन् while casting, युद्धाभिमुखम् facing war, उत्थितम् was standing, तत् that, खरसैन्यम् army of Khara, ददर्श he saw.

#### Explanation

Rama, wellversed in warfare, cast his eyes on all sides to see the army of Khara that had come prepared for war.

---

### Shloka 33

#### Shloka Text

वितत्य च धनुर्भीमं तूण्याश्चोद्धृत्य सायकान्। क्रोधमाहारयत्तीव्रं वधार्थं सर्वरक्षसाम्।।3.24.33।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

भीमम् formidable, धनुः bow, वितत्य  stretched, तूण्याः from the quiver, सायकान् arrows, उद्धृत्य took out, सर्व रक्षसाम् to all the demons, वधार्थम् to kill, तीव्रम् intense, क्रोधम् anger, आहारयत् gathered.

#### Explanation

Rama stretched the formidable bow and lifted the arrows from his quiver to kill the demons and assumed intense anger.

---

### Shloka 34

#### Shloka Text

दुष्प्रेक्ष्यस्सोऽभवत्कृद्धो युगान्ताग्निरिव ज्वलन्। तं दृष्ट्वा तेजसाविष्टं प्राद्रवन्वनदेवताः।।3.24.34।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

क्रुद्धः angry, सः Rama, ज्वलन् while burning, युगान्ताग्निरिव like fire rising at the end of the creation, दुष्प्रेक्ष्य: difficult even to look at अभवत्  became, तेजसा glow, आविष्टम् filled with, तम् him, दृष्ट्वा after seeing, वनदेवताः sylvan deities, प्राद्रवन् ran away.

#### Explanation

Rama stood burning in anger like the fire of the doom's day. It was difficult even to look at him. Seeing him full of glow even the sylvan deities ran away.

---

### Shloka 35

#### Shloka Text

तस्य क्रुद्धस्य रूपं तु रामस्य ददृशे तदा। दक्षस्येव क्रतुं हन्तुमुद्यतस्य पिनाकिनः।।3.24.35।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तदा then, क्रुद्धस्य of an angry one, तस्य his, रूपम् face, दक्षस्य Daksha's, क्रतुम् sacrifice, हन्तुम् to destroy, उद्यतस्य ready to, पिनाकिनः इव like that of Siva, ददृशे looked.

#### Explanation

Rama in anger looked like Lord Siva ready to destroy the sacrifice of Daksha.

---

### Shloka 36

#### Shloka Text

आविष्टं तेजसा रामं सङ्ग्रामशिरसि स्थितम्। दृष्ट्वा सर्वाणि भूतानि भयार्तानि प्रदुद्रुवुः।।3.24.36।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तेजसा with splendour, आविष्टम् occupied, सङ्ग्रामशिरसि at the head of the battle, स्थितम् stood, रामम् Rama, दृष्ट्वा seeing, सर्वाणि all, भूतानि beings, भयार्तानि frightened, प्रदुद्रुवुः ran away.

#### Explanation

Seeing Rama flashing (in anger) and standing on the warfront all creatures ran away frightened.

---

### Shloka 37

#### Shloka Text

तत्कार्मुकैराभरणैर्ध्वजैश्च तैर्वर्मभिश्चाग्निसमानवर्णैः। बभूव सैन्यं पिशिताशनानां सूर्योदये नीलमिवाभ्रबृन्दम्।।3.24.37।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पिशिताशनानाम् of the demons, तत् that, सैन्यम् army, कार्मुकैः with bows, आभरणैः with armour, ध्वजैश्च with flags, अग्निसमानवर्णैः the colour of fire, तैः by them, वर्मभिः by armours, सूर्योदये at Sunrise, नीलम् blue, अभ्रवृन्दमिव like a mass of cloud, बभूव looked.

#### Explanation

The army of demons equipped with blazing armour, bows and banners appeared like a mass of blue clouds at Sunrise. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये अरण्यकाण्डे चतुर्विंशस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the twentyfourth sarga of Aranyakanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 25

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

अवष्टब्धधनुं रामं क्रुद्धं च रिपुघातिनम्। ददर्शाऽश्रममागम्य खरस्सह पुरस्सरैः।।3.25.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

खरः Khara, पुरस्सरैः सह accompanied by the pilot party, आश्रमम् to the hermitage, आगम्य arrived, अवष्टब्धधनुम् holding the bow, क्रुद्धम् angry, रिपुघातिनम् destroyer of the enemy, रामम् Rama, ददर्श saw.

#### Explanation

Khara accompanied by his pilot party arrived at the hermitage of Rama, the destroyer of enemies and saw him in fury, standing ready with his bow.

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

तं दृष्ट्वा सशरं चापमुद्यम्य खरनिस्स्वनम्। रामस्याभिमुखं सूतं चोद्यतामित्यचोदयत्।।3.25.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तम् him, दृष्ट्वा seeing, खरनिस्स्वनम् producing terrible sound, सशरम् with the arrow, चापम् bow, उद्यम्य lifted, रामस्य Rama's, अभिमुखम् facing him, चोद्यताम् drive (the chariot), इति thus, सूतम् to charioteer, अचोदयत् ordered to go.

#### Explanation

On seeing Rama, Khara held his terribly twanging bow and arrow, stood facing him, and ordered the charioteer to drive towards him.

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

स खरस्याज्ञया सूतस्तुरगान् समचोदयत्। यत्र रामो महाबाहुरेको चुन्वन्स्थितो धनुः।।3.25.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः that, सूतः charioteer, खरस्य Khara's, आज्ञया by the order, महाबाहुः mightyarmed, एकः alone, रामः Rama, धनुः bow, चुन्वन् selecting, स्थितः stood, तुरगान् horses, समचोदयत् drove.

#### Explanation

Commanded by Khara, the charioteer also urged the horses towards the mightyarmed Rama standing alone and wielding the bow.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

तं तु निष्पतितं दृष्ट्वा सर्वे ते रजनीचराः। नर्दमाना महानादं सचिवाः पर्यवारयन्।।3.25.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

निष्पतितम् fell upon, तम् him, दृष्ट्वा seeing, सचिवाः ministers, ते they, सर्वे all, रजनीचराः demons, महानादम् very loud sound, नर्दमानाः while roaring, पर्यवारयन् surrounded.

#### Explanation

Seeing Khara attacking (Rama) all the demon ministers surrounded him  roaring.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

स तेषां यातुधानानां मध्ये रथगतः खरः। बभूव मध्ये ताराणां लोहिताङ्ग इवोदितः।।3.25.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तेषां यातुधानाम् of the demons, मध्ये in the midst, रथगतः seated on a chariot, खरः Khara, ताराणाम् of stars, मध्ये in the middle, उदितः risen, लोहिताङ्गः the planet Mars, इव like, बभूव looked.

#### Explanation

Seated on a chariot in the midst of the demons, Khara looked like the rising Mars in the middle of the stars.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

ततश्शरसहस्रेण राममप्रतिमौजसम्। अर्दयित्वा महानादं ननाद समरे खरः।।3.25.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, खरः Khara, समरे in war, शरसहस्रेण with a thousand arrows, अप्रतिमौजसम् of immeasureable prowess, रामम् to Rama, अर्दयित्वा striking, महानादम् a loud voice, ननाद made.

#### Explanation

Khara struck Rama of immeasurable prowess with a thousand arrows and roared loudly.

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

ततस्तं भीमधन्वानं क्रुद्धाः सर्वे निशाचराः। रामं नानाविधैः शस्स्रैरभ्यवर्षन्त दुर्जयम्।।3.25.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः thereafter, सर्वे all, क्रुद्धाः angry, निशाचराः demons, भीमधन्वानम् wielder of the fierce bow, दुर्जयम् invincible, तं रामम् to that Rama, नानाविधैः with many kinds of, शस्स्रैः weapons, अभ्यवर्षन्त rained.

#### Explanation

Thereafter all the infuriated demons showered on invincible Rama, wielder of a fierce bow, many kinds of weapons.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

मुद्गरैः पट्टसैश्शूलैः प्रासैः खङ्गै परश्वथैः। राक्षसास्समरे रामं निजघ्नू रोषतत्पराः।।3.25.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षसाः demons, रोषतत्पराः overtaken by anger, मुद्गरैः with hammers, पट्टसैः with spears, शूलैः with spikes, प्रासैः with barbed missiles, खङ्गै: with swords, परश्वधः battleaxes, समरे in war, रामम् to Rama, निजघ्नुः continously hurled.

#### Explanation

Overtaken by anger, the demons continuously hurled at Rama hammers, spears, spikes, barbed missiles, swords and battleaxes.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

ते वलाहसङ्काशा महानादा महौजसः। अभ्यधावन्त काकुत्स्थं रथैर्वाजिभिरेव च।।3.25.9।। गजैः पर्वतकूटाभै रामं युद्धे जिघांसवः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वलाहकसङ्काशाः resembling clouds, महानादाः with huge uproar, महौजसः very powerful, ते demons, रथैः on chariots, वाजिभिरेव च on horses and, पर्वतकूटाभैः looking like tops of mountains, गजैः by elephants, युद्धे in war, जिघांसवः eager to kill, रामम् to Rama, अभ्यधावन्त ran towards.

#### Explanation

Very powerful demons like huge clouds roared, eager to kill Rama in war. They ran forward on chariots, horses and tall elephants, looking like  mountain tops.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

ते रामे शरवर्षाणि व्यसृजन् रक्षसां गणाः।।3.25.10।। शैलेन्द्रमिव धाराभिर्वर्षमाणा वलाहकाः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ते they, रक्षसाम् of demons, गणाः battalions, रामे on Rama, वर्षमाणाः raining, वलाहकाः clouds, धाराभिः by downpour, शैलेन्द्रमिव like moutains, शरवर्षाणि rain of arrows, व्यसृजन् sent forth.

#### Explanation

The battalions of demons sent down streams of arrows on Rama just like clouds  downpour on mountains.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

स तैः परिवृतो घोरैः राघवो रक्षसां गणैः।।3.25.11।। तिथिष्वि महादेवो वृत परिषदाङ्गणै

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

स: that, राघवः Rama, घोरैः terrible, रक्षसां गणैः by hordes of demons, परिवृत: surrounded, महादेवः Lord Siva, वृतः surrounded, परिषदाङ्गणैः with hosts of retinues, तिथिष्वि at dusk.

#### Explanation

Surrounded by the terrible demons Rama appeared like Lord Siva at dusk with hosts of his retinues arounds.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

तानि मुक्तानि शस्त्राणि यातुधानैस्सराघवः। प्रतिजग्राह विशिखैर्नद्योघानिव सागरः।।3.25.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः that, राघवः Rama, यातुधानैः by the demons, मुक्तानि released, तानि those, शस्त्राणि weapons, सागरः sea, नद्योघानिव like the flow of rivers, विशिखैः with arrows, प्रतिजग्राह resisted.

#### Explanation

Rama resisted the weapons released by the demons like the sea, the inflow of river waters.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

स तैः प्रहरणैर्घोरैर्भिन्नगात्रो न विव्यथे। रामः प्रदीप्तैर्बहुभिर्वज्रैरिव महाचलः।।3.25.13।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

घोरैः by the dreadful, प्रहरणै by weapons, तैः by those, भिन्नगात्रः his limbs being hit, सः रामः that Rama, प्रदीपैः by the glowing, बहुभिः by many, वज्रैः by thunderbolts, महाचल इव like the great mountain, न विव्यथे was not hurt.

#### Explanation

Rama remained unhurt like a great mountain struck by many glowing thunderbolts. He felt no pain even though his limbs were hit by the dreadful weapons.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

स विद्धः क्षतजादिग्धः सर्वगात्रेषु राघवः। बभूव रामः सन्ध्याभ्रैर्दिवाकर इवावृतः।।3.25.14।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

विद्ध: wounded, सर्वगात्रेषु all over the body, क्षतजादिग्धः bleeding through the wounds, राघवः Rama, सः रामः that Rama, सन्ध्याभ्रैः by evening clouds, आवृतः surrounded, दिवाकरः इव like the Sun, बभूव became (appeared).

#### Explanation

Wounded all over the body, bleeding through the wounds caused by weapons, Rama looked like the evening Sun covered with clouds.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

विषेदुर्देवगन्धर्वास्सिद्धाश्च परमर्षयः। एकं सहस्रैर्भहुभिस्तदा दृष्ट्वा समावृतम्।।3.25.15।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तदा then, बहुभिः by many, सहस्रैः by thousands, समावृतम् surrounded by, एकम् single, दृष्ट्वा seeing, देवगन्धर्वाः gods and gandharvas, सिद्धाश्च siddhas, परमर्षयः great sages, विषेदुः became sad.

#### Explanation

Seeing Rama left alone surrounded by many thousands of demons, the gods, ghandarvas and siddhas became very sad.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

ततो रामस्सुसङ्कृद्धो मण्डलीकृतकार्मुकः। ससर्ज विशिखान्बाणाञ्छतशोऽथ सहस्रश।।3.25.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः thereafter, रामः Rama, सुसङ्कृद्धः mighty angry, मण्डलीकृतकार्मुकः bending the bow, शतशः in hundreds, अथ and, सहस्रशः thousands, विशिखान् unfeathered ones, बाणान् arrows, ससर्ज released.

#### Explanation

Thereafter Rama, mighty angry, bent his bow and released hundreds and thousands of unfeathered arrows.

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

दुरावारान्दुर्विषहान्कालदण्डोपमान् रणे। मुमोच लीलया रामः कङ्कपत्रानजिह्मगान्।।3.25.17।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रामः Rama, रणे in war, दुरावारान् those difficult to stop, दुर्विषहान् unbearable ones, कालदण्डोपमान् those looking like Yama's staff, अजिह्मगान् going straight without missing the goal, कङ्कपत्रान् arrows furnished with herons' feathers, लीलया very easily, मुमोच discharged.

#### Explanation

As if sporting, Rama discharged the arrows attached with herons' feathers. The arrows were inescapable, unbearable like that of Yama's staff, that went straight without missing the target.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

ते शराश्शत्रुसैन्येषु मुक्ता रामेण लीलया। अददू रक्षसां प्राणान्पाशाः कालकृता इव।।3.25.18।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रामेण by Rama, लीलया sportively, शत्रुसैन्येषु at the army of the enemy, मुक्ताः released, ते शराः those arrows, कालकृताः made by the god of death, पाशाः nooses, इव like, रक्षसाम् of the demons, प्राणान् life, आददुः pulled out.

#### Explanation

The arrows like the noose of the god of death released by Rama on the enemy army easily destroyed the lives of the demons.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

भित्त्वा राक्षसदेहां स्तांस्ते शरा रुधिराप्लुताः। अन्तरिक्षगता रेजुर्दीप्ताग्निसमतेजसः।।3.25.19।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ते शराः those arrows, तान् those, राक्षसदेहान् bodies of the demons, भित्त्वा after piercing, रुधिराप्लुताः drenched in blood, अन्तरिक्षगताः gone high up into the sky, दीप्ताग्निसमतेजसः glowing red like burning fire, रेजुः sparkled.

#### Explanation

Rama's arrows pierced the bodies of the demons and drenched in blood, they, looking like burning fire, shone in the sky.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

असङ्ख्येयास्तु रामस्य सायकाश्चापमण्डलात्। विनिष्पेतुरतीवोग्रा रक्षःप्राणापहारिणः।।3.25.20।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रामस्य Rama's, चापमण्डलात् from the bow bent into a circle, अतीव highly, उग्राः fierce, रक्षःप्राणापहारिणः which can take the lives of demons, असङ्ख्येयाः countless ones, सायकाः arrows, विनिष्पेतुः thrown out destroying.

#### Explanation

From Rama's bow bent into a cirle, countless fierce arrows able to take the lives of demons were released.

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

तैर्धनूंषि ध्वजाग्राणि वर्माणि च शिरांसि च। बाहून्सहस्ताभरणानूरून्करिकरोपमान्।।3.25.21।। चिच्छेद रामस्समरे शतशोऽथ सहस्रशः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रामः Rama, समरे in war, तैः by them (arrows), शतशः in hundreds, अथ and, सहस्रशः in thousands, धनूंषि bows, ध्वजाग्राणि tops of flags, वर्माणि च and armour, शिरांसि च and heads, सहस्ताभरणान् along with armlets, बाहून् shoulders, करिकरोपमान् like elephants' trunks, ऊरून् thighs, चिच्छेद cut.

#### Explanation

In war Rama cut asunder the bows, flag tops, armour and heads of demons, their shoulders and thighs that were like the trunks of elephants, in their hundreds and thousands.

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

हयान्काञ्चनसन्नाहान्रथयुक्तान्ससारथीन्।।3.25.22।। गजांश्च सगजारोहान्सहयान्सादिनस्तथा। पदातीन्समरे हत्वा ह्यनयद्यमसादनम्।।3.25.23।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

काञ्चनसन्नाहान् golden ornaments, रथयुक्तान् yoked to the chariots, ससारथीन् along with charioteers, हयान् horses, सगजारोहान् with elephant riders, गजांश्च and elephants, तथा likewise, सहयान् along with horses, सादिनः horse riders, पदातीन् infantry, समरे in war, हत्वा  killed, यमसादनम् to the abode of lord of death, अनयत् sent.

#### Explanation

He killed horses with golden armours yoked to the chariots along with charioteers, elephants along with riders, the cavalry along with their horses and infantry and sent them all to the abode of death.

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

हयान्काञ्चनसन्नाहान्रथयुक्तान्ससारथीन्।।3.25.22।। गजांश्च सगजारोहान्सहयान्सादिनस्तथा। पदातीन्समरे हत्वा ह्यनयद्यमसादनम्।।3.25.23।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

काञ्चनसन्नाहान् golden ornaments, रथयुक्तान् yoked to the chariots, ससारथीन् along with charioteers, हयान् horses, सगजारोहान् with elephant riders, गजांश्च and elephants, तथा likewise, सहयान् along with horses, सादिनः horse riders, पदातीन् infantry, समरे in war, हत्वा  killed, यमसादनम् to the abode of lord of death, अनयत् sent.

#### Explanation

He killed horses with golden armours yoked to the chariots along with charioteers, elephants along with riders, the cavalry along with their horses and infantry and sent them all to the abode of death.

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

ततो नालीकनाराचैस्तीक्ष्णाग्रैश्च विकर्णिभिः। भीममार्तस्वरं चक्रुर्भिद्यमाना निशाचराः।।3.25.24।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, नालीकनाराचैः with iron arrows, तीक्ष्णाग्रैः with sharp tops, विकर्णिभिः by shafts called vikarni, भिद्यमानाः torn into parts, निशाचराः demons, भीमम् terrible, आर्तस्वरम् pitiable cries, चक्रुः made.

#### Explanation

The bodies of the demons torn into pieces by the very sharp iron arrows and by the arrows called vikarni, they cried and shouted aloud in pitiable voices.

---

### Shloka 25

#### Shloka Text

तत्सैन्यं निशितैर्बाणैरर्दितं मर्मभेदिभिः। रामेण न सुखं लेभे शुष्कं वनमिवाग्निना।।3.25.25।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रामेण by Rama, मर्मभेदिभिः by those piercing into vital parts, निशितैः by sharp ones, बाणैः by arrows, अर्दितम् tormented, तत् सैन्यम् that army, शुष्कम् dried up, वनम् forest, अग्निना इव as if by fire, सुखम् relief न लेभे did not have.

#### Explanation

Just like the dried up forest burnt by the fire, the army was totally destroyed by Rama's arrows that pierced into the vital parts of the soldiers.

---

### Shloka 26

#### Shloka Text

केचिद्भीमबलाश्शूराश्शूलान्खङ्गान्परश्वधान्। रामस्याभिमुखं गत्वा चिक्षिपुः परमायुधान्।।3.25.26।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

भीमबलाः men of fierce strength, केचित् a few, शूराः warriors, रामस्य Rama's, अभिमुखम् going towards, गत्वा went, परमायुधान् very powerful weapons, शूलान् spikes, खङ्गान् swords, परश्वधान् axes, चिक्षिपुः threw.

#### Explanation

A few warriors of fierce strength approached Rama and hurled at him very powerful weapons, spears, swords and axes.

---

### Shloka 27

#### Shloka Text

तानि बाणैर्महाबाहुश्शस्त्राण्यावार्य राघवः। जहार समरे प्राणांश्चिच्छेद च शिरोधरान्।।3.25.27।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महाबाहुः strongarmed, राघवः Rama, समरे in war, बाणैः with arrows, तेषाम् their, शस्त्राणि weapons, आवार्य attacked, प्राणान् life, जहार took, शिरोधरान् heads, चिच्छेद cut off.

#### Explanation

Strongarmed Rama attacked them with his arrows and cut off their heads and took their lives.

---

### Shloka 28

#### Shloka Text

ते छिन्नशिरसः पेतुश्चिन्नवर्मशरासनाः। सुपर्णवातविक्षिप्ता जगत्यां पादपा यथा।।3.25.28।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ते they, छिन्नशिरसः broken heads, छिन्नवर्मशरासनाः broken armour and bows, सुपर्णवातविक्षिप्ताः broken tree tops fallen down by the wind of eagle's wing (blow of the the great eagle Garuda), पादपाः trees, यथा just as, जगत्याम् on the land, पेतुः fell.

#### Explanation

The heads, armour and bows fell down broken on the ground like trees in the wind gererated by Garuda's wings.

---

### Shloka 29

#### Shloka Text

अवशिष्टाश्च ये तत्र विषण्णाश्च निशाचराः। खरमेवाभ्यधावन्त शरणार्थं शरार्दिताः।।3.25.29।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तत्र there, अवशिष्टाः remaining ones, ये those, निशाचराः demons, शरार्दिताः hit by arrows, विषण्णाश्च desperate, खरमेव to Khara only, शरणार्थम् for refuge, अभ्यधावन्त ran towards.

#### Explanation

The rest of the demons hit by the arrows ran desperate towards Khara for refuge.

---

### Shloka 30

#### Shloka Text

तान्सर्वान्पुनरादाय समाश्वास्य च दूषणः। अभ्यधावत काकुत्स्थं क्रुद्धो रुद्रमिवान्तकः।।3.25.30।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

दूषणः Dusana, तान् स them, सर्वान् all, समाश्वास्य pacifying, पुनः again, आदाय bringing them together, क्रुद्धः wrathful, अन्तकः yama, रुद्रमिव like Rudra, काकुत्स्थं on Rama, अभ्यधावत ran towards.

#### Explanation

Pacifying them all, Dusana regathered them again and ran towards Rama like an angry Yama rushing towards Rudra.

---

### Shloka 31

#### Shloka Text

निवृत्तास्तु पुनस्सर्वे दूषणाश्रयनिर्भयाः। राममेवाभ्यधावन्त सालतालशिलायुधाः।।3.25.31।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सर्वे all, दूषणाश्रयनिर्भयाः were free from fear due to Dusana's protection, पुनः again, निवृत्ताः returned, सालतालशिलायुधाः carrying sal, palm and stones as weapons, राममेव to Rama, अभ्यधावन्त advanced towards.

#### Explanation

Under Dusana's protection, all of them felt relieved from fear and, carrying sal and palm trees and stones as weapons, advanced towards Rama again.

---

### Shloka 32

#### Shloka Text

शूलमुद्गरहस्ताश्च चापहस्ता महाबलाः। सृजन्तश्शरवर्षाणि शस्त्रवर्षाणि संयुगे।।3.25.32।। द्रुमवर्षाणि मुञ्चन्तश्शिलावर्षाणि राक्षसाः।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

शूलमुद्गरहस्ताश्च spears and hammers in their hands, चापहस्ताः bows in hands, महाबलाः extremely strong, राक्षसाः demons, संयुगे in war, शरवर्षाणि rain of  arrows, शस्त्रवर्षाणि rain of weapons, सृजन्तः poured, द्रुमवर्षाणि rain of trees, शिलावर्षाणि rain of stones, मुञ्चन्तः  continuously released.

#### Explanation

Holding spears, hammers and bows in their hands, the mighty demons rained on Rama weapons like arrows, trees and stones.

---

### Shloka 33

#### Shloka Text

तद्बभूवाद्भुतं युद्धं तुमुलं रोमहर्षणम्। रामस्य च महाघोरं पुनस्तेषां च रक्षसाम्।।3.25.33।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पुनः again, रामस्य Rama's, तेषां रक्षसां च of those demons, तुमुलम् fierce, अद्भुतम् wonderful, रोमहर्षणम् horripilating, महाघोरम् very dreadful, तत् युद्धम् that war, बभूव became.

#### Explanation

The thrilling, fierce, horrifying and horripilating battle again ensued betwen Rama and the demons.

---

### Shloka 34

#### Shloka Text

ते समन्तादतिक्रुद्धा राघवं पुनरभ्ययुः।।3.25.34।। तैश्च सर्वा दिशो दृष्ट्वा प्रदिशश्च समावृताः। राक्षसैरुद्यतप्रासैश्शरवर्षाभिवर्षिभिः।।3.25.35।। स कृत्वा भैरवं नादमस्त्रं परमभास्वरम्। संयोजयत गान्धर्वं राक्षसेषु महाबलः।।3.25.36।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ते they, अतिक्रुद्धाः mighty angry, पुनः again, राघवम् at Rama, समन्तात् from all directions, अभ्ययुः advanced towards, महाबलः mighty, सः he, उद्यतप्रासैः by raised missiles, शरवर्षिभिः raining arrows, तैः by them, सर्वाः all, दिशः quarters, प्रदिशश्च also intermediate directions, समावृताः were covered, दृष्ट्वा seeing, भैरवम् frightening, नादम् sound, कृत्वा produced, राक्षसेषु at the demons, परमभास्वरम् very dazzling, गान्धर्वम् belonging to gandharva, अस्त्रम् weapon, संयोजयत aimed at.

#### Explanation

As the infuriated demons again hurtled towards Rama, filling the quarters and the intermediate directions and raining missiles, Rama aimed at them dazzling gandharvastras, producing a frightening warcry.

---

### Shloka 35

#### Shloka Text

ते समन्तादतिक्रुद्धा राघवं पुनरभ्ययुः।।3.25.34।। तैश्च सर्वा दिशो दृष्ट्वा प्रदिशश्च समावृताः। राक्षसैरुद्यतप्रासैश्शरवर्षाभिवर्षिभिः।।3.25.35।। स कृत्वा भैरवं नादमस्त्रं परमभास्वरम्। संयोजयत गान्धर्वं राक्षसेषु महाबलः।।3.25.36।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ते they, अतिक्रुद्धाः mighty angry, पुनः again, राघवम् at Rama, समन्तात् from all directions, अभ्ययुः advanced towards, महाबलः mighty, सः he, उद्यतप्रासैः by raised missiles, शरवर्षिभिः raining arrows, तैः by them, सर्वाः all, दिशः quarters, प्रदिशश्च also intermediate directions, समावृताः were covered, दृष्ट्वा seeing, भैरवम् frightening, नादम् sound, कृत्वा produced, राक्षसेषु at the demons, परमभास्वरम् very dazzling, गान्धर्वम् belonging to gandharva, अस्त्रम् weapon, संयोजयत aimed at.

#### Explanation

As the infuriated demons again hurtled towards Rama, filling the quarters and the intermediate directions and raining missiles, Rama aimed at them dazzling gandharvastras, producing a frightening warcry.

---

### Shloka 36

#### Shloka Text

ते समन्तादतिक्रुद्धा राघवं पुनरभ्ययुः।।3.25.34।। तैश्च सर्वा दिशो दृष्ट्वा प्रदिशश्च समावृताः। राक्षसैरुद्यतप्रासैश्शरवर्षाभिवर्षिभिः।।3.25.35।। स कृत्वा भैरवं नादमस्त्रं परमभास्वरम्। संयोजयत गान्धर्वं राक्षसेषु महाबलः।।3.25.36।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ते they, अतिक्रुद्धाः mighty angry, पुनः again, राघवम् at Rama, समन्तात् from all directions, अभ्ययुः advanced towards, महाबलः mighty, सः he, उद्यतप्रासैः by raised missiles, शरवर्षिभिः raining arrows, तैः by them, सर्वाः all, दिशः quarters, प्रदिशश्च also intermediate directions, समावृताः were covered, दृष्ट्वा seeing, भैरवम् frightening, नादम् sound, कृत्वा produced, राक्षसेषु at the demons, परमभास्वरम् very dazzling, गान्धर्वम् belonging to gandharva, अस्त्रम् weapon, संयोजयत aimed at.

#### Explanation

As the infuriated demons again hurtled towards Rama, filling the quarters and the intermediate directions and raining missiles, Rama aimed at them dazzling gandharvastras, producing a frightening warcry.

---

### Shloka 37

#### Shloka Text

ततश्शरसहस्राणि निर्ययुश्चापमण्डलात्। सर्वा दश दिशो बाणैरावार्यन्त समागतैः।।3.25.37।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, चापमण्डलात् from the the bow bent round, शरसहस्राणि thousands of arrows, निर्ययुः let out, समागतैः by approaching, बाणैः by arrows, सर्वाः all, दश ten, दिशः quarters, अवार्यन्त were filled.

#### Explanation

Rama then let out from his bow, stretched round, thousands of arrows that covered all the ten quarters.

---

### Shloka 38

#### Shloka Text

नाददानं शरान्घोरान्नमुञ्चन्तं शिलीमुखान्। विकर्षमाणं पश्यन्ति राक्षसास्ते शरार्दिताः।।3.25.38।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

शरार्दिताः hurt by the arrows, ते राक्षसाः the demons, घोरान् terrible, शरान् arrows, आददानम् taking out, न पश्यन्ति could not see, शिलीमुखान् arrows, मुञ्चन्तम् sending forth, न not, विकर्षमाणम्  shooting arrow.

#### Explanation

The demons, though wounded, could not see whereform the terrible arrows came. They only saw Rama shooting the arrows.

---

### Shloka 39

#### Shloka Text

शरान्धकारमाकाशमावृणोत्सदिवाकरम्। बभूवावस्थितो रामः प्रवमन्निव ताञ्छरान्।।3.25.39।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

शरान्धकारम् darkness created by the arrows, सदिवाकरम् along with the Sun, आकाशम् sky, आवृणोत् covered, रामः Rama, तान् those, शरान् arrows, प्रवमन्निव as if vomitting, अवस्थितः stationed, भभूव appeared.

#### Explanation

Through the darkness created by the arrows that covered the sky and the Sun, Rama looked as though he was vomitting them.

---

### Shloka 40

#### Shloka Text

युगपत्पतमानैश्च युगपच्च हतैर्भृशम्। युगपत्पतितैश्चैव विकीर्णा वसुधा भवत्।।3.25.40।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वसुधा earth, युगपत् simultaneously, पतमानैश्च fallen down, हतैश्च by those slain, पतितैश्चैव by those who fell on the ground, भृशम् exceedingly, विकीर्णा scattered all over, अभवत् appeared.

#### Explanation

It seemed as if it all happened at the same time, the entire earth filling with demons seriously injured, slain and fallen down on the ground.

---

### Shloka 41

#### Shloka Text

निहताः पतिताः क्षीणाश्छिन्ना भिन्ना विदारिताः। तत्र तत्र स्म दृश्यन्ते राक्षसास्ते सहस्रशः।।3.25.41।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ते राक्षसाः they, demons, निहताः killed, तत्र तत्र all over there, सहस्रशः in their thousands, पतिताः fallen, क्षीणाः thinned down, छिन्नाः cut into pieces, भिन्नाः those broken pieces, विदारिताः those pierced, दृश्यन्ते स्म were found.

#### Explanation

There were bodies of demons pierced and torn, thinned and fallen down, destroyed, broken and cut into pieces scattered in their thousands.

---

### Shloka 42

#### Shloka Text

सोष्णीषैरुत्तमाङ्गैश्च साङ्गदैर्बाहुभिस्तथा। ऊरुभिर्जानुभिश्छिन्नैर्नानारूपैविभूषणैः।।3.25.42।। हयैश्च द्विपमुख्यैश्च रथैर्भिन्नैरनेकशः। चामरैर्व्यजनैश्छत्रैर्ध्वजैर्नानाविधैरपि।।3.25.43।। रामस्य बाणाभिहतैर्विचित्रैश्शूलपट्टिसैः। खङ्गै खण्डीकृतैः प्रासैर्विकीर्णैश्च परश्वधैः।।3.25.44।। चूर्णिताभिश्शिलाभिश्च शरैश्चित्रैरनेकशः। विच्छिन्नैस्समरे भूमिर्विकीर्णाऽभूद्भयङ्करा।।3.25.45।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

समरे in war, सोष्णीषैः with headgears, उत्तमाङ्गैश्च heads (best part of body), तथा like that, साङ्गदैः armlets, बाहुभिः with shoulders, छिन्नैः cut to pieces, ऊरुभिः with thighs, जानुभिः with knees, नानारूपैर्विभूषणैः with different kinds of arms, अनेकशः in many ways, हयैश्च with horses, द्विपमुख्यैश्च with best of elephants, भिन्नैः by those broken, रथैः with chariots, चामरैः by fans made of yaktail, व्यजनैः by fans, छत्रैः by umbrellas, नानाविधैः of several kinds, ध्वजैः by flags, रामस्य Rama's, बाणाभिहतैः destroyed by arrows, विचित्रैः by different kinds, शूलपट्टिशैः by spears, खण्डीकृतैः by the broken ones, खङ्गैः with swords, प्रासैः by darts, विकीर्णैः by the  scattered, परश्वधैः by battleaxes, चूर्णिताभिः by those crushed into small pieces, शिलाभिश्च stones, अनेकशः many, विच्छिन्नैः broken, शरैः arrows, विकीर्णा scattered, भूमिः on the ground, भयंकरा frightening, अभूत्  to see.

#### Explanation

Slain thus by Rama's arrows, the ground was littered with (bodies of) demons with limbs dismembered, headdresses and shoulders cut off, arms, thighs and knees decked with various ornaments amputated, chariots, horses, best of elephants, fans made of the tail of chamari deer (used as an insignia of royalty), umbrellas, several  flags, and weapons like spears, swords, darts, battleaxes and stones crushed into pieces and arrows splintered and scattered all over. The ground looked frightening. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये अरण्यकाण्डे पञ्चविंशस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the twentyfifth sarga of Aranyakanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 26

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

दूषणस्तु स्वकं सैन्यं हन्यमानं निरीक्ष्य सः। सन्दिदेश महाबाहुर्भीमवेगान्दुरासदान्।।3.26.1।। राक्षसान्पञ्चसहस्रान्समरेष्वनिवर्तिनः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः that, महाबाहुः  mightyarmed, दूषणः Dusana, हन्यमानम्  getting killed, स्वकम् his own, सैन्यम् army, निरीक्ष्य  seeing, भीमवेगान्  dreadfuly quick, दुरासदान्  dangerous to face, समरेषु in war, अनिवर्तिनः who will not beat a retreat, पञ्च सहस्रान् five thousand, राक्षसान् demons, सन्दिदेश led.

#### Explanation

Mightyarmed Dusana saw  the army  getting killed in war. He led the forces of five thousand demons, dreadfuly quick at fighting, dangeous to face and who will not beat a retreat.

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

ते शूलैः पट्टिशैः खङ्गै शिलावर्षैर्द्रुमैरपि।।3.26.2।। शरवर्षैरविच्छिन्नं ववर्षुस्तं समन्ततः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ते they, शूलैः  spears, पट्टिशैः  sharpedged spears, खङ्गै: swords, शिलावर्षैः by raining stones, द्रुमैरपि and with trees, शरवर्षैः by showers of arrows, अविच्छिन्नम् continuously, समन्ततः from all sides, तम् him(Rama), ववर्षुः rained.

#### Explanation

The demons continuously hurled at him (Rama)  spears and swords and rained stones, trees and arrows from all sides

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

स दृमाणां शिलानां च वर्षं प्राणहरं महत्।।3.26.3।। प्रतिजग्राह धर्मात्मा राघवस्तीक्ष्णसायकैः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

धर्मात्मा righteous self, सः राघवः  Rama, प्राणहरम् destroyers of life, महत् mighty, द्रुमाणाम् trees, शिलानां च of stones and, वर्षम् rain, तीक्ष्णसायकैः with sharp arrows, प्रतिजग्राह retaliated.

#### Explanation

Righteous Rama, retaliated with his sharp arrows such as the rain of trees and stones, the mighty destroyers of life.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

प्रतिगृह्य च तद्वर्षं निमीलित इवर्षभः।।3.26.4।। रामः क्रोधं परं भेजे वधार्थं सर्वरक्षसाम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रामः Rama, निमीलितः closed his eyes, ऋषभः इव like a bull, तत् that, वर्षम् rain, प्रतिगृह्य  retaliated, सर्वरक्षसाम् all the demons, वधार्थम् to kill, परं excessively, क्रोधम् angry, भेजे felt.

#### Explanation

Rama retaliated in great anger to kill without caring for the shower of weapons like a bull with its eyes closed.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

ततः क्रोधसमाविष्टः प्रदीप्त इव तेजसा।।3.26.5।। शरैरवाकिरत्सैन्यं सर्वतस्सहदूषणम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, क्रोधसमाविष्टः overpowered by anger, तेजसा glow, प्रदीप्त इव as though blazing, सहदूषणम् along with Dusana, सैन्यम् army, सर्वतः all over, शरैः with arrows, अवाकिरत् filled.

#### Explanation

Overpowered by anger, Rama, with a blazing glow, covered Dusana and his army with his arrows.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

ततस्सेनापतिः क्रुद्धो दूषणश्शत्रुदूषणः।।3.26.6।। शरैरशनिकल्पैस्तं राघवं समवाकिरत्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः thereafter, सेनापतिः chief of the army, दूषणः Dusana, क्रुद्धः angry, अशनिकल्पैः  comparable to thunderbolt, शरैः with arrows, तं राघवम् to that Rama, समवाकिरत् charged from everywhere.

#### Explanation

Thereafter Dusana, chief of the army, angrily released at Rama equally powerful arrows comparable to thunderbolt.

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

ततो रामस्सुसङ्कृद्धः क्षुरेणास्य महद्धनुः।।3.26.7।। चिच्छेद समरे वीरश्चतुर्भिश्चतुरो हयान्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः thereafter, सुसङ्कृद्धः became furious, समरे in war, वीरः hero, रामः Rama, क्षुरेण with a swordlike, अस्य his, महत् great, धनुः bow, चतुर्भिः  four more, चतुरः four, हयान् horses, चिच्छेद cut to pieces.

#### Explanation

Heroic Rama, mighty angry, then, broke the big bow of  Dusana, with his swordlike arrow. And with a series of four arrows cut his four horses to pieces.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

हत्वा चाश्वान्शरैस्तीक्ष्णैरर्धचन्द्रेण सारथेः।।3.26.8।। शिरो जहार तद्रक्षस्त्रिभिर्विव्याध वक्षसि।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तीक्ष्णैः sharp ones, शरैः  arrows, अश्वान् horses, हत्वा killed, अर्धचन्द्रेण with an arrow of the shape of the cresunt moon, सारथेः charioteer's, शिरः head, जहार cut, त्रिभिः with three arrows, तत् that, रक्षः demon( Dushana), वक्षसि chest, विव्याध pierced.

#### Explanation

After killing the horses with sharp arrows Rama severed the charioteer's head with an arrow of the shape of the crescent moon and pierced Dusana's chest with a set of three arrows.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

स च्छिन्नधन्वा विरथो हताश्वो हतसारथिः।।3.26.9।। जग्राह गिरिशृङ्गाभं परिघं रोमहर्षणम्। वेष्टितं काञ्चनैः पट्टैर्देवसैन्यप्रमर्दनम्।।3.26.10।। आयसैश्शङ्कुभिस्तीक्ष्णैः कीर्णं परवसोक्षितम्। वज्राशनिसमस्पर्शं परगोपुरदारणम्।।3.26.11।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

छिन्नधन्वा  with broken bow, विरथः no chariot, हताश्वः  horses killled, हतसारथिः  charioteer killed, सः he, गिरिशृङ्गाभम्  looking like a huge mountain top, रोमहर्षणम् causing horripilation, काञ्चनैः with golden, पट्टैः with bands, वेष्टितम् bound, देवसैन्यप्रमर्दनम् that which can crush even gods' army, तीक्ष्णैः sharp, आयसैः made of iron, शङ्कुभिः with nails, कीर्णम् covered, परवसोक्षितम्  sprinkled with enemy's marrow, वज्राशनिसमस्पर्शम् like the touch of thunderbolt, परगोपुरदारणम् that which can break open enemies fort, परिघम्  spear, जग्राह took up .

#### Explanation

With his bow and chariot broken, charioteer and horses killed,  Dusana took up a spear in his hand that appeared like a huge mountain top. Bound by golden bands, covered the iron nails, wetted with the enemy's marrow it created a horripilation. It appeared as though it  could crush the army of gods and render the enemy powerless. It carried the killing touch of the thunderbolt and could break open the enemy's fort.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

स च्छिन्नधन्वा विरथो हताश्वो हतसारथिः।।3.26.9।। जग्राह गिरिशृङ्गाभं परिघं रोमहर्षणम्। वेष्टितं काञ्चनैः पट्टैर्देवसैन्यप्रमर्दनम्।।3.26.10।। आयसैश्शङ्कुभिस्तीक्ष्णैः कीर्णं परवसोक्षितम्। वज्राशनिसमस्पर्शं परगोपुरदारणम्।।3.26.11।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

छिन्नधन्वा  with broken bow, विरथः no chariot, हताश्वः  horses killled, हतसारथिः  charioteer killed, सः he, गिरिशृङ्गाभम्  looking like a huge mountain top, रोमहर्षणम् causing horripilation, काञ्चनैः with golden, पट्टैः with bands, वेष्टितम् bound, देवसैन्यप्रमर्दनम् that which can crush even gods' army, तीक्ष्णैः sharp, आयसैः made of iron, शङ्कुभिः with nails, कीर्णम् covered, परवसोक्षितम्  sprinkled with enemy's marrow, वज्राशनिसमस्पर्शम् like the touch of thunderbolt, परगोपुरदारणम् that which can break open enemies fort, परिघम्  spear, जग्राह took up .

#### Explanation

With his bow and chariot broken, charioteer and horses killed,  Dusana took up a spear in his hand that appeared like a huge mountain top. Bound by golden bands, covered the iron nails, wetted with the enemy's marrow it created a horripilation. It appeared as though it  could crush the army of gods and render the enemy powerless. It carried the killing touch of the thunderbolt and could break open the enemy's fort.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

स च्छिन्नधन्वा विरथो हताश्वो हतसारथिः।।3.26.9।। जग्राह गिरिशृङ्गाभं परिघं रोमहर्षणम्। वेष्टितं काञ्चनैः पट्टैर्देवसैन्यप्रमर्दनम्।।3.26.10।। आयसैश्शङ्कुभिस्तीक्ष्णैः कीर्णं परवसोक्षितम्। वज्राशनिसमस्पर्शं परगोपुरदारणम्।।3.26.11।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

छिन्नधन्वा  with broken bow, विरथः no chariot, हताश्वः  horses killled, हतसारथिः  charioteer killed, सः he, गिरिशृङ्गाभम्  looking like a huge mountain top, रोमहर्षणम् causing horripilation, काञ्चनैः with golden, पट्टैः with bands, वेष्टितम् bound, देवसैन्यप्रमर्दनम् that which can crush even gods' army, तीक्ष्णैः sharp, आयसैः made of iron, शङ्कुभिः with nails, कीर्णम् covered, परवसोक्षितम्  sprinkled with enemy's marrow, वज्राशनिसमस्पर्शम् like the touch of thunderbolt, परगोपुरदारणम् that which can break open enemies fort, परिघम्  spear, जग्राह took up .

#### Explanation

With his bow and chariot broken, charioteer and horses killed,  Dusana took up a spear in his hand that appeared like a huge mountain top. Bound by golden bands, covered the iron nails, wetted with the enemy's marrow it created a horripilation. It appeared as though it  could crush the army of gods and render the enemy powerless. It carried the killing touch of the thunderbolt and could break open the enemy's fort.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

तं महोरगसङ्काशं प्रगृह्य परिघं रणे। दूषणोऽभ्यद्रवद्रामं क्रूरकर्मा निशाचरः।।3.26.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

क्रूरकर्मा cruel, निशाचरः demon, दूषणः Dusana, रणे in war, महोरगसङ्काशम् mighty serpent like, तं परिघम्  spear, प्रगृह्य holding, रामम् at Rama, अभ्यद्रवत् went forward.

#### Explanation

With the spear that looked like a mighty serpent, Dusana, the cruel demon, advanced towards Rama.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

तस्याभिपतमानस्य दूषणस्य स राघवः। द्वाभ्यां शराभ्यां चिच्छेद सहस्ताभरणौ भुजौ।।3.26.13।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः राघवः that Rama, अभिपतमानस्य  pouncing on him, तस्य दूषणस्य that Dusana, सहस्ताभरणौ adorned with armlets, भुजौ द्वाभ्याम् with two, शराभ्याम् with arrows, चिच्छेद cut.

#### Explanation

Seeing Dusana going to pounce upon him, Rama cut off with two arrows both his arms adorned with armlets.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

भ्रष्टस्तस्य महाकायः पपात रणमूर्धनि। परिघश्छिन्नहस्तस्य शक्रध्वज इवाग्रतः।।3.26.14।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रणमूर्धनि on the battle front, छिन्नहस्तस्य of one whose hands were cut off, तस्य his, महाकायः huge body, परिघः spear, भ्रष्टः fallen down, शक्रध्वजः इव like the banner of Indra, अग्रतः in front, पपात fell down.

#### Explanation

With the hands amputated, his huge spear, like the banner of Indra, slipped off his hands and fell down on the ground on the battle front.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

स कराभ्यां विकीर्णाभ्यां पपात भुवि दूषणः। विषाणाभ्यां विशीर्णाभ्यां मनस्वीव महागजः।।3.26.15।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः दूषणः  Dusana, विकीर्णाभ्याम्  scattered, कराभ्याम् with both hands, विशीर्णाभ्याम् shattered to pieces, विषाणाभ्याम् with two tusks, मनस्वी proud one, महागजः इव like a mighty elephant, भुवि ground, पपात fell down.

#### Explanation

Dusana fell down on the ground with broken hands like a proud elephant with both his tusks shattered.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

तं दृष्ट्वा पतितं भूमौ दूषणं निहतं रणे। साधुसाध्विति काकुत्स्थं सर्वभूतान्यपूजयन्।।3.26.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रणे in battle, निहतम् killed, भूमौ on the ground, पतितम् fallen down, तं दूषणम् that Dusana, दृष्ट्वा seeing, सर्वभूतानि all beings, काकुत्स्थम् Rama, साधु साधु इति saying, 'well done well done', अपूजयन् hailed.

#### Explanation

Seeing Dusaana collapse dead on the ground, all beings hailed Rama, saying, 'Well done, well done'.

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

एतस्मिन्नन्तरे क्रुद्धास्त्रयः सेनाग्रयायिनः। संहृत्याभ्यद्रवन् रामं मृत्युपाशावपाशिताः।।3.26.17।। महाकपालः स्थूलाक्षः प्रमाथी च महाबलः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

एतस्मिन् अन्तरे meanwhile, क्रुद्धाः angry  , महाकपालः Mahakapala, स्थूलाक्षः Stulaksha, महाबलः mighty, प्रमाथी च Pramathi, त्रयः all three, सेनाग्रयायिनः army chiefs, मृत्युपाशावपाशिताः  bound by the noose of death, संहृत्य all together, रामम् Rama, अभ्यद्रवन् ran towards.

#### Explanation

Meanwhile all the three army chiefs, Mahakapala, Sthulaksha, and the mighty Pramathi ran towards Rama in anger, bound (as though) by the noose of death.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

महाकपालो विपुलं शूलमुद्यम्य राक्षसः।।3.26.18।। स्थूलाक्षः पट्टिसं गृह्य प्रमाथी च परश्वधम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महाकपालः Mahakapala, विपुलम् huge, शूलम् spear, उद्यम्य  lifted up, स्थूलाक्षः Sthulaksha, राक्षसः demon, पट्टिसम् sharpedged spear, गृह्य holding, प्रमाथी च and Pramathi too, परश्वधं च with a battleaxe.

#### Explanation

Mahakapala had a huge spear, Sthulaksha, a sharpedged spear and Pramathi, a battleaxe.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

दृष्ट्वैवापततस्तूर्णं राघवस्सायकैश्शितैः।।3.26.19।। तीक्ष्णाग्रैः प्रतिजग्राह सम्प्राप्तानतिथीनिव।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राघवः Rama, तूर्णम् quickly, आपततः  attacking, दृष्ट्वैव on seeing, शितैः with sharp weapons, तीक्ष्णाग्रैः  pointed , सायकैः with arrows, सम्प्राप्तान् those who arrived, अतिथीनिव like guests, प्रतिजग्राह received.

#### Explanation

On seeing them rushing to attack him with sharp weapons, Rama received them quickly with pointed arrows as though they were guests.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

महाकपालस्य शिरश्चिच्छेद परमेषुभिः।।3.26.20।। असङ्ख्येयैस्तु बाणौघैः प्रममाथ प्रमाथिनम्। स पपात हतो भूमौ विटपीव महाद्रुमः।।3.26.21।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

परमेषुभिः sharp arrows, महाकपालस्य Mahakapala's, शिरः head, चिच्छेद cut to pieces, असङ्ख्येयैः with innumerable, बाणौघैः  arrows, प्रमाथिनम् Pramathi also, प्रममाथ killed, सः he, हतः dead, विटपी with branches, महाद्रुमः इव like a huge tree, भूमौ on the ground, पपात fell down.

#### Explanation

Mahakapala's head was cut to pieces with sharp arrows and Pramathi was killed with a countless series  of arrows. Pramathi's body, like a huge tree with many branches collapsed on the ground.

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

महाकपालस्य शिरश्चिच्छेद परमेषुभिः।।3.26.20।। असङ्ख्येयैस्तु बाणौघैः प्रममाथ प्रमाथिनम्। स पपात हतो भूमौ विटपीव महाद्रुमः।।3.26.21।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

परमेषुभिः sharp arrows, महाकपालस्य Mahakapala's, शिरः head, चिच्छेद cut to pieces, असङ्ख्येयैः with innumerable, बाणौघैः  arrows, प्रमाथिनम् Pramathi also, प्रममाथ killed, सः he, हतः dead, विटपी with branches, महाद्रुमः इव like a huge tree, भूमौ on the ground, पपात fell down.

#### Explanation

Mahakapala's head was cut to pieces with sharp arrows and Pramathi was killed with a countless series  of arrows. Pramathi's body, like a huge tree with many branches collapsed on the ground.

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

स्थूलाक्षस्याक्षिणी तीक्ष्णैः पूरयामास सायकैः। दूषणस्यानुगान्पञ्चसहस्रान्कुपितः क्षणात्।।3.26.22।। बाणौघैः पञ्चसाहस्रैरनयद्यमसादनम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

स्थूलाक्षस्य Sthulaksha's, अक्षिणी eyes, तीक्ष्णैः with sharp, सायकैः with arrows, पूरयामास filled, कुपितः an angry, पञ्चसाहस्रैः with five thousand, बाणौघैः with arrows, दूषणस्य Dusana's, पञ्चसाहस्रान् five thousand, अनुगान् followers, क्षणात् in a moment, यमसादनम् to the abode of Yama, अनयत् sent.

#### Explanation

Rama filled the eyes of Sthulaksha with sharp arrows, and in anger shot five thousand arrows at the five thousand followers of Dusana in a moment and sent them to the abode of Yama.

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

दूषणं निहतं दृष्ट्वा तस्य चैव पदानुगान्।।3.26.23।। व्यादिदेश खरः क्रुद्धः सेनाध्यक्षान्महाबलान्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

दूषणं Dusana, निहतम् killed, तस्य his, पदानुगान् followers, दृष्ट्वा after seeing, खरः Khara, क्रुद्धः angry , महाबलान् extremely strong, सेनाध्यक्षान् chiefs of the army, व्यादिदेश commanded.

#### Explanation

Seeing Dusana and his followers killed, enraged Khara commanded his extremely strong army chiefs this way:

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

अयं विनिहतस्संख्ये दूषणस्सपदानुगः।।3.26.24।। महत्या सेनया सार्धं युद्ध्वा रामं कुमानुषम्। शस्त्रैर्नानाविधाकारैर्हनध्वं सर्वराक्षसाः।।3.26.25।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अयं this one, सपदानुगः along with his followers, दूषणः Dooshana, सङ्ख्ये in battle, (वि)निहतः killed, सर्व राक्षसाः all the demons, महत्या with great, सेनयासार्धम् along with army, युद्ध्वा after fighting, नानाविधाकारैः different kinds, शस्त्रैः weapons, कुमानुषम् wicked human being, रामम् Rama, हनध्वम्  should kill.

#### Explanation

Dusana and his followers are killed in the battle. You, all the demons, along with your great army and different kinds of weapons should kill that wicked human being, Rama.

---

### Shloka 25

#### Shloka Text

अयं विनिहतस्संख्ये दूषणस्सपदानुगः।।3.26.24।। महत्या सेनया सार्धं युद्ध्वा रामं कुमानुषम्। शस्त्रैर्नानाविधाकारैर्हनध्वं सर्वराक्षसाः।।3.26.25।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अयं this one, सपदानुगः along with his followers, दूषणः Dooshana, सङ्ख्ये in battle, (वि)निहतः killed, सर्व राक्षसाः all the demons, महत्या with great, सेनयासार्धम् along with army, युद्ध्वा after fighting, नानाविधाकारैः different kinds, शस्त्रैः weapons, कुमानुषम् wicked human being, रामम् Rama, हनध्वम्  should kill.

#### Explanation

Dusana and his followers are killed in the battle. You, all the demons, along with your great army and different kinds of weapons should kill that wicked human being, Rama.

---

### Shloka 26

#### Shloka Text

एवमुक्त्वा खरः क्रुद्धो राममेवाभिदुद्रुवे। श्येनगामी पृथुग्रीवो यज्ञशत्रुर्विहङ्गमः।।3.26.26।। दुर्जयः करवीराक्षः परुषः कालकार्मुकः। मेघमाली महामाली सर्पास्यो रुधिराशनः।।3.26.27।। द्वादशैते महावीर्या बलाध्यक्षास्ससैनिकाः। राममेवाभ्यधावन्त विसृजन्तश्शरोत्तमान्।।3.26.28।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

खरः Khara, एवम् in that way, उक्त्वा having said, क्रुद्धः angry  one, राममेव towards Rama, अभिदुद्रुवे ran, श्येनगामी Syenagami, पृथुग्रीवः Pruthugriva, यज्ञशत्रुः Yajnasatru, विहङ्गमः Vihangama, दुर्जयः Durjaya, करवीराक्षः Karaveeraksha, परुषः Parusha, कालकार्मुकः Kalakarmuka, मेघमाली Meghamali, महामाली Mahamali, सर्पास्य: Sarpasya, रुधिराशनः Rudhirasana, एते these, महावीर्याः mighty heroes, द्वादश twelve, बलाध्यक्षाः army chiefs, ससैनिकाः with army, शरोत्तमान् sharpest of arrows, विसृजन्तः releasing, राममेव towards Rama  alone, अभ्यधावन्त ran forward.

#### Explanation

Having spoken thus, angry Khara ran towards Rama. Syenagami, Pruthugriva, Yajnasatru, Vihanga, Durjaya, Karaveeraksha, Parusha, Kalakarmuka, Meghamali, Mahamali, Sarpasya, Rudhirasanaall these twelve valiant army chiefs along with their forces rushed towards Rama, releasing the sharpest arrows at him.

---

### Shloka 27

#### Shloka Text

एवमुक्त्वा खरः क्रुद्धो राममेवाभिदुद्रुवे। श्येनगामी पृथुग्रीवो यज्ञशत्रुर्विहङ्गमः।।3.26.26।। दुर्जयः करवीराक्षः परुषः कालकार्मुकः। मेघमाली महामाली सर्पास्यो रुधिराशनः।।3.26.27।। द्वादशैते महावीर्या बलाध्यक्षास्ससैनिकाः। राममेवाभ्यधावन्त विसृजन्तश्शरोत्तमान्।।3.26.28।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

खरः Khara, एवम् in that way, उक्त्वा having said, क्रुद्धः angry  one, राममेव towards Rama, अभिदुद्रुवे ran, श्येनगामी Syenagami, पृथुग्रीवः Pruthugriva, यज्ञशत्रुः Yajnasatru, विहङ्गमः Vihangama, दुर्जयः Durjaya, करवीराक्षः Karaveeraksha, परुषः Parusha, कालकार्मुकः Kalakarmuka, मेघमाली Meghamali, महामाली Mahamali, सर्पास्य: Sarpasya, रुधिराशनः Rudhirasana, एते these, महावीर्याः mighty heroes, द्वादश twelve, बलाध्यक्षाः army chiefs, ससैनिकाः with army, शरोत्तमान् sharpest of arrows, विसृजन्तः releasing, राममेव towards Rama  alone, अभ्यधावन्त ran forward.

#### Explanation

Having spoken thus, angry Khara ran towards Rama. Syenagami, Pruthugriva, Yajnasatru, Vihanga, Durjaya, Karaveeraksha, Parusha, Kalakarmuka, Meghamali, Mahamali, Sarpasya, Rudhirasanaall these twelve valiant army chiefs along with their forces rushed towards Rama, releasing the sharpest arrows at him.

---

### Shloka 28

#### Shloka Text

एवमुक्त्वा खरः क्रुद्धो राममेवाभिदुद्रुवे। श्येनगामी पृथुग्रीवो यज्ञशत्रुर्विहङ्गमः।।3.26.26।। दुर्जयः करवीराक्षः परुषः कालकार्मुकः। मेघमाली महामाली सर्पास्यो रुधिराशनः।।3.26.27।। द्वादशैते महावीर्या बलाध्यक्षास्ससैनिकाः। राममेवाभ्यधावन्त विसृजन्तश्शरोत्तमान्।।3.26.28।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

खरः Khara, एवम् in that way, उक्त्वा having said, क्रुद्धः angry  one, राममेव towards Rama, अभिदुद्रुवे ran, श्येनगामी Syenagami, पृथुग्रीवः Pruthugriva, यज्ञशत्रुः Yajnasatru, विहङ्गमः Vihangama, दुर्जयः Durjaya, करवीराक्षः Karaveeraksha, परुषः Parusha, कालकार्मुकः Kalakarmuka, मेघमाली Meghamali, महामाली Mahamali, सर्पास्य: Sarpasya, रुधिराशनः Rudhirasana, एते these, महावीर्याः mighty heroes, द्वादश twelve, बलाध्यक्षाः army chiefs, ससैनिकाः with army, शरोत्तमान् sharpest of arrows, विसृजन्तः releasing, राममेव towards Rama  alone, अभ्यधावन्त ran forward.

#### Explanation

Having spoken thus, angry Khara ran towards Rama. Syenagami, Pruthugriva, Yajnasatru, Vihanga, Durjaya, Karaveeraksha, Parusha, Kalakarmuka, Meghamali, Mahamali, Sarpasya, Rudhirasanaall these twelve valiant army chiefs along with their forces rushed towards Rama, releasing the sharpest arrows at him.

---

### Shloka 29

#### Shloka Text

ततः पावकसङ्काशैर्हेमवज्रविभूषितैः। जघान शेषं तेजस्वी तस्य सैन्यस्य सायकैः।।3.26.29।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तेजस्वी powerful Rama, ततः then, पावकसङ्काशैः  glowing like fire, हेमवज्रविभूषितैः studded with gold and diamonds, सायकैः arrows, तस्य his, सैन्यस्य army's, शेषम् remaining, जघान slayed.

#### Explanation

Powerful Rama thereafter slayed the remaining army with his weapons studded with gold and diamonds and glowing like fire.

---

### Shloka 30

#### Shloka Text

ते रुक्मपुङ्खा विशिखास्सधूमा इव पावकाः। निजघ्नुस्तानि रक्षांसि वज्रा इव महाद्रुमान्।।3.26.30।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रुक्मपुङ्खाः goldtipped arrows, सधूमाः covered with smoke, पावकाः इव like fire, ते विशिखाः tips of arrows, वज्राः thunderbolts, महाद्रुमानिव like huge trees, तानि those, रक्षांसि demons, निजघ्नुः shattered.

#### Explanation

The goldtipped arrows which resembled fire covered with smoke shattered the army of demons like huge trees knocked down by thunder.

---

### Shloka 31

#### Shloka Text

रक्षसां तु शतं रामश्शतेनैकेन कर्णिना। सहस्रं च सहस्रेण जघान रणमूर्धनि।।3.26.31।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रामः Rama, रक्षसाम् of demons, शतम् one hundred, एकेन कर्णिना by a single stroke, शतेन one hundred, सहस्रम् thousand, सहस्रेण thousand, रणमूर्धनि on the battle front, जघान killed.

#### Explanation

Rama killed a hundred demons  with one hundred arrows by a single stroke. The same way he killed a thousand of them with a thousand arrows on the battle front.

---

### Shloka 32

#### Shloka Text

तैर्भिन्नवर्माभरणाश्छिन्नभिन्नशरासनाः। निपेतुश्शोणितादिग्धा धरण्यां रजनीचराः।।3.26.32।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तैः by them, भिन्नवर्माभरणाः  with broken shields and ornaments, छिन्नभिन्नशरासनाः the bows broken to pieces, रजनीचराः demons, शोणिता दिग्धाः smeared with blood, धरण्याम् on the ground, निपेतुः fell.

#### Explanation

The demons fell down on the ground with their shields  and ornaments broken, their  bows stained with blood splintered.

---

### Shloka 33

#### Shloka Text

तैर्मुक्तकेशैस्समरे पतितैश्शोणितोक्षितैः। आस्तीर्णा वसुधा कृत्स्ना महावेदिः कुशैरिव।।3.26.33।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

समरे in the battle, पतितैः by those fallen down, मुक्तकेशैः loose hair, शोणितोक्षितैः by those wet with blood, तैः those, कृत्स्ना entire, वसुधा ground, महावेदिः a great sacrificial altar, कुशैरिव like the Kusa grass, आस्तीर्णा was spread all over.

#### Explanation

The entire ground was littered with bodies of demons fallen in the battle with loose hair and with drops of blood on them looking like a great sacrificial altar strewn all over with kusa grass.

---

### Shloka 34

#### Shloka Text

क्षणेन तु महाघोरं वनं निहतराक्षसम्। बभूव निरयप्रख्यं मांसशोणितकर्दमम्।।3.26.34।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

निहतराक्षसम् with demons killed, वनम् the forest, क्षणेन तु in a moment, महाघोरम् very dreadful, मांसशोणितकर्दमम् soil soaked with the mud of flesh and blood, निरयप्रख्य like a hell, बभूव appeared.

#### Explanation

With the slaughtered bodies of demons lying on the ground, the forest in a moment looked very dreadful like hell with the mud of soil soaked with flesh and blood.

---

### Shloka 35

#### Shloka Text

चतुर्दश सहस्राणि रक्षसां भीमकर्मणाम्। हतान्येकेन रामेण मानुषेण पदातिना।।3.26.35।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मानुषेण by a human being, पदातिना going on foot, रामेण Rama, एकेन alone, भीमकर्मणाम्  of formidable  acts, रक्षसाम् demons, चतुर्दश fourteen, सहस्राणि thousands, हतानि were killed.

#### Explanation

Fourteen thousand formidable demons were killed by Rama, a pedestrian human,  singlehanded.

---

### Shloka 36

#### Shloka Text

तस्य सैन्यस्य सर्वस्य खरश्शेषो महारथः। राक्षसस्त्रिशिराश्चैव रामश्च रिपुसूदनः।।3.26.36।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महारथः great warrior, खरः Khara, राक्षसः demon, त्रिशिराः Trisira, तस्य सैन्सस्य सर्वस्य of that entire army, शेषः were remanant, रिपुसूदनः destroyer of foes, रामश्च Rama also.

#### Explanation

Of that entire army great warrior Khara,Trisira and Rama, destroyer of foes alone remained.

---

### Shloka 37

#### Shloka Text

शेषा हता महासत्वा राक्षसा रणमूर्धनि। घोरा दुर्विषहाः सर्वे लक्ष्मणस्याग्रजेन ते।।3.26.37।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महासत्त्वाः mighty, घोराः dreadful, दुर्विषहाः intolerable, सर्वे all, शेषाः the remaining, ते they, लक्ष्मणस्य Lakshmana's, अग्रजेन by the elder brother, रणमूर्धनि on the battle front, हताः were killed.

#### Explanation

The rest of the mighty, dreadful, intolerable demons were killed on the war front by Rama, elder brother to Lakshmana.

---

### Shloka 38

#### Shloka Text

ततस्तु तद्भीमबलं महाहवे समीक्ष्य रामेण हतं बलीयसा। रथेन रामं महता खरस्तदा समाससादेन्द्र इवोद्यताशनिः।।3.26.38।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः thereafter, खरः Khara, महाहवे in the great fight, बलीयसा by the mighty, रामेण by Rama, हतम् killed, तत् that, भीमबलम् formidable army, समीक्ष्य  seeing, तदा then, उद्यताशनिः  raised thunderbolt, इन्द्र इव like Indra, महता with a great, रथेन chariot, रामम् Rama, समाससाद went forward.

#### Explanation

Thereafter seeing the formidable army killed by mighty Rama, Khara lifted a thunderbolt like Indra, and advanced towards Rama in a great chariot. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वालमिकिय आदिकाव्ये अरण्यकाण्डे षङ्विंशस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the twentysixth sarga in Aranyakanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 27

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

खरं तु रामाभिमुखं प्रयान्तं वाहिनीपतिः। राक्षसस्त्रिशिरा नाम सन्निपत्येदमब्रवीत्।।3.27.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रामाभिमुखम् approaching Rama, प्रयान्तम् marching towards, खरम् Khara, वाहिनीपतिः chief of army, त्रिशिराः Trisira, नाम by name, राक्षसः demon, सन्निपत्य going near, इदम् these words, अब्रवीत् spoke.

#### Explanation

As Khara advanced towards Rama, Trisira the chief of the army approached him and said these words:

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

मां नियोजय विक्रान्त सन्निवर्तस्व साहसात्। पश्य रामं महाबाहुं संयुगे विनिपातितम्।।3.27.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

विक्रान्तः valiant Khara, माम् me, नियोजय assign, साहसात् audacity, निवर्तस्व put down, संयुगे in the combat, विनिपातितम् thrown down, महाबाहुम् mightyarmed , रामम् Rama, पश्य you can see.

#### Explanation

O valiant one assign this work to me. Refrain from this audacity. See how I am going to put down the mightyarmed Rama in the battle.

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

प्रतिजानामि ते सत्यमायुधं चाहमालभे। यथा रामं वधिष्यामि वधार्हं सर्वरक्षसाम्।।3.27.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अहम् I, ते to you, सत्यम् truth, प्रतिजानामि I promise, आयुधं च on my weapon, आलभे I am touching, यथा as, सर्वरक्षसाम् of all demons, वधार्हम् should be slain, रामम् Rama, वधिष्यामि I will kill.

#### Explanation

I promise you on my weapon that I will kill Rama.He who killed all demons deserves to be slain.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

अहं वास्य रणे मृत्युरेष वा समरे मम। विनिवृत्य रणोत्साहान्मुहूर्तं प्राश्निको भव।।3.27.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रणोत्साहात् from the enthusiasm to fight, विनिवृत्य  restraining, रणे in fight, अहम् I, अस्य his, मृत्युः death, वा or, समरे in war, एषः this person, मम mine, मुहूर्तम् for a moment, प्राश्निकः a judge भव become.

#### Explanation

Restrain yourself for a moment from the enthusiasm to fight the war. Please act as an arbitrator and see who is killing whom.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

प्रहृष्टो वा हते रामे जनस्थानं प्रयास्यसि। मयि वा निहते रामं संयुगायोपयास्यसि।।3.27.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रामे when Rama, हते is killed, प्रहृष्टः with joy, जनस्थानम् to Janasthanam, प्रयास्यसि वा you will go or else, मयि वा when myself, निहते am killed, संयुगाय to the fight, रामम् Rama, उपयास्यसि वा you can fight.

#### Explanation

If Rama is killed be happy and go to Janasthana or else if I am killed you can fight.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

खरस्त्रिशिरसा तेन मृत्युलोभात्प्रसादितः। गच्छ युध्येत्यनुज्ञातो राघवाभिमुखो ययौ।।3.27.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

खरः Khara, तेन by him, त्रिशिरसा by Trisira, मृत्युलोभात् due to eagerness to face death, प्रसादितः persuaded, गच्छ you may go, युध्य you may fight, इति thus, अनुज्ञातः permitted, राघवाभिमुखः forward to Rama, ययौ he went.

#### Explanation

When Khara was prevailed upon by Trisara in that manner with a wish to face death, Khara  permitted him  by saying 'You may go and fight'.Accordingly he advanced towards Rama.

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

त्रिशिराश्च रथेनैव वाजियुक्तेन भास्वता। अभ्यद्रवद्रणे रामं त्रिशृङ्ग इव पर्वतः।।3.27.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

त्रिशिराश्च Trisira also, वाजियुक्तेन  horses harnessed, भास्वता shining, रथेनैव in a chariot, त्रिशृङ्गः three peaked Trishringa, पर्वतः इव mountain like, रणे in war, रामम् Rama, अभ्यद्रवत् attacked.

#### Explanation

Trisira who looked a mountain with three peaks also got horses harnessed to a shining chariot and to attack Rama proceeded.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

शरधारासमूहान्स महामेघ इवोत्सृजन्। व्यसृजत्सदृशं नादं जलार्द्रस्य तु दुन्दुभेः।।3.27.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः that Trisira, महामेघ इव like a huge cloud, शरधारासमूहान् streams of arrows, उत्सृजन्  unleashing, जलार्द्रस्य drenched with water, दुन्दुभेः of the drum, सदृशम् similar, नादम् sound, व्यसृजत् released.

#### Explanation

Trisira unleashed a stream of arrows like a huge cloud (releasing rain). They produced sound, a drum drenched with water, creates.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

आगच्छन्तं त्रिशिरसं राक्षसं प्रेक्ष्य राघवः। धनुषा प्रतिजग्राह विधुन्वन्सायकान् शितान्।।3.27.9।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राघवः Rama, आगच्छन्तम् advancing towards him, राक्षसम् the demon, त्रिशिरसम् Trisira, प्रेक्षय observing, शितान् sharp, सायकान् arrow, धनुषा setting the bow, विधुन्वन् shaking, प्रतिजग्राह retaliated.

#### Explanation

On seeing demon Trisira advancing towards him, Rama shook sharp arrows off his bow.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

स सम्प्रहारस्तुमुलो रामत्रिशिरसोर्महान्। बभूवातीव बलिनोस्सिंहकुञ्जरयोरिव।।3.27.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रामत्रिशिरसोः between Rama and Trisira, तुमुलः tumultuous, महान् great, सः सम्प्रहारः that fight, अतीव very, बलिनोः strong ones, सिंहकुञ्जरयोः इव as between a lion and an elephant, बभूव took place.

#### Explanation

The tumultuous combat between Rama and Trisira looked like the strong fight between a lion and an elephant.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

ततस्त्रिशिरसा बाणैर्ललाटे ताडितस्त्रिभिः। अमर्षी कुपितोरामस्संरब्धमिदमब्रवीत्।।3.27.11।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, अमर्षी intolerant, रामः Rama, त्रिशिरसा by Trisira, त्रिभिः with three, बाणैः arrows, ललाटे on the forehead, ताडितः was hit, कुपितः a furious one, संरब्धम् very agitated, इदम् this way, अब्रवीत् he said.

#### Explanation

Then Rama hit  with three arrows on the forehead by Trisira flew into a fury and said in a state of great excitement:

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

अहो विक्रमशूरस्य राक्षसस्येदृशं बलम्। पुष्पैरिव शरैर्यस्य ललाटेऽस्मिन्परिक्षतः।।3.27.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अहो Oh, विक्रमशूरस्य of an undaunted hero, राक्षसस्य of the demon, बलम् strength, ईदृशम् this amount, यस्य his, शरैः with arrows, पुष्पैरिव with flowers, अस्मिन् ललाटे this forehead, परिक्षतः scratched.

#### Explanation

Oh see the strength of this undaunted demon whose arrows fell like flowers on my forehead and only left a scratch.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

ममापि प्रतिगृह्णीष्व शरांश्चापगुणाच्च्युतान्। एवमुक्त्वा तु संरब्धश्शरानाशीविषोपमान्।3.27.13।। त्रिशिरोवक्षसि क्रुद्धो निजघान चतुर्दश।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मम my, चापगुणातच्च्युतान् released from the string of the bow, शरानपि arrows too, प्रतिगृह्णीष्व you may receive, एवम् in that manner, उक्त्वा having said, संरब्धः agitated, क्रुद्धः angry, अशीविषोपमान् like the poison of a venomous snake, चतुर्दश fourteen, शरान् arrows, त्रिशिरोवक्षसि on the chest of Trisira, निजघान hit.

#### Explanation

Receive the arrows released from my bowstring. Saying so, the angry and agitated Rama discharged fourteen arrows, venomous like a snake, on the chest of Trisira .

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

चतुर्भिस्तुरगानस्य शरैः सन्नतपर्वभिः।।3.27.14।। न्यपातयत तेजस्वी चतुरस्तस्य वाजिनः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तेजस्वी mighty man, सन्नतपर्वभिः with those having strong joints, चतुर्भिः with four, शरैः with arrows, तस्य his, अस्य his (Rama's), तुरगान् swift, चतुरः four, वाजिनः horses, न्यपातयत threw down.

#### Explanation

With four arrows having strong joints, the mighty hero (Rama) threw down his enemy's four swiftfooted horses.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

अष्टभिस्सायकैस्सूतं रथोपस्थान्न्यपातयत्।।3.27.15।। रामश्चिच्छेद बाणेन ध्वजं चास्य समुच्छ्रितम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रामः Rama, अष्टभिः with eight, सायकैः arrows, सूतम् charioteer, रथोपस्थात् from the top of the chariot, न्यपातयत् brought down, बाणेन by one dart, अस्य his, समुच्छ्रितम् very lofty, ध्वजम् banner, चिच्छेद shattered to pieces.

#### Explanation

Rama brought down the charioteer with eight arrows and with one tore down the lofty banner of the chariot to shreds.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

ततो हतरथात्तस्मादुत्पतन्तं निशाचरम्।।3.27.16।। बिभेद रामस्तं बाणैर्हृदये सोऽभवज्जडः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, रामः Rama, तस्मात् from that, हतरथात् from a broken chariot, उत्पतन्तम् one who was running away, तं निशाचरम् that demon, बाणैः with arrows, हृदये in the chest, बिभेद hit, सः he, जडः a numb man, अभवत् became.

#### Explanation

Rama then hit the demon with arrows on his chest while he was running away from the broken chariot. The demon became numb.

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

सायकैश्चाप्रमेयात्मा सामर्षस्तस्य रक्षसः।।3.27.17।। शिरांस्यपातयद्रामो वेगवद्भिस्त्रिभिश्शितैः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अप्रमेयात्मा a man of boundless strength, रामः Rama, सामर्षः an enraged one, वेगवद्भि: swift, शितैः with sharp ones, त्रिभिः three, सायकैः with arrows, तस्य रक्षसः that demon's, शिरांसि head, अपातयत् cut down.

#### Explanation

The mighty Rama in fury dismembered the heads of that demon with three sharp and swift arrows.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

स भूमौ रुधिरोद्गारी रामबाणाभिपीडितः।।3.27.18।। न्यपतत्पतितैः पूर्वं स्वशिरोभिर्निशाचरः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रामबाणाभिपीडितः hit by the arrows of Rama, सः निशाचरः that demon, रुधिरोद्गारी vomitting blood, पूर्वम् a little while ago, पतितैः fallen down, स्वशिरोभिः with his heads, न्यपतत् fell down.

#### Explanation

The demon with his heads decapitated, hit by Rama's arrows a little while ago, fell down, vomitting blood.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

हतशेषास्ततो भग्ना राक्षसाः खरसंश्रया।।3.27.19।। द्रवन्ति स्म न तिष्ठन्ति व्याघ्रत्रस्ता मृगा इव।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, खरसंश्रया under the protection of Khara, हतशेषाः surviving, राक्षसाः demons, भग्नाः with broken hearts, व्याघ्रतस्ताः those scared of  tiger, मृगाः यथा like deer, द्रवन्ति स्म fast retreated, न तिष्ठन्ति did not stop.

#### Explanation

Those surviving demons who were under the protection of Khara got scared and retreated fast and fled like deer frightened by a tiger.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

तान्खरो द्रवतो दृष्ट्वा निवर्त्य रुषितस्स्वयम्।।3.27.20।। राममेवाभिदुद्राव राहुश्चन्द्रमसं यथा।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रुषितः angry, खरः Khara, द्रवतः retreating, तान् them, दृष्ट्वा on seeing, स्वयम् personally, निवर्त्य turning them back, राहुः Rahu, चन्द्रमसं यथा as towards Moon राममेव towards Rama alone, अभिदुद्राव ran after.

#### Explanation

Personally persuading the fleeing army to come back, the angry Khara leaped forward towards Rama just as Rahu approaches the moon. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये अरण्यकाण्डे सप्तविंशस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the twentyseventh sarga of Aranyakanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 28

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

निहतं दूषणं दृष्ट्वा रणे त्रिशिरसा सह। खरस्याप्यभवत्त्रासो दृष्ट्वा रामस्य विक्रमम्।।3.28.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रणे in combat, त्रिशिरसा सह with Trisira, निहतम्  killed, दूषणम् Dusana, दृष्ट्वा  seeing, रामस्य Rama's, विक्रमम् prowess, दृष्ट्वा  seeing, खरस्यापि to Khara also, त्रासः fear, अभवत् overcame.

#### Explanation

Khara was overtaken by fear at the prowess of Rama, when he saw Dusana and Trisira killed.

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

स दृष्ट्वा राक्षसं सैन्यमविसह्यं महाबलः। हतमेकेन रामेण त्रिशिरोदूषणावपि।।3.28.2।। तद्बलं हतभूयिष्ठं विमनाः प्रेक्ष्य राक्षसः। आससाद खरो रामं नमुचिर्वासवं यथा।।3.28.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महाबलः extremely strong, राक्षसः demon, खरः Khara, अविसह्यम् irrepressible, राक्षसम् demon, सैन्यम् army, एकेन  alone, रामेण by Rama, हतम् killed, त्रिशिरोदूषणावपि Trisira and Dusana also, दृष्ट्वा  seeing, हतभूयिष्टम् very many killed, तत् बलम् the strength, विमनाः nervous प्रेक्ष्य witnessing, नमुचिः Namuchi, वासवं यथा similar to Indra, रामम् Rama, आससाद went towards.

#### Explanation

Khara, the mighty demon, saw his large, irrepressible army killed. He saw Trisira and Dusana slain singlehanded by Rama. Nervous, he advanced towards Rama wage war just as Namuchi approached Indra.

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

स दृष्ट्वा राक्षसं सैन्यमविसह्यं महाबलः। हतमेकेन रामेण त्रिशिरोदूषणावपि।।3.28.2।। तद्बलं हतभूयिष्ठं विमनाः प्रेक्ष्य राक्षसः। आससाद खरो रामं नमुचिर्वासवं यथा।।3.28.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महाबलः extremely strong, राक्षसः demon, खरः Khara, अविसह्यम् irrepressible, राक्षसम् demon, सैन्यम् army, एकेन  alone, रामेण by Rama, हतम् killed, त्रिशिरोदूषणावपि Trisira and Dusana also, दृष्ट्वा  seeing, हतभूयिष्टम् very many killed, तत् बलम् the strength, विमनाः nervous प्रेक्ष्य witnessing, नमुचिः Namuchi, वासवं यथा similar to Indra, रामम् Rama, आससाद went towards.

#### Explanation

Khara, the mighty demon, saw his large, irrepressible army killed. He saw Trisira and Dusana slain singlehanded by Rama. Nervous, he advanced towards Rama wage war just as Namuchi approached Indra.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

विकृष्य बलवच्चापं नाराचान्रक्तभोजनान्। खरश्चिक्षेप रामाय क्रुद्धानाशीविषानिव।।3.28.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

खरः Khara, बलवत् strong, चापम् bowstring, विकृष्य drawing, क्रुद्धान् angry, आशीविषानिव like venom of a poisonous serpent, रक्तभोजनान् whose food is blood, नाराचान् arrows, रामाय at Rama, चिक्षेप sent forth.

#### Explanation

Khara drew his strong bowstring in anger and released the bloodhungry arrows, which appeared like venomous snakes.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

ज्यां विधून्वंसुबहुशश्शिक्षयास्त्राणि दर्शयन्। चचार समरे मार्गाञ्छरै रथगतः खरः।।3.28.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रथगतः sittting on the chariot, खरः Khara, सुबहुशः in many ways, ज्याम् bowstring, विधून्वन् while making a twang, शिक्षया with training, अस्त्राणि weapons, दर्शयन् while exhibiting, समरे in war, शरैः arrows, मार्गाणि in all directions, चचार released.

#### Explanation

Khara, sat on the chariot, and with a twang of his bowstring released arrows in all directions, exhibiting his skill in the use of weapons.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

स सर्वाश्च दिशो बाणैः प्रदिशश्च महारथः। पूरयामास तं दृष्ट्वा रामोऽपि सुमहद्धनुः।।3.28.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महारथः great warrior, सः he, सर्वाः all, दिशः sides, प्रदिशश्च intermediate directions, बाणैः arrows, पूरयामास filled, तम् him, दृष्ट्वा saw, रामोऽपि even Rama, सुमहत् powerful, धनुः bow, पूरयामास started filling.

#### Explanation

When Rama, the great warrior, saw Khara approaching him, he fixed powerful arrows  on his bow and covered all directions and intermediate quarters with his arrows.

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

स सायकैर्दुर्विसहैस्सस्फुलिङ्गैरिवाग्निभिः। नभश्चकाराविवरं पर्जन्य इव वृष्टिभिः।।3.28.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः he, दुर्विसहैः by irresistible, सस्फुलिङ्गैः with sparkling, अग्निभिः इव like fire, सायकैः with arrows, पर्जन्यः cloud, वृष्टिभिः इव with showers like, नभः the sky, अविवरम् not leaving any space, चकार filled.

#### Explanation

He rained sparkling arrows which were irresistible. He filled the sky with arrows just as the raingod fills it with rain without leaving any space.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

तद्बभूव शितैर्बाणैः खररामविसर्जितैः। पर्याकाशमनाकाशं सर्वतश्शरसङ्कुलम्।।3.28.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

खररामविसर्जितैः  released by Khara and Rama, शितैः by sharp, बाणैः with arrows, तत् that, पर्याकाशम्  surrounding sky, सर्वतः all over, शरसङ्कुलम् filled with arrows, अनाकाशम् as though there is no sky, बभूव appeared.

#### Explanation

The arrows released by Khara and Rama on one another filled the entire sky. Everywhere only arrows and no sky could be seen.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

शरजालावृतस्सूर्यो न तदा स्म प्रकाशते। अन्योन्यवधसंरम्भादुभयोस्संप्रयुध्यतोः।।3.28.9।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अन्योन्यवधसंरम्भात् eager to kill each other, उभयोः both, सम्प्रयुध्यतोः of those waging (fighting), तदा then, शरजालावृतः covered with arrows, सूर्यः Sun, न प्रकाशते स्म not seen.

#### Explanation

While they were engaged in the battle, eager to kill each other the sky was covered with arrow and the Sun could not be seen.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

ततो नालीकनाराचैस्तीक्ष्णाग्रैश्च विकर्णिभिः। आजघान खरो रामं तोत्रैरिव महाद्विपम्।।3.28.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, खरः Khara, नालीकनाराचैः by Nalika darts, तीक्ष्णाग्रैः pointed, विकर्णिभिः with darts called Vikarni, रामम् Rama, तोत्रैः with goads, महाद्विपमिव like a mighty elephant, आजघान shot.

#### Explanation

Then Khara shot Rama with Nalika darts and darts called Vikarni like goads hit a mighty elephant.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

तं रथस्थं धनुष्पाणिं राक्षसं पर्यवस्थितम्। ददृशुस्सर्वभूतानि पाशहस्तमिवान्तकम्।।3.28.11।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रथस्थम् on the chariot, धनुष्पाणिम् holding the bow in hand, पर्यवस्थितम् seated, तं राक्षसम् that demon, पाशहस्तम् holding the noose, अन्तकम् इव like the god of death (Yama), सर्वभूतानि all beings, ददृशुः observed.

#### Explanation

Demon Khara positioned on the chariot, bow in hand, appeared to all beings like Yama, god of death holding the noose.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

हन्तारं सर्वसैन्यस्य पौरुषे पर्यवस्थितम्। परिश्रान्तं महासत्वं मेने रामं खरस्तदा।।3.28.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

खरः Khara, सर्वसैन्यस्य of the whole army, हन्तारम् destroyer, पौरुषे in valour, पर्यवस्थितम् staying, महासत्त्वम् a very strong one, रामम् Rama, तदा then, परिश्रान्तम् exhausted, मेने thought.

#### Explanation

Khara thought valiant Rama who had destroyed his whole army might be exhausted by now.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

तं सिंहमिव विक्रान्तं सिंहविक्रान्तगामिनम्। दृष्ट्वा नोद्विजते रामः सिंहः क्षुद्रमृगं यथा।।3.28.13।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation
