सिंहमिव like a lion, विक्रान्तम् aggressive, सिंह विक्रान्तगामिनम् advancing to attack, तम् him, दृष्ट्वा seeing, रामः Rama, सिंहः lion, क्षुद्रमृगं यथा just like a fawn, नोद्विजते not perturbed.

#### Explanation

When Rama saw Khara aggressively advancing like a lion to attack him, he was not perturbed, for he knew he was a fawn before a lion (which Rama was).

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

ततः सूर्यनिकाशेन रथेन महता खरः। आससाद रणे रामं पतङ्ग इव पावकम्।।3.28.14।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, खरः Khara, सूर्यनिकाशेन glowing like the Sun, महता great, रथेन on a chariot, रामम् to Rama, पतङ्ग insect, पावकम् इव like going towards fire, रणे war, आससाद went forward.

#### Explanation

Then Khara mounting a great chariot which was glowing like the Sun, advanced towards Rama just as an insect hurtles towards fire.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

ततोऽस्य सशरं चापं मुष्टिदेशे महात्मनः। खरश्चिच्छेद रामस्य दर्शयन्पाणिलाघवम्।।3.28.15।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, खरः Khara, पाणिलाघवम् agility of the hand, दर्शयन् exhibiting, महात्मनः of the great self, अस्य रामस्य Rama's, सशरम् along with the arrow, चापम् bow, मुष्टिदेशे at the grip, चिच्छेद shattered.

#### Explanation

Exhibiting his agility of the hand, Khara shattered the bow and the arrow in the grip of Rama ,the  great soul.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

स पुनस्त्वपरान्सप्त शरानादाय वर्मणि। निजघान खरः क्रुद्धश्शक्राशनिसमप्रभान्।।3.28.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः that, खरः Khara, क्रुद्धः angry, पुनः again, शक्राशनिसमप्रभान् blazing like Indra's thunderbolt, अपरान् others, सप्त seven, शरान् arrows, आदाय taking up, वर्मणि at the  armour, निजघान hit.

#### Explanation

The influriated Khara this time took up seven arrows blazing like Indra's thunderbolt  and hit his armour.

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

ततस्तत्प्रहतं बाणैः खरमुक्तैस्सुपर्वभिः। पपात कवचं भूमौ रामस्यादित्यवर्चसः।।3.28.17।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः thereafter, खरमुक्तैः shot by Khara, सुपर्वभिः by the welljointed, बाणैः with darts, प्रहतम् struck, आदित्यवर्चसः a man radiant like the Sun, रामस्य Rama's, तत् that, कवचम् armour, भूमौ on the ground, पपात fell down.

#### Explanation

The armour of Rama who looked radiant like the Sun fell on the ground, struck by the welljointed darts released by Khara.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

ततश्शरसहस्रेण राममप्रतिमौजसम्। अर्दयित्वा महानादं ननाद समरे खरः।।3.28.18।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, खरः Khara, अप्रतिमौजसम् of unequal strength, रामम् to Rama, शरसहस्रेण  with a thousand darts, अर्दयित्वा attacking, समरे in war, महानादम् great sound, ननाद produced.

#### Explanation

Khara then attacked Rama of unequal strength in fight, with a thousand darts  and roared loudly.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

स शरैरर्दितः क्रुद्धस्सर्वगात्रेषु राघवः। रराज समरे रामो विधूमोऽग्निरिव ज्वलन्।।3.28.19।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

शरैः with darts, सर्वगात्रेषु in all limbs, अर्दितः afflicted, राघवः a scion of Raghu race, सः रामः that Rama, क्रुद्धः angry, विधूमः without smoke, ज्वलन् while burning, अग्निरिव like fire, समरे in fight, रराज glowed.

#### Explanation

Rama, scion of the Raghu race, afflicted by the darts in all parts of the body, glowed in his anger like smokeless fire burning.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

ततो गम्भीरनिर्ह्रादं रामश्शत्रुनिबर्हणः। चकारान्ताय स रिपोस्सज्यमन्यन्महद्धनुः।।3.28.20।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, शत्रुनिबर्हणः destroyer of foes, रामः Rama, रिपोः enemy, अन्ताय to put an end to, गम्भीरनिर्ह्रादम् deep sound, अन्यत् another one, महत् great, धनुः bow, सज्यम् string, चकार  did.

#### Explanation

Then Rama, destroyer of foes, strung another great bow of fearful sound in order to put an end to the enemy.

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

सुमहद्वैष्णवं यत्तदतिसृष्टं महर्षिणा। वरं तद्धनुरुद्यम्य खरं समभिधावत।।3.28.21।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तत् thereafter, यत् that, सुमहत् very powerful, वैष्णवम् Vaishnava, धनुः bow, महर्षिणा by the sage, अतिसृष्टम् given, वरम् best, तत् that, उद्यम्य aiming, खरम् Khara, समभिधावत advanced.

#### Explanation

Thereafter Rama advanced, aiming at Khara the superior Vaishnava bow given by sage Agastya.

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

ततः कनकपुङ्खैस्तु शरैस्सन्नतपर्वभिः। बिभेद रामस्सङ्क्रुद्धः खरस्य समरे ध्वजम्।।3.28.22।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, रामः Rama, सङ्क्रुद्धः enraged, कनकपुङ्खैः with golden feathers, सन्नतपर्वभिः welljointed and smooth, शरैः darts, समरे in war, खरस्य of Khara, ध्वजम् flag on the chariot, बिभेद broken to pieces.

#### Explanation

Rama took up in a rage the welljointed, smooth darts with golden feathers and broke the flag post of the chariot of Khara to pieces.

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

स दर्शनीयो बहुधा विकीर्णः काञ्चनध्वजः। जगाम धरणीं सूर्यो देवतानामिवाज्ञया।।3.28.23।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

बहुधा many ways, विकीर्णः broken, दर्शनीयः a beautiful one, सः that, काञ्चनध्वजः golden flag post, देवतानाम् of deities, आज्ञया by order, सूर्यः इव like Sun, धरणीम् on the ground, जगाम fell down.

#### Explanation

The beautiful, golden flag post, broken into several pieces, fell down on earth.It appeared as if the Sun had come down to earth at the behest of the gods.

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

तं चतुर्भिः खरः क्रुद्धो रामं गात्रेषु मार्गणैः। विव्याध युधि मर्मज्ञो मातङ्गमिव तोमरैः।।3.28.24।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

क्रुद्धः enraged, मर्मज्ञः one who knows the tender parts of the body, खरः Khara, युधि in war, तं रामम् that Rama, चतुर्भिः four, मार्गणैः with arrows, मातङ्गम् elephant, तोमरैरिव like with a javelin, गात्रेषु on his limbs, विव्याध hit.

#### Explanation

Quite enraged, Khara who knew how to hit the tender parts (of the body) in an encounter sent out four arrows on Rama. It was like poking an elephant's limbs with a javelin.

---

### Shloka 25

#### Shloka Text

स रामो बहुभिर्बाणैः खरकार्मुकनिस्सृतैः। विद्धो रुधिरसिक्ताङ्गो बभूव रुषितो भृशम्।।3.28.25।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः रामः that Rama, खरकार्मुकनिस्सृतैः by those arrows released from the bow of Khara, बहुभिः several, बाणैः arrows, विद्धः hit, रुधिरसिक्ताङ्गः one whose limbs were drenched in blood, भृशम् very, रुषितः furious, बभूव became.

#### Explanation

Rama, hit by the arrows released from the bow of Khara, all his limbs drenched in blood, became very furious.

---

### Shloka 26

#### Shloka Text

स धनुर्धन्विनां श्रेष्ठः प्रगृह्य परमाहवे। मुमोच परमेष्वासष्षट्छरानभिलक्षितान्।।3.28.26।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

परमाहवे in that great war, धन्विनाम् among archers, श्रेष्ठः best one, परमेष्वासः supreme shooter of arrows, सः he, धनुः bow, प्रगृह्य lifting, अभिलक्षितान् setting his goal, षट् six, शरान् arrows, मुमोच released.

#### Explanation

The best among archers and a supreme shooter, he lifted the bow, fixed his target and released six arrows in the great fight.

---

### Shloka 27

#### Shloka Text

शिरस्येकेन बाणेन द्वाभ्यां बह्वोरथार्दयत्। त्रिभिश्चन्द्रार्धवक्त्रैश्च वक्षस्यभिजघान ह।।3.28.27।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

एकेन with one, बाणेन with an arrow, शिरसि on head, अथ and, द्वाभ्याम् with two, बाह्वोः on shoulders, अर्दयत् hit, त्रिभिः three, चन्द्रार्धवक्त्रैः Moon shaped, वक्षसि on the chest, अभिजघान ह struck.

#### Explanation

Rama hit one arrow on the head, two on the shoulders and three of the shape of the crescent moon on the chest (of the enemy).

---

### Shloka 28

#### Shloka Text

ततः पश्चान्महातेजा नाराचान्भास्करोपमान्। जिघांसू राक्षसङ्कृद्धस्त्रयोदश समाददे।।3.28.28।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः thereafter, पश्चात् later, महातेजाः effulgent, कृद्धः enraged, राक्षसम् to the demon, जिघांसुः  wishing  to kill, भास्करोपमान् radiant like the Sun, त्रयोदश thirteen, नाराचान् arrows, समाददे took up.

#### Explanation

Enraged and effulgent Rama took up thirteen darts, radiant like the Sun, with a wish to kill the demon.

---

### Shloka 29

#### Shloka Text

ततोऽस्य युगमेकेन चतुर्भिश्चतुरो हयान्। षष्ठेन तु शिरस्सङ्ख्ये खरस्य रथसारथेः।।3.28.29।। त्रिभिस्त्रिवेणुं बलवान्द्वाभ्यामक्षं महाबलः। द्वादशेन तु बाणेन खरस्य सशरं धनुः।।3.28.30।। छित्वा वज्रनिकाशेन राघवः प्रहसन्निव। त्रयोदशेनेन्द्रसमो बिभेद समरे खरम्।।3.28.31।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

इन्द्रसमः like Indra, बलवान् strong, महाबलः powerful, राघवः Rama, सङ्ख्ये in war, एकेन with one, अस्य his, रथस्य chariot's, युगम् yoke, चतुर्भिः with four, चतुरः four, हयान् horses, षष्ठेन with the sixth one, खरस्य Khara's, रथसारथेः charioteer, शिरः head, त्रिभिः with three, त्रिवेणुम्  main pole, द्वाभ्याम् with two, अक्षम् axle, द्वादशेन twelfth, बाणेन arrow, खरस्य Khara's, सशरम् along with arrow, धनुः bow, छित्वा made into pieces, प्रहसन्निव as if laughing at, समरे in the fight, वज्रनिकाशेन diamond like arrow, त्रयोदशेन with the thirteenth, खरम् to Khara, बिभेद pierced.

#### Explanation

Rama, like Indra, strong and mighty, broke the yoke of Khara's chariot with one arrow, the four horses with four arrows, and with the sixth the charioteer's head, with three others the main pole of the chariot, with two the axle of the chariot, and with the twelfth Khara's bow and arrows were splintered. As if laughing at him, he pierced Khara's body with the thirteenth arrow.

---

### Shloka 30

#### Shloka Text

ततोऽस्य युगमेकेन चतुर्भिश्चतुरो हयान्। षष्ठेन तु शिरस्सङ्ख्ये खरस्य रथसारथेः।।3.28.29।। त्रिभिस्त्रिवेणुं बलवान्द्वाभ्यामक्षं महाबलः। द्वादशेन तु बाणेन खरस्य सशरं धनुः।।3.28.30।। छित्वा वज्रनिकाशेन राघवः प्रहसन्निव। त्रयोदशेनेन्द्रसमो बिभेद समरे खरम्।।3.28.31।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

इन्द्रसमः like Indra, बलवान् strong, महाबलः powerful, राघवः Rama, सङ्ख्ये in war, एकेन with one, अस्य his, रथस्य chariot's, युगम् yoke, चतुर्भिः with four, चतुरः four, हयान् horses, षष्ठेन with the sixth one, खरस्य Khara's, रथसारथेः charioteer, शिरः head, त्रिभिः with three, त्रिवेणुम्  main pole, द्वाभ्याम् with two, अक्षम् axle, द्वादशेन twelfth, बाणेन arrow, खरस्य Khara's, सशरम् along with arrow, धनुः bow, छित्वा made into pieces, प्रहसन्निव as if laughing at, समरे in the fight, वज्रनिकाशेन diamond like arrow, त्रयोदशेन with the thirteenth, खरम् to Khara, बिभेद pierced.

#### Explanation

Rama, like Indra, strong and mighty, broke the yoke of Khara's chariot with one arrow, the four horses with four arrows, and with the sixth the charioteer's head, with three others the main pole of the chariot, with two the axle of the chariot, and with the twelfth Khara's bow and arrows were splintered. As if laughing at him, he pierced Khara's body with the thirteenth arrow.

---

### Shloka 31

#### Shloka Text

ततोऽस्य युगमेकेन चतुर्भिश्चतुरो हयान्। षष्ठेन तु शिरस्सङ्ख्ये खरस्य रथसारथेः।।3.28.29।। त्रिभिस्त्रिवेणुं बलवान्द्वाभ्यामक्षं महाबलः। द्वादशेन तु बाणेन खरस्य सशरं धनुः।।3.28.30।। छित्वा वज्रनिकाशेन राघवः प्रहसन्निव। त्रयोदशेनेन्द्रसमो बिभेद समरे खरम्।।3.28.31।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

इन्द्रसमः like Indra, बलवान् strong, महाबलः powerful, राघवः Rama, सङ्ख्ये in war, एकेन with one, अस्य his, रथस्य chariot's, युगम् yoke, चतुर्भिः with four, चतुरः four, हयान् horses, षष्ठेन with the sixth one, खरस्य Khara's, रथसारथेः charioteer, शिरः head, त्रिभिः with three, त्रिवेणुम्  main pole, द्वाभ्याम् with two, अक्षम् axle, द्वादशेन twelfth, बाणेन arrow, खरस्य Khara's, सशरम् along with arrow, धनुः bow, छित्वा made into pieces, प्रहसन्निव as if laughing at, समरे in the fight, वज्रनिकाशेन diamond like arrow, त्रयोदशेन with the thirteenth, खरम् to Khara, बिभेद pierced.

#### Explanation

Rama, like Indra, strong and mighty, broke the yoke of Khara's chariot with one arrow, the four horses with four arrows, and with the sixth the charioteer's head, with three others the main pole of the chariot, with two the axle of the chariot, and with the twelfth Khara's bow and arrows were splintered. As if laughing at him, he pierced Khara's body with the thirteenth arrow.

---

### Shloka 32

#### Shloka Text

प्रभग्नधन्वा विरथो हताश्वो हतसारथिः। गदापाणिरवप्लुत्य तस्थौ भूमौ खरस्तदा।।3.28.32।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तदा then, प्रभग्नधन्वा with bow broken, विरथः without chariot, हताश्वः horses killed, हतसारधिः with charioteer dead, खरः Khara, गदापाणिः holding a mace in hand, अवप्लुत्य jumping down the chariot, भूमौ on the ground, तस्थौ stood.

#### Explanation

Then, with bow broken, chariot destroyed, horses and charioteer dead, Khara jumped  down the chariot and stood on the ground holding a mace.

---

### Shloka 33

#### Shloka Text

तत्कर्म रामस्य महारथस्य समेत्य देवाश्च महर्षयश्च। अपूजयन्प्राञ्जलयः प्रहृष्टाः तदा विमानाग्रगतास्समेताः।।3.28.33।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तदा then, देवाश्च gods, महर्षयश्च great sages, समेताः collected together, विमानाग्रगताः standing on the aerial chariots, समेत्य  assembled, प्रहृष्टाः delighted, प्राञ्जलयः with folded palms, महारथस्य of the great warrior, रामस्य Rama's, तत् that, कर्म task, अपूजयन् adored.

#### Explanation

Then the gods, along with great sages came down in aerial chariots and assembled there. Delighted, they adored Rama, the great warrior, with folded hands for the work done. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकिय आदिकाव्ये अरण्यकाण्डे अष्टाविंशस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the twentyeigth sarga of Aranyakanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 29

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

खरं तु विरथं रामो गदापाणिमवस्थितम्। मृदुपूर्वं महातेजाः परुषं वाक्यमब्रवीत्।।3.29.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महातेजा: briliant, रामः Rama, विरथम्: deprived of chariot, गदापाणिम् wielding a mace, अवस्थितम्  stood, खरम् Khara, मृदुपूर्वम् gentle first, परुषम् harsh वाक्यम् words, अब्रवीत् said.

#### Explanation

Effulgent Rama looked at Khara who stood with a mace in hand minus his chariot  and said to him first in a gentle voice and then harshly :

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

गजाश्वरथसम्बाधे बले महति तिष्ठता। कृतं सुदारुणं कर्म सर्वलोकजुगुप्सितम्।।3.29.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

गजाश्वरथसम्बाधे elephants, horses, chariots etc. महति in a great, बले army, तिष्ठता standing, सर्वलोकजुगुप्सितम् despised by the entire world, सुदारुणम् very cruel, कर्म deed, कृतम्  done.

#### Explanation

Although you command a huge army with many elephants, horses, chariots, etc.  you have done such cruel deeds that the entire world despises you.

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

उद्वेजनीयो भूतानां नृशंसः पापकर्मकृत्। त्रयाणामपि लोकानामीश्वरोऽपि न तिष्ठति।।3.29.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

भूतानाम् of all beings, उद्वेजनीयः causing distress, पापकर्मकृत् doer of sinful deeds, नृशंसः wicked, त्रयाणाम् of the three, लोकानाम् worlds, ईश्वरोऽपि even if he is the lord, न तिष्ठति  not survive.

#### Explanation

One who  is wicked, does sinful deeds and causes distress to all beings, will not survive even if he is the lord of the three worlds.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

कर्म लोकविरुद्धं तु कुर्वाणं क्षणदाचर। तीक्ष्णं सर्वजनो हन्ति सर्पं दुष्टमिवागतम्।।3.29.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

क्षणदाचर O nightranger  लोकविरुद्धम् that which is against the world, कर्म deeds, कुर्वाणम् who does, तीक्ष्णम् a terrific, सर्वजनः all people, आगतम् befallen, दुष्टम् cruel, सर्पमिव like a serpent, हन्ति will kill.

#### Explanation

O nightranger, one who does a horrific deed which is against the world will be killed like an evil serpent come near.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

लोभात्पापानि कुर्वाणः कामाद्वा यो न बुध्यते। भ्रष्टः पश्यति तस्यान्तं ब्राह्मणी करकादिव।।3.29.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लोभात् out of greed, कामाद्वा  out of passion, यः whoever, पापानि sinful deeds, कुर्वाणः commits, न बुध्यते not realise, भ्रष्ठः ruined, करकात् by a hailstone, ब्राह्मणीव like a lizard, तस्य his, अन्तम् end, पश्यति will see.

#### Explanation

Whoever commits sinful deeds out of greed or passion and without realisation will be ruined at the end like a lizard that eats hailstone (and dies).

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

वसतो दण्डकारण्ये तापसान्धर्मचारिणः। किन्नु हत्वा महाभागान्फलं प्राप्स्यसि राक्षस।।3.29.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षस O demon , दण्डकारण्ये in Dandaka forest, वसतः while residing, धर्मचारिणः those followers of righteous path, महाभागान् venerable ones , तापसान् ascetics, हत्वा after killing, किं नु what kind, फलम् fruit, प्राप्स्यसि will you get.

#### Explanation

O demon  what benefit will you  get by killing the venerable ascetics, followers of righteous path residing in Dandaka forest?

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

न चिरं पापकर्माणः क्रूरा लोकजुगुप्सिताः। ऐश्वर्यं प्राप्य तिष्ठन्ति शीर्णमूला इव द्रुमाः।।3.29.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पापकर्माणः who do sinful deeds, लोकजुगुप्सिताः  despised by the world, क्रूराः cruel, ऐश्वर्यम् prosperity, प्राप्य after obtaining, शीर्णमूलाः decayed at the roots, द्रुमाः इव like trees, चिरम् long time, न तिष्टन्ति will not stand.

#### Explanation

Cruel people, who are despised by the world for their sinful deeds, will not  be prosperous for long just like trees with decayed roots.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

अवश्यं लभते जन्तुः फलं पापस्य कर्मणः। घोरं पर्यागते काले द्रुमाः पुष्पमिवार्तवम्।।3.29.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

काले (at the right) time, पर्यागते comes in, जन्तुः people, पापस्य कर्मणः of sinful deeds, घोरम् terrific, फलम् fruit, द्रुमाः trees, आर्तवम् in proper season, पुष्पमिव like the flowers, अवश्यम् certainly, लभते will get.

#### Explanation

Just as the trees bear flowers at the proper season, so also people will certainly reap the fruits of their terrible, sinful deeds at appropriate time.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

नचिरात्प्राप्यते लोके पापानां कर्मणां फलम्। सविषाणामिवान्नानां भुक्तानां क्षणदाचर।।3.29.9।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

क्षणदाचर O night ranger  लोके in the world, पापानाम् of sinful, कर्मणाम् of actions, फलम् result, भुक्तानाम् eaten, सविषाणाम् poisonous, अन्नानामिव like the food, नचिरात् soon, प्राप्यते will obtain.

#### Explanation

O nightranger  people will reap the result of their sinful actions in this world soon like one eating poisonous food.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

पापमाचरतां घोरं लोकस्याप्रियमिच्छताम्। अहमासादितो राजा प्राणान्हन्तुं निशाचर।।3.29.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

निशाचर nightranger, घोरम् terrific, पापम् sin, आचरताम् doers, लोकस्य in this world, अप्रियम् offensive actions, इच्छताम्  who like to do, प्राणान् life, हन्तुम् to put an end, राजा king, अहम् I, आसादितः I have come.

#### Explanation

O nightranger  I have come with the orders of the king to put an end to the lives of the perpetrators of dreadful, sinful and offensive deeds in this world.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

अद्य हि त्वां मया मुक्ताश्शराः काञ्चनभूषणाः। विदार्यातिपतिष्यन्ति वल्मीकमिव पन्नगाः।।3.29.11।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अद्य now, मया by me, मुक्ताः unleashed, काञ्चनभूषणाः adorned with gold ornaments, शराः arrows, पन्नगाः like serpent, वल्मीकमिव like the anthill, त्वा you, विदार्य after piercing the body, अतिपतिष्यतनि will come out.

#### Explanation

Now when I unleash the golden  arrows, they will pierce through your body and come out like serpents emerging from the anthill.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

ये त्वया दण्डकारण्ये भक्षिता धर्मचारिणः। तानद्य निहतस्सङ्ख्ये ससैन्योऽनुगमिष्यसि।।3.29.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

त्वया by you, दण्डकारण्ये in Dandaka forest, ये those, धर्मचारिणः righteous people, भक्षिताः are eaten, तान् those, अद्य now, सङ्ख्ये in the war, निहतः killed, ससैन्यः along with your army, अनुगमिष्यसि will be following.

#### Explanation

Slain by me in the battle today, you and your army will follow the righteous men eaten by you in Dandaka forest.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

अद्य त्वां निहतं बाणैः पश्यन्तु परमर्षयः। निरयस्थं विमानस्था ये त्वया हिंसिताः पुरा।।3.29.13।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पुरा earlier, त्वया by you, ये whom, हिंसिताः tortured, परमर्षयः great seers, विमानस्थाः seated in the aerial vehicles, अद्य now, बाणैः with arrows, निहतम् killed, निरयस्थम् fallen into hell, त्वाम् you, पश्यन्तु  see you.

#### Explanation

The great seers whom you have earlier tortured and killed will, from their aerial vehicles, see you fallen into hell.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

प्रहर त्वं यथाकामं कुरु यत्नं कुलाधम। अद्य ते पातयिष्यामि शिरस्तालफलं यथा।।3.29.14।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कुलाधम O Nile of the race, त्वम् you, यथाकामम् as you wish, प्रहर freely strike at me, यत्नम् effort, कुरु you may do, अद्य now, ते your, शिरः head, तालफलं यथा like a palm fruit, पातयिष्यामि will strike you down.

#### Explanation

O vile fellow, now you may strike at me as you please. I will make your head fall  down like a palm fruit.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

एवमुक्तस्तु रामेण कृद्धस्संरक्तलोचनः। प्रत्युवाच खरो रामं प्रहसन्क्रोधमूर्छितः।।3.29.15।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रामेण by Rama, एवम् in that way, उक्तः having said, खरः Khara, संरक्तलोचनः eyes turned red, (ततः then), प्रहसन् laughed, क्रोधमूर्छितः lost his senses in anger, रामम् to Rama, प्रत्युवाच replied.

#### Explanation

Challenged by Rama  in that manner, Khara, his senses switched off, eyes turned red due to anger, replied laughing :

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

प्राकृतान्राक्षसान्हत्वा युद्धे दशरथात्मज। आत्मना कथमात्मानमप्रशस्यं प्रशंससि।।3.29.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

दशरथात्मज O Rama, son of Dasaratha, युद्धे in war, प्राकृतान्  ordinary ( people), राक्षसान् demons, हत्वा killing, अप्रशस्यम् not praiseworthy, आत्मानम् yourself, आत्मना by yourself, कथम् why, प्रशंससि praising.

#### Explanation

O son of Dasaratha, it is not praiseworthy to kill ordinary demons in war. Why do you praise yourself for it?

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

विक्रान्ता बलवन्तो वा ये भवन्ति नरर्षभाः। कथयन्ति न ते किञ्चित्तेजसा स्वेन गर्विताः।।3.29.17।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ये those, नरर्षभाः best of men, विक्रान्ताः valiant, बलवन्तो वा strong, भवन्ति they are, ते they, स्वेन by their own, तेजसा by valour, गर्विताः  proud of, किञ्चित् even a little, न कथयन्ति do not speak of.

#### Explanation

The valiant, strong and the best of men do not at all speak proudly of themselves  and their valour.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

प्राकृतास्त्वकृतात्मानो लोके क्षत्रियपांसनाः। निरर्थकं विकत्थन्ते यथा राम विकत्थसे।।3.29.18।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राम Rama, लोके in the world, अकृतात्मानः having no control over self, प्राकृतास्तु common people, क्षत्रियपांसनाः lowest of kshatriyas, यथा as, विकत्थन्ते they boast, निरर्थकम् in a meaningless, विकत्थसे you boast yourself.

#### Explanation

O Rama common people, having no control over themselves and the lowest of kshatriyas boast of themselves in the world in a meaningless way as you are doing (now).

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

कुलं व्यपदिशन्वीरस्समरे कोऽभिधास्यति। मृत्युकाले हि सम्प्राप्ते स्वयमप्रस्तवे स्तवम्।।3.29.19।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मृत्युकाले when death, सम्प्राप्ते comes, वीरः hero, कः who, समरे in war, कुलम् his race, व्यपदिशन् naming, अप्रस्तवे unnecessarily, स्वयम् himself, स्तवम् praise, अभिधास्यति will talk of.

#### Explanation

When death comes in a battle no hero will name his race and boast of himself unnessarily.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

सर्वथैव लघुत्वं ते कत्थनेन विदर्शितम्। सुवर्णप्रतिरूपेण तप्तेनेव कुशाग्निना।।3.29.20।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कत्थनेन by boasting, तप्तेन heated, सुवर्णप्रतिरूपेण  looking like gold, कुशाग्निना इव by the fire of kusa grass, सर्वथैव by all means, ते your, लघुत्वम् meanness, विदर्शितम् is exhibited.

#### Explanation

All your meanness is exhibited by your boasting just as the kusa grass caught by fire appears like gold.

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

न तु मामिह तिष्ठन्तं पश्यसि त्वं गदाधरम्। धराधरमिवाकम्प्यं पर्वतं धातुभिश्चितम्।।3.29.21।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

त्वम् you, गदाधरम् holding a mace, इह here, तिष्ठन्तम् while standing, धातुभिः with minerals, चितम् inlaid, पर्वतम् mountain, धराधरमिव like supporter of earth, अकम्प्यम् unshakeable, माम् me, पश्यसि you are seeing, ननु indeed.

#### Explanation

Indeed, (it is strange that) you do not see me holding a mace and standing here like an unshakeable mountain which contains minerals and supports the earth.

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

पर्याप्तोऽहं गदापाणिर्हन्तुं प्राणान्रणे तव। त्रयाणामपि लोकानां पाशहस्त इवान्तकः।।3.29.22।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पाशहस्तः holding the noose of death, अन्तकः Yama, त्रयाणाम् for the three, लोकानामपि इव  worlds also, रणे in war, तव your, प्राणान् life, हन्तुम् to kill, गदापाणिः weilding mace, अहम् I, पर्याप्तः enough.

#### Explanation

Just like lord Yama, holding a noose of death is alone enough to destroy the three worlds, I am alone sufficient to take away your life in war, a mace in hand.

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

कामं बह्वपि वक्तव्यं त्वयि वक्ष्यामि न त्वहम्। अस्तं गच्छेद्धि सविता युद्धविघ्नस्ततो भवेत्।।3.29.23।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

त्वयि on you, बहु much, वक्तव्यमपि has to be said, कामम् indeed, तु but, अहम् I, न वक्ष्यामि not speak any more, सविता Sun, अस्तं गच्छेत् will set, ततः thereafter, युद्धविघ्नः obstacle to wage war, भवेत् will be.

#### Explanation

Indeed, I shall not speak any more even though a lot can be said on you. But the Sun is about to set and it will be improper to wage war thereafter.

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

चतुर्दश सहस्राणि राक्षसानां हतानि ते। त्वद्विनाशात्करोम्येषां तेषामश्रुप्रमार्जनम्।।3.29.24।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ते for you, एषां these, राक्षसानाम् of demons, चतुर्दश fourteen, सहस्राणि thousand, हतानि killed, त्वद्विनाशात् by their destruction, तेषाम् their, अश्रुप्रमार्जनम् wiping their tears, करोमि I shall carry out.

#### Explanation

You have killed these fourteen thousand demons. Therefore, I will wipe their tears by destroying you.

---

### Shloka 25

#### Shloka Text

इत्युक्त्वा परमक्रुद्धस्तां गदां परमाङ्गदः। खरश्चिक्षेप रामाय प्रदीप्तामशनिं यथा।।3.29.25।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

परमाङ्गदः adorned with large shoulder bands, खरः Khara, इति thus, उक्त्वा having said, परमक्रुद्धः highly enraged, प्रदीप्ताम् blazing, अशनिं यथा like a thunderbolt, तां गदाम् that mace, रामाय at Rama, चिक्षेव hurled.

#### Explanation

Having said this, highly enraged Khara adorned with large shoulder bands, hurled  at Rama, his mace glowing like a thunderbolt.

---

### Shloka 26

#### Shloka Text

खरबाहुप्रयुक्ता सा प्रदीप्ता महती गदा। भस्मवृक्षांश्च गुल्मांश्च कृत्वागात्तत्समीपतः।।3.29.26।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

खरबाहुप्रयुक्ता unleashed by Khara's arms, प्रदीप्ता blazing, महती huge, गदा mace, वृक्षांश्च trees, गुल्मांश्च shrubs, भस्म ashes, कृत्वा reduced into, तत्समीपतः close to him, अगात् reached.

#### Explanation

The huge, blazing mace hurled by Khara's arms reduced the trees and shrubs into ashes and came close to Rama.

---

### Shloka 27

#### Shloka Text

तामापतन्तीं ज्वलतां मृत्युपाशोपमां गदाम्। अन्तरिक्षगतां रामचशिच्छेद बहुधा शरैः।।3.29.27।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

आपतन्ती falling down, ज्वलताम् burning, मृत्युपाशोपमाम् like the noose of death, तां गदाम्  that mace, अन्तरिक्षगताम् in the sky, रामः Rama, शरैः arrows, बहुधा several pieces, चिच्छेद shattered.

#### Explanation

The mace, which moved in the sky burning like the noose of death was shattered to pieces by Rama.

---

### Shloka 28

#### Shloka Text

सा विकीर्णा शरैर्भग्ना पपात धरणीतले। गदा मन्त्रौषधबलैर्व्यालीव विनिपातिता।।3.29.28।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

शरैः arrows, भग्ना broken, सा गदा the mace, मन्त्रौषधबलैः  on the strength of mantras and medicines, विनिपातिता fell down, व्यालीव like a female serpent, विकीर्णा scattered, धरणीतले on the ground, पपात fell down.

#### Explanation

The mace shattered by the arrows of Rama , fell down scattered to pieces on the ground like a female serpent subdued by the force of mantras and medicine. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये अरण्यकाण्डे एकोनत्रिंशस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the twentyninth sarga of Aranyakanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 30

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

भित्त्वा तु तां गदां बाणै राघवो धर्मवत्सलः। स्मयमानः खरं वाक्यं संरब्धमिदमब्रवीत्।।3.30.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

धर्मवत्सलः fond of dharma, राघवः Rama, तां गदाम् that mace, बाणैः by arrows, भित्त्वा after breaking into pieces, स्मयमानः smiling, संरब्धम् confused, खरम् Khara, इदं वाक्यम् these  words, अब्रवीत् spoke.

#### Explanation

Rama, fond of dharma, broke into pieces Khara's mace with his arrows, and smiling at bewildered Khara, said:

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

एतत्ते बलसर्वस्वं दर्शितं राक्षसाधम। शक्तिहीनतरो मत्तो वृथा त्वमवगर्जसि।।3.30.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षसाधम O lowly demon, ते your, एतत् this, बलसर्वस्वम् all your strength, दर्शितम् is shown, मत्तः from me, शक्तिहीनतरः inferior in strength, त्वम् you, वृथा unnecssarily, अवगर्जसि roared against.

#### Explanation

O lowly demon, you have already exhibited all your strength. Inferior to me in energy, you are needlessly roaring.

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

एषा बाणविनिर्भिन्ना गदा भूमितलं गता। अभिधानप्रगल्भस्य तव प्रत्ययघातिनी।।3.30.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

एषा this, गदा mace, बाणविनिर्भिन्ना broken by arrows, अभिधानप्रगल्भस्य spoke arrogantly, तव you, प्रत्ययघातिनी that which destroyed all your confidence, भूमितलम् on the ground, गता lying.

#### Explanation

The mace about which you boasted so much has betrayed your confidence. Shattered by my arrows it now lies on the ground.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

यत्त्वयोक्तं विनष्टानामहमश्रुप्रमार्जनम्। राक्षसानां करोमीति मिथ्या तदपि ते वचः।।3.30.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अहम् I, विनष्टानाम् of the dead, राक्षसानाम् of the demons, अश्रुप्रमार्जनम् wiping tears, करोमीति I will do, त्वया by  you, यत् whatever, उक्तम् uttered, ते by you, तत् that, वचः अपि word too, मिथ्या false.

#### Explanation

You said you will wipe the tears of the dead demons. (Now) those words have proved false.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

नीचस्य क्षुद्रशीलस्य मिथ्यावृत्तस्य रक्षसः। प्राणानहं हरिष्यामि गरुत्मानमृतं यथा।।3.30.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

नीचस्य mean, क्षुद्रशीलस्य of low behaviour, मिथ्यावृत्तस्य  of false pride, रक्षसः of the demon, प्राणान् life, अहम् I, गरुत्मान् Garuda, अमृतं यथा  like nectar, हरिष्यामि will take away.

#### Explanation

You are mean. You have false pride. You are of low behaviour. I shall take away your life, O demon,, Just as Garuda carried off the nectar.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

अद्य ते छिन्नकण्ठस्य फेनबुद्बुदभूषितम्। विदारितस्य मद्बाणैर्मही पास्यति शोणितम्।।3.30.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अद्य now, मद्बाणैः with my darts, विदारितस्य  split open, छिन्नकण्ठस्य (of a person) whose neck is cut, ते your, फेनबुद्बुदभूषितम् adorned with bubbles and foam, शोणितम् blood, मही ground, पास्यति will drink.

#### Explanation

Now with your throat split open by my arrows , the earth will drink your foamy blood full of bubbles.

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

पांसुरूषितसर्वाङ्गस्स्रस्तन्यस्त भुजद्वयः। स्वप्स्यसे गां समालिङ्ग्य दुर्लभां प्रमदामिव।।3.30.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पांसुरूषितसर्वाङ्गः all your limbs covered with dust, स्रस्तन्यस्तभुजद्वयः with both the arms  dropped and outstretched on the ground, दुर्लभाम् difficult, प्रमदामिव like a woman, गाम् the earth, समालिङ्ग्य after embracing, स्वप्स्यसे you will sleep.

#### Explanation

With all your limbs covered with dust, both the arms dropped down and stretched  out  on the ground, you will sleep as though hugging the earth, like embracing a rare woman.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

प्रबद्धनिद्रे शयिते त्वयि राक्षसपांसने। भविष्यन्तिशरण्यानां शरण्या दण्डका इमे।।3.30.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षसपांसने lowly demon, त्वयि you, प्रबद्धनिद्रे when you attain eternal sleep, शयिते when sleeping so, इमे these, दण्डकाः Dandaka grounds, शरण्यानाम् for the sheltered, शरण्याः refuge, भविष्यन्ति will be.

#### Explanation

When you, O vile demon, will go into eternal sleep the Dandaka grounds will provide shelter to those who deserve it here.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

जनस्थाने हतस्थाने तव राक्षस मच्छरैः। निर्भया विचरिष्यन्ति सर्वतो मुनयो वने।।3.30.9।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षस O demon , तव your, जनस्थाने when Janasthana, मच्छरैः by my darts, हतस्थाने when the hold is destroyed, मुनयः seers, निर्भयाः without fear, सर्वत्र all over, वने in the forest, विचरिष्यन्ति will wander.

#### Explanation

O demon, when my arrows destroy your hold over Janasthana the sages will move freely without fear all over this forest.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

अद्य विप्रसरिष्यन्ति राक्षस्यो हतबान्धवाः। बाष्पार्द्रवदना दीना भयादन्यभयावहाः।।3.30.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अन्यभयावहाः causing fear to others, राक्षस्यः the demonesses, हतबान्धवाः with slain relations, बाष्पार्द्रवदनाः faces drenched in tears, भयात् due to fear, दीनाः desperately, अद्य now, विप्रसरिष्यन्ति will move away.

#### Explanation

Having lost all their relations, the demonesses, who were causing fear to others,will move away with faces full of tears.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

अद्य शोकरसज्ञास्ता भविष्यन्ति निरर्थकाः। अनुरूपकुलाः पत्न्यो यासां त्वं पतिरीदृशः।।3.30.11।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

यासाम् those women, ईदृशः ruined , त्वम् you, पतिः husband, अनुरूपकुलाः born of a befitting race, ताः them, पत्न्यः wives, अद्य now, निरर्थकाः meaninglessly, शोकरसज्ञाः taste the pangs of sorrow, भविष्यन्ति will remain

#### Explanation

Your wives, who have got a husband like you, must have been born to a befitting race. They will taste the pangs of sorrow today, for no reasons whatsoever.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

नृशंस नीच क्षुद्रात्मन्नित्यं ब्राह्मणकण्टक। यत्कृते शङ्कितैरग्नौ मुनिभिः पात्यते हविः।।3.30.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

नृशंश cruel , नीच mean, क्षुद्रात्मन् a vile self, नित्यम् always, ब्राह्मणकण्टक opposing the brahmins, यत्कृते due to whom, शङ्कितैः by hesitant, मुनिभिः by seers, हविः an oblation, अग्नौ in fire, पात्यते is offered.

#### Explanation

O cruel, mean, vile fellow, you had been always opposing the brahmins who pour oblations in fire under fear.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

तमेवमभिसंरब्धं ब्रुवाणं राघवं रणे। खरो निर्भर्त्सयामास रोषात्खरतरस्वरः।।3.30.13।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

एवम् thus, रणे in war, अभिसंरब्धम् in a state of excitement, ब्रुवाणाम्  was speaking, तं राघवम् that Rama, रोषात् due to anger, खरतरस्वरः  in a harsher tone, खरः Khara, निर्भर्त्सयामास abused.

#### Explanation

When Rama was thus speaking in a state of excitement in the middle of war, the choleric Khara  started abusing him in a harsher tone.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

दृढं खल्ववलिप्तोऽसि भयेष्वपि च निर्भयः। वाच्यावाच्यं ततो हि त्वं मृत्युवश्यो न बुध्यसे।।3.30.14।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

भयेष्वपि even in the midst of fear, निर्भयः fearless, त्वम् you, दृढम् certainly, अवलिप्तः असि you are haughty, ततः then, मृत्युवश्यः under the grip of death, वाच्यावाच्यम् what  to speak and what not to speak, न बुध्यसे not knowing.

#### Explanation

You are very haughty. When there is every cause of fear you pretend fearlessness. Being in the grip of death you do not know what to speak and what not to speak.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

कालपाशपरिक्षिप्ता भवन्ति पुरुषा हि ये। कार्याकार्यं न जानन्ति ते निरस्तषडिन्द्रियाः।।3.30.15।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ये पुरुषाः those men, कालपाशपरिक्षिप्ताः bound by the noose of death, भवन्ति remain, ते you, निरस्तषडिन्द्रियाः  whose six senses (five sense organs and mind)  cease to function, कार्याकार्यम् (sense of discrimination) of what to and not to do, न जानन्ति do not know.

#### Explanation

Those bound by the noose of death, do not know what to and what not to do, as their six senses cease to function.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

एवमुक्त्वा ततो रामं संरुध्य भ्रुकुटीं ततः। स ददर्श महासालमविदूरे निशाचरः।।3.30.16।। रणे प्रहरणस्यार्थे सर्वतो ह्यवलोकयन्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः निशाचरः  nightwalker, रामम् to Rama, एवम् in that way, उक्त्वा having spoken, ततः then, भ्रुकुटीम् eyebrows, संरुध्य drawing together in frown, रणे in war, प्रहरणस्य अर्थे for striking, सर्वतः all over, अवलोकयन् observing, अविदूरे close by, महासालम् huge sal tree, ददर्श saw.

#### Explanation

Having said thus to Rama with a frown on his face, Khara, looking around for something to strike him with, saw a huge sal tree not very far.

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

स तमुत्पाटयामास संदश्य दशनच्छदम्।।3.30.17।। तं समुत्क्षिप्य बाहुभ्यां विनद्य च महाबलः। राममुद्दिश्य चिक्षेप हतस्त्वमिति चाब्रवीत्।।3.30.18।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः Khara, दशनच्छदम्   lower lip, संदस्य  biting, तम् that( tree), उत्पाटयामास uprooted, महाबलः mighty, तम् him, बाहुभ्याम् with arms, समुत्क्षिप्य  lifted, विनद्य shouted, रामम् उद्दिश्य targetting Rama, चिक्षेप hurled, त्वम् you, हतः  dead, इति thus, अब्रवीत् च said.

#### Explanation

Khara, biting his lower lip, uprooted the tree , lifted it with his mighty arms, and shouting at Rama loudly hurled it at him, saying, 'You are dead'.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

स तमुत्पाटयामास संदश्य दशनच्छदम्।।3.30.17।। तं समुत्क्षिप्य बाहुभ्यां विनद्य च महाबलः। राममुद्दिश्य चिक्षेप हतस्त्वमिति चाब्रवीत्।।3.30.18।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः Khara, दशनच्छदम्   lower lip, संदस्य  biting, तम् that( tree), उत्पाटयामास uprooted, महाबलः mighty, तम् him, बाहुभ्याम् with arms, समुत्क्षिप्य  lifted, विनद्य shouted, रामम् उद्दिश्य targetting Rama, चिक्षेप hurled, त्वम् you, हतः  dead, इति thus, अब्रवीत् च said.

#### Explanation

Khara, biting his lower lip, uprooted the tree , lifted it with his mighty arms, and shouting at Rama loudly hurled it at him, saying, 'You are dead'.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

तमापतन्तं बाणौघैच्छित्वा रामः प्रतापवान्। रोषमाहारयत्तीव्रं निहन्तुं समरे खरम्।।3.30.19।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

प्रतापवान् courageous, रामः Rama, आपतन्तम् falling, तम् that, बाणौघैः with a flood of arrows, छित्त्वा having shattered, समरे in war, खरम् Khara, निहन्तुम् to put an end, तीव्रम् intensely, रोषम् anger, आहारयत् gathered.

#### Explanation

Brave Rama shattered with a flood of arrows the tree falling over him and summoned sharp anger to end Khara in war.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

जातस्वेदस्ततो रामो रोषाद्रक्तान्तलोचनः। निर्भिभेद सहस्रेण बाणानां समरे खरम्।।3.30.20।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, जातस्वेदः sweating all over, रोषात् with anger, रक्तान्तलोचनः one whose  eyes turned red in the corners, रामः Rama, बाणानाम् of the arrows, सहस्रेण a thousand, समरे in war, खरम् Khara, निर्भिभेद hit.

#### Explanation

Full of sweat and with the corners of his eyes turned red, Rama hit Khara with a thousand arrows in the encounter.

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

तस्य बाणान्तराद्रक्तं बहु सुस्राव फेनिलम्। गिरेः प्रस्रवणस्येव तोयधारापरिस्रवः।।3.30.21।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तस्य his, फेनिलम् foamy, बहु a lot, रक्तम् blood, बाणान्तरात् by the arrows piercing, गिरेः  of the mount, प्रस्रवणस्य Prasravana's, तोयधारपरिस्रवः इव like the waterfalls from the mountain, सुस्राव came out.

#### Explanation

With his body pierced with arrows his blood started flowing like the waterfalls flow from mount Prasravana.

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

विह्वलस्सकृतो बाणैः खरो रामेण संयुगे। मत्तो रुधिरगन्धेन तमेवाभ्यद्रवद्द्रुतम्।।3.30.22।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

संयुगे in the war, रामेण by Rama, बाणैः with arrows, विह्वलः completely struck, कृतः done, मत्तः an intoxicated one, सः खरः Khara's, रुधिरगन्धेन with the smell of blood, द्रुतम् quickly, तं राममेव on Rama also, अभ्यद्रवत् ran charging.

#### Explanation

Completely struck by Rama's arrows, Khara, intoxicated with the smell of  blood, ran speedily charging towards Rama.

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

तमापतन्तं संरब्धं कृतास्त्रो रुधिराप्लुतम्। अपासर्पत्प्रतिपदं किञ्चित्वरितविक्रमः।।3.30.23।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कृतास्त्रः trained in the science of arms, (रामः Rama), आपतन्तम् falling upon, रुधिराप्लुतम् drenched in blood, संरब्धम् highly agitated, तम् him, त्वरितविक्रमः picked up his valour quickly, प्रतिपदम्  step, किञ्चित् a little, अपासर्पत् moved aside.

#### Explanation

Rama who was an adept in the science of arms picked up his valour quickly and moved a step back (not  to give him an opportunity to strike), looking at the highly agitated Khara falling upon him, with his body fully drenched in blood.

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

ततः पावकसङ्काशं वधाय समरे शरम्। खरस्य रामो जग्राह ब्रह्मदण्डमिवापरम्।।3.30.24।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः thereafter, रामः Rama, खरस्य Khara's, वधाय to kill, पावकसङ्काशम् glowing like fire, अपरम् इव like another, ब्रह्मदण्डमिव like Brahmadanda, शरम् dart, समरे in war, जग्राह took.

#### Explanation

Rama took a dart glowing like fire which resembled another Brahmadanda to kill Khara in the war.

---

### Shloka 25

#### Shloka Text

स तं दत्तं मघवता सुरराजेन धीमता। संदधे चापि धर्मात्मा मुमोच च खरं प्रति।।3.30.25।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

धर्मात्मा righteous, सः Rama, धीमता by the wise, मघवता by Indra, सुरराजेन by the king of the gods, दत्तम् was given, तम् that weapon, संदधे placed, खरं प्रति on Khara, मुमोच च and shot.

#### Explanation

Righteous Rama placed on his bow the dart given him by wise Indra, king of the gods, and shot it at Khara.

---

### Shloka 26

#### Shloka Text

स विमुक्तो महाबाणो निर्घातसमनिस्वनः। रामेण धनुरायम्य खरस्योरसिचापतत्।।3.30.26।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रामेण by Rama, धनुः bow, आयम्य on bending, खरस्य Khara's, उरसि on the chest, विमुक्तः released, निर्घातसमनिस्वनः sounding like a violent gust of wind, सः बाणः that arrow, अपतत् fell.

#### Explanation

Bending the bow fully, Rama released the arrow roaring like a violent gust of wind, which fell on Khara's chest.

---

### Shloka 27

#### Shloka Text

स पपात खरो भूमौ दह्यमानश्शरानग्निना। रुद्रेणेव विनिर्दग्धश्वेतारण्ये यथान्तकः।।3.30.27।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

शराग्निना by the fire of the arrow, दह्यमानः  burned सः that, खरः Khara, श्वेतारण्ये in Swetaranya, रुद्रेण by Rudra, विनिर्दग्धः burnt totally, अन्तकः यथा like Yama, भूमौ on the ground, पपात collapsed.

#### Explanation

Just as Yama was burnt by Rudra's fire(from his eyes) in Swetaranya, Khara collapsed on the ground consumed by the fire of Rama's arrow.

---

### Shloka 28

#### Shloka Text

स वृत्र इव वज्रेण फेनेन नमुचिर्यथा। बलो वेन्द्राशनिहतो निपपात हतः खरः।।3.30.28।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः खरः that Khara, हतः killed, स वज्रेण by the thunderbolt, वृत्र इव like Vrutra, फेनेन by froth from, नमुचिः Namuchi, यथा like that, इन्द्राशनिहतः struck by Indra's thunderbolt, बलो वा like Bala, निपपात fell down.

#### Explanation

Khara fell down just like Vrutra or Bala killed by Indra's thunderbolt or like Numuchi by the froth of the sea.

---

### Shloka 29

#### Shloka Text

ततो राजर्षयस्सर्वे सङ्गताः परमर्षयः। सभाज्य मुदिता राममिदं वचनमब्रुवन्।।3.30.29।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, सर्वे all, राजर्षयः rajarsis, परमर्षयः great seers, सङ्गताः together, मुदिताः joyfully, रामम् Rama, सभाज्य worshipped, इदम् this, वचनम् words, अब्रुवन् said.

#### Explanation

Then the rajarsis, great seers gathered together, offered their worship to Rama  and said these words joyfully :

---

### Shloka 30

#### Shloka Text

एतदर्थं महातेजा महेन्द्रः पाकशासनः। शरभङ्गाश्रमं पुण्यमाजगाम पुरन्दरः।।3.30.30।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पाकशासनः killer of Paka (Indra), महातेजाः glorious, पुरन्दरः one who can break a fortress, (Indra), महेन्द्रः Mahendra, एतदर्थम् for that purpose only, पुण्यम् holy, शरभङ्गाश्रमम् hermitage of Sarabhanga, आजगाम came.

#### Explanation

For this purpose, killer of Paka, the gIorious Indra (who can break a fortress), came to the hermitage of Sarabhanga.

---

### Shloka 31

#### Shloka Text

आनीतस्त्वमिमं देशमुपायेन महर्षिभिः। एषां वधार्थं क्रूराणां रक्षसां पापकर्मणाम्।।3.30.31।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

क्रूराणाम् cruel, पापकर्मणाम् sinners, एषाम् of these, रक्षसाम् of demons, वधार्थम् to kill, त्वम् you, महर्षिभिः by great sages, उपायेन cleverly planned, इमम् this, देशम् place, आनीतः you are brought.

#### Explanation

You are brought to this place by the great sages who had cleverly devised a plan to kill the cruel sinners, these demons৷৷

---

### Shloka 32

#### Shloka Text

तदिदं नः कृतं कार्यं त्वया दशरथात्मज। सुखं धर्मं चरिष्यन्ति दण्डकेषु महर्षयः।।3.30.32।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

दशरथात्मज O son of Dasaratha, नः our, तत् such, इदम् this, कार्यम् a task, कृतम् accomplished, महर्षयः sages, दण्डकेषु in Dandaka, सुखम् happily, धर्मम् rightful duty, चरिष्यन्ति discharge.

#### Explanation

O son of Dasaratha, you have accomplished such a task that hereafter the sages in Dandaka will discharge their rightful duties happily.

---

### Shloka 33

#### Shloka Text

एतस्मिन्तरे देवाश्चारणैस्सह सङ्गताः। दुन्दुभींश्चाभिनिघ्नन्तः पुष्पवर्षं समन्ततः।।3.30.33।। रामस्योपरि संह्रुष्टा ववृषुर्विस्मितास्तदा।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

एतस्मिन् in the, अन्तरे meanwhile, देवाः gods, चारणैः सह and charanas (celestial bards), सङ्गताः got together, संह्रुष्टा joyfully, दुन्दुभीन् drums, अभिनिघ्नन्तः beating, तदा then, विस्मिताः wonderstruck, रामस्य at Rama, उपरि on, समन्ततः from all sides, पुष्पवर्षम् shower of flowers, ववृषुः rained.

#### Explanation

Meanwhile the wonderstruck gods along with the charanas (celestial bards) got together and began beating the drums and raining showers of flowers on Rama from all sides.

---

### Shloka 34

#### Shloka Text

अर्धाधिकमुहूर्तेन रामेण निशितैश्शरैः।।3.30.34।। चतुर्दशसहस्राणि रक्षसां भीमकर्मणाम्। खरदूषणमुख्यानां निहतानि महाहवे।।3.30.35।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रामेण by Rama, अर्धाधिकमुहूर्तेन little more than half a moment (a moment i.e. मुहूर्त  is 48 seconds) निशितैः sharp, शरैः darts, महाहवे in the great war, भीमकर्मणाम् of terrific tasks, खरदूषणमुख्यानाम् led by Khara and Dusana, रक्षसाम् demons, चतुर्दश fourteen, सहस्राणि thousand, निहतानि were killed.

#### Explanation

'In a little more than half a moment of this great war Rama had accomplished the terrific task of killing with sharp darts fourteen thousand demons, led by Khara and Dusana.

---

### Shloka 35

#### Shloka Text

अर्धाधिकमुहूर्तेन रामेण निशितैश्शरैः।।3.30.34।। चतुर्दशसहस्राणि रक्षसां भीमकर्मणाम्। खरदूषणमुख्यानां निहतानि महाहवे।।3.30.35।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रामेण by Rama, अर्धाधिकमुहूर्तेन little more than half a moment (a moment i.e. मुहूर्त  is 48 seconds) निशितैः sharp, शरैः darts, महाहवे in the great war, भीमकर्मणाम् of terrific tasks, खरदूषणमुख्यानाम् led by Khara and Dusana, रक्षसाम् demons, चतुर्दश fourteen, सहस्राणि thousand, निहतानि were killed.

#### Explanation

'In a little more than half a moment of this great war Rama had accomplished the terrific task of killing with sharp darts fourteen thousand demons, led by Khara and Dusana.

---

### Shloka 36

#### Shloka Text

अहो बत महत्कर्म रामस्य विदितात्मनः। अहो वीर्यमहो दाक्ष्यं विष्णोरिव हि दृश्यते।।3.30.36।। इत्येवमुक्त्वा ते सर्वे ययुर्देवा यथागतम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

विदितात्मनः who knows the self, रामस्य Rama's, महत् great, कर्म performance, अहो बत what a great thing, वीर्यम् prowess, अहो Oh, दाक्ष्यम् efficiency, अहो Oh, विष्णोरिव like Visnu, दृश्यते appears, इत्येवम् like that, उक्त्वा saying, सर्वे all, ते देवाः the gods, यथागतम् to their destinations, ययुः went back.

#### Explanation

'Rama who knows the self has performed a great work. Oh what prowess what efficiency  only lord Visnu could do this'. So saying all the gods went back to their respective destinations.

---

### Shloka 37

#### Shloka Text

तस्मिन्नन्तरे वीरो लक्ष्मणस्सह सीतया। गिरिदुर्गाद्विनिष्क्रम्य संविवेशाश्रमं सुखी।।3.30.37।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तस्मिन् अन्तरे in the mean time, वीरः heroic, (सः) लक्ष्मणः Lakshmana, सीतया सह accompanied by Sita, गिरिदुर्गात् from the mountain cave, विनिष्क्रम्य came out, सुखी happily, आश्रमम् hermitage, (सं)विवेश entered.

#### Explanation

In the mean time, heroic Lakshmana accompanied by Sita came out of the mountain cave happily and entered the hermitage.

---

### Shloka 38

#### Shloka Text

ततो रामस्तु विजयी पूज्यमानो महर्षिभिः। प्रविवेशाश्रमं वीरो लक्ष्मणेनाभिपूजितः।।3.30.38।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, विजयी victorious man, वीरः hero, रामस्तु Rama too, महर्षिभिः by the seers, पूज्यमानः worshipped by, लक्ष्मणेन by Lakshmana, अभिपूजितः worshipped, आश्रमम् hermitage, प्रविवेश entered.

#### Explanation

Then victorious Rama having been worshipped by the seers and also by Lakshmana entered the hermitage.

---

### Shloka 39

#### Shloka Text

तं दृष्ट्वा शत्रुहन्तारं महर्षीणां सुखावहम्। बभूव हृष्टा वैदेही भर्तारं परिषस्वजे।।3.30.39।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वैदेही Vaidehi, शत्रुहन्तारम् one who has killed the enemies, महर्षीणाम् of the great sages, सुखावहम् for their happiness, तम् him, भर्तारम् husband, दृष्ट्वा seeing, हृष्टा glad, बभूव became, परिषस्वजे embraced.

#### Explanation

On seeing Rama killing the enemies of the great sages for their happiness Sita felt glad and embraced her husband.

---

### Shloka 40

#### Shloka Text

मुदा परमया युक्ता दृष्ट्वा रक्षोगणान्हतान्। रामं चैवाव्यथं दृष्ट्वा तुतोष जनकात्मजा।।3.30.40।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

जनकात्मजा daughter of Janaka, हतान् dead, रक्षोगणान् the demons, दृष्ट्वा seeing, परमया very much, मुदा happy, युक्ता became, रामं चैव by Rama, अव्यथम् unhurt, दृष्ट्वा seeing, तुतोष became glad.

#### Explanation

Sita, daughter of Janaka, was happy to see the demons killed by Rama who himself was unhurt.

---

### Shloka 41

#### Shloka Text

ततस्तु तं राक्षससङ्घमर्दनं सभाज्यमानं मुदितैर्महर्षिभिः। पुनः परिष्वज्य शशिप्रभानना बभूव हृष्टा जनकात्मजा तदा।।3.30.41।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, शशिप्रभानना face bright like the Moon, जनकात्मजा Sita, daughter of Janaka, राक्षससङ्घमर्दनम् one who destroyed the demons, मुदितैः joyful, महर्षिभिः by the great sages too, सभाज्यमानम् worshipped, तम् him, पुनः again, परिष्वज्य embraced, तदा then, हृष्टा happy, बभूव was.

#### Explanation

Then the daughter of Janaka, cheerful Sita, who had a face bright like the moon, embraced Rama, destroyer of demons who was worshipped by the delighted sages. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीये आदिकाव्ये अरण्यकाण्डे त्रिंशस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the thirtieth sarga of Aranyakanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 31

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

त्वरमाणस्ततो गत्वा जनस्थानादकम्पनः। प्रविश्य लङ्कां वेगेन रावणं वाक्यमब्रवीत्।।3.31.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः thereafter, अकम्पनः Akampana, त्वरमाणः hastened, जनस्थानात् from Janasthana, गत्वा went, वेगेन hurriedly, लङ्काम् Lanka, प्रविश्य entered, रावणम् Ravana, वाक्यम् words, अब्रवीत् said.

#### Explanation

Quickly did Akampana leave Janasthana, and quickly he entered Lanka. He said to Ravana:

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

जनस्थानस्थिता राजन्राक्षसा बहवो हताः। खरश्च निहतस्सङ्ख्ये कथञ्चिदहमागतः।।3.31.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राजन् O king, जनस्थानस्थिताः dwelling in Janasthana, बहवः many, राक्षसाः demons, हताः are killed, सङ्ख्ये in war, खरश्च Khara too, निहतः  killed, अहम् I, कथञ्चित् with much difficulty, आगतः came.

#### Explanation

O king many demons dwelling at Janasthana including Khara have been killed. I manged to escape with much difficulty.

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

एवमुक्तो दशग्रीवः क्रुद्ध स्संरक्तलोचनः। अकम्पनमुवाचेदं निर्दहन्निव चक्षुषा।।3.31.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

एवम् in that way, उक्तः  been told, दशग्रीवः the tenheaded Ravana, क्रुद्धः angry, संरक्तलोचनः with reddened eyes, चक्षुषा by the eyes, निर्दहन्निव as if burning, अकम्पनम् to Akampana, इदम् this, उवाच spoke.

#### Explanation

When Akampana thus reported to the tenheaded Ravana, his eyes reddened with anger. He spoke to Akampana as if he was burning him with his eyes :

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

केन रम्यं जनस्थानं हतं मम परासुना। को हि सर्वेषु लोकेषु गतिं चाधिगमिष्यति।।3.31.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

परासुना  whose life is in the enemy's hand , केन by whom, मम my, रम्यम् beautiful, जनस्थानम् Janasthnam, हतम्  destroyed, सर्वेषु all, लोकेषु in the worlds, कः who, गतिम् death, अधिगमिष्यति will surely get.

#### Explanation

He who has destroyed my beautiful Janasthana is sure to die. His life is under the control of others (his enemies). Where can he be(alive) in all the worlds?

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

न हि मे विप्रियं कृत्वा शक्यं मघवता सुखम्। प्राप्तुं वैश्रवणेनापि न यमेन न विष्णुना।।3.31.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मे to me, विप्रियम् displeasure, कृत्वा having caused, मघवता by Indra, सुखम् happily, प्राप्तुम्  to have, न शक्यं हि not possible, वैश्रवणेनापि or by Kubera (the god of wealth), न nor, यमेन by Yama, न or, विष्णुना by Visnu, न or.

#### Explanation

One who has caused displeasure to me cannot be happy, be it Indra or Kubera, or Yama or even Visnu.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

कालस्य चाप्यहं कालो दहेयमपि पावकम्। मृत्युं मरणधर्मेण संयोजयितुमुत्सहे।।3.31.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अहम् I, कालस्य चापि to Yama also, कालः destroyer, पावकमपि fire also, दहेयम् I will burn, मृत्युम् to Yama, मरणधर्मेण  property of  death, संयोजयितुम् unite with, उत्सहे I can do.

#### Explanation

I am the destroyer of the destroyer (Yama). I can burn even that which burns (fire). I can infuse Death with the characteristics of death itself.

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

दहेयमपि सङ्कृद्धस्तेजसाऽदित्यपावकौ। वातस्य तरसा वेगं निहन्तुमहमुत्सहे।।3.31.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सङ्क्रुद्धः if I am angry, तेजसा by my radiance, आदित्य Sun, पावकौ अपि even fire, दहेयम् I can burn, तरसा instantly, वातस्य wind's, वेगम् speed, निहन्तुम् to arrest, अहम् I, उत्सहे effort.

#### Explanation

If angry, I can burn the Sun and even fire with my radiance. I can arrest  the speed of the wind instantly with my effort.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

तथा क्रुद्धं दशग्रीवं कृताञ्जलिरकम्पनः। भयात्सन्दिग्धया वाचा रावणं याचतेऽभयम्।।3.31.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अकम्पनः Akampana, कृताञ्जलिः with folded palms, तथा likewise, क्रुद्धम् angry, दशग्रीवम् Ravana, भयात् out of fear, सन्दिग्धया hesitatengly, वाचा speech, अभयम् safety, याचते sought.

#### Explanation

Akampana, out of fear, implored the inflamed Ravana with folded hands and faltering voice to spare him.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

दशग्रीवोऽभयं तस्मै प्रददौ रक्षसां वरः। स विस्रब्धोऽब्रवीद्वाक्यमसन्दिग्धमकम्पनः।।3.31.9।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रक्षसाम् of demons, वरः best one, दशग्रीवः the tenheaded Ravana, तस्मै to him, अभयम् protection, प्रददौ offered, सः अकम्पनः that Akampana, विस्रब्धः grateful, असन्दिग्धम् without hesitation, वाक्यम् these words, अब्रवीत् said.

#### Explanation

The tenheaded Ravana, lord of the demons, assured safety to Akampana, who  gratefully said these words without hesitation:

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

पुत्रो दशरथस्यास्ति सिंहसंहननो युवा। रामो नाम वृषस्कन्धो वृत्तायतमहाभुजः।।3.31.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सिंहसंहननः with the body like a lion's, युवा youth, दशरथस्य Dasaratha's, पुत्रः son, वृषस्कन्धः  shoulders like a bull's, वृत्तायतमहाभुजः round and strong arms, रामो नाम by name Rama, अस्ति he is.

#### Explanation

Dasaratha has  a young son,  by name, Rama. He has a body like a lion's. He has strong, round arms and his shoulders resemble those of a bull.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

वीरः पृथुयशाश्श्रीमानतुल्यबलविक्रमः। हतं तेन जनस्थानं खरश्च सह दूषणः।।3.31.11।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वीरः hero, पृथुयशाः of great fame, अतुल्यबलविक्रमः a man of unequal prowess, ते your, जनस्थानम् Janasthanam, हतम् destroyed, सहदूषणः along with Dusana, खरश्च Khara too.

#### Explanation

He is a hero of great fame and unequal prowess. He has destroyed your Janasthana and also Khara along with Dusana.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

अकम्पनवच श्रुत्वा रावणो राक्षसाधिपः। नागेन्द्र इव निश्वस्य वचनं चेदमब्रवीत्।।3.31.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षसाधिपः lord of the demons, रावणः Ravana, अकम्पनवचः Akampana's words, श्रुत्वाः having heard, नागेन्द्रः इव like the king of serpents, निश्वस्य after sighing heavily, इदम् these, वचनं च words too, अब्रवीत् said.

#### Explanation

Having heard Akampana's words, Ravana, the lord of demons, sighed heavily like the king of serpents and said these words:

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

स सुरेन्द्रेण संयुक्तो रामस्सर्वामरैस्सह। उपयातो जनस्थानं ब्रूहि कच्चिदकम्पन।।3.31.13।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अकम्पन Akampana, सः रामः that Rama, सुरेन्द्रेण by Indra, सर्वामरैस्सह along with all deities, संयुक्तः is united, जनस्थानम् to Janasthana, उपयातः कच्चित् came perhaps, ब्रूहि tell?

#### Explanation

Did Rama come to Janasthana with Indra and the gods ? O Akampana   speak.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

रावणस्य पुनर्वाक्यं निशम्य तदकम्पनः। आचचक्षे बलं तस्य विक्रमं च महात्मनः।।3.31.14।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रावणस्य Ravana's, तत् that, वाक्यम् words, निशम्य  heard, महात्मनः of the great Rama, तस्य his, बलम् strength, विक्रमं च exploits, पुनः again, अचचक्षे started telling.

#### Explanation

Hearing Ravana's words, Akampana continued telling about great Rama's strength and his exploits in war.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

रामो नाम महातेजा श्रेष्ठस्सर्वधनुष्मताम्। दिव्यास्त्रगुणसम्पन्नः पुरन्दरसमो युधि।।3.31.15।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रामो नाम Rama by name, महातेजाः very radiant, सर्वधनुष्मताम् among all the archers, श्रेष्ठः best , दिव्यास्त्र गुणसम्पन्नः owner of divine weapons, युधि in war, पुरन्दरसमः like Indra.

#### Explanation

Rama is his name. He looks very radiant. He is the best among the archers. Owner of divine weapons, he is comparable to Indra in war.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

तस्यानुरूपो बलवान्रक्ताक्षो दन्दुभिस्वनः। कनीयान्लक्ष्मणो नाम भ्राता शशिनिभाननः।।3.31.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

बलवान् strong, रक्ताक्षः of bright red eyes, दुन्दुभिस्वनः deep resonance of a drum, शशिनिभाननः face like the Moon, लक्ष्मणो नाम Lakshmana by name, तस्य his, अनुरूपः similar in appearance, कनीयान् younger, भ्राता brother.

#### Explanation

He is strong with bright red eyes. He has a deep voice with the resonance of a drum. His face is like the moon. Lakshmana, his younger brother, is similar to him in appearance.

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

स तेन सह संयुक्तः पावकेनानिलो यथा। श्रीमान्राजवरस्तेन जनस्थानं निपातितम्।।3.31.17।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

श्रीमान् prosperous , राजवरः best among kings, सः he, अनिलः wind, पावकेन यथा like with fire, तेन सह along with him, संयुक्तः united with, तेन by him, जनस्थानम् Janasthana, निपातितम् is pulled down.

#### Explanation

Rama is prosperous. He is the best among kings. Lakshmana with him is like wind with fire. Rama has pulled down Janasthana.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

नैव देवा महात्मानो नात्र कार्या विचारणा। शरा रामेण तूत्सृष्टा रुक्मपुङ्खाः पतत्रिणः।।3.31.18।। सर्पाः पञ्चानना भूत्वा भक्षयन्ति स्म राक्षसान्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महात्मानः great people, देवाः gods, नैव not, अत्र here, विचारणा investigate , न कार्यम् not to be done, रामेण by Rama, उत्सृष्टा  released, रुक्मपुङ्खाः goldenfeathered, पतत्रिणः  arrows, शराः darts, पञ्चाननाः fivehooded, सर्पाः serpents, भूत्वा  turned into, राक्षसान् demons, भक्षयन्ति स्म consumed.

#### Explanation

Indeed, they are great men. No god came there (to lend him a hand). There is no need to investigate further about it.  The goldenfeathered arrows released by Rama turned into fivehooded serpents and consumed the demons.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

येन येन च गच्छन्ति राक्षसा भयकर्शिताः।3.31.19।। तेन तेन स्म पश्यन्ति राममेवाग्रतः स्थितम्। इत्थं विनाशितं तेन जनस्थानं तवानघ।।3.31.20।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

भयकर्शिताः gripped by fear, राक्षसाः demons, येन येन by whichever (path), गच्छन्ति went, तेन तेन by the same way, अग्रतः in front of them, स्थितम् stationed, राममेव only Rama, पश्यन्ति स्म they saw, अनघ O Sinless Sire, तेन by him, तव your, जनस्थानम् Janasthana, इत्थम् in this manner, विनाशितम् destroyed.

#### Explanation

Wherever the demons went they, gripped by fear, only saw Rama in front. O sinless sire In this manner your Janasthana was destroyed by him.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

येन येन च गच्छन्ति राक्षसा भयकर्शिताः।3.31.19।। तेन तेन स्म पश्यन्ति राममेवाग्रतः स्थितम्। इत्थं विनाशितं तेन जनस्थानं तवानघ।।3.31.20।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

भयकर्शिताः gripped by fear, राक्षसाः demons, येन येन by whichever (path), गच्छन्ति went, तेन तेन by the same way, अग्रतः in front of them, स्थितम् stationed, राममेव only Rama, पश्यन्ति स्म they saw, अनघ O Sinless Sire, तेन by him, तव your, जनस्थानम् Janasthana, इत्थम् in this manner, विनाशितम् destroyed.

#### Explanation

Wherever the demons went they, gripped by fear, only saw Rama in front. O sinless sire In this manner your Janasthana was destroyed by him.

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

अकम्पनवचश्रुत्वा रावणो वाक्यमब्रवीत्। जनस्थानं गमिष्यामि हन्तुं रामं सलक्ष्मणम्।।3.31.21।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अकम्पनवचः Akampana's words, श्रुत्वा  hearing, रावणः Ravana, वाक्यम् words, अब्रवीत् said, सलक्ष्मणम् along with Lakshmana, रामम् Rama, हन्तुम् to kill, जनस्थानम् to Janasthana, गमिष्यामि will go.

#### Explanation

Hearing Akampana's words, Ravana said, I will go to Janasthana and kill Rama along with Lakshmana.

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

अथैवमुक्ते वचने प्रोवाचेदमकम्पनः। शृणु राजन्यथावृत्तं रामस्य बलपौरुषम्।।3.31.22।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

एवम् in that manner, वचने in those words, उक्ते saying, अकम्पनः Akampana, इदम् thus, प्रोवाच spoke, राजन् king, यथावृत्तम् what happened, रामस्य Rama's, बलपौरुषम् strength and valour, शृणु listen.

#### Explanation

Thus said by Ravana, Akampana responded, I will tell you what happened there. O king   listen to the strength and valour of Rama.

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

असाध्यः कुपितो रामो विक्रमेण महायशाः। आपगायास्सुपूर्णाया वेगं परिहरेच्छरैः।।3.31.23।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महायशाः man of great fame, रामः Rama, विक्रमेण in valour, असाध्यः irrepressible, कुपितः when outraged, सुपूर्णायाः in full spate, आपगायाः of a river, वेगम् speed, शरैः with arrows, परिहरेत् he may arrest.

#### Explanation

Rama is of great fame. When outraged, he is irrepressible. He can arrest with his arrows even the speed of a river (in spate).

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

सतारग्रहनक्षत्रं नभश्चाप्यवसादयेत्। असौ रामस्तु सीदन्तीं श्रीमानभ्युद्धरेन्महीम्।।3.31.24।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

श्रीमान् powerful, असौ रामस्तु this Rama, सतारग्रहनक्षत्रं with planets and stars, नभः चापि even the sky, अवसादयेत् can destroy, सीदन्तीम् drowning in the sea, महीम् earth, अभ्युद्धरेत् can lift up.

#### Explanation

Powerful Rama can pull down the sky along with the planets and stars. He can lift the drowning earth from the depth of the sea.

---

### Shloka 25

#### Shloka Text

भित्त्वा वेलां समुद्रस्य लोकानाप्लावयेद्विभुः। वेगं वापि समुद्रस्य वायुं वा विधमेच्छरैः।।3.31.25।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

विभुः overlord, समुद्रस्य of the sea, वेलाम् boundary of the sea, भित्त्वा after breaking, लोकान् worlds, आप्लावयेत् can drown, वेगं वापि also speed, वायुं वा or wind, शरैः by his arrows, विधमेत् arrest.

#### Explanation

He is so very powerful that he can drown the earth by breaking the boundary of the sea. He can arrest the speed of the sea or of the wind with his arrows.

---

### Shloka 26

#### Shloka Text

संहृत्य वा पुनर्लोकान्विक्रमेण महायशाः। शक्तस्सपुरुषव्याघ्रः स्रष्टुं पुनरपि प्रजाः।।3.31.26।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महायशाः of great fame, सः he, पुरुषव्याघ्रः tiger among men, पुनः again, लोकान् worlds, विक्रमेण by his valour, संहृत्य after destroying, प्रजाः creatures, पुनःअपि again, स्रष्टुम् to create, शक्तः has the power.

#### Explanation

He is a tiger among men. He is so powerful that he can destroy the world and recreate the creatures once again by his valour.

---

### Shloka 27

#### Shloka Text

न हि रामो दशग्रीव शक्यो जेतुं त्वया युधि। रक्षसां वापि लोकेन स्वर्गः पापजनैरिव।।3.31.27।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

दशग्रीव O tenheaded one, रामः Rama, युधि in war, त्वया by you, रक्षसाम् of demons, लोकेन by the world, वापि or ever, जेतुम् to win, स्वर्गः heaven, पापजनैरिव by sinners, न शक्यः not possible.

#### Explanation

O tenheaded one, just as it is not possible for sinners to attain heaven it is not possible for you or for the entire world of demons to win him in war

---

### Shloka 28

#### Shloka Text

न तं वध्यमहं मन्ये सर्वैर्देवासुरैरपि। अयं तस्य वधोपायस्तन्ममैकमनाश्शृणु।।3.31.28।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सर्वैः by all, देवासुरैरपि by gods and demons together, तम् him, वध्यम् victim, अहम् I, न मन्ये I do not think, तस्य his, वधोपाय: means to kill, अयम् this, तत् that, मम from me, एकमनाः singleminded attention, शृणु listen.

#### Explanation

I do not think it is possible for gods and demons together to kill him, yet there is a way for his destruction. You may listen to me with single mindedattention:

---

### Shloka 29

#### Shloka Text

भार्या तस्योत्तमा लोके सीता नाम सुमध्यमा। श्यामा समविभक्ताङ्गी स्त्रीरत्नं रत्नभूषिता।।3.31.29।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लोके in the world, उत्तमा foremost, श्यामा beautiful woman, समविभक्ताङ्गी woman of wellformed limbs, स्त्रीरत्नं jewel among women, रत्नभूषिता adorned with gems, सीता नाम Sita by name, सुमध्यमा of slender waist, तस्य his, भार्या wife.

#### Explanation

Rama has a wife in this world, by name, Sita, who is beautiful, has a slender waist and a wellformed body. Adorned with gems, she is a jewel among women,

---

### Shloka 30

#### Shloka Text

नैव देवी न गन्धर्वी नाप्सरा नापि दानवी। तुल्या सीमन्तिनी तस्या मानुषीषु कुतो भवेत्।।3.31.30।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तस्याः for her, तुल्या equal, सीमन्तिनी woman, देवी a goddess, नैव not there, गन्धर्वी gandharvi, न not, अप्सराः apsara, न not, दानवी अपि among demonesses, न not there, मानुषीषु among humans, कुतः how can, भवेत् she be.

#### Explanation

She has no equal among goddesses nor among gandharvis nor apasaras nor among demonesses. How can there be a peer of her among human beings ?

---

### Shloka 31

#### Shloka Text

तस्यापहर भार्यांत्वं प्रमथ्य तु महावने। सीतया रहितः कामी रामो हास्यति जीवितम्।।3.31.31।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

त्वम् you, महावने in the great forest, प्रमथ्य forcefully, तस्य his, भार्याम् wife, अपहर you may abduct, कामी passionate, रामः Rama, सीतया by Sita, रहितः separated from, जीवितम्  life, हास्यति will give up.

#### Explanation

Abduct Rama's wife by force in that great forest and bereft of Sita, he will give up his life.

---

### Shloka 32

#### Shloka Text

अरोचयत तद्वाक्यं रावणो राक्षसाधिपः। चिन्तयित्वा महाबाहुरकम्पनमुवाच ह।।3.31.32।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षसाधिपः king of demons, रावणः Ravana, तत् वाक्यम् those words, अरोचयत relished, महाबाहुः of strong arms, चिन्तयित्वा  pondering over, अकम्पनम् to Akampana, उवाच ह said.

#### Explanation

Ravana, the strongarmed king of demons, relished the words of Akampana and pondering over the matter, said to him :

---

### Shloka 33

#### Shloka Text

बाढं काल्यं गमिष्यामि ह्येकस्सारथिना सह। आनयिष्यामि वैदेहीमिमां हृष्टो महापुरीम्।।3.31.33।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

बाढम् surely, काल्यम् early in the morning, एकः alone, सारथिना सह along with charioteer, गमिष्यामि will go, हृष्टः gladly, वैदेहीम् to Vaidehi, इमां (महा)पुरीम् to this city, आनयिष्यामि will bring.

#### Explanation

Yes, early in the morning I will go alone gladly with the charioteer and bring Vaidehi here to this great city.

---

### Shloka 34

#### Shloka Text

अथैवमुक्त्वा प्रययौ खरयुक्तेन रावणः। रथेनादित्यवर्णेन दिशस्सर्वाः प्रकाशयन्।।3.31.34।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रावणः Ravana, एवम् so, उक्त्वा having said, अथ then, खरयुक्तेन yoked with donkeys, आदित्यवर्णेन resembling the colour of the Sun, रथेन by chariot, सर्वाः all, दिशः directions, प्रकाशयन् illuminating, प्रययौ left.

#### Explanation

Having said so, Ravana got on a chariot yoked with donkeys. It had the colour of the Sun. It illuminated all directions. Ravana left.

---

### Shloka 35

#### Shloka Text

स रथो राक्षसेन्द्रस्य नक्षत्रपथगो महान्। सञ्चार्यमाणश्शुशुभे जलदे चन्द्रमा इव।।3.31.35।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षसेन्द्रस्य of the king of demons, सञ्चार्यमाणः as he was going, नक्षत्रपथगः in the sky, महान् great,  सः he, रथः chariot, जलदे in the clouds, चन्द्रमाः इव like the Moon, शुशुभे shone.

#### Explanation

The great chariot of the demon king (Ravana) flying in the sky shone like the Moon in the cloud.

---

### Shloka 36

#### Shloka Text

स मारीचाश्रमं प्राप्य ताटकेयमुपागमत्। मारीचेनार्चितो राजा भक्ष्यभोज्यैरमानुषैः।।3.31.36।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः he, मारीचाश्रमम् hermitage of Maricha, प्राप्य entering, ताटकेयम् the son of Tataka, उपागमत् reached, राजा king, अमानुषैः unfit for human consumption, भक्ष्यभोज्यैः food to be chewed and to be eaten, मारीचेन by Maricha, अर्चित: offered.

#### Explanation

Ravana reached the hermitage of Maricha and met him who was the son of Tataka. The demon king was treated with food that can be chewed and food that can be eaten.

---

### Shloka 37

#### Shloka Text

तं स्वयं पूजयित्वा तु आसनेनोदकेन च। अर्थोपहितया वाचा मारीचो वाक्यमब्रवीत्।।3.31.37।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मारीचः Maricha, तम् to him, आसनेन by offering a seat, उदकेन च and water, स्वयम् himself, पूजयित्वा after offering obeisance, अर्थोपहितया in a meaningful manner, वाचा with words, वाक्यम् words, अब्रवीत् said.

#### Explanation

Maricha personally offered Ravana a seat. He offered him water and paid him obeisance. Making meaningful enquiries he said in appropriate words :

---

### Shloka 38

#### Shloka Text

कच्चित्सुकुशलं राजन्लोकानां राक्षसेश्वर। आशङ्के नाथ जाने त्वं यतस्तूर्णमिहागतः।।3.31.38।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षसेश्वर O lord of demons  राजन् king, लोकानाम् of the world, सुकुशलं कच्चित् hope every one is fine, नाथ O lord, यतः since, त्वम् you, तूर्णम् so quickly, इह here, आगतः have come, आशङ्के doubt, जाने know.

#### Explanation

O lord of demons  O king hope every one is fine in the world of demons. O lord, (though) your sudden arrival puts me in doubt (about everything going right).

---

### Shloka 39

#### Shloka Text

एवमुक्तो महातेजा मारीचेन स रावणः। ततः पश्चादिदं वाक्यमब्रवीद्वाक्यकोविदः।।3.31.39।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मारीचेन by Maricha, एवम् in this way, उक्तः having spoken, महातेजाः glorious, वाक्यकोविदः one who is eloquent in speech, सः he, रावणः Ravana, ततः then, पश्चात् later, इदम् these, वाक्यम् words, अब्रवीत् spoke.

#### Explanation

Having thus been asked by Maricha, glorious Ravana, who was eloquent in speech, spoke to him :

---

### Shloka 40

#### Shloka Text

आरक्षो मे हतस्तात रामेणाक्लिष्टकर्मणा। जनस्थानमवध्य तत्सर्वं युधि निपातितम्।।3.31.40।। तस्य मे कुरु साचिव्यं तस्य भार्यापहारणे।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तात O dear, अक्लिष्टकर्मणा by one who is capable of doing difficult deeds, रामेण by Rama, मे my, आरक्षः sentinel, हतः is killed, तत् it, सर्वम् all, अवध्यम् indestructible, जनस्थानम् Janasthana, युधि in war, निपातितम् have been pulled down, तस्य मे by him, तस्य his, भार्यापहारणे to abduct his wife, साचिव्यम् ministerial help, कुरु do.

#### Explanation

O dear, Rama who is capable of doing difficult deeds has killed my sentinels. The indestructible Janasthana has been routed in war. Could you help me in abudcting his wife ? (That is the only way to bring him to his knees).

---

### Shloka 41

#### Shloka Text

राक्षसेन्द्रवचश्श्रुत्वा मारीचो वाक्यमब्रवीत्।।3.31.41।। आख्याता केन सीता सा मित्ररूपेण शत्रुणा। त्वया राक्षसशार्दूल को न नन्दति नन्दितः।।3.31.42।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षसेन्द्रवचः words of the demon king, श्रुत्वा having heard, मारीचः Maricha, वाक्यम् these words, अब्रवीत् said, मित्ररूपेण prtending to be friend, शत्रुणा by your enemy, केन by whom, सा सीता about that Sita, आख्याता is reported, राक्षसशार्दूल tiger among demons, नन्दितः  delighted by you, कः who, त्वया by you, न नन्दति not enjoy.

#### Explanation

Having heard Ravana ,the king of demons, Maricha said these words:  O king, who is that enemy of yours who, in the guise of a friend,  has told you about Sita? Though obliged by you, who is he that is not happy with you, O tiger among demons?

---

### Shloka 42

#### Shloka Text

राक्षसेन्द्रवचश्श्रुत्वा मारीचो वाक्यमब्रवीत्।।3.31.41।। आख्याता केन सीता सा मित्ररूपेण शत्रुणा। त्वया राक्षसशार्दूल को न नन्दति नन्दितः।।3.31.42।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षसेन्द्रवचः words of the demon king, श्रुत्वा having heard, मारीचः Maricha, वाक्यम् these words, अब्रवीत् said, मित्ररूपेण prtending to be friend, शत्रुणा by your enemy, केन by whom, सा सीता about that Sita, आख्याता is reported, राक्षसशार्दूल tiger among demons, नन्दितः  delighted by you, कः who, त्वया by you, न नन्दति not enjoy.

#### Explanation

Having heard Ravana ,the king of demons, Maricha said these words:  O king, who is that enemy of yours who, in the guise of a friend,  has told you about Sita? Though obliged by you, who is he that is not happy with you, O tiger among demons?

---

### Shloka 43

#### Shloka Text

सीतामिहानयस्वेति को ब्रवीति ब्रवीहि मे। रक्षोलोकस्य सर्वस्य कश्शृङ्गं छेत्तुमिच्छति।।3.31.43।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सीताम् Sita, इह here, आनयस्व bring, इति this, कः who, ब्रवीति said, मे to me, ब्रवीहि you tell, कः who, सर्वस्य every one's, रक्षोलोकस्य world of demons, शृङ्गम् peak, छेत्तुम् to cut, इच्छति desirous.

#### Explanation

Tell me who asked you to bring Sita ? Who wishes to demolish the peak of the entire clan of demons ?

---

### Shloka 44

#### Shloka Text

प्रोत्साहयति कश्च त्वां स च शत्रुरसंशयः। अशीविषमुखाद्दंष्ट्रामुद्धर्तुं चेच्छति त्वया।।3.31.44।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कश्च who is he, त्वाम् to you, प्रोत्साहयति he encourages, स च he, शत्रुः enemy, असंशयः no doubt, त्वया by you, अशीविषमुखात् from the serpent's mouth, दंष्ट्राम् fangs, उद्धर्तुम् to pluck out, इच्छति desires.

#### Explanation

He who has encouraged you in this is your enemy, indeed. He wants you to pluck out the fangs from the serpent's mouth.

---

### Shloka 45

#### Shloka Text

कर्मणा केन केनासि कापथं प्रतिपादितः। सुखसुप्तस्य ते राजन् प्रहृतं केन मूर्धनि।।3.31.45।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

केन by whom, केन by what, कर्मणा by action, कापथम् an evil direction, प्रतिपादितः advised you, असि you are, राजन् O king, सुखसुप्तस्य of a man sleeping comfortably, ते to you, मूर्धनि केन on your forehead by whom, प्रहृतम् is hit.

#### Explanation

By  whose action have you been led into this wrong direction? Who has hit you on your forehead while you were sleeping comfortably?

---

### Shloka 46

#### Shloka Text

विशुद्धवंशाभिजनाग्रहस्त स्तेजोमदस्संस्थितदोर्विषाणः। उदीक्षितुं रावण नेह युक्तः स संयुगे राघवगन्धहस्ती।।3.31.46।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रावण O Ravana, विशुद्धवंशाभिजनाग्रहस्तः his pure pedigree is his trunk, तेजोमदः his brilliance to his ichor, संस्थितदोर्विषाणः his wellbuilt, strong arms are his tasks, सः that, राघवगन्धहस्ती intoxicated elephant named Rama, संयुगे in war, इह here, न युक्तः not possible, उदीक्षितुम् to look at Rama.

#### Explanation

Rama is like a mighty tusker. His pure and high birth is his trunk. His wellbuilt and strong arms are his tusks. His brilliance in his ichor. Nobody can face this intoxicated elephant in battle.

---

### Shloka 47

#### Shloka Text

असौ रणान्तः स्थितिसंधिवालो विदग्धरक्षोमृगहा नृसिंहः। सुप्तस्त्वया बोधयितुं न युक्तः शराङ्गपूर्णो निशितासिदंष्ट्रः।।3.31.47।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रणान्तःस्थितिसंधिवालः lion on warfront with its tail touching the waist when angry, विदग्धरक्षोमृगहा destroyer of the deer in the form of learned demons, शराङ्गपूर्णः having arrows as sharp nails all over the body, निशितासिदंष्ट्रः sharp fangs, सुप्तः asleep, असौ that Rama, नृसिंहः lion in the form of man, त्वया by you, बोधयितुम् to awaken, न युक्तः not proper.

#### Explanation

Rama is a lion in the form of man, a lion on warfront with its tail touching the waist when angry. This lion is deft in killing deer in the form of clever demons. His body is full of arrows. The sharp swords are his pointed teeth. It does not behove you to rouse such a lion who is asleep.

---

### Shloka 48

#### Shloka Text

चापापहारे भुजवेगपङ्के शरोर्मिमाले सुमहाहवौघे। न रामपातालमुखेऽतिघोरे प्रस्कन्दितुं राक्षसराज युक्तम्।।3.31.48।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षसराज O king of demons , चापापहारे steals his bow, भुजवेगपङ्के his mighty arms are whirlpools of mud, शरोर्मिमाले his arrows are rows of waves, सुमहाहवौघे in the flow of the great war, अतिघोरे fierce, रामपातालमुखे Rama is in the mouth of the netherworld, प्रस्कन्दितुम् to leap into, न युक्तम् not proper.

#### Explanation

O king of demons  it is not proper (for you) to jump into the extremely fierce mouth of the nether world known as Rama. Who can steal the bow from his mighty arms which are whirlpools of mud. His arrows are rows of waves in the flow of the great war.

---

### Shloka 49

#### Shloka Text

प्रसीद लङ्केश्वर राक्षसेन्द्र लङ्कां प्रसन्नो भव साधु गच्छ। त्वं स्वेषु दारेषु रमस्व नित्यं रामस्सभार्यो रमतां वनेषु।।3.31.49।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लङ्केश्वर O lord of Lanka, राक्षसेन्द्र chief of demons, प्रसीद be pleased, साधु it is proper, लङ्काम् to Lanka, गच्छ may go, प्रसन्नः be pleased, भव be, त्वम् you, नित्यम् always, स्वेषु your own, दारेषु by wives, रमस्व enjoy, रामः Rama, सभार्यः with his wife, वनेषु in the forest, रमताम् may enjoy.

#### Explanation

O lord of Lanka, chief of the demons, be pleased to return to Lanka and enjoy yourself in  the company of your wives. Let Rama enjoy with his wife in the forest.

---

### Shloka 50

#### Shloka Text

एवमुक्तो दशग्रीवो मारीचेन स रावणः। न्यवर्तत पुरीं लङ्कां विवेश च गृहोत्तमम्।।3.31.50।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation
