मारीचेन by Maricha, एवम् thus, उक्तः advised, दशग्रीवः the ten headed demon, सः रावणः that Ravana, लङ्कां पुरीम् to the city of Lanka, न्यवर्तत returned, गृहोत्तमम् best of homes, विवेश च entered.

#### Explanation

Thus advised by Maricha ,the ten headed Ravana returned and entered his best of homes in the city of Lanka . इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये अरण्यकाण्डे एकत्रिंशस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the thirtyfirst sarga of Aranyakanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 32

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

ततश्शूर्पणखा दृष्ट्वा सहस्राणि चतुर्दश। हतान्येकेन रामेण रक्षसां भीमकर्मणाम्।।3.32.1।। दूषणं च खरं चैव हतं त्रिशिरसा सह। दृष्ट्वा पुनर्महानादं ननाद जलदो यथा।।3.32.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः thereafter, शूर्पणखा Surpanakha, एकेन alone, रामेण by Rama, भीमकर्मणाम् of terrible deeds, रक्षसाम् of demons चतुर्दशसहस्राणि fourteen thousand, हतानि killed, दृष्ट्वा after seeing, दूषणं च and Dusana, खरं चैव also Khara, त्रिशिरसा सह including Trisira, हतम् killed, दृष्ट्वा  seeing, पुनः again, जलदः यथा like a cloud, महानादम्  great noise, ननाद produced.

#### Explanation

Seeing Dusana, Khara and also Trisira along with the fourteen thousand demon warriors of terrific performance killed by Rama, singlehanded, Surpanakha roared     like a cloud.

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

ततश्शूर्पणखा दृष्ट्वा सहस्राणि चतुर्दश। हतान्येकेन रामेण रक्षसां भीमकर्मणाम्।।3.32.1।। दूषणं च खरं चैव हतं त्रिशिरसा सह। दृष्ट्वा पुनर्महानादं ननाद जलदो यथा।।3.32.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः thereafter, शूर्पणखा Surpanakha, एकेन alone, रामेण by Rama, भीमकर्मणाम् of terrible deeds, रक्षसाम् of demons चतुर्दशसहस्राणि fourteen thousand, हतानि killed, दृष्ट्वा after seeing, दूषणं च and Dusana, खरं चैव also Khara, त्रिशिरसा सह including Trisira, हतम् killed, दृष्ट्वा  seeing, पुनः again, जलदः यथा like a cloud, महानादम्  great noise, ननाद produced.

#### Explanation

Seeing Dusana, Khara and also Trisira along with the fourteen thousand demon warriors of terrific performance killed by Rama, singlehanded, Surpanakha roared     like a cloud.

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

सा दृष्ट्वा कर्म रामस्य कृतमन्यैस्सुदुष्करम्। जगाम परमोद्विग्ना लङ्कां रावणपालिताम्।।3.32.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सा she, अन्यैः by others, सुदुष्करम् very difficult to do, रामस्य Rama's, कृतम् done, कर्म deed, दृष्ट्वा after seeing, परमोद्विग्ना getting very scared, रावणपालिताम् a placed ruled by Ravana, लङ्काम् Lanka, जगाम went.

#### Explanation

Seeing the performance of Rama which is very difficult for others, Surpanakha got scared and went to Lanka, ruled by Ravana.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

सा ददर्श विमानाग्रे रावणं दीप्ततेजसम्। उपोपविष्टं सचिवैर्मरुद्भिरिव वासवम्।।3.32.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सा Surpanakha, मरुद्भि: with Maruts, वासवम् इव like Indra, सचिवैः with ministers, उपोपविष्टम् seated, दीप्ततेजसम् shining brilliantly, रावणम् to Ravana, विमानाग्रे on top of the aerial chariot, ददर्श saw.

#### Explanation

Surpanakha saw Ravana shining brilliantly, seated like Indra along with ministers and Maruts on top of the aerial chariot.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

आसीनं सूर्यसङ्काशे काञ्चने परमासने। रुक्मवेदिगतं प्राज्यं ज्वलन्तमिव पावकम्।।3.32.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सूर्यसङ्काशे radiating like the Sun, काञ्चने golden, परमासने on a magnificent seat, आसीनम् seated, रुक्मवेदिगतम् at the golden altar, प्राज्यम् ज्वलन्तम् blazing, पावकम् इव like fire.

#### Explanation

Ravana, sitting on the magnificent golden seat on a golden altar looked radiant like the Sun or the blazing fire.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

देवगन्धर्वभूतानामृषीणां च महात्मनाम्। अजेयं समरे शूरं व्यात्ताननमिवान्तकम्।।3.32.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

देवगन्धर्वभूतानाम् of gods, gandharvas, all living beings, महात्मनाम् of great sages, ऋषीणां च and seers, समरे in war, अजेयम् invincible, शूरम् warrior, व्यात्ताननम् wide open jaws, अन्तकम् इव like Yama, lord of death.

#### Explanation

Invincible to gods, gandharvas, all living beings, great sages and seers, he was like Yama, lord of death with wide open jaws.

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

देवासुरविमर्देषु वज्राशनिकृतव्रणम्। ऐरावतविषाणग्रैरुद्घृष्टकिणवक्षसम्।।3.32.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

देवासुरविमर्देषु in encounter between gods and demons, वज्राशनिकृतव्रणम् wounded by missiles and thunderbolts,  ऐरावतविषाणाग्रैः by the tips of the tusks of elephant Airavata, उद्धृष्टकिणवक्षसम् marks of scars on the chest.

#### Explanation

He had scars of wounds caused by the thunderbolt received in war between gods and demons, and marks of scars on his chest made by tips of the the tusks of elephant Airavata.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

विंशद्भुजं दशग्रीवं दर्शनीयपरिच्छदम्। विशालवक्षसं वीरं राजलक्षण शोभितम्।।3.32.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

विंशद्भुजम् with twenty arms, दशग्रीवम् ten heads, दर्शनीयपरिच्छदम्  attractive attire, विशालवक्षसम् broadchested  वीरम् valiant , राजलक्षण शोभितम् with royal marks.

#### Explanation

Valiant Ravana had an attractive appearance with beautiful dresses twenty arms,ten heads, a broad chest  and brilliant royal marks.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

स्निग्धवैडूर्यसङ्काशं तप्तकाञ्चनकुण्डलम्। सुभुजं शुक्लदशनं महास्यं पर्वतोपमम्।।3.32.9।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

स्निग्धवैडूर्यसंकाशम् comparable to glittering vaiduryas, तप्तकाञ्चनकुण्डलम् wearing bright burnished golden earrings, सुभुजम्  strong arms, शुक्लदशनम् with white teeth, महास्यम्  wide mouth, पर्वतोपम् like a mountain.

#### Explanation

Wearing burnished golden earrings, he looked bright like the glittering vaidurya. And with his wellformed arms, white teeth and  wide mouth, he looked like a mountain.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

विष्णुचक्रनिपातैश्च शतशो देवसंयुगे। अन्यैश्शस्त्रप्रहारैश्च महायुद्धेषु ताडितम्।।3.32.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

देवसंयुगे while in war with gods, शतश: in hundreds  विष्णुचक्रनिपातैश्च hit with the wheel of Visnu, महायुद्धेषु in great wars, अन्यैः by others, शस्त्रप्रहारैश्च hit by several weapons, ताडितम् beaten.

#### Explanation

He was hit by the wheel of Visnu for hundreds of times during great wars and beaten by other weapons.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

अहताङ्गं समस्तैश्च देवप्रहरणैस्तथा। अक्षोभ्याणां समुद्राणां क्षोभणं क्षिप्रकारिणम्।।3.32.11।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तथा so also, समस्तै: by all, देवप्रहरणैः with the striking weapons of gods, अहताङ्गम् one whose limbs are not hurt, अक्षोभ्याणाम् of the imperturbable, समुद्राणाम् of the seas, क्षोभणम् one who causes agitation, क्षिप्रकारिणम् quick in action.

#### Explanation

Ravana whose body was not hurt by the weapons in war with gods, who could perturb even the imperturbable seas, was quick  in action.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

क्षेप्तारं पर्वतेन्द्राणां सुराणां च प्रमर्दनम्। उच्छेत्तारं च धर्माणां परदाराभिमर्शनम्।।3.32.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पर्वतेन्द्राणाम् of great mountains, क्षेप्तारम् who threw, सूराणां च of even gods, प्रमर्दनम् tormentor, धर्माणाम् of dharmas, उच्छेत्तारम् destroyer at the root, परदाराभिमर्शनम् one who casts evil eye on the wives of others.

#### Explanation

(He was) one who could lift and throw great mountains, torment even gods, violate  dharma at the root, and cast an evil eye on the wives of others.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

सर्वदिव्यास्त्रयोक्तारं यज्ञविघ्नकरं सदा। पुरीं भोगवतीं प्राप्य पराजित्य च वासुकिम्।।3.32.13।। तक्षकस्य प्रियां भार्यां पराजित्य जहार यः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सर्वदिव्यास्त्रयोक्तारम् employ all divine weapons, सदा always, यज्ञविघ्नकरम् one who creates obstacles for sacrifices, यः such, भोगवतीम् to the city of  Bhogavati, पुरीम् city, प्राप्य having reached, वासुकिम् Vasuki the sepentking, पराजित्य च  having defeated, तक्षकस्य Takshaka's, प्रियाम् dear, भार्याम् wife, पराजित्य having defeated, जहार abducted.

#### Explanation

He who could employ all divine weapons, disrupt sacrifices, had gone to the city of Bhogavati, defeated Vasuki and abducted the dear wife of Takshaka.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

कैलासपर्वतं गत्वा विजित्य नरवाहनम्। विमानं पुष्पकं तस्य कामगं वै जहार यः।।3.32.14।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

यः such (Ravana), कैलासपर्वतम् mount Kailasa, गत्वा on going, नरवाहनम् Kubera, विजित्य having conquered, तस्य his, कामगम्  which can fly at the rider's desire, पुष्पकम् aerial chariot called Pushpaka, जहार took away forcibly.

#### Explanation

Ravana went to mount Kailasa, conquered Kubera and took away forcibly his aerial chariot Pushpaka which can fly as per the rider's desire

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

वनं चैत्ररथं दिव्यं नलिनीं नन्दनं वनम्। विनाशयति यः क्रोधाद्देवोद्यानानि वीर्यवान्।।3.32.15।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वीर्यवान् valiant, यः he was, चैत्ररथम् Chaitraratha, the garden of Kubera, वनम् garden, नलिनीम् lotuspond, नन्दनं वनम् pleasure garden of Indra, देवोद्यानानि gardens of gods, क्रोधात् with anguish, विनाशयति destroyed.

#### Explanation

Out of anger he destroyed all the gardens of gods like Chitraratha of Kubera full of lotusponds and Nandana, the pleasuregarden of Indra.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

चन्द्रसूर्यौ महाभागावुत्तिष्ठन्तौ परन्तपौ। निवारयति बाहुभ्यां यश्शैलशिखरोपमः।।3.32.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

परन्तपौ scorcher of enemies, शैलशिखरोपमः like the peak of a mountain, महाभागौ of great prowess, चन्द्रसूर्यौ Moon and Sun, बाहुभ्याम् with his arms, निवारयति  prevents, उत्तिष्ठन्तौ while they rise.

#### Explanation

He was like the peak of a mountain. He could stop with his arms the great Moon and Sun from rising. He was a scorcher of enemies.

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

दशवर्षसहस्राणि तपस्तप्वा महावने। पुरा स्वयंभुवे धीरश्शिरांस्युपजहार यः।।3.32.17।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

धीरः steadfast, यः who, पुरा in the past, महावने in a dense forest, दशवर्षसहस्राणि ten thousand years, तपः penance, तप्त्वा after performing, स्वयंभुवे to the Creator,                                                                                                                                                                                               शिरांसि heads, उपजहार offered.

#### Explanation

This steadfast Ravana who had in the past performed penance for ten thousand years in a dense forest offered his heads to the selfborn Brahma.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

देवदानवगन्धर्वपिशाचपतगोरगैः। अभयं यस्य सङ्ग्रामे मृत्युतो मानुषादृते।।3.32.18।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

यस्य for whom, सङ्ग्रामे in war, मानुषात् ऋते except by humans, देवदानवगन्धर्वपिशाचपतगोरगैः by gods, demons, gandharvas, pisachas birds and snakes, मृत्युतः death, अभयम् fearless.

#### Explanation

He was unafraid of death in the hands of gods or demons or gandharvas or pisachas or birds or snakes in war except humans.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

मन्त्रैरभिष्टुतं पुण्यमध्वरेषु द्विजातिभिः। हविर्धानेषु यस्सोममुपहन्ति महाबलः।।3.32.19।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महाबलः mighty, यः he, अध्वरेषु during sacrifices, हविर्धानेषु at places fit for offering oblations, द्विजातिभिः by brahmins, अभिष्टुतम् extracted, मन्त्रै: by chanting Vedic hymns, पुण्यम् sacred, सोमम् Soma juice, उपहन्ति made impure.

#### Explanation

The mighty Ravana  defiled the sacred Soma juice, worthy of oblations, extracted by Brahmins by chanting mantras in scrifices.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

आप्तयज्ञहरं क्रूरं ब्रह्मघ्नं दुष्टचारिणम्। कर्कशं निरनुक्रोशं प्रजानामहिते रतम्।।3.32.20।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

आप्तयज्ञहरम् a disrupter of sacrifices at the climax, क्रूरम् a cruel one, ब्रह्मघ्नम् destroyer of  brahmins, दुष्टचारिणम् one of ruthless conduct, कर्कशम् a harsh one, निरनुक्रोशम् a merciless one, प्रजानाम् towards people, अहिते against their welfare, रतम् involved.

#### Explanation

He disrupted sacrifices at the final stage. He killed brahmins. He was ruthless, harsh, and  merciless. He was involved in activities against human welfare.

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

रावणं सर्वभूतानां सर्वलोकभयावहम्। राक्षसी भ्रातरं शूरं सा ददर्श महाबलम्।।3.32.21।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सा that, राक्षसी demoness i, सर्वभूतानाम् of all beings, रावणम्  one who makes others cry, सर्वलोकभयावहम्  creates fear among all beings, शूरम् a valiant, महाबलम् very strong, भ्रातरम् brother, ददर्श saw.

#### Explanation

That demoness , Surpanakha saw  her very strong,valiant brother Ravana who made all beings cry and created fear in all worlds

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

तं दिव्यवस्त्राभरणं दिव्यमाल्योपशोभितम्। आसने सूपविष्टं च कालकालमिवोद्यतम्।।3.32.22।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

दिव्यवस्त्राभरणम् resplendently attired and adorned with jewels, दिव्यमाल्योपशोभितम्  wearing beautiful garlands, आसने on the seat, सूपविष्टम् well seated, उद्यतम् on an elevated throne, कालकालमिव like death even to the lord of death, तम् him.

#### Explanation

Resplendently attired, adorned with jewels, wearing beautiful gardlands, seated on a raised throne, he(Ravana) was like death to even the lord of death

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

राक्षसेन्द्रं महाभागं पौलस्त्यकुलनन्दनम्। 3.32.23।। रावणं शत्रुहन्तारं मन्त्रिभिः परिवारितम्। अभिगम्याब्रवीद्वाक्यं राक्षसी भयविह्वला।।3.32.24।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षसी demoness, भयविह्वला terrified, राक्षसेन्द्रम् lord of demons, महाभागम् of great prowess, पौलस्त्यकुलनन्दनम् delight of the Paulastya race, शत्रुहन्तारम् destroyer of enemies, मन्त्रिभिः by ministers, परिवारितम् surrounded, रावणम् Ravana, अभिगम्य   after reaching, वाक्यम् these words, अब्रवीत् said.

#### Explanation

On reaching Ravana, the lord of demons, the delight of the Paulastya dynasty, and the destroyer of enemies surrounded by ministers, the terrified, Surpanakha said these words:

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

राक्षसेन्द्रं महाभागं पौलस्त्यकुलनन्दनम्। 3.32.23।। रावणं शत्रुहन्तारं मन्त्रिभिः परिवारितम्। अभिगम्याब्रवीद्वाक्यं राक्षसी भयविह्वला।।3.32.24।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षसी demoness, भयविह्वला terrified, राक्षसेन्द्रम् lord of demons, महाभागम् of great prowess, पौलस्त्यकुलनन्दनम् delight of the Paulastya race, शत्रुहन्तारम् destroyer of enemies, मन्त्रिभिः by ministers, परिवारितम् surrounded, रावणम् Ravana, अभिगम्य   after reaching, वाक्यम् these words, अब्रवीत् said.

#### Explanation

On reaching Ravana, the lord of demons, the delight of the Paulastya dynasty, and the destroyer of enemies surrounded by ministers, the terrified, Surpanakha said these words:

---

### Shloka 25

#### Shloka Text

तमब्रवीद्दीप्तविशाललोचनं प्रदर्शयित्वा भयमोहमूर्छिता। सुदारुणं वाक्यमभीतचारिणी महात्मना शूर्पणखा विरूपिता।।3.32.25।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अभीतचारिणी fearless wanderer, महात्मना great Lakshmana, विरूपिता disfigured, शूर्पणखा Surpanakha, भयमोहमूर्छिता overcome by fear, प्रदर्शयित्वा showing, दीप्तविशाललोचनम् of large, glittering eyes, तम् him, सुदारुणम् cruel, वाक्यम् these words, अब्रवीत् said.

#### Explanation

Showing her body disfigured by great Lakshmana, Surpanakha, the fearless wanderer, overwhelmed with fear said these cruel words  to Ravana sitting with large, glittering eyes: इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये अरण्यकाण्डे द्वात्रिंशस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the thirtysecond sarga of Aranyakanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 33

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

ततः शूर्पणखा दीना रावणं लोकरावणम्। अमात्यमध्ये सङ्क्रुद्धा परुषं वाक्यमब्रवीत्।।3.33.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, दीना distressed , शूर्पणखा Surpanakha, सङ्क्रुद्धा  anguished , अमात्यमध्ये in the midst of ministers, लोकरावणम्  who makes the world cry, रावणम् Ravana, परुषम् harsh, वाक्यम् these words, अब्रवीत् spoke.

#### Explanation

When the distressed, anguished Surpanakha met Ravana who makes the world weep, he was seated in the midst of his ministers. She addressed him these harsh words:

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

प्रमत्तः कामभोगेषु स्वैरवृत्तो निरङ्कुशः। समुत्पन्नं भयं घोरं बोद्धव्यं नावबुध्यसे।।3.33.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

प्रमत्तः intoxicated, कामभोगेषु  with the pleasures of passion, स्वैरवृत्तः  acting impulsively, निरङ्कुशः ungoaded, बोद्धव्यम्  which ought to be understood, समुत्पन्नम्  arising, घोरम् dreadful, भयम् fear, नावबुध्यसे do not understand.

#### Explanation

Intoxicated with the pleasures of passion, acting on your own, ungoaded and unguided, you do not understand the problems arising out of great fear, problems you ought to know.

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

सक्तं ग्राम्येषु भोगेषु कामवृत्तं महीपतिम्। लुब्धं न बहुमन्यन्ते श्मशानाग्निमिव प्रजाः।।3.33.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ग्राम्येषु vulgar, भोगेषु pleasures, सक्तम् addicted to, कामवृत्तम् who acts as he wishes, लुब्धम् greedy, महीपतिम् king, प्रजाः people, श्मशानाग्निमिव like the fire in the crematorium, न बहुमन्यन्ते will not respect.

#### Explanation

Lustful and greedy, you are addicted to vulgar pleasures. People will not respect you like the fire on the crematorium.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

स्वयं कार्याणि यः काले नानुतिष्ठति पार्थिवः। स तु वै सह राज्येन तैश्च कार्यैर्विनश्यति।।3.33.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पार्थिवः king, काले at the right time, कार्याणि actions, नानुतिष्ठति does not execute, राज्येन सह along with kingdom, तैः with them, कार्यैश्च सह along with works, विनश्यति  is destroyed.

#### Explanation

A king who does not act at the right time will be ruined along with the kingdom and its works.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

अयुक्तचारं दुर्दर्शमस्वाधीनं नराधिपम्। वर्जयन्ति नरा दूरान्नदीपङ्कामिव द्विपाः।।3.33.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अयुक्तचारम् without employment of spies, दुर्दर्शम्  not giving audience (to  people), अस्वाधीनम् not independent, नराधिपम् king, नराः people, द्विपाः elephants, नदीपङ्कामिव like a muddy river, दूरात् from a distance, वर्जयन्ति keep away.

#### Explanation

Just as elephants keep away from a muddy river, people will keep distance from the king who does not employ spies, does not give audience to his people and is not independent.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

ये न रक्षन्ति विषयमस्वाधीना नराधिपाः। ते न वृद्ध्या प्रकाशन्ते गिरयस्सागरे यथा।।3.33.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अस्वाधीनाः not independent, ये those, नराधिपाः kings, विषयम् their country, न रक्षन्ति do not protect, ते they, वृद्ध्या by intellect, सागरे in the sea, गिरयः यथा like the mountains, न प्रकाशन्ते do not shine.

#### Explanation

Kings who are not under their own control and cannot protect the country, do not attract the minds of the people like mountains inside sea water.

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

आत्मवद्भिर्विगृह्य त्वं देवगन्धर्वदानवैः। अयुक्तचारश्चपलः कथं राजा भविष्यसि।।3.33.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

त्वम् you, आत्मवद्भि: by the selfrespecting, देवगन्धर्वदानवैः by gods, gandharvas and demons, विगृह्य having made enmity, अयुक्तचारः  not appointed spies, चपलः fickleminded, कथम् how, राजा king, भविष्यसि will you be?

#### Explanation

Having created enmity with selfrespecting persons like gods, gandharvas and demons, with a fickle mind and without spies how long can you continue as king ?

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

त्वन्तु बालस्वभावश्च बुद्धिहीनश्च राक्षस। ज्ञातव्यन्तु न जानीषे कथं राजा भविष्यसि।।3.33.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षस O demon, त्वं तु you are, बालस्वभावश्च childish in nature, बुद्धिहीनश्च devoid of any intellect, ज्ञातव्यम् what ought to be known, न जानीषे you do not know, कथं how, राज king, भविष्यसि will you be?

#### Explanation

O demon  you are childish and foolish. You do not even know what ought to be known. How can you rule as a king?

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

येषां चारश्च कोशश्च नयश्च जयतां वर। अस्वाधीना नरेन्द्राणां प्राकृतैस्ते जनैस्समाः।।3.33.9।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

जयताम् of the victorious, वर best, येषाम् of those, नरेन्द्राणाम् of kings, चारश्च spy, कोशश्च treasury, नयश्च judiciary, अस्वाधीनाः not under control, ते those, प्राकृतैः with commoners, जनैः with people, समाः are equals.

#### Explanation

The best among the victorious kings, who are not masters of their espionage, judicial system and their exchequer are equal to the laity.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

यस्मात्पश्यन्ति दूरस्थान् सर्वानर्थान्नराधिपाः। चारेण तस्मादुच्यन्ते राजानो दीर्घचक्षुषः।।3.33.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

नराधिपाः kings, यस्मात् since, दूरस्थान् things at distant places, सर्वान् all, अर्थान् issues, चारेण by spies, पश्यन्ति see, तस्मात् for that, राजानः kings, दीर्घचक्षुषः farsighted, उच्यन्ते are called.

#### Explanation

Since kings can see things from a faroff place with the help of the spies they are called farsighted.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

अयुक्तचारं मन्ये त्वां प्राकृतैस्सचिवैर्वृतम्। स्वजनं तु जनस्थाने हतं यो नावबुद्ध्यसे।।3.33.11।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अयुक्तचारम् without employing spies, त्वाम् you, प्राकृतैः ignoramus, सचिवैः ministers, वृतम् surrounding, मन्ये consider, यः who, जनस्थाने at Janasthana, स्वजनम् your relations, हतम् killed, नावबुद्ध्यसे you do not know.

#### Explanation

Since you have not engaged any spy you do not know when your own relatives were killed in Janasthana. I take you to be a fool surrounded by ordinary ministers.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

चतुर्दश सहस्राणि रक्षसां क्रूरकर्मणाम्। हतान्येकेन रामेण खरश्च सहदूषणः।।3.33.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

एकेन by one alone, रामेण by Rama, क्रूरकर्मणाम् perpetrators of cruel acts, रक्षसाम् demons, चतुर्दश fourteen, सहस्राणि thousand, हतानि are killed, सहदूषणः and Dusana too, खरश्च Khara also.

#### Explanation

One Rama alone killed all the fourteen thousand demons including Dusana and Khara, demons  capable of fierce exploits.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

ऋषीणामभयं दत्तं कृतक्षेमाश्च दण्डकाः। धर्षितं च जनस्थानं रामेणाक्लिष्टकर्मणा।।3.33.13।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अक्लिष्टकर्मणा by effortless performer of formidable tasks, रामेण by Rama, ऋषीणाम् of the ascetics, अभयम् safety, दत्तम् is given, दण्डकाः Dandaka's, कृतक्षेमाश्च peace restored, जनस्थानम् Janasthanam, धर्षितं च is attacked.

#### Explanation

Rama, the effortless performer of formindable tasks, has assured safety to the sages. Peace and happiness was restored to Dandaka forest but Janasthana has been ravaged.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

त्वन्तु लुब्दः प्रमत्तश्च पराधीनश्च रावण। विषये स्वे समुत्पन्नं भयं यो नावबुध्यसे।।3.33.14।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रावण O Ravana, त्वं तु you are, लुब्धः  avaricious, प्रमत्तः intoxicated, पराधीनश्च under others control, यः who, स्वे his own, विषये kingdom, (सम्) उत्पन्नम् generated, भयम् fear, नावबुध्यसे not aware of.

#### Explanation

O Ravana, you are not aware of what  has happened to your own kingdom. You are avaricious, intoxicated, and under others' control. A great fear has been generated in your own kingdom.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

तीक्ष्णमल्पप्रदातारं प्रमत्तं गर्वितं शठम्। व्यसने सर्वभूतानि नाभिधावन्ति पार्थिवम्।।3.33.15।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तीक्ष्णम् sharp one, अल्पप्रदातारम् not a generous giver, प्रमत्तम् intoxicated, गर्वितम् proud, शठम् fraudulent, पार्थिवम् to the king, व्यसने in calamity, सर्वभूतानि all beings, नाभिधावन्ति not run for protection.

#### Explanation

All living beings stop seeking refuge under a king who is, not a generous giver, but intoxicated, harsh, arrogant and fraudulent. They do not run to him for protection in times of calamity.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

अतिमानिनमग्राह्यमात्मसम्भावितं नरम्। क्रोधिनं व्यसने हन्ति स्वजनोऽपि महीपतिम्।।3.33.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अतिमानिनम् extremely arrogant, अग्राह्यम् one who is not acceptable (to good people), आत्मसम्भावितं नरम् selfconceited, क्रोधिनम् cholorictempered, महीपतिम् king, स्वजनोऽपि his own people also, व्यसने in adversity, हन्ति will murder.

#### Explanation

A king who is extremely arrogant, who is not acceptable to good people, who is selfconceited and cholerictempered will be murdered even by his own people at the time of adversity.

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

नानुतिष्ठति कार्याणि भयेषु न बिभेति च। क्षिप्रं राज्याच्युतो दीनस्तृणैस्तुल्यो भविष्यति।।3.33.17।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कार्याणि works, नानुतिष्ठति he does not do, भयेषु fear, न बिभेति च not afraid of, क्षिप्रम् surely, राज्यात् from the kingdom, च्युतः toppled down, दीनः wretched, तृणैः with a blade of grass, तुल्यः equal, भविष्यति will be.

#### Explanation

A king who does not do what ought to be done, does not fear what ought to be feared, will surely be dislodged from the kingdom and live a wretched life like a blade of grass.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

शुष्कैः काष्ठैर्भवेत्कार्यं लोष्टैरपि च पांसुभिः। न तु स्थानात्परिभ्रष्टैः कार्यं स्याद्वसुधाधिपैः।।3.33.18।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

शुष्कैः dried up, काष्ठैः logs of wood, लोष्टैरपि even clods of earth, पांसुभिरपि च even with the dust, कार्यम् may have some value, भवेत् will be, तु but, स्थानात् from the throne, परिभ्रष्टैः thrown out, वसुधाधिपै by the kings, कार्यम् work, न स्यात् will not be.

#### Explanation

Even dried up logs, or clods of earth, or a handful of dust may have some value but not a king who is dethroned.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

अपभुक्तं यथा वासस्स्रजो वा मृदिता यथा। एवं राज्यात्परिभ्रष्टस्समर्थोऽपि निरर्थकः।।3.33.19।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

उपभुक्तम् worn and discarded, वासः cloth, यथा as, मृदिताः crushed, स्रजो वा even garlands, यथा as, एवम् in that way, राज्यात् from the kingdom, परिभ्रष्टः fallen, समर्थोऽपि even if he be capable, निरर्थकः worthless.

#### Explanation

Like a piece of cloth that is worn and discarded, like garlands put on  and crushed, a king dislodged from the kingdom is worthless even if he is capable.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

अप्रमत्तश्च यो राजा सर्वज्ञो विजितेन्द्रियः। कृतज्ञो धर्मशीलश्च स राजा तिष्ठते चिरम्।।3.33.20।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

यः राजा such king, अप्रमत्तः alert, सर्वज्ञः omniscient, विजितेन्द्रियः has control over his senses, कृतज्ञः grateful, धर्मशीलश्च one with moral conduct, सः राजा that king, चिरम् for a long time, तिष्ठते continues to rule.

#### Explanation

A king who is alert, omniscient, has control over his senses, has a sense of gratitude and one of righteous conduct continues to rule for a long time.

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

नयनाभ्यां प्रसुप्तोऽपि जागर्ति नयचक्षुषा। त्यक्तक्रोधप्रमादश्च स राजा पूज्यते जनैः।।3.33.21।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

नयनाभ्याम् with both the eyes, प्रसुप्तोऽपि even when asleep, नयचक्षुषा eye of justice, जागर्ति  who is awake, त्यक्तक्रोधः devoid of anger, अप्रमादश्च attentive man, सः राजा such a king, जनैः by people, पूज्यते is worshipped.

#### Explanation

The king who keeps both his eyes shut but keeps the eye of justice open, one who is free from anger and remains ever alert, is worshipped by the people.

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

त्वं तु रावण दुर्बुद्धिर्गुणैरेतैर्विवर्जितः। यस्य तेऽविदितश्चारै रक्षसां सुमहान्वधः।।3.33.22।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रावण Ravana, दुर्बुद्धि: evilminded, त्वं तु you are, एतैः by these, गुणैः qualities, विवर्जितः you are devoid of, यस्य whose, ते your, रक्षसाम् of demons, सुमहान् very great, वधः destruction, चारैः by spies, अविदितः was not known.

#### Explanation

O Ravana you are evilminded. You  are devoid of all these good qualities. Your spies could not know that there had been a great massacre of your fellowdemons.

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

परावमन्ता विषयेषु सङ्गतो न देशकालप्रविभागतत्ववित्। अयुक्तबुद्धिर्गुणदोषनिश्चये विपन्नराज्यो नचिराद्विपत्स्यसे।।3.33.23।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

परावमन्ता accusing others, विषयेषु in pleasures, सङ्गतः addicted, देशकालप्रविभागतत्ववित्  sense of judgement of place and time, गुणदोषनिश्चये discrimination of vice and virtue, अयुक्तबुद्धिः lacks wisdom, विपन्नराज्यः with loss of kingdom, नचिरात् in no time, विपत्स्यसे will destroy yourself.

#### Explanation

A king who accuses others, who is addicted to sensual pleasures, who lacks a sense of judgement of  place and time, a sense of discrimination between virtue and vice and the right wisdom will destroy himself and the kingdom in no time.

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

इति स्वदोषान् परिकीर्तितांस्तया समीक्ष्य बुद्ध्या क्षणदाचरेश्वरः। धनेन दर्पेण बलेन चान्वितो विचिन्तयामास चिरं स रावणः।।3.33.24।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

क्षणदाचरेश्वरः lord of nightwalkers, धनेन by wealth, दर्पेण by arrogance, बलेन च and by strength, अन्वितः heeding, सः रावणः that Ravana, इति thus, तया by her, परिकीर्तितान् mentioned, स्वदोषान् his mistakes, बुद्ध्या by his intellect, समीक्ष्य after reviewing, चिरम् for a long time, विचिन्तयामास started thinking over.

#### Explanation

Ravana, king of demons, wealthy, arrogant and mighty, listened to Surpanakha and pondered over the mistakes she mentioned. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये अरण्यकाण्डे त्रयस्त्रिंशस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the thirtythird sarga of Aranyakanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 34

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

ततश्शूर्पणखां दृष्ट्वा ब्रुवन्तीं परुषं वचः। अमात्यमध्ये सङ्क्रुद्धः परिपप्रच्छ रावणः।।3.34.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, अमात्यमध्ये in the midst of ministers, परुषम् harsh, वचः words, ब्रुवन्तीम् while speaking, शूर्पणखाम् to Surpanakha, दृष्ट्वा after seeing, सङ्क्रुद्धः enraged, रावणः Ravana, परिपप्रच्छ asked.

#### Explanation

Incensed at the harsh words Surpanakha used in the midst of ministers, Ravana said:

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

कश्च रामः कथंवीर्यः किंरूपः किंपराक्रमः। किमर्थं दण्डकारण्यं प्रविष्टस्सुदुरासदम्।।3.34.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रामः Rama, कः who is he, कथंवीर्यः what is his strength?, किंरूपः how does he look, किंपराक्रमः how valiant, सुदुरासदम् impenetrable, दण्डकारण्यम् Dandaka forest, किमर्थम् for what purpose?, प्रविष्टः he entered.

#### Explanation

Who is Rama? What is his strength? How does he look? How valiant is he? For what purpose has he entered the impenetrable Dandaka forest?

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

आयुधं किञ्च रामस्य निहता येन राक्षसाः। खरश्च निहतस्संख्ये दूषणस्त्रिशिरास्तथा।।3.34.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

येन by what, राक्षसाः demons, खरश्च and Khara, दूषणः Dusana, तथा likewise, त्रिशिराः Thrisira, सङ्ख्ये in war, निहताः killed, रामस्य Rama's, आयुधम् weapon, किम् what.

#### Explanation

With what weapons he has killed demons like Khara, Dusana and Trisira ?

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

इत्युक्ता राक्षसेन्द्रेण राक्षसी क्रोधमूर्छिता। ततो रामं यथातत्वमाख्यातुमुपचक्रमे।।3.34.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षसेन्द्रेण by the king of demons, इति thus, उक्ता spoken, क्रोधमूर्छिता overtaken by anger, राक्षसी the demoness ततः then, रामम् Rama, यथातत्वम् faithfully, आख्यातुम् to describe, उपचक्रमे she started.

#### Explanation

Thus questioned by Ravana, the demoness, overcome by anger, began telling him about Rama faithfully:

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

दीर्घबाहुर्विशालाक्ष श्चीरकृष्णाजिनाम्बरः। कन्दर्पसमरूपश्च रामो दशरथात्मजः।।3.34.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

दशरथात्मजः Dasaratha's son, रामः Rama, दीर्घबाहुः  long armed, विशालाक्षः large eyed, चीरकृष्णाजिनाम्बरः clad in deer skin and bark , कन्दर्पसमरूपश्च resembled Cupid৷৷

#### Explanation

Rama, Dasaratha's son, has long arms, large eyes. He is clad in deerskin and bark. He resembles Cupid.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

शक्रचापनिभं चापं विकृष्य कनकाङ्गदम्। दीप्तान् क्षिपति नाराचान्सर्पानिव महाविषान्।।3.34.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

शक्रचापनिभम् bow like Indra's, कनकाङ्गदम् gold armlets, चापम् bow, विकृष्य draws, महाविषान् highly poisonous, सर्पानिव snake like, दीप्तान् glittering, नाराचान् iron arrows, क्षिपति releases.

#### Explanation

His bow resembles Indra's, He is adorned with gold bands. Drawing the bow, he releases glittering arrows like highly poisonous snakes.

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

नाददानं शरान्घोरान्नमुञ्चन्तं शिलीमुखान्। न कार्मुकं विकर्षन्तं रामं पश्यामि संयुगे।।3.34.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

संयुगे in war, घोरान् dreadful, शरान् arrows, आददानम् drawing from the quiver, रामम् Rama, पश्यामि I see, शिलीमुखान्  darts, मुञ्चन्तम् released, न not, कार्मुकम् a bow, विकर्षन्तम् pulling apart, न not.

#### Explanation

I could not see Rama in the dreadful war while he was pulling an arrow from the quiver, aiming the dart, and drawing the bowstring.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

हन्यमानं तु तत्सैन्यं पश्यामि शरवृष्टिभिः। इन्द्रेणेवोत्तमं सस्यमाहतन्त्वश्मवृष्टिभिः।।3.34.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

इन्द्रेण by Indra, अश्मवृष्टिभिः by raining hailstones, आहतम् destroyed, उत्तमं सस्यमिव like the crop ready for harvest, शरवृष्टिभिः rain of arrows, हन्यमानम्  killed, तत् सैन्यम् that army, पश्यामि witnessed.

#### Explanation

Just as Indra destroys the crop ready for harvest by raining hailstones, I have seen Rama killing the army with a shower of arrows.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

रक्षसां भीमरूपाणां सहस्राणि चतुर्दश। निहतानि शरै स्तीक्ष्णैस्तेनैकेन पदातिना।।3.34.9।। अर्धाधिकमुहूर्तेन खरश्च सहदूषणः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पदातिना on foot, एकेन alone, तेन by him, तीक्ष्णैः  sharp, शरैः arows, अर्धाधिकमुहूर्तेन within the span of one and a half muhurta ( muhurtha is fortyeight minutes), भीमरूपाणाम् dreadful in appearance, रक्षसाम् demons, चतुर्दश fourteen, सहस्राणि thousand, निहतानि are killed, सहदूषणः also Dusana, खरश्च and Khara.

#### Explanation

Fighting alone on foot with his sharp arrows Rama killed within a span of one and a half muhurta, the fourteen thousand dreadful demons including Dusana and Khara.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

ऋषीणामभयं दत्तं कृतक्षेमाश्च दण्डकाः।।3.34.10।। एका कथञ्चिन्मुक्ताहं परिभूय महात्मना। स्त्रीवधं शङ्कमानेन रामेण विदितात्मना।।3.34.11।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ऋषीणाम् for seers, अभयम् assurance of safety, दत्तम् is given, दण्डकाः Dandaka forest, कृतक्षेमाश्च peace restored, महात्मना great soul, विदितात्मना  knower of self, स्त्रीवधम् slaying a woman, शङ्कमानेन  hesitant of, रामेण by Rama, अहम् I, एका only, परिभूय insulted, कथञ्चित् somehow, मुक्ता left with life.

#### Explanation

Sages have been assured safety. Peace has been restored to Dandaka forest. As Rama, the great soul, who knows the self, hesitated to kill a woman, I was alone saved after this humiliation.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

ऋषीणामभयं दत्तं कृतक्षेमाश्च दण्डकाः।।3.34.10।। एका कथञ्चिन्मुक्ताहं परिभूय महात्मना। स्त्रीवधं शङ्कमानेन रामेण विदितात्मना।।3.34.11।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ऋषीणाम् for seers, अभयम् assurance of safety, दत्तम् is given, दण्डकाः Dandaka forest, कृतक्षेमाश्च peace restored, महात्मना great soul, विदितात्मना  knower of self, स्त्रीवधम् slaying a woman, शङ्कमानेन  hesitant of, रामेण by Rama, अहम् I, एका only, परिभूय insulted, कथञ्चित् somehow, मुक्ता left with life.

#### Explanation

Sages have been assured safety. Peace has been restored to Dandaka forest. As Rama, the great soul, who knows the self, hesitated to kill a woman, I was alone saved after this humiliation.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

भ्राता चास्य महातेजा गुणतस्तुल्यविक्रमः। अनुरक्तश्च भक्तश्च लक्ष्मणो नाम वीर्यवान्।।3.34.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महातेजाः  radiant , गुणतः in quality, तुल्यविक्रमः of equal courage, अनुरक्तश्च very loyal, भक्तश्च devoted, वीर्यवान् valiant, लक्ष्मणो नाम Lakshmana by name, अस्य his, भ्राता brother.

#### Explanation

His brother called Lakshmana looks radiant. He is virtuous and courageous like him, valiant, devoted and loyal.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

अमर्षी दुर्जयो जेता विक्रान्तो बुद्धिमान्बली। रामस्य दक्षिणो बाहुर्नित्यं प्राणो बहिश्चरः।।3.34.13।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अमर्षी impatient , दुर्जयः invincible, जेता victorious, विक्रान्तः very bold, बुद्धिमान् intelligent, बली strong, नित्यम् always, रामस्य Rama, दक्षिणः बाहुः right hand, बहिश्चरः  moving outside, प्राणः his own life.

#### Explanation

He is impatient, invincible, victorious, bold, intelligent and strong. He is even the right hand of Rama, his outer life.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

रामस्य तु विशालाक्षी पूर्णेन्दुसदृशानना। धर्मपत्नी प्रिया भर्तुर्नित्यं प्रियहिते रता।।3.34.14।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

विशालाक्षी large eyed lady, पूर्णेन्दुसदृशानना face like the full moon, रामस्य Rama's, धर्मपत्नी consort, भर्तुः husband's, प्रियाः darling, नित्यम् always, प्रियहिते in the interest of her husband, रता engaged.

#### Explanation

Rama's beloved consort is a largeeyed lady. Her face is like the full moon. She is engaged in the wellbeing of her husband.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

सा सुकेशी सुनासोरुस्सुरूपा च यशस्विनी। देवतेव वनस्यास्य राजते श्रीरिवापरा।।3.34.15।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सुकेशी she has long hair, सुनासोरुः has beautiful nose and thighs, सुरूपा च of good look, यशस्विनी illustrious lady, सा she, अस्य of this, वनस्य of the forest, देवतेव like the presiding deity, अपरा another, श्रीरिव like goddess of wealth, राजते shining.

#### Explanation

She is an illustrious lady. She is goodlooking with her long hair, wellshaped nose and things. She appears to be the presiding deity of the forest, another goddess of wealth (Laxmi).

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

तप्तकाञ्चनवर्णाभा रक्ततुङ्गनखी शुभा। सीता नाम वरारोहा वैदेही तनुमध्यमा।।3.34.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तप्तकाञ्चनवर्णाभा a lady of shining golden hue, रक्ततुङ्गनखी of red, pointed nails, शुभा auspicious, वैदेही daughter of Videha, तनुमध्यमा of slender waist, सीता नाम by name Sita, वरारोहा of heavy hips.

#### Explanation

She is Sita, princess of Videha, a virtuous lady of golden complexion, a beautiful lady of slender waist and heavy hips.

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

नैव देवी न गन्धर्वी न यक्षी न च किन्नरी। नैवंरूपा मया नारी दृष्टपूर्वा महीतले।।3.34.17।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

एवंरूपा such a beauty, देवी goddess, मया myself, नैव दृष्टपूर्वा not seen earlier, गन्धर्वी gandharvi, न not, यक्षी yakshi, न not, किन्नरी kinneri, न not, नारी woman, महीतले on this earth, न not.

#### Explanation

I had not seen such a beautiful woman earlier, among the gandharvas, or yakshas, or kinneras. I have not seen such a beauty among the humans on earth.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

यस्य सीता भवेद्भार्या यं च हृष्टा परिष्वजेत्। अतिजीवेत्स सर्वेषु लोकेष्वपि पुरन्दरात्।।3.34.18।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

यस्य whose, सीता Sita, भार्या as wife, भवेत् shall be, हृष्टा happily, यम् whom ever, परिष्वजेत्  she may embrace, सः he, सर्वेषु in all, लोकेषु in the worlds, पुरन्दरात् अपि more than Indra, the breaker of fortress, अतिजीवेत् may wish to live long.

#### Explanation

Whosoever gets Sita as his wife, whoever she embraces happily will wish to live longer than Indra, the breaker of fortresses in this world.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

सा सुशीला वपुश्श्लाघ्या रूपेणाप्रतिमा भुवि। तवानुरूपा भार्या स्यात्त्वं च तस्यास्तथा पतिः।।3.34.19।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सुशीला a woman of good conduct, वपुश्लाघ्या is a woman of praiseworthy beauty, रूपेण by appearance, भुवि on this earth, अप्रतिमा incomparable, सा Sita, तव your, अनुरूपा suitable  for you, भार्या wife, स्यात् will be, तथा similarly, त्वम् you, तस्याः to her, पतिः suitable husband.

#### Explanation

She is a woman of good conduct, of praiseworthy beauty. She is matchless in her beauty on earth. She will be a suitable wife for you and you, a suitable husband for her.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

तां तु विस्तीर्णजघनां पीनश्रोणिपयोधराम्। भार्यार्थे च तवानेतुमुद्यताहं वराननाम्।।3.34.20।। विरूपितास्मि क्रूरेण लक्ष्मणेन महाभुज।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महाभुज O mightyarmed one, विस्तीर्णजघनाम् of broad thighs, पीनश्रोणिपयोधराम् a lady of pointed breasts and high hips, तां वराननाम् of beautiful face, तव your, भार्यार्थे as your wife to be, आनेतुम् to bring, उद्यता I have tried, अहम् I, क्रूरेण by the cruel, लक्ष्मणेन by Lakshmana, विरूपिता अस्मि have been disfigured.

#### Explanation

O mightyarmed one  I have been disfigured by cruel Lakshmana as I was attempting to bring that lady of broad thighs, pointed breasts, heavy hips and a beautiful face to make her your wife.

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

तां तु दृष्ट्वाद्य वैदेहीं पूर्णचन्द्रनिभाननाम्। मन्मथस्य शराणां वै त्वं विधेयो भविष्यसि।।3.34.21।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पूर्णचन्द्रनिभाननाम् a lady with a face like the full Moon, ताम् her, वैदेहीम् Vaidehi, अद्य now, दृष्ट्वा by seeing, त्वम् you, मन्मथस्य of Cupid, शराणाम् arrows, विधेयः be a victim, भविष्यसि will be.

#### Explanation

If you see her today, this lady with a face like the fullmoon, you will (instantaneously) fall a victim to the arrows of the god of love.

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

यदि तस्यामभिप्रायो भार्यार्थे तव जायते। शीघ्रमुद्ध्रियतां पादो जयार्थमिह दक्षिणः।।3.34.22।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तस्याम् in her, तव your, भार्यार्थे desire to make her your wife, अभिप्रायः intention, जायते यदि if it arises, इह from here, जयार्थम् to win her over, दक्षिणः पादः your right foot, शीघ्रम् quickly, उद्घ्रियताम् may be raised.

#### Explanation

If you intend her to be your wife, raise your right foot now and proceed to win her over.

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

कुरु प्रियं तथा तेषां रक्षसां राक्षसेश्वर। वधात्तस्य नृशंसस्य रामस्याश्रमवासिनः।।3.34.23।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षसेश्वर O lord of the demons  तथा such, नृशंसस्य cruel ones, आश्रमवासिनः of the dweller of the hermitage, तस्य रामस्य of that Rama, वधात् by killing, तेषाम् of those, रक्षसाम् of demons, प्रियम् favour, कुरु do.

#### Explanation

O lord of the demons, do a favour to your clan by  killing that cruel Rama dwelling in the hermitage.

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

तं शरैर्निशितैर्हत्वा लक्ष्मणं च महारथम्। हतनाथां सुखं सीतां यथावदुपभोक्ष्यसि।।3.34.24।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महारथम् to that great warrior, तं that, लक्ष्मणं च Lakshmana also, निशितैः with sharp ones, शरैः with arrows, हत्वा after killing, हतनाथाम्  with her husband killed, सीताम् Sita, सुखम् happily, यथावत् as you like, उपभोक्ष्यसि you can enjoy.

#### Explanation

Kill Rama, that great warrior along with Lakshmana with sharp arrows and enjoy the  company of Sita happily as you like, with her husband dead.

---

### Shloka 25

#### Shloka Text

रोचते यति ते वाक्यं ममैतद्राक्षसेश्वर। क्रियतां निर्विशङ्केन वचनं मम रावण।।3.34.25।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षसेश्वर O lord of the demons  रावण Ravana, मम my, एतत् all this, वाक्यं word ते to you, रोचते यदि if you like, निर्विशङ्केन without any hesitation, मम mine, वचनम् words, क्रियताम् do as I said.

#### Explanation

If you like what I have said, do accordingly without any hesitation, O Ravana, lord of the demons.

---

### Shloka 26

#### Shloka Text

विज्ञायेहात्मशक्तिं च ह्रियतामबला बलात्। सीता सर्वानवद्याङ्गी भार्यार्थे राक्षसेश्वर।।3.34.26।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षसेश्वर O Lord of the demons, आत्मशक्तिम् your own strength, विज्ञाय  assessing,  सर्वानवद्याङ्गी charming in all respects, अबला delicate, सीता Sita, भार्यार्थे as wife, बलात् forcefully, ह्रियताम् abduct.

#### Explanation

Assess your own strength, O Lord of the demons, before you forcefully abduct that delicate Sita who is charming in all respects and make her your wife.

---

### Shloka 27

#### Shloka Text

निशम्य रामेण शरैरजिह्मगै र्हतान् जनस्थानगतान्निशाचरान्। खरं च बुद्ध्वा निहतं च दूषणं त्वमत्र कृत्यं प्रतिपत्तुमर्हसि।।3.34.27।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रामेण by Rama, अजिह्मगैः not moving obliquely, शरैः by arrows, जनस्थानगतान्  staying at Janasthana, निशाचरान् nightwalkers, demons, हतान् killed, निशम्य after hearing, खरं च Khara and, दूषणं च Dusana, निहतम् killed, बुद्ध्वा after knowing, त्वम् you, अत्र here, कृत्यम् your duty, प्रतिपत्तुम् to decide, अर्हसि should.

#### Explanation

On hearing about the death of the demons including Khara and Dusana who fell to the straight and swift arrows of Rama, you should decide your course of action. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये अरण्यकाण्डे चतुस्त्रिंशस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the thirtytfourth sarga of Aranyakanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 35

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

ततश्शूर्पणखावाक्यं तच्छ्रुत्वा रोमहर्षणम्। सचिवानभ्यनुज्ञाय कार्यं बुद्ध्वा जगाम ह।।3.35.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, रोमहर्षणम् horripilating, तत् that way, शूर्पणखावाक्यम् words of Surpanakha, श्रुत्वा on hearing, सचिवान् ministers, अभ्यनुज्ञाय  took leave, कार्यम् task, बुद्ध्वा thinking over, जगाम ह went.

#### Explanation

Then hearing the horripilating words of Surpanakha, Ravana took leave of the ministers and went thinking about the task ahead of him.

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

तत्कार्यमनुगम्याथ यथावदुपलभ्य च। दोषाणां च गुणानां च सम्प्रधार्य बलाबलम्।3.35.2।। इति कर्तव्यमित्येव कृत्वा निश्चयमात्मनः। स्थिरबुद्धिस्ततो रम्यां यानशालां जगाम ह।।3.35.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अथ and, तत् that, कार्यम् task, अनुगम्य thinking deeply, यथावत् duly, उपलभ्य च understanding, दोषाणाम् of demerits, गुणानां च merits, बलाबलम् strength and weakness, सम्प्रधार्य reflected on it, इति this, कर्तव्यम् duty, इत्येव that way, आत्मनः in himself, निश्चयम् decided, कृत्वा did, स्थिरबुद्धि: firmly, ततः then, रम्यां delightful, यानशालाम् coachshed, जगाम ह went.

#### Explanation

Deliberating on the task ahead, duly considering the pros and cons, demerits and merits, strength and weakness, of the issue and having firmly decided within himself, he left for the beautiful coachshed.

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

तत्कार्यमनुगम्याथ यथावदुपलभ्य च। दोषाणां च गुणानां च सम्प्रधार्य बलाबलम्।3.35.2।। इति कर्तव्यमित्येव कृत्वा निश्चयमात्मनः। स्थिरबुद्धिस्ततो रम्यां यानशालां जगाम ह।।3.35.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अथ and, तत् that, कार्यम् task, अनुगम्य thinking deeply, यथावत् duly, उपलभ्य च understanding, दोषाणाम् of demerits, गुणानां च merits, बलाबलम् strength and weakness, सम्प्रधार्य reflected on it, इति this, कर्तव्यम् duty, इत्येव that way, आत्मनः in himself, निश्चयम् decided, कृत्वा did, स्थिरबुद्धि: firmly, ततः then, रम्यां delightful, यानशालाम् coachshed, जगाम ह went.

#### Explanation

Deliberating on the task ahead, duly considering the pros and cons, demerits and merits, strength and weakness, of the issue and having firmly decided within himself, he left for the beautiful coachshed.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

यानशालां ततो गत्वा प्रच्छन्नो राक्षसाधिपः। सूतं सञ्चोदयामास रथस्संयोज्यतामिति।।3.35.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, राक्षसाधिपः the demonking, प्रच्छन्न: covertly, यानशालाम् to the coachshed, गत्वा having gone, रथः chariot, संयोज्यताम् harness, इति thus, सूतम् charioteer, सञ्चोदयामास ordered.

#### Explanation

Ravana went covertly to the coachshed and to the charioteer to get a chariot harnessed.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

एवमुक्तः क्षेणेनैव सारथिर्लघुविक्रमः। रथं संयोजयामास तस्याभिमतमुत्तमम्।।3.35.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

एवम् that way, उक्तः said, लघुविक्रमः a man of quick action, सारथिः charioteer, तस्य his, अभिमतम् choice, उत्तमम् great, रथम् chariot, क्षणेनैव in an instant, संयोजयामास harnessed.

#### Explanation

Thus ordered, the smart charioteer got the chariot of his choice harnessed in an instant.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

काञ्चनं रथमास्थाय कामगं रत्नभूषितम्। पिशाचवदनैर्युक्तं खरैः काञ्चनभूषणैः।।3.35.6।। मेघप्रतिमनादेन स तेन धनदानुजः। राक्षसाधिपतिश्श्रीमान्ययौ नदनदीपतिम्।।3.35.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

धनदानुजः younger brother of Kubera, श्रीमान् prosperous, सः राक्षसाधिपतिः the lord of the demons, काञ्चनभूषणैः embellished with gold ornaments, पिशाचवदनैः with demonfaced, खरैः donkeys, युक्तम् yoked, काञ्चनम् golden, कामगम्   go where one desires, रत्नभूषितम् decked with gems, रथम् chariot, आस्थाय riding, मेघप्रतिमनादेन rumbling like a cloud, तेन that Ravana, नदनदीपतिम् towards the ocean, ययौ went.

#### Explanation

The younger brother of Kubera (Ravana), the prosperous lord of the demons ascended the chariot which could go where he desired. Embellished with gold, studded with gems, harnessed with demonfaced donkeys, the chariot rolled towards the ocean, rumbling like a cloud.

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

काञ्चनं रथमास्थाय कामगं रत्नभूषितम्। पिशाचवदनैर्युक्तं खरैः काञ्चनभूषणैः।।3.35.6।। मेघप्रतिमनादेन स तेन धनदानुजः। राक्षसाधिपतिश्श्रीमान्ययौ नदनदीपतिम्।।3.35.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

धनदानुजः younger brother of Kubera, श्रीमान् prosperous, सः राक्षसाधिपतिः the lord of the demons, काञ्चनभूषणैः embellished with gold ornaments, पिशाचवदनैः with demonfaced, खरैः donkeys, युक्तम् yoked, काञ्चनम् golden, कामगम्   go where one desires, रत्नभूषितम् decked with gems, रथम् chariot, आस्थाय riding, मेघप्रतिमनादेन rumbling like a cloud, तेन that Ravana, नदनदीपतिम् towards the ocean, ययौ went.

#### Explanation

The younger brother of Kubera (Ravana), the prosperous lord of the demons ascended the chariot which could go where he desired. Embellished with gold, studded with gems, harnessed with demonfaced donkeys, the chariot rolled towards the ocean, rumbling like a cloud.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

स श्वेतवालव्यजनः श्वेतच्छत्रो दशाननः। स्निग्धवैदूर्यसंकाश स्तप्तकाञ्चनकुण्डलः।।3.35.8।। विंशद्भुजो दशग्रीवो दर्शनीयपरिच्छदः। त्रिदशारिर्मुनीन्द्रघ्नो दशशीर्ष इवाद्रिराट्।।3.35.9।। कामगं रथमास्थाय शुशुभे राक्षसेश्वरः। विद्युन्मण्डलवान्मेघस्सबलाक इवाम्बरे।।3.35.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

श्वेतवालव्यजनः with white yak tail fans, श्वेतच्छत्रः with white parasols, दशाननः ten headed, स्निग्धवैदूर्यसङ्काशः shining like smooth Vaidurya (Lapis Lazuli), तप्तकाञ्चनकुण्डलः wearing pure gold earrings, विंशद्भुजः twenty hands, दशग्रीवः tennecked, दर्शनीयपरिच्छदः attired in attractive robes, त्रिदशारिः enemy of gods, मुनीन्द्रघ्नः slayer of towering sages, दशशीर्षः tenpeaks, अद्रिराडिव like king of mountains, सः राक्षसेश्वरः that lord of demons, कामगम् takes where  one desires to go, रथम् chariot, आस्थाय having ascended, अम्बरे sky, विद्युन्मण्डलवान् encircled by lightnings, सबलाकः accompanied by cranes, मेघः इव cloudlike, शुशुभे shone.

#### Explanation

Ravana, enemy of the gods, slayer of towering sages, with white yaktail fans, and white parasols, shining like smooth vaidurya, wearing pure gold earrings, clad in attractive attire, ten necks with twenty strong hands, ten heads looking like ten mountain peaks, a cloud in the sky encircled by lightnings and accompanied by cranes, ascended the chariot that could go wherever he desired.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

स श्वेतवालव्यजनः श्वेतच्छत्रो दशाननः। स्निग्धवैदूर्यसंकाश स्तप्तकाञ्चनकुण्डलः।।3.35.8।। विंशद्भुजो दशग्रीवो दर्शनीयपरिच्छदः। त्रिदशारिर्मुनीन्द्रघ्नो दशशीर्ष इवाद्रिराट्।।3.35.9।। कामगं रथमास्थाय शुशुभे राक्षसेश्वरः। विद्युन्मण्डलवान्मेघस्सबलाक इवाम्बरे।।3.35.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

श्वेतवालव्यजनः with white yak tail fans, श्वेतच्छत्रः with white parasols, दशाननः ten headed, स्निग्धवैदूर्यसङ्काशः shining like smooth Vaidurya (Lapis Lazuli), तप्तकाञ्चनकुण्डलः wearing pure gold earrings, विंशद्भुजः twenty hands, दशग्रीवः tennecked, दर्शनीयपरिच्छदः attired in attractive robes, त्रिदशारिः enemy of gods, मुनीन्द्रघ्नः slayer of towering sages, दशशीर्षः tenpeaks, अद्रिराडिव like king of mountains, सः राक्षसेश्वरः that lord of demons, कामगम् takes where  one desires to go, रथम् chariot, आस्थाय having ascended, अम्बरे sky, विद्युन्मण्डलवान् encircled by lightnings, सबलाकः accompanied by cranes, मेघः इव cloudlike, शुशुभे shone.

#### Explanation

Ravana, enemy of the gods, slayer of towering sages, with white yaktail fans, and white parasols, shining like smooth vaidurya, wearing pure gold earrings, clad in attractive attire, ten necks with twenty strong hands, ten heads looking like ten mountain peaks, a cloud in the sky encircled by lightnings and accompanied by cranes, ascended the chariot that could go wherever he desired.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

स श्वेतवालव्यजनः श्वेतच्छत्रो दशाननः। स्निग्धवैदूर्यसंकाश स्तप्तकाञ्चनकुण्डलः।।3.35.8।। विंशद्भुजो दशग्रीवो दर्शनीयपरिच्छदः। त्रिदशारिर्मुनीन्द्रघ्नो दशशीर्ष इवाद्रिराट्।।3.35.9।। कामगं रथमास्थाय शुशुभे राक्षसेश्वरः। विद्युन्मण्डलवान्मेघस्सबलाक इवाम्बरे।।3.35.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

श्वेतवालव्यजनः with white yak tail fans, श्वेतच्छत्रः with white parasols, दशाननः ten headed, स्निग्धवैदूर्यसङ्काशः shining like smooth Vaidurya (Lapis Lazuli), तप्तकाञ्चनकुण्डलः wearing pure gold earrings, विंशद्भुजः twenty hands, दशग्रीवः tennecked, दर्शनीयपरिच्छदः attired in attractive robes, त्रिदशारिः enemy of gods, मुनीन्द्रघ्नः slayer of towering sages, दशशीर्षः tenpeaks, अद्रिराडिव like king of mountains, सः राक्षसेश्वरः that lord of demons, कामगम् takes where  one desires to go, रथम् chariot, आस्थाय having ascended, अम्बरे sky, विद्युन्मण्डलवान् encircled by lightnings, सबलाकः accompanied by cranes, मेघः इव cloudlike, शुशुभे shone.

#### Explanation

Ravana, enemy of the gods, slayer of towering sages, with white yaktail fans, and white parasols, shining like smooth vaidurya, wearing pure gold earrings, clad in attractive attire, ten necks with twenty strong hands, ten heads looking like ten mountain peaks, a cloud in the sky encircled by lightnings and accompanied by cranes, ascended the chariot that could go wherever he desired.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

स शैलं सागरानूपं वीर्यवानवलोकयन्। नानापुष्पफलैर्वृक्षैरनुकीर्णं सहस्रशः।।3.35.11।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वीर्यवान् valiant, सः he, शैलम् the mountain, सहस्रशः thousandfold, नानापुष्पफलैः varieties of flowers and fruits, वृक्षैः with trees, अनुकीर्णम् full of, सागरानूपम् shore of the sea, अवलोकयन् seeing.

#### Explanation

Valiant Ravana wentwatching the mountains full of thousands of trees bearing flowers and fruits on the shore of the sea.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

शीतमङ्गलतोयाभिः पद्मिनीभिस्समन्ततः। विशालैराश्रमपदैर्वेदिमद्भिस्समावृतम्।।3.35.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

शीतमङ्गलतोयाभिः by those filled with cool, sacred water, पद्मिनीभिः with lotusladen ponds, वेदिमद्भि: having elevated altars, विशालैः sprawlling one, आश्रमपदैः with hermitages, समन्ततः on all sides, समावृतम् surrounded.

#### Explanation

(The area) surrounded on all sides with lotusladen ponds filled with cool, sacred water, sprawling hermitages with elevated altars.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

कदल्याढकिसम्बाधं नारिकेलोपशोभितम्। सालैस्तालैस्तमालैश्च पुष्पितैस्तरुभिर्वृतम्।।3.35.13।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कदल्याढकिसम्बाधम् full of banana plants, नारिकेलोपशोभितम् looking beautiful with coconut trees, पुष्पितैः with flowers, सालैः with sal trees, तालैः with palms, तमालैः with tamala, तरुभिः trees, वृतम् surrounded.

#### Explanation

The place covered with banana plants and coconut trees  as well as blossoming sal and tamala trees was looking extremely beautiful.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

नागैस्सुपर्णैर्गन्धैर्वैः किन्नरैश्च सहस्रशः। अजैर्वैखानसैर्माषैर्वालखिल्यैर्मरीचिपैः।।3.35.14।। अत्यन्तनियताहारैश्शोभितं परमर्षिभिः। जितकामैश्च सिद्धैश्च चारणैरुपशोभितम्।।3.35.15।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

नागैः with nagas, सुपर्णैः with garudas, गन्धर्वैः gandharvas, सहस्रशः in thousands, किन्नरैश्च with kinneras, आजैः by ajas, वैखानसैः by vaikhanasas, माषैः by mashas, मरीचिपैः by mareechipas, वालखिल्यैः by valakhilyas, अत्यन्तनियताहारैः by those who are highly disciplined in food, परमर्षिभिः by great sages, शोभितम् looked charming, जितकामैः by  those with perfect control over desires, सिद्धैश्च by the spiritually accomplished, चारणैः by charanas, उपशोभितम् delightful.

#### Explanation

The place looked charming with nagas, garudas, gandharvas, kinneras in their thousands and with the descendants of ajas, vaikhanasas, valakhilyas and with those who drink the rays of Sun and Moon for survival, with  sages who were highly disciplined in the intake of food,  spiritually accomplished  and selfcontrolled hermits and charanas.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

नागैस्सुपर्णैर्गन्धैर्वैः किन्नरैश्च सहस्रशः। अजैर्वैखानसैर्माषैर्वालखिल्यैर्मरीचिपैः।।3.35.14।। अत्यन्तनियताहारैश्शोभितं परमर्षिभिः। जितकामैश्च सिद्धैश्च चारणैरुपशोभितम्।।3.35.15।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

नागैः with nagas, सुपर्णैः with garudas, गन्धर्वैः gandharvas, सहस्रशः in thousands, किन्नरैश्च with kinneras, आजैः by ajas, वैखानसैः by vaikhanasas, माषैः by mashas, मरीचिपैः by mareechipas, वालखिल्यैः by valakhilyas, अत्यन्तनियताहारैः by those who are highly disciplined in food, परमर्षिभिः by great sages, शोभितम् looked charming, जितकामैः by  those with perfect control over desires, सिद्धैश्च by the spiritually accomplished, चारणैः by charanas, उपशोभितम् delightful.

#### Explanation

The place looked charming with nagas, garudas, gandharvas, kinneras in their thousands and with the descendants of ajas, vaikhanasas, valakhilyas and with those who drink the rays of Sun and Moon for survival, with  sages who were highly disciplined in the intake of food,  spiritually accomplished  and selfcontrolled hermits and charanas.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

दिव्याभरणमाल्याभिर्दिव्यरूपाभिरावृतम्। क्रीडारतिविधिज्ञाभिरप्सरोभिस्सहस्रशः।।3.35.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

दिव्याभरणमाल्याभिः  bedecked with fine ornaments and garlands, दिव्यरूपाभिः  paragons of beauty, क्रीडारतिविधिज्ञाभिः ladies acquainted with various lovesports, सहस्रशः  thousands, अप्सरोभि: apsaras, आवृतम् full of.

#### Explanation

(The place was) full of apsaras  in their thousands and paragons of beauty adorned with divine ornaments and garlands, and experts in lovesports.

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

सेवितं देवपन्तीभिश्श्रीमतीभिश्श्रियाऽऽवृतम्। देवदानवसङ्घैश्च चरितं त्वमृताशिभिः।।3.35.17।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

श्रीमतीभिः prosperous (deities), देवपत्नीभिः goddesses, सेवितम्  inhabited, श्रिया resort , वृतम् endowed, अमृतार्थिभिः gods who sought nectar, देवदानवसङ्घैश्च by hosts of gods and demons, चरितम् wandered about.

#### Explanation

It was chosen by prosperous wives of gods as a reosrt. Hosts of gods and demons were wandering, there in the hope of tasting nectar.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

हंसक्रौञ्चप्लवाकीर्णं सारसैस्सम्प्रणादितम्। वैढूर्यप्रस्तरं रम्यं स्निग्धं सागरतेजसा।।3.35.18।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

हंसक्रौञ्चप्लवाकीर्णम् full of flocks of swans and kraunchas and small floats, सारसैः by sarasa birds, सम्प्रणादितम् filled with sound, वैढूर्यप्रस्तरम् having a stretch of vaidurya stones, रम्यम् delightful, सागरतेजसा by the radiance of the sea, स्निग्धम् shining.

#### Explanation

(It was ) full of small floats, flocks of swans, kraunchas, sarasas cackling in the ponds, with great joy. It had stretches of vaidurya stones on the shore and shone with the radiance of the sea

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

पाण्डुराणि विशालानि दिव्यमाल्ययुतानि च। तूर्यगीताभिजुष्टानि विमानानि समन्ततः।।3.35.19।। तपसा जितलोकानां कामगान्यभिसम्पतन्। गन्धर्वाप्सरसश्चैव ददर्श धनदानुजः।।3.35.20।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अभिसम्पतन् spleding along, धनदानुजः younger brother of Kubera, तपसा with penance, जितलोकानाम्  who has conquered all the worlds, पाण्डुराणि by pale ones, विशालानि large ones, दिव्यमाल्ययुतानि च with divine flower garlands, तूर्यगीताभिजुष्टानि ringing with divine strains of music, कामगानि fly where one desires, विमानानि aerial chariots, समन्ततः on all sides, गन्धर्वाप्सरसश्चैव by gandharvas and apsaras also, ददर्श saw.

#### Explanation

Speeding along, the brother of Kubera (Ravana) saw palewhite, spacious aerial chariots on all sides controlled by those who had conquered the higher worlds by their penances. These chariots, decked with heavenly garlands, and ringing with divine music could fly where one desired. He saw gandharvas and apsaras as well.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

पाण्डुराणि विशालानि दिव्यमाल्ययुतानि च। तूर्यगीताभिजुष्टानि विमानानि समन्ततः।।3.35.19।। तपसा जितलोकानां कामगान्यभिसम्पतन्। गन्धर्वाप्सरसश्चैव ददर्श धनदानुजः।।3.35.20।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अभिसम्पतन् spleding along, धनदानुजः younger brother of Kubera, तपसा with penance, जितलोकानाम्  who has conquered all the worlds, पाण्डुराणि by pale ones, विशालानि large ones, दिव्यमाल्ययुतानि च with divine flower garlands, तूर्यगीताभिजुष्टानि ringing with divine strains of music, कामगानि fly where one desires, विमानानि aerial chariots, समन्ततः on all sides, गन्धर्वाप्सरसश्चैव by gandharvas and apsaras also, ददर्श saw.

#### Explanation

Speeding along, the brother of Kubera (Ravana) saw palewhite, spacious aerial chariots on all sides controlled by those who had conquered the higher worlds by their penances. These chariots, decked with heavenly garlands, and ringing with divine music could fly where one desired. He saw gandharvas and apsaras as well.

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

निर्यासरसमूलानां चन्दनानां सहस्रशः। वनानि पश्यन्सौम्यानि घ्राणतृप्तिकराणि च।।3.35.21।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

निर्यासरसमूलानाम्  tree trunks with resins (emitting juices), चन्दनानाम् of sandalwood trees, सौम्यानि delighting, घ्राणतृप्तिकराणि satisfying to smell, च and, सहस्रशः by thousands, वनानि trees, पश्यन् seeing,

#### Explanation

He saw thousands of tree trunks with finesmelling resins and sandalwood trees.

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

अगरूणां च मुख्यानां वनान्युपवनानि च। तक्कोलानां च जात्यानां फलानां च सुगन्धिनाम्।।3.35.22।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मुख्यानाम् of the prominent ones, अगुरूणाम् aguru trees, वनानि forests, उपवनानि च pleasuregroves, तक्कोलानाम् of takkola fruits, फलानाम् of fruits, सुगन्धिनाम् of the fragrant ones, जात्यानाम् nutmegs,

#### Explanation

(He saw) very fine aguru trees in the forest as well as pleasuregroves of takkola fruits, fragrant  trees of a fine variety and fragrant nutmegs.

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

पुष्पाणि च तमालस्य गुल्मानि मरिचस्य च। मुक्तानां च समूहानि शुष्यमाणानि तीरतः।।3.35.23।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तमालस्य tamala trees, पुष्पाणि flowers in bloom, मरिचस्य of pepper, गुल्मानि shrubs, तीरतः on the shore, शुष्यमाणानि drying, मुक्तानाम् pearls, समूहानि च hoards,

#### Explanation

(He saw) tamala flowers in bloom, pepper shrubs in clusters and hoards of pearls kept for drying on the shores.

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

शङ्खानां प्रसरं चैव प्रवालनिचयं तथा। काञ्चनानि च शैलानि राजतानि च सर्वशः।।3.35.24।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

शङ्खानाम् conches, प्रसरं चैव stretch also, तथा likewise, प्रवालनिचयम् heaps of corals, सर्वशः all over, काञ्चनानि gold, राजतानि च and silver, शैलानि mounds,

#### Explanation

He saw stretches of conches, heaps of corals and mounds of gold and silver too.

---

### Shloka 25

#### Shloka Text

प्रस्रवाणि मनोज्ञानि प्रसन्नानि ह्रदानि च। धनधान्योपपन्नानि स्त्रीरत्नैश्शोभितानि च।।3.35.25।। हस्त्यश्वरथगाढानि नगराण्यवलोकयन्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मनोज्ञानि fascinating , प्रस्रवाणि waterfalls, प्रसन्नानि pleasing, ह्रदानि च ponds, धनधान्योपपन्नानि endowed with wealth and grains in abundance, स्त्रीरत्नैः best of women, शोभितानि  splendid looking, हस्त्यश्वरथगाढानि elephants with, horses and chariots, नगराणि cities, अवलोकयन् went on seeing.

#### Explanation

He went watching fascinating waterfalls, pleasing ponds, cities filled with wealth and grain, looking splendid with beautiful women and full of elephants, horses and chariots.

---

### Shloka 26

#### Shloka Text

तं समं सर्वतस्निग्धं मृदुसंस्पर्शमारुतम्। अनूपं सिन्धुराजस्य ददर्श त्रिदिवोपमम्।।3.35.26।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सर्वतः all over, समम् levelled, स्निग्धम् beautiful, मृदुसंस्पर्शमारुतम् the breeze gentle to touch, त्रिदिवोपमम् comparable to heaven, सिन्धुराजस्य of the sea, तम् अनूपम् backwaters, ददर्श saw.

#### Explanation

It was a veritable heaven with the plain land flooded with the backwaters of the sea and gentle breeze blowing.

---

### Shloka 27

#### Shloka Text

तत्रापश्यत्स मेघाभं न्यग्रोधमृषिभिर्वृतम्।।3.35.27।। समन्ताद्यस्य ताश्शाखाश्शतयोजनमायताः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तत्र there, सः he, यस्य whose, शाखा branches, शतयोजनम् one hundred yojanas, आयताः spread, मेघाभम् looking like a cloud, ऋषिभिः वृतम् occupied by sages, न्यग्रोधम् banyan tree, अपश्यत् saw.

#### Explanation

He saw a banyan tree with its branches spread on all sides measuring a hundred yojanas, looking like a cloud and providing shelter to the sages.

---

### Shloka 28

#### Shloka Text

यस्य हस्तिनमादाय महाकायं च कच्छपम्।।3.35.28।। भक्षार्थं गरुडश्शाखामाजगाम महाबलः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महाबलः very strong, गरुडः Garuda, भक्षार्थम् for food, हस्तिनम् elephant, महाकायम् of huge body, कच्छपं च and tortoise, आदाय after getting, यस्य whose, शाखाम् branch, आजगाम came to,

#### Explanation

In the past, the mighty Garuda brought an elephant and a huge tortoise and sat on a branch of this tree to feed on them.

---

### Shloka 29

#### Shloka Text

तस्य तां सहसा शाखां भारेण पतगोत्तमः।।3.35.29।। सुपर्णः पर्णबहुलां बभञ्ज च महाबलः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पतगोत्तमः the best of birds, महाबलः very strong, सुपर्णः Suparna (Garuda), तस्य his, तां शाखाम् that  branch, भारेण with weight, सहसा at once, बभञ्ज broke, पर्णबहुलां full of leaves.

#### Explanation

Due to the weight of this best of birds, the mighty Garuda, the leafy branch at once broke down.

---

### Shloka 30

#### Shloka Text

तत्र वैखानसा माषा वालखिल्या मरीचिपाः।।3.35.30।। अजा बभूवुर्धूम्राश्च सङ्गताः परमर्षयः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वैखानसा: vaikhanasas, माषाः mashas, मरीचिपाः marichipas, वालखिल्याः valakhilyas, अजाः ajas,धूम्राश्च dhumras, परमर्षयश्च great seers,तत्र there, सङ्गताः gathered,बभूवुः  were.

#### Explanation

Gathered together under the great tree were great sages like vaikhanasas, mashas, marichipas, valakhilyas, ajas and dhumras.

---

### Shloka 31

#### Shloka Text

तेषां दयार्थं गरुडस्तां शाखां शतयोजनाम्।।3.35.31।। जगामादाय वेगेन तौ चोभौ गजकच्छपौ।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

गरुडः Garuda, तेषाम् their, दयार्थम् out of compassion, शतयोजनाम् extending to a hundred yojanas, तां शाखाम् that branch, तौ उभौ both, गजकच्छपौ elephant and the tortoise, आदाय taking, वेगेन with speed, जगाम went.

#### Explanation

Out of compassion for the (endangered) seers Garuda carried away that hundredyojanalong branch along with the elephant and the tortoise with great speed.

---

### Shloka 32

#### Shloka Text

एकपादेन धर्मात्मा भक्षयित्वा तदामिषम्।।3.35.32।। निषादविषयं हत्वा शाखया पतगोत्तमः। प्रहर्षमतुलं लेभे मोक्षयित्वा महामुनीन्।।3.35.33।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

धर्मात्मा righteous self, पतगोत्तमः the best among birds (Garuda), तत् that, आमिषम् meat, एकपादेन (standing) on one foot, भक्षयित्वा eating, शाखया with the branch, निषादविषयम्  settlement  of nightwalkers, हत्वा destroying, महामुनीन् great seers, मोक्षयित्वा delivering, अतुलम् great, प्रहर्षम् happiness, लेभे felt.

#### Explanation

Righteous Garuda, the best among the birds, stood on one foot and ate the flesh (of elephant and tortoise) and  with the branch of the tree, destroyed the settlement of the demons. Over the destruction of the demons and deliverance of the great seers he felt immeasurable happiness.

---

### Shloka 33

#### Shloka Text

एकपादेन धर्मात्मा भक्षयित्वा तदामिषम्।।3.35.32।। निषादविषयं हत्वा शाखया पतगोत्तमः। प्रहर्षमतुलं लेभे मोक्षयित्वा महामुनीन्।।3.35.33।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

धर्मात्मा righteous self, पतगोत्तमः the best among birds (Garuda), तत् that, आमिषम् meat, एकपादेन (standing) on one foot, भक्षयित्वा eating, शाखया with the branch, निषादविषयम्  settlement  of nightwalkers, हत्वा destroying, महामुनीन् great seers, मोक्षयित्वा delivering, अतुलम् great, प्रहर्षम् happiness, लेभे felt.

#### Explanation

Righteous Garuda, the best among the birds, stood on one foot and ate the flesh (of elephant and tortoise) and  with the branch of the tree, destroyed the settlement of the demons. Over the destruction of the demons and deliverance of the great seers he felt immeasurable happiness.

---

### Shloka 34

#### Shloka Text

स तेनैव प्रहर्षेण द्विगुणीकृतविक्रमः। अमृतानयनार्थं वै चकार मतिमान्मतिम्।।3.35.34।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मतिमान् wise, सः he (Garuda), तेनैव प्रहर्षेण out of that joy, द्विगुणीकृतविक्रमः with redoubled vigour, अमृतानयनार्थम् to obtain nectar, मतिम् चकार made up his mind.

#### Explanation

Quite happy, his energy redoubled, wise Garuda made up his mind to obtain nectar.

---

### Shloka 35

#### Shloka Text

अयोजालानि निर्मथ्य भित्वा रत्नमयं गृहम्। महेन्द्रभवनाद्गुप्तमाजहारामृतं ततः।।3.35.35।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अयोजालानि grills of windows, निर्मथ्य twisting and twirling, रत्नमयम् गृहम् house made of gems, भित्वा breaking, ततः from there, महेन्द्रभवनात् from the palace of Indra, गुप्तम् secretly, अमृतम् nectar, आजहार carried away.

#### Explanation

Twisting and twirling the grills of the window, he broke open the room made of gems and quietly carried away the nectar from Indra's mansion.

---

### Shloka 36

#### Shloka Text

तं महर्षिगणैर्जुष्टं सुपर्णकृतलक्षणम्। नाम्ना सुभद्रं न्यग्रोधं ददर्श धनदानुजः।।3.35.36।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

धनदानुजः younger brother of Kubera, महर्षिगणैः by groups of seers, जुष्टम् favoured resort, सुपर्णकृतलक्षणम् marked with breaking of the branch by Garuda, नाम्ना by name, सुभद्रम् Subhadra, तं न्यग्रोधम् that banyan tree, ददर्श beheld.

#### Explanation

Ravana, the younger brother of Kubera, beheld that banyan tree called Subhadram ,which was a favoured resort of the sages bearing the ensignia of Garuda.

---

### Shloka 37

#### Shloka Text

तं तु गत्वा परं पारं समुद्रस्य नदीपतेः। ददर्शाश्रममेकान्ते रम्ये पुण्ये वनान्तरे।।3.35.37।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

नदीपतेः of lord of rivers(ocean), समुद्रस्य of the sea, तम् that, परं पारम् गत्वा going over to the other side, रम्ये in a beautiful, पुण्ये in a sacred, वनान्तरे in the middle of the forest, एकान्ते in a secluded place, आश्रमम् hermitage, ददर्श beheld.

#### Explanation

Ravana crossed over to the other side of the sea, the lord of rivers, and beheld a hermitage in a beautiful, secluded, sacred  place in the middle of the forest.

---

### Shloka 38

#### Shloka Text

तत्र कृष्णाजिनधरं जटावल्कलधारिणम्। ददर्श नियताहारं मारीचं नाम राक्षसाम्।।3.35.38।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तत्र there,कृष्णाजिनधरम् clad in deerskin, जटावल्कलधारिणम् with matted hair and bark  cloth, नियताहारम् living on regulated food, मारीचं नाम  named Maricha, राक्षसम् a demon, ददर्श saw.

#### Explanation

There he saw a demon Maricha, by name, clad in dark deerskin and bark ,with matted hair and living on restricted food.

---

### Shloka 39

#### Shloka Text

स रावणस्समागम्य विधिवत्तेन रक्षसा। मारीचेनार्चितो राजा सर्वकामैरमानुषैः।।3.35.39।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राजा king, सः रावणः Ravana, समागम्य reaching there, रक्षसा by the demon, तेन मारीचेन to Maricha, अमानुषैः superhuman, सर्वकामैः by fulfilling all desires, विधिवत् as per tradition, अर्चितः made worshipful offering.

#### Explanation

When king Ravana reached there, Maricha received him  in accordance with tradition  and made worshipful offering to the king, fulfilling all his superhuman desires.

---

### Shloka 40

#### Shloka Text

तं स्वयं पूजयित्वा तु भोजनेनोदकेन च। अर्थोपहितया वाचा मारीचो वाक्यमब्रवीत्।।3.35.40।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मारीचः Maricha, तम् to him, भोजनेन food, उदकेन च water, स्वयम् himself, पूजयित्वा after offering, अर्थोपहितया meaningful, वाचा words, वाक्यम् अब्रवीत् said.

#### Explanation

Maricha extended his hospitality with appropriate food and water and said with meaningful words:

---

### Shloka 41

#### Shloka Text

कच्चित्सुकुशलं राजन्लङ्कायां राक्षसेश्वर। केनार्थेन पुनस्त्वं वै तूर्णमेवमिहागतः।।3.35.41।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षसेश्वर O lord of demons, राजन् O king, लङ्कायाम् at Lanka, सुकुशलं कच्चित् I hope every thing is well, त्वम् you, केन अर्थेन to what purpose, तूर्णम् so soon, एवम् like this, पुनः again, इह here, आगतः came?

#### Explanation

O king  O lord of the demons  hope all is well in Lanka. What brings you here again so soon ?

---

### Shloka 42

#### Shloka Text

एवमुक्तो महातेजा मारीचेन स रावणः। ततः पश्चादिदं वाक्यमब्रवीद्वाक्यकोविदः।।3.35.42।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मारीचेन by Maricha, एवम् thus, उक्तः spoken, महातेजाः effulgent, वाक्यकोविदः a skilled speaker, सः रावणः that Ravana, ततः पश्चात् later, इदम् this, वाक्यम् words, अब्रवीत् said.

#### Explanation

Thus addressed by Maricha, the effulgent, eloquent Ravana replied with these words: इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये अरण्यकाण्डे पञ्चत्रिंशस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the thirtyfifth sarga of Aranyakanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 36

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

मारीच श्रूयातां तात वचनं मम भाषतः। आर्तोऽस्मि मम चार्तस्य भवान्हि परमा गतिः।।3.36.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तात O child, मारीच Maricha, भाषतः as I speak, मम my, वचनम् words, श्रूयताम् hear, आर्तः distressed, अस्मि I am, आर्तस्य of the distressed, मम my, भवान् you, परमा ultimate, गतिः हि  refuge.

#### Explanation

O dear Maricha hear my words. I am distressed and you are my ultimate refuge.

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

जानीषे त्वं जनस्थाने यथा भ्राता खरो मम। दूषणश्च महाबाहु स्वसा शूर्पणखा च मे।।3.36.2।। त्रिशिराश्च महातेजा राक्षसः पिशिताशनः। अन्ये च बहवश्शूरा लब्धलक्षा निशाचराः।।3.36.3।। वसत्नि मन्नियोगेन नित्यवासं च राक्षसाः। बाधमाना महारण्ये मुनीन्वै धर्मचारिणः।।3.36.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मम my, भ्राता brother, खरः Khara, महाबाहुः of mighty arms, दूषणश्च and Dusana, मे my, स्वसा sister, शूर्पणखा च Surpanakha too, महातेजाः brilliant, पिशिताशनः flesh eater, राक्षसः demon, त्रिशिराश्च and Trisira, शूराः brave, लब्धलक्षाः unfailing in their targets, अन्ये बहवः many other, निशाचराः राक्षसाः nightwalker demons, मन्नियोगेन at my behest, महारण्ये in the great forest, धर्मचारिणः followers of righteous path, मुनीन् seers, बाधमानाः harassing, जनस्थाने at Janasthana, यथा likewise, नित्यवासं वसन्ति always living there, त्वम् you, जानीषे know.

#### Explanation

You know that my brothers, Khara and Dusana, the strongarmed heroes, my sister Surpanakha and  the mighty cannibal Trisira, and many other heroic demons successful in hitting their targets are bonafide residents. They have been harassing the followers of righteous path (the sages) at my behest.

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

जानीषे त्वं जनस्थाने यथा भ्राता खरो मम। दूषणश्च महाबाहु स्वसा शूर्पणखा च मे।।3.36.2।। त्रिशिराश्च महातेजा राक्षसः पिशिताशनः। अन्ये च बहवश्शूरा लब्धलक्षा निशाचराः।।3.36.3।। वसत्नि मन्नियोगेन नित्यवासं च राक्षसाः। बाधमाना महारण्ये मुनीन्वै धर्मचारिणः।।3.36.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मम my, भ्राता brother, खरः Khara, महाबाहुः of mighty arms, दूषणश्च and Dusana, मे my, स्वसा sister, शूर्पणखा च Surpanakha too, महातेजाः brilliant, पिशिताशनः flesh eater, राक्षसः demon, त्रिशिराश्च and Trisira, शूराः brave, लब्धलक्षाः unfailing in their targets, अन्ये बहवः many other, निशाचराः राक्षसाः nightwalker demons, मन्नियोगेन at my behest, महारण्ये in the great forest, धर्मचारिणः followers of righteous path, मुनीन् seers, बाधमानाः harassing, जनस्थाने at Janasthana, यथा likewise, नित्यवासं वसन्ति always living there, त्वम् you, जानीषे know.

#### Explanation

You know that my brothers, Khara and Dusana, the strongarmed heroes, my sister Surpanakha and  the mighty cannibal Trisira, and many other heroic demons successful in hitting their targets are bonafide residents. They have been harassing the followers of righteous path (the sages) at my behest.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

जानीषे त्वं जनस्थाने यथा भ्राता खरो मम। दूषणश्च महाबाहु स्वसा शूर्पणखा च मे।।3.36.2।। त्रिशिराश्च महातेजा राक्षसः पिशिताशनः। अन्ये च बहवश्शूरा लब्धलक्षा निशाचराः।।3.36.3।। वसत्नि मन्नियोगेन नित्यवासं च राक्षसाः। बाधमाना महारण्ये मुनीन्वै धर्मचारिणः।।3.36.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मम my, भ्राता brother, खरः Khara, महाबाहुः of mighty arms, दूषणश्च and Dusana, मे my, स्वसा sister, शूर्पणखा च Surpanakha too, महातेजाः brilliant, पिशिताशनः flesh eater, राक्षसः demon, त्रिशिराश्च and Trisira, शूराः brave, लब्धलक्षाः unfailing in their targets, अन्ये बहवः many other, निशाचराः राक्षसाः nightwalker demons, मन्नियोगेन at my behest, महारण्ये in the great forest, धर्मचारिणः followers of righteous path, मुनीन् seers, बाधमानाः harassing, जनस्थाने at Janasthana, यथा likewise, नित्यवासं वसन्ति always living there, त्वम् you, जानीषे know.

#### Explanation

You know that my brothers, Khara and Dusana, the strongarmed heroes, my sister Surpanakha and  the mighty cannibal Trisira, and many other heroic demons successful in hitting their targets are bonafide residents. They have been harassing the followers of righteous path (the sages) at my behest.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

चतुर्दश सहस्राणि रक्षसां भीमकर्मणाम्। शूराणां लब्धलक्षाणां खरचित्तानुवर्तिनाम्।।3.36.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

भीमकर्मणाम् of terrible deeds, शूराणाम् of heroes, लब्धलक्षाणाम् skilled in hitting the targets, खरचित्तानुवर्तिनाम्  acting according to the will of Khara, रक्षसाम् of demons, चतुर्दश सहस्राणि fourteen thousand.

#### Explanation

These fourteen thousand heroes, perpetrators of terrible deeds, skilled in hitting the targets had been acting according to the direction of Khara.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

ते त्विदानीं जनस्थाने वसमाना महाबलाः। सङ्गताः परमायत्ता रामेण सह संयुगे।।3.36.6।। नानाप्रहरणोपेताः खरप्रमुखराक्षसाः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

जनस्थाने at Janasthana, वसमानाः living permanently, महाबलाः very strong, ते they, खरप्रमुखराक्षसाः demons led by Khara, इदानीम् now, परमायत्ताः with all support, नानाप्रहरणोपेताः wielding different kinds of weapons, संयुगे in war, रामेण सह with Rama, सङ्गताः have fought.

#### Explanation

These mighty demons, living in Janasthana, eqipped with different kinds of weapons, supported and led by Khara, met Rama in an encounter.

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

तेन सञ्जातरोषेण रामेण रणमूर्धनि। अनुक्त्वा परुषं किञ्चिच्छरैर्व्यापारितं धनुः।।3.36.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation
