सञ्जातरोषेण  even though wrathful, तेन रामेण by that  Rama, रणमूर्धनि on the battle front, परुषम् harsh , किञ्चित् a little, अनुक्त्वा not uttered, धनुः bow, शरैः arrows, व्यापारितम् shot.

#### Explanation

With his wrath generated, Rama, without even speaking anything, used his bow and the arrows.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

चतुर्दश सहस्राणि रक्षसामुग्रतेजसाम्। निहतानि शरैस्तीक्ष्णैर्मानुषेण पदातिना।।3.36.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पदातिना  fighting on foot, मानुषेण by a human being, उग्रतेजसाम् of ferocious, रक्षसाम् of demons, चतुर्दश fourteen, सहस्राणि thousand, तीक्ष्णैः with sharp, शरैः arrows, निहतानि are killed.

#### Explanation

Being a human being who fought on foot, he killed fourteen thousand ferocious demons with his sharp arrows.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

खरश्च निहतस्सङ्ख्ये दूषणश्च निपातितः। हतश्च त्रिशिराश्चापि निर्भया दण्डकाः कृताः।।3.36.9।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सङ्ख्ये in the war, खरश्च Khara also, निहतः killed, दूषणश्च Dusana also, निपातितः is ruined, त्रिशिराश्चापि even Trisira, हतः got destroyed, दण्डकाः forest of Dandaka, निर्भयाः safe and fearless, कृताः are rendered.

#### Explanation

Khara, Dusana and Trisira were killed in war.The residents of Dandaka forest are rendered safe and fearless.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

पित्रा निरस्तः क्रुद्धेन सभार्यः क्षीणजीवितः। स हन्ता तस्य सैन्यस्य रामः क्षत्रियपांसनः।।3.36.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

क्रुद्धेन by an angry one, पित्रा by father, सभार्यः with his wife, निरस्तः sent away, क्षीणजीवितः of a short life span, क्षत्रियपांसनः a calumny to kshatriyas, सः रामः that Rama, तस्य सैन्यस्य of the  army of demons, हन्ता killer.

#### Explanation

Rama, a victim of his father's anger, banished into the forest with his wife, a shortlived mortal and a calumny on the kshatriyas has killed the army of demons.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

दुश्शीलः कर्कशस्तीक्ष्णो मूर्खो लुब्धोऽजितेन्द्रियः।।3.36.11।। त्यक्तधर्मो ह्यधर्मात्मा भूतानामहिते रतः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

दुश्शीलः of bad conduct, कर्कशः harsh, तीक्ष्णः sharp one, मूर्खः stupid, लुब्धः avaricious, अजितेन्द्रियः slave to senses, त्यक्तधर्मः immoral, अधर्मात्मा unrighteous, भूतानाम् for others, अहिते in harming, रतः engaged,

#### Explanation

He is a man of bad conduct, harsh, sharptonged, stupid, miserly and a slave to his senses. He is immoral and unrighteous, one who is busy harming others.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

येन वैरं विनाऽरण्ये सत्वमाश्रित्य केवलम्।।3.36.12।। कर्णनासापहरणाद्भगिनी मे विरूपिता। तस्यभार्यां जनस्थानात्सीतां सुरसुतोपमाम्।3.36.13।। आनयिष्यामि विक्रम्य सहायस्तत्र मे भव।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

येन by whom, वैरं विना without enmity, केवलम् only, सत्वम् strength, आश्रित्य depending, कर्णनासापहरणात् cut off nose and ears, मे भगिनी my sister, विरूपिता disfigured, तस्य his, भार्याम् wife, सुरसुतोपमाम् like the daughter of god, सीताम् Sita, विक्रम्य with my valour, जनस्थानात् from Janasthana, आनयिष्यामि will bring, तत्र in that, मे to me, सहायः accomplice, भव you be,

#### Explanation

With no enmity whatsoever, merely because he has strength, he cut off the nose and ears of my sister and mutilated her. His wife Sita  is like the daughter of heaven. I would like to abduct her with force from Janasthana for which you should be my accomplice.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

येन वैरं विनाऽरण्ये सत्वमाश्रित्य केवलम्।।3.36.12।। कर्णनासापहरणाद्भगिनी मे विरूपिता। तस्यभार्यां जनस्थानात्सीतां सुरसुतोपमाम्।3.36.13।। आनयिष्यामि विक्रम्य सहायस्तत्र मे भव।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

येन by whom, वैरं विना without enmity, केवलम् only, सत्वम् strength, आश्रित्य depending, कर्णनासापहरणात् cut off nose and ears, मे भगिनी my sister, विरूपिता disfigured, तस्य his, भार्याम् wife, सुरसुतोपमाम् like the daughter of god, सीताम् Sita, विक्रम्य with my valour, जनस्थानात् from Janasthana, आनयिष्यामि will bring, तत्र in that, मे to me, सहायः accomplice, भव you be,

#### Explanation

With no enmity whatsoever, merely because he has strength, he cut off the nose and ears of my sister and mutilated her. His wife Sita  is like the daughter of heaven. I would like to abduct her with force from Janasthana for which you should be my accomplice.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

त्वया ह्यहं सहायेन पार्श्वस्थेन महाबल।।3.36.14।। भ्रातृभिश्च सुरान्युद्धे समग्रान्नाभिचिन्तये। तत्सहायो भव त्वं मे समर्थो ह्यसि राक्षस।।3.36.15।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महाबल of great  strength, सहायेन come for help, पार्श्वस्थेन at my side, त्वया by you, भ्रातृभिश्च by brothers also, युद्धे in war, समग्रान् together, सुरान् gods, नाभिचिन्तये I do not care, राक्षस demon, तत् therefore, त्वम् you, मे to me, सहायः accomplice, भव be, समर्थः capable, असि हि you are,

#### Explanation

You are mighty. I do not care to fight even the gods when you and my brothers are by my side. Therefore, O demon, be my accomplice. (I know) you are capable of that.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

त्वया ह्यहं सहायेन पार्श्वस्थेन महाबल।।3.36.14।। भ्रातृभिश्च सुरान्युद्धे समग्रान्नाभिचिन्तये। तत्सहायो भव त्वं मे समर्थो ह्यसि राक्षस।।3.36.15।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महाबल of great  strength, सहायेन come for help, पार्श्वस्थेन at my side, त्वया by you, भ्रातृभिश्च by brothers also, युद्धे in war, समग्रान् together, सुरान् gods, नाभिचिन्तये I do not care, राक्षस demon, तत् therefore, त्वम् you, मे to me, सहायः accomplice, भव be, समर्थः capable, असि हि you are,

#### Explanation

You are mighty. I do not care to fight even the gods when you and my brothers are by my side. Therefore, O demon, be my accomplice. (I know) you are capable of that.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

वीर्ये युद्धे च दर्पे च न ह्यस्ति सदृशस्तव। उपायज्ञो महान्शूरस्सर्वमायाविशारदः।।3.36.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वीर्ये in valour, युद्धे च in war, दर्पे च in conceit, तव your, सदृशः equal, नास्ति हि none, उपायज्ञः you know all methods, महान् great, शूरः brave, सर्वमायाविशारदः an expert in all deceitful arts.

#### Explanation

In valour,  in war and in conceit there is no one equal to you. You know all means (of success). You are great. You are brave. You are an expert in all deceitful arts.

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

एतदर्थमहं प्राप्तस्त्वत्समीपं निशाचर। शृणु तत्कर्म साहाय्ये यत्कार्यं वचनान्मम।।3.36.17।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

निशाचर demon, एतदर्थम् for this purpose, अहं I, त्वत्समीपम् to you, प्राप्तः I came, मम my, वचनात् words, साहाय्ये become a helper, यत् whatever, कार्यम् work, तत् this, कर्म task, शृणु  listen.

#### Explanation

I have come to you for this purpose seeking your help. Listen to  my words on what you should do:

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

सौवर्णस्त्वं मृगो भूत्वा चित्रो रजतबिन्दुभिः। आश्रमे तस्य रामस्य सीतायाः प्रमुखे चर।।3.36.18।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

त्वम् you, रजतबिन्दुभिः dappled with silver dots, चित्रः wonderful, सौवर्णः golden, मृगः deer, भूत्वा  become, तस्य रामस्य that Rama's, आश्रमे in hermitage, सीतायाः of Sita, प्रमुखे in front, चर move.

#### Explanation

Be a wonderful golden deer dappled with silver dots and roam in front of Sita in Rama's hermitage.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

त्वां तु निस्संशयं सीता दृष्ट्वा तु मृगरूपिणम्। गृह्यतामिति भर्तारं लक्ष्मणं चाभिधास्यति।।3.36.19।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सीता Sita, मृगरूपिणम् in the form of a deer, त्वाम् to you, दृष्ट्वा seeing, गृह्यताम् catch it, इति thus, भर्तारम् her husband, लक्ष्मणं च and Lakshmana, निस्संशयम् without any doubt, अभिधास्यति will tell.

#### Explanation

Seeing you in the form of a deer, Sita will undoubtedly ask her husband and Lakshmana to capture you.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

ततस्तयोरपाये तु शून्ये सीतां यथासुखम्। निराबाधो हरिष्यामि राहुश्चन्द्रप्रभामिव।।3.36.20।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, तयोः both (Rama and Lakshmana), अपाये तु in their absence, शून्ये in an isolated place, यथासुखम् with ease, निराबाधः without any impediment, सीताम् Sita, राहुः Rahu, चन्द्रप्रभामिव like the lustre of the Moon, हरिष्यामि I will abduct.

#### Explanation

Then during their absence it will be easy for me to abduct Sita in an isolated place without obstruction, like Rahu eclipsing the light of the moon.

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

ततः पश्चात्सुखं रामे भार्याहरणकर्शिते। विस्रब्धः प्रहरिष्यामि कृतार्थेनान्तरात्मना।।3.36.21।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः पश्चात् thereafter, भार्याहरणकर्शिते afflicted due to his wife's abduction, रामे  Rama, कृतार्थेन successfully, अन्तरात्मना inwardly, सुखम् happily, विस्रब्धः freely, प्रहरिष्यामि I can strike him.

#### Explanation

Thereafter, when Rama with his soul afflicted due to his wife's abduction. I can freely, happily, successfully strike him down.

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

तस्य रामकथां श्रुत्वा मारीचस्य महात्मनः। शुष्कं समभवद्वक्त्रं परितप्ता बभूव ह।।3.36.22।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रामकथाम् mention of Rama's name, श्रुत्वा after hearing, महात्मनः of the great soul, तस्य मारीचस्य that Maricha's, वक्त्रम् mouth, शुष्कम् got dried, समभवत् became, परितप्त:was worried, बभूव हि became.

#### Explanation

At the mention of Rama's name, great Maricha's mouth dried up and he became concerned.

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

ओष्ठौ परिलिहञ्चुष्कौ नेत्रैरनिमिषैरिव। मृतभूत इवार्तस्तु रावणं समुदैक्षत।।3.36.23।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

शुष्कौ both dried, ओष्ठौ lips, परिलिहन् while licking, अनिमिषैरिव as though without winking, नेत्रैः eyes, मृतभूत इव like a dead person, आर्तस्तु distressed one, रावणम् Ravana, समुदैक्षत looked at Ravana.

#### Explanation

Licking his dried up lips, as though his eyes stopped winking like those of the dead, Maricha stared at Ravana, distressed.

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

स रावणं त्रस्तविषण्णचेता महावने रामपराक्रमज्ञः। कृताञ्जलिस्तत्वमुवाच वाक्यं हितं च तस्मै हितमात्मनश्च।।3.36.24।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महावने in the great forest, रामपराक्रमज्ञः one who knows Rama's valour, सः Maricha, त्रस्तविषण्णचेताः mind depressed and frightened, कृताञ्जलिः with folded palms, तस्मै to Ravana, हितम् for his wellbeing, आत्मनश्च of himself too, हितम् salutary, तत्वम् truth, वाक्यम् words, रावणम् to Ravana, उवाच spoke.

#### Explanation

Maricha who knows of Rama's valour was frightened and depressed at heart. He revealed to Ravana with folded hands some facts for mutual benefit. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीये आदिकाव्ये अरण्यकाण्डे षट् त्रिंशस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the thirtysixth sarga of Aranyakanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 37

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

तच्छृत्वा राक्षसेन्द्रस्य वाक्यं वाक्यविशारदः। प्रत्युवाच महाप्राज्ञो मारीचो राक्षसेश्वरम्।।3.37.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षसेन्द्रस्य  lord of the demons (Ravana), तत् वाक्यम् the words, श्रुत्वा having heard, महाप्राज्ञः very wise, वाक्यविशारदः eloquent speaker, मारीचः Maricha, राक्षसेश्वरम् to Ravana, प्रत्युवाच replied.

#### Explanation

Having heard the words of Ravana, lord of the demons, wise Maricha who was also an eloquent speaker replied:

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

सुलभाः पुरुषा राजन्सततं प्रियवादिनः। अप्रियस्य तु पथ्यस्य वक्ता श्रोता च दुर्लभः।।3.37.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राजन् O king, प्रियवादिनः sweettongued, पुरुषाः men, सततम् always, सुलभाः easy to find, अप्रियस्य unpleasant to hear, तु but, पथ्यस्य salutary, वक्ता speaker, श्रोता च audience, दुर्लभः is difficult to find.

#### Explanation

O king, it is always easy to find men who speak pleasing words, but it is difficult to get a speaker and a listener who use words unpleasant (to the ears) but beneficial (in life).

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

न नूनं बुध्यसे रामं महावीर्यं गुणोन्नतम्। अयुक्तचारश्चपलो महेन्द्रवरुणोपमम्।।3.37.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अयुक्तचारः a man who does not employ a spy, चपलः  fickleminded, महावीर्यम्  very brave, गुणोन्नतम्  of good qualities, महेन्द्र वरुणोपमम् comparable to Indra and Varuna, रामम् Rama, नूनम् surely, न बुध्यसे not able to know.

#### Explanation

You  are fickleminded.  You have employed no spy ( who could have told you the truth about Rama's character). Rama is very brave and virtuous comparable to Indra and Varuna. Surely you are not able to know what Rama is.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

अपि स्वस्ति भवेत्तात सर्वेषां भुवि रक्षसाम्। अपि रामो नासङ्क्रुद्धः कुर्याल्लोकमराक्षसम्।।3.37.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तात O dear, भुवि in the world, सर्वेषाम् of all, रक्षसाम् of demons, स्वस्ति safe, अपिभवेत् may be I wish, रामः Rama, न सङ्क्रुद्धः gets angry, लोकम् world, अराक्षसम् without rakshasa, अपि न कुर्यात् will he not do?.

#### Explanation

O dear may there be good to all the demons in the world. If Rama gets enraged, he will make this world demonless.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

अपि ते जीवितान्ताय नोत्पन्ना जनकात्मजा। अपि सीतानिमित्तं च न भवेद्व्यसनं मम।।3.37.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

जनकात्मजा daughter of Janaka, ते to you, जीवितान्ताय to destroy your life, अपि नोत्पन्ना not born I hope, सीतानिमित्तम् on account of Sita, मम myself, व्यसनम् disaster, अपि न भवेत् hope it will not happen.

#### Explanation

Hope the daughter of Janaka is not born to put an end to your life. Hope I will not face any disaster on account of Sita.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

अपि त्वामीश्वरं प्राप्य कामवृत्तं निरङ्कुशम्। न विनश्येत्पुरी लङ्का त्वया सह सराक्षसा।।3.37.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कामवृत्तम् a slave to sensual pleasures, निरङ्कुशम् a dictator, त्वाम् you, ईश्वरम् lord, प्राप्य  after getting, सराक्षसा along with demons, पुरी लङ्का city of Lanka, त्वया सह along with you, न विनश्येदपि may it not be destroyed,

#### Explanation

I hope the city of Lanka along with the (entire) race of demons will not be destroyed, with a dictator like you who is a slave to sensual pleasure.

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

त्वद्विधः कामवृत्तो हि दुश्शीलः पापमन्त्रितः। आत्मानं स्वजनं राष्ट्रं स राजा हन्ति दुर्मतिः।।3.37.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कामवृत्तः passionate, दुश्शीलः a person of bad character, पापमन्त्रितः advised in evil ways, दुर्मतिः of wicked mind, त्वद्विधः one like you, सः राजा that king, आत्मानम् himself, स्वजनम् his own people, राष्ट्रम् country, हन्ति हि will ruin.

#### Explanation

A king  who is a slave to passion, who is of bad conduct, of wicked thoughts, advised in evil ways destroys himself, his kith and kin and the entire kingdom.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

न च पित्रा परित्यक्तो नामर्यादः कथञ्चन। न लुब्धो न च दुश्शीलो न च क्षत्रियपांसनः।।3.37.8।। न च धर्मगुणैर्हीनः कौसल्यानन्दवर्धनः। न तीक्ष्णो न च भूतानां सर्वेषामहिते रतः।।3.37.9।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कौसल्यानन्दवर्धनः delight of Kausalya (Rama), पित्रा by father, न च परित्यक्तः not at all forsaken, कथञ्चन to do so, अमर्यादः nor crossed the limits of dharma, न not, लुब्धः miserly, न not, दुश्शीलः of bad conduct, न च and not, तीक्ष्ण sharptongued, क्षत्रियपांसनः च calumny to kshatriyas, न not, धर्मगुणैः in righteousness, हीनः not lacking, न not, तीक्ष्णः cruel, न not, सर्वेषाम् for all, भूतानाम् of beings, अहिते in doing harm, रतः is involved,

#### Explanation

Rama, the delight of Kausalya, has not forsaken by his father. He has never crossed the limits of dharma. He is not miserly. He has no  bad conduct. He is not a slur on the kshatriyas. He does not lack in righteousness. He is not cruel nor is he engaged in doing harm to others.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

न च पित्रा परित्यक्तो नामर्यादः कथञ्चन। न लुब्धो न च दुश्शीलो न च क्षत्रियपांसनः।।3.37.8।। न च धर्मगुणैर्हीनः कौसल्यानन्दवर्धनः। न तीक्ष्णो न च भूतानां सर्वेषामहिते रतः।।3.37.9।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कौसल्यानन्दवर्धनः delight of Kausalya (Rama), पित्रा by father, न च परित्यक्तः not at all forsaken, कथञ्चन to do so, अमर्यादः nor crossed the limits of dharma, न not, लुब्धः miserly, न not, दुश्शीलः of bad conduct, न च and not, तीक्ष्ण sharptongued, क्षत्रियपांसनः च calumny to kshatriyas, न not, धर्मगुणैः in righteousness, हीनः not lacking, न not, तीक्ष्णः cruel, न not, सर्वेषाम् for all, भूतानाम् of beings, अहिते in doing harm, रतः is involved,

#### Explanation

Rama, the delight of Kausalya, has not forsaken by his father. He has never crossed the limits of dharma. He is not miserly. He has no  bad conduct. He is not a slur on the kshatriyas. He does not lack in righteousness. He is not cruel nor is he engaged in doing harm to others.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

वञ्चितं पितरं दृष्ट्वा कैकेय्या सत्यवादिनम्। करिष्यामीति धर्मात्मा तात प्रव्रजितो वनम्।।3.37.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तात dear, सत्यवादिनम् always speaks the truth, पितरम् father, कैकेय्या by Kaikeyi, वञ्चितम् deceived, दृष्ट्वा after seeing, धर्मात्मा righteous man, करिष्यामि इति saying I will do it, वनम् forest, प्रव्रजितः came.

#### Explanation

O dear, seeing that his truthful father was going to be deceived by Kaikeyi, righteous Rama himself offered to fulfil the promise of his father and came away to the forest.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

कैकेय्याः प्रियकामार्थं पितुर्दशरथस्य च। हित्वा राज्यं च भोगांश्च प्रविष्टो दण्डकावनम्।।3.37.11।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कैकेय्याः of Kaikeyi, पितुः father's दशरथस्य च and Dasaratha's, प्रियकामार्थम् to satisfy their desire, राज्यम् kingdom, भोगांश्च pleasures, हित्वा after leaving, दण्डकावनम् Dandaka forest प्रविष्टः he entered.

#### Explanation

To satisfy the desire of Kaikeyi and to fulfil his father's word he came to Dandaka forest,  leaving the kingdom and all royal pleasures, behind.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

न रामः कर्कशस्तात नाविद्वान्नाजितेन्द्रियः। अनृतं दुश्श्रुतं चैव नैव त्वं वक्तुमर्हसि।।3.37.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तात O dear, रामः Rama, कर्कशः hardhearted, न not, अविद्वान् not learned, न not, अजितेन्द्रियः not that he has no control over the senses, न not, त्वम् you, अनृतम् false, दुश्श्रुतं चैव illinformed, वक्तुम् to speak, नार्हसि not proper.

#### Explanation

O my dear, Rama is not harsh nor ignorant. It is not that he has no control over the senses. It is not proper  for you to say what is false. You are illinformed.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

रामो विग्रहवान् धर्मस्साधुस्सत्यपराक्रमः। राजा सर्वस्य लोकस्य देवानां मघवानिव।।3.37.13।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रामः Rama, विग्रहवान् a personification of, धर्मः  dharma, साधुः pious , सत्यपराक्रमः one whose strength is truth, देवानाम् for gods, मघवानिव like Indra, सर्वस्य for all, लोकस्य worlds, राजा king.

#### Explanation

Rama is dharma incarnate. He is pious. His strength is truth. He is king of all the worlds like Indra to the gods.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

कथं त्वं तस्य वैदेहीं रक्षितां स्वेन तेजसा। इच्छसि प्रसभं हर्तुं प्रभामिव विवस्वतः।।3.37.14।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

त्वम् you, तस्य his, स्वेन own, तेजसा strength of character, रक्षिताम् will be protected, वैदेहीम् Vaidehi, विवस्वतः Sun's, प्रभामिव like the radiance, प्रसभम् forcibly, हर्तुम् to take, कथम् how, इच्छसि you wish.

#### Explanation

Sita is protected by the strength of her character. How do you desire to carry her away forcibly (from Rama) ? Can any one separate the radiance of the Sun from the Sun itself ?

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

शरार्चिषमाधृष्यं चापखङ्गेन्धनं रणे। रामाग्निं सहसा दीप्तं न प्रवेष्टुं त्वमर्हसि।।3.37.15।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

शरार्चिषम् arrows are flames, अनाधृष्यम् cannot be overpowered, चापखड्गेन्धनम् bow and  sword as fuel, रणे in war, दीप्तम् blazing, रामाग्निम् fire of Rama, सहसा at once, प्रवेष्टुम् to enter, त्वम् you, न अर्हसि should not.

#### Explanation

You should not enter into the unquenchable fire that is  Rama,  the fire that has the  arrows (of Rama) as its flames and his sword and bow as fuel.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

धनुर्व्यादितदीप्तास्यं शरार्चिषममर्षणम्। चापबाणधरं तीक्ष्णं शत्रुसैन्यप्रहारिणम्।।3.37.16।। राज्यं सुखं च सन्त्यज्य जीवितं चेष्टमात्मनः। नात्यासादयितुं तात रामान्तकमिहार्हसि।।3.37.17।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तात O dear, धनुर्व्यादितदीप्तास्यम् his bow is an open burning mouth, शरार्चिषम् flaming arrows as fire, अमर्षणम् anger, चापबाणधरम् wielder of bow and arrows, तीक्ष्णम्  sharp, शत्रुसैन्यप्रहारिणम् one who can strike the enemy's army, रामान्तकम् Rama like Yama himself, आत्मनः your own, इष्टम् dear, राज्यम् kingdom, सुखं च and happiness, जीवितं च life, सन्त्यज्य after abandoning, इह here, आत्यासादयितुम् to enter, न not, अर्हसि should.

#### Explanation

You should not give up happiness,your dear life and kingdom and come to Rama who is a variable god of death. His bow is  like on open burning mouth, and his flaming arrows are like fire. He is all anger. He is wielder of bow and arrows. He can strike the enemy army (alone).

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

धनुर्व्यादितदीप्तास्यं शरार्चिषममर्षणम्। चापबाणधरं तीक्ष्णं शत्रुसैन्यप्रहारिणम्।।3.37.16।। राज्यं सुखं च सन्त्यज्य जीवितं चेष्टमात्मनः। नात्यासादयितुं तात रामान्तकमिहार्हसि।।3.37.17।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तात O dear, धनुर्व्यादितदीप्तास्यम् his bow is an open burning mouth, शरार्चिषम् flaming arrows as fire, अमर्षणम् anger, चापबाणधरम् wielder of bow and arrows, तीक्ष्णम्  sharp, शत्रुसैन्यप्रहारिणम् one who can strike the enemy's army, रामान्तकम् Rama like Yama himself, आत्मनः your own, इष्टम् dear, राज्यम् kingdom, सुखं च and happiness, जीवितं च life, सन्त्यज्य after abandoning, इह here, आत्यासादयितुम् to enter, न not, अर्हसि should.

#### Explanation

You should not give up happiness,your dear life and kingdom and come to Rama who is a variable god of death. His bow is  like on open burning mouth, and his flaming arrows are like fire. He is all anger. He is wielder of bow and arrows. He can strike the enemy army (alone).

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

अप्रमेयं हि तत्तेजो यस्य सा जनकात्मजा। न त्वं समर्थस्तां हर्तुं रामचापाश्रयं वने।।3.37.18।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सा she, जनकात्मजा Sita, daughter of Janaka, यस्य whose, तत् that, अप्रमेयम् immeasurable, तेजः हि is the power, वने in the forest, रामचापाश्रयम् under the refuge of Rama's bow, ताम् herself, हर्तुम् to abduct, त्वम् you, समर्थः competent, न not.

#### Explanation

Immeasurable indeed is the power of Rama to whom Sita, daughter of Janaka belongs. You are not competent to kidnap her, for she is under the refuge of Rama's bow in the forest.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

तस्य सा नरसिंहस्य सिंहोरस्कस्य भामिनी। प्राणेभ्योऽपि प्रियतरा भार्या नित्यमनुव्रता।।3.37.19।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सा भामिनी effulgent lady, सिंहोरस्कस्य of a lion's chest, तस्य his, नरसिंहस्य of a lion in the form of man, प्राणेभ्योपि more than his life, प्रियतरा dearer, भार्या wife, नित्यम् always, अनुव्रता loyal to him.

#### Explanation

Beautiful Sita happens to be the wife of Rama who is like a lion among men. Rama has a broad chest like that of a lion. He loves Sita who is ever loyal to him, more than his life.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

न सा धर्षयितुं शक्या मैथिल्योजस्विनः प्रिया। दीप्तस्येव हुताशस्य शिखा सीता सुमध्यमा।।3.37.20।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मैथिली Maithili, ओजस्विनः of the mighty, प्रिया darling, सुमध्यमा slender waist, सा सीता that Sita, दीप्तस्य of burning, हुताशस्य fire's, शिखा इव like a leaping flame, धर्षयितुम् to touch her, न शक्या not possible.

#### Explanation

Sita is the darling of mighty Rama. With her slender waist, she is like the leaping flames of blazing fire and it is not possible  for you to touch her.

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

किमुद्यममिमं व्यर्थं कृत्वा ते राक्षसाधिप। दृष्टश्चेत्वं रणे तेन तदन्तं तव जीवितम्।।3.37.21।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षसाधिप chief of the demons, व्यर्थम् futile, इमम् this, उद्यमम् attempt, कृत्वा doing, ते you, किम् why, त्वम् you, रणे in war, तेन by him, दृष्टः seen, चेत् if, तव your, जीवितम् life, तदन्तम् that is the end.

#### Explanation

O chief of the demons, why do you make this futile attempt ? The moment you are seen by Rama in war will be the last of your life.

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

जीवितं च सुखं चैव राज्यं चैव सुदुर्लभम्। यदीच्छसि चिरं भोक्तुं मा कृथा रामविप्रियम्।।3.37.22।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

जीवितं च life and, सुखं चैव also happiness, सुदुर्लभम् not easily attainable, राज्यं चैव even kingdom, चिरम् for long, भोक्तुम् to enjoy, इच्छसि यदि if you desire to, रामविप्रियम् displeasure to Rama, मा कृथाः do not incur.

#### Explanation

If you want to enjoy your life, happiness and even the kingdom which is not easily attainable for long, do not incur the displeasure of Rama.

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

स सर्वैस्सचिवैस्सार्धं विभीषणपुरोगमैः। मन्त्रयित्वा तु धर्मिष्ठैः कृत्वा निश्चयमात्मनः।।3.37.23।। दोषाणां च गुणानां च सम्प्रधार्य बलाबलम्। आत्मनश्च बलं ज्ञात्वा राघवस्य च तत्वतः।।3.37.24।। हिताहितं विनिश्चित्य क्षमं त्वं कर्तुमर्हसि।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः त्वम्  you, धर्मिष्ठैः followers of righteous path, विभीषणपुरोगमैः by prominent people like Vibhisana, सर्वैः by all, सचिवैः सार्धम् with all ministers, मन्त्रयित्वा after discussing, आत्मनः yourself, निश्चयम् decision, कृत्वा  take, दोषाणां च of demerits, गुणानां च of merits, बलाबलम् strength and weakness, सम्प्रधार्य assessing, आत्मनश्च your own, राघवस्य च Rama's, बलम् strength, तत्वतः in fact, ज्ञात्वा  knowing, हिताहितम् welfare and otherwise, विनिश्चित्य after deciding, क्षमम् propriety, कर्तुम् to do, अर्हसि should.

#### Explanation

After consulting all your ministers and the righteous persons led by Vibhisana, you may take a decision. Assess the real strength and weakness, merits and demerits  of your own and of Rama. Decide what is good or bad for you and then take steps you deem proper.

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

स सर्वैस्सचिवैस्सार्धं विभीषणपुरोगमैः। मन्त्रयित्वा तु धर्मिष्ठैः कृत्वा निश्चयमात्मनः।।3.37.23।। दोषाणां च गुणानां च सम्प्रधार्य बलाबलम्। आत्मनश्च बलं ज्ञात्वा राघवस्य च तत्वतः।।3.37.24।। हिताहितं विनिश्चित्य क्षमं त्वं कर्तुमर्हसि।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः त्वम्  you, धर्मिष्ठैः followers of righteous path, विभीषणपुरोगमैः by prominent people like Vibhisana, सर्वैः by all, सचिवैः सार्धम् with all ministers, मन्त्रयित्वा after discussing, आत्मनः yourself, निश्चयम् decision, कृत्वा  take, दोषाणां च of demerits, गुणानां च of merits, बलाबलम् strength and weakness, सम्प्रधार्य assessing, आत्मनश्च your own, राघवस्य च Rama's, बलम् strength, तत्वतः in fact, ज्ञात्वा  knowing, हिताहितम् welfare and otherwise, विनिश्चित्य after deciding, क्षमम् propriety, कर्तुम् to do, अर्हसि should.

#### Explanation

After consulting all your ministers and the righteous persons led by Vibhisana, you may take a decision. Assess the real strength and weakness, merits and demerits  of your own and of Rama. Decide what is good or bad for you and then take steps you deem proper.

---

### Shloka 25

#### Shloka Text

अहं तु मन्ये तव न क्षमं रणे समागमं कोसलराजसूनुना। इदं हि भूयश्श़ृणु वाक्यमुत्तमं क्षमं च युक्तं च निशाचरेश्वर।।3.37.25।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

निशाचरेश्वर Ravana, अहं तु on my part, तव your, रणे in war, कोसलराजसूनुना by Rama son of the king of Kosala, समागमम् meet, क्षमम् good, न मन्ये not consider, उत्तमम् best, क्षमम् capable, युक्तं च and proper, इदं वाक्यम् these words, भूयः further, शृणु you may listen.

#### Explanation

O Ravana  I think it is not good on your part to confront the prince of Kosala in a war. Listen to my words and consider what is good for you. इत्यार्षे श्री मद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये अरण्यकाण्डे सप्तत्रिंशस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the thirtyseventh sarga of Aranyakanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 38

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

कदाचिदप्यहं वीर्यात्पर्यटन्पृथिवीमिमाम्। बलं नागसहस्रस्य धारयन्पर्वतोपमः।।3.38.1।। नीलजीमूतसङ्काशस्तप्तकाञ्चनकुण्डलः। भयं लोकस्य जनयन्किरीटी परिघायुधः।।3.38.2।। व्यचरं दण्डकारण्ये ऋषिमांसानि भक्षयन्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कदाचित् at one time, अहमपि I also, वीर्यात् out of prowess, इमाम् this, पृथिवीम् earth, पर्यटन् while wandering, नागसहस्रस्य thousands of elephants, बलम् strength, धारयन् while possesed, पर्वतोपमः like a mountain, नीलजीमूतसङ्काशः looked like a dark cloud, तप्तकाञ्चनकुण्डलः bright gold earrings, किरीटी crown, परिघायुधः spear, लोकस्य among people, भयम् fear, जनयन्  creating, ऋषिमांसानि flesh of ascetics, भक्षयन् eating, दण्डकारण्ये in Dandaka forest, व्यचरम् I was moving about.

#### Explanation

Once I was roaming the earth, like a mountain possessing the  strength of a thousand elephants, like a dark cloud, wearing bright gold earrings and crown,holding a spear in hand, terrorising the people. I was wandering in Dandaka forest feeding on the flesh of ascetics.

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

कदाचिदप्यहं वीर्यात्पर्यटन्पृथिवीमिमाम्। बलं नागसहस्रस्य धारयन्पर्वतोपमः।।3.38.1।। नीलजीमूतसङ्काशस्तप्तकाञ्चनकुण्डलः। भयं लोकस्य जनयन्किरीटी परिघायुधः।।3.38.2।। व्यचरं दण्डकारण्ये ऋषिमांसानि भक्षयन्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कदाचित् at one time, अहमपि I also, वीर्यात् out of prowess, इमाम् this, पृथिवीम् earth, पर्यटन् while wandering, नागसहस्रस्य thousands of elephants, बलम् strength, धारयन् while possesed, पर्वतोपमः like a mountain, नीलजीमूतसङ्काशः looked like a dark cloud, तप्तकाञ्चनकुण्डलः bright gold earrings, किरीटी crown, परिघायुधः spear, लोकस्य among people, भयम् fear, जनयन्  creating, ऋषिमांसानि flesh of ascetics, भक्षयन् eating, दण्डकारण्ये in Dandaka forest, व्यचरम् I was moving about.

#### Explanation

Once I was roaming the earth, like a mountain possessing the  strength of a thousand elephants, like a dark cloud, wearing bright gold earrings and crown,holding a spear in hand, terrorising the people. I was wandering in Dandaka forest feeding on the flesh of ascetics.

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

विश्वामित्रोऽथ धर्मात्मा मद्वित्रस्तो महामुनिः।।3.38.3।। स्वयं गत्वा दशरथं नरेन्द्रमिदमब्रवीत्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अथ hence, धर्मात्मा righteous, विश्वामित्रः Visvamitra, महामुनिः great sage, मद्वित्रस्तः afraid of me, स्वयम् he himself, नरेन्द्रम् king, दशरथम् Dasaratha, गत्वा went  इदम् these words, अब्रवीत् said.

#### Explanation

Hence the great sage, righteous Visvamitra afraid of me, went to king Dasaratha  and said:

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

अद्य रक्षतु मां रामः पर्वकाले समाहितः।।3.38.4।। मारीचान्मे भयं घोरं समुत्पन्नं नरेश्वर।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

नरेश्वर king, मारीचात् from Maricha, मे myself, घोरं (महत्) very, भयम् fear, समुत्पन्नम् has been created, अद्य now, रामः Rama, समाहितः composed, पर्वकाले while doing sacrifice at the time of lunar conjunctions on full moon and new moon days, माम् रक्षतु protect me.

#### Explanation

'O king Maricha has created in me a great fear. Spare the self possessed Rama to protect me while I do the sacrifice.'

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

इत्येवमुक्तो धर्मात्मा राजा दशरथस्तदा।।3.38.5।। प्रत्युवाच महाभागं विश्वामित्रं महामुनिम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

इत्येवम्  thus, उक्तः  spoken, धर्मात्मा righteous, राजा king, दशरथः Dasaratha, तदा then, महाभागम् the glorious, महामुनिम् the great sage, विश्वामित्रम् Visvamitra, प्रत्युवाच replied.

#### Explanation

When the great, venerable sage Visvamitra said so, the righteous king Dasaratha replied:

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

बालो द्वादशवर्षोऽयमकृतास्त्रश्च राघवः।।3.38.6।। कामं तु मम यत्सैन्यं मया सह गमिष्यति।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अयम् this lad, राघवः Rama, बालः young one, द्वादशवर्षे: twelve years old, अकृतास्त्रश्च not experienced in the use of weapons, मम mine, यत् that, सैन्यम् army, कामम् surely, मया सह along with me, गमिष्यति will come,

#### Explanation

'My son Rama is only twelve. He is not experienced in the use of weapons. I shall accompany you with my army.

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

बलेन चतुरङ्गेण स्वयमेत्य निशाचरान्।।3.38.7।। वधिष्यामि मुनिश्रेष्ठ शत्रूंस्ते मनसेप्सितान्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मुनिश्रेष्ठ O distinguished sage, चतुरङ्गेन with four divisions (elephants, horses, cavalry and infantry)of the army, बलेन with army, स्वयम् I myself, एत्य after coming, ते your, शत्रून् enemies, मनसा in your mind, ईप्सितान् desired ones, निशाचरान् nightwalkers, वधिष्यामि I will slay.

#### Explanation

'O distinguished sage with the four divisions of my army I will slay the enemies you want me to.'

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

इत्येवमुक्तस्समुनी राजानमिदमब्रवीत्।।3.38.8।। रामान्नान्यद्बलं लोके पर्याप्तं तस्य रक्षसः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

इत्येवम् in that way, उक्त addressed, सः मुनिः that sage, राजानम् to the king, इदम् these words, अब्रवीत् said, रामात् excepting Rama, अन्यत् other, बलम् strength, तस्य रक्षसः for that demon, लोके in this world, न पर्याप्तम् not enough.

#### Explanation

When  the king thus responded to the sage,  he told him that none other than Rama in this world is a match for that demon.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

देवतानामपि भवान् समरेष्वभिपालकः।।3.38.9।। आसीत्तव कृतं कर्म त्रिलोके विदितं नृप।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

नृप O king, भवान् you, समरेषु in wars, देवतानामपि even to gods, अभिपालकः a guardian, कृतम् help, तव your, कर्म deed, त्रिलोके in the three worlds, विदितम् well known, आसीत् was.

#### Explanation

'O king, you have protected even gods in wars and have been a guardian. Your deeds are very wellknown in the three worlds.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

काममस्तु महत्सैन्यं तिष्ठत्विह परन्तप।।3.38.10।। बालोऽप्येष महातेजास्समर्थस्तस्य निग्रहे। गमिष्ये राममादाय स्वस्ति तेऽस्तु परन्तप।।3.38.11।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

परन्तप O scourge of the foes, महत् great, सैन्यम् army, अस्तु कामम् so you wish, इह here, तिष्ठतु let it stay, महातेजाः glorious one, एषः he, बालोऽपि even if he is a boy, तस्य his, निग्रहे in suppressing, समर्थः capable, परन्तप subduer of enemy, रामम् Rama, आदाय after taking, गमिष्ये I will go, ते to you, स्वस्ति auspicious, अस्तु be.

#### Explanation

'O scourge of foes as you wish, let your great army wait here. Even though Rama is young he is glorious, and capable of subduing the enemy. I will take him. Be blessed.'

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

काममस्तु महत्सैन्यं तिष्ठत्विह परन्तप।।3.38.10।। बालोऽप्येष महातेजास्समर्थस्तस्य निग्रहे। गमिष्ये राममादाय स्वस्ति तेऽस्तु परन्तप।।3.38.11।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

परन्तप O scourge of the foes, महत् great, सैन्यम् army, अस्तु कामम् so you wish, इह here, तिष्ठतु let it stay, महातेजाः glorious one, एषः he, बालोऽपि even if he is a boy, तस्य his, निग्रहे in suppressing, समर्थः capable, परन्तप subduer of enemy, रामम् Rama, आदाय after taking, गमिष्ये I will go, ते to you, स्वस्ति auspicious, अस्तु be.

#### Explanation

'O scourge of foes as you wish, let your great army wait here. Even though Rama is young he is glorious, and capable of subduing the enemy. I will take him. Be blessed.'

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

एवमुक्त्वा तु स मुनिस्तमादाय नृपात्मजम्। जगाम परमप्रीतो विश्वामित्रस्स्वमाश्रमम्।।3.38.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः that, विश्वामित्रः मुनिः sage Visvamitra, एवम् in that way, उक्त्वा having said, परमप्रीतः very happily, तम् him, नृपात्मजम् prince Rama, आदाय after taking, स्वम् his, आश्रमम् hermitage, जगाम went.

#### Explanation

Sage Visvamitra saying so took the son of the king and went back very happily to his hermitage.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

तं तदा दण्डकारण्ये यज्ञमुद्दिश्य दीक्षितम्। बभूवोपस्थितो रामश्चित्रं विष्फारयन्धनुः।।3.38.13।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तदा then, रामः Rama, दण्डकारण्ये in Dandaka forest, यज्ञम् Yagna, उद्दिश्य for the purpose, दीक्षितम् settled in rigorous religious discipline(vow), तम् him, उपस्थितः standing by, चित्रम् wonderful, धनुः bow, विष्फारयन् while stretching, बभूव became.

#### Explanation

Then Rama was initiated for the protection of sacrifices and stood on guard, stretching his wonderful bow.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

अजातव्यञ्जनश्रीमान्पद्मपत्रनिभेक्षणः। एकवस्त्रधरो धन्वी शिखी कनकमालया।।3.38.14।। शोभयन् दण्डकारण्यं दीप्तेन स्वेन तेजसा। अदृश्यत ततो रामो बालचन्द्र इवोदितः।।3.38.15।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः thereafter, अजातव्यञ्जनः with beards ungrown, श्रीमान् handsome, पद्मपत्रनिभेक्षणः having eyes like lotus petals, एकवस्त्रधरः clad in a single garment, धन्वी  wielding bow, शिखी wearing a knot of hair on the head, कनकमालया with a gold chain, दीप्तेन luminous, स्वेन  by his, तेजसा by lustre, दण्डकारण्यम् Dandaka forest, शोभयन्  beautifying,  रामः Rama, उदितः just risen, बालचन्द्र इव like young Moon, अदृश्यत looked.

#### Explanation

Thereafter, handsome Rama, with eyes like lotus petals, beards ungrown, clad in a single garment with knotted hair on the head, wielding a bow, with a chain of gold, luminous with his own lustre enhanced the glory of Dandaka forest by looking like the young moon just risen.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

अजातव्यञ्जनश्रीमान्पद्मपत्रनिभेक्षणः। एकवस्त्रधरो धन्वी शिखी कनकमालया।।3.38.14।। शोभयन् दण्डकारण्यं दीप्तेन स्वेन तेजसा। अदृश्यत ततो रामो बालचन्द्र इवोदितः।।3.38.15।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः thereafter, अजातव्यञ्जनः with beards ungrown, श्रीमान् handsome, पद्मपत्रनिभेक्षणः having eyes like lotus petals, एकवस्त्रधरः clad in a single garment, धन्वी  wielding bow, शिखी wearing a knot of hair on the head, कनकमालया with a gold chain, दीप्तेन luminous, स्वेन  by his, तेजसा by lustre, दण्डकारण्यम् Dandaka forest, शोभयन्  beautifying,  रामः Rama, उदितः just risen, बालचन्द्र इव like young Moon, अदृश्यत looked.

#### Explanation

Thereafter, handsome Rama, with eyes like lotus petals, beards ungrown, clad in a single garment with knotted hair on the head, wielding a bow, with a chain of gold, luminous with his own lustre enhanced the glory of Dandaka forest by looking like the young moon just risen.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

ततोऽहं मेघसङ्काशस्तप्तकाञ्चनकुण्डलः। बली दत्तवरोदर्पादाजगाम तदाश्रमम्।।3.38.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, मेघसङ्काशः looking like a dark hued cloud, तप्तकाञ्चनकुण्डलः wearing bright golden earrings, बली a strong man, दत्तवरः bestowed with boons, अहम् I, दर्पात् with pride, तदाश्रमम् the hermitage, आजगाम arrived.

#### Explanation

Then looking like a dark cloud, wearing bright golden earrings, proud of power bestowed on me with boons, I arrived at the hermitage.

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

तेन दृष्टः प्रविष्टोऽहं सहसैवोद्यतायुधः। मां तु दृष्ट्वा धनुस्सज्यमसम्भ्रान्तश्चकार सः।।3.38.17।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

उद्यतायुधः  with raised weapons, प्रविष्टः entered, अहम् I, सहसैव at once, तेन by him, Rama, दृष्टः seen, सः he, माम् me, दृष्ट्वा after seeing, असम्भ्रान्तः unperturbed, धनुः bow, सज्यम् stringing, चकार did.

#### Explanation

I entered at once and lifted my weapons. Rama saw me and unperturbed, started stringing his bow.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

अवजानन्नहं मोहाद्बालोऽयमिति राघवम्। विश्वामित्रस्य तां वेदिमभ्यधावं कृतत्वरः।।3.38.18।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अहम् I, मोहात् due to delusion, अयम् this, बालः young boy, इति in this way, राघवम् Rama, अवजानन् underestimating, कृतत्वरः making sounds, विश्वामित्रस्य Visvamitra's, ताम् that, वेदिम् sacrificial altar, अभ्यधावम् ran towards the same.

#### Explanation

Out of my delusion I underestimated the young boy, and screaming, ran towards Visvamitra's sacrificial altar.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

तेन मुक्तस्ततो बाणः शितश्शत्रुनिबर्हणः। तेनाहं त्वाहतः क्षिप्तस्समुद्रे शतयोजने।।3.38.19।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, तेन by him, शत्रुनिबर्हणः a subduer of enemy, शितः sharp, बाणः arrow, मुक्तः unleashed, अहम् on me, तेन with that, आहतः struck, शतयोजने one hundred yojanas away, समुद्रे into the sea, आक्षिप्त: I was thrown.

#### Explanation

Then Rama released a sharp arrow that could kill the enemy. It struck me and threw me at a distance of hundred yojanas into the sea.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

नेच्छता तात मां हन्तुं तदा वीरेण रक्षितः। रामस्य शरवेगेन निरस्तोऽहमचेतनः।।3.38.20।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तात O dear, तदा then, माम् me, हन्तुम् to kill, नेच्छता by not intending, वीरेण रक्षितः protected by the hero, रामस्य Rama's, शरवेगेन by the speed of his dart, अचेतनः lost conciousness, अहम् I, निरस्तः thrown away.

#### Explanation

O dear being thrown away by the speed of his dart I lost my consciousness. I was protected by Rama who did not want to kill me.

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

पातितोऽहं तदा तेन गम्भीरे सागराम्भसि। प्राप्य संज्ञां चिरात्तात लङ्कां प्रतिगतः पुरीम्।।3.38.21।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तात O dear, तदा then, अहम् I, गम्भीरे in deep, सागराम्भसि in sea water, पातितः was thrown, चिरात् after a long time, संज्ञाम् consciousess, प्राप्य after attaining, लङ्कां पुरीम् city of Lanka, प्रतिगतः went towards.

#### Explanation

O dear I was thrown into the deep sea and after a long time regained consciousness and  returned to the city of Lanka.

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

एवमस्मि तदा मुक्तस्सहायास्तु निपातिताः। अकृतास्त्रेण बालेन रामेणाक्लिष्टकर्मणा।।3.38.22।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तदा then, अकृतास्त्रेण who had not mastered archery, बालेन by the young boy, अक्लिष्टकर्मणा by one who acomplishes any task with ease, रामेण by Rama, एवम् in that way, मुक्तः अस्मि I was spared, सहायास्तु others who accompanied me, निपातिताः were destroyed.

#### Explanation

That way I was spared by the young boy who was not versed in archery and who could still accomplish the task with ease.Those who accompanied me were destroyed.

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

तन्मया वार्यमाणस्त्वं यदि रामेण विग्रहम्। करिष्यस्यापदं घोरां क्षिप्रं प्राप्स्यसि रावण।।3.38.23।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रावण Ravana, तत् that, मया by me, वार्यमाणः while being prevented, त्वम् you, रामेण with Rama, विग्रहम् conflict, करिष्यसि यदि if you wage, क्षिप्रम् very soon, घोराम् dreadful, आपदम् calamity, प्राप्स्यसि you will face.

#### Explanation

If you pick up conflict with Rama even while being warned by me you will soon face a dreadful calamity.

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

क्रीडारतिविधिज्ञानां समाजोत्सवशालिनाम्। रक्षसां चैव सन्तापमनर्थं चाहरिष्यसि।।3.38.24।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

क्रीडारतिविधिज्ञानाम् of those who know lovesport, समाजोत्सवशालिनाम् of those who celebrate community festivals, रक्षसां चैव of demons too, सन्तापम् grief, अनर्थं च disaster, आहरिष्यसि you will bring.

#### Explanation

You will bring grief and disaster into the lives of demons who are sporting in love and who are in the habit of celebrating community festivals with joy.

---

### Shloka 25

#### Shloka Text

हर्म्यप्रासादसम्बाधां नानारत्नविभूषिताम्। द्रक्ष्यसि त्वं पुरीं लङ्कां विनष्टां मैथिलीकृते।।3.38.25।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

हर्म्यप्रासादसम्बाधाम्  with magnificent royal mansions, नानारत्नविभूषिताम् embellisted with various gems, लङ्कां पुरीम् city of Lanka, त्वम् you, मैथिलीकृते for the sake of Sita, विनष्टाम् ruined, द्रक्ष्यसि you will see.

#### Explanation

You will see the city of Lanka dotted with magnificent royal mansions embellished  with different kinds of gems ruined for the sake of Sita.

---

### Shloka 26

#### Shloka Text

अकुर्वन्तोऽपि पापानि शुचयः पापसंश्रयात्। परपापैर्विनश्यन्ति मत्स्या नागह्रदे यथा।।3.38.26।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पापानि sins, अकुर्वन्तोऽपि even without committing, शुचयः pure men, पापसंश्रयात् by contact with sinners, नागह्रदे in a pool of serpents, मत्स्याः यथा like the fish, परपापैः sins committed by others, विनश्यन्ति will be destroyed.

#### Explanation

Even without committing any sin those pure souls because they keep company with sinners will be destroyed for the sins of others just like fishes in a pool of serpents.

---

### Shloka 27

#### Shloka Text

दिव्यचन्दनदिग्धाङ्गान्दिव्याभरणभूषितान्। द्रक्ष्यस्यभिहतान्भूमौ तव दोषात्तु राक्षसान्।।3.38.27।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

दिव्यचन्दनदिग्धाङ्गान् with limbs smeared with sandal paste and unguents, दिव्याभरणभूषितान् adorned with excellent ornaments, राक्षसान् demons, तव दोषात् for your mistakes, भूमौ on this earth, अभिहतान् killed, द्रक्ष्यसि you will see.

#### Explanation

You will see the demons who smear their limbs with sandal paste and unguents, who are adorned with excellent ornaments slain and laid on the ground for your mistakes.

---

### Shloka 28

#### Shloka Text

हृतदारान् सदारांश्च दश विद्रवतो दिशः। हतशेषानशरणान्द्रक्ष्यसि त्वं निशाचरान्।।3.38.28।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

हतशेषान् the surviving, निशाचरान् demons, हृतदारान् with their wives abducted, सदारांश्च those with wives, अशरणान् with none to take care of them, दश दिशः in ten directions, विद्रवतः while running, त्वम् you, द्रक्ष्यसि you will see.

#### Explanation

You will see the rest of the demons with their wives gone or fleeing in all directions helplessly with none to take care of them.

---

### Shloka 29

#### Shloka Text

शरजालपरिक्षिप्तामग्निज्वालासमावृताम्। प्रदग्धभवनां लङ्कां द्रक्ष्यसि त्वं न संशयः।।3.38.29।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

शरजालपरिक्षिप्ताम् scattered arrows, अग्निज्वालासमावृताम् surrounded by flames of fire, प्रदग्धभवनाम् with buildings on fire, लङ्काम् Lanka, त्वम् you, द्रक्ष्यसि you will see, संशयः doubt, न not.

#### Explanation

You will undoubtedly see Lanka filled with arrows, surrounded by flames of fire and its buildings on fire.

---

### Shloka 30

#### Shloka Text

परदाराभिमर्शात्तु नान्यत्पापतरं महत्। प्रमदानां सहस्रं च तव राजन्परिग्रहः।।3.38.30।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

परदाराभिमर्शात्  violating the chastity of others' wives, पापतरम् a greater  sin, महत् highly, अन्यत् other thing, न not, राजन् O king, प्रमदानाम् women  सहस्रम् thousand, तव your, परिग्रहःwhom you married,

#### Explanation

O king there is no sin greater than violating the chastity of others wives, when you have married a thousand lovely women.

---

### Shloka 31

#### Shloka Text

भव स्वदारनिरतस्वकुलं रक्ष राक्षस। मानमृद्धिं च राज्यं च जीवितं चेष्टमात्मनः।।3.38.31।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षस O demon, स्वदारनिरतः enjoy your own wives, भव be, स्वकुलम् your race, रक्ष you protect, मानम् prestige, ऋद्धिं च prosperity also, राज्यं च kingdom too, आत्मनः your, इष्टम्  dear, जीवितं च and your life.

#### Explanation

Enjoy with your wives. Protect your race, prestige, kingdom, prosperity and your own dear life.

---

### Shloka 32

#### Shloka Text

कलत्राणि च सौम्यानि मित्रवर्गं तथैव च। यदीच्छसि चिरं भोक्तं मा कृथा रामविप्रियम्।।3.38.32।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सौम्यानि beautiful ones, कलत्राणि wives, तथैव च similarly, मित्रवर्गम् friends, चिरम् for a long time, भोक्तुम् to enjoy, इच्छसि यदि wish to, राम विप्रियम् offence to Rama, मा कृथाः do not do.

#### Explanation

If you wish to enjoy your beautiful wives and your good friends for long, do not create enmity with Rama.

---

### Shloka 33

#### Shloka Text

निवार्यमाणस्सुहृदा मया भृशं प्रसह्य सीतां यदि धर्षयिष्यसि। गमिष्यसि क्षीणबलस्सबान्धवो यमक्षयं रामशरात्तजीवितः।।3.38.33।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सुहृदा by a friend, मया by myself, भृशम् profusely, निवार्यमाणः prevented, सीताम् Sita, प्रसह्य forcibly, धर्षयिष्यसि यदि if you violate Sita, क्षीणबलः with reduced strength, सबान्धवः along with your kin, रामशरात्तजीवितः your life taken away by Rama's arrows, यमक्षयम् abode of Yama , गमिष्यसि you will go.

#### Explanation

If you violate Sita forcibly notwithstanding my friendly warning, your life will be drained by the arrows of Rama and you will reach the abode of Yama with your kith and kin, your strength depleted. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये अरण्यकाण्डे अष्टत्रिंशस्सर्गः। Thus ends the thirtyeighth sarga of Aranyakanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 39

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

एवमस्मि तदा मुक्तः कथञ्चित्तेन संयुगे। इदानीमपि यद्वृत्तं तच्छृणुष्व निरुत्तरम्।।3.39.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तदा then, तेन by him, संयुगे in the combat, कथञ्चित् somehow , एवम् in that way, मुक्तः अस्मि I was released, इदानीमपि recently also, यत् whatever, वृत्तम् has taken place, तत् that, निरुत्तरम् without any reply, शृणुष्व listen to it.

#### Explanation

I was somehow saved by him this way in the combat.Listen without any reply to what has taken place recently.

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

राक्षसाभ्यामहं द्वाभ्यामनिर्विण्णस्तथा कृतः। सहितो मृगरूपाभ्यां प्रविष्टो दण्डकावनम्।।3.39.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तथाकृतः having done so, अनिर्विण्णः unworried, अहम् I, मृगरूपाभ्याम् in the form of deer, द्वाभ्याम् by two, राक्षसाभ्याम् by demons, सहितः accompanied by, दण्डकावनम् forest of Dandaka, प्रविष्टः entered.

#### Explanation

Having done so, I picked up courage and entered Dandaka forest, accompanied by the two other demons in the form of deer .

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

दीप्तजिह्वो महाकायस्तीक्ष्णदंष्ट्रो महाबलः। व्यचरं दण्डकारण्यं मांसभक्षो महामृगः।।3.39.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

दीप्तजिह्वः flaming red tongue, महाकायः big body, तीक्ष्णदंष्ट्रः sharp teeth, महाबलः very strong , मांसभक्षः feeding on flesh, महामृगः a huge animal, दण्डकारण्यम् Dandaka forest, व्यचरम् wandered.

#### Explanation

I wandered in Dandaka forest in the form of a huge animal with a big and strong body, a flaming red tongue and sharp teeth feeding on flesh.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

अग्निहोत्रेषु तीर्थेषु चैत्यवृक्षेषु रावण। अत्यन्तघोरो व्यचरं तापसान्सम्प्रधर्षयन्।।3.39.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रावण Ravana, अत्यन्तघोरः very dreadful , अग्निहोत्रेषु at the sacrificial sites, तीर्थेषु at sacred rivers, चैत्यवृक्षेषु alter at the root of fig trees in sacred places, तापसान् ascetics, सम्प्रधर्षयन् torturing, व्यचरम् I wandered.

#### Explanation

O Ravana, assuming a very dreadful form, I wandered about sacrificial spots, sacred places, river banks, under huge trees planted at quadranglestorturing the ascetics.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

निहत्य दण्डकारण्ये तापसान्धर्मचारिणः। रुधिराणि पिबंस्तेषां तथा मांसानि भक्षयन्।।3.39.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

दण्डकारण्ये in the Dandaka forest, धर्मचारिणः following righteousness, तापसान् ascetics, निहत्य after slaying, तेषाम् their, रुधिराणि  blood, पिबन् drinking, तथा likewise, मांसानि flesh, भक्षयन् eating.

#### Explanation

I wandered  about the Dandaka forest slaying righteous ascetics, drinking their blood and eating their flesh.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

ऋषिमांसाशनः क्रूरस्त्रासयन्वनगोचरान्। तथा रुधिरमत्तोऽहं विचरन्धर्मदूषकः।।3.39.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अहम् I, ऋषिमांसाशनः eater of the flesh of ascetics, क्रूरः cruel, वनगोचरान् roamers of the forest, त्रासयन् while frightening, तथा likewise, रुधिरमत्तः intoxicated with their blood, धर्मदूषकः harming the righteous, विचरन् wandered.

#### Explanation

While thus roaming, I harmed the wanderers of the forest. Intoxicated with their blood I used to frighten the righteous.

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

आसादयं तदा रामं तापसं धर्मचारिणम्। वैदेहीं च महाभागां लक्ष्मणं च महारथम्।।3.39.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तदा then, धर्मचारिणम् the followers of the righteous path, तापसम् ascetic, रामम् Rama, महाभागाम् venerable lady, वैदेहीं च Vaidehi too, महारथम् great  warrior, लक्ष्मणं च and Lakshmana, आसादयम् I encountered.

#### Explanation

I encountered again the righteous Rama who was in the form of an ascetic, with venerable Sita and the great warrior Lakshmana.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

तापसं नियताहारं सर्वभूतहिते रतम्। सोऽहं वनगतं रामं परिभूय महाबलम्।।3.39.8।। तापसोऽयमिति ज्ञात्वा पूर्ववैरमनुस्मरन्। अभ्यधावं हि संक्रुद्धस्तीक्ष्ण शृङ्गो मृगाकृतिः।।3.39.9।। जिघांसुरकृतप्रज्ञस्तं प्रहारमनुस्मरन्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मृगाकृतिः in the form of a deer, तीक्ष्णशृङ्ग: pointed horns, सः अहम् that me, पूर्ववैरम् earlier hostility, अनुस्मरन् remembering, तापसम् ascetic, नियताहारम् with restricted food, सर्वभूतहिते wellwisher of all beings, रतम् engaged, महाबलम् very strong, वनगतम् came to the forest, रामम् Rama, अयम् this, तापसः an ascetic, इति thus thinking, परिभूय disregarding, तं प्रहारम्  his blow in the past, अनुस्मरन् remembering, जिघांसुः with an intention of killing, सङ्कृद्धः very angry, अभ्यधावम् ran towards him.

#### Explanation

Although Rama was very strong, he was living on restricted diet, engaged in the welfare of all beings. Treating him as a mere ascetic living in the forest, remembering my past hostility with him and disregarding his (fatal) blow, I ran towards him angrily, assuming the form of a sharphorned animal with an intention to kill him.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

तापसं नियताहारं सर्वभूतहिते रतम्। सोऽहं वनगतं रामं परिभूय महाबलम्।।3.39.8।। तापसोऽयमिति ज्ञात्वा पूर्ववैरमनुस्मरन्। अभ्यधावं हि संक्रुद्धस्तीक्ष्ण शृङ्गो मृगाकृतिः।।3.39.9।। जिघांसुरकृतप्रज्ञस्तं प्रहारमनुस्मरन्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मृगाकृतिः in the form of a deer, तीक्ष्णशृङ्ग: pointed horns, सः अहम् that me, पूर्ववैरम् earlier hostility, अनुस्मरन् remembering, तापसम् ascetic, नियताहारम् with restricted food, सर्वभूतहिते wellwisher of all beings, रतम् engaged, महाबलम् very strong, वनगतम् came to the forest, रामम् Rama, अयम् this, तापसः an ascetic, इति thus thinking, परिभूय disregarding, तं प्रहारम्  his blow in the past, अनुस्मरन् remembering, जिघांसुः with an intention of killing, सङ्कृद्धः very angry, अभ्यधावम् ran towards him.

#### Explanation

Although Rama was very strong, he was living on restricted diet, engaged in the welfare of all beings. Treating him as a mere ascetic living in the forest, remembering my past hostility with him and disregarding his (fatal) blow, I ran towards him angrily, assuming the form of a sharphorned animal with an intention to kill him.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

तेन मुक्तास्त्रयो बाणाश्शिताश्शत्रुनिबर्हणाः।।3.39.10।। विकृष्य बलवच्चापं सुपर्णानिलनिस्स्वनाः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तेन by him, चापम् bow, बलवत् might, विकृष्य having drawn, शत्रुनिबर्हणाः slayers of enemies, शिताः sharp, सुपर्णानिलनिस्वनाः whizzing like Garuda or the wind, त्रयः three, बाणाः darts, मुक्ताः released.

#### Explanation

His mighty bow drawn, Rama released three sharp darts that came whizzing like Garuda or the wind. They could destroy the enemy.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

ते बाणा वज्रसङ्काशास्सुमुक्ता रक्तभोजनाः। आजग्मुस्सहितास्सर्वे त्रयस्सन्नतपर्वणः।।3.39.11।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वज्रसङ्काशाः  like the thunderbolt, रक्तभोजनाः  which feed on blood, सन्नतपर्वणः with strong joints, सर्वे all, ते त्रयः बाणाः the three darts, सुमुक्ताः welltargetted, सहिताः together, आजग्मुः came at once.

#### Explanation

All the three tightly jointed, welltargetted darts, strong as thunderbolt, and which could feed on blood came together all at once.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

पराक्रमज्ञो रामस्य शठो दृष्टभयः पुरा। समुद्भान्तस्ततोमुक्तस्तावुभौ राक्षसौ हतौ।।3.39.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रामस्य Rama's, शठ: deceitful, पुरा earlier, दृष्टभयः having seen that fearful, पराक्रमज्ञः  knower of his prowess, समुद्भ्रान्तः in a bewildered state, ततः then, मुक्तः let away, तौ two others, राक्षसौ two demons, उभौ both, हतौ were killed.

#### Explanation

Having known Rama's prowess earlier and experienced tremendous fear, I ran away in a bewildered state as I was deceitful. The other two demons (who had accompanied me) succumbed.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

शरेण मुक्तो रामस्य कथञ्चित्प्राप्य जीवितम्। इह प्रव्राजितो युक्तस्तापसोऽहं समाहितः।।3.39.13।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अहम् I, रामस्य Rama's, शरेण by the darts, कथञ्चित् somehow, मुक्तः escaped, जीवितम् life, प्राप्य having attained, युक्तः engaged in, समाहितः composed state, तापसः an ascetic, इह here, प्रव्राजितः have started living the life of a recluse.

#### Explanation

With my escape from Rama's darts I somehow got a fresh lease of life. Now I am composed. I have started living the life of a recluse here. I have turned an ascetic.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

वृक्षे वृक्षे च पश्यामि चीरकृष्णाजिनाम्बरम्। गृहीतधनुषं रामं पाशहस्तमिवान्तकम्।।3.39.14।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

चीरकृष्णाजिनाम्बरम् clad in bark and deerskin, गृहीतधनुषम् holding the bow, पाशहस्तम् having  a noose in hand, अन्तकमिव like the god of death, रामम् Rama, वृक्षे वृक्षे in every tree, पश्यामि I see.

#### Explanation

I see Rama in every tree, clad in bark and deerskin, wielding the bow, holding a noose in hand like the god of death.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

अपि रामसहस्राणि भीतः पश्यामि रावण। रामभूतमिदं सर्वमरण्यं प्रतिभाति मे।।3.39.15।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रावण Ravana, भीतः fearful, रामसहस्राण्यपि thousands of Ramas, पश्यामि I see, इदम् this, सर्वम् all over, अरण्यम् forest, रामभूतम् filled with Rama, मे to me, प्रतिभाति appears.

#### Explanation

O Ravana I see in my fear thousands of Ramas. The entire forest appears to me as though filled with Rama.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

राममेव हि पश्यामि रहिते राक्षसाधिप। दृष्ट्वा स्वप्नगतं राममुद्भ्रमामि विचेतनः।।3.39.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षसाधिप O king of the demons, रहिते in a solitary state, राममेव only Rama, पश्यामि I see, स्वप्नगतम् even in my dreams, रामम् Rama, दृष्ट्वा after seeing, विचेतनः senseless, उद्भ्रमामि I am bewildered.

#### Explanation

O king of the demons  I see only Rama even in solitude. When I see him in a dream  I lose my senses in a state of bewilderment.

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

रकारादीनि नामानि रामत्रस्तस्य रावण। रत्नानि च रथाश्चैव त्रासं संजनयन्ति मे।।3.39.17।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रावण Ravana, रामत्रस्तस्य for one frightened by Rama, मे to, me, रकारादीनि नामानि names beginning with 'Ra', रत्नानि gems, रथाश्चैव chariots also, त्रासम् fear, सञ्जनयन्ति create in me.

#### Explanation

O Ravana, every name beginning with the letter 'Ra', such as Ratna (gems), Ratha (chariot), etc. strike terror in me.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

अहं तस्य प्रभावज्ञो न युद्धं तेन ते क्षमम्। बलिं वा नमुचिं वापि हन्याद्धि रघुनन्दनः।।3.39.18।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अहम् I, तस्य his, प्रभावज्ञः knower of his power, ते to you, तेन with him, युद्धम् war, क्षमम्  proper, न not, रघुनन्दनः delight of the Raghu dynasty (Rama), बलिं वा Bali or, नमुचिं वापि even Namuchi, हन्याद्धि can slay.

#### Explanation

I know his power.It is not proper to wage a war with him. Rama, delight of the Raghu dynasty, can slay even Bali or Namuchi.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

रणे रामेण युद्ध्यस्व क्षमां वा कुरु राक्षस । न ते रामकथा कार्या यदि मां द्रष्टुमिच्छसि ।।3.39.19 ।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

Missing

#### Explanation

Missing

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

बहवस्साधवो लोके युक्ता धर्ममनुष्ठिताः। परेषामपराधेन विनष्ठास्सपरिच्छदाः।।3.39.20।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लोके in the world, युक्ताः absorbed in, धर्मम् righteous ways, अनुष्ठिताः followers, बहवः many, साधव: pious people, परेषाम् of others, अपराधेन by their mistake, सपरिच्छदाः along with all their followers, विनष्टाः are destroyed.

#### Explanation

There were many pious men in this world who practise yoga and dharma .They have been destroyed along with their retinue due to mistakes committed by others.

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

सोऽऽहं तवापराधेन विनश्येयं निशाचर। कुरु यत्ते क्षमं तत्त्वमहं त्वा नानुयामि ह।।3.39.21।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

निशाचर O demon, सः अहम् that I, तव अपराधेन by your mistake, विनश्येयम् I will be ruined, ते to you, यत् whatever, क्षमम् appropriate, तत् that, त्वम् you, कुरु you may do, अहम् I, त्वा you, न अनुयामि ह will not follow.

#### Explanation

O demon   As such, I will perish due to your offence. You may do anything you deem appropriate. I will not follow you.

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

रामश्च हि महातेजा महासत्त्वो महाबलः।।3.39.22।। अपि राक्षसलोकस्य न भवेदन्तकोऽपि सः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रामश्च Rama also, महातेजाः brilliant, महासत्त्व: very powerful, महाबलः very strong, सः he, राक्षसलोकस्य for the community of demons, अन्तकः god of death, न भवेदपि not be.

#### Explanation

Rama is brilliant, powerful and strong. I hope he will not be the god of death for the world of demons.

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

यदि शूर्पणखाहेतोर्जनस्थानगतः खरः।।3.39.23।। अतिवृत्तो हतः पूर्वं रामेणाक्लिष्टकर्मणा। अत्र ब्रूहि यथातत्त्वं को रामस्य व्यतिक्रमः।।3.39.24।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

जनस्थानगतः staying at Janasthana, खरः Khara, शूर्पणखाहेतोः for the sake of Surpanakha, पूर्वम् earlier, अतिवृत्तः a person who exceeded his limits, अक्लिष्टकर्मणा by one who can accomplish a work easily, रामेण by Rama, हतः slain, यदि if, अत्र here, रामस्य Rama's, व्यतिक्रमः violation, कः what, यथातत्त्वम् truly, ब्रूहि tell me.

#### Explanation

For the sake of Surpanakha, Khara of Janasthana committed excesses and got killed by Rama who can accomplish things with ease.Tell me truly if Rama did violate anything here.

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

यदि शूर्पणखाहेतोर्जनस्थानगतः खरः।।3.39.23।। अतिवृत्तो हतः पूर्वं रामेणाक्लिष्टकर्मणा। अत्र ब्रूहि यथातत्त्वं को रामस्य व्यतिक्रमः।।3.39.24।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

जनस्थानगतः staying at Janasthana, खरः Khara, शूर्पणखाहेतोः for the sake of Surpanakha, पूर्वम् earlier, अतिवृत्तः a person who exceeded his limits, अक्लिष्टकर्मणा by one who can accomplish a work easily, रामेण by Rama, हतः slain, यदि if, अत्र here, रामस्य Rama's, व्यतिक्रमः violation, कः what, यथातत्त्वम् truly, ब्रूहि tell me.

#### Explanation

For the sake of Surpanakha, Khara of Janasthana committed excesses and got killed by Rama who can accomplish things with ease.Tell me truly if Rama did violate anything here.

---

### Shloka 25

#### Shloka Text

इदं वचो बन्धुहितार्थिनामया यथोच्यमानं यदि नाभिपत्स्यसे। सबान्धवस्त्यक्ष्यसि जीवितं रणे हतोऽऽद्य रामेण शरैरजिह्मगैः।।3.39.25।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

बन्धुहितार्थिना one seeking the welfare of relations, मया by myself, उच्यमानम् being spoken, वचः word, यथा as, न no, अभिपत्यसे यदि you do not follow, सबान्धवः with all relations, अद्य now, रामेण by Rama, अजिह्मगैः that which moves straight, शरैः with darts, हतः you are  slayed, रणे in war, जीवितम् life, त्यक्षयसि you will give up.

#### Explanation

If you do not follow my advice given for the welfare of our kith and kin, O Ravana, you are killed today. With your relations you will succumb to his straightmoving arrows. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये अरण्यकाण्डे एकोनचत्वारिंशस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the thirtyninth sarga of Aranyakanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 40

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

मारीचेन तु तद्वाक्यं क्षमं युक्तं च निशाचरः। उक्तो न प्रतिजग्राह मर्तुकाम इवौषधम्।।3.40.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

उक्तः spoken, निशाचरः demon, (रावणः Ravana), मारीचेन by Maricha, क्षमम् competent, युक्तं च suitable, तत् that, वाक्यम् advice, मर्तुकामः one who wishes to die, औषधम् इव like medicine, न प्रतिजग्राह did not accept৷৷

#### Explanation

The demon Ravana did not heed the appropriate advice of the competent Maricha just like a man wishing to die refuses medicine.

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

तं पथ्यहितवक्तारं मारीचं राक्षसाधिपः। अब्रवीत्परुषं वाक्यमयुक्तं कालचोदितः।।3.40.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षसाधिपः king of the demons, कालचोदितः driven by fate, पथ्यहितवक्तारम् to one who spoke salutary and beneficial words, तं मारीचम् that Maricha, अयुक्तम् not appropriate, परुषम् harsh, वाक्यम् word, अब्रवीत् said.

#### Explanation

The king of the demons, driven by fate, replied with improper and harsh words to Maricha whose advice was salutary and beneficial to him :

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

यत्किलैतदयुक्तार्थं मारीच मयि कथ्यते। वाक्यं निष्फलमत्यर्थमुप्तं बीजमिवोषरे।।3.40.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मारीच O Maricha, अयुक्तार्थम् in appropriate, यत् which, वाक्यम् words, मयि towards me, कथ्यते  is being said, ऊषरे in saline soil, उप्तम् sown, बीजमिव like the seed, अत्यर्थम् very much, निष्फलम् useless.

#### Explanation

O Maricha, the words addressed to me are not appropriate.Your advice is extremely fruitless like a seed sown in saline, barren soil.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

त्वद्वाक्यैर्न तु मां शक्यं भेत्तुं रामस्य संयुगे। पापशीलस्य मूर्खस्य मानुषस्य विशेषतः।।3.40.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

त्वद्वाक्यैः by your words, पापशीलस्य of a sinner, मूर्खस्य of a foolish one, विशेषतः specially, मानुषस्य of a human being, रामस्य of Rama, माम् me, संयुगे in a combat, भेत्तुम् to break, न शक्यम् not possible.

#### Explanation

It is not possible to dissuade me from my encounter with Rama who is sinful, foolish and especially an ordinary human being.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

यस्त्यक्त्वा सुहृदो राज्यं मातरं पितरं तथा। स्त्रीवाक्यं प्राकृतं श्रुत्वा वनमेकपदे गतः।।3.40.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

यः he who, प्राकृतम् ordinary, स्त्रीवाक्यम् woman's word, श्रुत्वा after hearing, सुहृदः friends, राज्यम् kingdom, मातरम् mother, तथा also, पितरम् father, त्यक्त्वा after leaving, एकपदे at once, वनम् to the forest, गतः went.

#### Explanation

On hearing the casual words of a woman, (this) Rama came to the forest at once, leaving his friends, kingdom, mother and father.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

अवश्यन्तु मया तस्य संयुगे खरघातिनः। प्राणैः प्रियतरा सीता हर्तव्या तव सन्निधौ।।3.40.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

संयुगे in a war, खरघातिनः of one who killed Khara, तस्य his, प्राणैः more than his life, प्रियतरा dearer, (भार्या wife), सीता Sita, तव your, सन्निधौ in the presence, अवश्यम् surely, मया by myself, हर्तव्या will abduct.

#### Explanation

In your very presence I will surely abduct Sita, wife of Rama who killed Khara in war, Sita whom he loves more than his life.

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

एवं मे निश्चिता बुद्धिर्हृदि मारीच वर्तते। न व्यावर्तयितुं शक्या सेन्द्रैरपि सुरासुरैः।।3.40.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मारीच Maricha, मे my, बुद्धि mind, एवम् this way, निश्चिता made up, हृदि in my heart, वर्तते stop, सेन्द्रैः with Indra, सुरासुरैरपि deities or demons, व्यावर्तयितुम् to change, न शक्या not possible.

#### Explanation

O Maricha  I have made up my mind that way. Even demons or deities including Indra cannot alter this decision of mine.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

दोषं गुणं वा सम्पृष्टस्त्वमेवं वक्तुमर्हसि। अपायं वाप्युपायं वा कार्यस्यास्य विनिश्चये।।3.40.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अस्य कार्यस्य in this task, विनिश्चये  this decision, दोषम् demerits, गुणं वा or merit, अपायम् danger, उपायं वा or any way out, सम्पृष्टः asked of you, त्वम् you, एवम् in that way, वक्तुम् अर्हसि should say.

#### Explanation

If I asked you the merits or demerits, the risks involved or the way out while deciding a case, you should have said what you did.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

सम्पृष्टेन तु वक्तव्यं सचिवेन विपश्चिता। उद्यताञ्जलिना राज्ञे य इच्छेद्भूतिमात्मनः।।3.40.9।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

यः whoever, आत्मनः his, भूतिम् riches, इच्छेत् wants, सम्पृष्टेन by him when asked, विपश्चिता by a wise man, सचिवेन by a minister, उद्यताञ्जलिना by him with folded palms, राज्ञे to the king, वक्तव्यम् be said.

#### Explanation

A wise counsellor should give advice to the king only when his opinion is sought and that too with folded hands, if he wishes his own prosperity.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

वाक्यमप्रितकूलं तु मृदुपूर्वं हितं शुभम्। उपचारेण युक्तं च वक्तव्यो वसुधाधिपः।।3.40.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वसुधाधिपः lord of the earth, अप्रतिकूलम् not unfavourable, मृदुपूर्वम् politely, हितम्  welfare, शुभम् auspicious,उपचारेण with proper decorum, युक्तम् with, वाक्यम् word, वक्तव्यः  suggested.

#### Explanation

A king should be addressed with politeness and decorum, with words not unfavourble intended for his good luck and wellbeing.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

सावमर्दं तु यद्वाक्यं मारीच हितमुच्यते। नाभिनन्दति तद्राजा मानार्हो मानवर्जितम्।।3.40.11।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मारीच O Maricha, मानवर्जितम् disrespectful, यत् whatever, हितम् good, वाक्यम् words, सावमर्दम् in an oppressive way, उच्यते when spoken, तत् that, मानार्हः who deserves respect, राजा king, नाभिनन्दति does not like.

#### Explanation

A king who deserves honour does not feel happy to hear even good words of advice if said in disrespectful or authoritarian way, O Maricha

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

पञ्च रूपाणि राजानो धारयन्त्यमितौजसः। अग्नेरिन्द्रस्य सोमस्य वरुणस्य यमस्य च।।3.40.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अमितौजस: very powerful , राजानः kings, अग्नेः of fire, इन्द्रस्य of Indra, सोमस्य Moon's, वरुणस्य Varuna's, यमस्य च and of lord of death, पञ्च five, रूपाणि forms, धारयन्ति will assume.

#### Explanation

Very powerful kings assume five forms like fire, Indra, Moon, Varuna and Yama.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

औष्ण्यं तथा विक्रमं च सौम्यं दण्डं प्रसन्नताम्। धारयन्ति महात्मानो राजानः क्षणदाचर।।3.40.13।। तस्मात्सर्वास्ववस्थासु मान्याः पूज्याश्च पार्थिवाः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

क्षणदाचर of demon, महात्मनः great self, राजानः kings, औष्ण्यम् heat, तथा similarly, विक्रमं च   valour, सौम्यम् gentleness, दण्डम् command, प्रसन्नताम् grace, धारयन्ति possess, तस्मात् therefore, पार्थिवाः kings, सर्वासु at all, अवस्थासु stages, मान्याः deserve to be respected, पूज्याश्च worthy of reverence.

#### Explanation

Great kings possess heat, (of the firegod), valour (of Indra), coolness (of the Moon), command (of Varuna) and grace (of the Lord of death). Therefore kings at all stages deserve respect and reverence.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

त्वं तु धर्ममविज्ञाय केवलं मोहमास्थितः। अभ्यागतं मां दौरात्म्यात्परुषं वक्तुमिच्छसि।।3.40.14।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

त्वं तु you on your part, धर्मम् rightful duty, अविज्ञाय not knowing, केवलम् only, मोहम् because of delusion, आस्थितः have taken recourse to, अभ्यागतम् to a guest (who comes without prior announcement), माम् me, दौरात्म्यात् out of evil mind, परुषम् harsh, वक्तुम् to speak, इच्छसि want.

#### Explanation

You do not know your rightful duty.Because of delusion and an evil mind, you want to speak only harsh words to me, a guest who has come to you.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

गुणदोषौ न पृच्छामि क्षमं चात्मनि राक्षस। मयोक्तं तव चैतावत्संप्रत्यमितविक्रमः।।3.40.15।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षस O demon, गुणदोषौ good or bad, आत्मनि  yourself, क्षमम् welfare, न पृच्छामि not asked you, अमितविक्रमः extremely valiant one, सम्प्रति now, तव your, एतावत् as such, मया by me, उक्तम् asked.

#### Explanation

O demon  O extremely valiant demon  I ask you(to do) this much now. I am not asking you whether it is good or bad or whether it is for my welfare or not.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

अस्मिंस्तु त्वं महाकृत्ये साहाय्यं कर्तुमर्हसि। शृणु तत्कर्म साहाय्ये यत्कार्यं वचनान्मम।।3.40.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अस्मिन् in this, महाकृत्ये great task, त्वम् you, साहाय्यम् help, कर्तुम् to do, अर्हसि behove, मम वचनात् by my words, साहाय्ये render help, यत् that which, कार्यम् task, तत् that, कर्म action, शृणु listen.

#### Explanation

You have to help me in accomplishing this great task .Hear me on the kind of work you have to do.

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

सौवर्णस्त्वं मृगो भूत्वा चित्रो रजतबिन्दुभिः । आश्रमे तस्य रामस्य सीतायाः प्रमुखे चर ।।3.40.17 ।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

Missing

#### Explanation

Missing

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

सौवर्णस्त्वं मृगो भूत्वा चित्रो रजतबिन्दुभिः। आश्रमे तस्य रामस्य सीतायाः प्रमुखे चर। प्रलोभयित्वा वैदेहीं यथेष्टं गन्तुमर्हसि।।3.40.18।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

त्वम् you, रजतबिन्दुभिः with silver spots, चित्रः wonderful, सौवर्णः golden, मृगः deer, भूत्वा being, तस्य his, रामस्य Rama's, आश्रमे in hermitage, सीतायाः Sita's, प्रमुखे in front, चर go about, वैदेहीम् Sita, प्रलोभयित्वा after fascinating her, यथेष्टम् as you desire, गन्तुम् to go, अर्हसि you may.

#### Explanation

Transform yourself into a wonderful golden deer with silver spots and move about in front of Sita at the hermitage of Rama. After fascinating her, you may go whereever you desire.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

त्वां तु मायामृगं दृष्ट्वा काञ्चनं जातविस्मया। आनयैनमिति क्षिप्रं रामं वक्ष्यति मैथिली।।3.40.19।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मैथिली Sita, काञ्चनम् golden, मायामृगम् illusory deer, त्वाम् you, दृष्ट्वा after seeing, जातविस्मया  wonderstruck, एनं this deer, क्षिप्रम् quickly, आनय get me, इति thus, रामम् Rama, वक्ष्यति will say.

#### Explanation

Seeing the illusory golden deer, Sita, wonderstruck, will at once ask Rama to get the deer.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

अपक्रान्ते तु काकुत्स्थे दूरं यात्वाप्युदाहर। हा सीते लक्ष्मणेत्येवं रामवाक्यानुरूपकम्।।3.40.20।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

काकुत्स्थे  the scion of the Kakutsthas, अपक्रान्ते diverted, दूरम् to a distance, यात्वा  going, रामवाक्यानुरूपकम् resembling Rama's voice, हा Alas, सीते Sita, हा लक्ष्मण Alas, Lakshmana, इत्येवम् in this way, उदाहर अपि cry also.

#### Explanation

Divert Rama to a faroff place and cry, 'Alas, Sita, Alas, Lakshmana',  imitating the voice of Rama.

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

तच्छ्रुत्वा रामपदवीं सीतया च प्रचोदितः। अनुगच्छति सम्भ्रान्तः सौमित्रिरपि सौहृदात्।।3.40.21।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सौमित्रिरपि Lakshmana too, तत् that, श्रुत्वा on hearing, सीतया च by Sita, प्रचोदितः urged, सम्भ्रान्तः bewildered, सौहृदात् out of love , रामपदवीम् following the track of Rama, अनुगच्छति will go.

#### Explanation

On hearing you, Lakshmana will be bewildered. Urged by Sita and out of love for Rama, Lakshmana will follow the same track as Rama.

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

अपक्रान्ते च काकुत्स्थे लक्ष्मणे च यथासुखम्। आनयिष्यामि वैदेहीं सहस्राक्षश्शचीमिव।।3.40.22।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

काकुत्स्थे when Rama, लक्ष्मणे च and Lakshmana, अपक्रान्ते are away, वैदेहीम् Vaidehi, सहस्राक्षः thousandeyed Indra, शचीमिव like Sachi, यथासुखम् comfortably, आनयिष्यामि will get Sita.

#### Explanation

When Rama and Lakshmana are away I will comfortably abduct Vaidehi like the thousandeyed Indra brought Sachi.

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

एवं कृत्वा त्विदं कार्यं यथेष्टं गच्छ राक्षस। राज्यस्यार्धं प्रयच्छामि मारीच तव सुव्रत।।3.40.23।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षस O demon, इदम् this, कार्यम् work, एवम् in that way, कृत्वा having done, यथेष्टम् wherever you please, गच्छ go, सुव्रत a person of good determination, मारीच Maricha, तव to you, राज्यस्य of my kingdom, अर्धम् half, प्रयच्छामि I will offer.

#### Explanation

You may go wherever you want after accomplishing this task. I will give you, O demon of determination half my kingdom.

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

गच्छ सौम्य शिवं मार्गं कार्यस्यास्य विवृद्धये। अहं त्वानुगमिष्यामि सरथो दण्डकावनम्।।3.40.24।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सौम्य O handsome, अस्य of this, कार्यस्य of this task, विवृद्धये for fulfilment, शिवम् auspicious, मार्गम् path, गच्छ go, अहम् I, सरथः riding on the chariot, दण्डकावनम् to Dandaka forest, अनुगमिष्यामि I will follow.

#### Explanation

Go and accomplish this task. Let your path be auspicious. I will follow you on the chariot into the Dandaka forest.

---

### Shloka 25

#### Shloka Text

प्राप्य सीतामयुद्धेन वञ्चयित्वा तु राघवम्। लङ्कां प्रतिगमिष्यामि कृतकार्यस्सह त्वया।।3.40.25।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राघवम् Rama, वञ्चयित्वा  deceiving, सीताम् Sita, अयुद्धेन without a war, प्राप्य  obtain, कृतकार्यः having accomplished the task, त्वया सह along with you, लङ्कां प्रति towards Lanka, गमिष्यामि will go.

#### Explanation

I will deceive Rama and obtain Sita, without waging a war. With the work done, I will return to Lanka along with you.

---

### Shloka 26

#### Shloka Text

न चेत्करोषि मारीच हन्मि त्वामहमद्य वै। एतत्कार्यमवश्यं मे बलादपि करिष्यसि। राज्ञो हि प्रतिकूलस्थो न जातु सुखमेधते।।3.40.26।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मारीच Maricha, न करोषि चेत् if you do not do this, अहम् I, अद्य वै now itself, हन्मि I will kill, मे myself, एतत् this, कार्यम् task, बलादपि forcibly also , अवश्यम् certainly, करिष्यसि will make you do, राज्ञः to the king, प्रतिकूलस्थः against, जातु generally, सुखम् in comfort, न एधते हि will not live.

#### Explanation

O Maricha if you do not do this, I will kill you now. Or, by force make you do this work. No one who goes against the king lives in comfort.

---

### Shloka 27

#### Shloka Text

आसाद्य तं जीवितसंशयस्ते मृत्युर्ध्रुवो ह्यद्य मया विरुध्य। एतद्यथावत्प्रतिगृह्य बुद्ध्या यदत्र पथ्यं कुरु तत्तथा त्वम्।।3.40.27।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तम् to him, आसाद्य  facing in a combat, ते to you, जीवितसंशयः doubtful to be alive, मया with me, विरुध्य  picking up, अद्य now, मृत्युः death, ध्रुवः sure, एतत् as such, बुद्ध्या think, यथावत् do as you like, प्रतिगृह्य accept, अत्र here, यत् whatever, पथ्यम्  desirable, तत् that, त्वम् you, तथा like that, कुरु do.

#### Explanation

Your survival is doubtful after facing Rama in combat. But your death is certain if you oppose me now. Think carefuly and do whatever is desirable for you in the present circumstances. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये अरण्यकाण्डे चत्वारिंशस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the fortieth sarga of Aranyakanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 41

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

आज्ञप्तो राजवद्वाक्यं प्रतिकूलं निशाचरः। अब्रवीत्परुषं वाक्यं मारीचो राक्षसाधिपम्।।3.41.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

निशाचरः demon, प्रतिकूलम् adversely, राजवत् royal, आज्ञप्तः commanded, मारीचः Maricha, राक्षसाधिपम् to the king of the demons, परुषम् harsh, वाक्यम् words, अब्रवीत् said.

#### Explanation

At this royal command which was against his interest Maracha used harsh words to the demonking:

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

केनायमुपदिष्टस्ते विनाशः पापकर्मणा। सपुत्रस्य सराष्ट्रस्य सामात्यस्य निशाचर।।3.41.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

निशाचर Ravana, सपुत्रस्य with your sons, सराष्ट्रस्य with the kingdom, सामात्यस्य with the ministers, ते to you, अयम् this, विनाशः destruction, पापकर्मणा sinful action, केन by whom, उपदिष्टः advised.

#### Explanation

Who is that sinner who has given you this advice which will spell destruction on you, your sons, your kingdom and your ministers ?

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

कस्त्वया सुखिना राजन्नाभिनन्दति पापकृत्। केनेदमुपदिष्टं ते मृत्युद्वारमुपायतः।।3.41.3।।

#### Transliteration

None
