#### Translation

राजन् king, कः who, पापकृत् sinner, सुखिना happy one, त्वया by you, नाभिनन्दति not tolerate, मृत्युद्वारम् death gate, इदम् this, केन by whom, ते you, उपायतः cunningly, उपदिष्टम्  advised.

#### Explanation

Which sinner is not able to see you happy? O king by whom is this gate of death shown to you cunningly?

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

शत्रवस्तव सुव्यक्तं हीनवीर्या निशाचराः। इच्छन्ति त्वां विनश्यन्तमुपरुद्धं बलीयसा।।3.41.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तव your, शत्रवः enemies, हीनवीर्याः deprived of valour, निशाचराः demons, त्वाम् you, बलीयसा by a strong person, उपरुद्धम् obstructed, विनश्यन्तम् being destroyed, इच्छन्ति they like, सुव्यक्तम् it is very clear,

#### Explanation

This is clear that your enemy demons are now powerless. Hence they want you to be engaged with a stronger power and be destroyed.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

केनेदमुपदिष्टं ते क्षुद्रेणाहितवादिना। यस्त्वामिच्छति नश्यन्तं स्वकृतेन निशाचर।।3.41.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

निशाचर O nightwalker (Ravana), यः he who, त्वाम् to you, स्वकृतेन by your own action, नश्यन्तम् being destroyed, इच्छति wishes, क्षुद्रेण by a mean fellow, अहितवादिना one who gives wrong advice, केन by whom, इदम् this, ते to you, उपदिष्टम् advised.

#### Explanation

O Ravana by which mean fellow is this advice given against your welfare? Who wants you to be destroyed by your own action.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

वध्याः खलु न हन्यन्ते सचिवास्तव रावण। ये त्वामुत्पथमारूढं न निगृह्णन्ति सर्वशः।।3.41.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रावण Ravana, उत्पथम् wrong path, आरूढम् you ascended, त्वाम् you, ये those, सर्वशः by all means, न निगृह्णन्ति do not prevent, तव सचिवाः your ministers, वध्याः खलु deserve to be slayed, न हन्यन्ते they are not slain?

#### Explanation

O Ravana the ministers should have prevented you by all means from adopting this  wrong path. Since they have not done so, they deserve to be spain.

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

अमात्यैः कामवृत्तो हि राजा कापथमाश्रितः। निग्राह्यस्सर्वथा सदभिर्न निग्राह्यो निगृह्यसे।।3.41.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कामवृत्तः lustful, कापथम् evil path, आश्रितः resorted to, राजा king, सद्भि: by the good people, अमात्यैः by ministers, सर्वथा always, निग्राह्यः हि should be stopped, निग्राह्यः prevent, न निगृह्यसे not prevented.

#### Explanation

Good ministrers should always prevent a lustful king from taking resourse to an evil path. Why were you not stopped, even though you deserve to be checked.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

धर्ममर्थं च कामं च यशश्च जयतां वर। स्वामिप्रसादात्सचिवाः प्राप्नुवन्ति निशाचर।।3.41.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

जयताम् among the successful, वर best, निशाचर demons, Ravana, सचिवाः ministers, धर्मम् righteousness, अर्थं च prosperity, कामं च and pleasures, यशश्च and fame, स्वामिप्रसादात् by the grace of the king, प्राप्नुवन्ति attain.

#### Explanation

O Ravana  the best among the successful ministers attain piety, prosperity, pleasures and fame by the grace of the king.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

विपर्यये तु तत्सर्वं व्यर्थं भवति रावण। व्यसनं स्वामिवैगुण्यात्प्राप्नुवन्तीतरे जनाः।।3.41.9।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रावण Ravana, विपर्यये if it be otherwise, तत् then, सर्वम् everything (the four attainments), व्यर्थं भवति will become useless, स्वामि वैगुण्यात् by the vices of the king, इतरे जनाः others, व्यसनम् calamity, प्राप्नुवन्ति suffer.

#### Explanation

Otherwise evrything will become useless, O Ravana  Others will suffer calamities on account of their master's vices.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

राजमूलो हि धर्मश्च जयश्च जयतां वर। तस्मात्सर्वास्ववस्थासु रक्षितव्या नराधिपाः।।3.41.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

जयताम् among the victorious, वर best one, धर्मश्च righteousness, जयश्च victory, राजमूल: the king is the root, हि indeed, तस्मात् therefore, सर्वासु in all, अवस्थासु stages, नराधिपाः kings, रक्षितव्याः deserve to be protected.

#### Explanation

O Ravana, the best among the victorious indeed the king is at the root of righteousness and victory.Therefore, the king should be protected by all at every stage.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

राज्यं पालयितुं शक्यं न तीक्ष्णेन निशाचर। न चापि प्रतिकूलेन नाविनीतेन राक्षस।।3.41.11।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षस demons, निशाचर nightwalker, तीक्ष्णेन by a rude one, राज्यम् kingdom, पालयितुम् to rule, न शक्यम् is not possible, प्रतिकूलेन by a hostile one, न not, अविनीतेन by an impolite one, न not.

#### Explanation

O nightwalking demon  a kingdom cannnot be governed by a king who is rude or hostile or impolite.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

ये तीक्ष्णमन्त्रास्सचिवा भज्यन्ते सह तेन वै। विषमे तुरगा श्शीघ्रा मन्दसारथयो यथा।।3.41.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ये whoever, सचिवाः ministers, तीक्ष्णमन्त्राः who adopt improper strategies, मन्दसारथयः when the charioteer is driving slow, शीघ्राः swiftly, तुरगाः horses, विषमे यथा on a rugged surface, likewise, तेन by that, सह along with, भज्यन्ते वै are overthrown.

#### Explanation

Ministers who adopt improper strategies go down along with the king just like swift horses driven by a slow charioteer on a rugged terrain.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

बहवस्साधवो लोके युक्ता धर्ममनुष्ठिताः। परेषामपराधेन विनष्टास्सपरिच्छदाः।।3.41.13।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लोके in this world, युक्ताः engaged, धर्मम् rightful duty, अनुष्ठिताः following, बहवः many, साधवः pious, परेषाम् of others, अपराधेन by their mistakes, सपरिच्छदाः along with their kith and kin, विनष्टाः ruined.

#### Explanation

Persons who walk the righteous path go down in this world along with their kith and kin due to the mistakes committed by others .

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

स्वामिना प्रतिकूलेन प्रजास्तीक्ष्णेन रावण। रक्ष्यमाणा न वर्धन्ते मेषा गोमायुना यथा।।3.41.14।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रावण Ravana, प्रतिकूलेन by an unfavourable, तीक्ष्णेन by a rude one, स्वामिना by the  master, रक्ष्यमाणाः protected, प्रजाः people, गोमायुना by a jackal, मेषाः यथा like sheep, न वर्धन्ते do not grow.

#### Explanation

O Ravana, people who are ruled by a cruel and hostile king will not grow like the sheep protected by a jackal.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

अवश्यं विनशिष्यन्ति सर्वे रावण राक्षसाः। येषां त्वं कर्कशो राजा दुर्बुद्धिरजितेन्द्रियः।।3.41.15।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रावण O Ravana, येषाम् of whom, कर्कशः  rude , दुर्बुद्धि: evilminded, अजितेन्द्रियः having no control over the senses, त्वम् you, राजा king, सर्वे all, राक्षसाः demons, अवश्यम् surely, विनशिष्यन्ति perish.

#### Explanation

Under a rude, crooked king whose senses are not under his control the demons will surely perish.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

तदिदं काकतालीयं घोरमासादितं मया। अत्र किं शोचनीयस्त्वं ससैन्यो विनशिष्यसि।।3.41.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मया I, काकतालीयम् accidental, घोरम् dreadful, तत् that, इदम् this, आसादितम् reached, अत्र here, किम् what, त्वम् you, शोचनीयः deplorable, ससैन्यः along with your army, विनशिष्यसि   will perish.

#### Explanation

I am faced with  this dreadful disaster accidentally. You will not only be in a miserable state because of your misdeeds, you will perish along with your army.

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

मां निहत्य तु रामश्च न चिरात्त्वां वधिष्यति। अनेन कृतकृत्योऽस्मि म्रियेयमरिणा हतः।।3.41.17।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रामः Rama, माम् me, निहत्य after killing, न चिरात् soon, त्वाम् you, वधिष्यति will kill, अरिणा by the enemy, हतः slayed, म्रियेयम् I will die, अनेन by this, कृतकृत्यः accomplished, अस्मि I will be.

#### Explanation

Rama will kill you not long after killing me.Hence even if I die in the hands of the enemy, my desire will be fulfilled.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

दर्शनादेव रामस्य हतं मामवधारय। आत्मानं च हतं विद्धि हृत्वा सीतां सबान्धवम्।।3.41.18।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रामस्य Rama's, दर्शनादेव by mere sight, माम् me, हतम् a dead one, अवधारय presume, सीताम् Sita, हृत्वा abducting, सबान्धवम् along with your kith and kin, आत्मानम् yourself, हतम् killed, विद्धि  understand.

#### Explanation

Yu most understand that if I die at the very sight of Rama, you along with your relatives will perish with the abduction of Sita.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

आनयिष्यसि चेत्सीतामाश्रमात्सहितो मया। नैवत्वमसि नाहं च नैव लङ्का न राक्षसाः।।3.41.19।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मया सहितः along with me, आश्रमात् from the hermitage, सीताम् Sita, आनयिष्यसि चेत् if you bring, त्वम् नैव असि you will not survive, अहं च I also, न लङ्का nor Lanka, नैव not, राक्षसाः demons, न not.

#### Explanation

If you bring Sita from the hermitage with my assistance, know that none of us will surviveyou nor I, nor Lanka , nor the demons.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

निवार्यमाणस्तु मया हितैषिणा न मृष्यसे वाक्यमिदं निशाचर। परेतकल्पा हि गतायुषो नरा हितं न गृह्णन्ति सुहृद्भिरीरितम्।।3.41.20।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

निशाचर Ravana, हितैषिणा by your wellwisher, मया by me, निवार्यमाणः  warned, इदम् this, वाक्यम् words, न मृष्यसे do not comprehend, गतायुषः men of reduced life span, परेतकल्पाः almost dead, नराः people, सुहृद्भि: by friends, ईरितम् told, हितम्  good advice, न गृह्णन्ति हि not accept.

#### Explanation

O Ravana, you do not understand this word of warning coming from your wellwisher. Men who are going to die do not heed the advice given by their friends. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये अरण्यकाण्डे एकचत्वारिंशस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the fortyfirst sarga of Aranyakanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 42

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

एवमुक्त्वा तु वचनं मारीचो रावणं ततः। गच्छावेत्यब्रवीद्दीनो भयाद्रात्रिंचरप्रभोः।।3.42.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, मारीचः Maricha, एवम् in that way, वचनम् words, उक्त्वा spoken, रात्रिंचरप्रभोः of the king of the nightrangers, भयात् out of fear, दीनः distressed , गच्छाव let us both go, इति thus, रावणम् to Ravana, अब्रवीत् said.

#### Explanation

Having thus spoken to the demonking, the distressed Maricha said out of fear, Let both of us go.

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

दृष्टश्चाहं पुनस्तेन शरचापासिधारिणा। मद्वधोद्यतशस्त्रेऽण विनष्टं जीवितं च मे।।3.42.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अहम् I, शरचापासिधारिणा holding bow, arrows and sword, मद्वधोद्यतशस्त्रेऽण who raises weapon to slay me, तेन by him, पुनः again, दृष्टः seen, मे my, जीवितं च विनष्टम्  life will cease.

#### Explanation

If that warrior Rama carrying bow, arrows and sword sees me once again he will raise his weapon to kill me, and I will cease to be.

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

न हि रामं पराक्रम्य जीवन्प्रतिनिवर्तते। वर्तते प्रतिरूपोऽसौ यमदण्डहतस्य ते।।3.42.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रामम् Rama, पराक्रम्य after exhibiting bravery, जीवन् with life, न प्रतिनिवर्तते हि will not return, यमदण्डहतस्य hit by Yama's staff, असौ he, प्रतिरूपः another form, ते to you, वर्तते is being.

#### Explanation

No one who exhibits his heroism before Rama will come back alive. To you, already hit by Yama's staff, he is another form of the same Yama.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

किं नु शक्यं मया कर्तुमेवं त्वयि दुरात्मनि। एष गच्छाम्यहं तात स्वस्ति तेऽस्तु निशाचर।।3.42.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

त्वयि when you, एवम् in that way, दुरात्मनि  evilminded, मया I, कि नु what, कर्तुम् to do, शक्यम् is possible, तात dear one, निशाचर O demons, एष here, अहम् I am, गच्छामि  am going, ते to you, स्वस्ति be auspicious, अस्तु let it be.

#### Explanation

What can I do if you remain evilminded? O dear demon, I am going. May your path be auspicious

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

प्रहृष्टस्त्वभवत्तेन वचनेन स रावणः। परिष्वज्य सुसंश्लिष्टमिदं वचनमब्रवीत्।।3.42.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः रावणः that, Ravana, तेन वचनेन with those words, प्रहृष्टः felt happy, तु अभवत् he was, सुसंश्लिष्टम् tightly, परिष्वज्य  embracing, इदम् these, वचनम् words , अब्रवीत् said.

#### Explanation

Ravana was so overwhelmed with joy to hear those words that he embraced him tightly and said:.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

एतच्छौण्डीर्ययुक्तं ते मच्छन्दवशवर्तिनः। इदानीमसि मारीचः पूर्वमन्यो निशाचरः।।3.42.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मच्छन्दवशवर्तिनः act according to my wish, ते to you, एतत् this, शौण्डीर्ययुक्तम् it  befits your valour, इदानीम् now, मारीचः Maricha, असि you are, पूर्वम् earlier, अन्यः other, राक्षसः हि demons.

#### Explanation

Act according to my wish. It befits your valour. You were a different demon earlier. Now you are Maricha.

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

आरुह्यतामयं शीघ्रं रथो रत्नविभूषितः। मया सह तथा युक्तः पिशाचवदनैः खरैः।।3.42.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तथा similarly, पिशाचवदनैः with devilish faces, खरैः with donkeys, युक्तः yoked, रत्नविभूषितः encrusted with gems, रथः chariot, शीघ्रम् quickly, मया सह along with me, आरुह्यताम्  ascend.

#### Explanation

Ascend quickly with me this chariot encrusted with gems and yoked with donkeys with devils' faces৷৷

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

प्रलोभयित्वा वैदेहीं यथेष्टं गन्तुमर्हसि। तां शून्ये प्रसभं सीतामानयिष्यामि मैथिलीम्।।3.42.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वैदेहीम् to Vaidehi, प्रलोभयित्वा after tempting, यथेष्टम् wherever you like, गन्तुम् to go, अर्हसि should, शून्ये when no one is there, मैथिलीम् princess of Mithila, तां सीताम् that Sita, प्रसभम् forcibly, आनयिष्यामि will bring.

#### Explanation

You may go whereever you like after tempting Vaidehi. When the princess of Mithila  is alone, I will bring her by force.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

ततो रावणमारीचौ विमानमिव तं रथम्। आरुह्य ययतुश्शीघ्रं तस्मादाश्रममण्डलात्।।3.42.9।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः thereafter, रावणमारीचौ Ravana and Maricha, तं रथम् that chariot, विमानमिव like an aerial chariot, आरुह्य  ascending, तस्मात् from that, आश्रममण्डलात् cluster of hermitages, शीघ्रम् quickly, ययतुः departed.

#### Explanation

Thereafter Ravana and Maricha ascended the chariot, which was like an aerial car, and quickly departed from that cluster of hermitages.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

तथैव तत्र पश्यन्तौ पत्तनानि वनानि च। गीरींश्च सरितस्सर्वा राष्ट्राणि नगराणि च।।3.42.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तथैव and likewise, तत्र there, पत्तनानि towns, वनानि च and forests, गिरींश्च mountains, सर्वाः all, सरितः streams, राष्ट्राणि states, नगराणि च cities, पश्यन्तौ seeing

#### Explanation

And likewise, they went past towns, forests, mountains, streams, states and cities.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

समेत्य दण्डकारण्यं राघवस्याश्रमं ततः। ददर्श सह मारीचो रावणो राक्षसाधिपः।।3.42.11।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सहमारीचः accompanied by Maricha, राक्षसाधिपः king of demons, रावणः Ravana, दण्डकारण्यम् Dandaka forest, समेत्य reached, ततः then, राघवस्य Rama's, आश्रमम् hermitage, ददर्श noticed.

#### Explanation

Ravana, king of the demons, accompanied by Maricha, then reached Dandaka forest and saw Rama's hermitage.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

अवतीर्य रथात्तस्मात्ततः काञ्चनभूषणात्। हस्ते गृहीत्वा मारीचं रावणो वाक्यमब्रवीत्।।3.42.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रावणः Ravana, काञ्चनभूषणात् decorated with golden ornaments, तस्मात् from that, रथात् chariot, अवतीर्य  getting down, ततः then, मारीचम् Maricha, हस्ते by hand, गृहीत्वा, held, वाक्यम्  words, अब्रवीत् said.

#### Explanation

Then Ravana got off the chariot decorated with golden embellishments and, holding Maricha's hand, said:.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

एतदाश्रमपदं दृश्यते कदलीवृतम्। क्रियतां तत्सखे शीघ्रं यदर्थं वयमागताः।।3.42.13।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सखे O friend, कदलीवृतम् surrounded by banana plants, एतत् this, तत् that, आश्रमपदम् site of hermitage, दृश्यते is seen, वयम् we, यदर्थम् the purpose for which, आगताः we came, तत् that, शीघ्रम् quickly, क्रियताम् should be done.

#### Explanation

O friend here is the site of the hermitage surrounded by banana plants. Quickly get to work on the purpose for which we have come.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

स रावणवचश्श्रुत्वा मारीचो राक्षसस्तदा। मृगो भूत्वाऽश्रमद्वारि रामस्य विचचार ह।।3.42.14।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तदा then, सः that, राक्षसः demon, मारीचः Maricha, रावणवचः Ravana's words, श्रुत्वा on hearing, मृगः deer, भूत्वा transformed, रामस्य Rama's, आश्रमद्वारि at the entrance of the hermitage, विचचार ह started moving.

#### Explanation

Hearing Ravana's words, Maricha transformed himself into a deer and started moving at the entrance of Rama's hermitage.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

स तु तद्रूपमास्थाय महदद्भुतदर्शनम्। मणिप्रवरशृङ्गाग्रस्सितासितमुखाकृतिः।।3.42.15।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः Maricha, अद्भुतदर्शनम् wonderful to look at, महत् magnificent, तत् that, रूपम् form, आस्थाय  assumed, मणिप्रवरशृङ्गाग्रः antlers studded with the best of gems, सितासितमुखाकृतिः face dappled with luminous spots of white and black.

#### Explanation

Maricha assumed the form of a wonderful and magnificent deer with his antlers studded with the most precious gems, his face dappled with luminous spots of white and  black.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

रक्तपद्मोत्पलमुख इन्द्रनीलोत्पलश्रवाः। किंचिदभ्युन्नतग्रीव इंद्रनीलदलाधरः।।3.42.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रक्तपद्मोत्पलमुखः mouth like  pink lotus, इन्द्रनीलोत्पलश्रवाः  ears  like blue lotus, किञ्चित् a bit, अभ्युन्नतग्रीवः  raised neck, इन्द्रनीलदलाधरः lips like petals of blue lotus.

#### Explanation

The deer's face was like a pink lotus, his ears were like blue lotus. His neck slightly raised, his lips were like petals of blue lotus.

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

कुन्देन्दुवज्रसङ्काशमुदरं चास्य भास्वरम्। मधूकनिभपार्श्वश्च पद्मकिञ्जल्कसन्निभः।।3.42.17।। वैडूर्यसङ्काशखुरस्तनुजङ्घस्सुसंहतः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अस्य its, भास्वरम् shining, उदरम् stomach, कुन्देन्दुवज्रसङ्काशम् like white kunda flower, Moon and diamond, मधूकनिभपार्श्वश्च sides like madhuka flowers, पद्मकिञ्जल्कसन्निभः comparable to the filaments of lotus, वैडूर्यसङ्काशखुरः hooves like Vaidurya, तनुजङ्घः slim shank, सुसंहतः willshaped.

#### Explanation

His bright belly was shining like kunda flowers or the Moon or diamond. His flanks were like the golden madhuka flowers. comparable to the filaments of lotus. His hooves were like vaidurya, and his legs were slim and willshaped.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

इन्द्रायुधसवर्णेन पुच्छेनोर्ध्वं विराजता।।3.42.18।। मनोहरस्स्निग्धवर्णो रत्नैर्नानाविधैर्वृतः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ऊर्ध्वम् upward, विराजता stood, इन्द्रायुध like a rainbow, सवर्णेन colourful, पुच्छेन with the tail, मनोहरः delightful, स्निग्धवर्णः shining, नानाविधैः various, रत्नैः gems, वृतः decked.

#### Explanation

His tail stood up looking magnificent like a colourful rainbow. Bedecked with various gems, he shone beautiful.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

क्षणेन राक्षसो जातो मृगः परमशोभनः।।3.42.19।। वनं प्रज्वलयन्रम्यं रामाश्रमपदं च तत्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षसः the demon, वनम् forest, रम्यम् beautiful, तत् रामाश्रमपदम् that Rama's hermitage, प्रज्वलयन् while brightening, क्षणेन in a moment, परमशोभनः very beautiful, मृगः deer, जातः was transformed.

#### Explanation

Transformed into a most beautiful deer in a moment, Maricha brightened the beautiful forest and Rama's hermitage.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

मनोहरं दर्शनीयं रूपं कृत्वा स राक्षसः।।3.42.20।। प्रलोभनार्थं वैदेह्या नानाधातुविचित्रितम्। विचरन्गच्छते तस्माच्छाद्वलानि समन्ततः।।3.42.21।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः that, राक्षसः demon, नानाधातुविचित्रितम् painted with colours of different minerals, मनोहरम् delightful, दर्शनीयम् beautiful, रूपम् form, कृत्वा after transforming, वैदेह्याः to Sita, प्रलोभनार्थम् to tempt, विचरन् while grazing, तस्मात् from that place, समन्ततः all around, शाद्वलानि grassland, गच्छते moved about.

#### Explanation

Transfigured into a beautiful, magnificent form painted with the colours of different minerals in order to tempt Sita, Maricha roamed all around the grassland.

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

मनोहरं दर्शनीयं रूपं कृत्वा स राक्षसः।।3.42.20।। प्रलोभनार्थं वैदेह्या नानाधातुविचित्रितम्। विचरन्गच्छते तस्माच्छाद्वलानि समन्ततः।।3.42.21।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः that, राक्षसः demon, नानाधातुविचित्रितम् painted with colours of different minerals, मनोहरम् delightful, दर्शनीयम् beautiful, रूपम् form, कृत्वा after transforming, वैदेह्याः to Sita, प्रलोभनार्थम् to tempt, विचरन् while grazing, तस्मात् from that place, समन्ततः all around, शाद्वलानि grassland, गच्छते moved about.

#### Explanation

Transfigured into a beautiful, magnificent form painted with the colours of different minerals in order to tempt Sita, Maricha roamed all around the grassland.

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

रूप्यैर्बिन्दुशतैश्चित्रो भूत्वा स प्रियदर्शनः। विटपीनां किसलयान्भङ्त्क्वाऽदन्विचचार ह।।3.42.22।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः that, रूप्यैः by silver, बिन्दुशतैः with hudreds of spots, चित्रः was wonderful, भूत्वा on transforming, प्रियदर्शनः pleasing to the eyes, विटपीनाम् trees, किसलयान् tender leaves, भङ्त्क्वा nibbling, अदन् eating, विचचार ह wandered about.

#### Explanation

That deer with hundreds of silver spots on the body appeared enchanting and pleasing to the eyes while he wandered about nibbling the tender leaves of trees.

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

कदलीगृहकं गत्वा कर्णिकारानितस्ततः। समाश्रयन्मन्दगतिस्सीतासन्दर्शनं तथा।।3.42.23।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कदलीगृहकम् groves of banana, गत्वा on going, कर्णिकारान् the karnikara flowers, इतस्ततः hither and thither, मन्दगतिः at a slow pace, तथा likewise, सीतासन्दर्शनम् the sight of Sita, समाश्रयत् resorted.

#### Explanation

The deer went wandering about the banana groves surrounding Rama's hermitage and moved slowly towards the karnikara trees in order to catch the attention of Sita.

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

राजीवचित्रपृष्ठस्स विरराज महामृगः। रामाश्रमपदाभ्याशे विचचार यथासुखम्।।3.42.24।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः that, महामृगः magnificent deer, राजीवचित्रपृष्ठः his back with the colour of blue lotus, विरराज glittered, रामाश्रमपदाभ्याशे in the vicinity of Rama's hermitage, यथासुखम् comfortably, विचचार wandered.

#### Explanation

That magnificent deer glittered with the colour of blue lotus on its back, wandered merrily in the vicinity of Rama's hermitage.

---

### Shloka 25

#### Shloka Text

पुनर्गत्वा निवृत्तश्च विचचार मृगोत्तमः। गत्वा मुहूर्तं त्वरया पुनः प्रतिनिवर्तते।।3.42.25।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मृगोत्तमः best of deer, गत्वा on going, पुनः again, निवृत्तश्च came back, विचचार wandered, मुहूर्तम् for a moment, गत्वा after going, पुनः again, त्वरया swiftly, प्रतिनिवर्तते returned.

#### Explanation

The wonderful deer moved to and fro, now disappearing and now swiftly returning.

---

### Shloka 26

#### Shloka Text

विक्रीडंश्च क्वचिद्भूमौ पुनरेव निषीदति। आश्रमद्वारमागम्य मृगयूथानि गच्छति।।3.42.26।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

विक्रीडंश्च while sporting so, पुनरेव again, क्वचित् sometimes, भूमौ on the ground, पुनरेव again, निषीदति squat, आश्रमद्वारम् at the entrance of the hermitage, आगम्य after reaching, मृगयूथानि flock of deer, गच्छति goes.

#### Explanation

The deer is now sporting, now sitting on the ground, now standing at the entrance of the hermitage and now running with the herds of deer.

---

### Shloka 27

#### Shloka Text

मृगयूथैरनुगतः पुनरेव निवर्तते। सीतादर्शनमाकांक्षन्राक्षसो मृगतां गतः।।3.42.27।। परिभ्रमति चित्राणि मण्डलानि विनिष्पतन्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मृगताम् form of a deer, गतः transformed, राक्षसः demon, सीतादर्शनम् seeing Sita, आकांक्षन् intending, मृगयूथैः herds of deer, अनुगतः been followed, पुनरेव again, निवर्तते he retuned, विनिष्पतन्  cavorted and pranced, चित्राणि wonderful, मण्डलानि circles, परिभ्रमति turned round.

#### Explanation

Followed by herds of deer and coming back to see Sita again and again, the deer cavorted and pranced and frisked in circles in a wonderful way.

---

### Shloka 28

#### Shloka Text

समुद्वीक्ष्य च ते सर्वे मृगा ह्यन्ये वनेचराः।।3.42.28।। उपागम्य समाघ्राय विद्रवन्ति दिशो दश।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वनेचराः wandering in the forest, अन्ये other, ते those, सर्वे all, मृगाः deer, समुद्विक्ष्य seeing, उपागम्य on coming near, समाघ्राय sniffing, दश दिशः in all ten directions, विद्रवन्ति run away,

#### Explanation

Seeing the deer, other animals wandering in the forest come near and sniffing him run away in all ten directions.

---

### Shloka 29

#### Shloka Text

राक्षसस्सोऽपि तान्वन्यान्मृगान्मृगवधे रतः।।3.42.29।। प्रच्छादनार्थं भावस्य न भक्षयति संस्पृशन्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मृगवधे in killing deer, रतः interested, सः he, राक्षसोऽपि demon also, वन्यान् those wild, तान् those, मृगान् deer, संस्पृशन् touching and feeling, भावस्य his intention, प्रच्छादनार्थम् to hide, न भक्षयति does not feed on.

#### Explanation

The demon who was interested in killing wild deer touched and felt them but did not kill or feed on them in order to hide his intention.

---

### Shloka 30

#### Shloka Text

तस्मिन्नेव ततः काले वैदेही शुभलोचना।।3.42.30।। कुसुमावचयव्यग्रा पादपानभ्यवर्तत।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, तस्मिन्नेव काले at that time, शुभलोचना lady of auspicious looks, वैदेही Vaidehi, कुसुमावचयव्यग्रा busy plucking flowers, पादपान् the trees, अभ्यवर्तत came near,

#### Explanation

In the mean time Sita who, with her auspicious looks, was busy plucking flowers drew near the trees.

---

### Shloka 31

#### Shloka Text

कर्णिकारानशोकांश्च चूतांश्च मदिरेक्षणा।।3.42.31।। कुसुमान्यवचिन्वन्ती चचार रुचिरानना।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मदिरेक्षणा woman with bewitching eyes, रुचिरानना of dazzling face, कर्णिकारान् karnikara trees, अशोकांश्छ ashoka, चूतांश्च mango, कुसुमानि flowers, अवचिन्वन्ती while plucking, चचार went about.

#### Explanation

With her bewitching eyes and beautiful face, Sita, went about plucking flowers from karnikara, ashoka and mango trees.

---

### Shloka 32

#### Shloka Text

अनर्हाऽरण्यवासस्य सा तं रत्नमयं मृगम्।।3.42.32।। मुक्तामणि विचित्राङ्गं ददर्श परमाङ्गना।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अरण्यवासस्य of living in the forest, अनर्हा unworthy, सा that, परमाङ्गना great woman, रत्नमयम् encrusted with gold, मुक्तामणिविचित्राङ्गम् with a wonderful body dotted with pearls and gems, तं मृगम् that deer, ददर्श saw.

#### Explanation

The great lady Sita, unworthy of dwelling in the forest, saw the wonderful deer decked with gems, with  spots of pearls all over the body.

---

### Shloka 33

#### Shloka Text

सा तं रुचिरदन्तोष्ठी रूप्यधातुतनूरुहम्।।3.42.33।। विस्मयोत्फुल्लनयना सस्नेहं समुदैक्षत।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रुचिरदन्तोष्ठी a lady of beautiful teeth and lips, सा that Sita, रूप्यधातुतनूरुहम् animal with silver and mineral coloured hair on the body, तम् deer, विस्मयोत्फुल्लनयना with eyes of wonder and joy, सस्नेहम् with affection, समुदैक्षत gazed at.

#### Explanation

A lady with beautiful teeth and lips, Sita saw the deer having silver and mineral coloured hair on the body. She gazed at the animal with wonder and joy and love.

---

### Shloka 34

#### Shloka Text

स च तां रामदयितां पश्यन्मायामयो मृगः।।3.42.34।। विचचार पुनश्चित्रं दीपयन्निव तद्वनम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मायामयः illusory one, सः that, मृगश्च the deer, तां her, रामदयिताम् Rama's wife, पश्यन् on seeing, तत् वनम् that forest, दीपयन्निव as if illuminating, पुनः again, चित्रम् wonderfully, विचचार moved around.

#### Explanation

That illusory deer saw Rama's wife and moved around wonderfully as if illuminating the forest with his radiance.

---

### Shloka 35

#### Shloka Text

अदृष्टपूर्वं तं दृष्ट्वा नानारत्नमयं मृगम्।।3.42.35।। विस्मयं परमं सीता जगाम जनकात्मजा।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अदृष्टपूर्वम् never seen before, नानारत्नमयम् decorated with different gems, तं मृगम् that deer, दृष्ट्वा seeing, जनकात्मजा daughter of Janaka, सीता Sita, परमम् very much, विस्मयम् surprised, जगाम experienced.

#### Explanation

Seeing such  an unprecedented deer decorated with different gems, Sita, daughter of Janaka, stood amazed. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये अरण्यकाण्डे द्विचत्वारिंशस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the fortysecond sarga of Aranyakanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 43

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

सा तं सम्प्रेक्ष्य सुश्रोणी कुसुमान्यपचिन्वती। हेमराजतवर्णाभ्यां पार्श्वाभ्यामुपशोभितम्।।3.43.1।। प्रहृष्टा चानवद्याङ्गी मृष्टहाटकवर्णिनी। भर्तारमपि चक्रन्द लक्ष्मणं चापि सायुधम्।।3.43.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सुश्रोणी with fine hips, अनवद्याङ्गी a lady of flawless beautiful limbs, मृष्टहाटकवर्णिनी of pure golden complexion, कुसुमानि flowers, अपचिन्वती while picking, सा she, हेमराजतवर्णाभ्याम् with gold and silver colour, पार्श्वाभ्याम् on both sides of the body, उपशोभितम् looking beautiful, तम् that animal, सम्प्रेक्ष्य after seeing, प्रहृष्टा delighted, भर्तारमपि husband and, सायुधम् with arms, लक्ष्मणं चापि Lakshmana too, चक्रन्द cried out.

#### Explanation

Sita while plucking flowers Sita who had fine hips, flawless beautiful limbs and pure golden complexion felt delighted to see the deer with gold and silver colours on both sides of his body. She called out to Rama and Lakshmana who were equipped with arms.

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

सा तं सम्प्रेक्ष्य सुश्रोणी कुसुमान्यपचिन्वती। हेमराजतवर्णाभ्यां पार्श्वाभ्यामुपशोभितम्।।3.43.1।। प्रहृष्टा चानवद्याङ्गी मृष्टहाटकवर्णिनी। भर्तारमपि चक्रन्द लक्ष्मणं चापि सायुधम्।।3.43.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सुश्रोणी with fine hips, अनवद्याङ्गी a lady of flawless beautiful limbs, मृष्टहाटकवर्णिनी of pure golden complexion, कुसुमानि flowers, अपचिन्वती while picking, सा she, हेमराजतवर्णाभ्याम् with gold and silver colour, पार्श्वाभ्याम् on both sides of the body, उपशोभितम् looking beautiful, तम् that animal, सम्प्रेक्ष्य after seeing, प्रहृष्टा delighted, भर्तारमपि husband and, सायुधम् with arms, लक्ष्मणं चापि Lakshmana too, चक्रन्द cried out.

#### Explanation

Sita while plucking flowers Sita who had fine hips, flawless beautiful limbs and pure golden complexion felt delighted to see the deer with gold and silver colours on both sides of his body. She called out to Rama and Lakshmana who were equipped with arms.

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

तयाऽऽहूतौ नरव्याघ्रौ वैदेह्या रामलक्ष्मणौ। वीक्षमाणौ तु तौ देशं तदा ददृशतुर्मृगम्।।3.43.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तया by her, वैदेह्या by Vaidehi, आहूतौ called, नरव्याघ्रौ tigers among men, तौ both, रामलक्ष्मणौ Rama and Lakshmana, (तं) देशम् that place, वीक्षमाणौ while glancing, तदा then, मृगम् deer, ददृशतुः saw.

#### Explanation

When Sita called Rama and Lakshmana, the two tigers among men, they looked around and saw the deer.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

शङ्कमानस्तु तं दृष्ट्वा लक्ष्मणो राममब्रवीत्। तमेवैनमहं मन्ये मारीचं राक्षसं मृगम्।।3.43.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तम् him, दृष्ट्वा after seeing, शङ्कमानः  suspected, लक्ष्मणः Lakshmana, रामम् to Rama, अब्रवीत् said, अहम् I, एनम् him, मृगम् deer, तम् him,  मारीचमेव Maricha only, मन्ये I think.

#### Explanation

Seeing the deer, Lakshmana suspected him to be Maricha and said this to Rama.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

चरन्तोमृगयां हृष्टाः पापेनोपाधिना वने। अनेन निहता राजन् राजानः कामरूपिणा।।3.43.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राजन् O king, हृष्टाः happy, मृगयां चरन्तः while on hunting expedition, राजानः kings, कामरूपिणा assuming any form at his free will, अनेन by him, पापेन malicious, वने in the forest, उपाधिना deceitfully, निहताः killed.

#### Explanation

Many kings, while on hunting expedition in the forest, have been deceitfully killed by malicious Maricha, who could assume any form at his free will.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

अस्य मायाविदो मायामृगरूपमिदं कृतम्। भानुमत्पुरुषव्याघ्र गन्धर्वपुरसन्निभम्।।3.43.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पुरुषव्याघ्र O tiger among men, मायाविदः deceitful one, अस्य his, भानुमत् radiant, गन्धर्वपुरसन्निभम् like the city of the gandharvas (which does not exist), इदम् this kind, मायामृगरूपम् form of an illusory deer, कृतम् has assumed

#### Explanation

O tiger among men, this deceitful demon (Maricha) has been transformed into the illusory form of a radiant deer which does not exist.

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

मृगो ह्येवंविधो रत्नविचित्रो नास्ति राघव। जगत्यां जगतीनाथ मायैषा हि न संशयः।।3.43.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

जगतीनाथ O lord of the world, राघव Rama, जगत्याम् in the world, रत्नविचित्रः one with  wonderful and sparkling gems, एवंविधः this kind of, मृगः deer, नास्ति हि does not exist, एषा this is, माया trick, संशयः doubt, न not,

#### Explanation

O Rama, lord of the world nowhere on earth does exist this kind of wonderful deer sparkling with gems. That it is a mere trick, an illusion there is no doubt.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

एवं ब्रुवाणं काकुत्स्थं प्रतिवार्य शुचिस्मिता। उवाच सीता संहृष्टा चर्मणा हृतचेतना।।3.43.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

शुचिस्मिता a lady with a pure smile, चर्मणा by the beauty of the skin, हृतचेतना who lost her sense of descrimination, हृष्टा a joyful, सीता Sita, एवम् in that way, ब्रुवाणम् saying, काकुत्स्थम् Rama, प्रतिवार्य  intervened, उवाच said.

#### Explanation

Sita was too enchanted by the skin of the deer to retain her sense of discrimination. Intervening, she said to Rama joyfully with a pure smile on her face:

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

आर्यपुत्राभिरामोऽसौ मृगो हरति मे मनः। आनयैनं महाबाहो क्रीडार्थं नो भविष्यति।।3.43.9।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

आर्यपुत्र O prince, अभिरामः attractive, असौ this, मृगः deer, मे to me, मनः mind, हरति captivating, महाबाहो O longarmed Rama, एनम् this animal, आनय bring, नः our, क्रीडार्थम् playmate, भविष्यति will be.

#### Explanation

O prince O longarmed one, this deer has captivated my mind. Fetch him for  me.This  will be our playmate.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

इहाश्रमपदेऽस्माकं बहवः पुण्यदर्शनाः। मृगाश्चरन्ति सहिताश्चमरास्सृमरास्तथा।।3.43.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अस्माकम् for us, इह here, आश्रमपदे at the hermitage, पुण्यदर्शनाः piouslooking, बहवः many, मृगाः deer, सृमराः antelopes, तथा similarly, चमराः yaks, सहिताः in herds, चरन्ति wandering.

#### Explanation

Here at the hermitage there are many lovely deer, yaks and antelopes wandering in herds.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

ऋक्षाः पृषतसङ्घाश्च वानराः किन्नरास्तथा। विचरन्ति महाबाहो रुपश्रेष्ठा मनोहराः।।3.43.11।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महाबाहो O longarmed one, रूपश्रेष्ठाः best of forms, मनोहराः enchanting, ऋक्षाः bear, पृषतसङ्घाश्च herds of spotted antelopes, वानराः monkeys, तथा similarly, किन्नराः kinneras, विचरन्ति wander about.

#### Explanation

O longarmed one, most beautiful animals like bears, antelopes and groups of monkeys and kinneras are wandering here.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

न चास्य सदृशो राजन्दृष्टपूर्वो मृगो मया। तेजसा क्षमया दीप्त्या यथाऽयं मृगसत्तमः।।3.43.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राजन् O Prince, अयम् this, मृगसत्तमः great deer, यथा as, तेजसा by lustre, क्षमया by forbearance, दीप्त्या by glitter, अस्य his, सदृशः equal, मृगः animal, मया by me, न दृष्टपूर्वः never seen before.

#### Explanation

O prince  this kind of beautiful, tame and glittering deer I have never seen before.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

नानावर्णविचित्राङ्गो रत्नबिन्दुसमाचितः। द्योतयन्वनमव्यग्रं शोभते शशिसन्निभः।।3.43.13।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

नानावर्णविचित्राङ्गः one whose body is glittering with different colours, रत्नबिन्दुसमाचितः speckled with gems of different kinds, शशिसन्निभः like the Moon, अव्यग्रम् cool, वनम् forest, द्योतयन् while illuminating, शोभते shining.

#### Explanation

Its body glittering with different colours, speckled with gems of different kinds, is like the Moon shining and illuminating the forest.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

अहो रूपमहो लक्ष्मीस्स्वरसम्पच्च शोभना। मृगोऽद्भुतो विचित्राङ्गो हृदयं हरतीव मे।।3.43.14।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रूपम् form, अहो Oh, लक्ष्मीः beauty, अहो Oh, स्वरसम्पच्च rich call, शोभना is  delightful, अद्भुतः wonderful, विचित्राङ्गः graceful limbs, मृगः deer, मे to me, हृदयम् heart, हरतीव as if captivating.

#### Explanation

Oh what a beauty, Oh what rich call  what delightful, wonderful, graceful limbs  This deer captivates my heart.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

यदि ग्रहणमभ्येति जीवन्नेव मृगस्तव। आश्चर्यभूतं भवति विस्मयं जनयिष्यति।।3.43.15।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मृगः deer, जीवन्नेव alive, तव your, ग्रहणम् caught, अभ्येति यदि if it can be obtained, आश्चर्यभूतम् thrilling, भवति will be, विस्मयम् wonder, जनयिष्यति will create.

#### Explanation

If this deer can be caught  alive, it will be thrilling, it will work wonders.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

समाप्तवनवासानां राज्यस्थानां च नः पुनः। अन्तःपुरविभूषार्थो मृग एष भविष्यति।।3.43.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

एषः this, मृगः deer, समाप्तवनवासानाम् after the completion of exile in the forest, पुनः again, राज्यस्थानाम् and established in the kingdom, नः for us, अन्तःपुरविभूषार्थः add beauty to the  harem, भविष्यति will be.

#### Explanation

After the completion  of exile in the forest, when we are back in the kingdom, this deer will add beauty to the harem.

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

भरतस्यार्यपुत्रस्य श्वश्रूणां मम च प्रभो। मृगरूपमिदं व्यक्तं विस्मयं जनयिष्यति।।3.43.17।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

प्रभो O king, इदम् this, मृगरूपम् this form of a deer, भरतस्य to Bharata, आर्यपुत्रस्य for you, श्वश्रूणाम् for mothersinlaw, मम च to me, व्यक्तम् certainly, विस्मयम् wonder, जनयिष्यति will generate.

#### Explanation

O king this deer will create amazement in Bharata, in mothersinlaw, in you and in me as well.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

जीवन्न यदि तेऽभ्येति ग्रहणं मृगसत्तमः। अजिनं नरशार्दूल रुचिरं मे भविष्यति।।3.43.18।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

नरशार्दूल O tiger among men, मृगसत्तमः great deer, जीवन् with life, ते to you, ग्रहणम् catch, न not, अभ्येति यदि can be caught, मे for me, रुचिरम् this beautiful, अजिनम् skin, भविष्यति will be.

#### Explanation

O best among men, if this great deer cannot be captured alive, I will wear its beautiful skin.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

निहतस्यास्य सत्त्वस्य जाम्बूनदमयत्वचि। शष्पबृस्यां विनीतायामिच्छाम्यहमुपासितुम्।।3.43.19।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

शष्पबृस्यां on a cushion of tender grass, विनीतायाम् it spread, निहतस्य if killed, अस्य सत्त्वस्य this animal's, जाम्बूनदमयत्वचि its golden skin, अहम् I, उपासितुम् to sit, इच्छामि desire.

#### Explanation

If this animal is killed and its golden skin is spread on a tender grass cushion, there will I like to sit.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

कामवृत्तमिदं रौद्रं स्त्रीणामसदृशं मतम्। वपुषा त्वस्य सत्त्वस्य विस्मयो जनितो मम।।3.43.20।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रौद्रम् terrible, इदम् this, कामवृत्तम्  arising out of desire, स्त्रीणाम् for a woman, असदृशम् not desirable, मतम्  opinion, तु you, अस्य even so, सत्त्वस्य  this animal's, वपुषा beauty of skin, मम me, विस्मयः amazing, जनितः creating.

#### Explanation

This kind of request arising out of a woman's desire may sound terrible, still then the beauty of this animal's skin has produced in me a wonder.

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

तेन काञ्चनवर्णेन मणिप्रवरशृङ्गिणा। तरुणादित्यवर्णेन नक्षत्रपथवर्चसा।।3.43.21।। बभूव राघवस्यापि मनो विस्मयमागतम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

काञ्चनवर्णेन by its golden colour, मणिप्रवरशृङ्गिणा horned with excellent gems, तरुणादित्यवर्णेन by its colour resembling the rising Sun, नक्षत्रपथवर्चसा by shining like the milkyway, तेन by, राघवस्य of Rama, मनः अपि mind also, विस्मयम् amazement, आगतम् arose.

#### Explanation

By his golden colour resembling the rising Sun, by  his horns with excellent gems shining like the milkyway, even Rama's mind was wonderstruck.

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

एवं सीतावचः श्रुत्वा तं दृष्ट्वा मृगमद्भुतम्।।3.43.22।। लोभितस्तेन रूपेण सीतया च प्रचोदितः। उवाच राघवो हृष्टो भ्रातरं लक्ष्मणं वचः।।3.43.23।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राघवः Rama, एवम् in that manner, सीतावचः Sita's words, श्रुत्वा listening, अद्भुतम् wonderful, तं मृगम् that deer, दृष्ट्वा after seeing, तेन रूपेण by its appearance, लोभितः tempted, सीतया च by Sita, प्रचोदितः prompted, हृष्टः gladly, भ्रातरम् brother, लक्ष्मणम् Lakshmana, वचः words, उवाच said.

#### Explanation

On hearing Sita's words and seeing the wonderful deer, Rama was tempted by its appearance.Prompted by Sita,he joyfully said this to Lakshmana:

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

एवं सीतावचः श्रुत्वा तं दृष्ट्वा मृगमद्भुतम्।।3.43.22।। लोभितस्तेन रूपेण सीतया च प्रचोदितः। उवाच राघवो हृष्टो भ्रातरं लक्ष्मणं वचः।।3.43.23।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राघवः Rama, एवम् in that manner, सीतावचः Sita's words, श्रुत्वा listening, अद्भुतम् wonderful, तं मृगम् that deer, दृष्ट्वा after seeing, तेन रूपेण by its appearance, लोभितः tempted, सीतया च by Sita, प्रचोदितः prompted, हृष्टः gladly, भ्रातरम् brother, लक्ष्मणम् Lakshmana, वचः words, उवाच said.

#### Explanation

On hearing Sita's words and seeing the wonderful deer, Rama was tempted by its appearance.Prompted by Sita,he joyfully said this to Lakshmana:

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

पश्य लक्ष्मण वैदेह्याः स्पृहां मृगगतामिमाम्। रूपश्रेष्ठतया ह्येष मृगोऽद्य न भविष्यति।।3.43.24।। न वने नन्दनोद्धेशे न चैत्ररथसंश्रये। कुतःपृथिव्यां सौमित्रे योऽस्य कश्चित्समो मृगः।।3.43.25।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लक्ष्मण O Lakshmana, वैदेह्याः Sita's, मृगगताम् pertaining to this deer, स्पृहाम् desire, पश्य  you may see, सौमित्रे Lakshmana, अद्य now, रूपश्रेष्ठतया due to its excellent beauty, एषः this, मृगः deer, वने in the forest, न भविष्यति will not be found, नन्दनोद्धेशे in the Nandana garden of Indra, न not, चैत्ररथसंश्रये in Chaitraratha,( garden of Kubera), न not, यः any such, अस्य its, समः equal, पृथिव्याम् on the earth, कुतः where do (we find).

#### Explanation

O Lakshmana, see the eagerness of Sita. Such a deer of exceptional beauty will not be found in this forest. There is no equal to this animal even in the Nandan garden of Indra, or the Chaitraratha, the garden of Kubera. Where can such an animal be found on earth?

---

### Shloka 25

#### Shloka Text

पश्य लक्ष्मण वैदेह्याः स्पृहां मृगगतामिमाम्। रूपश्रेष्ठतया ह्येष मृगोऽद्य न भविष्यति।।3.43.24।। न वने नन्दनोद्धेशे न चैत्ररथसंश्रये। कुतःपृथिव्यां सौमित्रे योऽस्य कश्चित्समो मृगः।।3.43.25।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लक्ष्मण O Lakshmana, वैदेह्याः Sita's, मृगगताम् pertaining to this deer, स्पृहाम् desire, पश्य  you may see, सौमित्रे Lakshmana, अद्य now, रूपश्रेष्ठतया due to its excellent beauty, एषः this, मृगः deer, वने in the forest, न भविष्यति will not be found, नन्दनोद्धेशे in the Nandana garden of Indra, न not, चैत्ररथसंश्रये in Chaitraratha,( garden of Kubera), न not, यः any such, अस्य its, समः equal, पृथिव्याम् on the earth, कुतः where do (we find).

#### Explanation

O Lakshmana, see the eagerness of Sita. Such a deer of exceptional beauty will not be found in this forest. There is no equal to this animal even in the Nandan garden of Indra, or the Chaitraratha, the garden of Kubera. Where can such an animal be found on earth?

---

### Shloka 26

#### Shloka Text

प्रतिलोमानुलोमाश्च रुचिरा रोमराजयः। शोभन्ते मृगमाश्रित्य चित्राः कनकबिन्दवः।।3.43.26।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

प्रतिलोमानुलोमाः च hair bent in the opposite direction and hair bent in the natural order, रुचिराः beautiful, कनकबिन्दवः golden spots, चित्राः beautiful, रोमराजयः the stretches of hair on the body, मृगम् the deer, आश्रित्य resorting to, शोभन्ते are looking delightful.

#### Explanation

The stretches of hair on the deer's body bent both in the natural order and in the opposite direction look beautiful. The spots of gold on the skin are wonderful.

---

### Shloka 27

#### Shloka Text

पश्यास्य जृम्भमाणस्य दीप्तामग्निशिखोपमाम्। जिह्वां मुखान्निस्सरन्तीं मेघादिव शतह्रदाम्।।3.43.27।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

जृम्भमाणस्य while it is yawning, अस्य its, मुखात् from the mouth, निस्सरन्तीम् stretching, दीप्ताम् glowing, अग्निशिखोपमाम् like flaming fires, मेघात् from the cloud, शतह्रदाम् इव like the lightning, जिह्वाम् tongue, पश्य see.

#### Explanation

Look at his tongue stretched out of the mouth while yawning. It is glowing like flaming fire. It is like the lightning sparkling in the cloud.

---

### Shloka 28

#### Shloka Text

मसारगल्लर्कमुखश्शङ्खमुक्तानिभोदरः। कस्य नामाभिरूप्योऽसौ न मनो लोभयेन्मृगः।।3.43.28।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मसारगल्लर्कमुखः face like a drinking pot made of emerald, शङ्खमुक्तानिभोदरः belly like conch or pearl, अभिरूप्यः indescribable, असौ मृगः this deer, कस्य नाम whose, मनः mind, न लोभयेत् not be attracted.

#### Explanation

His face is a drinking pot of emerald, the belly is a conch or a pearl. Whose mind will not be attracted by this indescribable beauty ?

---

### Shloka 29

#### Shloka Text

कस्य रूपमिदं दृष्ट्वा जाम्बूनद मयप्रभम्। नानारत्नमयं दिव्यं न मनो विस्मयं व्रजेत्।।3.43.29।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

जाम्बूनदमयप्रभम् glittering like the lustre of gold, नानारत्नमयम् adorned with a variety of gems, दिव्यम् divine, इदम् this, रूपम् beauty, दृष्ट्वा after seeing, कस्य whose, मनः heart, विस्मयम् wonder, न व्रजेत् not attain.

#### Explanation

Whose mind will not be filled with wonder on seeing this divine beauty glittering like lustrous gold and studded with a variety of gems ?

---

### Shloka 30

#### Shloka Text

मांसहेतोरपि मृगान्विनोदार्थं च धन्वनः। घ्नन्ति लक्ष्मण राजानो मृगयायां महावने।।3.43.30।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लक्ष्मण Lakshmana, राजानः king, महावने in a great forest, मृगयायाम् in hunting expedition, मांसहेतोः for meat, धन्वनः archers, विनोदार्थम् for sporting, मृगान् deer, घ्नन्ति will kill.

#### Explanation

O Lakshmana kings who wield  bows  in hunting expeditions kill deer in the forest for sport as well as for venison.

---

### Shloka 31

#### Shloka Text

धनानि व्यवसायेन विचीयन्ते महावने। धातवो विविधाश्चापि मणिरत्नसुवर्णिनः।।3.43.31।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महावने in the huge forest, व्यवसायेन with great effort, धनानि wealth, मणिरत्नसुवर्णिनः gems and gold etc, विविधाः of variety , धातवः precious minerals, विचीयन्ते will be collected.

#### Explanation

From huge forests a variety of mineral wealth consisting of gems, stones and gold are collected with great effort.

---

### Shloka 32

#### Shloka Text

तत्सारमखिलं नृ़णां धनं निचयवर्धनम्। मनसा चिन्तितं सर्वं यथा शुक्रस्य लक्ष्मण।।3.43.32।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लक्ष्मण O Lakshmana, अखिलम् all, तत् all that, सारम् essence, धनम् wealth, नृ़णाम् of men, मनसा in the mind, चिन्तितम् thought over, सर्वम् all, शुक्रस्य यथा like Venus (Venus controls prosperity leading to procreation), निचयवर्धनम् growth of treasure.

#### Explanation

O Lakshmana the essence of forest wealth is mineral wealth, which helps the growth of the treasury. It is conceived mentally as in the case of Venus.

---

### Shloka 33

#### Shloka Text

अर्थी येनार्थकृत्येन संव्रजत्यविचारयन्। तमर्थमर्थशास्त्रज्ञाः प्राहुरर्थ्याश्च लक्ष्मण।।3.43.33।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लक्ष्मण Lakshmana, अर्थी materialist, येन by whichever, अर्थकृत्येन  done for material wealth, अविचारयन् without thinking, संव्रजति moves about, तम् that, अर्थशास्त्रज्ञाः economists, अर्थ्याः scientists, अर्थम् material, प्राहुः called.

#### Explanation

A materialist moves about thoughtlessly for making money. Economists call it material wealth.

---

### Shloka 34

#### Shloka Text

एतस्य मृगरत्नस्य परार्ध्ये काञ्चनत्वचि। उपवेक्ष्यति वैदेही मया सह सुमध्यमा।।3.43.34।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सुमध्यमा a lady of slender waist, वैदेही Vaidehi, एतस्य of this, मृगरत्नस्य of the gem of a deer, परार्ध्ये in an excellent, काञ्चनत्वचि in a golden skin, मया सह with me also, उपवेक्ष्यति  will sit.

#### Explanation

None

---

### Shloka 35

#### Shloka Text

न कादली न प्रियकी न प्रवेणी न चाविकी। भवेदेतस्य सदृशी स्पर्शनेनेति मे मतिः।।3.43.35।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

स्पर्शनेन by touch, कादली blackstriped antelope, एतस्य of this, सदृशी comparable, न भवेत् will be there, प्रियकी goat's, न or even, प्रवेणी a hairy one, न not, आविकी च of a sheep, न not, इति like this, मे I, मतिः I think.

#### Explanation

The skins of various types of deer like Priyaki, Kadali or goats or sheep cannot be compared in softness to the skin of this deer.

---

### Shloka 36

#### Shloka Text

एष चैव मृगश्श्रीमान् यश्च दिव्यो नभश्चरः। उभावेतौ मृगौ दिव्यौ तारामृगमहीमृगौ।।3.43.36।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

श्रीमान् majestic, एषः this one, मृगश्चैव that deer too, नभश्चरः flying in the sky, यश्च whoever, दिव्यः divine, दिव्यौ both are divine, एतौ these two, तारामृगमहीमृगौ deer of the stars and deer of the earth, उभौ both, मृगौ deer.

#### Explanation

This majestic deer and the deer flying in heaven (the deer's figure seen in the Moon called Mrgasira)  both the deer of the star in the sky and the deer of the earth are divine.

---

### Shloka 37

#### Shloka Text

यदि वाऽयं तथा यन्मां भवेद्वदसि लक्ष्मण। मायैषा राक्षसस्येति कर्तव्योस्य वधो मया।।3.43.37।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लक्ष्मण Lakshmana, एषा this, राक्षसस्य of a demon, माया an illusion, इति thus, माम् me, यत् which, वदसि you say, तथा so also, अयम् surely, भवेद्यदि if it is, वा or, मया by me, अस्य his, वधः death, कर्तव्यः should be done.

#### Explanation

Lakshmana, as you say, if it is the illusion of a demon, even then it should be killed by me.

---

### Shloka 38

#### Shloka Text

एतेन हि नृशंसेन मारीचेनाकृतात्मना। वने विचरता पूर्वं हिंसिता मुनिपुङ्गवाः।।3.43.38।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

नृशंसेन by  cruel, अकृतात्मना by evilminded, वने in the forest, विचरता while wandering, एतेन by him, मारीचेन Maricha, पूर्वम् earlier, मुनिपुङ्गवाः great sages, हिंसिताः are harassed.

#### Explanation

The great sages were tortured and killed earlier by this cruel, evilminded Maricha while wandering in the forest.

---

### Shloka 39

#### Shloka Text

उत्थाय बहवो येन मृगयायां जनाधिपाः। निहताः परमेष्वासास्तस्माद्वध्यस्त्वयं मृगः।।3.43.39।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

येन since, उत्थाय after rising, मृगयायाम् during hunting expedition, बहवः many, परमेष्वासाः great archers, जनाधिपाः kings, निहताः killed, तस्मात् therefore, मृगः deer, वध्यः तु should be killed.

#### Explanation

Many kings who were great archers who set out on hunting expedition were killed by him. Therefore, this animal deserves to be killed.

---

### Shloka 40

#### Shloka Text

पुरस्तादिह वातापिः परिभूय तपस्विनः। उदरस्थो द्विजान्हन्तिस्वगर्भोऽश्वतरीमिव।।3.43.40।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पुरस्तात् earlier, इह here, वातापिः Vatapi, तपस्विनः ascetics, परिभूय defeating, उदरस्थः   stationed in the stomach, अश्वतरीम् female mule, स्वगर्भः इव as its own womb, द्विजान् brahmins, हन्ति killed.

#### Explanation

Earlier in this forest, Vatapi used to humiliate the ascetic brahmins and to kill them by entering into their stomach like a womb killing the female mule.

---

### Shloka 41

#### Shloka Text

स कदाचिच्चिराल्लोभादाससाद महामुनिम्। अगस्त्यं तेजसा युक्तं भक्षस्तस्य बभूव ह।।3.43.41।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः that Vatapi, चिरात् after a long time, कदाचित् once, लोभात् greedily, तेजसा by prowess, युक्तम् one endowed, महामुनिम् great sage, अगस्त्यम् Agasthya, आससाद reached, तस्य his, भक्षः food, बभूव ह became.

#### Explanation

That Vatapi after a long time, once greedily entered into the stomach of the great lustrous sage Agastya and became his food.

---

### Shloka 42

#### Shloka Text

समुत्थाने व तद्रूपं कर्तुकामं समीक्ष्य तम्। उत्स्मयित्वा तु भगवान्वातापिमिदमब्रवीत्।।3.43.42।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

समुत्थाने  when he rose, तत् that, रूपम् form, कर्तुकामम् to turn into real form, तम् him, वातापिम् Vatapi, समीक्ष्य after  seeing, भगवान् venerable sage, उत्स्मयित्वा by smiling at him, इदम् this, अब्रवीत् said.

#### Explanation

When the venerable sage Agastya got up (after eating), he observed Vatapi wishing to turn into his real form. So smiling at him, he said:

---

### Shloka 43

#### Shloka Text

त्वयाऽविगण्य वातापे परिभूतास्स्वतेजसा। जीवलोके द्विजश्रेष्ठास्तस्मादसि जरां गतः।।3.43.43।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वातापे O Vatapi, जीवलेके  in the world of living beings, द्विजश्रेष्ठाः best of brahmins, त्वया by you, अविगण्य disregarding, स्वतेजसा with your power, परिभूताः are insulted, तस्मात् on account of that, जरां गतः got digested, असि you are.

#### Explanation

'O Vatapi you killed the best of brahmins living in this world, disregarding them because of your power. Hence you were digested by me.'

---

### Shloka 44

#### Shloka Text

तदेवं न भवेद्रक्षोवातापिरिव लक्ष्मण। मद्विधं योऽतिमन्येत धर्मनित्यं जितेन्द्रियम्।।3.43.44।। भवेद्धतोऽयं वातापिरगस्त्येनेव मां गतः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लक्ष्मण Lakshmana, एवम् in that way, वातापिरिव just as Vatapi, तत् that, रक्षः demon, न भवेत् not live, यः who, धर्मनित्यम् always righteous, जितेन्द्रियम् senses controlled , मद्विधम् my kind, अतिमन्येत whoever violates, अयम् this, मां me, गतः gone, अगस्त्येन by Agastya, वातापिरिव like Vatapi, हतः be killed,  भवेत् will be.

#### Explanation

O Lakshmana just like Vatapi, this demon will not live. Whoever violates a person of my kind who is ever righteous and selfcontrolled will be killed

---

### Shloka 45

#### Shloka Text

इह त्वं भव सन्नद्दो यन्त्रितो रक्ष मैथिलीम्।।3.43.45।। अस्यामायत्तमस्माकं यत्कृत्यं रघुनन्दन।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रघुनन्दन O delight of the Raghu dynasty, त्वम् you, इह here, सन्नद्धः be ready, भव your, यन्त्रितः  very alert, मैथिलीम् Maithili, रक्ष  protect, अस्माकम् for us, यत् whatever, कृत्यम् is the task, अस्याम् in her, आयत्तम् is dependent.

#### Explanation

O Lakshmana, delight of the Raghu dynasty, be very alert and restrained and protect Sita. Whatever duty we have to perform depends on her protection.

---

### Shloka 46

#### Shloka Text

अहमेनं वधिष्यामि ग्रहीष्याम्यपि वा मृगम्।।3.43.46।। यावद्गच्छामि सौमित्रे मृगमानयितुं द्रुतम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सौमित्रे Lakshmana, अहम् I, एनम् him, मृगम् deer, वधिष्यामि will kill, अपि वा or else, ग्रहीष्यामि will catch, मृगम् the deer, आनयितुम् to bring, द्रुतम् quickly, यावद्गच्छामि I will go to get the deer.

#### Explanation

O Lakshmana I will go quickly and either kill the deer or catch it.  Now let me go.

---

### Shloka 47

#### Shloka Text

पश्य लक्ष्मण वैदेहीं मृगत्वचि गतस्पृहाम्।।3.43.47।। त्वचा प्रधानया ह्येष मृगोऽद्य न भविष्यति।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लक्ष्मण Lakshmana, मृगत्वचि at this deerskin, गतस्पृहाम् desire is fixed, वैदेहीम् Vaidehi, पश्य see, प्रधानया ardently, त्वचा by his skin, एषः मृगः of this deer, अद्य now, न भविष्यति will not live.

#### Explanation

See, how Vaidehi ardently longs for the skin of this deer  I will not let the animal live, O Lakshmana

---

### Shloka 48

#### Shloka Text

अप्रमत्तेन ते भाव्यमाश्रमस्थेन सीतया।।3.43.48।। यावत्पृषतमेकेन सायकेन निहन्म्यहम्। हत्वैतच्चर्म चादाय शीघ्रमेष्यामि लक्ष्मण।।3.43.49।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लक्ष्मण Lakshmana, आश्रमस्थेन staying at the hermitage, ते by you, सीतया with Sita, अप्रमत्तेन   vigilant, भाव्यम् should be, पृषतम् speckled antelope, एकेन by only one, बाणेन by arrow, अहम् I, यावत् by the time, निहन्मि I will kill, हत्वा after killing, एतच्चर्म his skin, आदाय getting, शीघ्रम् quickly, एष्यामि I will come.

#### Explanation

Keep vigil over Sita at the hermitage, O Lakshmana  I will certainly kill the speckled antelope with a single arrow, get his skin and come back quickly.

---

### Shloka 49

#### Shloka Text

अप्रमत्तेन ते भाव्यमाश्रमस्थेन सीतया।।3.43.48।। यावत्पृषतमेकेन सायकेन निहन्म्यहम्। हत्वैतच्चर्म चादाय शीघ्रमेष्यामि लक्ष्मण।।3.43.49।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लक्ष्मण Lakshmana, आश्रमस्थेन staying at the hermitage, ते by you, सीतया with Sita, अप्रमत्तेन   vigilant, भाव्यम् should be, पृषतम् speckled antelope, एकेन by only one, बाणेन by arrow, अहम् I, यावत् by the time, निहन्मि I will kill, हत्वा after killing, एतच्चर्म his skin, आदाय getting, शीघ्रम् quickly, एष्यामि I will come.

#### Explanation

Keep vigil over Sita at the hermitage, O Lakshmana  I will certainly kill the speckled antelope with a single arrow, get his skin and come back quickly.

---

### Shloka 50

#### Shloka Text

प्रदक्षिणेनातिबलेन पक्षिणा जटायुषा बुद्धिमता च लक्ष्मण। भवाप्रमत्तः परिगृह्य मैथिलीं प्रतिक्षणं सर्वत एव शङ्कितः।।3.43.50।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लक्ष्मण Lakshmana, मैथिलीम् Sita, परिगृह्य  guarding her, प्रतिक्षणम् every moment, सर्वतः एव from all directions, शङ्कितः suspecting danger, प्रदक्षिणेन  going round, अतिबलेन by the  mighty, बुद्धिमता by wise, पक्षिणा by the bird, जटायुषा Jatayu, अप्रमत्तः vigilant, भव be.

#### Explanation

O Lakshmana, be vigilant in guarding Sita, suspecting danger every moment from all directions. Take the help of Jatayu, the mighty and wise bird who will be on his rounds. इत्याषे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये अरण्यकाण्डे त्रिचत्वारिंशस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the fortythird sarga in Aranyakanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 44

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

तथा तु तं समादिश्य भ्रातरं रघुनन्दनः। बबन्धासिं महातेजा जाम्बूनदमयत्सरुम्।।3.44.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महातेजाः brilliant, रघुनन्दनः delight of the Raghu dynasty, भ्रातरम् brother, तम् to him, तथा in that way, समादिश्य  instructed, जाम्बूनदमयत्सरुम्  golden  handle , असिम् the sword, बबन्ध tied.

#### Explanation

The brilliant Rama, delight of the Raghu dynasty, thus instructed his brother and fastened a sword with a golden handle to his waist.

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

तत स्त्र्यवनतं चापमादायाऽत्मविभूषणम्। आबध्य च कलापौ द्वौ जगामोदग्रविक्रमः।।3.44.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, उदग्रविक्रमः  of indomitable prowess, त्र्यवनतम् bent in three places, आत्मविभूषणम् an ornament to him, चापम् bow, आदाय  took, द्वौ both, कलापौ two quivers, आबद्ध्य tied at the back, जगाम went.

#### Explanation

A man of indomitable prowess, he took the bow with three curves that was like an ornament to him, tied two quivers at the back and left.

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

तं वञ्चयानो राजेन्द्रमापतन्तं निरीक्ष्यवै। बभूवान्तर्हितस्त्रासात्पुनस्सन्दर्शनेऽभवत्।।च3.44.3।। बद्धासिर्धनुरादाय प्रदुद्राव यतो मृगः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

आपतन्तम् approaching, राजेन्द्रम् to the king, तम् him, निरीक्षय seeing, वञ्चयानः while deceiving, त्रासात् out of fear, अन्तर्हितः disappeared, बभूव became, पुनः again, सन्दर्शने within sight, अभवत् appeared, बद्धासिः held his sword, धनुः bow, आदाय lifting, मृगः deer, यतः wherever, प्रदुद्राव ran.

#### Explanation

On seeing Rama approaching him, the deer disappeared due to fear and again came within sight. With his sword and the bow, Rama chased wherever the deer ran.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

तं स्म पश्यति रूपेण द्योतमानमिवाग्रतः।।3.44.4।। अवेक्ष्यावेक्ष्य धावन्तं धनुष्पाणिर्महावने। अतिवृत्तमिषोः पाताल्लोभयानं कदाचन।।3.44.5।। शङ्कितन्तु समुद्भ्रान्तमुत्पतन्तमिवाम्बरे। दृश्यमानमदृश्यं च वनोद्देशेषु केषुचित्।।3.44.6।। छिन्नाभ्रैरिव संवीतं शारदं चन्द्रमण्डलम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

धनुष्पाणिः wielder of the bow (Rama), अवेक्ष्य  saw, महावने in the huge forest, धावन्तम्  running, तम् him, रूपेण form, अग्रतः in front, पश्यति स्म saw, कदाचन sometimes, इषोः  scared of the arrow, पातात् ran, अतिवृत्तम् went out of reach, लोभयानम् enticing, शङ्कितम् frightened, समुद्भ्रान्तम् bewildered, अम्बरे in the sky, उत्पतन्तमिव as if flying  up, छिन्नाभ्रैः by the scattered clouds, संवीतम् surrounded, शारदम् autumnal,चन्द्रमण्डलम् इव like the orb of the Moon, केषुचित् in certain places, वनोद्देशेषु in the forest tract, दृश्यमानम् came within sight, अदृश्यं च again disappeared.

#### Explanation

Rama, wielder of the bow, saw that splendid animal ahead of him. Holding the bow he saw the deer running away, bewldered into the great forest looking at him again and again. The deer was getting out of the range of his arrow, while it enticed him now and then. Suspecting that he might be caught,  the deer was, as though jumping into the sky. Now it came within sight and now out of sight in the huge forest. It looked like the autumnal moon surrounded by clouds. (The clouds muffle the Moon and reveal him now and then).

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

तं स्म पश्यति रूपेण द्योतमानमिवाग्रतः।।3.44.4।। अवेक्ष्यावेक्ष्य धावन्तं धनुष्पाणिर्महावने। अतिवृत्तमिषोः पाताल्लोभयानं कदाचन।।3.44.5।। शङ्कितन्तु समुद्भ्रान्तमुत्पतन्तमिवाम्बरे। दृश्यमानमदृश्यं च वनोद्देशेषु केषुचित्।।3.44.6।। छिन्नाभ्रैरिव संवीतं शारदं चन्द्रमण्डलम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

धनुष्पाणिः wielder of the bow (Rama), अवेक्ष्य  saw, महावने in the huge forest, धावन्तम्  running, तम् him, रूपेण form, अग्रतः in front, पश्यति स्म saw, कदाचन sometimes, इषोः  scared of the arrow, पातात् ran, अतिवृत्तम् went out of reach, लोभयानम् enticing, शङ्कितम् frightened, समुद्भ्रान्तम् bewildered, अम्बरे in the sky, उत्पतन्तमिव as if flying  up, छिन्नाभ्रैः by the scattered clouds, संवीतम् surrounded, शारदम् autumnal,चन्द्रमण्डलम् इव like the orb of the Moon, केषुचित् in certain places, वनोद्देशेषु in the forest tract, दृश्यमानम् came within sight, अदृश्यं च again disappeared.

#### Explanation

Rama, wielder of the bow, saw that splendid animal ahead of him. Holding the bow he saw the deer running away, bewldered into the great forest looking at him again and again. The deer was getting out of the range of his arrow, while it enticed him now and then. Suspecting that he might be caught,  the deer was, as though jumping into the sky. Now it came within sight and now out of sight in the huge forest. It looked like the autumnal moon surrounded by clouds. (The clouds muffle the Moon and reveal him now and then).

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

तं स्म पश्यति रूपेण द्योतमानमिवाग्रतः।।3.44.4।। अवेक्ष्यावेक्ष्य धावन्तं धनुष्पाणिर्महावने। अतिवृत्तमिषोः पाताल्लोभयानं कदाचन।।3.44.5।। शङ्कितन्तु समुद्भ्रान्तमुत्पतन्तमिवाम्बरे। दृश्यमानमदृश्यं च वनोद्देशेषु केषुचित्।।3.44.6।। छिन्नाभ्रैरिव संवीतं शारदं चन्द्रमण्डलम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

धनुष्पाणिः wielder of the bow (Rama), अवेक्ष्य  saw, महावने in the huge forest, धावन्तम्  running, तम् him, रूपेण form, अग्रतः in front, पश्यति स्म saw, कदाचन sometimes, इषोः  scared of the arrow, पातात् ran, अतिवृत्तम् went out of reach, लोभयानम् enticing, शङ्कितम् frightened, समुद्भ्रान्तम् bewildered, अम्बरे in the sky, उत्पतन्तमिव as if flying  up, छिन्नाभ्रैः by the scattered clouds, संवीतम् surrounded, शारदम् autumnal,चन्द्रमण्डलम् इव like the orb of the Moon, केषुचित् in certain places, वनोद्देशेषु in the forest tract, दृश्यमानम् came within sight, अदृश्यं च again disappeared.

#### Explanation

Rama, wielder of the bow, saw that splendid animal ahead of him. Holding the bow he saw the deer running away, bewldered into the great forest looking at him again and again. The deer was getting out of the range of his arrow, while it enticed him now and then. Suspecting that he might be caught,  the deer was, as though jumping into the sky. Now it came within sight and now out of sight in the huge forest. It looked like the autumnal moon surrounded by clouds. (The clouds muffle the Moon and reveal him now and then).

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

मुहुर्तादेव ददृशे मुहुर्दूरात्प्रकाशते।।3.44.7।। दर्शनादर्शनादेवं सोऽपाकर्षत राघवम्। सुदूरमाश्रमस्यास्य मारीचो मृगतां गतः।।3.44.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मृगताम् in the form of a deer, गतः transformed, सः मारीचः that Maricha, मुहूर्तादेव for a moment, ददृशे was seen, मुहुः again, (मुहूर्तात् another moment), दूरात् at a distance, प्रकाशते appeared, एवं as, दर्शनादर्शनात् appearing and disappearing, राघवम् Rama, उपाकर्षत pulled away.

#### Explanation

Maricha, transformed into a deer, was seen now near, now faroff. He pulled Rama away from the hermitage through his exits and entrances.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

मुहुर्तादेव ददृशे मुहुर्दूरात्प्रकाशते।।3.44.7।। दर्शनादर्शनादेवं सोऽपाकर्षत राघवम्। सुदूरमाश्रमस्यास्य मारीचो मृगतां गतः।।3.44.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मृगताम् in the form of a deer, गतः transformed, सः मारीचः that Maricha, मुहूर्तादेव for a moment, ददृशे was seen, मुहुः again, (मुहूर्तात् another moment), दूरात् at a distance, प्रकाशते appeared, एवं as, दर्शनादर्शनात् appearing and disappearing, राघवम् Rama, उपाकर्षत pulled away.

#### Explanation

Maricha, transformed into a deer, was seen now near, now faroff. He pulled Rama away from the hermitage through his exits and entrances.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

आसीत् क्रुद्धस्तु काकुत्स्थो विवशस्तेन मोहितः। अथावतस्थे संभ्रान्तश्चायामाश्रित्य शाद्वले।।3.44.9।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तेन by that, मोहितः deluded, काकुत्स्थ:Rama, विवशः tired, क्रुद्धः in rage, आसीत्  was, अथ then, संभ्रान्तः perplexed, छायाम् shade, आश्रित्य seeking, शाद्वले on green grass, अवतस्थे waited.

#### Explanation

Deluded by that deer, Rama got tired, perplexed and angry. Then bewidered, he waited on a grassland under a shade.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

स तमुन्मादयामास मृगरूपो निशाचरः। मृगैः परिवृतो वन्यैरदूरात्प्रत्यदृश्यत।।3.44.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मृगरूपः assuming the form of a deer, सः निशाचरः that demon, तम् him, उन्मादयामास excited, वन्यैः with forest, मृगैः animals, परिवृतः surrounded by, अदूरात् not very far from him, अदृश्यत was seen.

#### Explanation

That demon transformed himself into a deer and, surrounded by the animals of the forest at close range, attracted his attention.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

गृहीतुकामं दृष्ट्वैवं पुनरेवाभ्यधावत। तत्क्षणादेव संत्रासात्पुनरन्तर्हितोऽभवत्।।3.44.11।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

गृहीतुकामम् intending to catch him, एवम् in that way, दृष्ट्वा after seeing, पुनरेव  once again, अभ्यधावत ran, तत्क्षणादेव in a moment, पुनरेव again, त्रासात् out of fear, अन्तर्हितः disappeared, अभवत् became.

#### Explanation

Seeing Rama's  intention to catch him, the deer ran away once again out of fear and disappeared in a moment.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

पुनरेव ततो दूराद्वृक्षषण्डाद्विनिस्सृतम्। दृष्ट्वा रामो महातेजास्तं हन्तुं कृतनिश्चयः।।3.44.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, महातेजाः resplendent, रामः Rama , पुनरेव again, दूरात् from a distance, वृक्षषण्डात् from a group of trees, विनिस्सृतम् coming out, तम् him, दृष्ट्वा  saw, हन्तुम् to kill, कृतनिश्चयः determined .

#### Explanation

Resplendent Rama saw the deer coming out of a cluster of trees at a distance and determined to kill him.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

भूयस्तु शरमुद्धृत्य कुपितस्तत्र राघवः। सूर्यरश्मिप्रतीकाशं ज्वलन्तमरिमर्दनः।।3.44.13।। सन्धाय सुदृढे चापे विकृष्य बलवद्बली। तमेव मृगमुद्दिश्य श्वसन्तमिव पन्नगम्।।3.44.14।। मुमोच ज्वलितं दीप्तमस्त्रं ब्रह्मविनिर्मितम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तत्र there, भूयः again, कुपितः enraged, अरिमर्दनः destroyer of foes, बली powerful,राघवः Rama, सूर्यरश्मिप्रतीकाशम् glowing like the rays of the Sun, ज्वलन्तम् blazing, शरम् dart, उद्धृत्य lifting, सुदृढे in a sturdy, चापे in bow, सन्धाय  aiming, बलवत् with force, विकृष्य drew, श्वसन्तम् sighing, पन्नगम् इव like the serpent, ज्वलितम् burning, दीप्तम् shining, ब्रह्मनिर्मितम् created by Brahma, अस्त्रम् weapon, तम् him, मृगमेव on the deer, उद्दिश्य aiming, मुमोच released.

#### Explanation

Powerful Rama, destroyer of foes, lifted in a rage his arrow blazing like the light of  the Sun, strung it to his sturdy bow and drew it with all his force. He then aimed at the deer and released the shining  arrow created by Brahma which went hissing like a  serpent.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

भूयस्तु शरमुद्धृत्य कुपितस्तत्र राघवः। सूर्यरश्मिप्रतीकाशं ज्वलन्तमरिमर्दनः।।3.44.13।। सन्धाय सुदृढे चापे विकृष्य बलवद्बली। तमेव मृगमुद्दिश्य श्वसन्तमिव पन्नगम्।।3.44.14।। मुमोच ज्वलितं दीप्तमस्त्रं ब्रह्मविनिर्मितम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तत्र there, भूयः again, कुपितः enraged, अरिमर्दनः destroyer of foes, बली powerful,राघवः Rama, सूर्यरश्मिप्रतीकाशम् glowing like the rays of the Sun, ज्वलन्तम् blazing, शरम् dart, उद्धृत्य lifting, सुदृढे in a sturdy, चापे in bow, सन्धाय  aiming, बलवत् with force, विकृष्य drew, श्वसन्तम् sighing, पन्नगम् इव like the serpent, ज्वलितम् burning, दीप्तम् shining, ब्रह्मनिर्मितम् created by Brahma, अस्त्रम् weapon, तम् him, मृगमेव on the deer, उद्दिश्य aiming, मुमोच released.

#### Explanation

Powerful Rama, destroyer of foes, lifted in a rage his arrow blazing like the light of  the Sun, strung it to his sturdy bow and drew it with all his force. He then aimed at the deer and released the shining  arrow created by Brahma which went hissing like a  serpent.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

शरीरं मृगरूपस्य विनिर्भिद्य शरोत्तमः।।3.44.15।। मारीचस्यैव हृदयं बिभेदाशनिसन्निभः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

आशनिसन्निभः  like thunderbolt, शरोत्तमः the great arrow, मृगरूपस्य  assuming deer form, शरीरम् body, विनिर्भिद्य  pierced, मारीचस्य Maricha's, हृदयमेव heart, बिभेद tore.

#### Explanation

The great arrow which was like a thunderbolt first pierced into the body of Maricha who had assumed the form of a deer and then tore his heart.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

तालमात्रमथोत्प्लुत्य न्यपतत्सशरातुरः।।3.44.16।। विनदन्भैरवं नादं धरण्यामल्पजीवितः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अथ and then, सः Maricha, शरातुरः hurt by the dart, अल्पजीवितः very little life left, भैरवं नादम् frightening sound, विनदन् released, तालमात्रम् to the height of a palm tree, उत्प्लुत्य leaped up, धरण्याम् on earth, न्यपतत् dropped.

#### Explanation

And, hurt by the dart, Maricha roared frighteningly, leaped as high as a  palm tree  and dropped down on earth almost dead.

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

म्रियमाणस्तु मारीचो जहौ तां कृत्रिमां तनुम्।।3.44.17।। स्मृत्वा तद्वचनं रक्षो दध्यौ केन तु लक्ष्मणम्। इह प्रस्थापयेत्सीता शून्ये तां रावणो हरेत्।।3.44.18।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मारीचः Maricha, म्रियमाणः breathing his last, कृत्रिमाम् artificial, ताम् that, तनुम् body, जहौ gave up, रक्षः the demon, तद्वचनम् those words, स्मृत्वा remembering, दध्यौ thought, सीता Sita, केन by what means, लक्ष्मणम् Lakshmana, इह here, प्रस्थापयेत् she may send, शून्ये  in seclusion, ताम् her, रावणः Ravana, हरेत् will abduct.

#### Explanation

Maricha gave up his false form of the deer and before breathing his last, remembered the words of Ravana. He thought over the means through which Sita could send Lakshmana to this spot so that  Ravana would abduct her when she is alone.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

म्रियमाणस्तु मारीचो जहौ तां कृत्रिमां तनुम्।।3.44.17।। स्मृत्वा तद्वचनं रक्षो दध्यौ केन तु लक्ष्मणम्। इह प्रस्थापयेत्सीता शून्ये तां रावणो हरेत्।।3.44.18।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मारीचः Maricha, म्रियमाणः breathing his last, कृत्रिमाम् artificial, ताम् that, तनुम् body, जहौ gave up, रक्षः the demon, तद्वचनम् those words, स्मृत्वा remembering, दध्यौ thought, सीता Sita, केन by what means, लक्ष्मणम् Lakshmana, इह here, प्रस्थापयेत् she may send, शून्ये  in seclusion, ताम् her, रावणः Ravana, हरेत् will abduct.

#### Explanation

Maricha gave up his false form of the deer and before breathing his last, remembered the words of Ravana. He thought over the means through which Sita could send Lakshmana to this spot so that  Ravana would abduct her when she is alone.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

स प्राप्तकालमाज्ञाय चकार च तत स्वनम्। सदृशं राघवस्येह हा सीते लक्ष्मणेति च।।3.44.19।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, सः Maricha, प्राप्तकालम् the right time, आज्ञाय having realised, राघवस्य Rama's, सदृशम् similar, हा सीते Alas, Sita, लक्ष्मण Lakshmana, इति च like that, स्वनम् a sound, चकार  released.

#### Explanation

Then Maricha realised that the appropriate time had come. In a voice similar to Rama's, he shouted loudly 'Alas Sita, Alas Lakshmana '

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

तेन मर्मणि निर्विद्धं शरेणानुपमेन हि। मृगरूपं तु तत्त्यक्त्वा राक्षसं रूपमास्थितः।।3.44.20।। चक्रे स सुमहाकायं मारीचो जीवितं त्यजन्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः मारीचः that, Maricha, तेन by that, अनुपमेन  matchless, शरेण by  the dart, मर्मणि in vital part, निर्विद्धम् struck, तत् then, मृगरूपम् form of a deer, त्वक्त्वा giving up, राक्षसं रूपम् form of a demon, आस्थितः assumed, जीवितम् life, त्यजन्  while giving up, सुमहाकायम् that huge body, चक्रे attained.

#### Explanation

Struck in the vital part by Rama's matchless dart, Maricha gave up the form of the deer and assumed his huge body of the demon.

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

तं दृष्ट्वा पतितं भूमौ राक्षसं घोरदर्शनम्।।3.44.21।। रामो रुधिरसिक्ताङ्गं चेष्टमानं महीतले। जगाम मनसा सीतां लक्ष्मणस्य वचस्स्मरन्।।3.44.22।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रामः Rama, भूमौ on the ground, पतितम् fallen down, घोरदर्शनम्  terrific figure, रुधिरसिक्ताङ्गम् drenched in blood, महीतले on the ground, चेष्टमानम् moving his limbs, तं राक्षसम् that demon, दृष्ट्वा seen, लक्ष्मणस्य Lakshmana's, वचः words, स्मरन् remembered, मनसा by his mind, सीताम् Sita, जगाम went.

#### Explanation

Seeing that terrific demon fallen on the ground, drenched in blood, and strring his limbs, Rama thought of Lakshmana's words and reached Sita mentally (thought of her).

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

तं दृष्ट्वा पतितं भूमौ राक्षसं घोरदर्शनम्।।3.44.21।। रामो रुधिरसिक्ताङ्गं चेष्टमानं महीतले। जगाम मनसा सीतां लक्ष्मणस्य वचस्स्मरन्।।3.44.22।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रामः Rama, भूमौ on the ground, पतितम् fallen down, घोरदर्शनम्  terrific figure, रुधिरसिक्ताङ्गम् drenched in blood, महीतले on the ground, चेष्टमानम् moving his limbs, तं राक्षसम् that demon, दृष्ट्वा seen, लक्ष्मणस्य Lakshmana's, वचः words, स्मरन् remembered, मनसा by his mind, सीताम् Sita, जगाम went.

#### Explanation

Seeing that terrific demon fallen on the ground, drenched in blood, and strring his limbs, Rama thought of Lakshmana's words and reached Sita mentally (thought of her).

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

मारीचस्य तु मायैषा पूर्वोक्तं लक्ष्मणेन तु। तत्तथा ह्यभवच्चाद्य मारीचोऽयं मया हतः।।3.44.23।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

एषा this way, मारीचस्य Maricha's, माया magic, लक्ष्मणेन by Lakshmana, पूर्वोक्तम् told earlier, तत् that, तथा in the same way, अभवत् हि it happened, मया by me, हतः killed, अयम् that, मारीचः Maricha.

#### Explanation

This is an illusion created by Maricha. It happened exactly the way Lakshmana had said earlier. Maricha is now killed.

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

हा सीते लक्ष्मणेत्येवमाक्रुश्य च महास्वरम्। ममार राक्षसस्सोऽयं श्रुत्वा सीता कथं भवेत्।।3.44.24।। लक्ष्मणश्च महाबाहुः कामवस्थां गमिष्यति। इति सञ्चिन्त्य धर्मात्मा रामो हृष्टतनूरुहः।।3.44.25।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः he, Maricha, अयं राक्षसः this demon, हा सीते Alas, Sita, लक्ष्मण (Alas,) Lakshmana, इत्येवम् in this manner, महास्वरम् loudly, आक्रुश्य  screamed, ममार died, श्रुत्वा heard, सीता Sita, कथम् how, भवेत् will she be, महाबाहुः long armed, लक्ष्मणश्च Lakshmana too, काम् what, अवस्थाम् situation, गमिष्यति will reach, धर्मात्मा righteous , रामः Rama, इति like this, सञ्चिन्त्य on thinking, हृष्टतनूरूहः his hair stood on end.

#### Explanation

This demon Maricha screamed aloud saying, 'Alas, Sita, Alas, Lakshmana'. He is now dead. How would Sita react to this voice? What would be the condition of Lakshmana? While righteous Rama reflected on this, his hair stood on end.

---

### Shloka 25

#### Shloka Text

हा सीते लक्ष्मणेत्येवमाक्रुश्य च महास्वरम्। ममार राक्षसस्सोऽयं श्रुत्वा सीता कथं भवेत्।।3.44.24।। लक्ष्मणश्च महाबाहुः कामवस्थां गमिष्यति। इति सञ्चिन्त्य धर्मात्मा रामो हृष्टतनूरुहः।।3.44.25।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः he, Maricha, अयं राक्षसः this demon, हा सीते Alas, Sita, लक्ष्मण (Alas,) Lakshmana, इत्येवम् in this manner, महास्वरम् loudly, आक्रुश्य  screamed, ममार died, श्रुत्वा heard, सीता Sita, कथम् how, भवेत् will she be, महाबाहुः long armed, लक्ष्मणश्च Lakshmana too, काम् what, अवस्थाम् situation, गमिष्यति will reach, धर्मात्मा righteous , रामः Rama, इति like this, सञ्चिन्त्य on thinking, हृष्टतनूरूहः his hair stood on end.

#### Explanation

This demon Maricha screamed aloud saying, 'Alas, Sita, Alas, Lakshmana'. He is now dead. How would Sita react to this voice? What would be the condition of Lakshmana? While righteous Rama reflected on this, his hair stood on end.

---

### Shloka 26

#### Shloka Text

तत्र रामं भयं तीव्रमाविवेश विषादजम्। राक्षसं मृगरूपं तं हत्वा श्रुत्वा च तत्स्वरम्।।3.44.26।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मृगरूपम् in the form of a deer, राक्षसम् demon, हत्वा having killed, तत्स्वरम् that sound, श्रुत्वा च having heard, तत्र there, रामम् Rama, विषादजम् born of sadness, तीव्रम् intense, भयम् fear, आविवेश came over.

#### Explanation

After killing the demon in the figure of a deer and hearing his voice, Rama was overtaken by intense fear born of despair.

---

### Shloka 27

#### Shloka Text

निहत्य पृषतं चान्यं मांसमादाय राघवः। त्वरमाणो जनस्थानं ससाराभिमुखस्तदा।।3.44.27।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तदा then, राघवः Rama, अन्यम् another, पृषतम् deer, निहत्य having killed, मांसम् venison, आदाय  collected, त्वरमाणः quickly, जनस्थानम् Janasthanam, अभिमुखः towards, ससार departed.

#### Explanation

Then Rama killed another deer, collected the venison and hastened to Janasthana. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये अरण्यकाण्डे चतुश्चत्वारिंशस्सर्गः। Thus ends the fortyfourth sarga of Aranyakanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 45

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

आर्तस्वरं तु तं भर्तुर्विज्ञाय सदृशं वने। उवाच लक्ष्मणं सीता गच्छ जीनीहि राघवम्।।3.45.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सीता Sita, वने in the forest, भर्तुः husband, सदृशम् similar, तम् that, आर्तस्वरम् voice in distress, विज्ञाय knowing, लक्ष्मणम् to Lakshmana, उवाच said, गच्छ go, राघवम् Rama, जानीहि know (what happened).

#### Explanation

On hearing the voice in distress and recognising that it was similar to her husband's voice, Sita said to Lakshmana, 'Go and find out Rama's whereabouts'.

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

न हि मे हृदयं स्थाने जीवितं वाऽवतिष्ठति। क्रोशतः परमार्तस्य श्रुतश्शब्दो मया भृशम्।।3.45.2।। आक्रन्दमानं तु वने भ्रातरं त्रातुमर्हसि।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation
