भृशम् greatly, क्रोशतः परमार्तस्य crying out in great distress, शब्द: voice, मया by me, श्रुतः heard, मे my, हृदयम् heart, जीवितं वा my life too, स्थाने in its place, न अवतिष्ठति is not resting, हि indeed, वने in the forest, आक्रन्दमानम् crying in pain, भ्रातरम् brother, त्रातुम् to protect, अर्हसि you ought to.

#### Explanation

I clearly heard Rama calling out in great distress. Indeed, my heart as well as my life is not in its place (restless). You ought to go and protect your brother who is crying in pain in the forest.

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

तं क्षिप्रमभिधाव त्वं भ्रातरं शरणैषिणम्।।3.45.3।। रक्षसां वशमापन्नं सिंहानामिव गोवृषम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सिंहानाम् of the lions, गोवृषमिव like a bull, रक्षसाम् of demons, वशम् grip, आपन्नम् been under it, शरणैषिणम् seeking help, तं भ्रातरम् that brother, क्षिप्रम् quickly, त्वम् you, अभिधाव run.

#### Explanation

Like a bull caught by lions, your brother is seeking help caught by demons. You should run at once to your brother.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

न जगाम तथोक्तस्तु भ्रातुराज्ञाय शासनम्।।3.45.4।। तमुवाच ततस्तत्र कुपिता जनकात्मजा।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तथा like that, उक्तस्तु though instructed , भ्रातुः brother's, शासनम् order, आज्ञाय knowing, न जगाम did not go, ततः then, कुपिता angry, जनकात्मजा Sita, तत्र there, तम् him, उवाच said.

#### Explanation

When he did not budge in obedience to his brother's order (to protect Sita), the daughter of Janaka in a rage said :

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

सौमित्रे मित्ररूपेण भ्रातुस्त्वमसि शत्रुवत्।।3.45.5।। यस्त्वमस्यामवस्थायां भ्रातरं नाभिपत्स्यसे।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सौमित्रे son of Sumitra,( Lakshmana), यः who, त्वम् you, अस्याम् in this, अवस्थायाम् situation, भ्रातरम् your brother, नाभिपत्स्यसे not going to, त्वम् you, भ्रातुः to brother, मित्ररूपेण in the form of a friend, शत्रुवत् enemy, असि you are.

#### Explanation

Since you, O son of Sumitra,  are not reaching out to your brother in this situation, you are an enemy to your brother in the guise of a friend৷৷

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

इच्छसि त्वं विनश्यन्तं रामं लक्ष्मण मत्कृते।।3.45.6।। लोभात्त्वं मत्कृते नूनं नानुगच्छसि राघवम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लक्ष्मण O Lakshmana, त्वम् you, रामम् Rama, विनश्यन्तम् being destroyed, मत्कृते for my sake, इच्छसि desirous, त्वम् you, मत्कृते for my sake, लोभात् out of greed, रामम् Rama, नानुगच्छसि you are not following him, नूनम्  surely.

#### Explanation

It is possess me that you wish Rama's death. You do not rush to him certainly because of greed for me.

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

व्यसनं ते प्रियं मन्ये स्नेहो भ्रातरिनास्तिते।।3.45.7।। तेन तिष्ठसि विश्रब्धंतमपश्यन्महाद्युतिम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ते to you, व्यसनम् distrees, प्रियम् dear, मन्ये I think, ते to you, भ्रातरि for your brother, स्नेहः love, नास्ति is not there, तेन by that, महाद्युतिम् brilliiant, तम् him, अपश्यन् not seen, विश्रब्धम्  confident, तिष्ठसि you are standing.

#### Explanation

I think Rama's adversity is welcome to you. You do not have any love towards your brother. It is for this that you stand unconcerned instead of proceeding to help your brilliant brother.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

किं हि संशयमापन्ने तस्मिन्निह मया भवेत्।।3.45.8।। कर्तव्यमिह तिष्ठन्त्या यत्प्रधानस्त्वमागतः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

त्वम् you, यत्प्रधानः इह primarily to serve Rama, आगतः came here, तस्मिन् when he , संशयम् in difficulty, आपन्ने has met with, इह here, तिष्ठन्त्या remaining, मया by me, किम् what, कर्तव्यम् duty भवेत् be.

#### Explanation

When he for whose service you have primary come here, has met with difficulty, what is the use of your being here? What purpose you serve by staying here with me ?

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

इति ब्रुवाणां वैदेहीं बाष्पशोकपरिप्लुताम्।।3.45.9।। अब्रवील्लक्ष्मणस्त्रस्तां सीतां मृगवधूमिव।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

इति thus, ब्रुवाणाम् while speaking, बाष्पशोकपरिप्लुताम् flooded with tears of sorrow, त्रस्ताम् frightened lady, मृगवधूमिव like a female deer, वैदेहीम् Vaidehi, सीताम् Sita, लक्ष्मणः Lakshmana, अब्रवीत् said.

#### Explanation

While Sita thus spoke, flooded with tears of sorrow, frightened like a doe, Lakshmana replied :

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

पन्नगासुरगन्धर्वदेवमानुषराक्षसैः।।3.45.10।। अशक्यस्तव वैदेहि भर्ता जेतुं न संशयः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वैदेहि Vaidehi, तव your, भर्ता husband, पन्नगासुरगन्धर्वदेवमानुषराक्षसैः pannagas, asuras, gandharvas, gods, men or even demons, जेतुम् to win, अशक्यः not possible, न संशयः there is no doubt.

#### Explanation

O Vaidehi there is no doubt that pannagas, asuras, gandharvas, gods, men or even demons cannot defeat him.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

देवि देवमनुष्येषु गन्धर्वेषु पतत्रिषु।।3.45.11।। राक्षसेषु पिशाचेषु किन्नरेषु मृगेषु च। दानवेषु च घोरेषु स न विद्येत शोभने।।3.45.12।। यो रामं प्रतियुध्येत समरे वासवोपमम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

शोभने देवि O brilliant Devi, समरे वासवोपमम् like Indra in war, रामम् Rama, यः who, प्रतियुध्येत may encounter, देवमनुष्येषु among gods or men, गन्धर्वेषु among gandharvas, पतत्रिषु among birds, राक्षसेषु among demons, पिशाचेषु among goblins, किन्नरेषु even among kinnaras, मृगेषुच even animals, घोरेषु dreadful, दानवेषु demons, न विद्येत not found.

#### Explanation

O brilliant Devi  none  among gods or men, gandharvas, birds or goblins kinnaras, wild animals or dreadful demons can encounter Rama in war.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

देवि देवमनुष्येषु गन्धर्वेषु पतत्रिषु।।3.45.11।। राक्षसेषु पिशाचेषु किन्नरेषु मृगेषु च। दानवेषु च घोरेषु स न विद्येत शोभने।।3.45.12।। यो रामं प्रतियुध्येत समरे वासवोपमम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

शोभने देवि O brilliant Devi, समरे वासवोपमम् like Indra in war, रामम् Rama, यः who, प्रतियुध्येत may encounter, देवमनुष्येषु among gods or men, गन्धर्वेषु among gandharvas, पतत्रिषु among birds, राक्षसेषु among demons, पिशाचेषु among goblins, किन्नरेषु even among kinnaras, मृगेषुच even animals, घोरेषु dreadful, दानवेषु demons, न विद्येत not found.

#### Explanation

O brilliant Devi  none  among gods or men, gandharvas, birds or goblins kinnaras, wild animals or dreadful demons can encounter Rama in war.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

अवध्यस्समरे रामो नैवं त्वं वक्तुमर्हसि।।3.45.13।। न त्वामस्मिन्वने हातुमुत्सहे राघवं विना।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रामः Rama, समरे in war, अवध्यः indestructible, त्वम् you, एवम् in this way, वक्तुम् to speak, नार्हसि it is not proper, त्वाम् you, राघवं विना without Rama, अस्मिन् in this, वने in the forest, हातुम् to leave alone, न उत्सहे I do not wish.

#### Explanation

Rama is indestructible in war. It is not proper for you to say what you said. When he is not here I do not wish to leave you alone here in the forest.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

अनिवार्यं बलं तस्य बलैर्बलवतामपि।।3.45.14।। त्रिभिर्लोकैस्समुद्युक्तैस्सेश्वरैरपि सामरैः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

बलवताम् of strong people, बलैरपि even with all their strength, सेश्वरैः by Indra, सामरैरपि even by those supported by gods, समुद्युक्तै: by the wellequipped, त्रिभिः लोकै by the three worlds, अपि also, तस्य his, बलम् strength, अनिवार्यम् irresistible.

#### Explanation

He cannot be stopped by the strength of the mighty, or by Indra supported by gods or by the wellequipped armies of all the three worlds.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

हृदयं निर्वृतं तेऽस्तुसन्तापस्त्यज्यतामयम्।।3.45.15।। आगमिष्यति ते भर्ता श्रीघ्रं हत्वा मृगोत्तमम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ते your, हृदयम् heart, निर्वृतम् relieved, अस्तु may be, अयम् this, सन्तापः sorrow, त्यज्यताम् give up, ते भर्ता your husband, मृगोत्तमम् excellent deer, हत्वा after killing, श्रीघ्रम् quickly, आगमिष्यति will come back.

#### Explanation

May your heart be relieved (of this fear)  Give up this grief. Your husband will soon return with the excellent deer killed.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

न च तस्य स्वरो व्यक्तं मायया केन चित्कृतः। गन्धर्वनगरप्रख्या माया सा तस्य रक्षसः।।3.45.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तस्य his, स्वरः voice, न not, व्यक्तम् it is clear, केन चित् by some one, मायया deceptively, कृतः is made, सा that, तस्य रक्षसः of that demon, गन्धर्वनगरप्रख्या like the city of the gandharvas, माया illusion.

#### Explanation

Surely it is not his voice. It is contrived by some one's illusory power. It is the illusion created by that demon, like the city of the gandharvas. (which does not exist.)

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

न्यासभूतासि वैदेहि न्यस्ता मयि महात्मना।।3.45.17।। रामेण त्वं वरारोहे न त्वां त्यक्तुमिहोत्सहे।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वरारोहे O beautiful lady, वैदेहि Vaidehi, न्यासभूतासि  entrusted for safety, महात्मना by the great self, रामेण by Rama, मयि with me, न्यस्ता  entrusted, इह here, त्वाम् you, त्यक्तुम् to leave, नोत्सहे  not act.

#### Explanation

O best of women Vaidehi  great Rama has entrusted your safty to me. I do not wish to leave you here alone.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

कृतवैराश्च वैदेहि वयमेतैर्निशाचरैः।।3.45.18।। खरस्य निधनादेव जनस्थानवधं प्रति।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वैदेहि Vaidehi, खरस्य Khara's, निधनादेव since his killing, जनस्थानवधं प्रति towards killing of the residents of Janasthana, वयम् we, एतैः by these, निशाचरैः by the demons, कृतवैराः  developed enmity.

#### Explanation

O Vaidehi since the killing of Khara and the residents of Janasthana we have developed enmity with the demons.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

राक्षसा विविधा वाचो विसृजन्ति महावने।।3.45.19।। हिंसाविहारा वैदेहि न चिन्तयितुमर्हसि।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वैदेहि Vaidehi, महावने in the great forest, हिंसा विहाराः who wander by torturing, राक्षसाः demons, विविधाः different , वाचः words, विसृजन्ति they spread, चिन्तयितुम् to worry, नार्हसि you need not.

#### Explanation

These demons wander about tormenting others and spreading rumours of all kinds. You need not worry about them.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

लक्ष्मणेनैवमुक्ता सा क्रुद्धा संरक्तलोचना।।3.45.20।। अब्रवीत्परुषं वाक्यं लक्ष्मणं सत्यवादिनम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लक्ष्मणेन by  Lakshmana, एवम् in that way, उक्ता having been told, सा Sita, क्रुद्धा an angry, संरक्तलोचना with eyes turned red, सत्यवादिनम् to the truthful, लक्ष्मणम् Lakshmana, परुषम् harsh, वाक्यम् words, अब्रवीत् she spoke.

#### Explanation

Addressed thus by Lakshmana, Sita got mighty angry. Her eyes turned red and she spoke harshly to truthful Lakshmana

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

अनार्याकरणारम्भ नृशंस कुलपांसन।।3.45.21।। अहं तव प्रियं मन्ये रामस्य व्यसनं महत्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अनार्यकरणारम्भ man of bad conduct, नृशंस cruel one, कुलपांसन disgrace to your family, रामस्य at Rama's, महत् great, व्यसनम् disaster, तव yours, प्रियम् pleasing, अहम् I, मन्ये I think.

#### Explanation

O ignoble, cruel Lakshmana, you are a disgrace to your family. I think this great disaster of Rama is a pleasure to you.

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

रामस्य व्यसनं दृष्ट्वा तेनैतानि प्रभाषसे।।3.45.22।। नैतच्चित्रं सपत्नेषु पापं लक्ष्मण यद्भवेत्। त्वद्विधेषु नृशंसेषु नित्यं प्रच्छन्नचारिषु।।3.45.23।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लक्ष्मण Lakshmana, रामस्य व्यसनम् Rama's disaster, दृष्ट्वा  seeing, तेन by that, एतानि these words, (प्र)भाषसे speak, नित्यम् always, प्रच्छन्नचारिषु  who move in disguise, नृशंसेषु cruel men, त्वद्विधेषु among men like you, सपत्नेषु among rivals, पापम् sin, भवेत् इति यत् will be thus, एतत् this, चित्रम् wonder, न not .

#### Explanation

O Lakshmana,do you speak such words seeing the disaster of Rama?  No wonder that cruel men who always move like you in disguise will thus resort to sinful action against rivals.

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

रामस्य व्यसनं दृष्ट्वा तेनैतानि प्रभाषसे।।3.45.22।। नैतच्चित्रं सपत्नेषु पापं लक्ष्मण यद्भवेत्। त्वद्विधेषु नृशंसेषु नित्यं प्रच्छन्नचारिषु।।3.45.23।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लक्ष्मण Lakshmana, रामस्य व्यसनम् Rama's disaster, दृष्ट्वा  seeing, तेन by that, एतानि these words, (प्र)भाषसे speak, नित्यम् always, प्रच्छन्नचारिषु  who move in disguise, नृशंसेषु cruel men, त्वद्विधेषु among men like you, सपत्नेषु among rivals, पापम् sin, भवेत् इति यत् will be thus, एतत् this, चित्रम् wonder, न not .

#### Explanation

O Lakshmana,do you speak such words seeing the disaster of Rama?  No wonder that cruel men who always move like you in disguise will thus resort to sinful action against rivals.

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

सुदुष्टस्त्वं वने राममेकमेकोऽनुगच्छसि। मम हेतोः प्रतिच्छन्नः प्रयुक्तोभरतेन वा।।3.45.24।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सुदुष्टः very wicked , त्वम् you, एकः alone, प्रतिच्छन्नः  hiding your true self, हेतोः reason, भरतेन by Bharata, प्रयुक्तः वा or employed,  एकम् alone, रामम् Rama, वने in forest, अनुगच्छसि you are following.

#### Explanation

You are very wicked. You are hiding your true identity, and employed by Bharata, you are following Rama in the forest as he is alone.

---

### Shloka 25

#### Shloka Text

तन्नसिध्यति सौमित्रे तव वा भरतस्य वा। कथमिन्दीवरश्यामं पद्मपत्रनिभेक्षणम्।।3.45.25।। उपसंश्रित्य भर्तारं कामयेयं पृथग्जनम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सौमित्रे Lakshmana, तव वा yours or, भरतस्य वा or Bharata's, तत् such, नसिध्यति will not be fulfilled, पद्मपत्रनिभेक्षणम् eyes like lotus petals, इन्दीवरश्यामम् looks like a blue lotus,  भर्तारम् husband, उपसंश्रित्य held the hands of, पृथग्जनम् other men, कथम् how, कामयेयम् can I prefer ?

#### Explanation

O Lakshmana such intention of yours or even of Bharata's will not be fulfilled. I have held the hands of Rama who has eyes like the lotus petal, who has the complexion of the blue lotus. How can I prefer some other man?

---

### Shloka 26

#### Shloka Text

समक्षं तव सौमित्रे प्राणांस्त्यक्षे न संशयः। रामं विना क्षणमपि न हि जीवामि भूतले।।3.45.26।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सौमित्रे O Saumitri, तव in your, समक्षम् presence, प्राणाम् life, त्यक्ष्ये I  give up, संशयः doubt, न not, रामं विना without Rama, क्षणमपि even a moment, भूतले on earth, न जीवामि हि I will not live.

#### Explanation

I will undoubtedly give up my life in your presence.I will not live even for a moment on earth without Rama, O son of Sumitra

---

### Shloka 27

#### Shloka Text

इत्युक्तः परुषं वाक्यं सीतया रोमहर्षणम्।।3.45.27।। अब्रवील्लक्ष्मणस्सीतां प्राञ्जलिर्विजितेन्द्रियः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

विजितेन्द्रियः  selfcontrolled, लक्ष्मणः Lakshmana, सीतया by Sita, इति in this way, रोमहर्षणम् causing horripilation, परुषम् harsh, वाक्यम् words, उक्तः uttered, प्राञ्जलिः with folded palms, सीताम् to Sita, अब्रवीत् said.

#### Explanation

The selfcontrolled Lakshmana with folded palms replied to Sita's harsh, horripilating words:

---

### Shloka 28

#### Shloka Text

उत्तरं नोत्सहे वक्तुं दैवतं भवती मम।।3.45.28।। वाक्यमप्रतिरूपं तु न चित्रं स्त्रीषु मैथिलि। स्वभावस्त्वेष नारीणामेवं लोकेषु दृश्यते।।3.45.29।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मैथिलि Mythili, उत्तरम् reply, वक्तुम् to tell, नोत्सहे not wish, भवती yourself, मम for me, दैवतम् deity, अप्रतिरूपम् unworthy, वाक्यम् word, स्त्रीषु among women, चित्रम् wonder, न not, एषः such, नारीणाम् women's, स्वभावस्तु nature, लोकेषु in the world, एवम् likewise, दृश्यते is  seen.

#### Explanation

O princess from Mithila, I do not want to use such harsh words. To me you are a deity (worthy of adoration). It is not surprising that women of the world use such undeserving words. It is their nature.

---

### Shloka 29

#### Shloka Text

उत्तरं नोत्सहे वक्तुं दैवतं भवती मम।।3.45.28।। वाक्यमप्रतिरूपं तु न चित्रं स्त्रीषु मैथिलि। स्वभावस्त्वेष नारीणामेवं लोकेषु दृश्यते।।3.45.29।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मैथिलि Mythili, उत्तरम् reply, वक्तुम् to tell, नोत्सहे not wish, भवती yourself, मम for me, दैवतम् deity, अप्रतिरूपम् unworthy, वाक्यम् word, स्त्रीषु among women, चित्रम् wonder, न not, एषः such, नारीणाम् women's, स्वभावस्तु nature, लोकेषु in the world, एवम् likewise, दृश्यते is  seen.

#### Explanation

O princess from Mithila, I do not want to use such harsh words. To me you are a deity (worthy of adoration). It is not surprising that women of the world use such undeserving words. It is their nature.

---

### Shloka 30

#### Shloka Text

विमुक्तधर्माश्चपलास्तीक्ष्णा भेदकराः स्त्रियः। न सहे हीदृशं वाक्यं वैदेहि जनकात्मजे।।3.45.30।। श्रोत्रयोरुभयोर्मेद्य तप्तनाराचसन्निभम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

स्त्रियः women, विमुक्तधर्माः devoid of dharma, चपलाः fickle, तीक्ष्णाः sharptongued, भेदकराः piercing, जनकात्मजे daughter of Janaka, वैदेहि Vaidehi, मे my, उभयोः two, श्रोत्रयोः ears, तप्तनाराचसन्निभम् like red hot darts(words that pierce ears like arrows), वाक्यम् words, अद्य now, न सहे हि cannot bear

#### Explanation

Women devoid dharma are fickle and inconsistent. They use sharp, piercing words. O daughter of Janaka, O princess from Videha, your words pierce my ears like redhot darts.I cannot tolerate them.

---

### Shloka 31

#### Shloka Text

उपशृण्वन्तु मे सर्वे साक्षिभूता वनेचराः।।3.45.31।। न्यायवादी यथान्यायमुक्तोऽहं परुषं त्वया।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

न्यायवादी am just in speaking, अहम् I, त्वया by you, यथा like this, परुषम् harsh words, अन्यायम् unjustified, उक्तः spoken, मे to me, साक्षिभूताः witnesses , सर्वे all, वनेचराः  foresters, उपशृण्वन्तु listen.

#### Explanation

I speak what is just while you use harsh and unjust words. Let all beings who move in the forest bear witness.

---

### Shloka 32

#### Shloka Text

धिक्त्वामद्य प्रणश्य त्वं यन्मामेवं विशङ्कसे। स्त्रीत्वलदुष्टं स्वभावेन गुरुवाक्ये व्यवस्थितम्।।3.45.32।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

त्वाम् you, धिक् fie upon, स्त्रीत्वात् दुष्टस्वभावेन  woman's wicked nature, त्वम् you, गुरुवाक्ये on the word of my brother, व्यवस्थितम् stood firmly, माम् me, यत्  since, एवम् thus, विशङ्कसे doubting, प्रणश्य you will fall to ruin.

#### Explanation

Fie upon you. Like a woman of wicked nature you doubt me when I stood firm by my brother's words. You will go to ruin.

---

### Shloka 33

#### Shloka Text

गमिष्ये यत्र काकुत्स्थ स्वस्ति तेऽस्तु वरानने।।3.45.33।। रक्षन्तु त्वां विशालाक्षि समग्रा वनदेवताः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वरानने O beautiful lady, काकुत्स्थ Rama, यत्र whereever he be, गमिष्ये will go, ते स्वस्ति अस्तु be happy, विशालाक्षि O largeeyed lady, त्वाम् to you, समग्राः all the, वनदेवताः sylvan deties, रक्षन्तु protect you.

#### Explanation

O beautiful lady, I will go to Rama wherever he may be. Be happy. O largeeyed one, may  all the deities of the forest protect you.

---

### Shloka 34

#### Shloka Text

निमित्तानि च घोराणि यानि प्रादुर्भवन्ति मे।।3.45.34।। अपि त्वां सह रामेण पश्येयं पुनरागतः। न वेत्येतन्न जानामि वैदेहि जनकात्मजे।।3.45.35।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

जनकात्मजे daughter of Janaka, वैदेहि Vaidehi, घोराणि dreadful, यानि those, निमित्तानि omens, मे me, प्रादुर्भवन्ति are manifesting, पुनः again, आगतः when I return, त्वाम् you, सह along with, रामेण with Rama, अपि पश्येयम् will I ever see, न वा or not, इत्येतत् in this way, न जानामि I do not know.

#### Explanation

O daughter of Janaka O princess from Videha, I see dreadful omens. I do not know whether I will see you when I am back with Rama.

---

### Shloka 35

#### Shloka Text

निमित्तानि च घोराणि यानि प्रादुर्भवन्ति मे।।3.45.34।। अपि त्वां सह रामेण पश्येयं पुनरागतः। न वेत्येतन्न जानामि वैदेहि जनकात्मजे।।3.45.35।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

जनकात्मजे daughter of Janaka, वैदेहि Vaidehi, घोराणि dreadful, यानि those, निमित्तानि omens, मे me, प्रादुर्भवन्ति are manifesting, पुनः again, आगतः when I return, त्वाम् you, सह along with, रामेण with Rama, अपि पश्येयम् will I ever see, न वा or not, इत्येतत् in this way, न जानामि I do not know.

#### Explanation

O daughter of Janaka O princess from Videha, I see dreadful omens. I do not know whether I will see you when I am back with Rama.

---

### Shloka 36

#### Shloka Text

लक्ष्मणेनैवमुक्ता सा रुदन्ती जनकात्मजा। प्रत्युवाच ततो वाक्यं तीव्रं बाष्पपरिप्लुता।।3.45.36।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लक्ष्मणेन by Lakshmana, एवम् likewise, उक्ता spoken, सा जनकात्मजा she, the daughter of Janaka, ततः then, रुदन्ती while crying, बाष्पपरिप्लुता eyes filled with tears, तीव्रम् harsh, वाक्यम् words, प्रत्युवाच replied.

#### Explanation

At these words of Lakshmana Sita started crying. With eyes filled with tears she replied with harshness :

---

### Shloka 37

#### Shloka Text

गोदावरीं प्रवेक्ष्यामि विना रामेण लक्ष्मण। अबन्धिष्येऽथवा त्यक्ष्ये विषमे देहमात्मनः।।3.45.37।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लक्ष्मण Lakshmana, रामेण विना without Rama, गोदावरीम् Godavari, प्रवेक्ष्यामि I will enter, अथवा or else, अबन्धिष्ये hang myself, विषमे  cliff ( dangerous place), आत्मनः myself, देहम् body, त्यक्ष्ये will give up.

#### Explanation

O Lakshmana without Rama I will drown myself in Godavari or hang myself or leap from a cliff into death.

---

### Shloka 38

#### Shloka Text

पिबाम्यहं विषं तीक्ष्णं प्रवेक्ष्यामि हुताशनम्। न त्वहं राघवादन्यं पदापि पुरुषं स्पृशे।।3.45.38।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अहम् I, तीक्ष्णम् विषम् venom, पिबामि I will drink, हुताशनम् fire, प्रवेक्ष्यामि I will enter, तु but, अहम् I, राघवात् Rama's, अन्यम् other, पुरुषम् man, पदापि even with feet, न स्पृशे I will not touch.

#### Explanation

I will drink deadly poison or enter fire and die, but will not touch any other man even with my feet.

---

### Shloka 39

#### Shloka Text

इति लक्ष्मणमाक्रुश्य सीता दुःखसमन्विता। पाणिभ्यां रुदती दुःखादुदरं प्रजघान ह।।3.45.39।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सीता Sita, इति thus, लक्ष्मणम् to Lakshmana, आक्रुश्य दुःखसमन्विता blaming and crying in distress, रुदती crying, दुःखात् with grief, पाणिभ्याम् by her hands, उदरम् belly, प्रजघान ह hit herself.

#### Explanation

Thus Sita cried in distress, sadly hitting her belly with both the hands, blaming Lakshmana (all the time).

---

### Shloka 40

#### Shloka Text

तामार्तरूपां विमना रुदन्तीं सौमित्रिरालोक्य विशालनेत्राम्। आश्वासयामास न चैव भर्तु स्तं भ्रातरंकिञ्चिदुवाच सीता।।3.45.40।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सौमित्रिः Saumitri, विमनाः dejected, आर्तरूपाम्  in a pitiable state, रुदन्तीम् crying, ताम् her, विशालनेत्राम् largeeyed, आलोक्य seeing, आश्वसयामास  consoled, सीता Sita, भर्तुः husband's, भ्रातरम् brother, तम् him, किञ्चित् any thing, न उवाच did not speak.

#### Explanation

Saumitri saw  the largeeyed, dejected Sita crying in distress.He pacified and consoled her but she did not say anything at all to her husband's brother.

---

## Sarga 46

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

तया परुषमुक्तस्तु कुपितो राघवानुजः। स विकाङ्क्षन्भृशं रामं प्रतस्थे नचिरादिव।।3.46.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तया by her, परुषम् harsh, उक्तः said, सः he, राघवानुजः brother of Rama, कुपितः angry, भृशम् very much, विकांक्षन्  wished, न चिरादिव very soon, रामम् to Rama, प्रतस्थे  went.

#### Explanation

Lakshmana, brother of Rama, hurt by the harsh words of Sita, hastened with Rama's wellbeing in mind.

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

तदासाद्य दशग्रीवः क्षिप्रमन्तरमास्थितः। अभिचक्राम वैदेहीं परिव्राजकरूपधृक्।।3.46.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अन्तरम् inside, आस्थितः in hiding, दशग्रीवः tenheaded Ravana, तत् there, आसाद्य assuming, परिव्राजकरूपधृक् assuming the form of a wandering mendicant, वैदेहीम् to Vaidehi, अभिचक्राम approached.

#### Explanation

The tenheaded Ravana who was in hiding assumed the guise of a wandering mendicant and approached Sita.

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

श्लक्ष्णकाषायसंवीतश्शिखी छत्री उपानही। वामे चांसेऽवसज्याथ शुभे यष्टिकमण्डलू।।3.46.3।। परिव्राजकरूपेण वैदेहीमन्वपद्यत।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अथ then, श्लक्ष्णकाषायसंवीतः wearing fine orange robes, शिखी with knotted hair on the head, छत्री with a parasol, उपानही wearing sandals, वामे अंसे on his left shoulder, शुभे auspicious, यष्टिकमण्डलू staff and water pot, आसज्य hung to the shoulder, परिव्राजकरूपेण as  a mendicant, वैदेहीम् to Vaidehi, अन्वपद्यत walked towards.

#### Explanation

And then Ravana wearing fine orange robes, with hair knotted on the head, carrying a parasol, wearing sandals, hanging a kamandalu (waterpot), and a staff on the auspicious left shoulder walked towards Vaidehi like a mendicant.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

तामाससादातिबलो भ्रातृभ्यां रहितां वने।।3.46.4।। रहितां चन्द्रसूर्याभ्यां सन्ध्यामिव महत्तमः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अतिबलः mighty Ravana, वने in the forest, भ्रातृभ्याम् with both brothers, रहिताम् in the absence of, ताम् them, चन्द्रसूर्याभ्याम् by both Sun and Moon, सन्ध्याम् in the evening twilight, महत् great, तमः इव like darkness, आससाद reached.

#### Explanation

Mighty Ravana approached Sita deprived of both the brothers, Like darkness encompasses the evening twilight in the absence of the Sun and the Moon.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

तामपश्यत्ततो बालां रामपत्नीं यशस्विनीम्।।3.46.5।। रोहिणीं शशिना हीनां ग्रहवद्भृशदारुणः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, भृशदारुणः extremely cruel, यशस्विनीम् illustrious lady, रामपत्नीम् Rama's wife, बालाम् young lady, ताम् her, शशिना Moon, हीनाम् without, रोहिणीम्  at  Rohini, ग्रहवत् like planet Ketu, अपश्यत् gazed.

#### Explanation

The extremely cruel Ravana saw the illustrious young wife of Rama just as Ketu looks at Rohini star in the absence of the Moon.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

तमुग्रतेजःकर्माणं जनस्थानरूहा द्रुमाः।।3.46.6।। समीक्ष्य सम्प्रकम्पन्ते न प्रवाति च मारुतः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

उग्रतेजःकर्माणम् of ferocious activities, तम् him, समीक्ष्य  saw, जनस्थानरुहाः at Janasthana, महाद्रुमाः huge trees, सम्प्रकम्पन्ते  shaken up, मारुतः च even the wind, न प्रवाति blows not.

#### Explanation

Seeing the ferocious Ravana, the huge trees at Janasthana shook vidently. Even the wind ceased to blow.

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

शीघ्रस्रोताश्च तं दृष्ट्वा वीक्षन्तं रक्तलोचनम्।।3.46.7।। स्तिमितं गन्तुमारेभे भयाद्गोदावरी नदी।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

शीघ्रस्रोताः swiftflowing, गोदावरीनदी river Godavari, रक्तलोचनम् bloodred eyes, वीक्षन्तम् looked at, तम् him, दृष्ट्वा on seeing, भयात् out of fear, स्तिमितम् slow, गन्तुम् आरेभे started flowing

#### Explanation

Seeing Ravana with his bloodred eyes, the swiftflowing river Godavari slowed down out of fear.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

रामस्य त्वन्तरप्रेप्सुर्दशग्रीवस्तदन्तरे।।3.46.8।। उपतस्थे च वै देहीं भिक्षुरूपेण रावणः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रामस्य Rama's, अन्तरप्रेप्सुः waiting for a chance, दशग्रीवः tenheaded, रावणः Ravana, तदन्तरे  in his absence, भिक्षुरूपेण disguised in the form of a mendicant, वैदेहीम् to Vaidehi, उपतस्थे went close.

#### Explanation

The tenheaded Ravana who was waiting for Rama's absence now got it. Disguised as a mendicant he stepped toward to where Sita was.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

अभव्यो भव्यरूपेण भर्तारमनुशोचतीम्।।3.46.9।। अभ्यवर्तत वैदेहीं चित्रामिव शनैश्चरः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अभव्यः evil one, भर्तारम् about husband, अनुशोचतीम्  worried , वैदेहीम् to Vaidehi, भव्यरूपेण disguised like a holy man, शनैश्चरः like the Saturn moving to, चित्रामिव towards Chitra , अभ्यवर्तत stood near.

#### Explanation

The evilminded Ravana in the guise of a holy man, like Saturn moves towards Chitra star, came close to Sita who was brooding over her husband.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

स पापो भव्यरूपेण तृणैः कूप इवावृतः।।3.46.10।। अतिष्ठत्प्रेक्ष्य वैदेहीं रामपत्निं यशस्विनीम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कूपः well, तृणै by grass, भव्यरूपेण disguised like a holy man, आवृतः concealed, इव like, सः he, पापः that sinner, यशस्विनीम् illustrious, रामपत्नीम् Rama's wife, वैदेहीम् Vaidehi, प्रेक्ष्य after seeing, अतिष्ठत् stood.

#### Explanation

Just like a well is concealed by an overgrowth of grass, Ravana guised as a holy man stood looking at illustrious Sita, the wife of Rama.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

शुभां रुचिरदन्तोष्ठीं पूर्णचन्द्रनिभाननाम्।।3.46.11।। आसीनां पर्णशालायां बाष्पशोकाभिपीडिताम्। स तां पद्मपलाशाक्षीं पीतकौशेयवासिनीम्।।3.46.12।। अभ्यगच्छत वैदेहीं दुष्टचेता निशाचरः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

दुष्टचेताः wicked, सः निशाचरः that nightwalker, शुभाम् auspicious lady, रुचिरदन्तोष्ठीम् lwith lovely teeth and lips, पूर्णचन्द्रनिभाननाम् face like the fullmoon, पर्णशालायाम् in the cottage, आसीनाम् seated, बाष्पशोकाभिपीडिताम् tormented by tears of grief, पद्मपलाशाक्षीम् eyes like lotus petals, पीतकौशेयवासिनीम् clad in yellow silk, तां वैदेहीम् that Vaidehi, अभ्यगच्छत came close.

#### Explanation

The wicked demon came close to that auspicious princess from Videha sitting in the cottage Her face was like the full moon. Her teeth and lips were lovely. She was clad in yellow silk. Her eyes were like lotus petals tormented by tears of grief.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

शुभां रुचिरदन्तोष्ठीं पूर्णचन्द्रनिभाननाम्।।3.46.11।। आसीनां पर्णशालायां बाष्पशोकाभिपीडिताम्। स तां पद्मपलाशाक्षीं पीतकौशेयवासिनीम्।।3.46.12।। अभ्यगच्छत वैदेहीं दुष्टचेता निशाचरः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

दुष्टचेताः wicked, सः निशाचरः that nightwalker, शुभाम् auspicious lady, रुचिरदन्तोष्ठीम् lwith lovely teeth and lips, पूर्णचन्द्रनिभाननाम् face like the fullmoon, पर्णशालायाम् in the cottage, आसीनाम् seated, बाष्पशोकाभिपीडिताम् tormented by tears of grief, पद्मपलाशाक्षीम् eyes like lotus petals, पीतकौशेयवासिनीम् clad in yellow silk, तां वैदेहीम् that Vaidehi, अभ्यगच्छत came close.

#### Explanation

The wicked demon came close to that auspicious princess from Videha sitting in the cottage Her face was like the full moon. Her teeth and lips were lovely. She was clad in yellow silk. Her eyes were like lotus petals tormented by tears of grief.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

स मन्मथशराविष्टो ब्रह्मघोषमुदीरयन्।।3.46.13।। अब्रवीत्प्रश्रितं वाक्यं रहिते राक्षसाधिपः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मन्मथशराविष्टः struck by the arrows of the god of love, सः राक्षसाधिपः that lord of the demons, ब्रह्मघोषम् chanting from the Vedas, उदीरयन् while chanting, रहिते in a solitary spot, प्रश्रितम् humbly, वाक्यम् these words, अब्रवीत् spoke.

#### Explanation

None

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

तामुत्तमां स्त्रियं लोके पद्महीनामिव श्रियम्।।3.46.14।। विभ्राजमानां वपुषा रावणः प्रशशंस ह।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लोके in the world, उत्तमाम् the best, स्त्रियम् lady, पद्महीनाम् without a lotus, श्रियम् इव like goddess of wealth, वपुषा with her body, विभ्राजमानाम् shining, ताम् her, रावणः Ravana, प्रशशंस ह he praised.

#### Explanation

Sita was the most lovely lady of the world shining in beauty. She was like the goddess of wealth (Laxmi) without her lotus. Ravana praised her thus:

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

का त्वं काञ्चनवर्णाभे पीतकौशेयवासिनि।।3.46.15।। कमलानां शुभां मालां पद्मिनीव हि बिभ्रती।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

काञ्चनवर्णाभे of golden hue, पीतकौशेयवासिनि clad in yellow silk, पद्मिनीव like a lotus pond, कमलानाम् of lotuses, शुभाम् auspicious, मालाम् garland, बिभ्रती wearing, त्वम् you, का who are you?

#### Explanation

Who are you with a golden complexion, clad in yellow silk, and looking like a lotuspond and wearing an auspicious lotus garland?

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

ह्रीः कीर्तिः श्रीश्शुभा लक्ष्मीरप्सरा वा शुभानने।।3.46.16।। भूतिर्वा त्वं वरारोहे रतिर्वा स्वैरचारिणी।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

शुभानने lady of beautiful face, वरारोहे with lovely things, त्वम् you, ह्रीः shyness personified, कीर्तिः goddess of fame, शुभा auspicious, श्रीः or goddess of wealth, लक्ष्मीः or Lakshmi, अप्सरा वा  or apsara?, भूतिर्वा Bhuti, goddess of fortune, स्वैरचारिणी Rati who makes at will freely, रतिर्वा or Rati, golden of love.

#### Explanation

O lady with a beautiful face, with lovely thighs, are you 'hri', shyness personified? Are you the auspicious lovely Lakshmi, the  goddess of wealth? Are you the goddess of fame? Are you an apsara? Are you Bhuti, the goddess of fortune? Or are you Rati, the goddess of love moving at your free will ?

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

समाश्शिखरिणस्स्निग्धाः पाण्डुरा दशनास्तव।।3.46.17।। विशाले विमले नेत्रे रक्तान्ते कृष्णतारके।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

दशनाः teeth, समाः even, शिखरिणः pointed, स्निग्धाः shining, तव your, पाण्डुराः pale white, नेत्रे eyes, विशाले large, विमले clear, रक्तान्ते having reddish tinge, कृष्णतारके having dark pupils.

#### Explanation

Your teeth are even and pointed, white and beautiful.Your eyes are large and clear and sparkling with dark pupils with a red tinge at the corner.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

विशालं जघनं पीनमूरू करिकरोपमौ।।3.46.18।। एतावुपचितौ वृत्तौ संहतौ सम्प्रवल्गितौ। पीनोन्नतमुखौ कान्तौ स्निग्धौ तालफलोपमौ।।3.46.19।। मणिप्रवेकाभरणौ रुचिरौ ते पयोधरौ।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

विशालम् big, जघनम् hips, पीनम् stout, ऊरू thighs, करिकरोपमौ like the elephant's trunk, ते your, एताः these two, मणिप्रवेकाभरणौ adorned with gems, पयोधरौ breasts, उपचितौ wellformed, वृत्तौ round, संहतौ coming together, सम्प्रवल्गितौ drooping due to weight, पीनोन्नतमुखौ  are stout and nipples projecting, कान्तौ shining, स्निग्धौ delightful, तालफलोपमौ like two palm friuts, रुचिरौ adding beauty.

#### Explanation

Your big hips are stout and strong, Your smooth thighs are like the trunk of an elephant, your breasts adorned with gems, are round and robust like palm fruits and drooping with heaviness with projected stiff nipples, shining and delightful, adding beauty.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

विशालं जघनं पीनमूरू करिकरोपमौ।।3.46.18।। एतावुपचितौ वृत्तौ संहतौ सम्प्रवल्गितौ। पीनोन्नतमुखौ कान्तौ स्निग्धौ तालफलोपमौ।।3.46.19।। मणिप्रवेकाभरणौ रुचिरौ ते पयोधरौ।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

विशालम् big, जघनम् hips, पीनम् stout, ऊरू thighs, करिकरोपमौ like the elephant's trunk, ते your, एताः these two, मणिप्रवेकाभरणौ adorned with gems, पयोधरौ breasts, उपचितौ wellformed, वृत्तौ round, संहतौ coming together, सम्प्रवल्गितौ drooping due to weight, पीनोन्नतमुखौ  are stout and nipples projecting, कान्तौ shining, स्निग्धौ delightful, तालफलोपमौ like two palm friuts, रुचिरौ adding beauty.

#### Explanation

Your big hips are stout and strong, Your smooth thighs are like the trunk of an elephant, your breasts adorned with gems, are round and robust like palm fruits and drooping with heaviness with projected stiff nipples, shining and delightful, adding beauty.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

चारुस्मिते चारुदति चारुनेत्रे विलासिनि।।3.46.20।। मनो हरसि मे कान्ते नदी कूलमिवाम्भसा। करान्तमितमध्यासि सुकेशी संहतस्तनी।।3.46.21।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

चारुस्मिते lady  with an elegant smile, चारुदति lady of lovely teeth, चारुनेत्रे beautiful eyes, विलासिनि lovely lady, कान्ते attractive lady, नदी river, अम्भसा with water, कूलमिव like a river bank, मे my, मनः mind, हरसि  capturing, करान्तमितमध्या  slender waist could be compassed with fist, सुकेशी of beautiful hair, संहतस्तनी breasts rubbing agaist each other, असि you are.

#### Explanation

O lady with an elegant smile, beautiful teeth, lovely  eyes you are enticing.Your slender waist can be compassed by my fist.Your hair is beautiful and your large breasts are rubbing against each other.You are capturing my mind  just as the flow of water in a river  touches its banks.

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

चारुस्मिते चारुदति चारुनेत्रे विलासिनि।।3.46.20।। मनो हरसि मे कान्ते नदी कूलमिवाम्भसा। करान्तमितमध्यासि सुकेशी संहतस्तनी।।3.46.21।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

चारुस्मिते lady  with an elegant smile, चारुदति lady of lovely teeth, चारुनेत्रे beautiful eyes, विलासिनि lovely lady, कान्ते attractive lady, नदी river, अम्भसा with water, कूलमिव like a river bank, मे my, मनः mind, हरसि  capturing, करान्तमितमध्या  slender waist could be compassed with fist, सुकेशी of beautiful hair, संहतस्तनी breasts rubbing agaist each other, असि you are.

#### Explanation

O lady with an elegant smile, beautiful teeth, lovely  eyes you are enticing.Your slender waist can be compassed by my fist.Your hair is beautiful and your large breasts are rubbing against each other.You are capturing my mind  just as the flow of water in a river  touches its banks.

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

नैव देवी न गन्धर्वी न यक्षी न च किन्नरी। नैवंरूपा मया नारी दृष्टपूर्वा महीतले।।3.46.22।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मया by  me, महीतले on this earth, एवं रूपा such a beauty, नारी lady, न दृष्टपूर्वा not seen earlier, देवी O goddess, नैव neither, गन्धर्वी gandharva woman, न not even, यक्षी yakshi, न not, किन्नरी kinnera woman.

#### Explanation

O beautiful lady I have not seen such a beauty earlier either among goddesses or among gandharavis, or yakshis or even among kinneris.

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

रूपमग्र्यं च लोकेषु सौकुमार्यं वयश्चते। इह वासश्च कान्तारे चित्तमुन्मादयन्ति मे।।3.46.23।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लोकेषु in the world, अग्र्यम् the best, ते your, रूपम् beauty, सौकुमार्यम् tenderness, वयश्च youth, इह here, कान्तारे in this forlorn forest, वासश्च living, मे my, चित्तम् heart, उन्मादयन्ति are maddening.

#### Explanation

You are the most beautiful among women in the world. With all your tenderness and youth you are living in this forlorn forest. This maddens my heart.

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

सा प्रतिक्राम भद्रं ते नैवं वस्तुमिहार्हसि। राक्षसानामयं वासो घोराणां कामरूपिणाम्।।3.46.24।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सा such a lady, प्रतिक्राम move out, ते of yours, भद्रम् safety, इह here, एवम् in this way, वस्तुम् to reside, न अर्हसि not proper, अयम् this is, घोराणाम् horrible, कामरूपिणाम् who can change their form at will, राक्षसानाम् of demons, वासः dwelling place.

#### Explanation

It is not safe for a lady like you to reside here. Move out at once. This is only fit for the dwelling of horrible demons who can change their form at will.

---

### Shloka 25

#### Shloka Text

प्रासादाग्राणि रम्याणि नगरोपवनानि च। सम्पन्नानि सुगन्धीनि युक्तान्याचरितुं त्वया।।3.46.25।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

प्रासादाग्राणि terraces of palaces, रम्याणि beautiful, सम्पन्नानि luxurious, सुगन्धीनि fragrant, नगरोपवनानि च city gardens, त्वया yourself, आचरितुम् to stroll, युक्तानि deserve.

#### Explanation

You deserve to stroll in the terraces of palaces, in beautiful, luxurious and fragrant city gardens.

---

### Shloka 26

#### Shloka Text

वरं माल्यं वरं भोज्यं वरं वस्त्रं च शोभने। भर्तारं च वरं मन्ये त्वद्युक्तमसितेक्षणे।।3.46.26।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

असितेक्षणे  black eyed lady, शोभने O beauty, वरम् best of, माल्यम् garlands, वरम् best of, भोज्यम् food, वरम् best of, वस्त्रम् clothes, वरम् choicest, भर्तारं च and husband, त्वद्युक्तम् suitable for you, मन्ये I think.

#### Explanation

O beautiful, blackeyed lady, I think you deserve the best of garlands, best of food, and clothes and a suitable husband.

---

### Shloka 27

#### Shloka Text

का त्वं भवसि रुद्राणां मरुतां वा वरानने। वसूनां वा वरावोहे देवता प्रतिभासि मे।।3.46.27।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वरानने O beautiful lady, वरारोहे O with beautiful buttocks lady, त्वम् you, का भवसि who are you, रुद्राणाम् among Rudras, मरुतां वा or Maruta's, वसूनां वा or Vasus, मे to me, देवता goddess, प्रतिभासि you appear.

#### Explanation

O best of women O lady with beautiful buttocks  can you be one  of the Rudras or Maruts or Vasus? To me, you appear like a goddess.

---

### Shloka 28

#### Shloka Text

नेह गच्छन्ति गन्धर्वा न देवा न च किन्नराः। राक्षसानामयं वासः कथं नु त्वमिहागता।।3.46.28।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

इह here, गन्धर्वाः gandharva, न गच्छन्ति do not go this way, देवाः gods, न not, किन्नराः च and kinnara, न not, अयम् this is, राक्षसानाम् for demons, वासः dwelling place, त्वम् you, कथम् how, इह here, आगता came?

#### Explanation

Neither gandharvas, nor kinnaras, nor gods move here. This is only a dwelling place for the demons. How did you come here ?

---

### Shloka 29

#### Shloka Text

इह शाखामृगास्सिंहा द्वीपिव्याघ्रमृगास्तथा। ऋक्षास्तरक्षवः कङ्काः कथं तेभ्यो न बिभ्यसि।।3.46.29।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

इह here, शाखामृगाः monkeys, सिंहाः lions, द्वीपिव्याघ्रमृगाः panthers, tigers and other wild animals, तथा so also, ऋक्षाः bears, तरक्षवः hyenas, कङ्काः Kanka birds, तेभ्यः by them, कथम् how is it, न बिभ्यसि you are not frightened.

#### Explanation

There are monkeys, lions, panthers, tigers, hyenas, bears, kanka birds and other wild animals here. How is it that you are not afraid of them?

---

### Shloka 30

#### Shloka Text

मदान्वितानां घोराणां कुञ्जराणां तरस्विनाम्। कथमेका महारण्ये न बिभेषि वरानने।।3.46.30।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वरानने beautiful lady, महारण्ये in dense woods, मदान्वितानाम्  in rut, घोराणाम् fierce, तरस्विनाम् swift and powerful, कुञ्जराणाम् of elephants, एका alone, कथम् how, न बिभेषि are you not scared of them?

#### Explanation

O Charming lady how is it that although alone you are not afraid of the dreadful, swift, powerful wild animals like elephants in rut ?

---

### Shloka 31

#### Shloka Text

कासि कस्य कुतश्चित्त्वं किं निमित्तं च दण्डकान्। एका चरसि कल्याणि घोरान्राक्षससेवितान्।।3.46.31।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कल्याणि O auspicious lady, त्वम् you, का असि who are you, कस्य to whose, कुतश्चित् where did you come from ?, राक्षससेवितान् inhabited by demons, घोरान् terrible, दण्डकान् Dandaka, एका alone, किं निमित्तम् for what reason, चरसि you roam.

#### Explanation

Who are you, O auspicious lady ? Who are your people? Why are you here? From where have you come? For what reason are you going about alone in this fierce Dandaka tract, inhabited by demons?

---

### Shloka 32

#### Shloka Text

इति प्रशस्ता वैदेही रावणेन दुरात्मना। द्विजातिवेशेण हि तं दृष्ट्वा रावणमागतम्।।3.46.32।। सर्वैरतिथिसत्कारैः पूजयामास मैथिली।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

दुरात्मना by the villain, रावणेन by Ravana, इति thus, प्रशस्ता praised, मैथिली Maithili, वैदेही princess of Videha, द्विजातिवेशेण in the guise of a brahmin, आगतम् come, तं रावणम् that Ravana, दृष्ट्वा after seeing, सर्वैः with all, अतिथिसत्कारैः  with hospitality, पूजयामास  honoured him.

#### Explanation

Seeing Ravana, a villain in the guise of a brahmin praising her, Sita, the princess of Videha, honoured him with all hospitality.

---

### Shloka 33

#### Shloka Text

उपनीयासनं पूर्वं पाद्येनाभिनिमन्त्र्य च।।3.46.33।। अब्रवीत्सिद्धमित्येव तदा तं सौम्यदर्शनम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तदा then, पूर्वम् first, आसनम् seat, उपनीय brought, पाद्येन water for his feet, अभिनिमन्त्र्य  च and inviting (for food), सौम्यदर्शनम् lovelylooking, तम् to him, सिद्धम् food ready, इत्येव  in this way, अब्रवीत् said.

#### Explanation

She offered the handsome guest a seat first and then water for washing his feet with, and then invited him for food.

---

### Shloka 34

#### Shloka Text

द्विजातिवेषेण समीक्ष्य मैथिली समागतं पात्रकुसुम्भधारिणम्। अशक्यमुद्वेष्टुमपायदर्शनम् न्यमन्त्रयद्ब्राह्मणवत्तदाङ्गना।।3.46.34।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तदा then, मैथिली Maithili, अङ्गना lady, द्विजातिवेशेण in the guise of a brahmin, समागतम् having come, पात्रकुसुम्भधारिणम् holding a bottlegourd as a begging bowl, उद्वेष्टुम् to hate, अशक्यम् not possible, अपायदर्शनम् dangerous intention, ब्राह्मणवत् brahmin, न्यमन्त्रयत् she invited.

#### Explanation

Sita, being a lady, was unable to doubt him since he came in the guise of a brahmin, holding a bottlegourd and a begging bowl. Without knowing his illintention she invited him in, mistaking him for a brahmin.

---

### Shloka 35

#### Shloka Text

इयं बृसी ब्राह्मण काममास्यतामिदं च पाद्यं प्रतिगृह्यतामिति। इदं च सिद्धं वनजातमुत्तमं त्वदर्थमव्यग्रमिहोपभुज्यताम्।।3.46.35।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ब्राह्मण O brahmin, इयम् this is, बृसी the seat of an ascetic, कामम् freely, आस्यताम् be seated, इदम् this is, पाद्यम् water for washing feet, प्रतिगृह्यताम् please take, इति these, इदम् this is, उत्तमम् best, वनजातम् available in the forest, त्वदर्थम् for you, सिद्धम् kept ready, इह here, अव्यग्रम् without hesitation, उपभुज्यताम् please eat.

#### Explanation

O brahmin, here is the seat for you. Feel free to sit. Here is water to wash your feet with. This is the best of food for you collected from the forest. Please partake this without hesitation.

---

### Shloka 36

#### Shloka Text

निमन्त्र्यमाणः प्रतिपूर्णभाषिणीं नरेन्द्रपत्नीं प्रसमीक्ष्य मैथिलीम्। प्रसह्य तस्याहरणे धृतं मनः समर्पयत्स्वात्मवधाय रावणः।।3.46.36।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

निमन्त्र्यमाणः as he was being invited, रावणः Ravana, प्रतिपूर्णभाषिणीम् softspoken, नरेन्द्रपत्नीम् wife of the king, मैथिलीम् Maithili, प्रसमीक्ष्य looking at her, स्वात्मवधाय for his own destruction, प्रसह्य forcibly, तस्याः her, हरणे to abduct, मनः set his mind, धृतम् intent upon, समर्पयत् presented.

#### Explanation

Ravana was observing the princess from Mithila, wife of a king inviting him  and speaking to him in cordial manner. Looking at her, he resolved to  abduct her forcibly, inviting his own death.

---

### Shloka 37

#### Shloka Text

ततः सुवेषं मृगयागतं पतिं प्रतीक्षमाणा सहलक्ष्मणं तदा। विवीक्षमाणा हरितं ददर्श तन्महद्वनं नैव तु रामलक्ष्णौ।।3.46.37।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, सुवेषम् that beautiful form of Rama, मृगयागतम् gone to hunt the deer, सहलक्ष्मणम् along with Lakshmana, पतिम् husband, प्रतीक्षमाणा while waited, तदा then, विवीक्षमाणा  while she looked with eagerness, हरितम् green, तत् that, महत् dense, वनम् forest, ददर्श saw, रामलक्ष्मणौ तु for Rama and Lakshmana, नैव not seen.

#### Explanation

Sita looked out for the beautiful figure of Rama who had gone to hunt the deer along with Lakshmana. Waiting eagerly for her husband, she only saw the vast stretch of green forest  but not Rama and Lakshmana. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये अरण्यकाण्डे षट्चत्वारिंशस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the fortysixth sarga of Aranyakanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 47

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

रावणेन तु वैदेही तदा पृष्टा जिहीर्षता। परिव्राजकरूपेण शशंसात्मानमात्मना।।3.47.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तदा then, जिहीर्षुणा of crooked intention of abducting, परिव्राजकरूपेण in the guise of a mendicant, रावणेन by Ravana, पृष्टा was asked, वैदेही Vaidehi, आत्मानम् about her, आत्मना herself, शशंस told.

#### Explanation

Then Ravana in the guise of a mendicant with crooked intention to abduct her, asked Vaidehi to tell him about herself.

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

ब्राह्मणश्चातिथिश्चायमनुक्तो हि शपेत माम्। इति ध्यात्वा मुहूर्तं तु सीता वचनमब्रवीत्।।3.47.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ब्राह्मणश्च a brahmin, अतिथिश्च also a guest, अयम् this man, अनुक्तः unanswered, माम् me, शपेत हि may curse, इति thus, मुहूर्तम् for a moment, ध्यात्वा thought, सीता Sita, वचनम् these words, अब्रवीत् said.

#### Explanation

'He is a brahmin and also a guest. If unanswered he may curse me'. Thinking this Sita thought for a moment and said:

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

दुहिता जनकस्याहं मैथिलस्य महात्मनः। सीता नाम्नास्मि भद्रं ते रामभार्या द्विजोत्तम।।3.47.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

द्विजोत्तम O best of brahmin, अहम् I am, मैथिलस्य of the king of Maithila, महात्मनः of the great king, जनकस्य Janaka's, दुहिता daughter, नाम्ना by name, सीता अस्मि Sita, रामभार्या wife of Rama, ते to you, भद्रम् be pleased.

#### Explanation

O best of brahmins be pleased to know I am Sita by name, daughter of the great Janaka, king of Mithila and wife of Rama.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

उषित्वा द्वादश समा इक्ष्वाकुणां निवेशने। भुञ्जाना मानुषान्भोगान्सर्वकामसमृद्धिनी।।3.47.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

द्वादश twelve, समाः years, इक्ष्वाकूणाम् of the Ikshvaku family, निवेशने in the house, उषित्वा  lived, मानुषान् of mortals, भोगान् pleasures, भुञ्जाना enjoying, सर्वकामसमृद्धिनी all my desires fulfilled.

#### Explanation

I lived in the house of the Ikshvakus for twelve years and enjoyed all kinds of pleasures meant for mortals.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

ततस्त्रयोदशे वर्षे राजामन्त्रयत प्रभुः। अभिषेचयितुं रामं समेतो राजमन्त्रिभिः।।3.47.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, प्रभुः राजा king and lord, त्रयोदशे वर्षे in the thirteenth year, राजमन्त्रिभिः   kings and ministers, समेतः together, रामम् for Rama, अभिषेचयितुम् to consecrate, अमन्त्रयत  consulted.

#### Explanation

In the thirteenth year, the king and lord Dasaratha consulted other kings and ministers to consecrate Rama.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

तस्मिन्सम्भ्रियमाणे तु राघवस्याभिषेचने। कैकेयी नाम भर्तारमार्या सा याचते वरम्।।3.47.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राघवस्य Rama's, तस्मिन् in that, अभिषेचने consecration, सम्भ्रियमाणे  arrangements were being made, कैकेयी नाम Kaikeyi by name, सा she, भर्तारम् her husband, आर्या lady, वरम् boon, याचते sought.

#### Explanation

Arrangements for consecration were on when queen Kaikeyi sought a boon from the king.

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

प्रतिगृह्य तु कैकेयी श्वशुरं सुकृतेन मे। मम प्रव्राजनं भर्तुर्भरतस्याभिषेचनम्।।3.47.7।। द्वावयाचत भर्तारं सत्यसन्धं नृपोत्तमम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कैकेयी Kaikeyi, मे श्वशुरम् my fatherinlaw, सुकृतेन by good luck, प्रतिगृह्य  extracted a  promise ( married for a consideration), सत्यसन्धम् truthful, नृपोत्तमम् best of kings, भर्तारम् husband's, मम भर्तुः of my husband, प्रव्राजनम् banishment, भरतस्य Bharata's, अभिषेचनम् consecration, द्वौ two (boons), अयाचत asked.

#### Explanation

For the fulfilment of the promise made to Kaikeyi earlier by my truthful fatherin law, who, to my good luck, was the best of kings, she asked for two boons. One was banishment of my husband and second, consecration of Bharata.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

नाद्य भोक्ष्ये न च स्वप्स्ये न पास्येच कथञ्चन।।3.47.8।। एष मे जीवितस्यान्तो रामो यद्यभिषिच्यते।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अद्य today, रामः Rama, यदिअभिषिच्यते if consecrated, न भोक्ष्ये I will not eat, न च स्वप्स्ये I will not sleep, कथञ्चन in any way, नपास्ये च I will not drink, एषः this is, मे my, जीवितस्य life's, अन्तः an end.

#### Explanation

'I shall not eat, or sleep or even drink if Rama is consecrated and I shall put an end to my life itself' (said Kaikeyi).

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

इति ब्रुवाणां कैकेयीं श्वशुरो मे स मानदः।।3.47.9।। अयाचतार्थैरन्वर्थैर्न च याञ्चां चकार सा।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

इति thus, ब्रुवाणाम् while speaking, कैकेयीम् to Kaikeyi, मानदः respectable, सः he, मे my, श्वशुरः fatherinlaw, अन्वर्थैः अर्थैः riches, अयाचत requested, सा she, याञ्चाम् soliciting, न च चकार not agreed.

#### Explanation

The king, my fatherinlaw, who respected her offered riches. Which she did not accept.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

मम भर्तामहातेजा वयसा पञ्चविंशकः।।3.47.10।। अष्टादश हि वर्षाणि मम जन्मनि गण्यते।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महातेजाः very bold, मम भर्ता my husband, वयसा by age, पञ्चविंशकः twenty five, मम mine, जन्मनि completed after birth, अष्टादश eighteen, वर्षाणि years, गण्यते is counted.

#### Explanation

My husband who was very bold was twentyfive years and I had completed eighteen years since my birth.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

रामेति प्रथितो लोके गुणवान्सत्यवान्शुचिः।।3.47.11।। विशालाक्षो महाबाहुस्सर्वभूतहिते रतः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रामेति known as Rama, लोके in the world, प्रथितः famous, गुणवान् virtuous, सत्यवान् truthful, शुचिः pure, विशालाक्षः  large eyed, महाबाहुः  largearmed, सर्वभूतहिते in the wellbeing of all, रतः engaged.

#### Explanation

My husband known in the world as Rama is largeeyed, longarmed, virtuous, truthful, pure and is always engaged in the welfare of all beings.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

कामार्तस्तु महातेजाः पिता दशरथस्स्वयम्।।3.47.12।। कैकेय्याः प्रियकामार्थं तं रामं नाभ्यषेचयत्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कामार्तः overcome by love, महातेजाः brilliant, पिता father, दशरथः Dasaratha, स्वयम् himself, कैकेय्याः Kaikeyi, प्रियकामार्थम् to please her, तं रामम् Rama, नाभ्यषेचयत् not consecrated.

#### Explanation

The brilliant king, Dasaratha, father of Rama, overcome by passion,  did not consecrate Rama in order to please Kaikeyi.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

अभिषेकाय तु पितुस्समीपं राममागतम्।।3.47.13।। कैकेयी मम भर्तारमित्युवाच धृतं वचः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अभिषेकाय for consecration, तु but, समीपम् near, आगतम् reached, मम भर्तारम् to my husband, रामम् Rama, इति धृतम् in unhesitating  वचः words, उवाच said.

#### Explanation

When my husband Rama approached his father for the consecration she said these unhesitating words:

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

तव पित्रा समाज्ञप्तं ममेदं शृणु राघव।।3.47.14।। भरताय प्रदातव्यमिदं राज्यमकण्टकम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राघव O Rama, तव your, पित्रा by father, समाज्ञप्तम् is ordered, इदम् this way, शृणु listen, अकण्टकम् without any obstacles, इदं राज्यम् this kingdom, भरताय to Bharata, प्रदातव्यम् should be bestowed.

#### Explanation

'O Rama, listen, your father has ordered that this kingdom be bestowed on Bharata without any obstacles.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

त्वया हि खलु वस्तव्यं नव वर्षाणि पञ्च च।।3.47.15।। वने प्रव्रज काकुत्स्थ पितरं मोचयानृतान्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

काकुत्स्थ Rama of the Kakutstha dynasty, त्वया by you, नव पञ्च च वर्षाणि fourteen years, वने in the forest, वस्तव्यम् खलु you should live indeed, प्रव्रज  proceed in exile, पितरम् to father, अनृतात् from falsehood, मोचय save him.

#### Explanation

'O Rama of the Kakutstha dynasty, you should live in the forest for fourteen years and save your father from falsehood'.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

तथेत्युक्त्वा च तां रामः कैकेयीमकुतोभयः।।3.47.16।। चकार तद्वचस्तस्या मम भर्ता दृढव्रतः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अकुतोभयः a fearless man, दृढव्रतः of firm resolution, मम भर्ता my husband, रामः Rama, तथेति be it so, ताम् her, कैकेयीम् to Kaikeyi, उक्त्वा said, तस्याः her, तत् those, वचः words, चकार executed.

#### Explanation

My husband Rama, a man of fearlessness and firm resolution, said to Kaikeyi 'Be it so' and carried out her words.

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

दद्यान्न प्रतिगृह्णीयात्सत्यं ब्रूयान्न चानृतम्।।3.47.17।। एतद्ब्राह्मण रामस्य ध्रृवं व्रतमनुत्तमम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

दद्यात् giver, न प्रतिगृह्णीयात् not receive, सत्यम् truth, ब्रूयात् utter, अनृतम् other than truth, न च not utter, ब्राह्मण brahmin, एतत् this is, रामस्य Rama's, धृवम् resolve, अनुत्तमम् very great, व्रतम् vow.

#### Explanation

O brahmin this is his firm resolve and his great vow: 'One should give and never take one should speak the truth and nothing but he truth.'

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

तस्य भ्राता तु द्वैमात्रो लक्ष्मणो नाम वीर्यवान्।।3.47.18।। रामस्य पुरुषव्याघ्रस्सहायस्समरेरिहा।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तस्य रामस्य such Rama's, द्वैमात्रः भ्राता second mother's son, half brother, वीर्यवान् strong, पुरुषव्याघ्रः tiger among men, अरिहा slayer of enemies, लक्ष्मणो नाम by name Lakshmana, समरे in battle, सहाय: companion.

#### Explanation

Son to the second mother of Rama, his mighty halfbrother, by name, Lakshmana, a tiger among men and a  slayer of enemies in battle is a companion to him.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

स भ्राता लक्ष्मणो नाम धर्मचारी दृढव्रतः।।3.47.19।। अन्वगच्छद्दनुष्पाणिः प्रव्रजन्तं मया सह।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

धर्मचारी righteous, दृढव्रतः steadfast, लक्ष्मणो नाम Lakshmana by name, सः भ्राता his brother, धनुष्पाणिः wielder of bow, मया सह along with me, प्रव्रजन्तम् as he was being exiled, अन्वगच्छत् came along.

#### Explanation

Lakshmana, his brother, is righteous and steadfast. Bow in hand, he followed Rama along with me, into exile.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

जटी तापसरूपेण मया सह सहानुजः।।3.47.20।। प्रविष्टो दण्डकारण्यं धर्मनित्यो जितेन्द्रियः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

धर्मनित्यः ever righteous, जितेन्द्रियः selfcontrolled, जटी with matted locks, मया सह along with me, सहानुजः with his brother, दण्डकारण्यम् Dandaka forest, प्रविष्टः entered.

#### Explanation

Rama who is ever righteous and selfcontrolled entered the Dandaka forest with matted locks, his brother and me.

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

ते वयं प्रच्युता राज्यात्कैकेय्यास्तु कृते त्रयः।।3.47.21।। विचरामो द्विजश्रेष्ठ वनं गम्भीरमोजसा।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

द्विजश्रेष्ठ O best among brahmins, ते those of us, वयम् we, त्रयः three of us, कैकेय्याः कृते on account of Kaikeyi, राज्यात् from kingdom, प्रच्युताः dislodged, ओजसा  with valour, गम्भीरम् deep, वनम् forest, विचरामः wandering.

#### Explanation

O best of brahmins three of us have been wandering with courage in the deep forest, dislodged from  the kingdom on account of  Kaikeyi.

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

समाश्वस मुहूर्तं तु शक्यं वस्तुमिह त्वया।।3.47.22।। आगमिष्यति मे भर्ता वन्यमादाय पुष्कलम्। रुरून्गोधा न्वराहांश्च हत्वाऽदायाऽमिषान्बहून्।।3.47.23।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मुहूर्तम् for a while, समाश्वस take rest, त्वया by you, इह here, वस्तुम् to stay, शक्यम् it is possible, मे भर्ता my husband, रुरून् deer, गोधाः alligators, वराहांश्च boars, हत्वा after killing, बहून् many of them, आमिषान् varieties of meat, आदाय would fetch, पुष्कलम् in plenty, वन्यम् from the forest, आदाय getting, आगमिष्यति will come.

#### Explanation

Rest here awhile. My husband will return with plenty of meat of many kinds from the forest, killing deer, alligators and wild boars.

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

समाश्वस मुहूर्तं तु शक्यं वस्तुमिह त्वया।।3.47.22।। आगमिष्यति मे भर्ता वन्यमादाय पुष्कलम्। रुरून्गोधा न्वराहांश्च हत्वाऽदायाऽमिषान्बहून्।।3.47.23।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मुहूर्तम् for a while, समाश्वस take rest, त्वया by you, इह here, वस्तुम् to stay, शक्यम् it is possible, मे भर्ता my husband, रुरून् deer, गोधाः alligators, वराहांश्च boars, हत्वा after killing, बहून् many of them, आमिषान् varieties of meat, आदाय would fetch, पुष्कलम् in plenty, वन्यम् from the forest, आदाय getting, आगमिष्यति will come.

#### Explanation

Rest here awhile. My husband will return with plenty of meat of many kinds from the forest, killing deer, alligators and wild boars.

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

स त्वं नाम च गोत्रञ्च कुलं चाचक्ष्व तत्त्वतः। एकश्च दण्डकारण्ये किमर्थं चरसि द्विज।।3.47.24।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

द्विज O brahmin, सः such as you, त्वम् you, नाम च name and, गोत्रञ्च gotram, कुलं च race, तत्त्वतः truly, आचक्ष्व tell me, एकः च alone, दण्डकारण्ये in Dandaka forest, किमर्थम् for what purpose, चरसि going about.

#### Explanation

O brahmin, tell me your name, your gotra and your pedigree. And the purpose for which you are going about all alone in this Dandaka forest

---

### Shloka 25

#### Shloka Text

एवं बृवन्त्यां सीतायां रामपत्न्यां महाबलः। प्रत्युवाचोत्तरं तीव्रं रावणो राक्षसाधिपः।।3.47.25।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रामपत्न्याम् when Rama's wife, सीतायाम् Sita, एवम् in that way, ब्रुवन्त्याम् speaking, महाबलः   powerful, राक्षसाधिपः king of demons, रावणः Ravana, तीव्रम् sharp, उत्तरम् reply, प्रत्युवाच replied.

#### Explanation

To these words of Sita, Rama's wife, Ravana, the powerful king of demons, replied  sharp:

---

### Shloka 26

#### Shloka Text

येन वित्रासिता लोकास्सदेवासुरपन्नगाः। अहं तु रावणो नाम सीते रक्षोगणेश्वरः।।3.47.26।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सीते O Sita, अहम् I am, येन by whom, सदेवासुरपन्नगाः asuras and pannagas along with gods, लोकाः worlds, वित्रासिताः are frightened, रक्षोगणेश्वरः lord of demons, रावणो नाम Ravana by name.

#### Explanation

O Sita I am called Ravana, the lord of demons by whom all the worlds of demons, snakes and gods are frightened.

---

### Shloka 27

#### Shloka Text

त्वां तु काञ्चनवर्णाभां दृष्ट्वा कौशेयवासिनीम्। रतिं स्वकेषु दारेषु नाधिगच्छाम्यनिन्दिते।।3.47.27।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अनिन्दिते O flawless lady, काञ्चनवर्णाभाम् of golden complexion, कौशेयवासिनीम् clad in silk, त्वाम् you, दृष्ट्वा after seeing, स्वकेषु my own, दारेषु wives, रतिम् love, नाधिगच्छामि do not feel.

#### Explanation

O flawless beauty of golden complexion seeing you clad in silk, I am not inclined to show any interest in my own wives.

---

### Shloka 28

#### Shloka Text

बह्वीनामुत्तमस्त्रीणामाहृतानामितस्ततः। सर्वासामेव भद्रं ते ममाग्रमहिषी भव।।3.47.28।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

इतस्तत: from various places, आहृतानाम् won over, बह्वीनाम् of many women, सर्वासामेव of all of them, उत्तमस्त्रीणाम् of the best of women, मम myself, अग्रमहिषी chief queen, भव be, ते भद्रम् be blessed.

#### Explanation

You will be the chief queen among the best of women I have won in wars. Be blessed.

---

### Shloka 29

#### Shloka Text

लङ्कानाम समुद्रस्य मम मध्ये महापुरी। सागरेण परिक्षिस्ता निविष्टा नगमूर्धनि।।3.47.29।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लङ्का नाम by name Lanka, मम my, महापुरी great city, समुद्रस्य of the sea, मध्ये in the  middle, सागरेण by sea, परिक्षिप्ता surrounded, नगमूर्धनि on the peak of a mountain, निविष्टा it is situated.

#### Explanation

My great city  called Lanka is on the peak of a mountain surrounded by, and in the middle of, the sea.

---

### Shloka 30

#### Shloka Text

तत्र सीते मया सार्धं वनेषु विहरिष्यसि। न चास्यारण्यवासस्य स्पृहयिष्यसि भामिनि।।3.47.30।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सीते O Sita, तत्र there, मया सार्धम् with me, वनेषु in the gardens, विहरिष्यसि can stroll with pleasure, भामिनि O beautiful woman, अस्य of this, अरण्यस्य वासस्य living in the forest, न च स्पृहयिष्यसि will not like.

#### Explanation

O Sita, you can stroll with me in my pleasuregardens. O  beautiful lady, you will not like living in the forest (thereafter).

---

### Shloka 31

#### Shloka Text

पञ्च दास्यस्सहस्राणि सर्वाभरणभूषिताः। सीते परिचरिष्यन्ति भार्या भवसि मे यदि।।3.47.31।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सीते Sita, मे my, भार्या wife, भवसि यदि if you become so, सर्वाभरणभूषिताः adorned with all kinds of ornaments, सहस्राणि thousands, पञ्च five, दास्यः female attendants, परिचरिष्यन्ति they will serve you.

#### Explanation

O Sita  if you become my wife, five thousand female attendants adorned with all ornaments will attend on you.

---

### Shloka 32

#### Shloka Text

रावणेनैवमुक्ता तु कुपिता जनकात्मजा। प्रत्युवाचानवद्याङ्गी तमनादृत्य राक्षसम्।।3.47.32।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रावणेन by Ravana, एवम् like that, उक्ता having been told, अनवद्याङ्गी a lady of blemishless limbs, जनकात्मजा Janaka's daughter(Janaki), कुपिता in rage, तम् that, राक्षसम् demon, अनादृत्य without caring, प्रत्युवाच replied.

#### Explanation

Thus addressed by Ravana, Janaki, a lady of blemishless limbs, got enraged and replied without caring for the demon:

---

### Shloka 33

#### Shloka Text

महागिरिमिवाकम्प्यं महेन्द्रसदृशं पतिम्। महोदधिमिवाक्षोभ्यमहं राममनुव्रता।।3.47.33।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महागिरिमिव like a huge mountain, अकम्प्यम् unshakeable, महेन्द्रसदृशम् equal to great Indra, महोदधिमिव like a mighty sea, अक्षोभ्यम् imperturbable, पतिम् husband, रामम् Rama, अहम् I, अनुव्रता faithful follower.

#### Explanation

I am devoted to Rama, my husband, who is unshakeable like a huge mountain, is comparable to lord Indra and is imperturbable like the mighty ocean.

---

### Shloka 34

#### Shloka Text

सर्वलक्षणसम्पन्नं न्यग्रोधपरिमण्डलम्। सत्यसन्धं महाभागमहं राममनुव्रता।।3.47.34।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अहम् I am, सर्वलक्षणसम्पन्नम् endowed with all excellences, न्यग्रोधपरिमण्डलम् a refuge like a spreading banyan tree, सत्यसन्धम् devoted to truth, महाभागम् venerable, रामम् Rama, अनुव्रता a loyal wife.

#### Explanation

I am the loyal wife of venerable Rama who is endowed with all excellences, a refuge to all like  a spreading banyan tree, and a votary of truth.

---

### Shloka 35

#### Shloka Text

महाबाहुं महोरस्कं सिंहविक्रान्तगामिनम्। नृसिंहं सिंहसङ्काशमहं राममनुव्रता।।3.47.35।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अहम् I am, महाबाहुम् longarmed hero, महोरस्कम् broadchested, सिंहविक्रान्तगामिनम् takes strides like an advancing lion, नृसिंहम् like a lion among men, सिंहसङ्काशम् invincible  like lion, रामम् Rama, अनुव्रता faithful wife.

#### Explanation

I am the faithful wife of the longarmed, broadchested  Rama,  a lion among men. His gait is the gait of an advancing, invincible lion.

---

### Shloka 36

#### Shloka Text

पूर्णचन्द्राननं रामं राजवत्सं जितेन्द्रियम्। पृथुकीर्तिं महात्मानमहं राममनुव्रता।।3.47.36।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अहम् I am, पुर्णचन्द्राननम् face like the full Moon, राजवत्सम् son of a king, जितेन्द्रियम्  who has conquered his senses, पृथुकीर्तिम् whose fame is widespread, महात्मानम् great self, रामम् Rama, अनुव्रता devoted wife.

#### Explanation

I am the devoted wife of great Rama, whose face is like a fullmoon, who is the son of a king, who has conquered his senses and whose fame is widespread (on earth).

---

### Shloka 37

#### Shloka Text

त्वं पुनर्जम्बुकस्सिंहीं मामिच्छसि सुदुर्लभाम्। नाहं शक्या त्वया स्प्रष्टुमादित्यस्य प्रभा यथा।।3.47.37।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

जम्बुकः jackal, त्वं पुनः you are, सुदुर्लभाम् most difficult to have, सिंहीम् a lioness, माम् me, इच्छसि you are desiring, आदित्यस्य like the Sun's, प्रभा यथा radiance, अहम् I am, त्वया by you, स्प्रष्टुम् even to touch, न शक्या not possible.

#### Explanation

You are a jackal, and you want a lioness like me who is difficult to win. I am like the Sun's radiance whom you cannot even touch.

---

### Shloka 38

#### Shloka Text

पादपान्काञ्चनान्नूनं बहून्पश्यसि मन्दभाक्। राघपस्य प्रियां भार्यां यस्त्वमिच्छसि रावण।।3.47.38।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रावण O Ravana, यः one who, त्वम् you, राघवस्य Rama's, प्रियाम् dear, भार्याम् wife, इच्छसि you want, मन्दभाक् unfortunate, नूनम् certainly, काञ्चनान् golden, पादपान् trees, पश्यसि you will see.

#### Explanation

O luckless Ravana  you want the beloved wife of Rama.You will, for sure, see golden trees. (which those close to death do).

---

### Shloka 39

#### Shloka Text

क्षुधितस्य हि सिंहस्य मृगशत्रोस्तरस्विनः। आशीविषस्य मुखाद्दंष्ट्रामादातुमिच्छसि।।3.47.39।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मृगशत्रोः enemy of the deer, तरस्विनः of the powerful, क्षुधितस्य of a hungry, सिंहस्य lion's, आशीविषस्य of a poisonous snake, मुखात् from the mouth, दंष्ट्राम् teeth, आदातुम् to seize, इच्छसि intending.

#### Explanation

You are seeking to pull the teeth from  the mouth of a hungry and powerful lion who is considered the enemy of the deer(which you are). You intend to pull the fang of a poisonous snake.

---

### Shloka 40

#### Shloka Text

मन्दरं पर्वतश्रेष्ठं पाणिना हर्तुमिच्छसि। कालकूटं विषं पीत्वा स्वस्तिमान्गन्तुमिच्छसि।।3.47.40।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पर्वतश्रेष्ठम् greatest of the mountains, मन्दरम् Mandara, पाणिना with a hand, हर्तुम् to carry, इच्छसि want, कालकूटं विषम् deadly poison (produced from the churning of the ocean), पीत्वा after drinking, स्वस्तिमान् safely, गन्तुम् to go, इच्छसि wishing.

#### Explanation

You are trying to carry the greatest of mountains, the Mandara, with one hand. You are wishing to go safe after drinking deadly poison (produced from the churning of the ocean).

---

### Shloka 41

#### Shloka Text

अक्षि सूच्या प्रमृजसि जिह्वया लेक्षि च क्षुरम्। राघवस्य प्रियां भार्यां योऽधिगन्तुं त्वमिच्छसि।।3.47.41।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

यः he who, त्वम् you, राघवस्य प्रियाम् dear to Rama, भार्याम् wife, अधिगन्तुम् violate, इच्छसि desiring to, सूच्या by needle, अक्षि eyes, प्रमृजसि rubbing, जिह्वया by your tongue, क्षुरम् razor, लेक्षि licking.

#### Explanation

You wish to violate the loving wife of Rama. You are rubbing your eyes with a needle and licking the razor with your tongue.

---

### Shloka 42

#### Shloka Text

अवसज्य शिलां कण्ठे समुद्रं तर्तुमिच्छसि। सूर्याचन्द्रमसौ चोभौ पाणिभ्यां हर्तुमिच्छसि।।3.47.42।। यो रामस्य प्रियां भार्यां प्रधर्षयितुमिच्छसि।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

यः he who, रामस्य Rama's, प्रियाम् very dear, भार्याम् wife, प्रधर्षयितुम् assault, इच्छसि desiring, कण्ठे in the neck, शिलाम् stone, अवसज्य after girding round the neck, समुद्रम् sea, तर्तुम् cross, इच्छसि desiring, सूर्याचन्द्रमसौ Sun and Moon, उभौ both, पाणिभ्याम् with both hands, हर्तुम् to carry, इच्छसि desiring,

#### Explanation

You want to assault Rama's dear wife. It is like girding a stone round the neck and trying to cross the sea.It is like intending to take the Sun and the Moon with both your hands.

---

### Shloka 43

#### Shloka Text

अग्निं प्रज्वलितं दृष्ट्वा वस्त्रेणाहर्तुमिच्छसि।।3.47.43।। काल्याणवृत्तां रामस्य यो भार्यांहर्तुमिच्छसि।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

यः one who, रामस्य Rama's, कल्याणवृत्ताम् a woman of virtuous conduct, भार्याम् wife, हर्तुम् to seize, इच्छसि you intend to, प्रज्वलितम् blazing, अग्निम् fire, दृष्ट्वा after seeing that, वस्त्रेण by cloth, आहर्तुम् to hold, इच्छसि desiring.

#### Explanation

You intend to assaut the wife of Rama, who is a lady of virtuous conduct. You wish to hold blazing fire in your cloth.

---

### Shloka 44

#### Shloka Text

अयोमुखानां शूलानामग्रे चरितुमिच्छसि।।3.47.44।। रामस्य सदृशीं भार्यां योऽधिगन्तुं त्वमिच्छसि।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

यः one who, त्वम् you, रामस्य Rama's, सदृशीम् his another worthy form, भार्याम् wife, अधिगन्तुम् to acquire, इच्छसि wish, अयोमुखानाम् sharp ironheaded, शूलानाम् spears, अग्रे on the top, चरितुम् to walk on, इच्छसि want.

#### Explanation

You want to acquire Rama's worthy wife. You wish to walk on sharp ironheaded spear.

---

### Shloka 45

#### Shloka Text

यदन्तरं सिंहशृगालयोर्वने यदन्तरं स्यन्दिनिका समुद्रयोः। सुराग्र्य सौवीरकयोर्यदन्तरं तदन्तरं वै तव राघवस्य च।।3.47.45।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वने in the forest, सिंहशृगालयोः between a lion and a jackal, यत् whatever, अन्तरम् difference, स्यन्दिनिकासमुद्रयोः between a ditch and a sea, यत् that, अन्तरम् difference, सुराग्र्य सौवीरकयोः best of wines and sour gruel, यत् whatever, अन्तरम् difference, तव your, राघवस्य च and Rama, तत् there, अन्तरम् difference.

#### Explanation

The difference between you and Rama is the difference between a jackal and a lion in the forest, between a ditch and the sea, and between sour gruel and the best of wines.

---

### Shloka 46

#### Shloka Text

यदन्तरं काञ्चनसीसलोहयोर्यदन्तरं चन्दनवारिपङ्कयोः। यदन्तरं हस्तिबिडालयोर्वने तदन्तरं दाशरथेस्तवैव च।।3.47.46।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

काञ्चनसीसलोहयोः between gold and lead, यत् whatever, अन्तरम् difference, चन्दनवारिपङ्कयोः between sandal and slime, यत् whatever, अन्तरम् difference, हस्तिबिडालयोर्वने between the elephant and the cat in the forest, दाशरथेः of Dasaratha's son, तवैव च and yours, तत् that, अन्तरम् difference.

#### Explanation

The difference between you and Rama is the difference  between gold and lead, sandal and slime, an elephant and a cat of the forest.

---

### Shloka 47

#### Shloka Text

यदन्तरं वायसवैनतेययोर्यदन्तरं मद्गुमयूरयोरपि। यदन्तरं सारसगृध्रयोर्वने तदन्तरं दाशरथेस्तवैव च।।3.47.47।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वायसवैनतेययोः between a crow and and Garuda, यत् whatever, अन्तरम् difference, मद्गुमयूरयोरपि beween a watercrane and a peacock, यत् whatever, अन्तरम् difference, वने in the forest, सारसगृध्रयोः between a vulture and a swan, यत् that amount, अन्तरम् difference, दाशरथेः Dasaratha's son, तवैव च and you, तत् that, अन्तरम् difference.

#### Explanation

The difference between you and Dasaratha's son is the difference between a crow and Garuda, a watercrane and a peacock, a vulture and a swan in the forest.

---

### Shloka 48

#### Shloka Text

तस्मिन्सहस्राक्षसमप्रभावे रामे स्थिते कार्मुकबाणपाणौ। हृतापि तेहं न जरां गमिष्ये वज्रं यथा मक्षिकयावगीर्णम्।।3.47.48।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

हृतापि even if I am abducted, अहम् I, सहस्राक्षसमप्रभावे a man equal in prowess to the thousandeyed Indra, कार्मुक बाणपाणौ armed with bow and arrows, तस्मिन् when he, रामे while Rama, स्थिते when he stands, मक्षिकया by fly, अवगीर्णम् gulped (presuming it to be a grain of  rice), वज्रं यथा like a diamond, ते to you, जरां न गमिष्ये will not be withered.

#### Explanation

So long Rama, equal in prowess to the thousandeyed Indra stands with bow and arrows in hand, I will not, although abducted, wither away like a diamond gulped by an insect (fly).

---

### Shloka 49

#### Shloka Text

इतीव तद्वाक्यमदुष्टभावा सुदुष्टमुक्त्वा रजनीचरं तम्। गात्रप्रकम्पाद्व्यथिता बभूव वातोद्धता सा कदलीव तन्वी।।3.47.49।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अदुष्टभावा pure in thought, तन्वी slim, सा that, सुदुष्टम् forcefully, तं रजनीचरम् nightwalker, तत् that, वाक्यम् words, इति thus, उक्त्वा said, गात्रप्रकम्पात् due to trembling of the body, वातोद्धता shaken by violent wind, कदलीव banana, व्यथिता distressed, बभूव became.

#### Explanation

Having said thus to the wicked nightwalker Ravana, Sita, pure in thought remained  distressed.Her slender body started trembling like a banana plant becomes shaken by a violent wind.

---

### Shloka 50

#### Shloka Text

तां वेपमानामुपलक्ष्य सीतां स रावणो मृत्युसमप्रभावः। कुलं बलं नाम च कर्म चात्मनः समाचचक्षे भयकारणार्थम्।।3.47.50।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मृत्युसमप्रभावः powerful like death, सः that, रावणः Ravana, वेपमानाम् trembling, ताम् she, उपलक्ष्य beholding, भयकारणार्थम् to create more fear, आत्मनः of himself, कुलम् clan, बलं च and strength, कर्म च deeds, समाचचक्षे gave an account of.

#### Explanation

Beholding Sita who was trembling, Ravana, powerful like death, started telling about himself, his clan, his strength and his deeds to instil more fear in her. इत्तयार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये अरण्यकाण्डे सप्तचत्वारिंशस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the fortyseventh sarga of Aranyakanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 48

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

एवं बृवन्त्यां सीतायां संरब्दः परुषं वचः। ललाटे भृकुटीं कृत्वा रावणः प्रत्युवाच ह।।3.48.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सीतायाम् when Sita, एवम् in that way, बृवन्त्याम् as she spoke, रावणः Ravana, संरब्धः roused to anger, ललाटे forehead, भृकुटीम् knitted eyebrows, कृत्वा doing, परुषम् harsh, वचः words, प्रत्युवाच replied.

#### Explanation

Addressed thus by Sita, Ravana, his anger roused, knitted his eyebrows with a frown on his forehead and replied to her harshly:

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

भ्राता वैश्रवणस्याहं सापत्न्यो वरवर्णिनि। रावणो नाम भद्रं ते दशग्रीवः प्रतापवान्।।3.48.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वरवर्णिनि most beautiful woman, अहम् I, वैश्रवणस्य of Kubera's, सापत्न्यः stepmother's son, भ्राता brother, दशग्रीवः ten headed, प्रतापवान् powerful, रावणो नाम called Ravana, ते to you, भद्रम् be pleased.

#### Explanation

O paragon of beauty  I am the halfbrother of Kubera, born of his stepmother. I am known as the tenheaded, powerful Ravana. Be blessed

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

यस्य देवास्सगन्धर्वाः पिशाचपतगोरगाः। विद्रवन्ति भयाद्भीता मृत्योरिव सदा प्रजाः।।3.48.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

प्रजाः people, मृत्योरिव like death, यस्य whose, भयात् out of fear, भीताः scared of, सगन्धर्वाः including gandharvas, देवाः gods, पिशाचपतगोरगा: evil spirits, birds and serpents, विद्रवन्ति run away.

#### Explanation

Just as people get scared of death, all (creatures) including gandharvas, gods, evil spirits, birds and snakes run away from me.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

येन वैश्रवणो राजा द्वैमात्रः कारणान्तरे। द्वन्द्वमासादितः क्रोधाद्रणे विक्रम्य निर्जितः।।3.48.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

येन by whom, कारणान्तरे for some other reason, क्रोधात् in anger, द्वन्द्वम् duel, आसादितः took place, द्वैमात्रः stepmother, वैश्रवणः राजा king Vaisravana, रणे in war, विक्रम्य with my prowess, निर्जितः was defeated.

#### Explanation

For some reason or other a duel took place between me and king Kubera. With my prowess, I defeated him in the duel.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

यद्भयार्तः परित्यज्य स्वमधिष्ठानमृद्धिमत्। कैलासं पर्वतश्रेष्ठमध्यास्ते नरवाहनः।।3.48.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

नरवाहनः Kubera (having a human being as his carrier), यद्भयार्तः due to fear of whom, ऋद्धिमत् prosperous, स्वम् his own, अधिष्ठानम् place of residence, परित्यज्य giving up, पर्वतश्रेष्ठम् mighty mountain, कैलासम् Kailasa, अध्यास्ते  residing.

#### Explanation

Out of fear for me, Kubera is residing on the mighty mount Kailasa, leaving his own earlier prosperous place of residence.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

यस्य तत्पुष्पकं नाम विमानं कामगं शुभम्। वीर्यादेवार्जितं भद्रे येन यामि विहायसम्।।3.48.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

भद्रे O noble lady, यस्य whose, तत् that, पुष्पकं नाम by name Pushpaka, कामगम् goes where one desires, शुभम् auspicious, विमानम् chariot, वीर्यादेव by valour alone, आर्जितम् gained over, येन in which, विहायसम् sky, यामि I move about.

#### Explanation

O noble lady  with my valour I gained over from Kubera the aerial chariot 'Pushpaka', which can go wherever one desires and in which I move about in the sky.

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

मम सञ्जातरोषस्य मुखं दृष्ट्वैव मैथिलि। विद्रवन्ति परित्रस्तास्सुराश्शक्रपुरोगमाः।।3.48.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मैथिलि O Maithili, सञ्जातरोषस्य when I get angry, मम my, मुखम् face, दृष्ट्वैव on seeing alone, परित्रस्ताः frightened , शक्रपुरोगमाः led by Indra, सुराः gods, विद्रवन्ति run away.

#### Explanation

O Princess from Mithila, seeing my angry face  even gods led by Indra take to their heels in fear.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

यत्र तिष्ठाम्यहं तत्र मारुतो वाति शङ्कितः। तीव्रांशुश्शिशिरांशुश्च भयात्सम्पद्यते रविः।।3.48.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अहम् I am, यत्र wherever, तिष्ठामि stay, तत्र there, मारुतः the Windgod, शङ्कितः hesitantly, वाति blows, तीव्रांशुः the Sun with scorching rays, रविः च even Sun, भयात् in fear, शिशिरांशुः Moon of cool radiance, सम्पद्यते discharges duties.

#### Explanation

Wherever I am, the Windgod blows with hesitation. So are the Sun and the Moon afraid of me. Afraid of me the Sun duly discharges his duties.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

निष्कम्पपत्रास्तरवो नद्यश्च स्तिमितोदकाः। भवन्ति यत्र यत्राहं तिष्ठामि विचरामि च।।3.48.9।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अहम् I am, यत्र यत्र wherever, तिष्ठामि stay, विचरामि move about, उदकाः water, निष्कम्पपत्राः  with their leaves, तरवः trees, नद्यश्च the rivers also, स्तिमितोदकाः have still waters, भवन्ति   remain.

#### Explanation

Wherever I stay or move about, the leaves of trees do not shake, and the waters in rivers remain still.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

मम पारे समुद्रस्य लङ्का नाम पुरी शुभा। सम्पूर्णा राक्षसैर्घोरैर्यथेन्द्रस्यामरावती।।3.48.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation
