घोरैः by the terrific, राक्षसैः by demons, सम्पूर्णा filled with, लङ्का नाम known as Lanka, शुभा auspicious, इंद्रस्य Indra's, अमरावतीव like Amaravati, मम my, पुरी city, समुद्रस्य on these, पारे shore.

#### Explanation

My city known as Lanka, teeming with terrific demons, is on the other side of the sea. It is  beautiful like the city of Indra's Amaravati.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

प्राकारेण परिक्षिप्ता पाण्डुरेण विराजता। हेमकक्ष्या पुरी रम्या वैढूर्यमयतोरणा।।3.48.11।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रम्या beautiful, पाण्डुरेण pale white, विराजता  shining , प्राकारेण by a ramparts, परिक्षिप्ता surrounded, हेमकक्ष्या interiors built of gold, वैढूर्यमयतोरणा entrance gates encrusted with vaidurya (cat's eye).

#### Explanation

The beautiful city of Lanka  shines with its pale white ramparts and golden interiors. The entrance gates are encrusted with vaidurya (cat'seye).

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

हस्त्यश्वरथसम्बाधा तूर्यनादविनादिता। सर्वकालफलैर्वृक्षैस्सङ्कुलोद्द्यानशोभिता।।3.48.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

हस्त्यश्वरथसम्बाधा crowded with elephants, horses, and chariots, तूर्यनादविनादिता resounding with trumpets, सर्वकालफलैः yielding fruits in all seasons, वृक्षैः by  trees, सङ्कुलोद्यानशोभिता looks splendid with delightful pleasuregardens.

#### Explanation

It is crowded with elephants, horses and chariots. It resounds with the music of trumpets. The trees in the delightful pleasuregardens yield fruits in all seasons.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

तत्र त्वं वसती सीते राजपुत्रि मया सह। न स्मरिष्यसि नारीणां मानुषीणां मनस्विनि।।3.48.13।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राजपुत्रि princess, मनस्विनि highminded, सीते Sita, तत्र there, मया सह along with me, वसती  while residing, मानुषीणाम् of human beings, नारीणाम् of women, न स्मरिष्यसि you will not think of.

#### Explanation

O highminded princess, O Sita while living with me, you will not even think of other women.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

भुञ्जाना मानुषान्भोगान्दिव्यांश्च वरवर्णिनि। न स्मरिष्यसि रामस्य मानुषस्य गतायुषः।।3.48.14।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वरवर्णिनि blessed lady of excellant complexion, मानुषान् mortals, भोगान् pleasures, दिव्यांश्च  wonderful as well as divine, भुञ्जाना enjoying, मानुषस्य mortals, गतायुषः of a short lifespan, रामस्य Rama's, न स्मरिष्यिसि not think of.

#### Explanation

O blessed lady of fine complexion, while enjoying both human and heavenly  pleasures with me there, you will not remember Rama who is a mortal of short lifespan.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

स्थापयित्वा प्रियं पुत्रं राज्ञा दशरथेन यः। मन्दवीर्यस्सुतो ज्येष्ठस्ततः प्रस्थापितो वनम्।।3.48.15।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राज्ञा by the king, दशरथेन Dasaratha's, प्रियम् dear, पुत्रम्  son (Bharata), स्थापयित्वा consecrating, ततः then, मन्दवीर्यः weakling, ज्येष्ठः सुतः eldest son, यः one who is, वनम् forest, प्रस्थापितः sent away.

#### Explanation

Considering the eldest son a weakling, king Dasaratha has sent him away to the forest so that he could consecrate his dear son(Bharata).

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

तेन किं भ्रष्टराज्येन रामेण गतचेतसा। करिष्यसि विशालाक्षि तापसेन तपस्विना।।3.48.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

विशालाक्षि O largeeyed, भ्रष्टराज्येन  dislodged from the kingdom, गतचेतसा lost the capacity to think, तापसेन ascetic, तपस्विना by a pitiable man, तेन रामेण such Rama, किं करिष्यसि what can he do (for you) ?

#### Explanation

O largeeyed Sita dislodged from the kingdom, Rama has lost his capacity to think. He is doing penance, assuming an ascetic role. What can he do (for you)?

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

सर्वराक्षसभर्तारं कामात्स्वयमिहागतम्। न मन्मथशराविष्टं प्रत्याख्यातुं त्वमर्हसि।।3.48.17।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कामात् owing to passion, स्वयम् I myself, इह here, आगतम् came, मन्मथशराविष्टम्   overcome by the arrows of the god of love, सर्वराक्षसभर्तारम् lord of all demons, त्वम्  you, प्रत्याख्यातुम् to reject, न अर्हसि not proper.

#### Explanation

I am lord of all the demons. You should not reject me I am here (only because I am) overcome by the arrows of the god of love.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

प्रत्याख्याय हि मां भीरु परितापं गमिष्यसि। चरणेनाभिहत्येव पुरूरवसमूर्वशी।।3.48.18।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

भीरु O timid lady, माम् me, प्रत्याख्याय after you reject, पुरूरवसम् Pururava, चरणेन by the feet, अभिहत्य kicking, ऊर्वशीव like Urvasi, परितापम् regret, गमिष्यसि will you experience later.

#### Explanation

O timid lady if you reject me, you will regret like Urvasi who kicked Pururava and regretted later.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

अङ्गुल्या न समो रामो मम युद्धे स मानुषः। तव भाग्येन सम्प्राप्तं भजस्व वरवर्णिनि।।3.48.19।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वरवर्णिनि O lady of fine complexion, मानुषः a mere mortal, सः रामः that Rama, युद्धे in war, मम for me, अङ्गुल्या with my finger, समः equal, न not, तव your, भाग्येन by your luck, सम्प्राप्तम्  have I come, भजस्व take refuge.

#### Explanation

O lady of fine complexion, Rama is a (mere) mortal. In war he is no match (even) for my finger. It is your good fortune that you have got me here. Take refuge in me.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

एवमुक्ता तु वैदेही क्रुद्धा संरक्तलोचना। अब्रवीत्परुषं वाक्यं रहिते राक्षसाधिपम्।।3.48.20।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

एवम् that way, उक्ता told, वैदेही Vaidehi, क्रुद्धा enraged, संरक्तलोचना eyes turned red, रहिते in that solitary place, राक्षसाधिपम् chief of the demons, परुषम् harsh, वाक्यम् words, अब्रवीत्  said.

#### Explanation

Thus addressed, Sita was enraged and her eyes turned red. She replied in harsh words to the chief of the demons in that solitary place:.

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

कथं वैश्रवणं देवं सर्वभूतनमस्कृतम्। भ्रातरं व्यपदिश्य त्वमशुभं कर्तुमिच्छसि।।3.48.21।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सर्वभूतनमस्कृतम् worshipped by all beings, देवम् god, वैश्रवणम्  Kubera, भ्रातरम् brother, व्यपदिश्य addressing him, त्वम् you, अशुभम् inauspicious deed, कर्तुम् to do, कथम् how come, इच्छसि you desire.

#### Explanation

Why do you wish to do something inauspicious by calling Lord Kubera who is worshipped by all beings your brother ?

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

अवश्यं विनशिष्यन्ति सर्वे रावण राक्षसाः। येषां त्वं कर्कशो राजा दुर्बुद्धिरजितेन्द्रियः।।3.48.22।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रावण O Ravana, कर्कशः cruel, दुर्बुद्धि: evilminded, अजितेन्द्रियः having no control over the senses, त्वम्  you, येषाम् of whom, राजा king, सर्वे all of them, राक्षसाः demons, अवश्यम् certainly, विनशिष्यन्ति will be destroyed.

#### Explanation

O Ravana ,you are cruel and evilminded. You have no control over your senses. (And yet) you are the ruler of your breed. Surely they are going to be destroyed.

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

अपहृत्य शचीं भार्यां शक्यमिन्द्रस्य जीवितुम्। न च रामस्य भार्यां मामपनीयास्ति जीवितम्।।3.48.23।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

इन्द्रस्य Indra's, भार्याम् wife, शची Sachi, अपहृत्य after abducting, जीवितुम् to live, शक्यम् may be possible, रामस्य Rama's, भार्याम् wife, माम् me, अपनीय after carrying away, जीवितम् life, नास्ति not there.

#### Explanation

It may be possible (for some one) to live after abducting Indra's wife, Sachi, but it is not possible for any one to live after kidnapping the wife of Rama.

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

जीवेच्चिरं वज्रधरस्य हस्ताच्छचीं प्रधृष्याप्रतिरूपरूपाम्। न मादृशीं राक्षस दूशयित्वा पीतामृतस्यापि तवास्ति मोक्षः।।3.48.24।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षस demon, अप्रातिरूपरूपाम् most beautiful lady, शचीम् Sachi, वज्रधरस्य wielder of the thunderbolt, Indra's, हस्ताम् from the hands, प्रधृष्य after snatching, चिरम् long, जीवेत्  may live, मादृशीम् one like me, दूषयित्वा after assualting, पीतामृतस्यापि even after drinking  nectar, तव your, मोक्षः release from death, न not possible.

#### Explanation

O demon, you may live long after forcibly snatching the most beautiful Sachi, from the hands of Indra, the wielder of the thunderbolt, but after abusing a woman like me, you will not be released from death even if you drink nectar. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये अरण्यकाण्डे अष्टचत्वारिंशस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the fortyeighty sarga of Aranyakanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 49

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

सीताया वचनं श्रुत्वा दशग्रीवः प्रतापवाम्। हस्ते हस्तं समाहत्य चकार सुमहद्वपुः।।3.49.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

प्रतापवाम् powerful, दशग्रीवः tenheaded Ravana, सीतायाः Sita's, वचनम् words, श्रुत्वा having heard, हस्ते by the palm, हस्तम् palm, समाहत्य  striking, सुमहत् very great, वपुः body, चकार  assumed.

#### Explanation

On hearing the Sita's words, the powerful Ravana struck his palms one over the other and assumed a huge form (original figure).

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

स मैथिलीं पुनर्वाक्यं बभाषे च ततो भृशम्। नोन्मत्तया श्रुतौ मन्ये मम वीर्यपराक्रमौ।।3.49.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः Ravana, मैथिलीम् Maithili, पुनः again, वाक्यम् these words, भृशम् very much, बभाषे said, उन्मत्तया by madness, मम my, वीर्यपराक्रमौ strength and valour, न श्रुतौ not heard, मन्ये I suppose.

#### Explanation

Ravana again said to that princess from Mithila, I suppose you are too insane to disregard my strength and valour.

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

उद्वहेयं भुजाभ्यां तु मेदिनीमम्बरे स्थितः। आपिबेयं समुद्रं च हन्यां मृत्युं रणे स्थितः।।3.49.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अम्बरे in the sky, स्थितः standing, भुजाभ्याम् with my arms, मेदिनीम् the earth, उद्वहेयम् lift, समुद्रं च and oceans, आपिबेयम् can drink away, रणे in war, स्थितः standing, मृत्युम् for death, हन्याम् can cause death.

#### Explanation

Standing in the sky, I can  lift the earth with my arms.  I can drink the oceans. I can kill even the god of death in a combat.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

अर्कं ऱुन्ध्यां शरैस्तीक्ष्णैर्विभिन्द्या हि महीतलम्। कामरूपिणमुन्मत्ते पश्य मां कामदं पतिम्।।3.49.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अर्कम् Sun, ऱुन्ध्याम् can obstruct, तीक्ष्णैः with sharp, शरैः arrows, महीतलम् earth, विभिन्द्याम् I can shatter, उन्मत्ते O mad woman, कामदम् who can fulfil desires, पतिम् husband, कामरूपिणम् who can assume any form at will, माम् me, पश्य see.

#### Explanation

I can obstruct the Sun and shatter the earth with my sharp arrows. O mad woman I am a husband who can fulfil your desires, and can assume any form at my own free will. Look at me.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

एवमुक्तवतस्तस्य सूर्यकल्पे शिखिप्रभे। क्रुद्धस्य हरिपर्यन्ते रक्ते नेत्रे बभूवतुः।।3.49.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

एवम् in that manner, उक्तवतः as he spoke, क्रुद्धस्य  full of anger, तस्य his, सूर्यकल्पे almost like the Sun, शिखिप्रभे  radiance of flaming fire, हरिपर्यन्ते ambercoloured at the corners, नेत्रे eyes, रक्ते red, बभुवतुः became.

#### Explanation

As Ravana exploded in anger, his ambercoloured eyes turned red like the radiance of the flaming fire, and almost as bright as the Sun.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

सद्यस्सौम्यं परित्यज्य भिक्षुरूपं स रावणः। स्वं रूपं कालरूपाभं भेजे वैश्रवणानुजः।।3.49.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वैश्रवणानुजः younger brother of Kubera, सद्यः instantly, सौम्यम् gentle, भिक्षुरूपम् form of a  mendicant, परित्यज्य shed, स्वम् his, कालरूपाभम् the form of the god of death, रूपम् figure, भेजे assumed.

#### Explanation

Ravana, brother of Kubera, shed the gentle figure of a mendicant and assumed the form of the god of death.

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

संरक्तनयनश्श्रीमांस्तप्तकाञ्चनभूषणः। क्रोधेन महताविष्टो नीलजीमूतसन्निभः।।3.49.7।। दशास्यः कार्मुकी बाणी बभूव क्षणदाचरः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महता with intense, क्रोधेन with anger, आविष्टः overcome, संरक्तनयनः with reddened eyes, श्रीमान् shining, तप्तकाञ्चनभूषणः  glittering golden ornaments, नीलजीमूतसन्निभः looking like a dark cloud, दशास्यः the tenfaced Ravana, कार्मुकी with bow, बाणी with arrows, क्षणदाचरः nightranger, बभूव became.

#### Explanation

Overcome by intense anger, Ravana's eyes reddened. The nightranger, stood armed with bow and arrows and shining with glittering gold ornaments, appeared like a black cloud.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

स परिव्राजकच्छद्म महाकायो विहाय तत्।।3.49.8।। प्रतिपद्य स्वकं रूपं रावणो राक्षसाधिपः। संरक्तनयनः क्रोधाज्जीमूतनिचयप्रभः।।3.49.9।। रक्ताम्बरधरस्तस्थौ स्त्रीरत्नं प्रेक्ष्य मैथिलीम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षसाधिपः chief of the demons, सः रावणः that Ravana, तत् that, परिव्राजकच्छद्म figure of a mendicant, विहाय shedding, स्वकम् his real, रूपम् form, प्रतिपद्य assuming, क्रोधात् in anger, संरक्त नयनः red eyes, जीमूतनिचयप्रभः radiating like layers of clouds, रक्ताम्बरधरः wearing red garments, स्त्रीरत्नम् gem of a woman, मैथिलीम् to Sita, प्रेक्ष्य seeing, तस्थौ stood in front.

#### Explanation

Ravana, chief of the demons, shed the mendicant's form and assumed his real self. Clad in red garments, his eyes turned red in anger. Looking like layers of clouds, he stood in front of Sita, a gem among women.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

स परिव्राजकच्छद्म महाकायो विहाय तत्।।3.49.8।। प्रतिपद्य स्वकं रूपं रावणो राक्षसाधिपः। संरक्तनयनः क्रोधाज्जीमूतनिचयप्रभः।।3.49.9।। रक्ताम्बरधरस्तस्थौ स्त्रीरत्नं प्रेक्ष्य मैथिलीम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षसाधिपः chief of the demons, सः रावणः that Ravana, तत् that, परिव्राजकच्छद्म figure of a mendicant, विहाय shedding, स्वकम् his real, रूपम् form, प्रतिपद्य assuming, क्रोधात् in anger, संरक्त नयनः red eyes, जीमूतनिचयप्रभः radiating like layers of clouds, रक्ताम्बरधरः wearing red garments, स्त्रीरत्नम् gem of a woman, मैथिलीम् to Sita, प्रेक्ष्य seeing, तस्थौ stood in front.

#### Explanation

Ravana, chief of the demons, shed the mendicant's form and assumed his real self. Clad in red garments, his eyes turned red in anger. Looking like layers of clouds, he stood in front of Sita, a gem among women.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

स तामसितकेशान्तां भास्करस्य प्रभामिव।।3.49.10।। वसनाभरणोपेतां मैथिलीं रावणोऽब्रवीत्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः that, रावणः Ravana, असितकेशान्ताम् a lady with shining black hair, भास्करस्य the Sun's, प्रभामिव like the radiance, वसनाभरणोपेताम् robed in the best of clothes and ornaments, मैथिलीम् to SIta, अब्रवीत् said.

#### Explanation

Ravana said to the princess from Mithila, whose black hair was shining, who was luminous like the Sun and was clad in the best of clothes and ornaments.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

त्रिषु लोकेषु विख्यातं यदि भर्तारमिच्छसि।।3.49.11।। मामाश्रय वरारोहे तवाहं सदृशः पतिः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वरारोहे O beautiful lady, त्रिषुलोकेषु in the three worlds, विख्यातम् famous, भर्तारम् husband, इच्छसि you desire, यदि so, माम् me, आश्रय take refuge in, अहम् I, तव your, सदृशः fit, पतिः husband.

#### Explanation

O beautiful lady  if you desire a husband who is famous in the three worlds, take refuge in me. I am fit to be your husband.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

मां भजस्व चिराय त्वमहं श्लाघ्यः प्रियस्तव।।3.49.12।। नैव चाहं क्वचिद्भद्रे करिष्ये तव विप्रियम्। त्यज्यतां मानुषे भावो मयि भावः प्रणीयताम्।।3.49.13।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

भद्रे O auspicious lady, चिराय for long, माम् me, भजस्व accept, अहम् I am, तव your, श्लाघ्यः praiseworthy, प्रियः (पतिः) husband, अहम् I am, क्वचित् at any time, तव your, विप्रियम् unpleasantness, न करिष्ये चwill not cause, मानुषे mortal , भावः your attachment, त्यज्यताम्  abandon, मयि in me, भावः inclination, प्रणीयताम् diverted.

#### Explanation

I am your praiseworthy husband. I will not cause any unpleasantness to you at any time. Abandon on your inclination towards, a (mere) mortal. Divert your love to me.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

मां भजस्व चिराय त्वमहं श्लाघ्यः प्रियस्तव।।3.49.12।। नैव चाहं क्वचिद्भद्रे करिष्ये तव विप्रियम्। त्यज्यतां मानुषे भावो मयि भावः प्रणीयताम्।।3.49.13।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

भद्रे O auspicious lady, चिराय for long, माम् me, भजस्व accept, अहम् I am, तव your, श्लाघ्यः praiseworthy, प्रियः (पतिः) husband, अहम् I am, क्वचित् at any time, तव your, विप्रियम् unpleasantness, न करिष्ये चwill not cause, मानुषे mortal , भावः your attachment, त्यज्यताम्  abandon, मयि in me, भावः inclination, प्रणीयताम् diverted.

#### Explanation

I am your praiseworthy husband. I will not cause any unpleasantness to you at any time. Abandon on your inclination towards, a (mere) mortal. Divert your love to me.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

राज्याच्च्युतमसिद्धार्थं रामं परिमितायुषम्। कैर्गुणैरनुरक्तासि मूढे पण्डितमानिनि।।3.49.14।। यः स्त्रिया वचनाद्राज्यं विहाय ससुहृज्जनम्। अस्मिन्व्यालानुचरिते वने वसति दुर्मतिः।।3.49.15।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मुढे O stupid woman, पण्डितमानिनि you think you are wise, दुर्मतिः wicked, यः he, स्त्रियाः woman's, वचनात् word, ससुहृज्जनम् including friends, राज्यम् kingdom, विहाय after leaving, व्यालानुचरिते  haunted by wild animals, अस्मिन् in this, वने in the forest, चरति moves, राज्यात् from the kingdom, च्युतम् banished, असिद्धार्थम् who has not accomplished any objective, परिमितायुषम् of limited life, रामम् Rama, कैः by what, गुणैः virtues, अनुरक्ता a attached, असि are you.

#### Explanation

O stupid woman you think you are very wise. What is there in Rama so that you are attached to him ? He has left the kingdom and his kith and kin due to the words of a woman. He has not accomplished anything (in life). He has a limited life span and now lives in this forest haunted by wild animals.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

राज्याच्च्युतमसिद्धार्थं रामं परिमितायुषम्। कैर्गुणैरनुरक्तासि मूढे पण्डितमानिनि।।3.49.14।। यः स्त्रिया वचनाद्राज्यं विहाय ससुहृज्जनम्। अस्मिन्व्यालानुचरिते वने वसति दुर्मतिः।।3.49.15।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मुढे O stupid woman, पण्डितमानिनि you think you are wise, दुर्मतिः wicked, यः he, स्त्रियाः woman's, वचनात् word, ससुहृज्जनम् including friends, राज्यम् kingdom, विहाय after leaving, व्यालानुचरिते  haunted by wild animals, अस्मिन् in this, वने in the forest, चरति moves, राज्यात् from the kingdom, च्युतम् banished, असिद्धार्थम् who has not accomplished any objective, परिमितायुषम् of limited life, रामम् Rama, कैः by what, गुणैः virtues, अनुरक्ता a attached, असि are you.

#### Explanation

O stupid woman you think you are very wise. What is there in Rama so that you are attached to him ? He has left the kingdom and his kith and kin due to the words of a woman. He has not accomplished anything (in life). He has a limited life span and now lives in this forest haunted by wild animals.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

इत्युक्त्वा मैथिलीं वाक्यं प्रियार्हां प्रियवादिनीम्। अभिगम्य सुदुष्टात्मा राक्षसः काममोहितः।।3.49.16।। जग्राह रावणस्सीतां बुधः खे रोहिणीमिव।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सुदुष्टात्मा evilminded, काममोहितः infatuated, राक्षसः demon, रावणः Ravana, प्रियार्हाम् worthy of pity, प्रियवादिनीम् speaks pleasing words, मैथिलीम् Maithili, इति thus, उक्त्वा said, अभिगम्य going towards, खे बुधः Mercury in the sky, रोहिणीमिव like Rohini, सीताम् Sita, जग्राह caught hold of.

#### Explanation

Just as Budha (planet Mercury) catches Rohini (a cluster of five stars) in the sky, the evilminded, infatuated Ravana advanced towards the pitiable, pleasing Sita and caught hold of her.

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

वामेन सीतां पद्माक्षीं मूर्धजेषु करेण सः।।3.49.17।। ऊर्वोस्तु दक्षिणेनैव परिजग्राह पाणिना।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः that Ravana, पद्माक्षीम् lotuseyed, सीताम् Sita, वामेन करेण with his left hand, मूर्धजेषु  holding her hair, दक्षिणेन पाणिना with right hand, ऊर्वोः under her thighs, प्रतिजग्राह held her.

#### Explanation

Ravana seized with his left hand the lotuseyed Sita by the hair and lifted her by the thighs with his right hand.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

तं दृष्ट्वा मृत्युसङ्काशं तीक्ष्णदंष्ट्रं महाभुजम्।।3.49.18।। प्राद्रवन्गिरिसङ्काशं भयार्ता वनदेवताः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मृत्युसङ्काशम् like the god of death, तीक्ष्णदंष्ट्रम् with sharp teeth, महाभुजम् mightyarmed, गिरिसङ्काशम्  like a mountain, तम् him, दृष्ट्वा  seeing, वनदेवताः deities of the forest, भयार्ताः out of fear, प्राद्रवन् fled.

#### Explanation

The sylvan deities fled out of fear on seeing the dreadful appearance of Ravana who had sharp teeth and mighty arms. He looked like a mountain, a veritable god of death.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

स च मायामयो दिव्यः खरयुक्तः खरस्वनः।।3.49.19।। प्रत्यदृश्यत हेमाङ्गो रावणस्य महारथः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मायामयः illusive form, दिव्यः divine, खरयुक्तः harnessed by donkeys, खरस्वनः brayins of  asses, हेमाङ्गः goldenlimbed, रावणस्य Ravana's, सः महारथः that great chariot, प्रत्यदृश्यत apeared.

#### Explanation

There arrived the great  golden chariot of Ravana, illusive and wonderful, harnessed with donkeys and braying like donkeys.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

ततस्तां परुषैर्वाक्यैर्भर्त्सयन्स महास्वनः।।3.49.20।। अङ्केनादाय वैदेहीं रथमारोपयत्तदा।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तदा then, महास्वनः Ravana in a loud voice, सः he, तां वैदेहीम् Vaidehi, परुषैः with harsh, वाक्यैः words, भर्त्सयन् reproaching, अङ्केन on his lap, आदाय on lifting, रथम् chariot, आरोपयत् placed.

#### Explanation

Reproaching the princess from Videha loudly and harshly, Ravana took her on his lap and put her on the chariot.

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

सा गृहीता विचुक्रोश रावणेन यशस्स्विनी।।3.49.21।। रामेति सीता दुःखार्ता रामं दूरगतंवने।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रावणेन by Ravana, गृहीता seized, यशस्स्विनी illustrious, सा सीता that Sita, दुःखार्ता helpless in grief, वने in the forset, दूरगतम् who was far away, रामम् Rama, राम इति saying, Alas Rama, विचुक्रोश screamed aloud.

#### Explanation

Seized by Ravana, illustrious Sita screamed aloud Alas, Rama. for one who was far away in the forest.

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

तामकामां स कामार्तः पन्नगेन्द्रवधूमिव।।3.49.22।। विवेष्टमानामादाय उत्पपाताथ रावणः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अथ then, कामार्तः passionate, सः that, रावणः Ravana, अकामाम् not willing, पन्नगेन्द्रवधूमिव like a serpent queen, विवेष्टमानाम् writhing hard, ताम् her, आदाय seized,उत्पपात flew up.

#### Explanation

Passionate Ravana took hold of Sita who was not willing and was writhing in pain. He seized her  like (an eagle carrying away) a serpent queen and flew up.

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

ततस्सा राक्षसेन्द्रेण ह्रियमाणा विहायसा।।3.49.23।। भृशं चुक्रोश मत्तेव भ्रान्तचित्ता यथाऽऽतुरा।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, राक्षसेन्द्रेण by the king of demons, विहायसा through the sky, ह्रियमाणा being carried off, सा that Sita, मत्तेव like a mad woman, भ्रान्तचित्ता with a bewildered mind, आतुरा anxious, यथा like, भृशम् चुक्रोश screamed a lot in agony.

#### Explanation

While Sita was being carried off in the sky by the king of demons,   she screamed a lot in agony and anxiety like a mad woman in a bewildered state:

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

हा लक्ष्मण महाबाहो गुरुचित्तप्रसादक।।3.49.24।। ह्रियमाणां न जानीषे रक्षसा माममर्षिणा।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महाबाहो O longarmed Lakshmana, गुरुचित्तप्रसादक  who entertains the brother's mind, हा लक्ष्मण Alas, Lakshmana, अमर्षिणा by this angry, रक्षसा demon, माम् me, ह्रियमाणाम् being carried away, न जानीषे you do not know.

#### Explanation

Alas, O longarmed Lakshmana, you who used to entertain your brother do not know that I am being carried away by this ruthless demon.

---

### Shloka 25

#### Shloka Text

जीवितं सुखमर्थांश्च धर्महेतोः परित्यजन्।।3.49.25।। ह्रियमाणामधर्मेण मां राघव न पश्यसि।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

जीवितम् life, सुखम् comfort, अर्थांश्च treasure, धर्महेतोः for the sake of righteousness, परित्यजन् giving up, राघव O Rama, अधर्मेण by unrighteous, ह्रियमाणाम्   carried off, माम् me, न पश्यसि do you not see.

#### Explanation

O Rama, you have given up your life, pleasure and treasure for the sake of righteousness. Do you not see me carried off by an unrighteous fellow ?

---

### Shloka 26

#### Shloka Text

ननु नामाविनीतानां विनेतासि परन्तप।।3.49.26।। कथमेवंविधं पापं न त्वं शासि हि रावणम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

परन्तप scorcher of enemies, अविनीतानाम् of the haughty, विनेता a chastiser, असि ननु as such, एवंविधम् such, पापम् sinner, रावणम् Ravana, कथम् how is it, हि indeed, शासि you are not punishing?

#### Explanation

O scorcher of enemies you are the chastiser of the haughty. Why do you not punish such a sinner like Ravana.

---

### Shloka 27

#### Shloka Text

ननु सद्योऽविनीतस्य दृश्यते कर्मणःफलम्।।3.49.27।। कालोऽप्यङ्गीभवत्यत्र सस्यानामिव पक्तये।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अविनीतस्य arrogant, कर्मणः action, फलम् result, सद्यः right now, न दृश्यते नु not seen, सस्यानाम् crop, पक्तये इव ripening, अत्र in the same way, कालोऽपि it takes time also, अङ्गीभवति serves as a supporting factor.

#### Explanation

The effect of arrogant action cannot be seen right away. Just as the crop yields results only after ripening, in the same way it takes time. Time acts as a supporting factor.

---

### Shloka 28

#### Shloka Text

त्वं कर्म कृतवानेतत्कालोपहतचेतनः।।3.49.28।। जीवितान्तकरं घोरं रामाद्व्यसनमाप्नुहि।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

त्वम् you, कालोपहतचेतनः time has taken a toll of your senses, एतत् this, कर्म action, कृतवान् have done, रामात् from Rama, जीवितान्तकरम् leading towards the end of your life, घोरम् dreadful, व्यसनम् sorrow, आप्नुहि will befall.

#### Explanation

Time has taken a toll of your senses. This has made you do what you have done. A great calamity is awaiting you in the hands of Rama. It will end your life.

---

### Shloka 29

#### Shloka Text

हन्तेदानीं सकामास्तु कैकेयी सह बान्धवैः।।3.49.29।। ह्रिये यद्धर्मकामस्य धर्मपत्नी यशस्विनः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

धर्मकामस्य of one who wants to discharge righteous duties, यशस्विनः of the glorious, धर्मपत्नी lawful wife, यत् such, ह्रिये being carried away, इदानीम् now, कैकेयी Kaikeyi, बान्धवैः सह along with her relations, सकामा her desires, अस्तु may be fulfilled, हन्त alas.

#### Explanation

Alas, the lawful wife of the glorious and righteous Rama is being abducted now. Let Kaikeyi along with her relations be happy with her desire fulfilled.

---

### Shloka 30

#### Shloka Text

आमन्त्रये जनस्थाने कर्णिकारान्सुपुष्पितान्।।3.49.30।। क्षिप्रं रामाय शंसध्वं सीतां हरति रावणः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

जनस्थाने in Janasthana, सुपुष्पितान् blossomed, कर्णिकारान् karnikara trees, आमन्त्रये pray, रावणः Ravana, सीताम् Sita, हरति is abducting, क्षिप्रम् at once, रामाय to Rama, शंसध्वम् tell him.

#### Explanation

O karnikar trees in full bloom in Janasthana, tell Rama quickly that Ravana is kidnapping Sita.

---

### Shloka 31

#### Shloka Text

माल्यवन्तं शिखरिणं वन्दे प्रस्रवणं गिरिम्।।3.49.31।। क्षिप्रं रामाय शंस त्वं सीतां हरति रावणः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

माल्यवन्तम् Malyavan, शिखरिणम्  mountain, प्रस्रवणं गिरिम् mount Prasravana, वन्दे I salute, रावणः Ravana, सीताम् Sita, हरति abducting, त्वम् you, क्षिप्रम् at once, रामाय to Rama, शंस tell.

#### Explanation

I salute to Malyavan and  Prasravana mountains. Tell Rama at once that Ravana is carrying away Sita.

---

### Shloka 32

#### Shloka Text

हंसकारण्डवाकीर्णां वन्दे गोदावरीं नदीम्।।3.49.32।। क्षिप्रं रामाय शंस त्वं सीतां हरति रावणः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

हंसकारण्डवाकीर्णां filled with swans and cranes, गोदावरीं नदीम् river Godavari, वन्दे my salutations to you, रावणः Ravana, सीताम् Sita, हरति is carrying away, त्वम् you, क्षिप्रम् immediately, रामाय Rama, शंस tell.

#### Explanation

O river Godavari abounding in swans and cranes, my salutations to you  Tell Rama immediately that Ravana is taking away Sita.

---

### Shloka 33

#### Shloka Text

दैवतानि च यान्यस्मिन्वने विविधपादपे।।3.49.33।। नमस्करोम्यहं तेभ्योभर्तुश्शंसत मां हृताम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

विविधपादपे various trees, अस्मिन् वने in this forest, यानि those, दैवतानि deities, (सन्ति residing here), तेभ्यः to them, अहम् I, नमस्करोमि I offer salutations, माम् me, हृताम् taken away, भर्तुः to my husband, शंसत Tell.

#### Explanation

I offer my salutations to different deities residing in this forest abounding in trees of many kinds. Tell my husband about me that I am being whisked away.

---

### Shloka 34

#### Shloka Text

यानि कानि चिदप्यत्र सत्त्वानि निवसन्त्युत।।3.49.34।। सर्वाणि शरणं यामि मृगपक्षिगणानपि।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अत्र here, यानि कानि चित् all the animals of the forest, सत्त्वानि living beings, निवसन्ति are  residing, सर्वाणि all, मृगपक्षिगणानपि including herds of animals and birds, शरणम् I seek refuge, यामि I seek.

#### Explanation

I seek refuge in all those living beings as well as herds of animals and birds in the forest.

---

### Shloka 35

#### Shloka Text

ह्रियमाणां प्रियां भर्तुः प्राणेभ्योऽपि गरीयसीम्।।3.49.35।। विवशाऽपहृता सीता रावणेनेति शंसत।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

प्राणेभ्योऽपि more than his life, गरीयसीम्  valuable lady, ह्रियमाणाम्  carried away, प्रियाम् beloved, विवशा helpless lady, सीता Sita, रावणेन by Ravana, अपहृता इति is abducted like this, भर्तुः to my husband, शंसत tell.

#### Explanation

Tell Rama that his dear wife Sita whom he loves more than his life is abducted helplessly.

---

### Shloka 36

#### Shloka Text

विदित्वा मां महाबाहुरमुत्रापि महाबलः।।3.49.36।। आनेष्यति पराक्रम्य वैवस्वतहृतामपि।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महाबलः mighty, महाबाहुः longarmed, वैवस्वतहृतामपि even if abducted by Vaivasvata, lord of death, माम् me, अमुत्रापि from there also, विदित्वा knowing, पराक्रम्य by his valour, आनेष्यति can get back.

#### Explanation

If he knows that I am abducted by Yama, the god of death, that mighty, longarmed hero will save me even from there by virtue of his valour.

---

### Shloka 37

#### Shloka Text

सा तदा करुणा वाचो विलपन्ती सुदुःखिता।।3.49.37।। वनस्पतिगतं गृध्रं ददर्शाऽयतलोचना।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तदा then, करुणाः piteously, वाचः words, विलपन्ती pleading crying, आयतलोचना large eyed, सा that lady, सुदुःखिता very sadly, वनस्पतिगतम् seated on the tree, गृध्रम् vulture, ददर्श saw.

#### Explanation

When the griefstricken, largeeyed Sita was lamenting piteously, she saw the vulture (Jatayu) seated on a tree.

---

### Shloka 38

#### Shloka Text

सा तमुद्वीक्ष्य सुश्रोणी रावणस्य वशं गता।3.49.38।। समाक्रन्दद्भयपरा दुःखोपहतया गिरा।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रावणस्य Ravana's, वशम् grip, गता gone, सुश्रोणी heavyhipped Sita, भयपरा frightened, सा she, तम् him, उद्वीक्ष्य seeing him, दुःखोपहतया shaken up by sorrow, गिरा with words, समाक्रन्दत् screamed.

#### Explanation

The heavyhipped Sita felt frightened in the grip of Ravana. When she saw Jatayu she screamed, shaken by sorrow:

---

### Shloka 39

#### Shloka Text

जटायो पश्य मामद्य ह्रियमाणामनाथवत्।।3.49.39।। अनेन राक्षसेन्द्रेण करुणं पापकर्मणा।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

जटायो O Jatayu, अद्य now, अनेन by this one, पापकर्मणा by sinful deeds, राक्षसेन्द्रेण by the king of demons, अनाथवत् like an orphan, करुणम् in a desparate way, ह्रियमाणाम् being carried away, माम् me, पश्य see.

#### Explanation

O Jatayu, see me now being carried away by the sinful king of demons in this pitiable condition as though I am an orphan.

---

### Shloka 40

#### Shloka Text

नैष वारयितुं शक्यस्तव क्रूरो निशाचरः।।3.49.40।। सत्त्वाञ्जितकाशी च सायुधश्चैव दुर्मतिः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

क्रूरः cruel, सत्त्वान् strong, जितकाशी victorious, सायुधश्चैव wielding weapons, दुर्मतिः evilminded, एषः this, निशाचरः nightranger, तव you, वारयितुम् to prevent, न शक्यः not possible.

#### Explanation

This nightranger is equipped with weapons. Cruel, strong and evilminded, he has won wars. You cannot stop him.

---

### Shloka 41

#### Shloka Text

रामाय तु यथातत्त्वं जटायो हरणं मम।।3.49.41।। लक्ष्मणाय च तत्सर्वमाख्यातव्यमशेषतः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

जटायोः Jatayu, मम my, हरणम् abduction, यथातत्त्वम् exact fact, तत् सर्वम् everything, अशेषतः without any omission, रामाय to Rama, लक्ष्मणाय च and Lakshmana, आख्यातव्यम् please narrate.

#### Explanation

O Jatayu narrate to Rama and Lakshmana the exact facts of my abduction and all the details without any omission. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये अरण्यकाण्डे एकोनपञ्चाशस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the fortyninth sarga of Aranyakanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 50

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

तं शब्दमवसुप्तस्तु जटायुरथ शुश्रुवे। निरीक्ष्य रावणं क्षिप्रं वैदेहीं च ददर्श सः।।3.50.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अवसुप्तः reposing, जटायुः Jatayu, तं शब्दम् that sound, शुश्रुवे heard, अथ and, सः he, क्षिप्रम् at once, निरीक्ष्य observed, रावणम् Ravana, वैदेहीं च and Vaidehi, ददर्श saw.

#### Explanation

While reposing, Jatayu heard the cry and at once looked up and saw Ravana and Vaidehi.

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

ततः पर्वतकूटाभस्तीक्ष्णतुण्डः खगोत्तमः। वनस्पतिगतश्श्रीमान्व्याजहार शुभां गिरम्।।3.50.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, पर्वतकूटाभः looking like a part of the mountain, तीक्ष्णतुण्डः sharpbeaked, श्रीमान् glorious, वनस्पतिगतः from the top of the tree, खगोत्तमः best among birds, शुभाम् auspicious, गिरम् words, व्याजहार uttered.

#### Explanation

Glorious Jatayu, the best among the birds, looked like the peak of a mountain. His beak was sharp. His words were auspious. He spoke from the top of the tree:

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

दशग्रीव स्थितो धर्मे पुराणे सत्यसंश्रयः। जटायुर्नाम नाम्नाहं गृध्रराजो महाबलः।।3.50.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

दशग्रीव tenheaded Ravana, अहम् I, पुराणे धर्मे in eternal dharma, स्थितः adhering to dharma, सत्यसंश्रयः  who abides in truth, महाबलः mighty, गृध्रराजः king of vultures, नाम्ना by name, जटायुर्नाम Jatayu.

#### Explanation

O tenheaded Ravana, I am Jatayu, king of vultures. I am mighty. An eternal follower, of dharma, I am an adherent to truth.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

राजा सर्वस्य लोकस्य महेन्द्रवरुणोपमः। लोकानां च हिते युक्तो रामो दशरथात्मजः।।3.50.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

दशरथात्मजः Dasaratha's son, रामः Rama, सर्वस्य of the entire, लोकस्य world, राजा king, महेन्द्रवरुणोपमः equal to Indra and Varuna, लोकानाम् in the world, हिते in the wellbeing, युक्तः engaged.

#### Explanation

Rama, Dasaratha's son, is the king of the entire world. He is equal to Indra and Varuna. He is ever engaged in the welfare of the entire world.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

तस्यैषा लोकनाथस्य धर्मपत्नी यशस्विनी। सीता नाम वरारोहा यां त्वं हर्तुमिहेच्छसि।।3.50.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

याम् whom, त्वम् you, इह from here, हर्तुम् to take away, इच्छसि are desiring, एषा she is, सीता नाम Sita by name, वरारोहा a beautiful lady, यशस्विनी illustrious, लोकनाथस्य of the lord of the world, तस्य his, धर्मपत्नी wife.

#### Explanation

The lady you want to abduct from here is the famous Sita, the lawful wife of Rama, the lord of the world.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

कथं राजास्थितो धर्मे परदारान्परामृशेत्। रक्षणीया विशेषेण राजदारा महाबल।।3.50.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

धर्मे in righteousness, स्थितः adheres, राजा king, परदारान् another's wife, कथम् how, परामृशेत् can any one violate, महाबल powerful, राजदाराः king's wife, विशेषेण specially, रक्षणीयाः should be protected.

#### Explanation

How can a king who adheres to righteousness outrage another's wife ? O powerful one, a king's wife in particular should be protected.

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

निवर्तय मतिं नीचां परदाराभिमर्शनात्। न तत्समाचरेद्धीरो यत्परोऽस्य विगर्हयेत्।।3.50.7।। यथात्मनस्तथान्येषां दारा रक्ष्या विपश्चिता।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

नीचाम् mean, मतिम् in mind, परदाराभिमर्शनात् thought of touching another's wife, निवर्तय refrain yourself, परः others, अस्य his, यत् such deeds, विगर्हयेत् if he reproaches, तत् that, धीरः learned, न समाचरेत् should not adopt, विपश्चिता by the wise man, आत्मनः his own, यथा likewise, तथा in the same way, अन्येषाम् another's, दाराः wife, रक्ष्याः be protected.

#### Explanation

Refrain yourself from the vile thought of violating other's wives. Wise men do not adopt what is censured by others. Just like one's own, the wise should protect other's wives.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

धर्ममर्थं वा कामं वा शिष्टाश्शास्त्रेष्वनागतम्।।3.50.8।। व्यवस्यन्त्यनु राजानं धर्मं पौलस्त्यनन्दन।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पौलस्त्यनन्दन O son of Paulastya, शिष्टाः learned, शास्त्रेषु sastras, अनागतम् unknown, अर्थं वा wish for material, यदि वा even if, कामम् pleasures, धर्मम् righteousness, राजानम् अनु kings follow, व्यवस्यन्ति prescribe.

#### Explanation

O son of Paulastya learned men determine the path of dharma artha and kama,  following a king's conduct even if  they are not declared in sastras.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

राजा धर्मस्य कामस्य द्रव्याणां चोत्तमो निधिः।।3.50.9।। धर्मश्शुभं वा पापं वा राजमूलं प्रवर्तते।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

धर्मस्य of righteousness, कामस्य of pleasures, द्रव्याणां च and for material wealth, राजा king, उत्तमः best, निधिः repository, धर्मः righteousness, शुभंवा good or, पापं वा or bad, राजमूलम् king is at the root, प्रवर्तते is practised.

#### Explanation

A king is the best repository of rigteousness, material wealth and pleasures. The king is the root of dharma, virtue and sin.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

पापस्वभावश्चपलः कथं त्वं रक्षसां वर।।3.50.10।। ऐश्वर्यमभिसम्प्राप्तो विमानमिव दुष्कृतिः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रक्षसाम् of demons, वर best one, पापस्वभावः of sinful nature, चपलः fickleminded, त्वम् you are, दुष्कृतिः who does forbidden acts, विमानमिव like this celestial chariot, ऐश्वर्यम् wealth, कथम् how, अभिसम्प्राप्तः did you get?

#### Explanation

O best of demons  by nature you are sinful, and fickleminded. You do all forbidden acts. Otherwise,how could you get this wealth, like the celestial chariot?

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

कामं स्वभावो यो यस्य न शक्यः परिमार्जितुम्।।3.50.11।। न हि दुष्टात्मनामार्यमावसत्यालये चिरम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

यः whatever, यस्य  his, स्वभावः nature, परिमार्जितुम् to erase, कामम् indeed, न शक्यः not possible, दुष्टात्मनाम् evilminded people, आलये in the house, चिरम् for long time, आर्यम् prosperity, न आवसति हि will not be.

#### Explanation

Indeed, it is not possible to erase the nature of any one, whatever it be. Prosperity does not rest for long in the house of the evilminded.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

विषये वा पुरे वा ते यदा रामो महाबलः।।3.50.12।। नापराध्यति धर्मात्मा कथं तस्यापराध्यसि।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महाबलः powerful, धर्मात्मा righteous self, रामः Rama, यदा since, ते to you, विषये वा in your kingdom, पुरे वा or in the city, नापराध्यति has not offended, तस्य his, कथम् how, अपराध्यसि do you offend.

#### Explanation

Powerful and righteous Rama has not offended you, your kingdom or your city. Why do you offend him?

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

यदि शूर्पणखाहेतोर्जनस्थानगतः खरः।।3.50.13।। अतिवृत्तो हतः पूर्वं रामेणाक्लिष्टकर्मणा। अत्र ब्रूहि यथातत्त्वं को रामस्य व्यतिक्रमः।।3.50.14।। यस्य त्वं लोकनाथस्य भार्यां हृत्वा गमिष्यसि।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

शूर्पणखाहेतोः on account of Surpanakha, जनस्थानगतः staying in Janasthana, खरः Khara, अतिवृत्तः trespassed, आक्लिष्टकर्मणा by one who does something without much effort, रामेण Rama, पूर्वम् earlier, हतः यदि if killed, अत्र here, लोकनाथस्य lord of the world (Rama), यस्य whose, भार्याम् wife, त्वम् you, हृत्वा गमिष्यसि (हरिष्यसि) taking away, रामस्य Rama's, व्यतिक्रमः transgression of dharma, कः what, यथातत्त्वम् truly, ब्रूहि tell.

#### Explanation

On account of Surpanakha, Khara trespassed into Janasthana and got killed by Rama, without much effort. Tell me truly, what transgression (of dharma) did he commit for which you are stealing away the wife of Rama who is the lord of this world?.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

यदि शूर्पणखाहेतोर्जनस्थानगतः खरः।।3.50.13।। अतिवृत्तो हतः पूर्वं रामेणाक्लिष्टकर्मणा। अत्र ब्रूहि यथातत्त्वं को रामस्य व्यतिक्रमः।।3.50.14।। यस्य त्वं लोकनाथस्य भार्यां हृत्वा गमिष्यसि।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

शूर्पणखाहेतोः on account of Surpanakha, जनस्थानगतः staying in Janasthana, खरः Khara, अतिवृत्तः trespassed, आक्लिष्टकर्मणा by one who does something without much effort, रामेण Rama, पूर्वम् earlier, हतः यदि if killed, अत्र here, लोकनाथस्य lord of the world (Rama), यस्य whose, भार्याम् wife, त्वम् you, हृत्वा गमिष्यसि (हरिष्यसि) taking away, रामस्य Rama's, व्यतिक्रमः transgression of dharma, कः what, यथातत्त्वम् truly, ब्रूहि tell.

#### Explanation

On account of Surpanakha, Khara trespassed into Janasthana and got killed by Rama, without much effort. Tell me truly, what transgression (of dharma) did he commit for which you are stealing away the wife of Rama who is the lord of this world?.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

क्षिप्रं विसृज वैदेहीं मा त्वा घोरेण चक्षुषा।।3.50.15।। दहेद्दहनभूतेन वृत्रमिन्द्राशनिर्यथा।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

क्षिप्रम् at once, वैदेहीम् Vaidehi, विसृज leave, त्वा you, इन्द्राशनिः Indra's thunderbolt, वृत्रं यथा like Vritra, दहनभूतेन with fury, घोरेण with fierce, चक्षुषा with looks, मा दहेत् may not burn.

#### Explanation

Leave Vaidehi at once lest with her fierce looks you should be burnt down like demon Vrtra by the thunderbolt of Indra.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

सर्पमाशीविषं बद्ध्वा वस्त्रान्ते नावबुध्यसे।।3.50.16।। ग्रीवायां प्रतिसक्तं च कालपाशं न पश्यसि।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

आशीविषम् venomous, सर्पम् serpent, वस्रान्ते with the skirt of your cloth, बद्ध्वा binding, नावबुध्यसे not understand, ग्रीवायाम् to the neck, प्रतिसक्तम् fastened tightly, कालपाशम् noose of death, न पश्यसि do not see.

#### Explanation

You are not aware that you are tying a venomous snake with the skirt of your cloth. worn by you. You are not able to see the noose of death tightened around your neck.

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

स भारस्सौम्य भर्तव्यो यो नरं नावसादयेत्।।3.50.17।। तदन्नमपि भोक्तव्यं जीर्यते यदनामयम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सौम्य O gentle one, यः that which, नरम् man, नावसादयेत् does not fatigue, सः such, भारः weight, भर्तव्यः should be carried, यत् which, अनामयम् free from any disease, जीर्यते is digested, तत् that only, अन्नम् food, भोक्तव्यम् should be eaten, अपि even.

#### Explanation

O gentle Ravana one should carry that much of weight which does not tire him. One should eat only that wholesome food which can be easily digested.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

यत्कृत्वा न भवेद्धर्मो न कीर्तिर्न यशो भुवि।।3.50.18।। शरीरस्य भवेत्खेदः कस्तत्कर्म समाचरेत्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

यत् by what, कृत्वा doing, धर्मः duty, न भवेत् does not beget, भुवि world, कीर्तिः fame, न nor, यशः glory, न nor, शरीरस्य to the body, खेदः exhaustion, भवेत् brings, तत् that, कर्म deed, कः who, समाचरेत् will practise?

#### Explanation

Who will do such a deed which cannot beget dharma, fame or glory in the world? Who will do such deeds that bring only exhaustion to the body?

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

षष्टिर्वर्षसहस्राणि मम जातस्य रावण।।3.50.19।। पितृपैतामहं राज्यं यथावदनुतिष्ठतः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रावण Ravana, पितृपैतामहम् hereditary, राज्यम् kingdom, यथावत् justly, अनुतिष्टतः while ruling, जातस्य since birth, मम me, षष्टिः sixty, वर्षसहस्राणि thosand years are completed.

#### Explanation

Sixty thousand years ago I was born, O Ravana  Since then I have ruled justly my hereditary kingdom.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

वृद्धोऽहं त्वं युवा धन्वी सशरः कवची रथी।।3.50.20।। तथाप्यादाय वैदेहीं कुशली न गमिष्यसि।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अहम् I am, वृद्धः old, त्वम् you, युवा young, धन्वी wielding bow, सशरः and arrows, कवची shield, रथी riding on the chariot, तथापि even then, वैदेहीम् Vaidehi, आदाय carrying, कुशली safely, न गमिष्यसि will not go.

#### Explanation

I am old, you are young, wielding a bow and arrows, with shield to protect and chariot to ride. Even then I will not let you go safe with Vaidehi kidnapped.

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

न शक्तस्त्वं बलाद्धर्तुं वैदेहीं मम पश्यतः।।3.50.21।। हेतुभिर्न्यायसंसिद्धैद्धृवां वेदश्रुतीमिव।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मम पश्यतः within my very sight, त्वम् you, वैदेहीम् Vaidehi, न्यायसंसिद्धैः by those bound by logic, हेतुभिः by reason, ध्रुवाम् everlasting, वेदश्रुतीमिव like revelations of the Vedas, बलात् by force, हर्तुम् abduct, न शक्तः not possible for you.

#### Explanation

You cannot carry Vaidehi by force within my very sight just as the everlasting revelations of the Vedas cannot be robbed by mere logic of reason.

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

युद्ध्यस्व यदि शूरोऽसि मुहूर्तं तिष्ठ रावण।।3.50.22।। शयिष्यसे हतो भूमौ यथापूर्वं खरस्तथा।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रावण O Ravana, शूरः असि यदि if you are a hero, युद्ध्यस्व do fight with me, मुहूर्तम् तिष्ठ stay awhile, पूर्वम् earlier, खरः Khara, यथा as he was, तथा likewise, हतः slayed, भूमौ on the earth, शयिष्यसे will sleep.

#### Explanation

O Ravana, if you are brave, fight with me. In a moment, you will be killed like Khara and lie down on the ground.

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

असकृत्संयुगे येन निहता दैत्यदानवाः।।3.50.23।। नचिराच्चीरवासास्त्वां रामो युधि वधिष्यति।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

येन by whom, असकृत् many times, संयुगे in war, दैत्यदानवाः demons, निहताः were slain, त्वाम् you, नचिरात् soon, चीरवासाः clad in bark robes, रामः Rama, युधि in war, वधिष्यति will slay৷৷

#### Explanation

Clad in bark garments, Rama, who has often slain demons in the past will soon kill you in war.

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

किं नु शक्यं मया कर्तुं गतौदूरं नृपात्मजौ।।3.50.24।। क्षिप्रं त्वं नश्यसे नीच तयोर्भीतो न संशयः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मया by me, किं नु what else, कर्तुम् to do, शक्यम् am able, नृपात्मजौ the two princes, दूरम् far away, गतौ have gone, नीच mean fellow, तयोः both of them, भीतः fearing, त्वम् you, क्षिप्रम् at once, नश्यसे will be destroyed, संशयः doubt, न not.

#### Explanation

What can I do? O mean fellow the two princes have gone far away.You will soon be destroyed out of fear for them. There is no doubt.

---

### Shloka 25

#### Shloka Text

न हि मे जीवमानस्य नयिष्यसि शुभामिमाम्।।3.50.25।। सीतां कमलपत्राक्षीं रामस्य महिषीं प्रियाम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मे myself, जीवमानस्य while I am alive, शुभाम् auspicious, कमलपत्राक्षीम् lotuseyed lady, रामस्य प्रियाम् Rama's dear, महिषीम् consort, इमाम् this lady, सीताम् Sita, न नयिष्यसि will not take.

#### Explanation

I will not let you take away the auspicious, lotuseyed Sita, the dear consort of Rama as long as I am alive.

---

### Shloka 26

#### Shloka Text

अवश्यं तु मया कार्यं प्रियं तस्य महात्मनः।।3.50.26।। जीवितेनापि रामस्य तथा दशरथस्य च।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महात्मनः great soul, तस्य his, रामस्य Rama's, तथा so, दशरथस्य च and Dasaratha's, मया my, अवश्यम् certainly, जीवितेनापि even giving up my life, प्रियम् dear, कार्यम् task.

#### Explanation

I should do this important work of those great souls, Rama and Dasaratha, even if it claims my life.

---

### Shloka 27

#### Shloka Text

तिष्ठ तिष्ठ दशग्रीव मुहूर्तं पश्य रावण।।3.50.27।। युद्धातिथ्यं प्रदास्यामि यथाप्राणं निशाचर। वृन्तादिव फलं त्वां तु पातयेयं रथोत्तमात्।।3.50.28।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

दशग्रीव tenheaded, मुहूर्तम् for a short while, तिष्ठ तिष्ठ stay, stay, रावण Ravana, पश्य look, निशाचर nightwalker, यथाप्राणम् as long as I am alive, युद्धातिथ्यम् hospitality of war , प्रदास्यामि will accord, वृन्तात् from the stalk, फलमिव like a ripe fruit is dropped down, त्वाम् you, रथोत्तमात् from the best of the chariots, पातयेयम् will pull you down.

#### Explanation

O ten headed Ravana stay, stay for a while. O demon  as long as I am alive I will treat you with the hospitality of war. You will be pulled down from this best of the chariots like a ripe fruit drops from the stalk. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये अरण्यकाण्डे पञ्चाशस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the fiftieth sarga of Aranyakanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 51

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

इत्युक्तस्य यथान्यायं रावणस्य जटायुषा। क्रुद्धस्याग्निनिभास्सर्वा रेजुर्विंशतिदृष्टयः।।3.51.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

जटायुषा by Jatayu, यथान्यायम् justly, इति in this manner, उक्तस्य  told, क्रुद्धस्य of the enraged, रावणस्य Ravana's, सर्वाः all, विंशतिदृष्टयः twenty eyes, अग्निनिभाः like fire, रेजुः glowed.

#### Explanation

Ravana's twenty eyes glowed like fire in anger after Jatayu thus rightly said.

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

संरक्तनयनः कोपात्तप्तकाञ्चनकुण्डलः। राक्षसेन्द्रोऽभिदुद्राव पतगेन्द्रममर्षणः।।3.51.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तप्तकाञ्चनकुण्डलः bright gold earrings, अमर्षणः intolerant, राक्षसेन्द्रः king of demons, कोपात् in anger, संरक्तनयनः eyes turned red, पतगेन्द्रम् to lord of birds (Jatayu), अभिदुद्राव leaped.

#### Explanation

As the bright gold earrings of the intolerant king of demons glittered, his eyes turned red in rage and he pounced upon Jatayu, the lord of birds.

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

स सम्प्रहारस्तुमुल स्तयोस्तस्मिन्महावने। बभूव वातोद्धतयोर्मेघयोर्गगने यथा।।3.51.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तस्मिन् in that, महावने in the huge forest, तयोः between them, तुमुलः tumultuous, सः सम्प्रहारः fight, गगने in the sky, वातोद्धतयोः stormy wind that arose, मेघयोर्यथा like two flakes of clouds, बभूव appeared.

#### Explanation

The clash between them was tumultuous and it appeared like the clash of two pieces of clouds in the sky raised by stormy winds.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

तद्बभूवाद्भुतं युद्धं गृध्रराक्षसयोस्तदा। सपक्षयोर्माल्यवतोर्महापर्वतयोरिव।।3.51.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तदा then, (तयोः of both of them), गृध्रराक्षसयोः vulture and demon, तत् that, अद्भुतम् wonderful, युद्धम् war, सपक्षयोः of the winged ones, माल्यवतोः of Malyavan range, महापर्वतयोरिव between two huge mountains, बभूव took place.

#### Explanation

The duel between Ravana and Jatayu looked as though it was a fight between two huge winged mountains of Malyavan range.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

ततो नालीकनाराचैस्तीक्ष्णाग्रैश्च विकर्णिभिः। अभ्यवर्षन्महाघोरैर्गृध्रराजं महाबलः।।3.51.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then,महाबलः mighty, तीक्ष्णाग्रैः with sharp tips, महाघोरैः dreadful, नालीकनाराचैः by spears and shafts, विकर्णिभिः a weapon named Vikarni, गृध्रराजम्  king of vultures, अभ्यवर्षत् rained.

#### Explanation

Then the mighty Ravana rained sharp, dreadful shafts, spears and Vikarnis at Jatayu,  king of vultures.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

स तानि शरजालानि गृध्रः पत्त्ररथेश्वरः। जटायुः प्रतिजग्राह रावणास्त्राणि संयुगे।।3.51.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पत्ररथेश्वरः lord of birds, गृध्रः vulture, जटायुः Jatayu, संयुगे in encounter, तानि those, शरजालानि all the arrows, रावणास्त्राणि Ravana's weapons, प्रतिजग्राह endured.

#### Explanation

Jatayu, lord of the birds, faced the arrows and other weapons showered  by Ravana.

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

तस्य तीक्ष्णनखाभ्यां तु चरणाभ्यां महाबलः। चकार बहुधा गात्रे व्रणान्पतगसत्तमः।।3.51.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महाबलः very strong, पतगसत्तमः virtuous vulture, तीक्ष्णनखाभ्याम् with sharp claws, चरणाभ्याम् with both feet, तस्य his, गात्रे on the limbs, बहुधा many ways, व्रणान् wounds, चकार pierced.

#### Explanation

The mighty, virtuous vulture with his sharp claws wounded many parts of the body of Ravana.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

अथ क्रोधाद्धशग्रीवो जग्राह दश मार्गणान्। मृत्युदण्डनिभान्घोरान्छत्रुमर्दनकाङ्क्षया।।3.51.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अथ at that time, दशग्रीवः the tenheaded Ravana, क्रोधात् enraged, शत्रुमर्दनकाङ्क्षया with a desire to crush the enemy, मृत्युदण्डनिभान् like the staff of death, घोरान् fierce, दश मार्गणान् ten darts, जग्राह unleashed.

#### Explanation

Ravana in a rage took ten dreadful darts (looking) like the staff of death with a desire to crush the enemy.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

स तैर्बाणैर्महावीर्यः पूर्णमुक्तैरजिह्मगैः। बिभेद निशितैस्तीक्ष्णैर्गृध्रं घोरैश्शिलीमुखैः।।3.51.9।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महावीर्यः mighty, सः he, पूर्णमुक्तै: fully released, अजिह्मगैः going straight, निशितैः whetted, तीक्ष्णै with sharp, घोरैः dreadful, शिलीमुखैः with arrows, तैः those, बाणैः arrows, गृध्रम् at the vulture, बिभेद pierced.

#### Explanation

The mighty Ravana fully released his dreadful, sharp arrows that went straight and pierced the vulture.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

स राक्षसरथे पश्यन्जानकीं बाष्पलोचनाम्। अचिन्तयित्वा तान्बाणान्राक्षसं समभिद्रवत्।।3.51.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः that, राक्षसरथे on the demon's chariot, बाष्पलोचनाम् eyes filled with tears, जानकीम् Sita, daughter of Janaka, पश्यन् observing, तान् those, बाणान् arrows, अचिन्तयित्वा without minding, राक्षसम् to the demon, समभिद्रवत् attacked again.

#### Explanation

Seeing Sita, daughter of Janaka, on the demon's chariot with eyes full of tears, Jatayu without minding the arrows renewed his attack.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

ततोऽस्य सशरं चापं मुक्तामणिविभूषितम्। चरणाभ्यां महातेजा बभञ्ज पतगेश्वरः।।3.51.11।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, महातेजाः glorious, पतगेश्वरः lord of birds, अस्य his, मुक्तामणिविभूषितम् encrusted with pearls and gems, सशरम् along with that arrow, चापम् bow, चरणाभ्याम् with his feet, बभञ्ज broke.

#### Explanation

The glorious lord of birds crushed with his feet the bow and arrow of Ravana with pearls and gems encrusted.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

ततोऽन्यद्धनुरादाय रावणः क्रोधमूर्छितः। ववर्ष शरवर्षाणि शतशोऽथ सहस्रशः।।3.51.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, रावणः Ravana, क्रोधमूर्छितः overcome with anger, अन्यत् other, धनुः bow, आदाय after taking, शतशः hundreds of, अथ and, सहस्रशः in thousands, शरवर्षाणि rain of arrows, ववर्ष rained.

#### Explanation

Overcome with anger, Ravana took another bow and rained (on him) hundreds and thousands of arrows.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

शरैरावारितस्तस्य संयुगे पतगेश्वरः। कुलायमुपसम्प्राप्तः पक्षीव प्रबभौ तदा।।3.51.13।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तदा then, संयुगे in the encounter, तस्य his, शरैः  arrows, आवारितः surrounded, पतगेश्वरः Jatayu, कुलायम् nest of the bird, उपसम्प्राप्तः returned, पक्षीव resemled the bird, प्रबभौ shone.

#### Explanation

Jatayu, shot with arrows on all sides in the combat, shone like the bird that returned to its nest.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

स तानि शरवर्षाणि पक्षाभ्यां च विधूयच। चरणाभ्यां महातेजा बभञ्जास्य महद्धनुः।।3.51.14।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महातेजाः powerful, सः Jatayu, तानि those, शरवर्षाणि rain of arrows, पक्षाभ्याम् by the wings, विधूय च on scattering, अस्य his, महत् great, धनुः bow, चरणाभ्याम् with claws, बभञ्ज broke.

#### Explanation

Powerful Jatayu scattered the shower of arrows with his wings and broke down the great bow with his claws.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

तच्चाग्निसदृशं दीप्तं रावणस्य शरावरम्। पक्षाभ्यां स महावीर्यो व्याधुनोत्पतगेश्वरः।।3.51.15।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महावीर्यः mighty, सः Jatayu, पतगेश्वरः lord of the birds, रावणस्य Ravana's, अग्निसदृशम् burning like fire, दीप्तम् glowing, तत् that, शरावरम् armour, पक्षाभ्याम् with the wings, व्याधुनोत् tore asunder.

#### Explanation

Mighty Jatayu, lord of the birds, shattered with his wings the armour of Ravana that was glowing like fire.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

काञ्चनोरश्छदान्दिव्यान्पिशाचवदनान्खरान्। तांश्चास्य जवसम्पन्नाञ्जघान समरे बली।।3.51.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

बली powerful, काञ्चनोरश्छदान् golden shield, दिव्यान् splendid, पिशाचवदनान् devil's face, जवसम्पन्नान् endowed with great speed, तान् those, खरान् च asses, समरे in the combat, जघान killed.

#### Explanation

Powerful Jatayu killed in the combat, the splendid, swiftmoving, devilfaced asses with  golden shields yoked to the chariot.

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

वरं त्रिवेणुसम्पन्नं कामगं पावकार्चिषम्। मणिहेमविचित्राङ्गं बभञ्ज च महारथम्।।3.51.17।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वरम् excellent, त्रिवेणुसम्पन्नम् endowed with three bamboo reeds, कामगम् go wherever one desired, पावकार्चिषम् glowing like fire, मणिहेमविचित्राङ्गम् glittering with gold and gems, महारथम् great chariot, बभञ्ज broke.

#### Explanation

Then he(Jatayu) broke down the great chariot(of Ravana) glowing like fire, glittering with gold and gems, made of three bamboo reeds, and capable of flying wherever the rider willed.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

पूर्णचन्द्रप्रतीकाशं छत्रं च व्यजनैस्सह। पातयामास वेगेन ग्राहिभी राक्षसैस्सह।।3.51.18।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पूर्णचन्द्रप्रतीकाशम् shining like the full moon, छत्रं च parasol also, व्यजनैः सह with  the fans, ग्राहिभिः with those holding them, राक्षसैः सह along with the demons, वेगेन violently, पातयामास pulled down.

#### Explanation

Jatayu violently pulled down the parasol shining like the full moon and the fans of the chariot along with the demons holding them.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

सारथेश्चास्य वेगेन तुण्डेनैव महच्छिरः। पुनर्व्यपाहरच्छ्रीमान्पक्षिराजो महाबलः।।3.51.19।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पुनः again, महाबलः powerful, श्रीमान् glorious, पक्षिराजः king of the birds, अस्य his, सारथेः charioteer, महत् huge, शिरः head, वेगेन तुण्डेनैव violently with the beak, व्यपाहरत् shattered.

#### Explanation

Again the glorious, powerful king of birds swiftly shattered the huge head of the charioteer with his beak.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

स भग्नधन्वा विरथो हताश्वो हतसारथिः। अङ्केनादाय वैदेहीं पपात भुवि रावणः।।3.51.20।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

भग्नधन्वा with the bow broken, विरथः chariot destroyed, हताश्वः asses (drawing the chariot) dead, हतसारथिः charioteer killed, सः रावणः that Ravana, वैदेहीम् Vaidehi, अङ्केन on his lap, आदाय held, भुवि on to the ground, पपात fell.

#### Explanation

With the bow broken, the chariot destroyed, asses (drawing the chariot) and the charioteer dead,  Ravana fell down on the ground holding Vaidehi on his lap.

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

दृष्ट्वा निपतितं भूमौ रावणं भग्नवाहनम्। साधुसाध्विति भूतानि गृध्रराजमपूजयन्।।3.51.21।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

भग्नवाहनम् with the broken chariot, भूमौ on the ground, निपतितम् fallen down, रावणम् Ravana, दृष्ट्वा  seeing, साधु साधु इति staying well done, well done, भूतानि all beings, गृध्रराजम् king of the birds, अपूजयन् honoured.

#### Explanation

Seeing Ravana fallen on the ground with his broken chariot, all beings honoured the king of the birds, saying, Well done Well done

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

परिश्रान्तं तु तं दृष्टवा जरया पक्षियूथपम्। उत्पपात पुनर्हृष्टो मैथिलीं गृह्य रावणः।।3.51.22।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पक्षियूथपम् the lord of the flocks of birds (Jatayu), तम् him, जरया with old age, परिश्रान्तम् exhausted (after a long fight), दृष्ट्वा seeing, रावणः Ravana, हृष्टः glad, मैथिलीम् Maithili, गृह्य took, पुनः again, उत्पपात flew into the sky.

#### Explanation

Seeing the lord of the flocks of birds exhausted due to age still fighting, Ravana felt glad. He flew back into the sky taking Sita, princess of Mithila, with him.

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

तं प्रहृष्टं निधायाङ्के गच्छन्तं जनकात्मजाम्। गृध्रराजस्समुत्पत्य समभिद्रुत्य रावणम्।।3.51.23।। समावार्य महातेजा जटायुरिदमब्रवीत्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

गृध्रराजः king of the vultures, महातेजाः mighty, जटायुः Jatayu, समुत्पत्य flew up.प्रहृष्टम् happy, जनकात्मजाम् Janaka's daughter ( Sita), अङ्के on the lap, निथाय placed, गच्छन्तम् while going, तं रावणम् to that Ravana, समभिद्रुत्य chased, समावार्य obstructing, इदम् this, अब्रवीत् said.

#### Explanation

Seeing Sita, Janaka's daughter, happily carried away into the sky by Ravana on his lap. Jatayu, the mighty king of vultures chased him, and obstructing his path said :

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

वज्रसंस्पर्शबाणस्य भार्यां रामस्य रावण।।3.51.24।। अल्पबुद्धे हरस्येनां वधाय खलु रक्षसाम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अल्पबुद्धे foolish, रावण Ravana, वज्रसंस्पर्शबाणस्य whose arows can hit like a thunderbolt, रामस्य Rama's, भार्याम् wife, एनाम् this lady, रक्षसाम् of demons, वधाय for destruction, हरसि  kidnapping.

#### Explanation

Foolish Ravana, you are kidnapping this lady, wife of Rama, whose arrows can hit like a thunderbolt. This will lead to the destruction of all demons.

---

### Shloka 25

#### Shloka Text

समित्रबन्धुस्सामात्यस्सबलस्सपरिच्छदः।।3.51.25।। विषपानं पिबस्येतत्पिपासित इवोदकम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मित्रबन्धुः along with kith and kin, सामात्यः with ministers, सबलः with army, सपरिच्छदः with all your allies, पिपासितः thirsty man, उदकमिव like water, एतत् this, विषपानम् poison, पिबसि you  are drinking.

#### Explanation

You are drinking poison along with all your friends, relatives, ministers, the army and  your allies. like a thirsty man drinks water.

---

### Shloka 26

#### Shloka Text

अनुबन्धमजानन्तः कर्मणामविचक्षणाः।।3.51.26।। शीघ्रमेव विनश्यन्ति यथा त्वं विनशिष्यसि।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कर्मणाम् of the deeds, अनुबन्धम् consequences, अजानन्तः not knowing, अविचक्षणाः unwise, यथा similarly, श्रीघ्रमेव soon, विनश्यन्ति get destroyed, त्वम् you, विनशिष्यसि will be destroyed.

#### Explanation

Fools who do not know the consequences of their action perish soon. Similarly you are going to die soon.

---

### Shloka 27

#### Shloka Text

बद्धस्त्वं कालपाशेन क्व गतस्तस्य मोक्ष्यसे।।3.51.27।। वधाय बडिशं गृह्य सामिषं जलजो यथा।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

त्वम् you, कालपाशेन by the noose of death, वधाय to die, बद्धः are bound, सामिषम् with a bait, बडिशम् fishhook, गृह्य having taken, जलजः fish, यथा so also, तस्य its, क्व गतः where can it go, मोक्ष्यसे will you get out.

#### Explanation

You are bound by the noose of death like a fish holding on to the bait of the fishing hook. Where can you go now?

---

### Shloka 28

#### Shloka Text

न हि जातु दुराधर्षौ काकुत्स्थौ तव रावण।।3.51.28।। धर्षणं चाश्रमस्यास्य क्षमिष्येते तु राघवौ।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रावण Ravana, दुराधर्षौ both invincible, काकुत्स्थौ both Kakutsthas, राघवौ Rama and Lakshmana, तव your, अस्य आश्रमस्य their hermitage, धर्षणम् assault, जातु surely, न क्षमिष्येते not forgive.

#### Explanation

O Ravana  know that Rama and Lakshmana, the Kakutsthas, are invincible. They will not forgive the asssault you have made on their hermitage.

---

### Shloka 29

#### Shloka Text

यथा त्वया कृतं कर्म भीरुणा लोकगर्हितम्।।3.51.29।। तस्कराचरितो मार्गो नैव वीरनिषेवितः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

भीरुणा by a timid, त्वया you are, यथा like this, लोकगर्हितम् condemned by the whole world, कर्म  act, कृतम् is done, तस्कराचरितः adopted by thieves, मार्गः this path, वीरनिषेवितः followed by heroes, नैव not at all.

#### Explanation

Your cowardly action will be condemned by the whole world. The path you have chosen is the path of a thief and not of a hero.

---

### Shloka 30

#### Shloka Text

युध्यस्व यदि शूरोऽसि मुहूर्तं तिष्ठरावण।।3.51.30।। शयिष्यसे हतो भूमौ यथा भ्राता खरस्तथा।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रावण Ravana, यदि if you, शूरोऽसि are courageous, युध्यस्व may fight, मुहूर्तं for a moment, तिष्ठ stay, भ्राता brother, खर: Khara, तथा likewise, शयिष्यसे all down, यथा as, भूमौ on the ground, हत: killed.

#### Explanation

O Ravana if you are courageous, tarry awhile and fight with me. Like your brother Khara, you will lie on the ground dead.

---

### Shloka 31

#### Shloka Text

परेतकाले पुरुषो यत्कर्म प्रतिपद्यते।।3.51.31।। विनाशायात्मनोऽधर्म्यं प्रतिपन्नोऽसि कर्म तत्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पुरुषः man, परेतकाले at the time of death, यत् whatever, कर्म actions, प्रतिपद्यते does, अधर्म्यम् unrighteous, तत् such, कर्म action, आत्मनः yourself, विनाशाय for destruction, प्रतिपन्नः  undertaken, असि you are.

#### Explanation

You have resorted to unrighteous action like a dying man. This will spell your destruction.

---

### Shloka 32

#### Shloka Text

पापानुबन्धो वै यस्य कर्मणः कर्मको नु तत्।।3.51.32।। कुर्वीत लोकाधिपतिस्स्वयंभूर्भगवानपि।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

यस्य by whomsoever, कर्मणः action, पापानुबन्धः resulting in sin, तत् such, कर्म action, लोकाधिपतिः lord of the world, भगवान् स्वयंभूरपि selfborn lord (Brahma) too, कः who, कुर्वीत may do, नु indeed, तत् that.

#### Explanation

If the consequences of an action be sin, who will do it ? Even the selfborn lord of the world may not.

---

### Shloka 33

#### Shloka Text

एवमुक्त्वा शुभं वाक्यं जटायुस्तस्य रक्षसः।।3.51.33।। निपपात भृशं पृष्ठे दशग्रीवस्य वीर्यवान्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वीर्यवान् brave,जटायुः Jatayu, एवम् in that manner, शुभम् auspicious (cautious), वाक्यम् words, उक्त्वा having  said, रक्षसः of that demon, तस्य रावणस्य पृष्ठे at Ravana's back, भृशम् repeatedly, निपपात dashed.

#### Explanation

Brave Jatayu having thus said these cautious words to the demon dashed against his back again and again.

---

### Shloka 34

#### Shloka Text

तं गृहीत्वा नखैस्तीक्ष्णैर्विददार समन्ततः।।3.51.34।। अधिरूढो गजारोहो यथा स्याद्दुष्टवारणम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

(ततः thereafter), तं गृहीत्वा seizing him, अधिरूढः ascended, गजारोहः riding on an elephant, दुष्टवारणम् unruly elephant, यथा so also, स्यात् he, तीक्ष्णैः sharp, नखैः claws, समन्ततः all over, विददार pierced.

#### Explanation

Thereafter, seizing Ravana, Jatayu ascended on his back and pierced his body all over with his sharp claws just as a rider goads an unruly elephant.

---

### Shloka 35

#### Shloka Text

विददार नखैरस्य तुण्डं पृष्ठे समर्पयन्।।3.51.35।। केशांश्चोत्पाटयामास नखपक्षमुखायुधः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अस्य his, पृष्ठे back, तुण्डम् beak, समर्पयन् pressing, नखैः claws, विददार pierced, नखपक्षमुखायुधः a warrior with claws, wings and beak as the only weapons, केशांश्च hair too, उत्पाटयामास pulled out.

#### Explanation

Jatayu who had  his claws, wings and beak as the only weapons  pierced and pricked  Ravana all over with his nails by pressing his beak on his back and pulled out his hair.

---

### Shloka 36

#### Shloka Text

स तथा गृध्रराजेन क्लिश्यमानो मुहुर्मुहुः।।3.51.36।। अमर्षस्फुरितोष्ठस्सन्प्राकम्पत च रावणः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

गृध्रराजेन by the king of vultures, मुहुर्मुहुः again and again, तथा in that manner, क्लिश्यमानः being tormented, सः रावणः he, Ravana, अमर्षफुरितोष्ठः सन् with his lips quivering with anger, प्राकम्पत was shaken.

#### Explanation

Thus tormented again and again by the king of vultures, Ravana shook with anger, his lips quivering.

---

### Shloka 37

#### Shloka Text

स परिष्वज्य च वैदेहीं वामेनाङ्केन रावणः।।3.51.37।। तलेनाभिजघानाशु जटायुं क्रोधमुर्छीतः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रावणः Ravana, क्रोधमूर्छितः losing senses with rage, वामेन अङ्केन left arm, वैदेहीम् Vaidehi, परिष्वज्य holding firmly, आशु quickly, जटायुम् to Jatayu, तलेन palm, अभिजघान hit him.

#### Explanation

Losing his senses, with rage, Ravana held Vaidehi (Sita) with his left arm and hit Jatayu with his palm.

---

### Shloka 38

#### Shloka Text

जटायुस्तमभिक्रम्य तुण्डेनास्य खगाधिपः।।3.51.38।। वामबाहून्दश तदा व्यपाहरदरिन्दमः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तदा then, खगाधिपः lord of the birds, अरिन्दमः  subduer of enemies, जटायुः Jatayu, तम् him, अभिक्रम्य  occupied, तुण्डेन by his beak, अस्य Ravana's, दश ten, वामबाहून् left arms, व्यपाहरत् cut off.

#### Explanation

Then Jatayu, lord of the birds, subduer of enemies, overpowered Ravana and dismembered his ten left arms with his beak.

---

### Shloka 39

#### Shloka Text

सञ्छिन्नबाहोस्सद्यैव बाहवस्सहसाभवन्।।3.51.39।। विषज्वालावलीयुक्ता वल्मीकादिव पन्नगाः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सञ्छिन्नबाहोः whose arms were cut off, बाहवः arms, सद्यैव instantly from them, वल्मीकात् from an anthill, विषज्वालाः poisonous flames, वलीयुक्ताः emitting, पन्नगाः इव like serpants, सहसा all of a sudden, अभवन् came out.

#### Explanation

As the left arms were cut off, there came out from them all the ten arms instantaneously like serpents emitting poisonous flames from anthills

---

### Shloka 40

#### Shloka Text

ततः क्रोधाद्दशग्रीवस्सीतामुत्सृज्य रावणः।।3.51.40।। मुष्टिभ्यां चरणाभ्यां च गृध्रराजमपोथयत्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, दशग्रीवः tenheaded, रावणः Ravana, सीताम् Sita, उत्सृज्य left, मुष्टिभ्याम् with his fists, चरणाभ्यां च and feet, गृध्रराजम् Jatayu, क्रोधात् with anger, अपोथयत् attacked.

#### Explanation

Then the tenheaded Ravana left Sita and attacked Jatayu with his fists and feet out of anger.

---

### Shloka 41

#### Shloka Text

ततो मुहूर्तं सङ्ग्रामो बभूवातुल्यवीर्ययोः।।3.51.41।। राक्षसानां च मुख्यस्य पक्षिणां प्रवरस्य च।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, अतुलवीर्ययो: of unequal valour, राक्षसानां मुख्यस्य of the chief of the demons, पक्षिणां प्रवरस्य king of the birds, मुहूर्तम् for a moment, सङ्ग्रामः fight, बभूव took place.

#### Explanation

Between the warriors of unequal valour, Ravana and Jatayu, the chief of the demons and the foremost of the birds, the fight took place for a moment.

---

### Shloka 42

#### Shloka Text

तस्य व्यायच्छमानस्य रामस्यार्थे स रावणः।।3.51.42।। पक्षौ पार्श्वौ च पादौ च खड्गमुद्धृत्य सोऽच्छिनत्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः that, रावणः Ravana, खडगम् sword, उद्यम्य after lifting, रामस्य Rama's, अर्थे for the sake of, व्यायच्छमानस्य of one who was fighting relentlessly, तस्य his, पक्षौ two wings, पार्श्वौ च  both sides, पादौ च feet, अच्छिनत् cut off.

#### Explanation

Ravana lifted his sword and cut off both the wings and feet of Jatayu who was fighting relentlessly (for Rama's sake).

---

### Shloka 43

#### Shloka Text

स च्छिन्नपक्षस्सहसा रक्षसा रौद्रकर्मणा।।3.51.43।। निपपात हतो गृद्रो धरण्यामल्पजीवितः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रौद्रकर्मणा one who performs terrible deeds, रक्षसा by the demon, छिन्नपक्षः clipped wings, सः that, गृध्रः vulture, हतः hurt, अल्पजीवितः with very little life left, धरण्याम् on the ground, सहसा immediately, निपपात fell.

#### Explanation

(Seriously) wounded and almost dead,the vulture fell down on the ground all at once, when his wings were chipped by the demon of terrible deeds.

---

### Shloka 44

#### Shloka Text

तं दृष्ट्वा पतितं भूमौ क्षतजार्द्रं जटायुषम्।।3.51.44।। अभ्यधावत वैदेही स्वबन्धुमिव दुःखिता।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वैदेही Sita, princess of Videha, भूमौ on the ground, पतितम् fallen down, क्षतजार्द्रम् drenched in blood , तं जटायुषम् that Jatayu, दृष्ट्वा  seeing, दुःखिता grieved, स्वबन्धुमिव as if he was her relative, अभ्यधावत ran towards him.

#### Explanation

Sita, princess of Videha, saw the wounded Jatayu fallen on the ground drenched in blood. Grieving, she ran towards him as if he was her relative.

---

### Shloka 45

#### Shloka Text

तं नीलजीमूतनिकाशकल्पं सुपाण्डुरोरस्कमुदारवीर्यम्। ददर्श लङ्काधिपतिः पृथिव्यां जटायुषं शान्तमिवाग्निदावम्।।3.51.45।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लङ्काधिपतिः lord of Lanka, नीलजीमूतनिकाशकायम् of colour like the dark bluish cloud, सुपाण्डुरोरस्कम् of fair chest, उदारवीर्यम् bold, शान्तम् made calm, अग्निदावमिव like the forest fire, तं जटायुषम् that Jatayu, पृथिव्याम् on the ground, ददर्श saw.

#### Explanation

Lord of Lanka saw that very bold Jatayu of dark blue colour of the cloud, fair chest, fallen on the ground, looking like the forest fire that had been put out.

---

### Shloka 46

#### Shloka Text

ततस्तु तं पत्ररथं महीतले निपातितं रावणवेगमर्दितम्। पुनः परिश्वज्य शशिप्रभानना रुरोद सीता जनकात्मजातदा।।3.51.46।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, शशिप्रभानना with her face like the radiant moon, जनकात्मजा Janaka's daughter, सीता Sita, तां him, रावणवेगमर्थितम् crushed by Ravana's onslaught, महीतले on the ground, निपातितम् fallen, तं पत्ररथम् that bird who rides on wings, पुनः again, परिष्वज्य took hold of him, रुरोद wept.

#### Explanation

Sita, daughter of Janaka, whose face was radiant like the Moon once again embraced  Jatayu, the winged warrior, fallen on the ground, crushed by Ravana's onslaught and wept. इतयार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये अरण्यकाण्डे एकपञ्चाशस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the fiftyfirst sarga of Aranyakanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 52

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

सा तु ताराधिपमुखी रावणेन समीक्ष्य तम्। गृध्रराजं विनिहतं विललाप सुदुःखिता।।3.52.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ताराधिपमुखी  with Moonlike face, सा तु that Sita, रावणेन by Ravana, विनिहतम् struck down, तं गृध्रराजम् king of the birds, समीक्ष्य  seeing, सुदुःखिता very  much agonised, विललाप wept.

#### Explanation

Sita,whose face was like the lord of the stars (Moon), saw Jatayu, king of birds, struck down by Ravana, and lamented in extreme grif :

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

निमित्तं लक्षणज्ञानं शकुनिस्वरदर्शनम्। अवश्यं सुखदुःखेषु नराणां प्रतिदृश्यते।।3.52.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लक्षणज्ञानम् prognostication, शकुनिस्वरदर्शनम् sounds of ominous birds, नराणाम् for people, सुखदुःखेषु in joy and sorrow, अवश्यम् certainly, निमित्तम् indications, प्रतिदृश्यते are perceived.

#### Explanation

People, for sure, foresee weal and woe in life from auspicious symptoms and ominous sounds.

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

नूनं राम न जानासि महद्व्यसनमात्मनः। धावन्ति नूनं काकुत्स्थं मदर्थं मृगपक्षिणः।।3.52.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राम Rama, आत्मनः your, महत् great, व्यसनम् disaster, न जानासि not aware, नूनम् surely, मदर्थम् for my sake, मृगपक्षिणः animals and birds, काकुत्स्थम् towards Kakutstha, धावन्ति running to tell you, नूनम् surely.

#### Explanation

O Rama you are, indeed, unaware of the great disaster that has befallen you. All the animals and birds are running for my sake to tell you about me, O scion  of the Kakutsthas.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

अयं हि पापचारेण मां त्रातुमभिसङ्गतः। शेते विनिहतो भूमौ ममाभाग्याद्विहंगमः।।3.52.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

माम् me, त्रातुम् to rescue, अभिसङ्गतः has come forward, अयम् his, विहङ्गमः bird, मम for me, अभाग्यात् due to bad luck, पापचारेण by the sinner, विनिहतः is hit down, भूमौ on the ground, शेते lies down.

#### Explanation

'This bird that came forward to save me is hit by the sinner(Ravana) and lies down on the ground, as ill luck would have it.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

त्राहि मामद्य काकुत्स्थ लक्ष्मणेति वराङ्गना। सुसन्त्रस्ता समाक्रन्दच्छृण्वतां तु यथान्तिके।।3.52.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वराङ्गना beautiful lady, सुसन्त्रस्ता terribly frightened, काकुत्स्थ O Rama, लक्ष्मण O Lakshmana, अद्य and now, माम् me, त्राहि save, इति thus, अन्तिके close by, शृण्वतां यथा as if listening to her, समाक्रन्दत् cried bitterly.

#### Explanation

Terribly frightened, the beautiful lady (Sita) cried bitterly, 'O Rama, O Lakshmana, save me', as if they were close by and were listening to her.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

तां क्लिष्टमाल्याभरणां विलपन्तीमनाथवत्। अभ्यधावत् वैदेहीं रावणो राक्षसाधिपः।।3.52.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

क्लिष्टमाल्याभरणाम् lady whose garlands were crushed and jewels let loose, अनाथवत् like orphan, विलपन्तीम् weeping lady, तां वैदेहीम् that Vaidehi, राक्षसाधिपः demon king, रावणः Ravana, अभ्यधावत ran towards.

#### Explanation

Ravana, king of the demons, ran towards Sita who was weeping like an orphan with her garlands and ornaments crushed.

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

तां लतामिव वेष्टन्तीमालिङ्गन्तीं महाद्रुमान्। मुञ्च मुञ्चेति बहुशः प्रवदन्राक्षसाधिपः।।3.52.7।। क्रोशन्तीं राम रामेति रामेण रहितां वने। जीवितान्ताय केशेषु जग्राहान्तकसन्निभः।।3.52.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अन्तकसन्निभः like Yama, lord of death, राक्षसाधिपः lord of the demons, लतामिव like a creeper, महाद्रुमान् huge trees, वेष्टन्तीम् coiled  आलिङ्गन्तीम् embracing, वने in the forest, रामेण by Rama, रहिताम् separated from, राम रामेति saying 'Rama, Rama', क्रोशन्तीम् lamenting, ताम् her, मुञ्च मुञ्च इति leave it, leave it, बहुशः several times, प्रवदन् uttering, जीवितान्ताय to bring an end to his life, केशेषु by her hair, जग्राह caught hold.

#### Explanation

Separated from Rama in the forest, Sita hugged huge trees, running round and round like a creeper coiled, crying loudly, 'Rama, O Rama' . The king of the demons, like Yama, caught hold of her hair only to invite his death, all the time saying, Leave it, Leave it  (Leave the tree).

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

तां लतामिव वेष्टन्तीमालिङ्गन्तीं महाद्रुमान्। मुञ्च मुञ्चेति बहुशः प्रवदन्राक्षसाधिपः।।3.52.7।। क्रोशन्तीं राम रामेति रामेण रहितां वने। जीवितान्ताय केशेषु जग्राहान्तकसन्निभः।।3.52.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अन्तकसन्निभः like Yama, lord of death, राक्षसाधिपः lord of the demons, लतामिव like a creeper, महाद्रुमान् huge trees, वेष्टन्तीम् coiled  आलिङ्गन्तीम् embracing, वने in the forest, रामेण by Rama, रहिताम् separated from, राम रामेति saying 'Rama, Rama', क्रोशन्तीम् lamenting, ताम् her, मुञ्च मुञ्च इति leave it, leave it, बहुशः several times, प्रवदन् uttering, जीवितान्ताय to bring an end to his life, केशेषु by her hair, जग्राह caught hold.

#### Explanation

Separated from Rama in the forest, Sita hugged huge trees, running round and round like a creeper coiled, crying loudly, 'Rama, O Rama' . The king of the demons, like Yama, caught hold of her hair only to invite his death, all the time saying, Leave it, Leave it  (Leave the tree).

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

प्रधर्षितायां सीतायां बभूव सचराचरम्। जगत्सर्वममर्यादं तमसान्धेन संवृतम्।।3.52.9।। न वाति मारुतस्तत्र निष्प्रभोऽभूद्दिवाकरः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सीतायाम् when Sita, प्रधर्षितायाम् was assaulted, सचराचरम् with all beings animate and inanimate, सर्वम् all, जगत् world, अमर्यादम् crossed all moral law, अन्धेन तमसा  by pitch darkness, संवृतम्  enveloped, बभूव became, तत्र then, मारुतः wind, न वाति did not blow, दिवाकरः the Sun, निष्प्रभः lustreless, अभूत् became.

#### Explanation

While Sita was being assaulted, the earth with the animate and the inanimate crossed all limits of moral law and became enveloped in blinding darkness. The wind  ceased to blow and the Sun, to shine.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

दृष्ट्वा सीतां परामृष्टां दीनां दिव्येन चक्षुषा।।3.52.10।। कृतं कार्यमिति श्रीमान्व्याजहार पितामहः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation
