श्रीमान् glorious, पितामहः grandfather( Brahma), दीनाम् a desperate woman, सीताम् Sita, परामृष्टाम् assaulted by the enemy, दिव्येन divine, चक्षुषा eyes, दृष्ट्वा saw, कार्यम् the task, कृतम् is accomplished, इति thus, व्याजहार said.

#### Explanation

Brahma, the glorious Grandfather, saw with his divine eyes, Sita in a desperate state caught by the enemy and said, 'The task is accomplished.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

प्रहृष्टा व्यथिताश्चासन्सर्वे ते परमर्षयः।।3.52.11।। दृष्ट्वा सीतां परामृष्टां दण्डकारण्यवासिनः। रावणस्य विनाशं च प्राप्तं बुद्ध्वा यदृच्छया।।3.52.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

दण्डकारण्यवासिनः residents of Dandaka forest, सर्वे all, ते those, परमर्षयः great seers, सीताम् Sita, परामृष्टाम्  abducted, दृष्ट्वा  seeing, यदृच्छया that which happened naturally, रावणस्य Ravana's, विनाशं च destruction, प्राप्तम् has come, बुद्ध्वा knowing, प्रहृष्टाः happy, व्यथिताश्च distressed, आसन् became.

#### Explanation

All the great seers residing in Dandaka forest felt both happy and sad, happy because the destruction of Ravana is at hand and sad because Sita was in distress.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

प्रहृष्टा व्यथिताश्चासन्सर्वे ते परमर्षयः।।3.52.11।। दृष्ट्वा सीतां परामृष्टां दण्डकारण्यवासिनः। रावणस्य विनाशं च प्राप्तं बुद्ध्वा यदृच्छया।।3.52.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

दण्डकारण्यवासिनः residents of Dandaka forest, सर्वे all, ते those, परमर्षयः great seers, सीताम् Sita, परामृष्टाम्  abducted, दृष्ट्वा  seeing, यदृच्छया that which happened naturally, रावणस्य Ravana's, विनाशं च destruction, प्राप्तम् has come, बुद्ध्वा knowing, प्रहृष्टाः happy, व्यथिताश्च distressed, आसन् became.

#### Explanation

All the great seers residing in Dandaka forest felt both happy and sad, happy because the destruction of Ravana is at hand and sad because Sita was in distress.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

स तु तां राम रामेति रुदन्तीं लक्ष्मणेति च। जगामादाय चाकाशं रावणो राक्षसाधिपः।।3.52.13।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षसाधिपः lord of the demons, सः रावणः that Ravana, राम रामेति saying, Rama, O Rama, लक्ष्मणेति च O Lakshmana, रुदन्तीम् crying, ताम् her, आदाय taking her, आकाशम् into the  sky, जगाम went.

#### Explanation

While Sita was crying, 'O Rama, O Lakshmana', Ravana, lord of the demons, carried her back into the sky.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

तप्ताभरणजुष्टाङ्गी पीतकौशेयवासिनी। रराजराजपुत्री तुविद्युत्सौदामिनी यथा।।3.52.14।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तप्ताभरणजुष्टाङ्गी lady wearing on her limbs pure gold ornaments, पीतकौशेयवासिनी lady clad in yelow silk, राजपुत्री princess, विद्युत् सौदामिनी यथा like lightning, रराज shone.

#### Explanation

SIta who had put on pure gold ornaments on her body and who was clad in yellow silk  shone like lightning.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

उद्धूतेन च वस्त्रेण तस्याः पीतेन रावणः। अधिकं परिबभ्राज गिरिर्दीप्त इवाग्निना।।3.52.15।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तस्याः her, उद्धूतेन blown, पीतेन yellow, वस्त्रेण clothes, रावणः Ravana, अग्निना with fire, दीप्तः glowing, गिरिरिव like a mountain अधिकम् more, परिबभ्राज shone.

#### Explanation

Ravana was shining all the more with(her) yellow clothes blowing in the air, and looked like a mountain shining with fire burning around.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

तस्याः परमकल्याण्यास्ताम्राणि सुरभीणि च। पद्मपत्राणि वैदेह्या अभ्यकीर्यन्त रावणम्।।3.52.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

परमकल्याण्याः of the most auspicious, तस्याः her, वैदेह्याः Vaidehi's, ताम्राणि copper  coloured, सुरभीणि fragrant, पद्मपत्राणि lotus petals, रावणम् Ravana, अभ्यकीर्यन्त were showered on him.

#### Explanation

The red lotus petals worn by the most auspicious Sita, princess of Videha, were showered on Ravana.

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

तस्याः कौशेयमुद्धूतमाकाशे कनकप्रभम्। बभौ चादित्यरागेण ताम्रमभ्रमिवातपे।।3.52.17।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तस्याः her, आकाशे in the sky, उद्धूतम् blown by the wind, कनकप्रभम् goldenhued, कौशेयम् silken robes, आतपे exposed to the evening Sun , आदित्यरागेण by the Sun's redness, ताम्रम् red, अभ्रमिव cloudlike, बभौ appeared.

#### Explanation

As the goldencoloured garment worn by Sita blew in the wind, it looked like a coppercoloured cloud illumined by the evening Sun in the sky.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

तस्यास्तत्सुनसं वक्त्रमाकाशे रावणाङ्कगम्। न रराज विना रामं विनालमिव पङ्कजम्।।3.52.18।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

आकाशे in the sky, रावणाङ्कगम् in the lap of Ravana, सुनसम् with a beautiful nose, तस्याः वक्त्रम् her face, रामं विना without Rama, विनालम् without stalk, पङ्कजम् इव like lotus, न रराज did not shine.

#### Explanation

Sita's face with her beautiful nose on Ravana's lap and not on Rama's, shone no more like a lotus without its stalk.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

बभूव जलदं नीलं भित्त्वा चन्द्र इवोदितः। सुललाटं सुकेशान्तं पद्मगर्भाभमव्रणम्।।3.52.19।। शुक्लैस्सुविमलैर्दन्तै प्रभावद्भिरलङ्कृतम्। तस्यास्तद्विमलं वक्त्रमाकाशे रावणाङ्कगम्।।3.52.20।। रुदितं व्यपमृष्टास्रं चन्द्रवत्प्रियदर्शनम्। सुनासं चारुताम्रोष्ठमाकाशे हाटकप्रभम्।।3.52.21।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सुललाटम् a fine forehead, सुकेशान्तम् beautiful hair, पद्मगर्भाभम्  the glow of the inner side of a fully blossomed lotus, अव्रणम् whose face had no marks, शुक्लैः white, सुविमलैः  clean, प्रभावद्भि: shining, दन्तैः teeth, अलङ्कृतम् decorated, रुदितम् as she wept, व्यपमृष्टास्रम् wiped her tears, सुनासम् of nice nose, चारुताम्रोष्ठम् with lovely red lips, आकाशे in the sky, हाटकप्रभम् having golden glow, रावणाङ्कगम् held by Ravana, तस्याः her, तत् that, विमलम् pure, वक्त्रम् face, नीलम् dark blue, जलदम् rain cloud, भित्त्वा breaking through, उदितः risen, चन्द्र इव like Moon, बभूव appeared.

#### Explanation

Sita's face looked beautiful with her smooth forehead, shining hair and spotless complexion like the inside of a lotus, with clean, white, shining teeth, a nice nose and lovely red lips. She was continuously weeping and wiping her tears. Her face which was pleasing like the Moon on the lap of Ravana appeared as though the Moon was rising through dark clouds.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

बभूव जलदं नीलं भित्त्वा चन्द्र इवोदितः। सुललाटं सुकेशान्तं पद्मगर्भाभमव्रणम्।।3.52.19।। शुक्लैस्सुविमलैर्दन्तै प्रभावद्भिरलङ्कृतम्। तस्यास्तद्विमलं वक्त्रमाकाशे रावणाङ्कगम्।।3.52.20।। रुदितं व्यपमृष्टास्रं चन्द्रवत्प्रियदर्शनम्। सुनासं चारुताम्रोष्ठमाकाशे हाटकप्रभम्।।3.52.21।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सुललाटम् a fine forehead, सुकेशान्तम् beautiful hair, पद्मगर्भाभम्  the glow of the inner side of a fully blossomed lotus, अव्रणम् whose face had no marks, शुक्लैः white, सुविमलैः  clean, प्रभावद्भि: shining, दन्तैः teeth, अलङ्कृतम् decorated, रुदितम् as she wept, व्यपमृष्टास्रम् wiped her tears, सुनासम् of nice nose, चारुताम्रोष्ठम् with lovely red lips, आकाशे in the sky, हाटकप्रभम् having golden glow, रावणाङ्कगम् held by Ravana, तस्याः her, तत् that, विमलम् pure, वक्त्रम् face, नीलम् dark blue, जलदम् rain cloud, भित्त्वा breaking through, उदितः risen, चन्द्र इव like Moon, बभूव appeared.

#### Explanation

Sita's face looked beautiful with her smooth forehead, shining hair and spotless complexion like the inside of a lotus, with clean, white, shining teeth, a nice nose and lovely red lips. She was continuously weeping and wiping her tears. Her face which was pleasing like the Moon on the lap of Ravana appeared as though the Moon was rising through dark clouds.

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

बभूव जलदं नीलं भित्त्वा चन्द्र इवोदितः। सुललाटं सुकेशान्तं पद्मगर्भाभमव्रणम्।।3.52.19।। शुक्लैस्सुविमलैर्दन्तै प्रभावद्भिरलङ्कृतम्। तस्यास्तद्विमलं वक्त्रमाकाशे रावणाङ्कगम्।।3.52.20।। रुदितं व्यपमृष्टास्रं चन्द्रवत्प्रियदर्शनम्। सुनासं चारुताम्रोष्ठमाकाशे हाटकप्रभम्।।3.52.21।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सुललाटम् a fine forehead, सुकेशान्तम् beautiful hair, पद्मगर्भाभम्  the glow of the inner side of a fully blossomed lotus, अव्रणम् whose face had no marks, शुक्लैः white, सुविमलैः  clean, प्रभावद्भि: shining, दन्तैः teeth, अलङ्कृतम् decorated, रुदितम् as she wept, व्यपमृष्टास्रम् wiped her tears, सुनासम् of nice nose, चारुताम्रोष्ठम् with lovely red lips, आकाशे in the sky, हाटकप्रभम् having golden glow, रावणाङ्कगम् held by Ravana, तस्याः her, तत् that, विमलम् pure, वक्त्रम् face, नीलम् dark blue, जलदम् rain cloud, भित्त्वा breaking through, उदितः risen, चन्द्र इव like Moon, बभूव appeared.

#### Explanation

Sita's face looked beautiful with her smooth forehead, shining hair and spotless complexion like the inside of a lotus, with clean, white, shining teeth, a nice nose and lovely red lips. She was continuously weeping and wiping her tears. Her face which was pleasing like the Moon on the lap of Ravana appeared as though the Moon was rising through dark clouds.

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

राक्षसेन समाधूतं तस्यास्तद्वदनं शुभम्। शुशुभे न विना रामंदिवा चन्द्र इवोदितः।।3.52.22।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षसेन by the demon, समाधूतम् being shaken, तस्याः her, शुभम् auspicious, तत् that, वदनम् face, रामं विना without Rama, दिवा by daytime, उदितः arisen, चन्द्रः इव Moon like, न शुशुभे did not glow.

#### Explanation

Shaken up by the demon and deprived of Rama's company, Sita's beautiful face looked like the Moon during daytime without any glow.

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

सा हेमवर्णा नीलाङ्गं मैथिली राक्षसाधिपम्। शुशुभे काञ्चनी काञ्ची नीलं मणिमिवाश्रिता।।3.52.23।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

नीलाङ्गम् dark body, राक्षसाधिपम् lord of the demons, आश्रिता was held, हेमवर्णा golden complexioned, सा मैथिली that Maithili, नीलम् मणिम् sapphire, काञ्चनी golden, काञ्ची इव like a girdle, शुशुभे shone.

#### Explanation

Goldencomplexioned Sita, princess of Mithila, held by the darkcoloured king of the demons, shone like a girdle resting on a dark blue sapphire.

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

सा पद्मगौरी हेमाभा रावणं जनकात्मजा। विद्युद्घनमिवाविश्य शुशुभे तप्तभूषणा।।3.52.24।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पद्मगौरी a lady like a bright lotus, हेमाभा of golden colour, तप्तभूषणा with bright gold ornaments, सा जनकात्मजा  that Janaka's daughter, रावणम् to Ravana, आविश्य clinging, घनम् dark cloud, आविश्य held by, विद्युदिव like lightning, शुशुभे shone bright.

#### Explanation

Bright like a golden lotus, adorned with gold ornaments, Sita, the daughter of Janaka, held by Ravana, looked like a streak of lightning shining bright in a dark cloud.

---

### Shloka 25

#### Shloka Text

तस्या भूषणघोषेण वैदेह्या राक्षसाधिपः। बभौ सचपलो नीलस्सघोष इव तोयदः।।3.52.25।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षसाधिपः the lord of the demons, तस्याः वैदेह्याः that Vaidehi's, भूषणघोषेण by the tinkling ornaments, सचपलः along with lightning, सघोषः roaring, नीलः dark blue, तोयदः इव like raincloud, बभौ seemed.

#### Explanation

The lord of the demons by the side of Sita, princess of Videha with her jingling ornaments looked like the darkblue raincloud with its lightning and thunder.

---

### Shloka 26

#### Shloka Text

उत्तमाङ्गाच्च्युता तस्याः पुष्पवृष्टिस्समन्ततः। सीताया ह्रिममाणायाः पपात धरणीतले।।3.52.26।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ह्रियमाणायाः as she was being carried away, तस्याः सीतायाः that Sita's, उत्तमाङ्गात् from the head, च्युताः dropped, पुष्पवृष्टिः rain of flowers, समन्ततः all over, धरणीतले on the earth, पपात fell down.

#### Explanation

As Sita was being carried away, the shower of flowers dropped from her head and scattered on the earth.

---

### Shloka 27

#### Shloka Text

सा तु रावणवेगेन पुष्पवृष्टिः समन्ततः। समाधूता दशग्रीवं पुनरेवाभ्यवर्तत।।3.52.27।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

समन्ततः all over, सा पुष्पवृष्टिः that shower of flowers, रावणवेगेन by the speed of Ravana, समाधूता swirling together, पुनः again, दशग्रीवमेव on to the tenheaded Ravana only, अभ्यवर्तत went towards.

#### Explanation

The shower of flowers that fell flew towards the tenheaded Ravana again in the swirling motion (of the chariot).

---

### Shloka 28

#### Shloka Text

अभ्यवर्तत पुष्पाणां धारा वैश्रवणानुजम्। नक्षत्रमाला विमला मेरुं नगमिवोन्नतम्।।3.52.28।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पुष्पाणाम् of the flowers, धारा flow, विमला clean, नक्षत्रमाला rows of stars, उन्नतम् high, मेरुं नगमिव mount Meru, वैश्रवणानुजम् on the brother of Vaisravana, अभ्यवर्तत went towards.

#### Explanation

The shower of flowers that dropped went round towards Ravana, brother of Vaisravana, like the bright garland of stars move towards mount Meru.

---

### Shloka 29

#### Shloka Text

चरणान्नूपुरं भ्रष्टं वैदेह्या रत्नभूषितम्। विद्युन्मण्डलसङ्काशं पपात मधुरस्वनम्।।3.52.29।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वैदेह्याः Vaidehi's, चरणात् from her feet, भ्रष्टम् dropped, रत्नभूषितम् ornament studded with gems, नूपुरम् anklet, विद्युन्मण्डलसङ्काशम् looking like an orb of lightning, मधुरस्वनम् making gentle sound, पपात fell down.

#### Explanation

The gemstudded anklet of Sita, looking like an orb of lightning, dropped with a gentle sound.

---

### Shloka 30

#### Shloka Text

तरुप्रवालरक्ता सा नीलाङ्गं राक्षसेश्वरम्। प्राशोभयत वैदेही गजं कक्ष्येव काञ्चनी।।3.52.30।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तरुप्रवालरक्ता red like tender leaves, सा वैदेही that Vaidehi, नीलाङ्गम् dark coloured, राक्षसेश्वरम् Ravana, काञ्चनी golden, कक्ष्ये binding chain, गजमिव like an elephant, प्राशोभयत made him glow.

#### Explanation

With her golden girdle, Sita whose complexion resembled tender leaves looked like a golden chain to bind an elephant with. (Here Ravana is the elephant).

---

### Shloka 31

#### Shloka Text

तां महोल्कामिवाकाशे दीप्यमानां स्वतेजसा। जहाराऽकाशमाविस्य सीतां वैश्रवणानुजः।।3.52.31।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वैश्रवणानुजः brother of Vaisravana, महोल्कामिव  like a massive comet, स्वतेजसा in her natural radiance, आकाशे in the sky, दीप्यमानाम् glowing, तां सीताम् Sita, आकाशम् into the sky, आविश्य having entered, जहार carried.

#### Explanation

The younger brother of Vaisravana (Ravana), like a massive comet, carried Sita, who was glowing in the radiance of her own beauty, and entered the sky.

---

### Shloka 32

#### Shloka Text

तस्यास्तान्यग्निवर्णानि भूषणानि महीतले। सघोषाण्यवकीर्यन्त क्षीणास्तारा इवाम्बरात्।।3.52.32।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तस्याः her, अग्निवर्णानि bright as flame, तानि भूषणानि those ornaments, सघोषाणि tinkling, अम्बरात् from the sky, क्षीणाः dim, ताराः इव like the stars, महीतले on the ground, अवकीर्यन्त  scattered.

#### Explanation

Like the dim stars falling from the sky, her ornaments, bright as flame, scattered on the groundtinkling.

---

### Shloka 33

#### Shloka Text

तस्यास्स्तनान्तराद्भ्रष्टो हारस्ताराधिपद्युतिः। वैदेह्या निपतन्भाति गङ्गेव गगनाच्च्युता।।3.52.33।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तस्याः वैदेह्याः Vaidehi's, स्तनान्तरात् from between the breasts, भ्रष्टः fell down, ताराधिपद्युतिः shining like the Moon, हारः necklace, निपतन् fell, गगनात् from the sky, च्युता dropped, गङ्गेव like river Ganga, भाति seemed.

#### Explanation

Sita's necklace shining like the bright Moon slipped from between her breasts like the river Ganga falling down from the sky.

---

### Shloka 34

#### Shloka Text

उत्पातवाताभिहता नानाद्विजगणायुताः। माभैरिति विधूताग्रा व्याजह्रुरिव पादपाः।।3.52.34।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

उत्पातवाताभिहताः hit by the stormy wind, नानाद्विजगणायुताः filled with different flocks of birds, पादपाः trees, विधूताग्राः 'Be not afraid', मा भैः इति saying, 'Be not afraid', व्याजह्रुः इव as if they said.

#### Explanation

The trees filled with birds of all kinds, shaken at the top by stormy winds seemed to be saying to Sita, 'Be not afraid'.

---

### Shloka 35

#### Shloka Text

नलिन्यो ध्वस्तकमलास्त्रस्तमीनजलेचराः। सखीमिव गतोच्छ्वासामन्वशोचन्त मैथिलीम्।।3.52.35।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ध्वस्तकमलाः with lotuses withering, त्रस्तमीनजलेचराः with disturbed aquatic animals like fishes, नलिन्यः lotus ponds, गतोच्छ्वासाम्  who lost her power to breathe, सखीमिव like a companion, मैथिलीम् Maithili, अन्वशोचन्त felt sorry for her.

#### Explanation

Lotusponds with withering lotuses and with disturbed aquatic life like fishes in fright were as if grieving for Sita as one would grieve for a companion who is unable to breathe.

---

### Shloka 36

#### Shloka Text

समन्तादभिसम्पत्य सिंहव्याघ्रमृगद्विजाः। अन्वधावंस्तदा रोषात्सीतां छायानुगामिनः।।3.52.36।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तदा then, सिंहव्याघ्रमृगद्विजाः lions, tigers, deer and birds, समन्तात् अभिसम्पत्य coming together from all sides, रोषात् due to anguish, छायानुगामिनः followers of the shadow, सीताम् Sita, अन्वधावन् ran following her.

#### Explanation

Then the lions, tigers, deer and birds gathered together in anger (also anguish) and ran after Sita's shadow.

---

### Shloka 37

#### Shloka Text

जलप्रपातास्रमुखाश्शृङ्गैरुच्छ्रितबाहुभिः। सीतायां ह्रियमाणायां विक्रोशन्तीव पर्वताः।।3.52.37।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सीतायाम् Sita, ह्रियमाणायाम् carried away, पर्वताः mountains, जलप्रपातास्रमुखाः with tears rolling down in the form of waterfalls  शृङ्गैः representing the peaks, उच्छ्रितबाहुभिः lifting the hands high, विक्रोशन्तीव seemed as if they were crying in grief.

#### Explanation

The  mountains were  shedding tears in the form of waterfalls, and  crying with hands lifted up in the form of peaks, while Sita was being carried away.

---

### Shloka 38

#### Shloka Text

ह्रियमाणां तु वैदेहीं दृष्ट्वा दीनो दिवाकरः। प्रतिध्वस्तप्रभश्श्रीमानासीत्पाण्डरमण्डलः।।3.52.38।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ह्रियमाणाम् while being hijacked, वैदेहीम् Sita, दृष्ट्वा seeing, श्रीमान् glorious, दिवाकरः Sun, दीनः distressed, प्रतिध्वस्तप्रभः with dimmed lustre, पाण्डुरमण्डलः white disk around, आसीत् had.

#### Explanation

Seeing Sita abducted, the glorious Sun looked lustreless and distraught. He developed a pale white disk around him.

---

### Shloka 39

#### Shloka Text

नास्ति धर्मः कुतस्सत्यं नार्जवं नानृशंसता। यत्र रामस्य वैदेहीं भार्यां हरति रावणः।।3.52.39।। इति सर्वाणि भूतानि गणशः पर्यदेवयन्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

यत्र where, रामस्य Rama's, भार्या wife, वैदेहीम् Vaidehi, रावणः Ravana, हरति is abducting, धर्मः dharma, नास्ति is not there, सत्यम् truth, कुतः where is it , आर्जवम् uprightness, न not, अनृशंसता compassion, न not, इति thus, सर्वाणि all, भूतानि beings, गणशः in groups, पर्यदेवयन्  wailed.

#### Explanation

When Rama's consort, the princess of Videha, is being carried away by Ravana, how can there be rightousness or truth or uprightness or compassion for men? Thus bewailed all beings in groups.

---

### Shloka 40

#### Shloka Text

वित्रस्तका दीनमुखा रुरुदुर्मृगपोतकाः।।3.52.40।। उद्वीक्ष्योद्वीक्ष्य नयनैरस्रपाताविलेक्षणाः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मृगपोतकाः young deer, वित्रस्तकाः with fear, दीनमुखाः with distressed looks, अस्रपाताविलेक्षणाः unable to see clearly due to eyes filled with tears, नयनैः with eyes, उद्वीक्ष्योद्वीक्ष्य looked up (at the direction) again and again, रुरुदुः wept.

#### Explanation

The young deer full of fear and distress looked again and again with eyes blurred with tears at the direction in which Sita went. And wept.

---

### Shloka 41

#### Shloka Text

सुप्रवेपितगात्राश्च बभूवुर्वनदेवताः।।3.52.41।। विक्रोशन्तीं दृढं सीतां दृष्ट्वा दुःखं तथा गताम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तथा likewise, दुःखम् grief, गताम् experiencing, दृढम् strongly, विक्रोशन्तीम् called out weeping, सीताम् to Sita, दृष्टवा seeing, वनदेवताः sylvan deities, सुप्रवेपितगात्राश्च with their bodies shivering, बभूवुः became.

#### Explanation

The  sylvan deities shivered, seeing Sita's grief and cried aloud.

---

### Shloka 42

#### Shloka Text

तां तु लक्ष्मण रामेति क्रोशन्तीं मधुरस्वरम्।।3.52.42।। अवेक्षमाणां बहुशो वैदेहीं धरणीतलम्। स तामाकुलकेशान्तां विप्रमृष्टविशेषकाम्।।3.52.43।। जहारात्म विनाशाय दशग्रीवो मनस्स्विनीम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लक्ष्मण Lakshmana, राम Rama, इति saying so, मधुरस्वरम् in a sweet voice, क्रोशन्तीम् while she was weeping, बहुशः many times, धरणीतलम् down to the ground, अवेक्षमाणाम् looknig at, आकुलकेशान्ताम् hair dishevelled, विप्रमृष्ट विशेषकाम् with the  auspicious mark on the forehead erased, मनस्स्विनीम् highminded woman, तां वैदेहीम् to that Vaidehi, सः दशग्रीवः that tenheaded Ravana, आत्मविनाशाय for his own destruction, जहार abducted.

#### Explanation

The ten headed Ravana abducted Sita who was calling out, O Rama, O Lakshmana with her sweet voice, weeping , looking down on the earth her hair dishevelled and auspicious, (vermilion) mark on the forehead erased. Ravana abducted this highminded Sita for his own destruction.

---

### Shloka 43

#### Shloka Text

तां तु लक्ष्मण रामेति क्रोशन्तीं मधुरस्वरम्।।3.52.42।। अवेक्षमाणां बहुशो वैदेहीं धरणीतलम्। स तामाकुलकेशान्तां विप्रमृष्टविशेषकाम्।।3.52.43।। जहारात्म विनाशाय दशग्रीवो मनस्स्विनीम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लक्ष्मण Lakshmana, राम Rama, इति saying so, मधुरस्वरम् in a sweet voice, क्रोशन्तीम् while she was weeping, बहुशः many times, धरणीतलम् down to the ground, अवेक्षमाणाम् looknig at, आकुलकेशान्ताम् hair dishevelled, विप्रमृष्ट विशेषकाम् with the  auspicious mark on the forehead erased, मनस्स्विनीम् highminded woman, तां वैदेहीम् to that Vaidehi, सः दशग्रीवः that tenheaded Ravana, आत्मविनाशाय for his own destruction, जहार abducted.

#### Explanation

The ten headed Ravana abducted Sita who was calling out, O Rama, O Lakshmana with her sweet voice, weeping , looking down on the earth her hair dishevelled and auspicious, (vermilion) mark on the forehead erased. Ravana abducted this highminded Sita for his own destruction.

---

### Shloka 44

#### Shloka Text

ततस्तु सा चारुदती शुचिस्मिता विनाकृता बन्धुजनेन मैथिली। अपश्यती राघवलक्ष्मणावुभौ विवर्णवक्त्राभयभारपीडिता।।3.52.44।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, चारुदती of beautiful teeth, शुचिस्मिला a lady gifted with a sweet smile, बन्धुजनेन विनाकृता  distanced from her relatives, मैथिली Maithili, राघवलक्ष्मणौ Rama and Lakshmana, उभौ both, अपश्यती unable to see, विवर्णवक्त्रा face grew pale, भयभारपीडिता with the stress of fear.

#### Explanation

Then Sita  whose teeth were  beautiful and smile, sweet was distanced from her kins, and, unable to see both Rama and Lakshmana, grew pale in intense fear. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये अरण्यकाण्डे द्विपञ्चाशस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the fiftysecond sarga of Aranyakanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 53

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

खमुत्पतन्तं तं दृष्ट्वा मैथिली जनकात्मजा। दुःखिता परमोद्विग्ना भये महति वर्तिनी।।3.53.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

जनकात्मजा Janaka's daughter, मैथिली Maithili, खम् into the sky, उत्पतन्तम् while flying, तम् Ravana, दृष्ट्वा seeing, परमोद्विग्ना agitated greatly, महति intense, भये fear, वर्तिनी became, दुःखिता miserable.

#### Explanation

Sita, daughter of Janaka, princess of Mithila felt miserable, looking at Ravana taking off and got very  much agitated out of fear.

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

रोषरोदनताम्राक्षी भीमाक्षं राक्षसाधिपम्। रुदन्ती करुणं सीता ह्रियमाणेदमब्रवीत्।।3.53.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ह्रियमाणा while being carried away, सीता Sita, करुणम् piteously, रुदन्ती weeping, रोषरोदनताम्राक्षी eyes reddened due to crying out of anger and grief, भीमाक्षम् with frightening  eyes, राक्षसाधिपम् to Ravana, the lord of demons, इदम् these (words), अब्रवीत् said.

#### Explanation

While being carried away, Sita wept piteously and her eyes went red with tears of anger and grief. Looking at the frightening eyes of the king of the demons, she said these words :

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

न व्यपत्रपसे नीच कर्मणानेन रावण। ज्ञात्वा विरहितां यन्मां चोरयित्वा पलायसे।।3.53.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

नीच O mean, रावण Ravana, विरहिताम् when I am separated, माम् me, चोरयित्वा having  stolen, पलायसे  run away, इति यत् like this, अनेन by this, कर्मणा by action, न व्यपत्रपसे are you not ashamed?

#### Explanation

O mean Ravana, are you not ashamed of kidnapping me when I was separated (from my husband)?

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

त्वयैव नूनं दुष्टात्मन् भीरुणा हर्तुमिच्छता। ममापवाहितो भर्ता मृगरूपेण मायया।।3.53.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

दुष्टात्मन् O wicked fellow, हर्तुम् to abduct, इच्छता desiring, भीरुणा by a timid one, त्वयैव  yourself, मम my, भर्ता husband, मायया with deceit, मृगरूपेण in the form of deer, अपवाहितः led to a distance.

#### Explanation

O wicked fellow, with an intention to abduct me you enticed my husband to a distance deceitfully in the form of a deer.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

यो हि मामुद्यतस्त्रातुं सोऽप्ययं विनिपातितः। गृध्रराजः पुराणोऽसौ श्वशुरस्य सखा मम।।3.53.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

यः who, गृध्रराजः king of vultures, माम् me, त्रातुम् to protect, उद्यतः tried, मम my, श्वशुरस्य of fatherinlaw, पुराणः old, सखा friend, अयम् he, असौ अपि he too, विनिपातितः is fallen.

#### Explanation

This Jatayu, an old friend of my fatherinlaw tried to protect me but he too has fallen a prey.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

परमं खलु ते वीर्यं दृश्यते राक्षसाधम। विश्राव्य नामधेयं हि युद्धे नास्मि जिता त्वया।।3.53.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षसाधमः O lowly demon, ते विर्यम्  bravery, परमम् very great, दृश्यते खलु is seen indeed, नामधेयम् your name, विश्राव्य after announcing, त्वया by your, युद्धे war, जिता won, नास्मि हि I am not.

#### Explanation

O lowly demon, you have, indeed, shown your heroism  (in killing this old vulture). You have not won me in a battle declared by you.

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

ईदृशं गर्हितं कर्म कथं कृत्वा न लज्जसे। स्त्रियाश्च हरणं नीच रहिते च परस्य च।।3.53.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

नीच O lowly fellow, रहिते when alone, परस्य च other's, स्त्रियाः woman's, हरणम् abduction, ईदृशम् this way, गर्हितम् despised, कर्म deed, कृत्वा after doing, कथम् how is it, न लज्जसे  are you not ashamed

#### Explanation

O lowly fellow how is it that you are not ashamed of commiting such a despicable act  abducting another's wife when she is alone ?

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

कथयिष्यन्ति लोकेषु पुरुषाः कर्म कुत्सितम्। सुनृशंसमधर्मिष्ठं तव शौण्डीर्यमानिनः।।3.53.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

शौण्डीर्यमानिनः considering yourself to be brave, तव yourself, सुनृशंसम् this very cruel act, अधर्मिष्ठम् unrighteous, कुत्सितम् despised by others, कर्म action, लोकेषु in the worlds, पुरुषाः people, कथयिष्यन्ति will talk about.

#### Explanation

You consider yourself to be brave after committing such a cruel, despicable and unrighteous act for which people of the world will condemn you.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

धिक्ते शौर्यं च सत्त्वं च यत्त्वं कथितवांस्तदा। कुलाक्रोशकरं लोके धिक्ते चारित्रमीदृशम्।।3.53.9।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

strength, धिक् fie, लोके in the world, कुलाक्रोशकरम् that which would bring calumny to the family, ते to you, ईदृशम् such, चारित्रम् behaviour, धिक् I condemn.

#### Explanation

Fie upon your heroism and strength about which you boasted. Fie upon your character that would bring calumny to the race and the world. I condemn such behaviour of yours.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

किं कर्तुं शक्यमेवं हि यज्जवेनैव धावसि। मुहूर्तमपि तिष्ठस्व न जीवन्प्रतियास्यसि।।3.53.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

किम् what, कर्तुम् to do, शक्यम् is possible, यत् since, एवम् in this way, जवेनैव  so swiftly, धावसि you are running, मुहूर्तमपि for a moment also, तिष्ठस्व you stay, जीवन् with life, न प्रतियास्यसि you will not.

#### Explanation

Tarry a little, you will not go back with life. What can be done now since you are running away so swiftly?

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

न हि चक्षुष्पथं प्राप्य तयोः पार्थिवपुत्रयोः। ससैन्योऽपि समर्थस्त्वं मुहूर्तमपि जीवितुम्।।3.53.11।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तयोः of both of them, पार्थिवपुत्रयोः of the two princes, चक्षुष्पथम् within the range of their sight, प्राप्य on reaching, त्वम् you, ससैन्योऽपि even with your army, मुहूर्तमपि for a moment, जीवितुम् to live, न समर्थः हि not possible.

#### Explanation

If you come within sight of these two princes, it will not be possible for you to live for a moment even if you are with your entire army.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

न त्वं तयोश्शरस्पर्शं सोढुं शक्तः कथञ्चन। वने प्रज्वलितस्येव स्पर्शमग्नेर्विहंगमः।।3.53.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वने in the forest, प्रज्वलितस्य of burning, अग्नेः fire, स्पर्शम् touch, विहङ्गमः इव like bird, त्वम् you, तयोः of both of them, शरस्पर्शम् touch of arrows, सोढुम् to endure, कथञ्चन anyhow, न शक्तः it is not possible for you.

#### Explanation

Just as a bird cannot bear the touch of forest fire, you will not be able to endure the mere touch of arrows of the two heroes indeed.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

साधु कुर्वाऽऽत्मनः पथ्यं साधु मां मुञ्च रावण। मत्प्रधर्षणरुष्टो हि भ्रात्रा सह पतिर्मम।।3.53.13।। विधास्यति विनाशाय त्वं मां यदि न मुञ्चसि।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रावण Ravana, साधु it is only proper, आत्मनः your, पथ्यम् good for your wellbeing, कुरु you may do, माम् me, साधु be good, मुञ्च you may release, त्वम् you, माम् me, न मुञ्चसि you do not leave, यदि what, मत्प्रधर्षणरुष्टः enraged for the assaulting me, मम पतिः my husband, भ्रात्रा सह along with his brother, विनाशाय for destruction, विधास्यति he will arrange.

#### Explanation

O Ravana be good and do whatever is beneficial to you. It is proper for you to release me. If you do not, enraged over my assault, my husband along with his brother, will bring about your destruction.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

येन त्वं व्यवसायेन बलान्मां हर्तुमिच्छसि।।3.53.14।। व्यवसायस्स ते नीच भविष्यति निरर्थकः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

नीच O mean fellow, त्वम् you, येन by which, व्यवसायेन  effort, माम् me, बलात् by force, हर्तुम् to abduct, इच्छसि desire, ते your, सः that, व्यवसायः determination, निरर्थकः fruitless, भविष्यति will become.

#### Explanation

O vile creature, the effort you are making to abduct me forcibly will be fruitless.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

न ह्यहं तमपश्यन्ती भर्तारं विबुधोपमम्।।3.53.15।। उत्सहे शत्रुवशगा प्राणान्धारयितुं चिरम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अहम् I am, विबुधोपमम् godlike, भर्तारम् husband, तम् him, अपश्यन्ती without seeing him, शत्रुवशगा at the mercy of an enemy, चिरम् long, प्राणान् life, धरयितुम् to hold on, न उत्सहे हि I am not enthused.

#### Explanation

I will not be eager to hold on to my life for long to live at the mercy of the enemy without being able to see my godlike husband.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

न नूनं चात्मनः पथ्यं श्रेयो वा समवेक्षसे।।3.53.16।। मृत्युकाले यथा मर्त्यो विपरीतानि सेवते।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मर्त्य: a mortal, मृत्युकाले at the time of death, यथा as, विपरीतानि very differently, सेवते he will resort to, आत्मनः your own, पथ्यम् whatever is beneficial to you, श्रेयो वा your welfare, न समवेक्षसे you are not able to foresee.

#### Explanation

At the time of death, mortals behave very differently. They act contrary to their own welfare. Likewise you are not able to foresee what is beneficial and good for you.

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

मुमूर्षूणां हि सर्वेषां यत्पथ्यं तन्न रोचते।।3.53.17।। पश्याम्यद्य हि कण्ठेत्वां कालपाशावपाशितम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मुमूर्षूणाम्  who want to die, सर्वेषाम्  all of them, यत् what, पथ्यम् is salutary, तत् that, न रोचते not like, अद्य now, त्वाम् you, कण्ठे in your neck, कालपाशावपाशितम् noose of death wound round, पश्यामि हि I see.

#### Explanation

Those who wish to die, do not like anything salutary. I see the noose of death wound round your neck now.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

यथा चास्मिन्भयस्थाने न बिभेषि दशानन।।3.53.18।। व्यक्तं हिरण्मयान् हि त्वं सम्पश्यसि महीरुहान्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

दशानन tenheaded Ravana, त्वम् you, अस्मिन् in this, भयस्थाने cause of fear, यथा even so, न बिभेषि you are not scared, हिरण्मयान् golden, महीरुहान् trees, सम्पश्यसि you see, व्यक्तम्  evident.

#### Explanation

O tenheaded Ravana, since you do not fear the source of fear, evidently you see golden trees. (a symptom of death).

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

नदीं वैतरणीं घोरां रुधिरौघनिवाहिनीम्।।3.53.19।। असिपत्रवनं चैव भीमं पश्यसि रावण।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रावण Ravana, रुधिरौघनिवाहिनीम् river carrying torrents of blood flowing, घोराम् dreadful, वैतरणीं नदीम् river Vaitarani, भीमम् frightening, असिपत्रवनं चैव forest of trees with swords as leaves, पश्यसि see.

#### Explanation

O Ravana, you see the dreadful river Vaitarani with torrents of blood flowing and frightening forest of trees with swords as leaves.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

तप्तकाञ्चनपुष्पां च वैडूर्यप्रवरच्छदाम्।।3.53.20।। द्रक्ष्यसे शाल्मलीं तीक्ष्णामायसैः कण्टकैश्चिताम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तप्ककाञ्चनपुष्पाम् with bright golden flowers, वैडूर्यप्रवरच्छदाम् with barks of excellent Vaidurya stones like bark, आयसैः with iron, कण्टकैः thorns, चिताम् logged, तीक्ष्णाम् sharp, शाल्मलीम् salmali (silkcotton) tree, द्रक्ष्यसे you see.

#### Explanation

You will see a log of salmali tree with bright golden flowers and excellent vaidurya stone with pointed iron thorns.

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

न हि त्वमीदृशं कृत्वा तस्यालीकं महात्मनः।।3.53.21।। धरितुं शक्ष्यसि चिरं विषं पीत्वेव निर्घृणः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

निर्घृणः pitiless, त्वम् you, महात्मनः of the noble, तस्य his, ईदृशम् such, अलीकम् deceiving, कृत्वा after doing, विषम् poison, पीत्वेव as if after drinking, चिरम् for long, धरितुम् to survive, न शक्ष्यसि not possible.

#### Explanation

You are pitiless. You will not be able to survive for long after deceiving such noble Rama just as man cannot survive after drinking poison.

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

बद्धस्त्वं कालपाशेन दुर्निवारेण रावण।।3.53.22।। क्वगतो लप्स्यसे शर्म भर्तुर्मम महात्मनः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रावण Ravana, त्वम् you, दुर्निवारेण by the inevitable, कालपाशेन noose of death, बद्धः bound, मम my, भर्तुः husband's, महात्मनः great Rama, क्व where can you, गतः go, शर्म peace, लप्स्यसे will you get.

#### Explanation

O Ravana you are bound by the inevitable noose of death. Where shall you get away from great Rama, my husband to obtain peace.

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

निमेषान्तरमात्रेण विना भ्रात्रा महावने।।3.53.23।। राक्षसा निहता येन सहस्राणि चतुर्दश। स कथं राघवो वीरस्सर्वास्त्रकुशलो बली।।3.53.24।। न त्वां हन्याच्छरैस्तीक्ष्णैरिष्टभार्यापहारिणम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

येन by whom, महावने in this great forest, भ्रात्रा विना without the help of his brother, निमेषान्तरमात्रेण in a short while, चतुर्दश सहस्राणि fourteen thousand, राक्षसाः demons, निहताः were killed, वीरः warrior, सर्वास्त्रकुशली wellversed in the use of all kinds of weapons, बली strong man, सः that, राघवः Rama, इष्टभार्यापहारिणम्  who has stolen his beloved wife, त्वाम् you, तीक्ष्णैः with sharp, शरैः arrows, कथम् how, न हन्यात् will he not kill?

#### Explanation

Rama has killed fourteen thousand demons alone without his brother within a moment. He is a strong warrior and wellversed in the use of all kinds of weapons. You have stolen his loving wife. How can he live without killing you.

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

निमेषान्तरमात्रेण विना भ्रात्रा महावने।।3.53.23।। राक्षसा निहता येन सहस्राणि चतुर्दश। स कथं राघवो वीरस्सर्वास्त्रकुशलो बली।।3.53.24।। न त्वां हन्याच्छरैस्तीक्ष्णैरिष्टभार्यापहारिणम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

येन by whom, महावने in this great forest, भ्रात्रा विना without the help of his brother, निमेषान्तरमात्रेण in a short while, चतुर्दश सहस्राणि fourteen thousand, राक्षसाः demons, निहताः were killed, वीरः warrior, सर्वास्त्रकुशली wellversed in the use of all kinds of weapons, बली strong man, सः that, राघवः Rama, इष्टभार्यापहारिणम्  who has stolen his beloved wife, त्वाम् you, तीक्ष्णैः with sharp, शरैः arrows, कथम् how, न हन्यात् will he not kill?

#### Explanation

Rama has killed fourteen thousand demons alone without his brother within a moment. He is a strong warrior and wellversed in the use of all kinds of weapons. You have stolen his loving wife. How can he live without killing you.

---

### Shloka 25

#### Shloka Text

तच्चान्यच्च परुषं वैदेही रावणाङ्कगा।।3.53.25।। भयशोकसमाविष्टा करुणं विललाप ह।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रावणाङ्कगा a lady under Ravana's grip, वैदेही Vaidehi (Sita), भयशोकसमाविष्टा full of fear and sorrow, एतत् च these words, अन्यच्च and other words, परुषम् harsh, करुणम् piteously,  विललाप ह wept.

#### Explanation

Sita, daughter of Videha, full of sorrow and fear under Ravana's grip uttered these partly harsh and partly piteous wordsweeping.

---

### Shloka 26

#### Shloka Text

तथा भृशार्तां बहुचैव भाषिणीं विलापपूर्वं करुणं च भामिनीम्। जहार पापः करुणं विचेष्टतीं नृपात्मजामागतगात्रवेपथुम्।।3.53.26।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तथा likewise, भृशार्ताम् greatly terrified, विलापपूर्वम् weeping, करुणं च piteously, बहुचैव in many ways, भाषिणीम्  talking, करुणम् mournfully, विचेष्टतीम् struggling, नृपात्मजाम् princess, आगतगात्रवेपथुम्  whose  body started shivering, भामिनीम् to the beautiful lady, पापः sinner Ravana, जहार carried off.

#### Explanation

Sinful Ravana carried away the beautiful princess trembling with great fear, struggling helplessly, crying pathetically and pleading in many mournful ways. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये अरण्यकाण्डे त्रिपञ्चाशस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the fiftythird sarga of Aranyakanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 54

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

ह्रियमाणा तु वैदेही कञ्चिन्नाथमपश्यती। ददर्श गिरिशृङ्गस्थान्पञ्च वानरपुङ्गवान्।।3.54.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ह्रियमाणा taken away, वैदेही Vaidehi, नाथम् protector, कञ्चित् some one, अपश्यती not seeing, गिरिशृङ्गस्थान् standing on the peak of the mountain, पञ्च five, वानरपुङ्गवान् great monkeys, ददर्श saw.

#### Explanation

While Sita was being kidnapped, she saw no one to protect her. Only five great monkeys stood on the top of a mountain.

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

तेषां मध्ये विशालाक्षी कौशेयं कनकप्रभम्। उत्तरीयं वरारोहा शुभान्याभरणानि च।।3.54.2।। मुमोच यदि रामाय शंसेयुरिति मैथिली।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

विशालाक्षी large eyed, वरारोहा charming, भामिनी effulgent, कनकप्रभम् shining like gold, कौशेयम् silk, उत्तरीयम् upper cloth, शुभानि auspicious, आभरणानि च ornaments also, रामाय for Rama, यदि if at all, शंसेयुः they may tell, इति thus, तेषाम् them, मध्ये between, मुमोच dropped.

#### Explanation

Largeeyed, bright and beautiful Sita dropped her upper silk garment shining like gold and auspicious ornaments in their midst with a hope they may report this  to Rama.

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

वस्त्रमुत्सृज्य तन्मध्ये निक्षिप्तं सहभूषणम्।।3.54.3।। सम्भ्रमात्तु दशग्रीवस्तत्कर्म न स बुद्धवान्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सहभूषणम् along with ornaments, वस्त्रम् clothes, उत्सृज्य discarding, तन्मध्ये in their midst, निक्षिप्तम् thrown down, सः दशग्रीवः that tenheaded Ravana, सम्भ्रमात् due to excitement, तत्  that, कर्म action of Sita, न बुद्धवान् did not know.

#### Explanation

In his excitement Ravana failed to notice when Sita dropped down that garment along with ornaments in their midst.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

पिङ्गाक्षास्तां विशालाक्षीं नेत्रैरनिमिषैरिव।।3.54.4।। विक्रोशन्तीं तथा सीतां ददृशुर्वानरर्षभाः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पिङ्गाक्षाः tawney eyed, वानरर्षभाः best among monkeys, विशालाक्षीम् largeeyed, तथा like that, विक्रोशन्तीम् lamenting, तां सीताम् that Sita, अनिमिषैरिव without blinking, नेत्रैः eyes, ददृशुः gazed at.

#### Explanation

The best among the tawneyeyed monkeys gazed, without blinking, at the  largeeyed Sita lamenting.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

स च पम्पामतिक्रम्य लङ्कामभिमुखः पुरीम्।।3.54.5।। जगाम रुदतीं गृह्य वैदेहीं राक्षसेश्वरः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः राक्षसेश्वरः that lord of the demons, पम्पाम् Pampa, अतिक्रम्य on crossing, लङ्कां पुरीम् to the city of Lanka, अभिमुखः towards, रुदतीम् crying, वैदेहीम् Vaidehi, गृह्य taking, जगाम went.

#### Explanation

Ravana crossed the lake Pampa and flew towards the city of Lanka carrying with him lamenting daughter of Videha.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

तां जहार सुसंहृष्टो रावणो मृत्युमात्मनः।।3.54.6।। उत्सङ्गेनेव भुजगीं तीक्ष्णदंष्ट्रां महाविषाम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रावणः Ravana, सुसंहृष्टः delighted, आत्मनः his own, मृत्युम् death, ताम् her, तीक्ष्णदंष्ट्राम् sharp fanged, महाविषाम् highly poisonous, भुजगीम् female snake, उत्सङ्गेनेव on his lap, जहार  carried.

#### Explanation

Delighted Ravana, carried off Sita, the very embodiment of his death, like one carrying a highly poisonous sharpfanged female snake on his lap.

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

वनानि सरितश्शैलांत्सरांसि च विहायसा।।3.54.7।। स क्षिप्रं समतीयाय शरश्चापादिव च्युतः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः he, चापात् from the bow, च्युतः released, शरः इव like an arrow, क्षिप्रम् at once, वनानि forests, सरितः rivers, शैलान् mountains, सरांसि च tanks, विहायसा through the sky, समतीयाय crossed.

#### Explanation

Like an arrow released from the bow, Ravana went through the sky, at once crossing forests, rivers mountains and tanks.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

तिमिनक्रनिकेतं तु वरुणालयमक्षयम्।।3.54.8।। सरितां शरणं गत्वा समतीयाय सागरम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तिमिनक्रनिकेतम् the abode of whales and crocodiles, अक्षयम् inexhaustible,सरितां शरणम् refuge of all rivers, वरुणालयम् abode Varuna, god of the sea, गत्वा having covered, सागरम् ocean, समतीयाय crossed.

#### Explanation

Ravana soon crossed the refuge of rivers, the abode of Varuna, god of the sea, inexhaustible ocean, and the home for whales and crocodiles.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

सम्भ्रमात्परिवृत्तोर्मी रुद्धमीनमहोरगः।।3.54.9।। वैदेह्यां ह्रियमाणायां बभूव वरुणालयः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वैदेह्याम् to Sita, daughter of Videha, ह्रियमाणायाम्  was being carried away, वरुणालयः the abode of Varuna, the ocean, सम्भ्रमात् out of bewilderment, परिवृत्तोर्मिः waves turned back, रुद्धमीनमहोरगः movement of big serpents and fishes stopped, बभूव became.

#### Explanation

While Sita, princess of Videha was being carried away, the ocean, abode of Varuna got bewildered, waves rolled back, the movement of big serpents and fishes stopped.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

अन्तरिक्षगता वाचस्ससृजुश्चारणास्तदा।।3.54.10।। एतदन्तो दशग्रीव इति सिद्धास्तदाब्रुवन्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तदा then, अन्तरिक्षगताः flying in the sky, चारणाः bards, दशग्रीवः tenheaded Ravana, एतदन्तः  इति this is the end, वाचः words, ससृजुः spoke, तदा then, सिद्धाः siddhas, अब्रुवन् uttered.

#### Explanation

The charanas and siddhas moving in the sky said, O tenheaded Ravana this is your  end .

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

स तु सीतां विवेष्टन्तीमङ्केनादाय रावणः।।3.54.11।। प्रविवेश पुरीं लङ्कां रूपिणीं मृत्युमात्मनः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः रावणः that Ravana, आत्मनः his own, रूपिणीम् personified form, मृत्युम् death, विवेष्टन्तीम् struggling, सीताम् Sita, अङ्केन by his arm, आदाय holding her, लङ्कां पुरीम्  city of Lanka, प्रविवेश entered.

#### Explanation

Ravana entered the city of Lanka holding Sita who was tossing from side to side and who was the very embodiment of his death.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

सोऽभिगम्य पुरीं लङ्कां सुविभक्तमहापथाम्।।3.54.12।। संरूढकक्ष्याबहुलं स्वमन्तःपुरमाविशत्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः Ravana, सुविभक्तमहापथाम्  well laidout roads, लङ्कां पुरीम् city of Lanka, अभिगम्य entering, संरूढकक्ष्याबहुलम् with many wellguarded halls, स्वम् his, अन्तःपुरम् inner apartment, आविशत् entered.

#### Explanation

Ravana went through the well laidout roads of the city of Lanka and through the well many guarded halls of the palace and entered the inner apartment.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

तत्र तामसितापाङ्गां शोकमोहपरायणाम्।।3.54.13।। निदधे रावणस्सीतां मयो मायामिवस्त्रियम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रावणः Ravana, असितापाङ्गाम् a lady of dark eyelashes, शोकमोहपरायणाम् lady lost in sorrow, तां सीताम् that Sita, मयः demon Maya, मायामिव like Maya, तत्र there ( in the harem), नदधे kept.

#### Explanation

Sita, with her dark eye lashes and lost in utter sorrow was kept in the harem by Ravana, like demon Maya kept Mayamayi.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

अब्रवीच्च दशग्रीवः पिशाचीर्घोरदर्शनाः।।3.54.14।। यथा नेमां पुमान् स्त्री वा सीतां पश्यत्यसम्मतः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

दशग्रीवः tenheaded Ravana, घोरदर्शनाः  of fearful appearance, पिशाचीः female goblins, अब्रवीत् च said, असम्मतः without my permission, पुमान् man, स्त्री वा or a woman, इमाम् this lady, सीताम् Sita, यथा in a way, न पश्यति will not see.

#### Explanation

Ravana ordered the female goblins of fearful appearance,'Let no outsider whether man or woman, see her, without my permission'.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

मुक्तामणिसुवर्णानि वस्त्राण्याभरणानि च।।3.54.15।। यद्यदिच्छेत्तदेवास्या देयं मच्छन्दतो यथा।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मुक्तामणिसुवर्णानि pearls, gems or gold, वस्त्राणि clothes, आभरणानि च and ornaments, यत् यत् whatever, इच्छेत् she desires, तदेव all that, अस्याः to her, मच्छन्दतो यथा  without hesitation, देयम् should be given.

#### Explanation

Pearls, gems or gold, clothes or ornaments, whatever she desires, be made available to her without any hesitation.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

या च वक्ष्यति वैदेहीं वचनं  किञ्चिदप्रियम्।।3.54.16।। अज्ञानाद्यदि वा ज्ञानान्न तस्या जीवितं प्रियम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

या च whosoever, अज्ञानात् out of ignorance, ज्ञानात् knowingly, वैदेहीम् to Vaidehi, किञ्चित् even a little, अप्रियम् displeasing, वचनम् words, वक्ष्यति utters, तस्याः her, जीवितम् life, न not, प्रियम्  worthy,

#### Explanation

Whoever utters any unpleasant words knowingly or unknowingly to Sita, princess of Videha is not worthy to survive (must be killed).

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

तथोक्त्वा राक्षसीस्तास्तु राक्षसेन्द्रः प्रतापवान्।।3.54.17।। निष्क्रम्यान्तः पुरात्तस्मात्किंकृत्यमिति चिन्तयन्। ददर्शाष्टौ महावीर्यान्राक्षसान्पिशिताशनान्।।3.54.18।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

प्रतापवान्  courageous, राक्षसेन्द्रः lord of demons (Ravana), राक्षसीः demoness, तथा likewise, उक्त्वा having said so, तस्मात् from that, अन्तःपुरात् from the inner apartments, निष्क्रम्य  coming out, किम् what, कृत्यम् can be done, चिन्तयन् while thinking, महावीर्यान् most powerful ones, पिशिताशनान् flesheaters, अष्टौ eight, राक्षसान् demons, ददर्श saw.

#### Explanation

The courageous lord of the demons, having said so to the demonesses, came out of the inner apartment. While contemplating the future course of action, he saw eight powerful, carnivorous demons.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

तथोक्त्वा राक्षसीस्तास्तु राक्षसेन्द्रः प्रतापवान्।।3.54.17।। निष्क्रम्यान्तः पुरात्तस्मात्किंकृत्यमिति चिन्तयन्। ददर्शाष्टौ महावीर्यान्राक्षसान्पिशिताशनान्।।3.54.18।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

प्रतापवान्  courageous, राक्षसेन्द्रः lord of demons (Ravana), राक्षसीः demoness, तथा likewise, उक्त्वा having said so, तस्मात् from that, अन्तःपुरात् from the inner apartments, निष्क्रम्य  coming out, किम् what, कृत्यम् can be done, चिन्तयन् while thinking, महावीर्यान् most powerful ones, पिशिताशनान् flesheaters, अष्टौ eight, राक्षसान् demons, ददर्श saw.

#### Explanation

The courageous lord of the demons, having said so to the demonesses, came out of the inner apartment. While contemplating the future course of action, he saw eight powerful, carnivorous demons.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

स तान्दृष्ट्वा महावीर्यो वरदानेन मोहितः। उवाचैतानिदं वाक्यं प्रशस्य बलवीर्यतः।।3.54.19।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तान्दृष्ट्वा seeing them, महावीर्यः mighty, वरदानेन by the boons, मोहितः  a deluded one, सः he, बलवीर्यतः about their strength, प्रशस्य after praising them, एतान् them, इदम् this, वाक्यम् word, उवाच said.

#### Explanation

The mighty Ravana, deluded by the boons, praised the strength and valour of the demons and said :

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

नानाप्रहरणाः क्षिप्रमितो गच्छत सत्वराः। जनस्थानं हतस्थानं भूतपूर्वं खरालयम्।।3.54.20।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

नानाप्रहरणाः a variety of weapons, सत्वराः very quickly, इतः from here, क्षिप्रम् at once, हतस्थानम्  where he was slain, खरालयम् the abode of Khara, भूतपूर्वम् the earlier (dwelling), जनस्थानम् Janasthana, गच्छत you may go.

#### Explanation

Armed with different weapons, proceed at once to Janasthana where Khara lived and died.

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

तत्रोष्यतां जनस्थाने शून्ये निहतराक्षसे। पौरुषं बलमाश्रित्य त्रासमुत्सृज्य दूरतः।।3.54.21।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पौरुषम् prowess, बलम् vigour, आश्रित्य relying on, त्रासम् fear, दूरतः leaving, उत्सृज्य given up, निहतराक्षसे where all demons were killed, शून्ये desolate, तत्र there, जनस्थाने at Janasthana, उष्यताम् dwell there.

#### Explanation

Go and there in the desolate Janasthana where all demons had been killed, leaving all your apprehensions and relying on your strength and vigour.

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

बलं हि सुमहद्यन्मे जनस्थाने निवेशितम्। सदूषणखरं युद्धे हतं रामेण सायकैः।।3.54.22।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मे mine, सुमहत् very great, यत् since, बलम् army, जनस्थाने at Janasthana, निवेशितम् had stationed, सदूषणखरम् with Dusana and Khara, रामेण by Rama, सायकैः arrows, हतम् killed.

#### Explanation

I had stationed a great army at Janasthana, who were all killed along with Khara and Dusana by Rama's arrows.

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

तत क्रोधो ममामर्षाद्धैर्यस्योपरि वर्तते। वैरं च सुमहज्जातं रामं प्रति सुदारुणम्।।3.54.23।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, मम my, अमर्षात् due to that intolerance, क्रोधः wrath, धैर्यस्य one's patience, उपरि over and above, वर्तते is arising, रामं प्रति towards Rama, सुमहत् very great, सुदारुणम् terrific, वैरं च enmity, जातम् is created.

#### Explanation

My anger engineered by my intolerance has crossed the limits of my patience. It has created in me a terrific enmity against Rama.

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

निर्यातयितुमिच्छामि तच्च वैरमहं रिपोः। न हि लप्स्याम्यहं निद्रामहत्वा संयुगे रिपुम्।।3.54.24।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अहम् I am, रिपोः enemy, तत् that, निर्यातयितुम् avenge, इच्छामि I am desirous, संयुगे in combat, रिपुम् enemy, अहत्वा without slaying, अहम् I, निद्राम् sleep, न लप्स्यामि हि I am not getting.

#### Explanation

I want to take revenge on my foe. I shall not sleep until I kill him in a combat.

---

### Shloka 25

#### Shloka Text

तं त्विदानीमहं हत्वा खरदूषणघातिनम्। रामं शर्मोपलप्स्यामि धनं लब्ध्वेव निर्धनः।।3.54.25।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अहम् I am, इदानीम् now, खरदूषणघातिनम् the killer of Khara and Dusana, तम् him, रामम् Rama, हत्वा after slaying, निर्धनः one without wealth, धनम् wealth, लब्ध्वा having acquired, इव like that, शर्म pleasure, उपलप्स्यामि I will have.

#### Explanation

I will be happy only after killing the destroyer of Khara and Dusana, just as a poor man feels happy only on acquiring wealth.

---

### Shloka 26

#### Shloka Text

जनस्थाने वसद्भिस्तु भवद्भीराममाश्रिता। प्रवृत्तिरुपनेतव्या किञ्करोतीति तत्त्वतः।।3.54.26।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

जनस्थाने at Janasthana, वसद्भि: while dwelling, भवद्भि: by you all, किं what, करोति इति he does, रामम् Rama, आश्रिता pertaining to, प्रवृत्ति: activities, तत्त्वतः exactly, उपनेतव्या get the information.

#### Explanation

While dwelling at Janasthana, all of you should collect exact information about the activities of Rama.

---

### Shloka 27

#### Shloka Text

अप्रमादाच्च गन्तव्यं सर्वैरपि निशाचरैः। कर्तव्यश्च सदा यत्नो राघवस्य वधं प्रति।।3.54.27।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

निशाचरैः by nightrangers, सर्वैरपि by all of them, अप्रमादात् alertly, गन्तव्यम् go there, राघवस्य Rama's, वधं प्रति to slay Rama, सदा always, यत्नः effort, कर्तव्यश्च should be done.

#### Explanation

All of you, O nightrangers, should be on the alert there and it should be always your duty to try to kill Rama.

---

### Shloka 28

#### Shloka Text

युष्माकं च बलज्ञोऽहं बहुशो रणमूर्धनि। अतश्चास्मिन् जनस्थाने मया यूयं नियोजिताः।।3.54.28।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अहम् I, रणमूर्धनि on war front, बहुशः many times, युष्माकम् your, बलज्ञः knower of strength, अतश्च therefore, यूयम् you, अस्मिन् in this, जनस्थाने at Janasthana, मया by me, नियोजिताः are  engaged.

#### Explanation

I have seen your strength on many war fronts. That is why you have been assigned this  duty at Janasthana.

---

### Shloka 29

#### Shloka Text

ततः प्रियं वाक्यमुपेत्य राक्षसा महार्थमष्टावभिवाद्य रावणम्। विहाय लङ्कां सहिताः प्रतस्थिरे यतो जनस्थानमलक्ष्यदर्शनाः।।3.54.29।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, अष्टौ eight, राक्षसाः demons, प्रियम् dear, महार्थम्  words of profound meaningl, वाक्यम् statement, उपेत्य after receiving, रावणम् Ravana, अभिवाद्य offering salutations, लङ्काम् Lanka, विहाय  leaving, सहिताः together, अलक्ष्यदर्शनाः without being seen by any one, जनस्थानम् to Janasthana, यतः in that direction, प्रतस्थिरे left.

#### Explanation

The eight demons were glad to hear the pleasing and meaningful words of Ravana. They offered him salutations and left Lanka in the direction of Janasthana without being seen by any one.

---

### Shloka 30

#### Shloka Text

ततस्तु सीतामुपलभ्य रावणः सुसम्प्रहृष्टः परिगृह्य मैथिलीम्। प्रसज्य रामेण च वैरमुत्तमं बभूव मोहान्मुदितस्सराक्षसः।।3.54.30।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, राक्षसः the demons, सः रावणः that Ravana, सीताम् Sita, उपलभ्य seeing, मैथिलीम् Maithili, परिगृह्य getting, सुसम्प्रहृष्टः feeling extremely happy, रामेण at Rama, उत्तमम्   highest, वैरम् enmity, प्रसज्य incited, मोहात् in delusion, मुदितः rejoiced, बभूव became.

#### Explanation

Then the demon Ravana felt greatly happy for getting Sita, princess of Mithila, without knowing in his delusion that he had created the bitterest enmity with Rama. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वामलीकीये आदिकाव्ये अरण्यकाण्डे चतुःपञ्चाशस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the fiftyfourth sarga of Aranyakanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 55

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

सन्दिश्य राक्षसान्घोरान् रावणोऽष्टौ महाबलान्। आत्मानं बुद्धिवैक्लब्यात्कृतकृत्यममन्यत।।3.55.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राणवः Ravana, महाबलान् very powerful, धोरान् fierce, अष्टौ eight, राक्षसान् demons, सन्दिश्य ordered, बुद्धिवैक्लब्यात् out of perversion, आत्मानम् himself, कृतकृत्यम् an accomplished one, अमन्यत felt.

#### Explanation

After ordering the eight most fierce  and powerful demons, Ravana had a sense of accomplishment within himself because of his perverse intellect.

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

स चिन्तयानो वैदेहीं कामबाणसमर्पितः। प्रविवेश गृहं रम्यं सीतां द्रष्टुमभित्वरन्।।3.55.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः he (Ravana), वैदेहीम् Vaidehi, चिन्तयानः  thinking about, कामबाणैः समर्पितः succumbed to the arrows of Cupid (the god of love), सीताम् Sita, द्रष्टुम् to see, अभित्वरन् hastened, रम्यम् beautiful, गृहम् home, प्रविवेश entered again.

#### Explanation

Thinking about Vaidehi and hit by the arrows of Cupid (the god of love). Ravana hastened to see Sita and entered the beautiful home.

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

स प्रविश्य तु तद्वेश्म रावणो राक्षसाधिपः। अपश्यद्राक्षसीमध्ये सीतां शोकपरायणाम्।।3.55.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षसाधिपः lord of the demons, सः रावणः that Ravana, तत् that, वेश्म residence, प्रविश्य entered, राक्षसीमध्ये in the midst of demonesses, शोकपरायणाम् full of grief, सीताम् Sita, अपश्यत् saw.

#### Explanation

Ravana, lord of the demons, entered his residence and saw Sita full of grief in the midst of the demonesses.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

अश्रुपूर्णमुखीं दीनां शोकभाराभिपीडिताम्। वायुवेगैरिवाक्रान्तां मज्जन्तीं नावमर्णवे।।3.55.4।। मृगयूथपरिभ्रष्टां मृगीं श्वभिरिवावृताम्। अधोमुखमुखीं सीतामभ्येत्य च निशाचरः।।3.55.5।। तां तु शोकपरां दीनामवशां राक्षसाधिपः। स बलाद्दर्शयामास गृहं देवगृहोपमम्।।3.55.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अश्रुपूर्णमुखीम्  with face full of tears, दीनाम् piteous , शोकभाराभिपीडिताम् inflicted with the burden of sorrow, वायुवेगैः driven by the wind, (सम्)आक्रान्ताम् overpowered, अर्णवे in the sea, मज्जन्तीम् sinking, नावमिव like a boat, मृगयूथपरिभ्रष्टाम् separated from the herd, श्वभिः by hounds, आवृताम् surrounded by, मृगीमिव like a female deer, अधोमुखमुखीम् with head bent, सीताम् to Sita, निशाचर: demon, अभ्येत्य having gone near, शोकपराम् lady full of grief, दीनाम् pathetic woman, अवशाम् not under her own control, ताम् her, सः राक्षसाधिपः king of the demons(Ravana), बलात् forcibly, देवगृहोपमम् like a heavenly home, गृहम् home, दर्शयामास showed.

#### Explanation

The demon Ravana forcibly took Sita, her face full of tears, to show her his heavenlike home. Inflicted with grief she looked wretched. She resembled a female deer hounded out of the herd. With her head bent in grief, she looked pathetic like one not under her control, like a boat sinking under the sea driven by the wind.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

अश्रुपूर्णमुखीं दीनां शोकभाराभिपीडिताम्। वायुवेगैरिवाक्रान्तां मज्जन्तीं नावमर्णवे।।3.55.4।। मृगयूथपरिभ्रष्टां मृगीं श्वभिरिवावृताम्। अधोमुखमुखीं सीतामभ्येत्य च निशाचरः।।3.55.5।। तां तु शोकपरां दीनामवशां राक्षसाधिपः। स बलाद्दर्शयामास गृहं देवगृहोपमम्।।3.55.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अश्रुपूर्णमुखीम्  with face full of tears, दीनाम् piteous , शोकभाराभिपीडिताम् inflicted with the burden of sorrow, वायुवेगैः driven by the wind, (सम्)आक्रान्ताम् overpowered, अर्णवे in the sea, मज्जन्तीम् sinking, नावमिव like a boat, मृगयूथपरिभ्रष्टाम् separated from the herd, श्वभिः by hounds, आवृताम् surrounded by, मृगीमिव like a female deer, अधोमुखमुखीम् with head bent, सीताम् to Sita, निशाचर: demon, अभ्येत्य having gone near, शोकपराम् lady full of grief, दीनाम् pathetic woman, अवशाम् not under her own control, ताम् her, सः राक्षसाधिपः king of the demons(Ravana), बलात् forcibly, देवगृहोपमम् like a heavenly home, गृहम् home, दर्शयामास showed.

#### Explanation

The demon Ravana forcibly took Sita, her face full of tears, to show her his heavenlike home. Inflicted with grief she looked wretched. She resembled a female deer hounded out of the herd. With her head bent in grief, she looked pathetic like one not under her control, like a boat sinking under the sea driven by the wind.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

अश्रुपूर्णमुखीं दीनां शोकभाराभिपीडिताम्। वायुवेगैरिवाक्रान्तां मज्जन्तीं नावमर्णवे।।3.55.4।। मृगयूथपरिभ्रष्टां मृगीं श्वभिरिवावृताम्। अधोमुखमुखीं सीतामभ्येत्य च निशाचरः।।3.55.5।। तां तु शोकपरां दीनामवशां राक्षसाधिपः। स बलाद्दर्शयामास गृहं देवगृहोपमम्।।3.55.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अश्रुपूर्णमुखीम्  with face full of tears, दीनाम् piteous , शोकभाराभिपीडिताम् inflicted with the burden of sorrow, वायुवेगैः driven by the wind, (सम्)आक्रान्ताम् overpowered, अर्णवे in the sea, मज्जन्तीम् sinking, नावमिव like a boat, मृगयूथपरिभ्रष्टाम् separated from the herd, श्वभिः by hounds, आवृताम् surrounded by, मृगीमिव like a female deer, अधोमुखमुखीम् with head bent, सीताम् to Sita, निशाचर: demon, अभ्येत्य having gone near, शोकपराम् lady full of grief, दीनाम् pathetic woman, अवशाम् not under her own control, ताम् her, सः राक्षसाधिपः king of the demons(Ravana), बलात् forcibly, देवगृहोपमम् like a heavenly home, गृहम् home, दर्शयामास showed.

#### Explanation

The demon Ravana forcibly took Sita, her face full of tears, to show her his heavenlike home. Inflicted with grief she looked wretched. She resembled a female deer hounded out of the herd. With her head bent in grief, she looked pathetic like one not under her control, like a boat sinking under the sea driven by the wind.

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

हर्म्यप्रासादसम्बाधं स्त्रीसहस्रनिषेवितम्। नानापक्षिगणैर्जुष्टं नानारत्नसमन्वितम्।।3.55.7।। दान्तकैस्तापनीयैश्च स्फाटिकै राजतैरपि। वज्रवैडूर्यचित्रैश्च स्तम्भैर्दृष्टिमनोहरैः।।3.55.8।। दिव्यदुन्दुभिनिर्ह्रादं तप्तकाञ्चनतोरणम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

हर्म्यप्रासादसम्बाधम् complex of palaces, स्त्रीसहस्रनिषेवितम् served by thousands of women, नानापक्षिगणैः by different flocks of birds, जुष्टम् attended, नानारत्नसमन्वितम्  studded with a variety of gems, दान्तकैः with ivory, तापनीयैः च and golden, स्फाटिकैः crystals, राजतैरपि silver, वज्रवैडूर्यचित्रैः diamonds and vaiduryas, दृष्टिमनोहरैः captivating, स्तम्भैः with pillars, दिव्यदुन्दुभिनिर्ह्रादम् resounding with wonderful drums, तप्तकाञ्चनतोरणम् having glittering golden entrance.

#### Explanation

His home was a complex of palaces and mansions served by thousands of women. It was an abode of different flocks of birds. It had captivating pillars studded with gold, crystals, silver, diamonds and vaiduryas. It resounded with wonderful sounds of drums. Its golden entrances were glittering.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

हर्म्यप्रासादसम्बाधं स्त्रीसहस्रनिषेवितम्। नानापक्षिगणैर्जुष्टं नानारत्नसमन्वितम्।।3.55.7।। दान्तकैस्तापनीयैश्च स्फाटिकै राजतैरपि। वज्रवैडूर्यचित्रैश्च स्तम्भैर्दृष्टिमनोहरैः।।3.55.8।। दिव्यदुन्दुभिनिर्ह्रादं तप्तकाञ्चनतोरणम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

हर्म्यप्रासादसम्बाधम् complex of palaces, स्त्रीसहस्रनिषेवितम् served by thousands of women, नानापक्षिगणैः by different flocks of birds, जुष्टम् attended, नानारत्नसमन्वितम्  studded with a variety of gems, दान्तकैः with ivory, तापनीयैः च and golden, स्फाटिकैः crystals, राजतैरपि silver, वज्रवैडूर्यचित्रैः diamonds and vaiduryas, दृष्टिमनोहरैः captivating, स्तम्भैः with pillars, दिव्यदुन्दुभिनिर्ह्रादम् resounding with wonderful drums, तप्तकाञ्चनतोरणम् having glittering golden entrance.

#### Explanation

His home was a complex of palaces and mansions served by thousands of women. It was an abode of different flocks of birds. It had captivating pillars studded with gold, crystals, silver, diamonds and vaiduryas. It resounded with wonderful sounds of drums. Its golden entrances were glittering.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

सोपानं काञ्चनं चित्रमारुरोह तया सह।।3.55.9।। दान्तका राजताश्चैव गवाक्षाः प्रियदर्शनाः। हेमजालावताश्चासन्स्तत्र प्रासादपङ्क्तयः।।3.55.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

काञ्चनम् gold, चित्रम् wonderful, सोपानम् staircase, तया सह along with her, आरुरोह he climbed, तत्र there, दान्तकाः ivory, राजताश्च silver, प्रियदर्शनाः beautiful, गवाक्षाः windows, हेमजालावृताः covered by golden trellis, प्रासादपङ्त्कयः rows of mansions, आसन् there were.

#### Explanation

He ascended the wonderful golden staircase taking Sita along with him.Beautiful looked the rows of mansions with silver and ivory windows and golden trellis.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

सोपानं काञ्चनं चित्रमारुरोह तया सह।।3.55.9।। दान्तका राजताश्चैव गवाक्षाः प्रियदर्शनाः। हेमजालावताश्चासन्स्तत्र प्रासादपङ्क्तयः।।3.55.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

काञ्चनम् gold, चित्रम् wonderful, सोपानम् staircase, तया सह along with her, आरुरोह he climbed, तत्र there, दान्तकाः ivory, राजताश्च silver, प्रियदर्शनाः beautiful, गवाक्षाः windows, हेमजालावृताः covered by golden trellis, प्रासादपङ्त्कयः rows of mansions, आसन् there were.

#### Explanation

He ascended the wonderful golden staircase taking Sita along with him.Beautiful looked the rows of mansions with silver and ivory windows and golden trellis.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

सुधामणिविचित्राणि भूमिभागानि सर्वशः। दशग्रीवस्स्वभवने प्रादर्शयत मैथिलीम्।।3.55.11।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

दशग्रीवः the tenheaded Ravana, स्वभवने in his own mansion, सर्वशः all over, सुधामणिविचित्राणि with wonderful crystals, भूमिभागानि  the floors, मैथिलीम् to Sita, the princess from Mithila, प्रादर्शयत showed.

#### Explanation

The tenheaded Ravana showed Sita, the princess from Mithila the floors with wonderful crystals.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

दीर्घिकाः पुष्करिण्यश्च नानावृक्षसमन्विताः। रावणो दर्शयामास सीतां शोकपरायणाम्।।3.55.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रावणः Ravana, दीर्घिकाः stepwells, नानावृक्षसमन्विताः full of different kinds of trees, पुष्करिण्यश्च lotus tanks, शोकपरायणाम् in deep sorrow, सीताम् Sita, दर्शयामास showed.

#### Explanation

Ravana showed Sita who was overwhelmed with sorrow, stepwells surrrounded by various trees and tanks.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

दर्शयित्वा तु वैदेह्याः कृत्स्नं तद्भवनोत्तमम्। उवाच वाक्यं पापात्मा सीतां लोभितुमिच्छया।।3.55.13।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पापात्मा sinner, कृत्स्नम् entirely, तत् that, भवनोत्तमम् best of mansions, वैदेह्याः to Vaidehi, दर्शयित्वा on showing, सीताम् Sita, इच्छया desiring to, लोभितुम् to allure, वाक्यम् these words, उवाच said.

#### Explanation

The sinful Ravana showed Sita, princess of Videha, the best of all his mansions in order to allure her, saying:

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

दश राक्षसकोट्यश्च द्वाविंशतिरथापराः। तेषां प्रभुरहं सीते सर्वेषां भीमकर्मणाम्।।3.55.14।। वर्जयित्वा जरावृद्धान्बालांश्च रजनीचरान्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सीते O Sita, जरावृद्धान् the aged, बालांश्च children, रजनीचरान् night rangers, वर्जयित्वा apart from, दश ten, राक्षसकोट्यश्च crores of demons, अथ and, अपराः over that, द्वाविंशतिः twentytwo crore, भीमकर्मणाम् terrorists, तेषाम् them, सर्वेषाम् for all, अहम् I am, प्रभुः the king.

#### Explanation

O Sita, leaving aside children and the aged, I am the lord of crores of nightrangers in addition to ten crore demons and twentytwo crore terrorists.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

सहस्रमेकमेकस्य मम कार्यपुरस्सरम्।।3.55.15।। यदिदं राजतन्त्रं मे त्वयि सर्वं प्रतिष्ठितम्। जीवितं च विशालाक्षि त्वं मे प्राणैर्गरीयसी।।3.55.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

एकस्य for me alone, मम for me, एकम् one, सहस्रम् thousand, कार्यपुरस्सरम् attending, यत् since, इदम् this, राज्यतन्त्रम् the administration of the kingdom, सर्वम् all, जीवितं च life also, त्वयि you, प्रतिष्ठितम् is resting, विशालाक्षि O largeeyed lady, त्वम् you, मे my, प्राणैः more than life, गरीयसी dearer.

#### Explanation

For me alone there are a thousand attendants.The  administration of the kingdom and all my subjects will be under your control. O largeeyed Sita you are dearer to me than my life.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

सहस्रमेकमेकस्य मम कार्यपुरस्सरम्।।3.55.15।। यदिदं राजतन्त्रं मे त्वयि सर्वं प्रतिष्ठितम्। जीवितं च विशालाक्षि त्वं मे प्राणैर्गरीयसी।।3.55.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

एकस्य for me alone, मम for me, एकम् one, सहस्रम् thousand, कार्यपुरस्सरम् attending, यत् since, इदम् this, राज्यतन्त्रम् the administration of the kingdom, सर्वम् all, जीवितं च life also, त्वयि you, प्रतिष्ठितम् is resting, विशालाक्षि O largeeyed lady, त्वम् you, मे my, प्राणैः more than life, गरीयसी dearer.

#### Explanation

For me alone there are a thousand attendants.The  administration of the kingdom and all my subjects will be under your control. O largeeyed Sita you are dearer to me than my life.

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

बहूनांस्त्रीसहस्राणां मम योऽसौ परिग्रहः। तासां त्वमीश्वरा सीते मम भार्या भव प्रिये।।3.55.17।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सीते Sita, बहूनाम् of many, स्त्री सहस्राणाम् thousands of women, यः such, असौ this, परिग्रहः married to me, तासाम् to all of them, त्वम् you, ईश्वरी chief, भव be, प्रिये O dear, मम my, भार्या wife, भव be.

#### Explanation

O Sita dear  be my chief queen among the many thousands of women I have married.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

साधु किं तेऽन्यया बुद्ध्या रोचयस्व वचो मम। भजस्वमाभितप्तस्य प्रसादं कर्तुमर्हसि।।3.55.18।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ते to you, अन्यया by another, बुद्ध्या by thought, किम् what, साधु be good, मम वचः my words, रोचयस्व you may accept, अभितप्तस्य consumed by thoughts of you, मम me, प्रसादम् grace, कर्तुम् to do, अर्हसि should.

#### Explanation

Do not think otherwise. Be good and accept my request. I am consumed by thoughts of you. You should be gracious to me.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

परिक्षिप्ता समुद्रेण लङ्केयं शतयोजना। नेयं धर्षयितुं शक्या सेन्द्रैरपि सुरासुरैः।।3.55.19।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

शतयोजना extinding to a hundred yojanas, इयम् this, लङ्का Lanka, समुद्रेण by the sea, परिक्षिप्ता surrounded, सेन्द्रैः by deities including Indra, सुरासुरै रपि even sods and demons, इयम् this, धर्षयितुम् to assault, न शक्या not possible.

#### Explanation

This Lanka extending to a hundred yojanas and surrounded by the sea is vulnerable to neither demons nor gods including Indra.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

न देवेषु न यक्षेषु न गन्धर्वेषु पक्षिषु। अहं पश्यामि लोकेषु यो मे वीर्यसमो भवेत्।।3.55.20।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लोकेषु in the world, यः whoever, मे to me, वीर्यसमः equal in valour, भवेत् may be, अहम् I, देवेषु among gods, न पश्यामि I do not not see, यक्षेषु among yakshas, न not, गन्धर्वेषु in gandharvas, न not, पक्षिषु in birds, न not.

#### Explanation

I do not see any one equal to me in valour among gods, or yakshas or gandharvas or birds.

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

राज्यभ्रष्टेन दीनेन तापसेन गतायुषा। किं करिष्यसि रामेण मानुषेणाल्पतेजसा।।3.55.21।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राज्यभ्रष्टेन who lost his kingdom, दीनेन by a dejected person, तापसेन an ascetic, गतायुषा with a short life one, मानुषेण human being, अल्पतेजसा with one who lacks lustre, रामेण Rama, किं करिष्यसि what is the use for you ,

#### Explanation

Of what use is Rama who has lost his kingdom, who is depressed and leads an ascetic life and who is a shortlived, lacklustre human being.

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

भजस्व सीते मामेव भर्ताहं सदृशस्तव। यौवनं ह्यध्रुवं भीरु रमस्वेह मया सह।।3.55.22।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सीते O Sita, मामेव me alone, भजस्व accept, अहम् I am, तव your, सदृशः befitting, भर्ता husband, भीरु timid one, यौवनम् youth, अध्रुवं हि is not permanent, इह here, मया सह with me, रमस्व may enjoy.

#### Explanation

O Sita take refuge only in me. I am a befitting husband to you. O timid one youth is impermanent. Enjoy (your life) with me while it lasts.

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

दर्शने माकृथा बुद्धिं राघवस्य वरानने। कास्य शक्तिरिहागन्तुमपि सीते मनोरथैः।।3.55.23।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वरानने O lovely Sita, राघवस्य Rama's, दर्शने in seeing, बुद्धिं thought, मा do not, कृथा: entertain, सीते O Sita, अस्य his, मनोरथैरपि with his mind also, इह here, आगन्तुम् to come, शक्तिः power, का where is it?

#### Explanation

O lovely Sita, give up the thought of seeing Rama. Where does he possess the power to reach this place even on the mental plane ?

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

न शक्यो वायुराकाशे पाशैर्बद्धुं महाजवः। दीप्यमानस्य वाप्यग्नेर्गृहीतुं विमलां शिखाम्।।3.55.24।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महाजवः of high speed, वायुः wind, आकाशे in the sky, पाशैः by rope, बध्धुम् to bind, न शक्यः not possible, दीप्यमानस्य  of burning flame, आग्नेः fire, विमलाम् brilliant , शिखाम् flame, गृहीतुम् to catch.

#### Explanation

It is not possible to bind  the wind blowing at high speed in the sky. Similarly it is not possible to catch the brilliant glow of flaming fire.

---

### Shloka 25

#### Shloka Text

त्रयाणामपि लोकानां न तं पश्यामि शोभने। विक्रमेण नयेद्यस्त्वां मद्बाहुपरिपालिताम्।।3.55.25।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

शोभने O beautiful lady, त्रयाणाम् in the three, लोकानामपि worlds too, यः whoever, मद्बाहुपरिपालिताम् protected by my arms, त्वाम् you, विक्रमेण by valour, नयेत् can take away, तम् him, न पश्यामि not see.

#### Explanation

O lovely lady, I do not see any one in these three worlds who can, by his valour, take you away from me guarded by my arms.

---

### Shloka 26

#### Shloka Text

लङ्कायां सुमहद्राज्यमिदं त्वमनुपालय। त्वत्प्रेष्या मद्विधाश्चैव देवाश्चापि चराचराः।।3.55.26।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

त्वम् you, लङ्कायां in Lanka's, सुमहत् vast, इदम् this, राज्यम् kingdom, अनुपालय you may rule, मद्विधाः people like me, देवाश्चापि even gods, चराचराः all mobile and immobile beings, त्वत्प्रेष्याः will be at beck and call.

#### Explanation

Rule this vast kingdom of Lanka. People like me, even gods and all beings, mobile and immobile, will be at your beck and call.

---

### Shloka 27

#### Shloka Text

अभिषेकोदकक्लिन्ना तुष्टा च रमयस्व माम्। दुष्कृतं यत्परा कर्म वनवासेन तद्गतम्।।3.55.27।। यश्च ते सुकृतो धर्मस्तस्येह फलमाप्नुहि।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अभिषेकोदकक्लिन्ना drenched with waters of consecration, तुष्टा च and satisfied , माम् me, रमयस्व enjoy, पुरा previous birth, यत् whatever, दुष्कृतम् evil deeds, कर्म तत् that, वनवासेन by dwelling in the forest, गतम् gone, ते your, यः whatever, सुकृतः  good deeds, धर्मः righteousness, तस्य of that, फलम् fruit, इह here, आप्नुहि enjoy.

#### Explanation

Drenched with the waters of consecration, entertain me. The effect of the bad deeds done in the past are over with your dwelling in the forest. Now enjoy the fruits  of your good and righteous deeds here with me.

---

### Shloka 28

#### Shloka Text

इह माल्यानि सर्वाणि दिव्यगन्धानि मैथिलि।।3.55.28।। भूषणानि च मुख्यानि सेवस्व च मया सह।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मैथिलि Maithili, इह here, दिव्यगन्धानि of divine fragrance, सर्वाणि all, माल्यानि garlands, मुख्यानि finest, भूषणानि च jewels, मया सह with me, सेवस्व you put on.

#### Explanation

O princess from Mithila, put on all garlands of wonderful fragrance and finest of jewels here with me.

---

### Shloka 29

#### Shloka Text

पुष्पकं नाम सुश्रोणि भ्रातुर्वैश्रवणस्य मे।।3.55.29।। विमानं सूर्यसङ्काशं तरसा निर्जितं मया।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सुश्रोणि O charming lady, मे my, भ्रातुः brother, वैश्रवणस्य Vaisravana's (Kubera's), पुष्पकं नाम called Pushpaka, सूर्यसङ्काशम् radiant like the Sun, विमानम् aerial chariot, तरसा with strength, निर्जितम् is won over.

#### Explanation

O finehipped lady, I won by my strength from my brother Vaisravana (Kubera) an aerial chariot called Pushpaka shining with the radiance of the Sun.

---

### Shloka 30

#### Shloka Text

विशालं रमणीयं च तद्विमानमनुत्तम्।।3.55.30।। तत्र सीते मया सार्धं विहरस्व यथासुखम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सीते O Sita, अनुत्तमम् excellent, तत् that, विमानम् chariot, विशालम् huge, रमणीयं च beautiful, तत्र on that, मया सार्धम् along with me, यथासुखम् happily, विहरस्व can roam with pleasure.

#### Explanation

O Sita that excellent chariot is big and beautiful. Where on you can roam happily along with me.

---

### Shloka 31

#### Shloka Text

वदनं पद्मसङ्काशममलं चारुदर्शनम्। शोकार्तंतु वरारोहे न भ्राजति वरानने।।3.55.31।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वरारोहे O heavyhipped lady, वरानने lady with  a comely face , पद्मसङ्काशम् looking like lotus, अमलम् clean, चारुदर्शनम् pleasing to the eye, वदनम् face, शोकार्तम् striken with grief, न भ्राजति is not shining.

#### Explanation

O heavyhipped lady your face which is a clean lotus pleasing to the eye has lost its shine, stricken with grief.

---

### Shloka 32

#### Shloka Text

एवं वदति तस्मित्सा वस्त्रान्तेन वराङ्गना। पिधायेन्दुनिभं सीता मुखमश्रूण्यवर्तयत्।।3.55.32।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तस्मिन् while he, एवम् in that manner, वदति was talking, वराङ्गना the best of beauty, सा सीता that Sita, इन्दुनिभम् Moonlike, मुखम् face, वस्त्रान्तेन by the edge of the garment, पिधाय covering, अश्रूणि tears, अवर्तयत् stood.

#### Explanation

As Ravana went on speaking thus, Sita covered her beautiful, moonlike face with the edge of her garment and stood shedding tears.

---

### Shloka 33

#### Shloka Text

ध्यायन्तीं तामिवास्वस्थां दीनां चिन्ताहतप्रभाम्। उवाच वचनं पापो रावणो राक्षसेश्वरः।।3.55.33।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पापः sinful, राक्षसेश्वरः lord of the demons, रावणः Ravana, ध्यायन्तीमिव as though in a contemplative mood, अस्वस्थाम् who was not well, दीनाम् piteously, चिन्ताहतप्रभाम् her beauty dulled by her anxiety, ताम् her, वचनम् these words, उवाच said.

#### Explanation

While Sita was lost in thought, feeling ill at lase, looking wretched with her beauty dulled by her anxiety, the sinful lord of the demons continued :

---

### Shloka 34

#### Shloka Text

अलं व्रीडेन वैदेहि धर्मलोपकृतेन च।।3.55.34।। आर्षोऽयं दैवनिष्यन्दो यस्त्वामभिगमिष्यति।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वैदेहि Sita, धर्मलोपकृतेन thinking it to be a violation of righteous conduct, व्रीडेन feeling shamed, अलम् no need to, यः whatever, त्वाम् अभिगमिष्यति will come your way, अयम् this one, दैवनिष्यन्दः granted by fate, आर्षः acceptable to sages.

#### Explanation

O Sita you need not feel shamed that it is a violation of righteous conduct. Take it as godsend which has the approval of sages.

---

### Shloka 35

#### Shloka Text

एतौ पादौ मया स्निग्धौ शिरोभिः परिपीडितौ।।3.55.35।। प्रसादं कुरु मे क्षिप्रं वश्यो दासोऽहमस्मिते।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

एतौ these two, स्निग्धौ shining brightly, पादौ feet, मया शिरोभिः by my heads too, परिपीडितौ are touched and pressed, क्षिप्रम् quickly, प्रसादम् favour, कुरु do, अहम् I am, ते to you, वश्यः under your control, दासः slave, अस्मि I am.

#### Explanation

I am bowing down, pressing my heads at your shining feet. I am at your mercy. Grant me your favourI will remain your slave and remain ever under your control.

---

### Shloka 36

#### Shloka Text

इमाश्शून्य मया वाचश्शुष्यमाणेन भाषिताः।।3.55.36।। न चापि रावणः काञ्चिन्मूर्ध्ना स्त्रीं प्रणमेत ह।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

शुष्यमाणेन with parched throat, मया by myself, इमाः these, शून्याः desolate, वाचः words, भाषिताः spoken, रावणः Ravana, काञ्चित् स्त्रीं any woman, मूर्ध्ना  head bowed down, न प्रणमेत हि will not pay obeisance.

#### Explanation

My throat has dried up. I have spoken these words in a forlorn state. Ravana has never bowed down to a woman in obeisance.

---

### Shloka 37

#### Shloka Text

एवमुक्त्वा दशग्रीवो मैथिलीं जनकात्मजाम्।।3.55.37।। कृतान्तवशमापन्नो ममेयमिति मन्यते।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

दशग्रीवः the tenheaded Ravana, जनकात्मजाम् to the daughter of Janaka, मैथिलीम् Maithili, एवम् in that manner, उक्त्वा having said, कृतान्तवशम् under the control of Yama, आपन्नः being distressed, इयम् she is, मम mine, इति like this, मन्यते thinks.

#### Explanation

Having said this to the daughter of Janaka, the tenheaded Ravana who had fallen under the control of Yama, lord of death, misconceived that she had become his own. इतयार्ष श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये अरण्यकाण्डे पञ्चपञ्चाशस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the fiftyfifth sarga of Aranyakanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 56

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

सा तथोक्ता तु वैदेही निर्भया शोककर्शिता। तृणमन्तरतः कृत्वा रावणं प्रत्यभाषत।।3.56.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation
