तथा thus, उक्ता having addressed, शोककर्शिता sunk in deep sorrow, सा वैदेही that Sita, princess from Videha, निर्भया unafraid , तृणम्  blade of grass, अन्तरतः between him and her, कृत्वा placed, रावणम् Ravana, प्रत्यभाषत replied.

#### Explanation

Thus addressed by Ravana, Sita, immersed in deep sorrow and unafraid of him, placed  a blade of grass between her and him (intending not to speak to him  straight) and replied:

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

राजा दशरथो नाम धर्मसेतुरिवाचलः। सत्यसन्धः परिज्ञातो यस्य पुत्रस्सराघवः।।3.56.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

दशरथो नाम named Dasaratha, राजा king, अचलः steadfast, धर्मसेतुरिव as an embankment (protector) of righteousness, सत्यसन्धः true to his promise, परिज्ञातः renowned, सः राघवः that Rama, यस्य whose, पुत्रः son.

#### Explanation

Rama is a son of  the renowned king Dasaratha, who was a firm ambankment (protector) of righteousness and was true to his promise.

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

रामो नाम स धर्मात्मा त्रिषु लोकेषु विश्रुतः। दीर्घबाहुर्विशालाक्षो दैवतं हि पतिर्मम।।3.56.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

त्रिषु in the three, लोकेषु in the worlds, विश्रुतः highly renowned, दीर्घबाहुः longarmed, विशालाक्षः largeeyed, रामो नाम named Rama, सः he, धर्मात्मा righteous soul, मम my, पतिः husband, दैवतम् god to all.

#### Explanation

The longarmed, largeeyed Rama is famous in all the three worlds. He is my husband, a righteous soul and a god to all.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

इक्ष्वाकूणां कुले जातस्सिंहस्कन्धो महाद्युतिः। लक्ष्मणेन सह भ्रात्रा यस्ते प्राणान्हरिष्यति।।3.56.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

इक्ष्वाकूणाम् of Ikshvakus, कुले in the dynasty, जातः born, सिंहस्कन्धः shoulders of a lion, महाद्युतिः  effulgent, भ्रात्रा with his brother, लक्ष्मणेन सह along with Lakshmana, यः who, ते your, प्राणान् life, हरिष्यति will take away.

#### Explanation

The brilliant Rama of the Ikshvaku dynasty with the lion's shoulders will come with his brother Lakshmana and take away your life.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

प्रत्यक्षं यद्यहं तस्य त्वया स्यां धर्षिता बलात्। शयिता त्वं हतस्संख्ये जनस्थाने यथा खरः।।3.56.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अहम् I, त्वया by your, तस्य his, प्रत्यक्षम् (in his) presence, बलात् with force, धर्षिता attacked, स्यां यदि if you were to be, त्वम् you, जनस्थाने in Janasthana, खरः Khara, यथा just as, हतः killed, शयिता lying down.

#### Explanation

Had you abducted me forcibly in his presence, you would have been lying down dead just as Khara in Janasthana.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

य एते राक्षसाः प्रोक्ता घोररूपा महाबलाः। राघवे निर्विषास्सर्वे सुपर्णे पन्नगा यथा।।3.56.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

घोररूपाः of dreadful forms, महाबलाः very strong , ये those, एते such of, राक्षसाः demons, प्रोक्ताः has been said, सर्वे all, सुपर्णे Suparna, पन्नगाः serpents, यथा like, राघवे with regard to Rama, निर्विषाः are devoid of poison.

#### Explanation

Those dreadful, mighty demons you have described are ineffective in his presence just like the serpents who with their venom are ineffective  in the presence of  Suparna (Garuda).

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

तस्य ज्याविप्रमुक्तास्ते शराः काञ्चनभूषणाः। शरीरं विधमिष्यन्ति गङ्गाकूलमिवोर्मयः।।3.56.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तस्य his, ज्याविप्रमुक्ताः released from the string of the bow, काञ्चनभूषणाः goldtipped, शराः arrows, ऊर्मयः waves, गङ्गाकूलमिव like the bank of the Ganges, ते शरीरम् your body, विधमिष्यन्ति will shatter.

#### Explanation

The goldtipped arrows released from his bowstring will shatter your body like the waves erode the banks of river Ganga.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

असुरैर्वा सुरैर्वा त्वं यद्यवध्योऽसि रावण। उत्पाद्य सुमहद्वैरं जीवंस्तस्य न मोक्ष्यसे।।3.56.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रावण O Ravana, त्वम् you, असुरैर्वा by demons, सुरैर्वा or by gods, अवध्यः are not to be killed, असि यदि even if, सुमहत् great, वैरम् enmity, उत्पाद्य after creating, जीवन् with life, तस्य his, न मोक्ष्यसे you will not be released.

#### Explanation

O Ravana, even if you stand invulnerable to gods and demons, you will not come out alive in the combat with Rama with whom you have earned great enmity.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

स ते जीवितशेषस्य राघवोऽन्तकरो बली। पशोर्यूपगतस्येव जीवितं तव दुर्लभम्।।3.56.9।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

बली powerful, सः राघवः  Rama, ते your, जीवितशेषस्य of the little life left, अन्तकरः destroyer of life, यूपगतस्य  tied to the sacrificial post, पशोरिव like a beast ready for sacrifice, तव your, जीवितम् life, दुर्लभम् difficult to survive.

#### Explanation

Powerful Rama will put an end to whatever little life you are left with. Just as a beast tied to a sacrificial post, you will not  survive.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

यदि पश्येत्स रामस्त्वां रोषदीप्तेन चक्षुषा। रक्षस्त्वमद्य निर्धग्धो गच्छेस्सद्यः पराभवम्।।3.56.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रक्षः O demon, सः रामः that Rama, रोषदीप्तेन burning with anger, चक्षुषा with eyes, त्वाम् you, पश्येत् if he sees, अद्य now, सद्यः instantly, त्वम् you, निर्धग्धः totally burnt, पराभवम् गच्छेः you will be destroyed.

#### Explanation

O demon, you will be completely consumed if Rama looks at you with eyes burning in anger.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

यश्चन्द्रं नभसो भूमौ पातयेन्नाशयेत वा। सागरं शोषयेद्वापि स सीतां मोचयेदिह।।3.56.11।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

यः he who, चन्द्रम् Moon, नभसः from the sky, भूमौ on the earth, पातयेत् can bring down, वा or even, नाशयेत destroy, सागरम् sea, शोषयेद्वापि will make it dry, सः he, इह here, सीताम् Sita, मोचयेत् will liberate

#### Explanation

He who can bring down the Moon to the earth from the sky or even dash it down, he who can drain the sea dry will definitely release me from here.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

गतायुस्त्वं गतश्रीको गतसत्त्व गतेन्द्रियः। लङ्का वैधव्यसंयुक्ता त्वत्कृतेन भविष्यति।।3.56.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

त्वम् you, गतायुः life is gone, गतश्रीकः deprived of your fortune, गतसत्त्व your strength sapped, गतेन्द्रियः your senses dissipated, त्वत्कृतेन  by  your action, लङ्का Lanka, वैधव्यसंयुक्ता भविष्यति attain widowhood.

#### Explanation

Your life is coming to an end. Your fortunes will wane, your strength will be sapped. Your senses will be dissipated. By your deeds Lanka will attain widowhood.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

न ते पापमिदं कर्म शुखोदर्कं भविष्यति। याऽहं नीता विनाभावं पतिपार्श्वात्त्वया वने।।3.56.13।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ते you, इदम् this, कर्म deed, शुखोदर्कम् will result in happiness, न भविष्यति will not be, या which, अहम् I, त्वया by you, वने in the forest, पतिपार्श्वात् from my husband, विनाभावम् without any feeling, नीता I am brought.

#### Explanation

This evil deed(of abduction) will not yield happy results.You have brought me from my husband from the forest without any forethought.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

स हि दैवतसंयुक्तो मम भर्ता महाद्युतिः। निर्भयो वीर्यमाश्रित्य शून्ये वसति दण्डके।।3.56.14।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महाद्युतिः effulgent, मम my, भर्ता husband, सः he, वीर्यम् valour, आश्रित्य resorting to, निर्भयः a fearless, दैवतसंयुक्तः supported by divine power, शून्ये in a desolate, दण्डके in Dandaka, वसति is dwelling.

#### Explanation

My effulgent, valiant husband armed with divine power resides fearlessly in the desolate Dandaka.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

स ते दर्पं बलं वीर्यमुत्सेकं च तथाविधम्। अपनेष्यति गात्रेभ्यश्शरवर्षेण संयुगे।।3.56.15।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः he, संयुगे in combat, शरवर्षेण with showers of arrows, ते your, गात्रेभ्यः from limbs, दर्पम् pride, बलम् power, वीर्यम् heroism, तथाविधम् as such, उत्सेकं च haughtiness, अपनेष्यति will remove.

#### Explanation

He will remove your pride, your power, your valour and haughtiness from your limbs with a shower of arrows in the fight.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

यदा विनाशो भूतानां दृश्यते कालचोदितः। तदा कार्ये प्रमाद्यन्ति नराः कालवशं गताः।।3.56.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

भूतानाम् for the living beings, यदा whenever, कालचोदितः prompted by time (death), विनाशः destruction, दृश्यते is seen, तदा then, नराः men, कालवशम् overcome by time, गताः become, कार्ये deeds, प्रमाद्यन्ति commit blunders.

#### Explanation

When destruction draws near, people being prompted by time start commiting blunders in their deeds.

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

मां प्रधृष्य स ते कालः प्राप्तोऽयं राक्षसाधम। आत्मनो राक्षसानां च वधायान्तःपुरस्य च।।3.56.17।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षसाधम O vilest of demons, माम् me, प्रधृष्य having assaulted, आत्मनः your, राक्षसानां च of all  demons, अन्तःपुरस्य च and of women of the harem, वधाय for destruction, सः कालः that time, ते to you, प्राप्तः has come near.

#### Explanation

You vilest of demons, since you have assaulted me, the time of your death and destruction of all demons and of all women in the harem has drawn near.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

न शक्या यज्ञमध्यस्था वेदी सृग्भाण्डमण्डिता। द्विजातिमन्त्रपूता च चण्डालेनावमर्दितुम्।।3.56.18।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

यज्ञमध्यस्था in the midst of the sacrificial ceremony, सुग्भाण्डमण्डिता decorated with ladles  and other vessels, द्विजातिमन्त्रपूता च sanctified by brahmins uttering sacred Vedic hymns, वेदी altar, चण्डालेन by a man of low caste, अवमर्दितुम् to violate, न शक्या is not possible.

#### Explanation

The sacrificial altar adorned with ladles and vessels and sanctified by the sacred mantras recited by brahmins cannot be defiled by a chandala (man of lowest caste).

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

तथाऽहं धर्मनित्यस्य धर्मपत्नी पतिव्रता। त्वया स्पृष्टुं न शक्यास्मि राक्षसाधम पापिना।।3.56.19।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षसाधम lowly demon, तथा similarly, धर्मनित्यस्य of the ever righteous, धर्मपत्नी rigteous wife, पतिव्रता chaste woman, अहम् I am, पापिना sinner, त्वया you, स्पृष्टुम् to touch, शक्या possible, नास्मि not.

#### Explanation

O lowly demon  similarly I am a chaste woman and wife of the ever righteous Rama who cannot be defiled by a sinner like you.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

क्रीडन्ती राजहंसेन पद्मषण्डेषु नित्यदा। हंसी सा तृणषण्डस्थं कथं पश्येत मद्गुकम्।।3.56.20।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पद्मषण्डेषु lotus ponds, नित्यदा always, राजहंसेन with royal swan, क्रीडन्ती sporting, सा हंसी for that female swan, तृणषण्डस्थम् resting on grass land, मद्गुकम् watercrow, कथम् how can, पश्येत will see.

#### Explanation

How can a female swan ever sporting in lotusponds in the company of a royal swan look at the watercrow resting on a grassy land?

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

इदं शरीरं निस्संज्ञं बन्ध वा खातयस्व वा। नेदं शरीरं रक्षयं मे जीवितं वापि राक्षस।।3.56.21।। न तु शक्ष्याम्युपक्रोशं पृथिव्यां दातुमात्मनः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षस demon, निस्संज्ञम् unconscious, इदम् this, शरीरम् body, बन्ध वा bind it on, वा खातयस्व or bury, मे to me, इदम् this, शरीरम् body, जीवितं वा even life, रक्षयम् to protect, न not, तु but, पृथिव्याम् on earth, आत्मन: of myself, उपक्रोशम् ignominy, दातुम् to give, न शक्ष्यामि not possible.

#### Explanation

O demon  this body has (now) no consciousness. You may bind it or bury it. I do not think that this body or even this life need to be protected. But at no cost can I make this an object of censure on earth (must not be branded an unchaste woman).

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

एवमुक्त्वा तु वैदेही क्रोधात्सुपरुषं वचः।।3.56.22।। रावणं मैथिली तत्र पुनर्नोवाच किञ्चन।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वैदेही Sita (Vaidehi), मैथिली princess from Mithila, क्रोधात् in anger, रावणम् Ravana, एवम् in that way, सुपरुषम् very harsh, वचः words, उक्त्वा having uttered, तत्र there, पुनः again, किञ्चन not, नोवाच speak.

#### Explanation

Having uttered these very harsh words out of anger, the daughter of Videha, the princess from Mithila spoke no more.

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

सीताया वचनं श्रुत्वा परुषं रोमहर्षणम्।।3.56.23।। प्रत्युवाच ततस्सीतां भयसन्दर्शनं वचः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सीतायाः Sita's such, परुषम् harsh, रोमहर्षणम् horripilating, वचनम् words, श्रुत्वा having heard, ततः then, सीताम् Sita, भयसन्दर्शनम् creating fear, वचः words, प्रत्युवाच  replied.

#### Explanation

Having heard such harsh, horripilating words from Sita, Ravana replied in a way designed to create fear in her:

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

शृणु मैथिलि मद्वाक्यं मासान्द्वादश भामिनि।।3.56.24।। कालेनानेन नाभ्येषि यदि मां चारुहासिनि। ततस्त्वां प्रातराशार्थं सूदाश्छेत्स्यन्ति लेशशः।।3.56.25।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मैथिलि Princess from Mithila, भामिनि lovely one, मद्वाक्यम् my statement, शृणु listen, द्वादश twelve, मासान् months, चारुहासिनि a lady with sweet smile, अनेन otherwise, कालेन in course of time, माम् me, नाभ्येषि यदि if you do not accept my hand, ततः thereupon, सूदाः cooks, त्वाम् you, प्रातराशार्थे for breakfast, लेशशः in small bits, छेत्स्यन्ति will cut you.

#### Explanation

O comely princess from Mithila, O lady with a sweet smile listen to my words. If you do not accept my hand in the course of twelve months, the cooks will cut you off into small bits for the (my) breakfast.

---

### Shloka 25

#### Shloka Text

शृणु मैथिलि मद्वाक्यं मासान्द्वादश भामिनि।।3.56.24।। कालेनानेन नाभ्येषि यदि मां चारुहासिनि। ततस्त्वां प्रातराशार्थं सूदाश्छेत्स्यन्ति लेशशः।।3.56.25।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मैथिलि Princess from Mithila, भामिनि lovely one, मद्वाक्यम् my statement, शृणु listen, द्वादश twelve, मासान् months, चारुहासिनि a lady with sweet smile, अनेन otherwise, कालेन in course of time, माम् me, नाभ्येषि यदि if you do not accept my hand, ततः thereupon, सूदाः cooks, त्वाम् you, प्रातराशार्थे for breakfast, लेशशः in small bits, छेत्स्यन्ति will cut you.

#### Explanation

O comely princess from Mithila, O lady with a sweet smile listen to my words. If you do not accept my hand in the course of twelve months, the cooks will cut you off into small bits for the (my) breakfast.

---

### Shloka 26

#### Shloka Text

इत्युक्त्वा परुषं वाक्यं रावणश्शत्रुरावणः। राक्षसीश्च ततः क्रुद्ध इदं वचनमब्रवीत्।।3.56.26।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

शत्रुरावणः who makes enemies wail, रावणः Ravana, इति these, परुषम् harsh, वाक्यम् words, उक्त्वा having said, ततः then, क्रुद्धः enraged, राक्षसीः demoness, इदम् these, वचनम् words, अब्रवीत् said.

#### Explanation

Enraged Ravana, who used to make enemies wail, turned to the demonesses and said these words.

---

### Shloka 27

#### Shloka Text

शीघ्रमेव हि राक्षस्यो विकृता घोरदर्शनाः। दर्पमस्या विनेष्यध्वं मांसशोणितभोजनाः।।3.56.27।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

विकृताः loathsome, घोरदर्शनाः fearful, मांसशोणितभोजनाः flesheaters and bloodsulkers, राक्षस्यः demonesses, शीघ्रमेव at once, अस्याः her, दर्पम् pride, विनेष्यध्वम् humble.

#### Explanation

Addressing the flesheating, blooddrinking, loathsome and fearful demonesses Ravana said, Humble her pride at once.

---

### Shloka 28

#### Shloka Text

वचनादेव तास्तस्य सुघोरा राक्षसीगणाः। कृतप्राञ्जलयो भूत्वा मैथिलीं पर्यवारयन्।।3.56.28।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सुघोराः very dreadful one, ताः they, राक्षसीगणाः demonesses, तस्य his, वचनादेव soon after he said, कृत प्राञ्जलयः (offered obeisance) with folded hands, भूत्वा being, मैथिलीम् पर्यवारयन् surrounded Sita.

#### Explanation

No sooner had Ravana said it than the dreadful demonesses offered obeisance to him with folded hands and surrounded Sita.

---

### Shloka 29

#### Shloka Text

स ताः प्रोवाच राजा तु रावणो घोरदर्शनः। प्रचाल्य चरणोत्कर्षैर्दारयन्निव मेदिनीम्।।3.56.29।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

घोरदर्शनः of terrific form, राजा king, सः रावणः that Ravana, चरणोत्कर्षैः stamping his feet, मेदिनीम् on the ground, प्रचाल्य after shaking, दारयन्निव as if tearing the ground, ताः to them, प्रोवाच said.

#### Explanation

Stamping his feet and shaking the ground as if tearing the earth, Ravana said to those fiercelooking demonesses:

---

### Shloka 30

#### Shloka Text

अशोकवनिकामध्ये मैथिली नीयतामियम्। तत्रेयं रक्ष्यतां गूढं युष्माभिः परिवारिता।।3.56.30।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

इयं मैथिली this Maithili (sita), अशोकवनिकामध्ये in the middle of Asoka grove, नीयताम् take, तत्र there, इयम् she, युष्माभिः by you, परिवारिता surrounded by, गूढम् secretly, रक्ष्यताम् protect.

#### Explanation

Let this Sita be led into the Asoka grove. There sit round her and protect her in secret (her presence there should not be made public).

---

### Shloka 31

#### Shloka Text

तत्रैनां तर्जनैर्घोरैः पुनस्सान्त्वैश्च मैथिलीम्। आनयध्वं वशं सर्वा वन्यां गजवधूमिव।।3.56.31।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तत्र there, सर्वाः all, एनां मैथिलीम् this Sita, घोरैः terrrific, तर्जनैः threatening, पुनः again, सान्त्वश्च by gently coaxing, वन्याम् wild, गजवधूमिव like a female elephant, वशम् under control, आनयध्वम् being her.

#### Explanation

There you all bring her under control through forcing and cajoling as one would do to tame a wild sheelephant.

---

### Shloka 32

#### Shloka Text

इति प्रतिसमादिष्टा राक्षस्यो रावणेन ताः। अशोकवनिकां जग्मुर्मैथिलीं परिगृह्य तु।।3.56.32।। सर्वकालफलैर्वृक्षैर्नानापुष्पफलैर्वृताम्। सर्वकालमदैश्चापि द्विजैस्समुपसेविताम्।।3.56.33।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रावणेन Ravana, इति like that, प्रतिसमादिष्टाः instructed, ताः those, राक्षस्यः demonesses, मैथिलीम् to Sita, परिगृह्य  took, सर्वकालफलैः with fruits of all seasons, नानापुष्पफलैः with different kinds of flowers and fruits, वृक्षैः with trees, वृताम् surrounded, सर्वकालमदैः cheerful in all seaons, द्विजैः birds, समुपसेविताम् served by, अशोकवनिकाम् to the Asoka grove, जग्मुः went.

#### Explanation

Thus instructed by Ravana, the demonesses took Sita into the Asoka grove, which was full of perenmal fruits and flowers of different kinds, where trees yielded fruits of all seasons and birds were always cheerful.

---

### Shloka 33

#### Shloka Text

इति प्रतिसमादिष्टा राक्षस्यो रावणेन ताः। अशोकवनिकां जग्मुर्मैथिलीं परिगृह्य तु।।3.56.32।। सर्वकालफलैर्वृक्षैर्नानापुष्पफलैर्वृताम्। सर्वकालमदैश्चापि द्विजैस्समुपसेविताम्।।3.56.33।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रावणेन Ravana, इति like that, प्रतिसमादिष्टाः instructed, ताः those, राक्षस्यः demonesses, मैथिलीम् to Sita, परिगृह्य  took, सर्वकालफलैः with fruits of all seasons, नानापुष्पफलैः with different kinds of flowers and fruits, वृक्षैः with trees, वृताम् surrounded, सर्वकालमदैः cheerful in all seaons, द्विजैः birds, समुपसेविताम् served by, अशोकवनिकाम् to the Asoka grove, जग्मुः went.

#### Explanation

Thus instructed by Ravana, the demonesses took Sita into the Asoka grove, which was full of perenmal fruits and flowers of different kinds, where trees yielded fruits of all seasons and birds were always cheerful.

---

### Shloka 34

#### Shloka Text

सा तु शोकपरीताङ्गी मैथिली जनकात्मजा। राक्षसीवशमापन्ना व्याघ्रीणां हरिणी यथा।।3.56.34।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

शोकपरीताङ्गी engulfed in grief, जनकात्मजा daughter of Janaka, सा मैथिली that Sita, हरिणी doe, व्याघ्रीणां यथा as among tigresses, राक्षसीवशम् under the control of the demonesses, आपन्ना was placed.

#### Explanation

Sita, daughter of Janaka, engulfed in grief found herself like a doe under the control of tigresses.

---

### Shloka 35

#### Shloka Text

शोकेन महता ग्रस्ता मैथिली जनकात्मजा। न शर्म लभते भीरुः पाशबद्धा मृगी यथा।।3.56.35।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महता by great, शोकेन by grief, ग्रस्ता immersed, भीरुः helpless lady, जनकात्मजा daughter of Janaka, मैथिली Sita, पाशबद्धा ensnared, मृगी यथा like a female, शर्म peace, न लभते not had.

#### Explanation

Sita, daughter of Janaka, immersed in deep grief knew no peace just like a doe caught in a snare.

---

### Shloka 36

#### Shloka Text

न विन्दते तत्र तु शर्म मैथिली विरूपनेत्राभिरतीव तर्जिता। पतिं स्मरन्ती दयितं च देवतं विचेतनाऽभूद्भयशोकपीडिता।।3.56.36।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

विरूपनेत्राभिः by women of deformed eyes, अतीव excessively, तर्जिता threatened, मैथिली Sita,  तत्र there, शर्म peace, न विन्दते not had, दयितम् beloved, पतिम् husband, देवतं च desired deity, स्मरन्ती while remembering, भयशोकपीडिता tortured by fear and grief, विचेतना lost conciousness, अभूत् became.

#### Explanation

Sita had no peace as she was threatened excessively by those women of deformed  looks. Remembering her beloved husband who was also her god, and overtaken by fear and grief , she fell unconscious. इतयार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये अरण्यकाण्डे षटपञ्चाशस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the fiftysixth sarga of Aranyakanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 57

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

राक्षसं मृगरूपेण चरन्तं कामरूपिणम्। निहत्य रामो मारीचं तूर्णं पथि न्यवर्तत।।3.57.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रामः Rama, मृगरूपेण in the form of a deer, चरन्तम् moving, कामरूपिणम् assuming a form at will, राक्षसम् to the demon, मारीचम् Maricha, निहत्य slaying, तूर्णम् swiftly, पथि  on the path, न्यवर्तत returned.

#### Explanation

Rama killed Maricha, the demon assuming the form of the deer as per his will and swiftly took the return path.

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

तस्य सन्त्वरमाणस्य द्रष्टुकामस्य मैथिलीम्। क्रूरस्वनोऽथ गोमायुर्विननादास्य पृष्ठतः।।3.57.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मैथिलीम् Maithili (Sita), द्रष्टुकामस्य desiring to see, तस्य his, सन्त्वरमाणस्य hastening, अथ at that time, क्रूरस्वनः ghastly sound, गोमायुः jackal, अस्य his, पृष्ठतः from behind, विननाद screamed.

#### Explanation

While Rama was hasting to see Sita, he heard from behind the ghastly howl of a jackal.

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

स तस्य स्वरमाज्ञाय दारुणं रोमहर्षणम्। चिन्तयामास गोमायोस्स्वरेण परिशङ्कितः।।3.57.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः he, तस्य गोमायोः the jackal's, दारुणम् dreadful, रोमहर्षणम् horripilating, स्वरम् tone, आज्ञाय recognising, स्वरेण voice, परिशङ्कितः doubting it, चिन्तयामास started worrying.

#### Explanation

Rama knew it was the horrible, horripilating cry of a jackal which made him apprehensive . It made him think.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

अशुभं बत मन्येऽहं गोमायुर्वाश्यते यथा। स्वस्ति स्यादपि वैदेह्या राक्षसैर्भक्षणं विना।।3.57.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

बत alas, गोमायुः jackal, यथा as, वाश्यते howling, अहम् I, अशुभम् inauspicious, मन्ये thinking, राक्षसैः by demons, भक्षणं विना without being eaten up, वैदेह्याः Vaidehi's, स्वस्ति wellbeing, स्यादपि may be.

#### Explanation

'The way the jackal is howling appears ominous. May Sita be safe without being eaten up by demons

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

मारीचेन तु विज्ञाय स्वरमालम्ब्य मामकम्। विक्रुष्टं मृगरूपेण लक्ष्मणश्शृणुयाद्यदि।।3.57.5।। स सौमित्रिस्स्वरं श्रुत्वा तां च हित्वाच मैथिलीम्। तयेह प्रहितः क्षिप्रं मत्सकाशमिहैष्यति।।3.57.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मृगरूपेण in the form of a deer, मारीचेन by Maricha, विज्ञाय having known, मामकम् my , स्वरम् voice, आलम्ब्य imitating, विक्रुष्टम् cried out, लक्ष्मणः Lakshmana, शृणुयाद्यदि if he listens, सः सौमित्रिः that Saumitri, स्वरम् voice, श्रुत्वा after hearing, तां मैथिलीम् to Sita, हित्वा च leaving her, तया by her, इह here, प्रहितः sent, क्षिप्रम् at once, इह here, मत्सकाशम् for me, एष्यति he will come,

#### Explanation

'If  Lakshmana listens to Maricha imitating my voice in the form of a deer, he, sent by Sita will come for me at once, leaving her behind.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

मारीचेन तु विज्ञाय स्वरमालम्ब्य मामकम्। विक्रुष्टं मृगरूपेण लक्ष्मणश्शृणुयाद्यदि।।3.57.5।। स सौमित्रिस्स्वरं श्रुत्वा तां च हित्वाच मैथिलीम्। तयेह प्रहितः क्षिप्रं मत्सकाशमिहैष्यति।।3.57.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मृगरूपेण in the form of a deer, मारीचेन by Maricha, विज्ञाय having known, मामकम् my , स्वरम् voice, आलम्ब्य imitating, विक्रुष्टम् cried out, लक्ष्मणः Lakshmana, शृणुयाद्यदि if he listens, सः सौमित्रिः that Saumitri, स्वरम् voice, श्रुत्वा after hearing, तां मैथिलीम् to Sita, हित्वा च leaving her, तया by her, इह here, प्रहितः sent, क्षिप्रम् at once, इह here, मत्सकाशम् for me, एष्यति he will come,

#### Explanation

'If  Lakshmana listens to Maricha imitating my voice in the form of a deer, he, sent by Sita will come for me at once, leaving her behind.

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

राक्षसैस्सहितैर्नूनं सीताया ईप्सितो वधः। काञ्चनश्च मृगो भूत्वा व्यपनीयाश्रमात्तु माम्।।3.57.7।। दूरं नीत्वा तु मारीचो राक्षसोऽभूच्छरा हतः। हा लक्ष्मण हतोऽस्मीति यद्वाक्यं व्याजहार ह।।3.57.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

यत् that, मारीचः Maricha, काञ्चनः मृगः golden deer, भूत्वा having become, माम् me, आश्रमात् from the hermitage, व्यपनीय dragging away, दूरम् far off, नीत्वा after taking, शराहतः hit by my arrows, राक्षसः demon, अभूत् he became, हा लक्ष्मण Alas, Lakshmana, हतः अस्मि I am killed, इति these, वाक्यम् words, व्याजहार he said, सहितैः accompanying, राक्षसैः by demons, सीतायाः Sita's, वधः slaughter, ईप्सितः desired, नूनम् surely.

#### Explanation

'Hence the murder of Sita must have been jointly planned by the demons. Maricha who had assumed the form of a golden deer, took me away from the hermitage to a faroff place where, killed by my arrows, he turned a demon uttering the words, Alas, Lakshmana, I am killed.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

राक्षसैस्सहितैर्नूनं सीताया ईप्सितो वधः। काञ्चनश्च मृगो भूत्वा व्यपनीयाश्रमात्तु माम्।।3.57.7।। दूरं नीत्वा तु मारीचो राक्षसोऽभूच्छरा हतः। हा लक्ष्मण हतोऽस्मीति यद्वाक्यं व्याजहार ह।।3.57.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

यत् that, मारीचः Maricha, काञ्चनः मृगः golden deer, भूत्वा having become, माम् me, आश्रमात् from the hermitage, व्यपनीय dragging away, दूरम् far off, नीत्वा after taking, शराहतः hit by my arrows, राक्षसः demon, अभूत् he became, हा लक्ष्मण Alas, Lakshmana, हतः अस्मि I am killed, इति these, वाक्यम् words, व्याजहार he said, सहितैः accompanying, राक्षसैः by demons, सीतायाः Sita's, वधः slaughter, ईप्सितः desired, नूनम् surely.

#### Explanation

'Hence the murder of Sita must have been jointly planned by the demons. Maricha who had assumed the form of a golden deer, took me away from the hermitage to a faroff place where, killed by my arrows, he turned a demon uttering the words, Alas, Lakshmana, I am killed.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

अपि स्वस्ति भवेत्ताभ्यां रहिताभ्यां महावने। जनस्थाननिमित्तं हि कृतवैरोऽस्मि राक्षसैः।।3.57.9।। निमित्तानि च घोराणि दृश्यन्तेऽद्य बहूनि च।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महावने in the dense forest, रहिताभ्याम् staying, ताभ्याम् those two, स्वस्ति safe, अपि भवेत् will be, जनस्थान निमित्तम् on account of Janasthana, राक्षसैः with demons, कृतवैरः have earned enmity, अस्मि हि because of me, अद्य now, घोराणि dreadful, बहूनि many, निमित्तानि च  portents, दृश्यन्ते are seen.

#### Explanation

'On account of living in this dense forest in Janasthana I have earned enmity with the demons. Will Lakshmana and Sita be safe without my protection? I see many terrible portents.'

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

इत्येवं चिन्तयन्रामश्श्रुत्वा गोमायुनिस्स्वनम्।।3.57.10।। आत्मनश्चापनयनान्मृगरूपेण रक्षसा। आजगाम जनस्थानं राघवः परिशङ्कितः।।3.57.11।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राघवः Rama, गोमायुनिस्स्वनम् the jackal's howl, श्रुत्वा on hearing, इत्येवम् like this, चिन्तयन् worried, मृगरूपेण in the form of a deer, रक्षसा by the demon, आत्मनः his, अपनयनात् due to being drawn away, परिशङ्कितः  terrified, जनस्थानम् Janasthana, आजगाम came.

#### Explanation

On hearing the jackal's howl, Rama began thinking how the demon in the form of a deer drew him away. Thus in the midst of apprehensions he reached Janasthana.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

इत्येवं चिन्तयन्रामश्श्रुत्वा गोमायुनिस्स्वनम्।।3.57.10।। आत्मनश्चापनयनान्मृगरूपेण रक्षसा। आजगाम जनस्थानं राघवः परिशङ्कितः।।3.57.11।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राघवः Rama, गोमायुनिस्स्वनम् the jackal's howl, श्रुत्वा on hearing, इत्येवम् like this, चिन्तयन् worried, मृगरूपेण in the form of a deer, रक्षसा by the demon, आत्मनः his, अपनयनात् due to being drawn away, परिशङ्कितः  terrified, जनस्थानम् Janasthana, आजगाम came.

#### Explanation

On hearing the jackal's howl, Rama began thinking how the demon in the form of a deer drew him away. Thus in the midst of apprehensions he reached Janasthana.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

तं दीनमनसो दीनमासेदुर्मृगपक्षिणः। सव्यं कृत्वा महात्मानं घोरांश्च ससृजुस्स्वरान्।।3.57.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

दीनमनसः dejected at heart, मृगपक्षिणः animals and birds, दीनम् piteous, महात्मानम् great soul, तम् him, सव्यम् कृत्वा keeping him to the left, आसेदुः remained, घोरान् horrifying, स्वरान् sounds, ससृजुश्च released.

#### Explanation

Dejected at heart, the birds and animals piteously kept the great soul (Rama) to the left and produced a dreadful cacophony.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

तानि दृष्ट्वा निमित्तानि महाघोराणि राघवः। न्यवर्तताथ त्वरितो जवेनाश्रममात्मनः।।3.57.13।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राघवः Rama, महाघोराणि very dreadful, तानि निमित्तानि such omens, दृष्ट्वा after seeing, अथ then, त्वरितः hastened, जवेन with speed, आत्मनः his, आश्रमम् hermitage, न्यवर्तत returned.

#### Explanation

On seeing such dreadfu omens at that time, Rama hurried back to his hermitage.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

सीतां स तु वरारोहां लक्ष्मणं च महाबलम्। आजगाम जनस्थानं चिन्तयन्नेव राघवः।।3.57.14।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः राघवः that Rama, वरारोहाम् charming, सीताम् Sita, महाबलम्  mighty, लक्ष्मणं च Lakshmana, चिन्तयन्नेव thinking of them, जनस्थानम् Janasthana, आजगाम arrived.

#### Explanation

Rama arrived at Janasthana thinking (of the safety) of charming Sita and mighty Lakshmana.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

ततो लक्ष्मणमायान्तं ददर्श विगतप्रभम्। ततोऽविदूरे रामेण समीयाय स लक्ष्मणः।।3.57.15।। विषण्णस्सुविषण्णेन दुःखितो दुःखभागिना।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः thereafter, आयान्तम् while coming, विगतप्रभम् dull, लक्ष्मणम् to Lakshmana, ददर्श saw, ततः then, विषण्णः a despondent one, दुःखितः one in grief, सः लक्ष्मणः that Lakshmana, सुविषण्णेन with a despondent one, दुःखभागिना immersed in sorrow, रामेण Rama, अविदूरे not very far, समीयाय went near.

#### Explanation

He then saw Lakshmana coming towards him with a cheerless face. When the sad, depressed Lakshmana came near, he saw Rama (equally) despondent and griefstricken.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

सञ्जगर्हेऽथ तं भ्राता ज्येष्ठो लक्ष्मणमागतम्।।3.57.16।। विहाय सीतां विजने वने राक्षससेविते।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अथ at that time, ज्येष्ठः भ्राता elder brother Rama, राक्षससेविते in a place infested with demons, विजने in a desolate, वने forest, सीताम् Sita, विहाय leaving, आगतम् one who came, तं लक्ष्मणम् to that Lakshmana, सञ्जगर्हे rebuked.

#### Explanation

In this situation Rama the elder brother rebuked Lakshmana for having left Sita behind in a desolate forest infested with demons.

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

गृहीत्वा च करं सव्यं लक्ष्मणं रघुनन्दनः।।3.57.17।। उवाच मधुरोदर्कमिदं परुषमार्तिमत्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रघुनन्दनः a delight of the Raghu dynasty, आर्तिमत् piteously, सव्यं करम् left hand, गृहीत्वा on taking hold, मधुरोदर्कम् sweet as ever, परुषम् harsh, इदम् this, लक्ष्मणम् to Lakshmana, उवाच said.

#### Explanation

Rama, the delight of the Raghus, took hold of Lakshmana's left hand, and in sadness said these sweet harsh words :

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

अहो लक्ष्मण गर्ह्यं ते कृतं यस्त्वं विहाय ताम्।।3.57.18।। सीतामिहाऽगतस्सौम्य कच्चित्स्वस्ति भवेदिह।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सौम्य O gentle one, लक्ष्मण Lakshmana, यः who, त्वम् you, ताम् her, सीताम् Sita, विहाय leaving, इह here, आगतः came, ते to you, गर्ह्यम् blameworthy, कृतम् done, इह here, स्वस्ति safe, भवेत् कच्चित् will she be safe, अहो Alas.

#### Explanation

O gentle Lakshmana you are to blame for coming here, leaving Sita behind. Alas will she be safe now?

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

न मेऽस्ति संशयो वीर सर्वथा जनकात्मजा।।3.57.19।। विनष्टा भक्षिता वापि राक्षसैर्वनचारिभिः। अशुभान्येव भूयिष्ठं यथा प्रादुर्भवन्ति मे।।3.57.20।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वीर O great hero, मे to me, भूयिष्ठम् many, अशुभान्येव inauspicious omens only, यथा similarly, प्रादुर्भवन्ति got manifested, जनकात्मजा Sita, सर्वथा by all means, विनष्टा she is lost, वनचारिभिः by forsetrangers, राक्षसैः demons, भक्षिता वापि must have been eaten up, मे for me, संशयः doubt, नास्ति is not.

#### Explanation

O heroic Lakshmana I see many inauspicious omens. I think Sita might have been lost or eaten away by the demons of the forest. There is no doubt about it.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

न मेऽस्ति संशयो वीर सर्वथा जनकात्मजा।।3.57.19।। विनष्टा भक्षिता वापि राक्षसैर्वनचारिभिः। अशुभान्येव भूयिष्ठं यथा प्रादुर्भवन्ति मे।।3.57.20।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वीर O great hero, मे to me, भूयिष्ठम् many, अशुभान्येव inauspicious omens only, यथा similarly, प्रादुर्भवन्ति got manifested, जनकात्मजा Sita, सर्वथा by all means, विनष्टा she is lost, वनचारिभिः by forsetrangers, राक्षसैः demons, भक्षिता वापि must have been eaten up, मे for me, संशयः doubt, नास्ति is not.

#### Explanation

O heroic Lakshmana I see many inauspicious omens. I think Sita might have been lost or eaten away by the demons of the forest. There is no doubt about it.

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

अपि लक्ष्मण सीतायास्सामग्र्यं प्राप्नुयावहे। जीवन्त्याः पुरुषव्याघ्र सुताया जनकस्य वै।।3.57.21।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पुरुषव्याघ्र O tiger among men, लक्ष्मण Lakshmana, जीवन्त्या: of the living, जनकस्य सुतायाः of the daughter of Janaka, सीतायाः Sita's, सामग्र्यम् total well being, अपि प्राप्नुयावहे will we be able to get her back.

#### Explanation

O Lakshmana, a tiger among men, will we be able to see the daughter of Janaka, living in a state of total wellbeing? Will we be able to get her back ?

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

यथा वै मृगसङ्घाश्च गोमायुश्चैव भैरवम्। वाश्यन्ते शकुनाश्चापि प्रदीप्तामभितो दिशम्।।3.57.22।। अपि स्वस्ति भवेत्तस्या राजपुत्र्या महाबल।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महाबल mighty, मृगसङ्घाश्च herds of beasts, गोमायुश्च jackal also, शकुनाश्चापि birds too, प्रदीप्ताम् shining bright, दिशम् direction, अभितः towards, यथा as, भैरवम् in frightful manner, वाश्यन्ते howling, राजपुत्र्याः of the princess, तस्याः of that  Sita, स्वस्ति अपि भवेत् may her be well.

#### Explanation

O  mighty Lakshmana  from the way the beasts, jackals and birds too have turned in the bright direction of the Sun, crying in a frightful manner, can we hope princess Sita is safe ?

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

इदं हि रक्षो मृगसन्निकाशं प्रलोभ्य मां दूरमनुप्रयान्तम्। हतं कथञ्चिन्महता श्रमेण स राक्षसोऽभून्म्रियमाण एव।।3.57.23।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मृगसन्निकाशम् appearing like a deer, इदं रक्षः this demon, अनुप्रयान्तम् who followed, माम् me, दूरम् far, प्रलोभ्य alluring, महता with great, श्रमेण with effort, कथञ्चित् somehow, हतम् killed, सः he, म्रियमाणः while on the verge of death, एव too, राक्षसः demon, अभूत् turned.

#### Explanation

Appearing in the guise of a deer, this demon allured me to a great distance. With much effort he was somehow killed. but he assumed his demoniac form while dying.

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

मनश्च मे दीनमिहाप्रहृष्टं चक्षुश्च सव्यं कुरुते विकारम्। असंशयं लक्ष्मण नास्ति सीता हृता मृता वा पथि वर्तते वा।।3.57.24।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

इह here, दीनम् depressed, मे मनश्च my heart, अप्रहृष्टम् sorrowful, सव्यं चक्षुश्च left eye, विकारम् throbbing, कुरुते doing, लक्ष्मण Lakshmana, असंशयम् no doubt, सीता Sita, नास्ति is not alive, हृता either abducted, मृता वा or died, पथि on the way, वर्तते वा lies.

#### Explanation

With my heart dejected and depressed, my left eye throbbing, O Lakshmana, I have no doubt that Sita is either abducted or dead or abandoned on the way. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये अरण्यकाण्डे सप्तपञ्चाशस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the fiftyseventh sarga of Aranyakanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 58

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

स दृष्ट्वा लक्ष्मणं दीनं शून्ये दशरथात्मजः। पर्यपृच्छत धर्मात्मा वैदेहीमागतं विना।।3.58.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

धर्मात्मा righteous self, सः दशरथात्मजः that son of Dasaratha, शून्ये downcast, वैदेहीं विना without Vaidehi, आगतम् who came, दीनम् depressed, लक्ष्मणम् Lakshmana, दृष्ट्वा seeing, पर्यपृच्छत questioned.

#### Explanation

Rama, son of Dasaratha, the righteous self, saw Lakshmana downcast and dejected. Seeing him  without Sita, Rama enquired:

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

प्रस्थितं दण्डकारण्यं या मामनुजगाम ह। क्व सा लक्ष्मण वैदेही यां हित्वा त्वमिहागतः।।3.58.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लक्ष्मण Lakshmna, दण्डकारण्यम् to Dandakaranyam, प्रस्थितम् entered, माम् me, या who,   अनुजगाम ह followed, याम् whom, हित्वा having left, त्वम् you, इह here, आगतः came, सा वैदेही that Vaidehi, क्व where is she?

#### Explanation

Where is that princess from Videha who accompanied me when I entered Dandaka forest ? Why have you come here leaving her behind ?

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

राज्यभ्रष्टस्य दीनस्य दण्डकान्परिधावतः। क्व सा दुःखसहाया मे वैदेही तनुमध्यमा।।3.58.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राज्यभ्रष्टस्य forsaking the kingdom, दण्डकान् Dandaka, परिधावतः running about, दीनस्य of a dejected, मे mine, दुःखसहाया a sharer of my sorrow, तनुमध्यमा lady of slender waist, सा वैदेही that Vaidehi, क्व where is she?

#### Explanation

Where is that princess from Videha, a sharer of my sorrows, Sita of slender waist who followed this hapless prince disloged from the kingdom into the Dandaka forest?

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

यां विना नोत्सहे वीर मुहूर्तमपि जीवितुम्। क्व सा प्राणसहाया मे सीता सुरसुतोपमा।।3.58.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वीर O Warrior, यां विना without whom, मुहूर्तमपि even for a moment, जीवितुम् to live, नोत्सहे I have no interest, सा she, सुरसुतोपमा who resembles the daughter of a god, मे my, प्राणसहाया sustainer of my life, सीता Sita, क्व Where is she?

#### Explanation

O warrior Lakshmana where is that Sita without whom I have no interest to live for a moment, who was like the daughter of a god and a sustainer of my life?

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

पतित्वममराणां वा पृथिव्याश्चापि लक्ष्मण। तां विना तपनीयाभां नेच्छेयं जनकात्मजाम्।।3.58.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लक्ष्मण Lakshmana, तपनीयाभाम् goldenhued lady, ताम् she, जनकात्मजां विना without Janaka's daughter, अमराणाम् of gods, पतित्वम् lordship, पृथिव्याश्च of the earth, नेच्छेयम् do not desire.

#### Explanation

O Lakshmana I desire no lordship of the gods  or of the entire earth without that daughter of Janaka, that lady of golden complexion.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

कच्चिज्जीवति वैदेहि प्राणैः प्रियतरा मम। कच्चित्प्रव्राजनं सौम्य न मे मिथ्या भविष्यति।।3.58.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सौम्य O gentle, मम my, प्राणैः than life, प्रियतरा dearer, वैदेही Vaidehi, जीवति कच्चित् will she be alive, मे my, प्रव्राजनम् banishment from kingdom, मिथ्या prove to be false, न भविष्यति कच्चित् shall not become.

#### Explanation

O gentle Lakshmana I wonder if Vaidehi, who is dearer to me than my life is still alive I hope my banishment from the kingdom is not going to prove false.

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

सीतानिमित्तं सौमित्रे मृते मयि गते त्वयि। कच्चित्सकामा सुखिता कैकेयी सा भविष्यति।।3.58.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सौमित्रे Saumitri, सीतानिमित्तम् on account of Sita, मयि when I will be, मृते dead, त्वयि when you, गते will be gone, सा कैकेयी that Kaikeyi, सकामा with her desire fulfilled, सुखिता a happy soul, भविष्यति कच्चित् will be indeed.

#### Explanation

O Saumitri when I am dead on account of Sita, and you are back, Kaikeyi with her desire fulfilled will be happy, indeed.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

सपुत्रराज्यां सिद्धार्थां मृतपुत्रा तपस्विनी। उपस्थास्यतिकौसल्या कच्चित्सौम्य न केकयीम्।।3.58.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सौम्य O handsome one, मृतपुत्रा  with her son dead, तपस्विनी a lady of renunciation, कौसल्या Kausalya, सपुत्रराज्याम् who enjoys the company of a son and power of the throne, सिद्धार्थाम् accomplished her desire, कैकयीम् Kaikeyi, न उपस्थास्यति कच्चित् cease to serve indeed.

#### Explanation

O handsome one  I wonder if that lady of renunciation, Kausalya with her son dead, will have to serve Kaikeyi whose desire would be fulfilled, and who will enjoy the power of the throne in the company of her son

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

यदि जीवति वैदेही गमिष्याम्याश्रमं पुनः। सुवृत्ता यदि वृत्ता प्राणांस्त्यक्ष्यामि लक्ष्मण।।3.58.9।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लक्ष्मण O Lakshmana, वैदेही Vaidehi, जीवति यदि if she is living only, पुनः again, आश्रमम् to the cottage, गमिष्यामि will go, यदि वा or else, सुवृत्ता virtuous, वृत्ता dead, प्राणान् life, त्यक्ष्यामि I will give up.

#### Explanation

O Lakshmana, only if Vaidehi is alive will I enter the cottage again or else I will give up my life if that virtuous lady is no more.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

यदि मामाश्रमगतं वैदेही नाभिभाषते। पुनः प्रहसिता सीता विनशिष्यामि लक्ष्मण।।3.58.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लक्ष्मण Lakshmana, आश्रमगतम् when I return to the cottage, माम् me, वैदेही Vaidehi, सीता Sita, प्रहसिता with her sweet smile, पुनः again, नाभिभाषते यदि if she does not speak to me, विनशिष्यामि I will die.

#### Explanation

I will die if Sita, princess from Videha, is not there to speak to me with her sweet smile when I return to the cottage, O Lakshmana

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

ब्रूहि लक्ष्मण वैदेही यदि जीवति वा न वा। त्वयि प्रमत्ते रक्षोभिर्भक्षिता वा तपस्विनी।।3.58.11।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लक्ष्मण Lakshmana, वैदेही Vaidehi, जीवति वा alive or, न वा not, त्वयि you, प्रमत्ते were not alert, तपस्विनी lady of renunciation, रक्षोभिः by the demons, भक्षिता वा have they eaten, ब्रूहि tell me.

#### Explanation

Tell me, O Lakshmana   if Sita is alive or not, whether she was eaten up by demons when you were off your guard.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

सुकुमारी च बाला च नित्यं चादुःखदर्शिनी। मद्वियोगेन वैदेही व्यक्तं शोचति दुर्मनाः।।3.58.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सुकुमारी च a delicate, बाला च young also, नित्यम् always, अदुःखदर्शिनी never experienced sorrow, वैदेही Vaidehi, दुर्मनाः troubled mind, मद्वियोगेन by separation from me, शोचति thinking of, व्यक्तम् it is true.

#### Explanation

Vaidehi is delicate, young and she has never faced any sorrow.She will be certainly disheartened due to separation from me.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

सर्वदा रक्षसा तेन जिह्मेन सुदुरात्मना। वदता लक्ष्मणेत्युच्चैस्तवापि जनितं भयम्।।3.58.13।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

जिह्मेन by the wicked, सुदुरात्मना by the evilminded, लक्ष्मण इति saying Lakshmana thus, उच्चैः loudly, वदता calling out, तेन by him, रक्षसा demon, सर्वथा by all means, तवापि for you also, भयम् fear, जनितम् is created.

#### Explanation

The evilminded, wicked demon calling out loudly 'O Lakshmana' must have by all means instilled fear in your mind.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

श्रुतस्तु शङ्के वैदेह्या स स्वरस्सदृशो मम। त्रस्तया प्रेषितस्त्वं च द्रष्टुं मां शीघ्रमागतः।।3.58.14।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वैदेह्या by Vaidehi, मम of me, सदृशः similar, सः that, स्वरः voice, श्रुतः was heard, शङ्के       I doubt, त्रस्तया by a frightened lady, प्रेषितः sent, त्वं च you too, माम् me, द्रष्टुम् to see, शीघ्रम् quickly, आगतः came.

#### Explanation

I am afraid, Vaidehi, frightened by hearing the voice which was like mine sent you to see me, and you came all at once.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

सर्वथा तु कृतं कष्टं सीतामुत्सृजता वने। प्रतिकर्तुं नृशंसानां रक्षसां दत्तमन्तरं।।3.58.15।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सीताम् Sita, वने in the forest, उत्सृजता leaving alone, सर्वथा by all means, कष्टम् difficult, कृतम् done, प्रतिकर्तुम् to take revenge, नृशंसानाम् of the cruel, रक्षसाम् of demons, अन्तरम् chance, दत्तम् is given.

#### Explanation

You have definitely done a great mistake by leaving Sita alone in the forest. This has given a chance to the cruel demons to wreak revenge.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

दुःखिताः खरघातेन राक्षसाः पिशिताशनाः। तैस्सीता निहता घोरैर्भविष्यति न संशयः।3.58.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पिशिताशनाः flesheaters, राक्षसाः demons, खरघातेन by the death of Khara, दुःखिताः are aggrieved, घोरैः dreadful, तैः by them, सीता Sita, निहता killed, भविष्यति may be, संशयः doubt, न no.

#### Explanation

The dreadful, carnivorous demons aggrieved over my murder of Khara must have undoubtedly killed Sita.

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

अहोऽस्मिन् व्यसने मग्नस्सर्वथा शत्रुसूदन। किं न्विदानीं करिष्यामि शङ्के प्राप्तव्यमीदृशम्।।3.58.17।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

शत्रुसूदन O destroyer of enemies, सर्वथा in all respects, अस्मिन् in this, व्यसने in sorrow, मग्नः I am drowned, इदानीम् at this time, किं नु करिष्यामि what can I do?, ईदृशम् such, प्राप्तव्यम् destined, शङ्के I fear, अहो alas

#### Explanation

O destroyer of enemies  I am completely drowned in sorrow. What can I do at this moment. Alas, I am destined to suffer.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

इति सीतां वरारोहां चिन्तयन्नेव राघवः। आजगाम जनस्थानं त्वरया सह लक्ष्मणः।।3.58.18।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राघवः Raghava, इति thus, वरारोहाम् beautiful, सीताम् Sita, चिन्तयन्नेव while brooding over only, सह लक्ष्मणः along with Lakshmana, त्वरया quickly, जनस्थानम्  Janasthanam, आजगाम he went.

#### Explanation

While thus brooding over the beautiful Sita, the scion of the Raghus along with Lakshmana hurried towards Janasthana.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

विगर्हमाणोऽनुजमार्तरूपं क्षुधा श्रमाच्चैव पिपासया च। विनिश्श्वसन् शुष्कमुखो विवर्णः प्रतिश्रयं प्राप्य समीक्ष्य शून्यम्।।3.58.19।। स्वमाश्रमं सम्प्रविगाह्य वीरो विहारदेशाननुसृत्य कांश्चित्। एतत्तदित्येव निवासभूमौ प्रहृष्टरोमा व्यथितो बभूव।।3.58.20।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वीरः hero, आर्तरूपम् dejected, अनुजम् brother, विगर्हमाणः  blaming him, क्षुधा hunger, श्रमाच्चैव due to exhaustion, पिपासया and thirsty, विनिःश्वसन् sighing deeply, शुष्कमुखः face dried up, विवर्णः turned pale, प्रतिश्रयम् restingplace, प्राप्य reaching, शून्यम् empty, समीक्ष्य seeing, स्वम् his, आश्रमम् hermitage, सम्प्रविगाह्य searching thoroughly, कांश्चित् some of the places, विहारदेशान् places where she was moving about, अनुसृत्य after following her, निवासभूमौ in the dwelling place, एतत् all that, तदेव इति this itself, प्रहृष्टरोमा with horripilation, व्यथितः pained, बभूव became.

#### Explanation

Thus blaming his dejected brother, the valiant Rama entered the hermitage, sighing deeply, his face pale and his throat dried up with hunger, exhaustion and thirst. Reaching his dwellingplace, he found it empty. He thoroughly searched the hermitage and the places where she used to move about on the premises. And 'so it has happened', he sadly said to himself, his hair raised on end. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये अरण्यकाण्डे अष्टपञ्चाशस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the fiftyeighth sarga of Aranyakanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 59

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

अथाश्रमादुपावृत्तमन्तरा रघुनन्दनः। परिपप्रच्छ सौमित्रिं रामो दुःखार्दितं पुनः।।3.59.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अथ and then, रघुनन्दनः delight of the Raghus, रामः Rama, दुःखार्दितम् afflicted, आश्रमात् from the hermitage, उपावृत्तम् returned, सौमित्रिम् Lakshmana, अन्तरा in the midst, पुनः again, परिपप्रच्छ questioned.

#### Explanation

Rama, the delight of the Raghus, once again questioned Lakshmana who had come out of the hermitage, deeply hurt:

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

तमुवाच किमर्थं त्वमागतोऽपास्य मैथिलीम्। यदा सा तव विश्वासाद्वने विरहिता मया।।3.59.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तम् him, उवाच had said, तव your, विश्वासात् with the confidence, मया myself, सा she, वने  in the forest, यदा as such, विरहिता left, मैथिलीम् Maithili, किमर्थम् for what reason, उपास्य having left her, त्वम् you, आगतः came.

#### Explanation

Why did you come, leaving the princess from Mithila alone in the forest when I had left her under your trust ?

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

दृष्ट्वैवाभ्यागतं त्वां मे मैथिलीं त्यज्य लक्ष्मण। शङ्कमानं महत्पापं यत्सत्यं व्यथितं मनः।।3.59.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लक्ष्मण Lakshmana, मैथिलीम् Maithili, त्यज्य left, अभ्यागतम् your coming, त्वाम् your, दृष्ट्वैव on seeing, मनः in my mind, महत् great, पापम् evil, शङ्कमानम् doubted, व्यथितम् (इति) यत् I was pained, सत्यम् it was true.

#### Explanation

When I saw you, O Lakshmana, coming without Sita, my mind was oppressed with evil thought which have come true.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

स्फुरते नयनं सव्यं बाहुश्च हृदयं च मे। दृष्ट्वा लक्ष्मण दूरे त्वां सीताविरहितं पथि।।3.59.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लक्ष्मण Lakshmana, सीताविरहितम् without Sita, त्वाम् you, दूरे at a distance, पथि on the way, दृष्ट्वा seeing, मे mine, सव्यम् left, नयनम् eye, बाहुश्च shoulder, स्फुरते throbbing, मे my, हृदयं च heart too.

#### Explanation

O Lakshmana, as I saw you from a distance without Sita, my left eye, my shoulder and my heart throbbed.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

एवमुक्तन्तु सौमित्रिर्लक्ष्मणश्शुभलक्षणः। भूयो दुःखसमाविष्टो दुःखितं राममब्रवीत्।।3.59.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

एवम् in that way, उक्तः having been spoken, सौमित्रिः Lakshmana, शुभलक्षणः endowed with good  traits, लक्ष्मणः Lakshmana, भूयः again, दुःखसमाविष्टः drowned in greater sorrow, दुःखितम् sorrowful, रामम् to Rama, अब्रवीत् said.

#### Explanation

Thus addressed, virtuous Lakshmana, overwhelmed with sorrow replied to the grief stricken Rama:

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

न स्वयं कामकारेण तां त्यक्त्वाहमिहागतः। प्रचोदित स्तयैवोग्रैस्त्वत्सकाशमिहागतः।।3.59.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अहम् I, ताम् her, स्वयम् on my own, त्यक्त्वा after leaving, कामकारेण by my own desire, इह here, न आगतः not come, तयैव by herself, उग्रैः with angry words, प्रचोदितः provoked, इह here, त्वत्सकाशम् to you, आगतः I came.

#### Explanation

I did not leave her on my own nor have I  come here of my own accord. I came to you, provoked by her angry  words.

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

आर्येणेव पराक्रुष्टं हासीते लक्ष्मणेति च। परित्राहीति यद्वाक्यं मैथिल्यास्तच्छ्रुतिं गतम्।।3.59.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

हा सीते Alas, Sita, हा लक्ष्मण Alas, Lakshmana, इति च these words, परित्राहि इति save  me, यत् such, आर्येणेव as if your noble self, पराक्रुष्टम् loudly, तत् then, मैथिल्याः Maithili's, श्रुतिम् to her ears, गतम् reached.

#### Explanation

Uttered loudly, 'Alas Sita, Alas, Lakshmana, save me', as if these were the words of your noble self, reached Sita's ears.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

सा तमार्तस्वरं श्रुत्वा तव स्नेहेन मैथिली। गच्छ गच्छेति मामाह रुदन्ती भयविह्वला।।3.59.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सा मैथिली that Maithili, तम् that, आर्तस्वरम् cry of pain, श्रुत्वा hearing, रुदन्ती crying, तव स्नेहेन  because of her love for you, भयविह्वला wild with fear, गच्छ गच्छ इति go, go, माम् to me said, आह pleaded.

#### Explanation

The princess from Mithila heard this cry of pain and, wild with fear, urged me out of her love for you to go.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

प्रचोद्यमानेन मया गच्छेति बहुशस्तया। प्रत्युक्ता मैथिली वाक्यमिदं त्वत्प्रत्ययान्वितम्।3.59.9।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

गच्छ इति saying go, तया Sita, बहुशः many ways, प्रचोद्यमानेन while being provoked, मया by me, मैथिली Maithili, त्वत्प्रत्ययान्वितम्  cofidence in her, इदम् these, वाक्यम् words, प्रत्युक्ता replied.

#### Explanation

When I was repeated asked to go, I said this in order to instil confidence in the princess from Mithila :

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

न तत्पश्याम्यहं रक्षो यदस्य भयमावहेत्। निर्वृता भव नास्त्येतत्केनाप्येवमुदाहृतम्।।3.59.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अस्य his, यत् Rama, भयम् fear, आवहेत् who can cause, तत् such, रक्षः demon, अहम् I, न पश्यामि I do not see, निर्वृता give up fear, भव your, एतत् this, नास्ति does not exist, केनापि by some one, एवम् in that way, उदाहृतम् is uttered.

#### Explanation

'No one  can cause fear in Rama. I do not see any demon who can hold a threat to him. Give up fear. Some one might have said it.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

विगर्हितं च नीचं च कथमार्योऽभिधास्यति। त्राहीति वचनं सीते यस्त्रायेत्रिदशानपि।।3.59.11।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सीते O Sita, यः he, त्रिदशानपि even gods, त्रायेत् he may save, आर्यः capable one, विगर्हितम् demeaning, नीचं च lowly, त्राहि इति saying 'save me', वचनम् words, कथम् how, अभिधास्यति will speak out?

#### Explanation

'How can Rama who is capable of saving even gods speak such unworthy, demeaning words, 'Save me, O Sita'

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

किं निमित्तं तु केनापि भ्रातुरालम्ब्य मे स्वरम्। राक्षसेनेरितं वाक्यं त्राहि त्राहीति शोभने।।3.59.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

शोभने O fair lady, किं निमित्तं तु for some reason, केनापि राक्षसेन by some demon, मे भ्रातुः my brother's, स्वरम् voice, आलम्ब्य adapting, त्राहि त्राहि इति 'save me, save me', वाक्यम् these words, ईरितम् is said.

#### Explanation

O fair lady, for some reason some demon  has uttered these words 'Save me, save me' adapting my brother's voice.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

विस्वरं व्याहृतं वाक्यं लक्ष्मण त्राहि मामिति। न भवत्या व्यथा कार्या कुनारीजनसेविता।।3.59.13।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लक्ष्मण Lakshmana, माम् me, त्राहि save me, इति thus, विस्वरम् in an altered voice, वाक्यम्  these words, व्याहृतम् uttered, भवत्या yourself, कुनारीजनसेविता like an ordinary lowborn woman, व्यथा pain, न कार्या not to be taken.

#### Explanation

'Lakshmana, save me' are the words uttered in an altered tone. Like an ordinary, lowborn woman you are not to feel concerned for this.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

अलं वैक्लब्यमालम्ब्य स्वस्था भव निरुत्सुका। न सोऽस्ति त्रिषु लोकेषु पुमान्वै राघवं रणे।।3.59.14।। जातो वा जायमानो वा संयुगे यः पराजयेत्। न जय्यो राघवो युद्धे देवैश्शक्रपुरोगमैः।।3.59.15।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वैक्लब्यम् bewilderment, आलम्ब्य resorting to, अलम् no need, निरुत्सुका frustrated, स्वस्था भव you should be calm, यः whoever, संयुगेरणे in an encounter, राघवम् Rama, पराजयेत् defeat, सः he, पुमान् a man, त्रिषु लोकेषु in the three worlds, जातो वा is born in the past, जायमानो वा or going to be born, नास्ति is not there, राघवः Rama, शक्रपुरोगमैः by those led by Indra, देवैः by gods, युद्धे in war, न जय्यः cannot be conquered.

#### Explanation

'You should not feel agitated or dispirited. Be composed. There is no one either born or going to be born in the three worlds who can defeat Rama in war. He cannot be conquered in a battle even by gods led by indra.'

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

अलं वैक्लब्यमालम्ब्य स्वस्था भव निरुत्सुका। न सोऽस्ति त्रिषु लोकेषु पुमान्वै राघवं रणे।।3.59.14।। जातो वा जायमानो वा संयुगे यः पराजयेत्। न जय्यो राघवो युद्धे देवैश्शक्रपुरोगमैः।।3.59.15।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वैक्लब्यम् bewilderment, आलम्ब्य resorting to, अलम् no need, निरुत्सुका frustrated, स्वस्था भव you should be calm, यः whoever, संयुगेरणे in an encounter, राघवम् Rama, पराजयेत् defeat, सः he, पुमान् a man, त्रिषु लोकेषु in the three worlds, जातो वा is born in the past, जायमानो वा or going to be born, नास्ति is not there, राघवः Rama, शक्रपुरोगमैः by those led by Indra, देवैः by gods, युद्धे in war, न जय्यः cannot be conquered.

#### Explanation

'You should not feel agitated or dispirited. Be composed. There is no one either born or going to be born in the three worlds who can defeat Rama in war. He cannot be conquered in a battle even by gods led by indra.'

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

एवमुक्ता तु वैदेही परिमोहितचेतना। उवाचाश्रूणि मुञ्चन्ती दारुणं मामिदं वचः।।3.59.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

एवम् in that way, उक्ता  consoled, वैदेही Vaidehi, परिमोहितचेतना of deluded intellect, अश्रूणि tears, मुञ्चन्ती shedding, माम् me, दारुणं harsh, इदं वचः these words, उवाच said.

#### Explanation

Deluded by her senses, Vaidehi said these words, shedding tears when I  tried to console  her:

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

भावो मयि तवात्यर्थं पाप एव निवेशितः। विनष्टे भ्रातरि प्राप्तुं न च त्वं मामवाप्स्यसि।।3.59.17।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

भ्रातरि brother, विनष्टे is deceased, प्राप्तुम् to get, मयि me, अत्यर्थम् extremely, पापः भाव एव sinful thought only, तव you, निवेशितः is set, त्वम् you, माम् me, न च अवाप्स्यसि will not be able to gain.

#### Explanation

'You have an extremely sinful intention of getting me  when your brother is dead. But you will not be able to gain me.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

सङ्केताद्भरतेन त्वं रामं समनुगच्छसि। क्रोशन्तं हि यथात्यर्थं नैवमभ्यवपद्यसे।।3.59.18।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अत्यर्थम् intensely, क्रोशन्तम्  shouting for help, यथा as, एवम् in this, नाभ्यवपद्यसे  are not going, त्वम् you, भरतेन by Bharata, सङ्केतात् instruction, रामम् Rama,समनुगच्छसि followed.

#### Explanation

'Since you are not reaching out to Rama who is helplessly crying for his rescue, you must have followed Rama, instructed by Bharata (to do him harm)'৷৷

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

रिपुः प्रच्छन्नचारी त्वं मदर्थमनुगच्छसि। राघवस्यान्तरप्रेप्सुस्तथैनं नाभिपद्यसे।।3.59.19।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

प्रच्छन्नचारी in disguise, रिपुः enemy, त्वम् you, राघवस्य Rama's, अन्तरप्रेप्सुः waiting for an opportunity, मदर्थम् for my sake, अनुगच्छसि are following, तथा like this, एनम् him, नाभिपद्यसे not going now.

#### Explanation

'You are an enemy moving in disguise with an interest in me. You are waiting for an opportunity to set aside Rama. That is why you are not rushing to him.'

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

एवमुक्तो हि वैदेह्या संरब्धो रक्तलोचनः। क्रोधात्प्रस्फुरमाणोष्ठ आश्रमादभिनिर्गतः।।3.59.20।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वैदेह्या by Vaidehi, एवम् in that way, उक्तः having spoken, संरब्धः an agitated person, क्रोधात् in anger, रक्तलोचनः with reddened eye, स्फुरमाणोष्ठः quivering lips, आश्रमात् from the hermitage, अभिनिर्गतः came out.

#### Explanation

At these words from the princess of Videha, I came out of the hermitage in an agitated mood with my eyes turned red and lips quivering in anger.

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

एवं ब्रुवाणं सौमित्रिं रामस्सन्तापमोहितः। अब्रवीद्दुष्कृतं सौम्य तां विना यत्त्वमागतः।।3.59.21।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

एवम् in that way, ब्रुवाणम् as he was telling, सौमित्रिम् Lakshmana, सन्तापमोहितः overtaken by grief, रामः Rama, अब्रवीत् said, सौम्य noble, यत् since, त्वम् you, तां विना without her, आगतः came, दुष्कृतम् a wrong act, कृतम् is done.

#### Explanation

To Lakshmana thus reporting, Rama, overcome with grief, said,(Even then) O noble brother your coming here, leaving her behind, is a wrong step taken.

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

जानन्नपि समर्थं मां रक्षसां विनिवारणे। अनेन क्रोधवाक्येन मैथिल्या निस्सृतो भवान्।।3.59.22।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

माम् me, रक्षसाम् of demons, विनिवारणे in warding off, समर्थम् capable, जानन्नपि even knowing , भवान् yourself, मैथिल्याः Maithili's, अनेन by this, क्रोधवाक्येन angry words, निस्सृतः came out.

#### Explanation

You have come out of the hermitage provoked by Maithili's angry words, even though you are aware that I am capable of warding off the demons.

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

न हि ते परितुष्यामि त्यक्त्वा यद्यासि मैथिलीम्। क्रुद्धायाः परुषं श्रुत्वा तां विहाय त्वमागतः।।3.59.23।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मैथिलीम् Maithili, त्यक्त्वा left, यद्यासि that you came, ते with you, न परितुष्यामि not pleased, त्वम् you, क्रुद्धायाः angry, परुषम् harsh, श्रुत्वा  listening, ताम् her, विहाय  deserting, इह here, आगतः came.

#### Explanation

I am not happy with you as you have come here deserting Sita on the plea that she was angry and spoke harsh words.

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

सर्वथा त्वविनीतं ते सीतया यत्प्रचोदितः। क्रोधस्य वशमापन्नो नाकरोश्शासनं मम।।3.59.24।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सीतया by Sita, प्रचोदितः you are urged, क्रोधस्य by anger's, वशम् grip, आपन्नः came, मम my, शासनम् orders, नाकरोः (इति) यत् not respected, सर्वथा by all means, ते your, अविनीतम् act of disobedience.

#### Explanation

Provoked by the words of Sita spoken in anger you came without respecting my orders. It is definitely an act of disobedience.

---

### Shloka 25

#### Shloka Text

असौ हि राक्षसश्शेते शरेणाभिहतो मया। मृगरूपेण येनाहमाश्रमादपवाहितः।।3.59.25।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मृगरूपेण in the guise of a deer, येन by whom, अहम् I, आश्रमात् from the hermitage, अपवाहितः was taken away, असौ राक्षसः this demon, मया by me, शरेण with an arrow, अभिहतः he is killed, शेते lies.

#### Explanation

This demon who misled me in the guise of a deer and took me away from the hermitage, lies dead, killed by my arrow.

---

### Shloka 26

#### Shloka Text

विकृष्य चापं परिधाय सायकं सलीलबाणेन च ताडितो मया। मार्गीं तनुं त्यज्य स विक्लबस्वरो बभूव केयूरधरस्सराक्षसः।।3.59.26।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मया by me, चापम् bow, विकृष्य bending, सायकम् an arrow, परिधाय after fixing, सलीलबाणेन arrow released with ease, ताडितः beaten , सः he, मार्गीम्  a deer, तनुम् body, त्यज्य having, सविक्लबस्वर: painful voice, केयूरधरः with a bracelet, राक्षसः demon, बभूव lay down.

#### Explanation

Bending the bow I fixed the arrow and released it at ease. Hit by that arrow he gave up his guise of the deer and assuming the form of a demon with a bracelet on his arm fell down groaning.

---

### Shloka 27

#### Shloka Text

शराहतेनैव तदार्तया गिरा स्वरं समालम्ब्य सुदूरसुश्रवम्। उदाहृतं तद्वचनं सुदारुणं त्वमागतो येन विहाय मैथिलीम्।।3.59.27।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तदा then, शराहतेनैव as soon as he was hit by the arrow, आर्तया in distress, गिरा with a voice, सुदूरसुश्रवम्  even heard from a distance, स्वरम् voice, समालम्ब्य adapting, सुदारुणम् very dreadful, तत् that, वचनम् words, उदाहृतम्  uttered, येन by which, त्वम् you, मैथिलीम् Maithili, विहाय after deserting, इह here, आगतः came.

#### Explanation

Hit by the arrow, the demon imitating my voice cried out in desperation which could be clearly audible even from a distance. On hearing the dreadful words you deserted Sita and came here. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये अरण्यकाण्डे एकोनषष्टितमस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the fiftyninth sarga of Aranyakanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 60

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

भृशमाव्रजमानस्य तस्याधो वामलोचनम्। प्रास्फुरच्चास्खलद्रामो वेपथुश्चाप्य जायत।।3.60.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

आव्रजमानस्य Rama was moving about, तस्य his, वामलोचनम् left eye, अधः underneath, भृशम् excessively, प्रास्फुरत् throbbed, रामः Rama, प्रास्खलच्च stumbled, अस्य his, वेपथुश्च shivering, जायत was produced.

#### Explanation

As  Rama was returning( to the hermitage), his left eye throbbed repeatedly  and he stumbled and his body trembled.

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

उपालक्ष्य निमित्तानि सोऽशुभानि मुहुर्मुहुः। अपि क्षेमं नु सीताया इति वै व्याजहार च।।3.60.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः he, अशुभानि inauspicious, निमित्तानि omens, उपालक्ष्य  having marked, सीतायाः of Sita, क्षेमम् अपि नु doubting the welfare, मुहुर्मुहुः again and again, व्याजहार च said also.

#### Explanation

As inauspicious omens appeared again and again, doubting if all is well with Sita, he said to himself, Can Sita be safe?

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

त्वरमाणो जगामाथ सीतादर्शनलालसः। शून्यमावसथं दृष्ट्वा बभूवोद्विग्नमानसः।।3.60.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अथ then, त्वरमाणः hastening , सीता दर्शनलालसः eager to see, जगाम went, शून्यम् empty, आवसथम् hermitage, दृष्ट्वा saw, उद्विग्नमानसः with an anxious mind, बभूव became.

#### Explanation

Anxious to see Sita, he hastened to the hermitage and finding it empty, became restless.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

उद्भ्रमन्निव वेगेन विक्षिपन्रघुनन्दनः। तत्र तत्रोटजस्थानमभिवीक्षय समन्ततः।।3.60.4।। ददर्श पर्णशालां च रहितां सीतया तदा। श्रिया विरहितां ध्वस्तां हेमन्ते पद्मिनीमिव।।3.60.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रघुनन्दनः delight of the Raghu race, वेगेन quickly, उद्भ्रमन्निव turning round and round, विक्षिपन्  throwing his hands, तत्र तत्र  here and there, उटजस्थानम् place of hermitage, समन्ततः all round, अभिवीक्ष्य scaninng, तदा then, सीतया by Sita, रहिताम् devoid of, हेमन्ते in winter season, ध्वस्ताम् destroyed, पद्मिनीमिव like the lotus pond, श्रिया with beauty, विरहिताम् without, पर्णशालाम् cottage, ददर्श saw.

#### Explanation

Rama, the delight of the Raghu dynasty, hurtled in, turning round, throwing his hands to and fro, casting his looks all around the cottage where she used to move. The cottage, devoid of Sita looked like a lotuspond, the beauty of its lotuses destroyed by winter.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

उद्भ्रमन्निव वेगेन विक्षिपन्रघुनन्दनः। तत्र तत्रोटजस्थानमभिवीक्षय समन्ततः।।3.60.4।। ददर्श पर्णशालां च रहितां सीतया तदा। श्रिया विरहितां ध्वस्तां हेमन्ते पद्मिनीमिव।।3.60.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रघुनन्दनः delight of the Raghu race, वेगेन quickly, उद्भ्रमन्निव turning round and round, विक्षिपन्  throwing his hands, तत्र तत्र  here and there, उटजस्थानम् place of hermitage, समन्ततः all round, अभिवीक्ष्य scaninng, तदा then, सीतया by Sita, रहिताम् devoid of, हेमन्ते in winter season, ध्वस्ताम् destroyed, पद्मिनीमिव like the lotus pond, श्रिया with beauty, विरहिताम् without, पर्णशालाम् cottage, ददर्श saw.

#### Explanation

Rama, the delight of the Raghu dynasty, hurtled in, turning round, throwing his hands to and fro, casting his looks all around the cottage where she used to move. The cottage, devoid of Sita looked like a lotuspond, the beauty of its lotuses destroyed by winter.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

रुदन्तमिव वृक्षैश्च म्लानपुष्पमृगद्विजम्। श्रिया विहीनं विध्वस्तं सन्त्यक्तवनदेवतम्।।3.60.6।। विप्रकीर्णाजिनकुशं विप्रविद्धब्रुसीकटम्। दृष्ट्वा शून्यं निजस्थानं विललाप पुनः पुनः।।3.60.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वृक्षैः by the trees, रुदन्तमिव as if crying, म्लानपुष्पमृगद्विजम्  flowers withered and animals and birds turned pale, श्रिया the beauty, विहीनम् devoid of, विध्वस्तम् destroyed, संत्यक्तवनदेवतम् sylvan deities left, विप्रकीर्णाजिनकुशम् with deerskin and kusa grass scattered, विप्रविद्धब्रुसीकटम् with grass cushions and straw mat thrown pellmell, शून्यम् desolate, निजस्थानम् own place, दृष्ट्वा seeing, पुनः पुनः again and again, विललाप wept.

#### Explanation

The trees with flowers withered, the animals and birds turned pale looked as if they were weeping. Bereft of their beauty they wore a ruinous look. The sylvan deities had left.  The deerskin and kusa grass were strewn here and there, the grass cushions and straw mats lay scattered. Seeing his cottage so desolate Rama wept again and again.

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

रुदन्तमिव वृक्षैश्च म्लानपुष्पमृगद्विजम्। श्रिया विहीनं विध्वस्तं सन्त्यक्तवनदेवतम्।।3.60.6।। विप्रकीर्णाजिनकुशं विप्रविद्धब्रुसीकटम्। दृष्ट्वा शून्यं निजस्थानं विललाप पुनः पुनः।।3.60.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वृक्षैः by the trees, रुदन्तमिव as if crying, म्लानपुष्पमृगद्विजम्  flowers withered and animals and birds turned pale, श्रिया the beauty, विहीनम् devoid of, विध्वस्तम् destroyed, संत्यक्तवनदेवतम् sylvan deities left, विप्रकीर्णाजिनकुशम् with deerskin and kusa grass scattered, विप्रविद्धब्रुसीकटम् with grass cushions and straw mat thrown pellmell, शून्यम् desolate, निजस्थानम् own place, दृष्ट्वा seeing, पुनः पुनः again and again, विललाप wept.

#### Explanation

The trees with flowers withered, the animals and birds turned pale looked as if they were weeping. Bereft of their beauty they wore a ruinous look. The sylvan deities had left.  The deerskin and kusa grass were strewn here and there, the grass cushions and straw mats lay scattered. Seeing his cottage so desolate Rama wept again and again.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

हृता मृता वा नष्टा वा भक्षिता वा भविष्यति। निलीनाप्यथवा भीरुरथवा वनमाश्रिता।।3.60.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

भिरुः timid, हृता abducted, मृता वा or dead, नष्टा वा or ruined, भक्षिता वा or eaten up, भविष्यति may be, अथवा or else, निलीना hiding, अथवा or else, वनम् forest, आश्रिता taken shelter,

#### Explanation

'Timid Sita might have been abducted or dead or crushed or eaten up by demons. Or, she may be hiding for protection in the forest'

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

गता विचेतुं पुष्पाणि फलान्यपि च वा पुनः। अथवा पद्मिनीं याता जलार्थं वा नदीं गता।।3.60.9।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अपिवा or else, पुष्पाणि flowers, फलानि च and fruits, विचेतुम् to pluck, गता gone, अथवा or may be, पद्मिनीम् to a lotus pond, याता to get, जलार्थम्  to get water, नदीम् river, गता gone.

#### Explanation

'Maybe she has gone to pluck flowers or fruits. Or to the lotuspond or river to  fetch water.'

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

यत्नान्मृगयमाणस्तु नाससाद वने प्रियाम्। शोकरक्तेक्षणश्शोकादुन्मत्त इव लक्ष्यते।।3.60.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

यत्नात् by effort, मृगयमाणः तु while searching, वने in the forest, प्रियाम् darling, नाससाद could not see, शोकरक्तेक्षणः eyes turned red with tears, शोकात् owing to grief, उन्मत्त इव like a mad person, लक्ष्यते he appeared.

#### Explanation

He ransacked the forest, yet did not find his beloved. He  appeared like a mad man, his eyes turned red with tears of sorrow.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

वृक्षाद्वृक्षं प्रधावन्सगिरेश्चाद्रिं नदान्नदीम्। बभूव विलपन्रामश्शोकार्णवपरिप्लुतः।।3.60.11।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रामः Rama, वृक्षात् from tree, वृक्षम् to another tree, गिरेः mountain, अद्रिम् to another mountain, नदात् from one river, नदीम् to another river, प्रधावन् while running, विलपन्  while weeping, शोकार्णवपरिप्लुतः immersed in a sea of sorrow, बभूव remained.

#### Explanation

Running from tree to tree, hill to hill, and river to river and weeping, Rama was immersed in a sea of sorrow.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

अपि काचित्त्वया दृष्टा सा कदम्बप्रिया प्रिया। कदम्ब यदि जानीषे शंस सीतां शुभाननाम्।।3.60.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कदम्ब O Kadamba, कदम्बप्रिया O lover of kadamba flowers, प्रिया my beloved, सा she, काचित् some lady, त्वया by you, अपि दृष्टा is seen, जानीषे यदि if you know, शुभाननाम्  fair face, सीताम् Sita, शंस tell.

#### Explanation

O Kadamba tree, tell me if you have seen my beloved with a fair face and with love for kadamba flowers.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

स्निग्धपल्लवसङ्काशा पीतकौशेयवासिनी। शंसस्व यदि वा दृष्टा बिल्व बिल्वोपमस्तनी।।3.60.13।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

बिल्व O Bilva, स्निग्धपल्लवसङ्काशा lovely like tender leaf, पीतकौशेयवासिनी dressed in yellow silk, बिल्वोपमस्तनी lady whose breasts are round (like Bilva fruit), यदि वा दृष्टा if you have seen her, शंसस्व let me know.

#### Explanation

O Bilva tree, tell me if you have seen a lady delicate like your tender leaf, dressed in yellow silk, a lady whose breasts are round like Bilva fruits.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

अथवाऽर्जुन शंस त्वं प्रियां तामर्जुनप्रियाम्। जनकस्य सुता भीरुर्यदि जीवति वा न वा।।3.60.14।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अथवा or else, अर्जुन O Arjuna tree, त्वम् you, अर्जुनप्रियाम् lover of Arjuna tree, तां प्रियाम् that beloved of mine, शंस tell me if you know, भीरुः a timid lady, जनकस्य सुता daughter of Janaka, यदि जीवति वा is she living, न वा or not.

#### Explanation

O Arjuna tree, tell me if you know a timid lady, daughter of Janaka and my beloved, fond of Arjuna tree. Is the living or not ?

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

ककुभः ककुभोरूं तां व्यक्तं जानाति मैथिलीम्। यथा पल्लवपुष्पाढ्यो भाति ह्येष वनस्पतिः।।3.60.15।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वनस्पतिः the tree, पल्लवपुष्पाढ्यः rich with tender leaves and flowers, यथा as, भाति appears, ककुभः kakubha, ककुभोरूम् lady with beautiful thighs, ताम् her, मैथिलीम् Maithili's, व्यक्तम् clearly, जानाति the tree knows.

#### Explanation

This Kakubha tree rich with tender leaves and flowers (perhaps)knows Maithili whose thighs are beautiful like the trunk of the Kakubha tree.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

भ्रमरैरुपगीतश्च यथा द्रुमवरो ह्ययम्। एष व्यक्तं विजानाति तिलकस्तिलकप्रियाम्।।3.60.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

द्रुमवरः best of trees, अयम् this, तिलकः Tilaka, यथा as, भ्रमरैः with bees, उपगीतः is sung, एषः he that, तिलकप्रियाम् a lover of mark on the forehead, व्यक्तम् it is sure, विजानाति knows?

#### Explanation

This great Tilaka tree round which bees bumble surely knows the lady who loves to put tilaka marks on the forehead.

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

अशोक शोकापनुद शोकोपहतचेतसम्। त्वन्नामानं कुरु क्षिप्रं प्रियासन्दर्शनेन माम्।।3.60.17।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

शोकापनुद O dispeller of sorrow, अशोक O Ashoka tree, प्रियासन्दर्शनेन by showing me my darling, शोकोपहतचेतसम् with a griefstricken heart , माम् to me, क्षिप्रम् quickly, त्वन्नामानम् let me be called by your name, कुरु do it.

#### Explanation

O Ashoka tree, dispeller of sorrow, by quickly showing me my darling make me ashoka (free from sorrow) as I am griefstricken at heart.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

यदि ताल त्वया दृष्टा पक्वतालफलस्तनी। कथयस्व वरारोहां कारुण्यं यदि ते मयि।।3.60.18।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ताल Palmyra tree, पक्वतालफलस्तनी one who has breasts like ripened palmyra fruit, त्वया by you, दृष्टा यदि if seen, ते you, मयि my, कारुण्यं यदि kind to me, वरारोहाम् Sita, कथयस्व tell me.

#### Explanation

O Palmyra tree be kind to me and tell me if you have seen my beautiful beloved who has breasts like ripe palmyra fruit?

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

यदि दृष्टा त्वया सीता जम्बु जम्बूफलोपमा। प्रियां यदि विजानीषे निःशङ्कं कथयस्व मे।।3.60.19।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

जम्बु O Jambu tree, त्वया by you, सीता Sita, यदि दृष्टा if seen, जम्बूफलोपमा a lady whose body is shining lovely like gold, प्रियाम् the beloved one, (वि)जानीषे यदि know of her, मे to me, निःशङ्कम् without hesitation, कथयस्व tell me.

#### Explanation

O Jambu tree, do not hesitate to tell me if you know Sita whose body shines lovely like gold.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

अहो त्वं कर्णिकाराद्य सुपुष्पैश्शोभसे भृशम्। कर्णिकारप्रिया साध्वी शंस दृष्टा प्रिया यदि।।3.60.20।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कर्णिकाराः O Karnikara tree, त्वम् you, अद्य now, सुपुष्पैः with flowers in full doom, भृशम् शोभसे shine, अहो Oh, कर्णिकारप्रिया lover of Karnikara, साध्वी chaste woman, प्रिया beloved, दृष्टा यदि seen her, शंस tell.

#### Explanation

O Karnikara tree with flowers in full bloom, tell me if you have seen my faithful beloved who is fond of karnikara flowers.

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

चूतनीपमहासालान्पनसान्कुरवान्धवान्। दाडिमाननसान्गत्वा दृष्ट्वा रामो महायशाः।।3.60.21।। मल्लिका माधवीश्चैव चम्पकान्केतकीस्तथा। पृच्छन्रामो वने भान्तः उन्मत्त इव लक्ष्यते।।3.60.22।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महायशाः renowned, रामः Rama, वने forest, चूतनीपमहासालान् big trees like mango, kadamba and sal, पनसान् jackfruit tree, धवान् dhaba tree, दाडिमान् Pomegranate tree, अपि also,तान् them, मल्लिकाः jasmines, माधवीश्चैव Madhavi creepers, चम्पकान् champaks, तथा similarly, केतकीः Ketaki, दृष्ट्वा seeing, गत्वा going near, पृच्छन्  enquired, भ्रान्तः wandering in confusion, उन्मत्तः इव like a madman, लक्ष्यते looked.

#### Explanation

Renowned Rama, like a madman confused, approached the Mango, Kadamba , Sal and Jackfruit, Dhava, Champak, Ketaki trees and Pomegranate, Jasmine and Madhavi creepers, in order to make similar queries.

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

चूतनीपमहासालान्पनसान्कुरवान्धवान्। दाडिमाननसान्गत्वा दृष्ट्वा रामो महायशाः।।3.60.21।। मल्लिका माधवीश्चैव चम्पकान्केतकीस्तथा। पृच्छन्रामो वने भान्तः उन्मत्त इव लक्ष्यते।।3.60.22।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महायशाः renowned, रामः Rama, वने forest, चूतनीपमहासालान् big trees like mango, kadamba and sal, पनसान् jackfruit tree, धवान् dhaba tree, दाडिमान् Pomegranate tree, अपि also,तान् them, मल्लिकाः jasmines, माधवीश्चैव Madhavi creepers, चम्पकान् champaks, तथा similarly, केतकीः Ketaki, दृष्ट्वा seeing, गत्वा going near, पृच्छन्  enquired, भ्रान्तः wandering in confusion, उन्मत्तः इव like a madman, लक्ष्यते looked.

#### Explanation

Renowned Rama, like a madman confused, approached the Mango, Kadamba , Sal and Jackfruit, Dhava, Champak, Ketaki trees and Pomegranate, Jasmine and Madhavi creepers, in order to make similar queries.

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

अथवा मृगशाबाक्षीं मृग जानासि मैथिलीम्। मृगविप्रेक्षणी कान्ता मृगीभिस्सहिता भवेत्।।3.60.23।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अथवा or, मृग O deer, मृगशाबाक्षीम् doe like eyes, मैथिलीम् Maithili, जानासि known, मृगविप्रेक्षणी  whose eyes  look like eyes of young deer, कान्ता beloved, मृगीभिः among the female deer, सहिता with, भवेत् may be.

#### Explanation

Or,  O deer do you know about Maithili who has the eyes of a fawn? My beloved who has the restless eyes of a doe may be found in their company.

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

गज सा गजनासोरूर्यदि दृष्टा त्वया भवेत्। तां मन्ये विदितां तुभ्यमाख्याहि वरवारण।3.60.24।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

गज O elephant, गजनासोरूः whose thighs are like the trunk of an elephant, सा she, त्वया by you, दृष्टा is seen, यदि भवेत् if she be, ताम् her, तुभ्यम् to you, विदिताम् known, मन्ये I think, वरवारण O best of elephants, आख्याहि tell me.

#### Explanation

O elephant tell me if you have seen her whose thighs are like the trunk of an elephant. O best of elephants, tell me if you know her.

---

### Shloka 25

#### Shloka Text

शार्दूल यदि सा दृष्टा प्रिया चन्द्रनिभानना। मैथिली मम विस्रब्धं कथयस्व न ते भयम्।।3.60.25।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

शार्दूल tiger, चन्द्रनिभानना lady with a Moon like face, प्रिया dear, सा मैथिली that Mythili, दृष्टा यदि if at all seen by you, विस्रब्धम् with confidence, मम to me, कथयस्व tell me, ते to you, भयम् fear, न not.

#### Explanation

O tiger tell me freely and fearlessly if you have seen my beloved princess from Mithila with a face like the moon.

---

### Shloka 26

#### Shloka Text

किं धावसि प्रिये दूरे दृष्टासि कमलेक्षणे। वृक्षैराच्छाद्य चात्मानं किं मां न प्रतिभाषसे।।3.60.26।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कमलेक्षणे O lotuseyed, प्रिये dear, दूरे to a distance, किम् why, धावसि you are running, दृष्टा seen, असि you, आत्मानम् yourself, वृक्षैः with trees, आच्छाद्य covering, माम् me, किम् why, न प्रतिभाषसे not replying.

#### Explanation

O lotus eyed darling, why are you running away? I have already seen you.  Why are you hiding behind trees and not replying to me?

---

### Shloka 27
