#### Shloka Text

तिष्ठ तिष्ठ वरारोहे न तेऽस्ति करुणा मयि। नात्यर्थं हास्यशीलासि किमर्थं मामुपेक्षसे।।3.60.27।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वरारोहे O beautiful lady, तिष्ठ तिष्ठ stay, stay, ते to you, मयि in me, करुणा compassion, नास्ति is not there, अत्यर्थम् very much, हास्यशीला lady fond of making fun, नासि you are not, माम् me, किमर्थम् why, उपेक्षसे do you avoid me.

#### Explanation

O my beautiful beloved, stay, stay  Have you no compassion for me ? You are so fond of fun. Why do you avoid me?

---

### Shloka 28

#### Shloka Text

पीतकौशेयकेनासि सूचिता वरवर्णिनि। धावन्त्यपि मया दृष्टा तिष्ठ यद्यस्ति सौहृदम्।।3.60.28।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वरवर्णिनि O lady of lovely complexion, पीतकौशेयकेन by your yellow silk, सूचिता  indicated, असि you are, धावन्त्यपि even while you are running away, मया by me, दृष्टा you are seen, सौहृदम् love for me, अस्ति यदि if you have, तिष्ठ stay.

#### Explanation

O lady of lovely complexion, I can see your yellow silk (flying), while you are running away. If you have love for me, stay on.

---

### Shloka 29

#### Shloka Text

नैव सा नूनमथवा हिंसिता चारुहासिनी। कृच्छ्रप्राप्तं न मां नूनं यथोपेक्षितुमर्हति।।3.60.29।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अथवा even so, सा she, नैव never, चारुहासिनी lady with sweet smile, हिंसिता hurt, नूनम् surely, यथा as such, कृच्छ्रप्राप्तम् when in difficulties, माम् me, उपेक्षितुम् to ignore, नार्हति should not, नूनम् surely.

#### Explanation

O lady with a sweet smile I have never hurt you. When I am in difficulty it does not behove you to ignore me.

---

### Shloka 30

#### Shloka Text

व्यक्तं सा भक्षिता बाला राक्षसैः पिशिताशनैः। विभज्याङ्कानि सर्वाणि मया विरहिता प्रिया।।3.60.30।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मया by me, विरहिता separated, प्रिया my beloved, सा बाला that young lady, व्यक्तम् it is clear, सर्वाणि all, अङ्गानि limbs, विभज्य tearing, पिशिताशनैः by flesheaters, राक्षसैः by demons  भक्षिता is eaten away.

#### Explanation

It is clear that separated from me, the body of my young beloved is torn off and eaten away by carnivorous demons.

---

### Shloka 31

#### Shloka Text

नूनं तच्छुभदन्तोष्ठं सुनासं चारुकुण्डलम्। पूर्णचन्द्रनिभं ग्रस्तं मुखंनिष्प्रभतां गतम्।।3.60.31।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

नूनम् surely, शुभदन्तोष्ठम् having beautiful teeth and lips, सुनासम् having beautiful nose, चारुकुण्डलम् shining earrings, पूर्णचन्द्रनिभम् full Moon like, तत् that, मुखम् face, ग्रस्तम् eclipsed, निष्प्रभताम् dull, गतम् is rendered.

#### Explanation

Her beautiful face with her sparkling teeth and lips, wellshaped nose, shining earrings has been like the full moon, eclipsed and rendered pale.

---

### Shloka 32

#### Shloka Text

सा हि चम्पकवर्णाभा ग्रीवा ग्रैवेयशोभिता। कोमला विलपन्त्यास्तु कान्ताया भक्षिता शुभा।।3.60.32।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

विलपन्त्याः wept, कान्तायाः  dear wife, चम्पकवर्णाभा like the colour of champak flowers, ग्रैवेयशोभिता adorned with necklaces and other golden chains, कोमला a delicate, शुभा auspicious, सा ग्रीवा of lovely neck, भक्षिता was eaten.

#### Explanation

That delicate, beautiful neck of my beloved having the complexion of a champak flower and adorned with necklaces was perhaps eaten up.

---

### Shloka 33

#### Shloka Text

नूनं विक्षिप्यमाणौ तौ बाहू पल्लवकोमलौ। भक्षितौ वेपमानाग्रौ सहस्ताभरणाङ्गदौ।।3.60.33।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

विक्षिप्यमाणौ  thrown, पल्लवकोमलौ tender like leaves, वेपमानाग्रौ with trembling tips, सहस्ताभरणाङ्गदौ shining with armlets, bracelets, तौ बाहू those two arms, नूनम् surely, भक्षितौ eaten away.

#### Explanation

Her arms, delicate like tender leaves, their tips (fingers) quivering, adorned with armlets and bracelets have surely been eaten away.

---

### Shloka 34

#### Shloka Text

मया विरहिता बाला रक्षसां भक्षणाय वै। सार्धेनेव परित्यक्ता भक्षिता बहुबान्धवा।।3.60.34।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

बाला young lady, रक्षसाम् of the demons, भक्षणाय वै for eating by, मया by me, विरहिता  separated, बहुबान्धवा having many relations, सार्धेन when half of it is left, परित्यक्ता इव as if abandoned, भक्षिता eaten.

#### Explanation

The young lady having many relations, forsaken by me, has been (perhaps) picked up by a demon for food and later abandoned halfeaten.

---

### Shloka 35

#### Shloka Text

हा लक्ष्मण महाबाहो पश्यसि त्वं प्रियां क्वचित्। हा प्रिये क्व गता भद्रे हा सीतेति पुनः पुनः।।3.60.35।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महाबाहो O longarmed one, हाः लक्ष्मण 'Alas Lakshmana ', त्वम् you, क्वचित् in any place, प्रियाम् dear, पश्यसि are you able to see, भद्रे  'O noble lady', हा प्रिये 'O darling', क्व where, गता you are gone, हा सीते O Sita', इति  thus, पुनः again, पुनः again.

#### Explanation

O longarmed Lakshmana, are you able to see my beloved anywhere? O noble lady, O darling, where have you gone? O Sita  SIta

---

### Shloka 36

#### Shloka Text

इत्येवं विलपन्रामः परिधावन्वनाद्वनम्। क्वचिदुद्भ्रमते वेगात्क्वचिद्विभ्रमते बलात्।।3.60.36।। क्वचिन्मत्त इवाभाति कान्तान्वेषणतत्परः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रामः Rama, इत्येवम् in that manner, विलपन्  weeping, वनात् from one forest, वनम् to the other forest, परिधावन्  running, क्वचित् at some place, वेगात् with speed, उद्भ्रमते jumped, क्वचित् at some place, बलात् with his strength, विभ्रमते moves fast, क्वचित्  at some place, कान्तान्वेषणतत्परः busy in search of his beloved, मत्तः इव like a madman, आभाति was seen.

#### Explanation

Rama ran from forest to forest, now jumping, now taking strong strides, but all the time weeping, a madman busy in search of his beloved.

---

### Shloka 37

#### Shloka Text

स वनानि नदीश्शैलान् गिरिप्रस्रवणानि च।।3.60.37।। काननानि च वेगेन भ्रमत्यपरिसंस्थितः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः he, वनानि the forests, नदी: rivers, शैलान् hills, गिरिप्रस्रवणानि च streams of mountains, काननानि च dense forests, अपरिसंस्थितः not stable, वेगेन rushed, भ्रमति he wandered.

#### Explanation

He rushed through forests, rivers, hills and mountain streams and through dense jungles with an unstable mind.

---

### Shloka 38

#### Shloka Text

तथा स गत्वा विपुलम् महद्वनं परीत्य सर्वं त्वथ मैथिलीं प्रति। अनिष्ठिताशस्सचकार मार्गणे पुनः प्रियायाः परमं परिश्रमम्।।3.60.38।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः Rama, विपुलम् vast, महत् great, वनम् forest, तथा in that way, गत्वा after going, सर्वम् all, परीत्य searching, मैथिलीं प्रति pertaining to Maithili, अनिष्ठिताशः giving up hope of finding her, सः Rama, पुनः again, प्रियायाः his beloved's, मार्गणे in search, परमम् supreme, परिश्रमम् effort, चकार made.

#### Explanation

Rama went about the vast forest searching for the princess from Mithila all over not giving up hope of finding her. He continued to make great efforts for searching his beloved. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये अरण्यकाण्डे षष्टितमस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the sixtieth sarga of Aranyakanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 61

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

दृष्ट्वाश्रमपदं शून्यं रामो दशरथात्मजः। रहितां पर्णशालां च विध्वस्तान्यासनानि च।।3.61.1।। अदृष्ट्वा तत्र वैदेहीं सन्निरीक्ष्य च सर्वशः। उवाच रामः प्राक्रुश्य प्रगृह्य रुचिरौ भुजौ।।3.61.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

दशरथात्मजः son of Dasaratha, रामः Rama, शून्यम् empty, आश्रमपदम् hermitage, रहिताम्  devoid of, च and, विध्वस्तानि disturbed, आसनानि च seats, दृष्ट्वा saw, तत्र thereafter, वैदेहीम् Vaidehi, अदृष्ट्वा having not seen, सर्वशः all over, सन्निरीक्ष्य च scanning, प्राक्रुश्य etched, रुचिरौ lovely, भुजौ shoulders, प्रगृह्य holding, उवाच said.

#### Explanation

Rama, son of Dasaratha, saw the empty cottage devoid of Sita. The mats were displaced. He searched everywhere but unable to see Vaidehi lifted his lovely arms and said:

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

दृष्ट्वाश्रमपदं शून्यं रामो दशरथात्मजः। रहितां पर्णशालां च विध्वस्तान्यासनानि च।।3.61.1।। अदृष्ट्वा तत्र वैदेहीं सन्निरीक्ष्य च सर्वशः। उवाच रामः प्राक्रुश्य प्रगृह्य रुचिरौ भुजौ।।3.61.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

दशरथात्मजः son of Dasaratha, रामः Rama, शून्यम् empty, आश्रमपदम् hermitage, रहिताम्  devoid of, च and, विध्वस्तानि disturbed, आसनानि च seats, दृष्ट्वा saw, तत्र thereafter, वैदेहीम् Vaidehi, अदृष्ट्वा having not seen, सर्वशः all over, सन्निरीक्ष्य च scanning, प्राक्रुश्य etched, रुचिरौ lovely, भुजौ shoulders, प्रगृह्य holding, उवाच said.

#### Explanation

Rama, son of Dasaratha, saw the empty cottage devoid of Sita. The mats were displaced. He searched everywhere but unable to see Vaidehi lifted his lovely arms and said:

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

क्व नु लक्ष्मण वैदेही कं वा देशमितो गता। केनाहृता वा सौमित्रे भक्षिता केन वा प्रिया।।3.61.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लक्ष्मण O Lakshmana, वैदेही Vaidehi, क्व नु where is she, कं वा देशम् in which land, गता gone, सौमित्रे O son of Sumitra, केन by whom, आहृता वा is abducted, प्रिया my beloved, केन वा by whom, भक्षिता eaten.

#### Explanation

O son of Sumitra, where is my beloved ? Where did she go? By whom was she abducted? Or, devoured? O Lakshmana

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

वृक्षेणाच्छाद्य यदि मां सीते हसितुमिच्छसि। अलं ते हसितेनाद्य मां भजस्व सुदुःखितम्।।3.61.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सीते O Sita, वृक्षेण by tree, आच्छाद्य hiding behind, माम् me, हसितुम् to make fun, इच्छसि यदि are you intending to, ते you, हसितेन fun, अलम् enough, अद्य now, सुदुःखितम् very muchgrieved, माम् me, भजस्व come to me.

#### Explanation

O Sita if you intend to make fun by hiding behind a tree, I tell you it is enough. Stop it and come to this extremely sorrowful man.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

यैस्सह क्रीडसे सीते विश्वस्तैर्मृगपोतकैः। एते हीनास्त्वया सौम्ये ध्यायन्त्यस्राविलेक्षणाः।।3.61.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सौम्ये O gentle lady, सीते Sita, विश्वस्तैः relied on you, यैः by those, मृगपोतकैः सह with the fawn, क्रीडसे play, एते these, त्वया by you, हीनाः separated, अस्राविलेक्षणाः eyes filled with tears, ध्यायन्ति they are thinking of you.

#### Explanation

O gentle  Sita those fawns who trusted you and played with you, are now separated from you. Brooding over you, their eyes are brimming with tears.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

सीतया रहितोऽहं वै न हि जीवामि लक्ष्मण। मृतं शोकेन महता सीताहरणजेन माम्।।3.61.6।। परलोके महाराजो नूनं द्रक्ष्यति मे पिता।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लक्ष्मण Lakshmana, सीतया by Sita, रहितः without, अहम् I, न हि जीवामि I cannot live, सीताहरणजेन arising out of Sita's abduction, महता by deep, शोकेन by grief, मृतं dead, माम् me, महाराजः the king (Dasaratha), मे पिता my father, नूनम् certainly, परलोके in the other world, द्रक्ष्यति will meet me.

#### Explanation

O Lakshmana, I cannot live without Sita. I am filled with deep grief on account of Sita's abduction. My late father, the king will certainly see me in the other world (I will definitely commit suicide).

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

कथं प्रतिज्ञां संश्रुत्य मया त्वमभियोजितः।।3.61.7।। अपूरयित्वा तं कालं मत्सकाशमिहागतः। कामवृत्तमनार्यं मां मृषावादिनमेव च।।3.61.8।। धिक्त्वामिति परे लोके व्यक्तं वक्ष्यति मे पिता।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

माम् me, मया by me, अभियोजितः employed by me, त्वम् you, प्रतिज्ञाम् a vow, संश्रुत्य having promised, तं कालम् that full term, अपूरयित्वा without completing, मत्सकाशम् into my presence, कथम् how, इह here, आगतः came, कामवृत्तम् an irresponsible person, अनार्यम् ignoble, मृषावादिनमेव च liar, त्वाम् you, धिक् fie upon, इति thus, परे लोके in the other world, मे पिता my father, व्यक्तम् surely, वक्ष्यति will tell me.

#### Explanation

My father say will surely in the other world: Without completing the appointed period of the vow taken, how could you come here ? Fie upon you  You are irresponsible. You are ignoble. You are a liar.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

कथं प्रतिज्ञां संश्रुत्य मया त्वमभियोजितः।।3.61.7।। अपूरयित्वा तं कालं मत्सकाशमिहागतः। कामवृत्तमनार्यं मां मृषावादिनमेव च।।3.61.8।। धिक्त्वामिति परे लोके व्यक्तं वक्ष्यति मे पिता।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

माम् me, मया by me, अभियोजितः employed by me, त्वम् you, प्रतिज्ञाम् a vow, संश्रुत्य having promised, तं कालम् that full term, अपूरयित्वा without completing, मत्सकाशम् into my presence, कथम् how, इह here, आगतः came, कामवृत्तम् an irresponsible person, अनार्यम् ignoble, मृषावादिनमेव च liar, त्वाम् you, धिक् fie upon, इति thus, परे लोके in the other world, मे पिता my father, व्यक्तम् surely, वक्ष्यति will tell me.

#### Explanation

My father say will surely in the other world: Without completing the appointed period of the vow taken, how could you come here ? Fie upon you  You are irresponsible. You are ignoble. You are a liar.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

विवशं शोकसन्तप्तं दीनं भग्नमनोरथम्।।3.61.9।। मामिहोत्सृज्य करुणं कीर्तिर्नरमिवानृजुम्। क्व गच्छसि वरारोहे मां नोत्सृज सुमध्यमे।।3.61.10।। त्वया विरहितश्चाहं मोक्ष्ये जीवितमात्मनः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वरारोहे lovely lady, विवशम् helpless, शोकसन्तप्तम् tormented by sorrow, दीनम् piteous, भग्नमनोरथम् disappointed , माम् me, कीर्तिः fame, अनृजुम् crook, नरमिव like such a man, इह here, करुणम् pitiable, उत्सृज्य leaving, क्व गच्छसि where have you gone, सुमध्यमे lady with a slender waist, मा myself, मा उत्सृज leave me not, त्वया by you, विरहितः separated, अहम् I, आत्मनः my, जीवितम् life, मोक्ष्ये I will give up.

#### Explanation

O lovely lady where have you gone, leaving me here like fame deserting a crook? I am helpless griefstricken, pitiable, pathetic and disappointed. O lady of slender waist, leave me not. Separated from you, I will give up my life.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

विवशं शोकसन्तप्तं दीनं भग्नमनोरथम्।।3.61.9।। मामिहोत्सृज्य करुणं कीर्तिर्नरमिवानृजुम्। क्व गच्छसि वरारोहे मां नोत्सृज सुमध्यमे।।3.61.10।। त्वया विरहितश्चाहं मोक्ष्ये जीवितमात्मनः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वरारोहे lovely lady, विवशम् helpless, शोकसन्तप्तम् tormented by sorrow, दीनम् piteous, भग्नमनोरथम् disappointed , माम् me, कीर्तिः fame, अनृजुम् crook, नरमिव like such a man, इह here, करुणम् pitiable, उत्सृज्य leaving, क्व गच्छसि where have you gone, सुमध्यमे lady with a slender waist, मा myself, मा उत्सृज leave me not, त्वया by you, विरहितः separated, अहम् I, आत्मनः my, जीवितम् life, मोक्ष्ये I will give up.

#### Explanation

O lovely lady where have you gone, leaving me here like fame deserting a crook? I am helpless griefstricken, pitiable, pathetic and disappointed. O lady of slender waist, leave me not. Separated from you, I will give up my life.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

इतीव विलपन्रामस्सीतादर्शनलालसः।।3.61.11।। न ददर्श सुदुःखार्तो राघवो जनकात्मजाम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सीतादर्शनलालसः eager to see Sita, सुदुःखार्तः deeply distressed, राघवः Rama, इतीव in that manner, विलपन् wailed, जनकात्मजाम् the daughter of Janaka, न ददर्श did not see.

#### Explanation

Deeply distressed, Rama who was anxious to see Sita, daughter of Janaka, wailed and wailed but could not see her.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

अनासादयमानं तं सीतां दशरथात्मजम्।।3.61.12।। पङ्कमासाद्य विपुलं सीदन्तमिव कुञ्जरम्। लक्ष्मणो राममत्यर्थमुवाच हितकाम्यया।।3.61.13।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सीताम् Sita, अनासादयमानम् unable to find, दशरथात्मजम् the son of Dasaratha, विपुलम् deep, पङ्कम् mud, आसाद्य reached, सीदन्तम् sinking,  कुञ्जरम् इव like an elephant, तं रामम् that Rama, लक्ष्मणः Lakshmana, अत्यर्थम् great, हितकाम्यया for his welfare, उवाच said.

#### Explanation

Lakshmana, dedicated to his wellbeing, spoke to Rama, son of Dasaratha, who was sinking like an elephant into the quagmire, when he failed to trace Sita.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

अनासादयमानं तं सीतां दशरथात्मजम्।।3.61.12।। पङ्कमासाद्य विपुलं सीदन्तमिव कुञ्जरम्। लक्ष्मणो राममत्यर्थमुवाच हितकाम्यया।।3.61.13।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सीताम् Sita, अनासादयमानम् unable to find, दशरथात्मजम् the son of Dasaratha, विपुलम् deep, पङ्कम् mud, आसाद्य reached, सीदन्तम् sinking,  कुञ्जरम् इव like an elephant, तं रामम् that Rama, लक्ष्मणः Lakshmana, अत्यर्थम् great, हितकाम्यया for his welfare, उवाच said.

#### Explanation

Lakshmana, dedicated to his wellbeing, spoke to Rama, son of Dasaratha, who was sinking like an elephant into the quagmire, when he failed to trace Sita.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

मा विषादं महाबाहो कुरु यत्नं मया सह। इदं च हि वनं शूर बहुकन्दरशोभितम्।।3.61.14।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महाबाहो  longarmed, विषादम् sorrow, मा do not resort to, मया सह along with me, यत्नम् effort, कुरु do, शूर O hero, इदम् this,वनम् forest,बहुकन्दरशोभितं हि full of caves.

#### Explanation

O longarmed, heroic Rama, do not grieve. This forest is full of caves. Let us try together.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

प्रियकाननसञ्चारा वनोन्मत्ता च मैथिली। सा वनं वा प्रविष्टा स्यान्नलिनीं वा सुपुष्पिताम्।।3.61.15।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

प्रियकाननसञ्चारा one who is fond of wandering in the forest, वनोन्मत्ताच mad after forest , सा मैथिली that Maithili, वनं वा or in the forest, सुपुष्पिताम् flowering, नलिनीं वा or lotuspond, प्रविष्टा has entered, स्यात् perhaps.

#### Explanation

Sita is madly in love with the forest. She might have entered deep into the jungle or into a fully blossomed lotuspond.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

सरितं वापि सम्प्राप्ता मीनवञ्जुलसेविताम्। स्नातुकामा निलीना स्याद्धासकामा वने क्वचित्।।3.61.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

स्नातुकामा ta take a dip, मीनवञ्जुलसेविताम् full of reeds and fishes, सरितं वापि or rivers, सम्प्राप्ता has reached, हासकामा to make fun, क्वचित् some where, निलीना वा might have hidden, स्यात् it may be so.

#### Explanation

Maybe she has gone to take a dip in the river full of reeds and fishes  Or has hidden somewhere for the sake of fun.

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

वित्रासयितुकामा वा लीना स्यात्कानने क्वचित्। जिज्ञासमाना वैदेही त्वां मां च पुरुषर्षभ।।3.61.17।। तस्याह्यन्वेषणे श्रीमन् क्षिप्रमेव यतावहे।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पुरुषर्षभ O bull among men, वैदेही Vaidehi, त्वां च at you, मां च at me, वित्रासयितुकामा वा intending to scare us, जिज्ञासमाना  desire to know, कानने in the forest, क्वचित् somewhere, लीना hidden, स्यात् may be, श्रीमन् O blessed , तस्याः her, अन्वेषणे in search, क्षिप्रमेव immediately, यतावहे both of us will strive.

#### Explanation

O best of men, maybe Vaidehi has hidden in the forest intending to scare you and me. She wishes to test our love. O blessed Rama let us ransack the forest immediately.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

वनं सर्वं विचिनुवो यत्र सा जनकात्मजा।।3.61.18।। मन्यसे यदि काकुत्स्थ मा स्म शोके मनः कृथाः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

काकुत्स्थ Rama, मन्यसे यदि if you think , सा she, जनकात्मजा Sita, यत्र wherever, सर्वम् all, वनम् forest, विचिनुव: we both will search, शोके in grief, मनः mind, मा स्म कृथाः do not indulge.

#### Explanation

O scion of the Kakutsthas, let us search all over the forest if you please. Do not indulge in sorrow.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

एवमुक्तस्तु सौहार्दाल्लक्ष्मणेन समाहितः।।3.61.19।। सह सौमित्रिणा रामो विचेतुमुपचक्रमे।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लक्ष्मणेन by Lakshmana, सौहार्दात् very affectionately, एवम् in that way, उक्तः having been told, रामः Rama, समाहितः became composed, सौमित्रिणा सह along with Lakshmana the son of Sumitra, विचेतुम् to search, उपचक्रमे he started.

#### Explanation

When Lakshmana consoled him so affectionately Rama became composed. He continued the search with Lakshmana, son of Sumitra.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

तौ वनानि गिरींश्चैव सरितश्च सरांसि च।।3.61.20।। निखिलेन विचिन्वानौ सीतां दशरथात्मजौ।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तौ दशरथात्मजौ those two sons of Dasaratha, वनानि forests, गिरीन् चैव and the mountains, सरितश्च and the rivers, सरांसि च and the ponds, निखिलेन entirly, सीताम् for Sita, विचिन्वानौ while they searched.

#### Explanation

The two sons of Dasaratha went on searching for Sita in forests, mountains, rivers, ponds everywhere.

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

तस्य शैलस्य सानूनि गुहाश्च शिखराणि च।।3.61.21।। निखिलेन विचिन्वानौ नैव तामभिजग्मतुः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तस्य शैलस्य that mountain's, सानूनि slopes, गुहाश्च caves also, शिखराणि च mountain peaks too, निखिलेन entirely, विचिन्वानौ searched, ताम् her, नैव अभिजग्मतुः were not able to find.

#### Explanation

They were not able to find Sita even though they searched all over the mountain slopes, caves and peaks.

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

विचित्य सर्वतश्शैलं रामो लक्ष्मणमब्रवित्।।3.61.22।। नेह पश्यामि सौमित्रे वैदेहीं पर्वते शुभाम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रामः Rama, सर्वतः everywhere, शैलम् mountain, विचित्य after searching, लक्ष्मणम् to Lakshmana, अब्रवीत् said, सौमित्रे O son of Sumitra, इह here, पर्वते on mountains, शुभाम् noble lady, वैदेहीम् Vaidehi, न पश्यामि not seen.

#### Explanation

After searching her all over, Rama said  Saumitri, I am unable to find the noble Vaidehi on the mountain

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

ततो दुःखाभिसन्तप्तो लक्ष्मणो वाक्यमब्रवीत्।।3.61.23।। विचरन्दण्डकारण्यं भ्रातरं दीप्ततेजसम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, दुःखाभिसन्तप्तः  consumed by sorrow, लक्ष्मणः Lakshmana, दण्डकारण्यम् Dandaka forest, विचरन् while going about, दीप्ततेजसम् glowing with brilliance, भ्रातरम् brother, वाक्यम् these words, अब्रवीत् said.

#### Explanation

Going about the Dandaka forest Lakshmana, consumed by sorrow, said  to his brother, glowing with brilliance:

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

प्राप्स्यसि त्वं महाप्राज्ञ मैथिलीं जनकात्मजाम्।।3.61.24।। यथा विष्णुर्महाबाहुर्बलिं बद्ध्वा महीमिमाम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महाप्राज्ञ O wise sire, त्वम् you, जनकात्मजाम् daughter of Janaka, मैथिलीम् princess from Mithila, महाबाहुः one with long arms, विष्णुः Visnu, बलिम् Bali, बद्ध्वा after binding, इमाम् this, महीं यथा earned this earth, प्राप्स्यसि you will attain.

#### Explanation

O longarmed brother O wise sire  just as Visnu obtained the earth by binding Bali you will obtain Sita certainly.

---

### Shloka 25

#### Shloka Text

एवमुक्तस्तु सौहार्दाल्लक्ष्मणेन स राघवः।।3.61.25।। उवाच दीनया वाचा दुःखाभिहतचेतनः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लक्ष्मणेन by Lakshmana, सौहार्दात् affectionately, एवम् in that way, उक्तः having been said, सः राघवः that Rama, दुःखाभिहतचेतनः  with griefstriken heart, दीनया piteously, वाचा by words, उवाच said.

#### Explanation

Having been addressed thus by Lakshmana, Raghava with a griefstriken heart said these piteous words :' Lakshmana,having  spoken like that very affectionately to Rama,who was disturbed by excessive grief said these words piteously:

---

### Shloka 26

#### Shloka Text

वनं सर्वं सुविचितं पद्मिन्यः फुल्लपङ्कजाः।।3.61.26।। गिरिश्चायं महाप्राज्ञ बहुकन्दरनिर्झरः। न हि पश्यामि वैदेहीं प्राणेभ्योऽपि गरीयसीम्।।3.61.27।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महाप्राज्ञ wise sire, सर्वम् all over, वनम् the forest, सुविचितम् searched thoroughly, फ़ुल्लपङ्कजाः fully blossomed lotuses, पद्मिन्यः lotusponds, बहुकन्दर निर्झरः having many caves and streams, अयम् I, गिरिश्च mountains also, प्राणेभ्योऽपि more than my life, गरीयसीम् more important, वैदेहीम् Vaidehi, न हि पश्यामि do not see.

#### Explanation

O wise Lakshmana I have searched the entire forest, the ponds with fully blossomed lotuses, the mountains with many caves and streams. But could not see Vaidehi who is dearer to me than my life.

---

### Shloka 27

#### Shloka Text

वनं सर्वं सुविचितं पद्मिन्यः फुल्लपङ्कजाः।।3.61.26।। गिरिश्चायं महाप्राज्ञ बहुकन्दरनिर्झरः। न हि पश्यामि वैदेहीं प्राणेभ्योऽपि गरीयसीम्।।3.61.27।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महाप्राज्ञ wise sire, सर्वम् all over, वनम् the forest, सुविचितम् searched thoroughly, फ़ुल्लपङ्कजाः fully blossomed lotuses, पद्मिन्यः lotusponds, बहुकन्दर निर्झरः having many caves and streams, अयम् I, गिरिश्च mountains also, प्राणेभ्योऽपि more than my life, गरीयसीम् more important, वैदेहीम् Vaidehi, न हि पश्यामि do not see.

#### Explanation

O wise Lakshmana I have searched the entire forest, the ponds with fully blossomed lotuses, the mountains with many caves and streams. But could not see Vaidehi who is dearer to me than my life.

---

### Shloka 28

#### Shloka Text

एवं स विलपन्रामस्सीताहरणकर्शितः। दीनश्शोकसमाविष्टो मुहूर्तं विह्वलोऽभवत्।।3.61.28।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सीताहरणकर्शितः tormented by Sita's abduction, सः रामः  Rama, एवम् thus, विलपन् cried, दीनः was dejected, शोकसमाविष्टः overpowered by grief, मुहूर्तम् for a moment, विह्वलः consciousess lost, अभवत् became.

#### Explanation

Thus tormented by the abduction of Sita, Rama felt dejected and cried.Overpowered by grief he lost his consciousness for a moment.

---

### Shloka 29

#### Shloka Text

सन्तप्तो ह्यवसन्नाङ्गो गतबुद्धिर्विचेतनः। निषसादातुरो दीनो निश्श्वस्याशीतमायतम्।।3.61.29।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सन्तप्तः tormented, अवसन्नाङ्गः with limbs enervated, गतबुद्धि: intellect atrophied, विचेतनः lost senses, आतुरः anxious, दीनः dejected, अशीतम् hot, आयतम् long, निश्श्वस्य sighing, निषसाद reclined.

#### Explanation

By grief tormented, limbs enervated, intellect atrophied, senses switched off, he felt anxious and depressed and sighed long and hot.

---

### Shloka 30

#### Shloka Text

बहुलं स तु निश्श्वस्य रामो राजीवलोचनः। हा प्रियेति विचुक्रोश बहुशो बाष्पगद्गदः।।3.61.30।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राजीवलोचनः lotuseyed, सः रामः that Rama, बहुलम् long, निश्श्वस्य sighing, बाष्पगद्गदः throat choked with sobbing, हा प्रियेति saying 'Alas, my darling', बहुशः many times, विचुक्रोश cried lotuseyed.

#### Explanation

Rama, his throat choked, shed propuse tears, sighing, O my darling.

---

### Shloka 31

#### Shloka Text

तं ततस्सान्त्वयामास लक्ष्मणः प्रियबान्धवः। बहुप्रकारं धर्मज्ञः प्रश्रितं प्रश्रिताञ्जलिः।।3.61.31।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, प्रियबान्धवः  dear friend, धर्मज्ञः knower of dharma, लक्ष्मणः Lakshmana, प्रश्रिताञ्जलिः with folded palms, प्रश्रितम् humbly, बहुप्रकारम् many ways, तम् him, सान्त्वयामास consoled.

#### Explanation

Then Lakshmana, the knower of dharma and a dear friend offered obeisance most humbly with folded palms and consoled Rama in many ways.

---

### Shloka 32

#### Shloka Text

अनादृत्य तु तद्वाक्यं लक्ष्मणोष्ठपुटाच्च्युतम्। अपश्यंस्तां प्रियां सीतां प्राक्रोशत्स पुनः पुनः।।3.61.32।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः he, तु on his part, लक्ष्मणोष्ठपुटात् from the lips of Lakshmana, च्युतम् uttered, तत् that, वाक्यम् words, अनादृत्य not caring, प्रियाम् beloved, तां सीताम् that Sita, अपश्यन् not able to see, पुनः पुनः again and again, प्राक्रोशत् cried loudly.

#### Explanation

Without caring  for the words from the lips of Lakshmana, Rama sobbed loudly, unable to see his beloved Sita. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये अरण्यकाण्डे एकषष्टितमस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the sixtyfirst sarga of Aranyakanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 62

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

सीतामपश्यन्धर्मात्मा शोकोपहतचेतनः। विललाप महाबाहू रामः कमललोचनः।।3.62.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

धर्मात्मा righteous Rama, महाबाहुः longarmed, कमललोचनः lotuseyed, रामः Rama, सीताम् Sita, अपश्यन् not able to see, शोकोपहतचेतनः senses overwhelmed by grief, विललाप wept.

#### Explanation

The righteous, the longarmed,lotuseyed Rama, unable to see Sita wept with his senses overwhilmed by grief.

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

पश्यन्निव च तां सीतामपश्यन्मदनार्दितः। उवाच राघवो वाक्यं विलापाश्रयदुर्वचम्।।3.62.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मदनार्दितः under the spell of Cupid, राघवः Rama, ताम् her, सीताम् Sita, अपश्यन् while not seeing, पश्यन्निव as if he was seeing, विलापाश्रयदुर्वचम्  words which cannot be uttered  in grief, वाक्यम् words, उवाच said.

#### Explanation

Under the spell of Cupid, Rama uttered such words in grief which are otherwise unutterable. As if he was seeing Sita (before him) which was not a fact.

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

त्वमशोकस्य शाखाभिः पुष्पप्रियतया प्रिये। आवृणोषि शरीरं ते मम शोकविवर्धिनी।।3.62.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

प्रिये O my beloved, त्वम् you, पुष्पप्रियतया due to your love for flowers, मम my, शोकविवर्धिनी augmenting sorrow, अशोकस्य Ashoka's, शाखाभिः by branches, ते your, शरीरम् body, आवृणोषि  you are covering.

#### Explanation

O my beloved  as you love flowers you are hiding your body behind the branches of the Ashoke tree that enhances my sorrow (paradoxically).

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

कदलीस्कन्धसदृशौ कदल्या संवृतावुभौ। ऊरू पश्यामि ते देवि नासि शक्ता निगूहितुम्।।3.62.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

देवि O Devi, कदलीस्कन्धसदृशौ appearing like banana trunk, कदल्या with banana plant, संवृतौ are covered, ते ऊरू both your thighs, पश्यामि I see, निगूहितुम् to hide, शक्ता possible, नासि not.

#### Explanation

O Devi I can see both your thighs which resemble banana trunk hidden behind a banana plant. It is not possible for you to hide them from me.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

कर्णिकारवनं भद्रे हसन्ती देवि सेवसे। अलं ते परिहासेन मम बाधावहेन वै।।3.62.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

देवि O lady, भद्रे O gentle one, हसन्ती  laughing, कर्णिकारवनम्  Karnikara garden, सेवसे wandering, मम myself, बाधावहेन causing pain, ते परिहासेन by your fun, अलम् enough of it.

#### Explanation

O gentle one  you are wandering in the karnikara garden, laughing and making fun. Enough of this , that causes my pain.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

परिहासेन किं सीते परिश्रान्तस्य मे प्रिये। अयं स परिहासोऽपि साधु देवि न रोचते।।3.62.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

प्रिये O beloved, सीते Sita, परिश्रान्तस्य when I am exhausted, मे my, परिहासेन by joking, किम् why, अयम् this, सः that, परिहासोऽपि fun also, साधु well, न रोचते not liked.

#### Explanation

O my dear Sita, why are you joking when I am exhausted? Such fun cannot be relished  now.

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

विशेषेणाश्रमस्थाने हासोऽयं न प्रशस्यते। अवगच्छामि ते शीलं परिहासप्रियं प्रिये।।3.62.7।। आगच्छ त्वं विशालाक्षि शून्योऽयमुटजस्तव।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

विशेषेण especially, आश्रमस्थाने in a place like hermitage, अयम् this, हासः jest, न प्रशस्यते not appreciated, प्रिये O dear, ते you, परिहासप्रियम् lover of fun, शीलम् nature, अवगच्छामि I am aware, विशालाक्षि largeeyed lady, त्वम् you, आगच्छ come here, तव yours, अयम् this, उटजः cottage, शून्यः empty.

#### Explanation

O darling  joking specially in a hermitage  is not appreciated. I know your nature.You love fun. O largeeyed Sita, come here. This cottage of yours is empty.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

सुव्यक्तं राक्षसैस्सीता भक्षिता वा हृतापि वा।।3.62.8।। न हि सा विलपन्तं मामुपसम्प्रैति लक्ष्मण।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लक्ष्मण Lakshmana, सीता Sita, सुव्यक्तम् surely, राक्षसैः by demons, भक्षिता वा devoured or, हृतापि वा or abducted, सा that, विलपन्तम् while I cry like this, माम् me, न उपसम्प्रैति हि not coming to me.

#### Explanation

O Lakshmana surely Sita is either devoured by demons or abducted. When I am weeping like this she is not coming to me.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

एतानि मृगयूथानि साश्रुनेत्राणि लक्ष्मण।।3.62.9।। शंसन्तीव हि वैदेहीं भक्षितां रजनीचरैः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लक्ष्मण O Lakshmana, साश्रुनेत्राणि eyes filled with tears, एतानि these, मृगयूथानि herds of deer, वैदेहीम् Vaidehi, रजनीचरैः night walkers, भक्षिताम् eaten away, शंसन्ति इव as if they are telling me.

#### Explanation

O Lakshmana, these herds of deer with their eyes filled with tears are as if telling me that she is eaten away by the demons.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

हा ममार्ये क्व यातासि हा साध्वि वरवर्णिनि।।3.62.10।। हा सकामा त्वया देवी कैकेयी सा भविष्यति।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

हा Alas, मम my, आर्ये noble lady, क्व where, याता असि have you gone?, हा साध्वि Ha chaste lady, वरवर्णिनि a woman of excellent complexion, हा Ha, त्वया by you, देवी the queen, सा that, कैकेयी Kaikeyi, अद्य now, सकामा desire fulfilled, भविष्यति will become.

#### Explanation

Alas, my noble lady, where have you gone? O chaste lady of excellent complexion, that queen Kaikeyi's desire will be fulfilled now.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

सीतया सह निर्यातो विना सीतामुपागतः।।3.62.11।। कथं नाम प्रवेक्ष्यामि शून्यमन्तःपुरं पुनः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सीतया सह along with Sita, निर्यातः came out, विना सीताम् without Sita, उपागतः coming back, शून्यम् empty, अन्तःपुरम्  linner apartment (of queens), पुनः again, कथं नाम how can I, प्रवेक्ष्यामि enter?

#### Explanation

I left home with Sita. How can I reenter the inner apartment without her ?

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

निर्वीर्य इति लोको मां निर्दयश्चेति वक्ष्यति।।3.62.12।। कातरत्वं प्रकाशं हि सीतापनयनेन मे।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लोकः the world, माम् me, निर्वीर्यः without valour, इति so also, निर्दयश्चेति a man who lack compassion, वक्ष्यति will say, सीतापनयनेन by Sita's abduction, मे my, कातरत्वम् timid nature, प्रकाशं हि will manifest.

#### Explanation

By Sita's abduction it will be  clear to the world that I lack manliness and compassion. My cowardly nature will clearly manifest.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

निवृत्तवनवासश्च जनकं मिथिलाधिपम्।।3.62.13।। कुशलं परिपृच्छन्तं कथं शक्ष्ये निरीक्षितुम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

निवृत्तवनवासश्च after completing the period of exile, कुशलम् welfare, परिपृच्छन्तम् as he enquires, मिथिलाधिपम् to lord of Mithila, जनकम् Janaka, निरीक्षितुम् to face, कथम् how do I, शक्ष्ये will be able.

#### Explanation

After completing the period of my exile when Janaka , king of Mithila, enquires of her welfare how can I face him?

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

विदेहराजो नूनं मां दृष्ट्वा विरहितं तया।।3.62.14।। सुतास्नेहेन सन्तप्तो मोहस्य वशमेष्यति।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

विदेहराजः king of Videha, तया विरहितम् without her, माम् me, दृष्ट्वा seeing, सुतास्नेहेन out of love for his daughter, सन्तप्तः distressed, मोहस्य delusion's, वशम् grip, एष्यति he will be, नूनम् surely.

#### Explanation

If the king of Videha sees me without her, he will surely be distressed and will fall into a swoon for the love of his daughter.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

अथवा न गमिष्यामि पुरीं भरतपालिताम्।।3.62.15।। स्वर्गोऽपि सीतया हीनश्शून्य एव मतो मम।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अथवा or else, भरतपालिताम् ruled by Bharata, पुरीम् city, न गमिष्यामि not go, सीतया by Sita,मम for me, हीनः empty, स्वर्गोऽपि even heaven, शून्य empty, एव too, मतः to me.

#### Explanation

Or else, I will not go to the city ruled by Bharata. Without Sita even heaven will be empty for me.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

मामिहोत्सृज्य हि वने गच्छायोध्यां पुरीं शुभाम्।।3.62.16।। न त्वहं तां विना सीतां जीवेयं हि कथञ्चन।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

माम् to me, इह here, वने in the woods, उत्सृज्य deserting, शुभाम् auspicious, अयोध्यां पुरीम् to the city of Ayodhya, गच्छ you may go, अहं तु I also , तां सीतां विना without Sita, कथञ्चन indeed, न जीवेयं हि will not live.

#### Explanation

Go to the auspicious city of Ayodhya, leaving me here in the woods. I can never live without Sita.

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

गाढमाश्लिष्य भरतो वाच्यो मद्वचनात्त्वया।।3.62.17।। अनुज्ञातोऽसि रामेण पालयेति वसुन्धराम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

भरतः Bharata, गाढम् tightly, आश्लिष्य embracing, मद्वचनात् by my words, त्वया by you, वाच्यः  should be told, वसुन्धराम् earth, पालयेति may rule, रामेण by Rama's, अनुज्ञातः ordered, असि you are.

#### Explanation

While embracing Bharata tightly, repeat my words, 'You are permitted by Rama to rule the earth.'

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

अम्बा च मम कैकेयी सुमित्रा च त्वया विभो।।3.62.18।। कौसल्या च यथान्यायमभिवाद्या ममाऽज्ञया। रक्षणीया प्रयत्नेन भवता सूक्तकारिणा।।3.62.19।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

विभो O Lord, मम my, अम्बा mother, कैकेयी Kaikeyi, सुमित्रा च and Sumitra, कौसल्या च Kausalya also, त्वया by you, मम my, आज्ञया by orders, यथान्यायम् duly, अभिवाद्याः offer obeisance, (सु)उक्तकारिणा who does as instructed, भवता by you, प्रयत्नेन by effort, रक्षणीया: should be protected.

#### Explanation

My mother Kaikeyi, Sumitra and Kausalya  should also be offered due obeisance. You should protect them with great effort, carrying out my instructions.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

अम्बा च मम कैकेयी सुमित्रा च त्वया विभो।।3.62.18।। कौसल्या च यथान्यायमभिवाद्या ममाऽज्ञया। रक्षणीया प्रयत्नेन भवता सूक्तकारिणा।।3.62.19।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

विभो O Lord, मम my, अम्बा mother, कैकेयी Kaikeyi, सुमित्रा च and Sumitra, कौसल्या च Kausalya also, त्वया by you, मम my, आज्ञया by orders, यथान्यायम् duly, अभिवाद्याः offer obeisance, (सु)उक्तकारिणा who does as instructed, भवता by you, प्रयत्नेन by effort, रक्षणीया: should be protected.

#### Explanation

My mother Kaikeyi, Sumitra and Kausalya  should also be offered due obeisance. You should protect them with great effort, carrying out my instructions.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

सीतायाश्च विनाशोऽयं मम चामित्रकर्शन। विस्तरेण जनन्या मे विनिवेद्यस्त्वया भवेत्।।3.62.20।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अमित्रकर्शन  subduer of enemy, सीतायाश्च Sita's, मम च and of me, अयम् this, विनाशः loss, त्वया by you, मे my, जनन्याः mother, विस्तरेण in detail, विनिवेद्यः make her know, भवेत् you will.

#### Explanation

O Lakshmana, subduer of the enemy this loss of Sita and me should be explained in detail to my mother.

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

इति विलपति राघवे सुदीने वनमुपगम्य तया विना सुकेश्या। भयविकलमुखस्तु लक्ष्मणोऽपि व्यथितमना भृशमातुरो बभूव।।3.62.21।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सुदीने very piteous, राघवे when Rama, वनम् woods, उपगम्य after reaching, सुकेश्या by that lady of beautiful hair, तया विना without her, इति thus, विलपति was wailing, लक्ष्मणोऽपि and Lakshmana too, भयविकलमुखः face perturbed by fear, व्यथितमनाः distressed mentally, भृशम् exceedingly, आतुरः agitated, बभूव became.

#### Explanation

When Rama, devoid of  Sita, the lady with beautiful hair, wailed piteously after reaching the forest, Lakshmana too, his face pale out of fear and mind dejected, felt extremely agitated. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये अरण्यकाण्डे द्विषष्टितमस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the sixtysecond sarga of Aranyakanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 63

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

स राजपुत्रः प्रियया विहीनः शोकेन मोहेन च पीड्यमानः। विषादयन्भ्रातरमार्तरूपो भूयो विषादं प्रविवेश तीव्रम्।।3.63.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

प्रियया by the beloved, विहीनः separated, सः राजपुत्रः prince,Rama, शोकेन  out of  sorrow, मोहेन च delusion, पीड्यमानः being afflicted, आर्तरूपः in distressed state, भ्रातरम् brother, विषादयन्  worrying , भूयः again, तीव्रम् intense, विषादम् grief, प्रविवेश sunk.

#### Explanation

Prince Rama, separated from his beloved, lost his senses out of sorrow. Seeing his brother in a distressed state, he sank again into intense grief.

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

स लक्ष्मणं शोकवशाभिपन्नं शोके निमग्नो विपुले तु रामः। उवाच वाक्यं व्यसनानुरूपमुष्णं विनिश्श्वस्य रुदंत्सशोकम्।।3.63.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

विपुले शोके in intense grief, निमग्नः plunged, सः that, रामः Rama, सशोकम् with sorrow, रुदन् crying, शोकवशाभिपन्नम्  who was caught in sorrow, लक्ष्मणम् Lakshmana, उष्णम् hot, विनिश्वस्य  sighing, व्यसनानुरूपम् in his grief, वाक्यम् these words, उवाच said.

#### Explanation

Plunged in deep grief, Rama heaved hot sighs and said these words to griefstricken Lakshmanaweeping:

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

न मद्विधो दुष्कृतकर्मकारी मन्ये द्वितीयोऽस्ति वसुन्धरायाम्। शोकेन शोको हि परम्पराया मा मेति भिन्दन्हृदयं मनश्च।।3.63.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वसुन्धरायाम् on earth, मद्विधः like me, दुष्कृतकर्मकारी who undertakes forbidden acts,  द्वितीयः second person, नास्ति not, मन्ये I think, शोकेन grief, शोकः grief, परम्परायाः continuously, हृदयम् heart, मनश्च mind also, भिन्दन् shattered, माम् my, एति हि is befalling.

#### Explanation

I think there is none on earth like me who has performed such forbidden acts.  One grief after the other is successively piercing my heart and my mind.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

पूर्वं मया नूनमभीप्सितानि पापानि कर्माण्यसकृत्कृतानि। तत्रायमद्यापतितो विपाको दुःखेन दुःखं यदहं विशामि।।3.63.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मया by me, पूर्वम् in the past, नूनम् certainly, अभीप्सितानि  dear to me, पापानि कर्माणि sinful deeds, असकृत् often, कृतानि done, तत्र there, अद्य now, विपाकः consequence of that, आपतितः has descended, यत् since, अहम् I, दुःखेन by sorrow, दुःखम् sorrow, विशामि I am entering.

#### Explanation

In the past I had certainly done some sinful deeds I often liked the consequences of which have descended on me now as I am experiencing one sorrow after another.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

राज्यप्रणाशस्स्वजनैर्वियोगः पितुर्विनाशो जननीवियोगः। सर्वाणि मे लक्ष्मण शोकवेगमापूरयन्ति प्रविचिन्तितानि।।3.63.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लक्ष्मण Lakshmana, राज्यप्रणाशः loss of kingdom, स्वजनैः kith and kin, वियोगः separation from, पितुः father, विनाशः death, जननीवियोगः separation from mother, सर्वाणि all, प्रविचिन्तितानि thinking over, मे I, शोकवेगम् fast increase sorrow, आपूरयन्ति greater measure.

#### Explanation

O Lakshmana, loss of kingdom, separation from kith and kin, death of father, separation from mother--all these thoughts augment my sorrow faster and in greater measure.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

सर्वं तु दुःखं मम लक्ष्मणेदं शान्तं शरीरे वनमेत्य शून्यम्। सीतावियोगात्पुनरप्युदीर्णं काष्ठैरिवाग्निस्सहसा प्रदीप्तः।।3.63.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लक्ष्मण Lakshmana, इदम् this, सर्वम् all, दुःखम् sorrow, शून्यम् solitary, वनम् woods, एत्य on coming, शरीरे in the body, शान्तम्  calmness, सीतावियोगात् with the separation from Sita, काष्ठैः  logs of wood, सहसा along with, प्रदीप्तः blazing flame, अग्निः fire, पुनरपि again rises, उदीर्णम् came up.

#### Explanation

O Lakshmana, all this sorrow calmed down when I came to this solitary forest. But with Sita's separation, it was rekindled just as fire blazes forth again with logs of wood.

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

सा नूनमार्या मम राक्षसेन बलाद्दृता खं समुपेत्य भीरुः। अपस्वरं सस्वरविप्रलापा भयेन विक्रन्दितवत्यभीक्ष्णम्।।3.63.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मम my, भीरुः timid, सा आर्या that princess, राक्षसेन by the demon, बलात् forcibly, हृता is carried away, खम् into the sky, समुपेत्य  taken, सस्वरविप्रलापा cried aloud, भयेन in fear, अभीक्ष्णम् repeatedly, अपस्वरम् in a broken voice, आक्रन्दितवती wept bitterly, नूनम् surely.

#### Explanation

The timid princess of mine forcibly carried away into the sky by a demon must have again and again cried aloud out of fear in a broken voice.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

तौ लोहितस्य प्रियदर्शनस्य सदोचितावुत्तमचन्दनस्य। वृत्तौ स्तनौ शोणितपङ्कदिग्धौ नूनं प्रियाया मम नाभिभातः।।3.63.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लोहितस्य of copper complexion, प्रियदर्शनस्य lovely to look at, उत्तमचन्दनस्य with the best of sandal paste, सदा always, उचितौ used, वृत्तौ two circular ones, मम प्रियायाः my beloved, तौ स्तनौ her breasts, नूनम् surely, शोणितपङ्कदिग्धौ drenched in blood and mud, नाभिभातः will not be pleasing.

#### Explanation

Her captivating copper coloured skin, smeared with the best of red sandal paste would not be surely pleasing now, drenched in blood and mud.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

तच्छलक्ष्णसुव्यक्तमृदुप्रलापं तस्या मुखं कुञ्चितकेशभारम्। रक्षोवशं नूनमुपागताया न भ्राजते राहुमुखे यथेन्दुः।।3.63.9।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रक्षोवशम् demons' hold, उपागतायाः coming under, तस्याः her, श्लक्ष्णसुव्यक्त very clear words, मृदुप्रलापम् sweet and gentle, कुञ्चितकेशभारम् beautiful curly hair, तत् मुखम् her face, राहुमुखे Rahu's mouth, इन्दुः Moon, यथा like that, न भ्राजते not shine, नूनम् certainly.

#### Explanation

Her face with her clear, sweet and gentle words and her curly hair falling will no more look bright coming under the grip of demons like the moon eclipsed by Rahu.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

तां हारपाशस्य सदोचिताया ग्रीवां प्रियाया मम सुव्रतायाः। रक्षांसि नूनं परिपीतवन्ति विभिद्य शून्ये रुधिराशनानि।।3.63.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सदा always, हारपाशस्य necklace, उचितायाः accustomed to, सुव्रतायाः of a lady of exemplary conduct, मम प्रियायाः of my beloved, तां ग्रीवाम् that neck, रुधिराशनानि blood thirsty beings, रक्षांसि by demons, शून्ये in desolate , विभिद्य breaking, नूनम् surely, परिपीतवन्ति drunk.

#### Explanation

The bloodthirsty demons must have drunk the blood of my faithful beloved in a lonely place breaking her neck that used to wear beautiful necklaces.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

मया विहीना विजने वने या रक्षोभिराहृत्य विकृष्यमाणा। नूनं निनादं कुररीव दीना सा मुक्तवत्यायतकान्तनेत्रा।।3.63.11।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

या that, विजने वने in the desolate forest, मया by me, विहीना separated, सा that, आयतकान्तनेत्रा beautiful largeeyed lady, रक्षोभिः by the demons, आहृत्य after abducting, विकृष्यमाणा dragging, दीना  helpless, कुररीव like a female osprey, नूनम् surely, निनादम् sound, मुक्तवती would have released.

#### Explanation

The lovely, largeeyed lady separated from me in the desolate forest, abducted and dragged by demons must have cried at that time desperately like a female osprey.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

अस्मिन्मयासार्धमुदारशीला शिलातले पूर्वमुपोपविष्टा। कान्तस्मिता लक्ष्मण जातहासा त्वामाह सीता बहुवाक्यजातम्।।3.63.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लक्ष्मण Lakshmana, उदारशीला good natured, कान्तस्मिता lovely smile, सीता Sita, अस्मिन् on this, शिलातले on this rock, मयासार्धम् along with me, पूर्वम् earlier, उपोपविष्टा sitting here, जातहासा laughing away, त्वाम् seeing you, बहुवाक्यजातम् many words, आह spoke.

#### Explanation

On this rock, O Lakshmana, my goodnatured lady with her lovely smile sat with me in the past and chatted with you.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

गोदावरीयं सरितां वरिष्ठा प्रिया प्रियाया मम नित्यकालम्। अप्यत्र गच्छेदिति चिन्तयामि नैकाकिनी याति हि सा कदाचित्।।3.63.13।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सरिताम् among rivers, वरिष्ठा foremost, इयं गोदावरी this Godavari, मम प्रियायाः of my beloved, नित्यकालम् at all times, प्रिया  dear, अत्र there, गच्छेदपि has she gone, इति thus, चिन्तयामि I think, सा that, एकाकिनी alone, कदाचित् any time, न याति हि will not go there.

#### Explanation

Would she have gone to Godavari, the foremost of the rivers so dear at all times to my beloved ? But I think she has not gone there alone at any time.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

पद्मानना पद्मविशालनेत्रा पद्मानि वानेतुमभिप्रयाता। तदप्ययुक्तं न हि सा कदाचिन्मया विना गच्छति पङ्कजानि।।3.63.14।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पद्मानना a lady with a lotuslike face, पद्मविशालनेत्रा  lotus petal eyed, पद्मानि वा आनेतुम् or to fetch lotuses, अभिप्रयाता वा she would have gone, तदपि that also, अयुक्तम् not possible, सा she, कदाचित् at any time, मया विना without me, पङ्कजानि lotuses, न गच्छति हि will not go there.

#### Explanation

The lady whose face was like the lotus, whose large eyes were like lotus petals could  have gone to fetch lotuses (but) that also is not possible as she has never gone there without me.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

कामं त्विदं पुष्पितवृक्षषण्डं नानाविधैः पक्षिगणैरुपेतम्। वनं प्रयाता नु तदप्ययुक्त मेकाकिनी सातिबिभेति भीरुः।।3.63.15।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कामम् surely, पुष्पितवृक्षषण्डम् this thicket of trees in bloom, नानाविधैः by many kinds, पक्षिगणैः flocks of birds, उपेतम् inhabited, इदम् this, वनम् forest, प्रयाता नु could she have gone?, तदपि that too, अयुक्तम् not possible, भीरुः she is timid, सा that Sita, एकाकिनी alone, अतिबिभेति is scared a lot.

#### Explanation

Could she have gone to the forest where there are many kinds of trees in full bloom inhabited by a variety of birds? That is also not possible as she is timid and too scared to go there alone.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

आदित्य भो लोककृताकृतज्ञ लोकस्य सत्यानृतकर्मसाक्षिन्। मम प्रिया सा क्व गता हृता वा शंसस्व मे शोकवशस्य नित्यम्।।3.63.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लोककृताकृतज्ञ knower of what is going on and not going on, लोकस्य in the world, सत्यानृतकर्मसाक्षिन् a witness to all true and false deeds of the people, आदित्यः Sun, मम my, प्रिया dear, सा Sita, क्व where, गता gone, हृता वा or taken away, नित्यम् always, शोकवशस्य  a griefstricken, मे to me, शंसस्व tell.

#### Explanation

O Sungod, you know all the happenings of the world. You are witness to all the deeds of the people, true and false. Tell me where my beloved Sita has gone or has been carried off. My grief never ceases.

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

लोकेषु सर्वेषु च नास्ति किञ्चिद्यत्ते न नित्यं विदितं भवेत्तत्। शंसस्व वायो कुलशालिनीं तां हृता मृता वा पथि वर्तते वा।।3.63.17।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वायो O wind, सर्वेषु all over, लोकेषु in all worlds, यत् since, नित्यम् always, ते to you, विदितम् known, न भवेत् not be, तत् that, किञ्चित् a little, नास्ति is not, कुलशालिनीम् woman of the high birth, ताम् her, शंसस्व you may tell me, हृता taken away, मृता वा or dead, पथि on the way, वर्तते वा or where she is moving.

#### Explanation

O Windgod, there is nothing that you know not in all the worlds. Do tell me if  that lady of a noble race has been abducted or dead or is on the way.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

इतीव तं शोकविधेयदेहं रामं विसंज्ञं विलपन्तमेवम्। उवाच सौमित्रिरदीनसत्त्वो न्याये स्थितः कालयुतं च वाक्यम्।।3.63.18।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

इतीव like this, शोकविधेयदेहम् one whose body was gripped by grief, विसंज्ञम्  in a state of swoon, एवम् in that way, विलपन्तम् weeping, तं रामम् that Rama, अदीनसत्त्वः a man of fortitude, न्याये स्थितः rational, सौमित्रिः Lakshmana, कालयुतम् appropriate to time, वाक्यम् these words, उवाच spoke.

#### Explanation

While Rama was thus in tears, in a state of sorrow and swoon, Lakshmana who was rational and courageous spoke to him words appropriate to the time :

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

शोकं विमुञ्चार्य धृतिं भजस्व सोत्साहता चास्तु विमार्गणेऽस्याः। उत्साहवन्तो हि नरा न लोके सीदन्ति कर्मस्वतिदुष्करेषु।।3.63.19।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

आर्याः O noble one, शोकम् grief, विमुञ्च give up, धृतिम् take courage, भजस्व you may adopt, अस्याः of Sita,  विमार्गणे in searching, सोत्साहता enthusiasm, अस्तु  be cultivated, लोके in the world, उत्साहवन्तः enthusiastic people, नराः men, अतिदुष्करेषु in most difficult, कर्मसु in tasks also, न सीदन्ति हि not get despondent.

#### Explanation

O noble prince, give up grief. Take courage. Show enthusiasm to search and find Sita. Enthusiastic people will not get despondent in carrying out the  most difficult tasks.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

इतीव सौमित्रिमुदग्रपौरुषं ब्रुवन्तमार्तो रघुवंशवर्धनः। न चिन्तयामास धृतिं विमुक्तवान्पुनश्च दुःखं महदभ्युपागमत्।।3.63.20।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

आर्तः dejected , रघुवंशवर्धनः one who carries on the progeny of Raghu dynasty, इतीव in this manner, ब्रुवन्तम् saying, उदग्रपौरुषम् man of great prowess, सौमित्रिम् to Lakshmana, न चिन्तयामास not count, धृतिम् courage, विमुक्तवान् he gave up, पुनश्च again, महत् deep, दुःखम् sorrow, अभ्युपागमत् was overtaken.

#### Explanation

Rama who carries on the (glorious) progeny of the Raghu dynasty, did not (however) consider seriously the words of Lakshmana, a man of great prowess. Forsaking his courage he was once again overtaken by deep sorrow. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये अरण्यकाण्डे त्रिषष्टितमस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the sixtythird sarga of Aranyakanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 64

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

स दीनो दीनया वाचा लक्ष्मणं वाक्यमब्रवीत्। शीघ्रं लक्ष्मण जानीहि गत्वा गोदावरीं नदीम्।।3.64.1।। अपि गोदावरीं सीता पद्मान्यानयितुं गता।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः that Rama, दीनः piteous, दीनया pitiable, वाचा words, लक्ष्मणम् to Lakshmana, वाक्यम् these words, अब्रवीत् said, लक्ष्मण Lakshmana, शीघ्रम् swiftly, गोदावरीं नदीम् Godavari river, गत्वा going, सीता Sita, पद्मानि lotuses, आनयितुम् to get, गोदावरीम् Godavari, अपि गता has  gone, जानीहि find out.

#### Explanation

Rama in dejection said these pitiable words to Lakshmana,O Lakashmana, go swiftly to the river Godavari and find out if Sita has gone there to get lotuses.

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

एवमुक्तस्तु रामेण लक्ष्मणः परवीरहा।।3.64.2।। नदीं गोदावरीं रम्यां जगाम लघुविक्रमः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रामेण by Rama, एवम् in that way, उक्तः having been told, परवीरहा destroyer of enemy warriors, लक्ष्मणः Lakshmana, लघुविक्रमः with quick steps, रम्याम् delightful, गोदावरीं नदीम् river Godavari, जगाम went.

#### Explanation

Lakshmana, destroyer of enemywarriors, went to the beautiful river Godavari with quick steps following the instruction of Rama.

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

तां लक्ष्मणस्तीर्थवती विचित्वा राममब्रवीत्।।3.64.3।। नैनां पश्यामि तीर्थेषु क्रोशतो न शृणोति मे।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लक्ष्मणः Lakshmana, तीर्थवतीम् having landings, ताम् her, विचित्वा searching, रामम् to Rama, अब्रवीत् said, एनाम् her, तीर्थेषु at the landings, न पश्यामि do not see, मे when I, क्रोशतः called out, न शृणोति no one hears.

#### Explanation

Searching for Sita at the landings of river Godavari, Lakshmana said to Rama, I am unable to find her at these landings.No one responds to my call.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

कं नु वा देशमापन्ना वैदेही क्लेशनाशिनी।।3.64.4।। न ह्यहं वेद तं देशं यत्र सा जनकात्मजा।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

क्लेशनाशिनी destroyer of sorrow, वैदेही Vaidehi, कं वा where else, देशम् place, आपन्ना नु had landed, सा that, जनकात्मजा daughter of Janaka, यत्र where, तं देशम् that place, अहम् I, न वेद हि do not know.

#### Explanation

Where could Vaidehi, destroyer of sorrow, go? I wonder. I am unable to trace the spot where the daughter of Janaka could be found.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

लक्ष्मणस्य वचश्श्रुत्वा दीनस्सन्तापमोहितः।।3.64.5।। रामस्समभिचक्राम स्वयं गोदावरीं नदीम्। स तामुपस्थितो रामः क्व सीतेत्येवमब्रवीत्।।3.64.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रामः Rama, लक्ष्मणस्य Lakshmana's, वचः words, श्रुत्वा on hearing, दीनः a dejected man, सन्तापमोहितः overwhelmed with grief, स्वयम् himself, गादावरीं नदीम् to river Godavari, समभिचक्राम went, सः रामः that Rama, ताम् the river, उपस्थितः approached, सीते Sita, क्व where, इत्येवम् in this manner, अब्रवीत् said.

#### Explanation

Overwhelmed with grief on hearing Lakshmana's words, Rama himself went to river Godavari, and shouted, where are you, Sita?

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

लक्ष्मणस्य वचश्श्रुत्वा दीनस्सन्तापमोहितः।।3.64.5।। रामस्समभिचक्राम स्वयं गोदावरीं नदीम्। स तामुपस्थितो रामः क्व सीतेत्येवमब्रवीत्।।3.64.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रामः Rama, लक्ष्मणस्य Lakshmana's, वचः words, श्रुत्वा on hearing, दीनः a dejected man, सन्तापमोहितः overwhelmed with grief, स्वयम् himself, गादावरीं नदीम् to river Godavari, समभिचक्राम went, सः रामः that Rama, ताम् the river, उपस्थितः approached, सीते Sita, क्व where, इत्येवम् in this manner, अब्रवीत् said.

#### Explanation

Overwhelmed with grief on hearing Lakshmana's words, Rama himself went to river Godavari, and shouted, where are you, Sita?

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

भूतानि राक्षसेन्द्रेण वधार्हेण हृतामिति। न तां शशंसू रामाय तथा गोदावरी नदि।।3.64.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वधार्हेण by one who deserved to be killed, राक्षसेन्द्रेण by the lord of the demons, हृताम् kidnapped, ताम् her, इति like that, भूतानि beings, तथा similarly, गोदावरी नदी river Godavari, रामाय to Rama, न शशंसुः did not reply.

#### Explanation

Like the elements of Nature, river Godavari did not disclose Rama that Sita had been abducted by the king of demons who deserved death.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

ततः प्रचोदिता भूतैश्शंसास्मैतां प्रियामिति। न तु साभ्यवदतसितां पृष्टा रामेण शोचता।।3.64.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः thereafter, शोचता  brooding, रामेण by Rama, पृष्टा asked, सा that, प्रियाम् about his beloved, अस्मै to him, शंस you may tell, इति this, भूतैः beings, प्रचोदिता prompted, सीताम् Sita, नाभ्यवदत् no one has said.

#### Explanation

River Godavari (although) prompted by creatures (of Nature) to tell him about his beloved did not respond to brooding Rama.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

रावणस्य तु तद्रूपं कर्माणि च दुरात्मनः। ध्यात्वा भयात्तु वैदेहीं सा नदी न शशंस ताम्।।3.64.9।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सा नदी that river, दुरात्मनः of the evilminded, रावणस्य Ravana's, तत् that, रूपम् figure, कर्माणि च actions too, ध्यात्वा thinking over, भयात् out of fear, तां वैदेहीम् about Vaidehi, न शशंस did not speak.

#### Explanation

Remembering the evilminded Ravana's appearance and action, the river revealed nothing about Vaidehi out of fear.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

निराशस्तु तया नद्या सीताया दर्शने कृतः। उवाच रामस्सौमित्रिं सीतादर्शनकर्शितः।।3.64.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सीतादर्शनकर्शितः emaciated on account of not finding Sita, रामः Rama, तया नद्या by that river not replying, सीतायाः of Sita, दर्शने see, निराशः disappointed, कृतः was made, सौमित्रिम् to Lakshmana, उवाच said.

#### Explanation

Disappointed by the river and emaciated by not being able to see Sita, Rama said to Lakshmana :

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

एषा गोदावरी सौम्य किंचिन्न प्रति भाषते। किं नु लक्ष्मण वक्ष्यामि समेत्य जनकं वचः।।3.64.11।। मातरं चैव वैदेह्या विना तामहमप्रियम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सौम्य O handsome, एषा गोदावरी this Godavari, किञ्चित् even a little, न प्रतिभाषते is not answering, लक्ष्मण Lakshmana, जानकीं Janaki, विना without, अहम् I am, जनकम् Janaka, समेत्य after meeting him, मातरं चैव to mother also, अप्रियम् unhappy, किं नु what can, वचनम् words, वक्ष्यामि tell.

#### Explanation

O handsome Lakshmana this Godavari does not at all respond. How can I go to Janaka and my mother without Sita and break this unhappy news?

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

या मे राज्यविहीनस्य वने वन्येन जीवतः।।3.64.12।। सर्वं व्यपनयेच्छोकं वैदेही क्व नु सा गता।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राज्यविहीनस्य  who lost his kingdom, वने in the forest, वन्येन food available in the forest, जीवतः lived, मे me, सर्वम् all, शोकम् grief, या वैदेही that Vaidehi, व्यपनयेत्  remove sorrow, सा she, क्व where, गता नु has she gone?

#### Explanation

Where could have gone that Vaidehi, who  removed all my sorrows and sufferings when I lost my kingdom and survived on food available in the forest?

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

ज्ञातिपक्षविहीनस्य राजपुत्रीमपश्यतः।।3.64.13।। मन्ये दीर्घा भविष्यन्ति रात्रयो मम जाग्रतः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ज्ञातिपक्षविहीनस्य away from relatives, राजपुत्रीम् princess, अपश्यतः not seeing, जाग्रतः keeping awake, मम for me, रात्रयः nights, दीर्घाः long, भविष्यन्ति appear, मन्ये I think.

#### Explanation

Away from relatives and away from the princess, I keep awake during nights which seem too long.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

गोदावरीं जनस्थानमिमं प्रस्रवणं गिरिम्।।3.64.14।। सर्वाण्यनुगमिष्यामि यदि सीता हि दृश्यते।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

गोदावरीम् Godavari, जनस्थानम् Janasthanam, इमम् this, प्रस्रवणं Prasravana, गिरिम् mountain, सर्वाणि all, अनुगमिष्यामि will follow up, सीता Sita, यदि हि दृश्यते if Sita can be found.

#### Explanation

I will roam about the river Godavari, Janasthana and this mount Prasravana till I find Sita.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

एते मृगा महावीर्य मामीक्षन्ते मुहुर्मुहुः।।3.64.15।। वक्तुकामा इव हि मे इङ्गितान्युपलक्षये।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महावीर्य O mighty one, एते these, मृगाः deer, मुहुर्मुहुः again and again, माम् me,ईक्षन्ते looking, इङ्गितानि their signs, उपलक्षये indicating, मे to me, वक्तुकामा इव as if they wish to tell me.

#### Explanation

O mighty Lakshmana these deer are looking at me again and again indicating that they wish to tell me something.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

तांस्तु दृष्ट्वा नरव्याघ्रो राघवः प्रत्युवाच ह।।3.64.16।। क्व सीतेति निरीक्षन्वै बाष्पसंरुद्धया दृशा।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

नरव्याघ्रः tiger among men, राघवः Rama, तान् them, दृष्ट्वा seeing, बाष्पसंरुद्धया blurred by tears, दृशा with their sight, निरीक्षन् beholding, सीता Sita, क्व where, इति like this, प्रत्युवाच ह replied.

#### Explanation

With his vision blurred by tears, Rama, the best among men, looked at them and asked them 'Where is Sita'?

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

एवमुक्ता नरेन्द्रेण ते मृगास्सहसोत्थिताः।।3.64.17।। दक्षिणाभिमुखास्सर्वे दर्शयन्तो नभस्थलम्। मैथिली ह्रियमाणा सा दिशं यामन्वपद्यत।।3.64.18।। तेन मार्गेण धावन्तो निरीक्षन्ते नराधिपम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

नरेन्द्रेण by the prince, एवम् in that way, उक्ताः said, मृगाः deer, सहसा suddenly, उत्थिताः got, सर्वे all, दक्षिणाभिमुखाः facing south, नभस्थलम् to the region of the sky, दर्शयन्तः pointing, ह्रियमाणा taken away, मैथिली Maithili, याम् such, दिशम् direction, अन्वपद्यत followed, तेन मार्गेण by that path, धावन्तः while running, नराधिपम् to the lord of men, निरीक्षन्ते were looking at.

#### Explanation

Thus asked by the prince, all the deer got up suddenly, and pointed towards the southern sky, the direction in which the princess from Mithila was carried off. Looking at the Lord of men (Rama), they started running in that direction.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

एवमुक्ता नरेन्द्रेण ते मृगास्सहसोत्थिताः।।3.64.17।। दक्षिणाभिमुखास्सर्वे दर्शयन्तो नभस्थलम्। मैथिली ह्रियमाणा सा दिशं यामन्वपद्यत।।3.64.18।। तेन मार्गेण धावन्तो निरीक्षन्ते नराधिपम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

नरेन्द्रेण by the prince, एवम् in that way, उक्ताः said, मृगाः deer, सहसा suddenly, उत्थिताः got, सर्वे all, दक्षिणाभिमुखाः facing south, नभस्थलम् to the region of the sky, दर्शयन्तः pointing, ह्रियमाणा taken away, मैथिली Maithili, याम् such, दिशम् direction, अन्वपद्यत followed, तेन मार्गेण by that path, धावन्तः while running, नराधिपम् to the lord of men, निरीक्षन्ते were looking at.

#### Explanation

Thus asked by the prince, all the deer got up suddenly, and pointed towards the southern sky, the direction in which the princess from Mithila was carried off. Looking at the Lord of men (Rama), they started running in that direction.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

येन मार्गं च भूमिं च निरीक्षन्ते स्म ते मृगाः।।3.64.19।। पुनश्च मार्गमिच्छन्ति लक्ष्मणेनोपलक्षिताः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ते मृगाः those deer, येन by which, मार्गं च direction, भूमिं च and the land, निरीक्षन्ते स्म looking at, पुनश्च again, मार्गं that path, इच्छन्ति intend to go, लक्ष्मणेन even Lakshmana, उपलक्षिताः niticed.

#### Explanation

Even Lakshmana marked that the deer were showing and looking at the path lying in that direction.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

तेषां वचनसर्वस्वं लक्षयामास चेङ्गितम्।।3.64.20।। उवाच लक्ष्मणो ज्येष्ठं धीमान्भ्रातरमार्तवत्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

धीमान् wise, लक्ष्मणः Lakshmana, तेषाम् their, वचनसर्वस्वम् meaning of their words fully, इङ्गितम् indication, लक्षयामास he observed, आर्तवत्  distressed,  ज्येष्ठं भ्रातरम् elder brother, उवाच said.

#### Explanation

Observing intelligently their indications and understanding the meaning of their message, Lakshmana said to his distressed elder brother:

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

क्व सितेति त्वया पृष्टा यथेमे सहसोत्थिताः।।3.64.21।। दर्शयन्ति क्षितिं चैव दक्षिणां च दिशं मृगाः। साधु गच्छावहे देव दिशमेतां हि नैऋतिम्।।3.64.22।। यदि स्यादागमः कश्चिदार्या वा साऽथ लक्ष्यते।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

देव lord, इमे these, मृगाः deer, क्व where, (गता gone), इति thus, त्वया by you, पृष्टाः enquired, सहसा suddenly, उत्थिताः got up, क्षितिं चैव land as well as, दक्षिणां दिशम् southern side, यथा thus, दर्शयन्ति  are showing, एताम् this, नैऋताम् दिशम् south western direction, साधु well, गच्छावहे both will go, कश्चित् some, आगमः source, यदि स्यात् may be, अथ at that, सा that, आर्या वा noble lady, लक्ष्यते may be found.

#### Explanation

O lord these deer are indicating the land on the southern side.Therefore it will be proper for both of us to proceed in the southwestern direction. Maybe we will get some clue or find that noble Sita.

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

क्व सितेति त्वया पृष्टा यथेमे सहसोत्थिताः।।3.64.21।। दर्शयन्ति क्षितिं चैव दक्षिणां च दिशं मृगाः। साधु गच्छावहे देव दिशमेतां हि नैऋतिम्।।3.64.22।। यदि स्यादागमः कश्चिदार्या वा साऽथ लक्ष्यते।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

देव lord, इमे these, मृगाः deer, क्व where, (गता gone), इति thus, त्वया by you, पृष्टाः enquired, सहसा suddenly, उत्थिताः got up, क्षितिं चैव land as well as, दक्षिणां दिशम् southern side, यथा thus, दर्शयन्ति  are showing, एताम् this, नैऋताम् दिशम् south western direction, साधु well, गच्छावहे both will go, कश्चित् some, आगमः source, यदि स्यात् may be, अथ at that, सा that, आर्या वा noble lady, लक्ष्यते may be found.

#### Explanation

O lord these deer are indicating the land on the southern side.Therefore it will be proper for both of us to proceed in the southwestern direction. Maybe we will get some clue or find that noble Sita.

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

बाढमित्येव काकुत्स्थः प्रस्थितो दक्षिणां दिशम्।।3.64.23।। लक्ष्मणानुगतश्शीमन्वीक्षमाणो वसुन्धराम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

श्रीमान् Handsome one, काकुत्स्थः Rama, बाढमित्येव saying 'yes', लक्ष्मणानुगतः followed by Lakshmana, वसुन्धराम् land, वीक्षमाणः while looking at, दक्षिणां दिशम् southwards, प्रस्थितः he started.

#### Explanation

Handsome Rama said, 'yes'and started in southern direction, carefully looking at the ground, followed by Lakshmana.

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

एवं सम्भाषमाणौ तावन्योन्यं भ्रातरावुभौ।।3.64.24।। वसुन्धरायां पतितं पुष्पमार्गमपश्यताम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

एवम् in that way, अन्योन्यम् with each other, सम्भाषमाणौ conversing, (तौ) उभौ both, भ्रातरौ brothers, वसुन्धरायाम् on the path, पतितम् पुष्पमार्गम् path scattered with flowers, अपश्यताम् saw.

#### Explanation

Talking with each other, the two brothers took the path on earth strewn with flowers.

---

### Shloka 25

#### Shloka Text

तां पुष्पवृष्टिं पतितां दृष्ट्वा रामो महीतले।।3.64.25।। उवाच लक्ष्मणं वीरो दुःखितो दुःखितं वचः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वीरः Valiant, रामः Rama, महीतले on the ground, पतिताम् fallen, तां पुष्पवृष्टिम् that shower of flowers, दृष्ट्वा seeing, दुःखितः sadly, दुःखितम् sorrowful, लक्ष्मणम् to Lakshmana, वचः these words, उवाच spoke.

#### Explanation

Seeing showers of flowers fallen on the ground, valiant Rama sadly said these words to sorrowful Lakshmana:

---

### Shloka 26

#### Shloka Text

अभिजानामि पुष्पाणि तानीमानीह लक्ष्मण।।3.64.26।। पिनद्धानि हि वैदेह्या मया दत्तानि कानने।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लक्ष्मण O Lakshmana, इह here, तानि those, इमानि the very same ones, पुष्पाणि flowers, अभिजानामि I recognise them, इह here, कानने in the forest, मया by me, दत्तानि given, वैदेह्या by Vaidehi, पिनद्धानि worn, हि indeed.

#### Explanation

O Lakshmana, here are the same flowers I had given Vaidehi to wear in the forest. I can recognize them.

---

### Shloka 27

#### Shloka Text

मन्ये सूर्यश्च वायुश्च मेदिनी च यशस्विनी।।3.64.27।। अभिरक्षन्ति पुष्पाणि प्रकुर्वन्तो मम प्रियम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सूर्यश्च Sun too, वायुश्च wind also, यशस्विनी famed, मेदिनी च earth also, मम my, प्रियम् pleasure, प्रकुर्वन्तः while rendering, पुष्पाणि flowers, अभिरक्षन्ति preserving, मन्ये I think.

#### Explanation

I think the Sun, the wind  and the famed earth have done me a favour by preserving these flowers.

---

### Shloka 28

#### Shloka Text

एवमुक्त्वा महाबाहुं लक्ष्मणं पुरुषर्षभः।।3.64.28।। उवाच रामो धर्मात्मा गिरिं प्रस्रवणाकुलम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पुरुषर्षभः a bull among men, धर्मात्मा righteous, रामः Rama, महाबाहुम् longarmed, लक्ष्मणम् Lakshmana, एवम् in that way, उक्त्वा having said, प्रस्रवणाकुलम् full of streams, गिरिम् mountain, उवाच said.

#### Explanation

Rigteous Rama, the best among men, having said these words to the longarmed Lakshmana, asked mount Prasravana, full of streams:

---

### Shloka 29

#### Shloka Text

कच्चित् क्षितिभृतां नाथ दृष्टा सर्वाङ्गसुन्दरी।।3.64.29।। रामा रम्ये वनोद्देशे मया विरहिता त्वया।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

क्षितिभृतां नाथ king of the mountains, रम्ये beautiful, वनोद्धेशे in the woods, मया by me, विरहिता separated from me, सर्वाङ्गसुन्दरी charming in all herlimbs, रामा woman, त्वया you, कच्चित्  whether, दृष्टा is seen?

#### Explanation

O king of the mountains have you seen a lady with beautiful limbs separated from me in this forest tract ?

---

### Shloka 30

#### Shloka Text

क्रुद्धोऽब्रवीद्गिरिं तत्र सिंहः क्षुद्रमृगं यथा।।3.64.30।। तां हेमवर्णां हेमाभां सीतां दर्शय पर्वत। यावत्सानूनि सर्वाणि न ते विध्वंसयाम्यहम्।।3.64.31।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तत्र there, सिंहः lion, क्षुद्रमृगं petty animal, गिरिम् mountain, क्रुद्धः angry, अब्रवीत् said, पर्वत mountain, अहम् I, ते your, सर्वाणि all, सानूनि slopes, यावत् before, न विध्वंसयामि I do not destroy, हेमवर्णाम् golden complexioned, हेमाभाम् with lustre like gold, सीताम् Sita, दर्शय show.

#### Explanation

Like a lion aiming at a petty animal, angry Rama  said, O Mountain before I destroy  all your slopes show me that goldenhued Sita with lustre like gold.

---

### Shloka 31

#### Shloka Text

क्रुद्धोऽब्रवीद्गिरिं तत्र सिंहः क्षुद्रमृगं यथा।।3.64.30।। तां हेमवर्णां हेमाभां सीतां दर्शय पर्वत। यावत्सानूनि सर्वाणि न ते विध्वंसयाम्यहम्।।3.64.31।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तत्र there, सिंहः lion, क्षुद्रमृगं petty animal, गिरिम् mountain, क्रुद्धः angry, अब्रवीत् said, पर्वत mountain, अहम् I, ते your, सर्वाणि all, सानूनि slopes, यावत् before, न विध्वंसयामि I do not destroy, हेमवर्णाम् golden complexioned, हेमाभाम् with lustre like gold, सीताम् Sita, दर्शय show.

#### Explanation

Like a lion aiming at a petty animal, angry Rama  said, O Mountain before I destroy  all your slopes show me that goldenhued Sita with lustre like gold.

---

### Shloka 32

#### Shloka Text

एवमुक्तस्तु रामेण पर्वतो मैथिलीं प्रति। शंसन्निव ततस्सीतां नादर्शयत राघवे।।3.64.32।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रामेण by Rama, मैथिलीं Maithili, प्रति towards, एवम् in that way, उक्तः having said, पर्वतः mountain, ततः thereafter, सीताम् Sita, शंसन्निव appeared as if telling, राघवे to Rama, नादर्शयत not shown.

#### Explanation

Thus addressed by Rama, the mountain echoed as if it was telling about Sita but did not show her to Rama.

---

### Shloka 33

#### Shloka Text

ततो दाशरथी राम उवाच च शिलोच्चयम्। मम बाणाग्निनिर्दग्धो भस्मीभूतो भविष्यसि।।3.64.33।। असेव्यः सततं चैव निस्तृणद्रुमपल्लवः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, दाशरथिः Dasaratha's son, रामः Rama, शिलोच्चयम् to the mountain, उवाच च said, मम my, बाणाग्निनिर्दग्धः burnt by the fire of my arrows, भस्मीभूतः reduced to ashes, सततम् always, असेव्यः unfit to be served, निस्तृणद्रुमपल्लवः with no grass, no trees, no twig, भविष्यसि you will be there.

#### Explanation

Rama, Dasaratha's son said to the mountain, The fire of my arrows will reduce you to ashes.You will have no grass, no tree, no twig. You will be unfit to be served any more.

---

### Shloka 34

#### Shloka Text

इमां वा सरितं चाद्य शोषयिष्यामि लक्ष्मण।।3.64.34।। यदि नाख्याति मे सीतामार्यां चन्द्रनिभाननाम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लक्ष्मण Lakshmana, आर्याम् noble, चन्द्रनिभाननाम् Moonlike face, सीताम् Sita, मे to me, नाख्याति not tell, अद्य right now, इमाम् this, सरितं वा to the river too, शोषयिष्यामि will make it dry.

#### Explanation

O Lakshmana if this river does not tell me about that noble Sita with countenance like the moon, I will drain it dry right now.

---

### Shloka 35

#### Shloka Text

एवं स रुषितो रामो दिधक्षन्निव चक्षुषा।।3.64.35।। ददर्श भूमौ निष्क्रान्तं राक्षसस्य पदं महत्। त्रस्ताया रामकाङ्क्षिण्याः प्रधावन्त्या इतस्ततः।।3.64.36।। राक्षसेनानुवृत्ताया मैथिल्याश्च पदान्यथ।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

एवम् in that way, रुषितः in anger, चक्षुषा with eyes, दिधक्षन्निव as if wishing to burn, रामः Rama, भूमौ ground, निष्क्रान्तम् crossed, राक्षसस्य the demon's, महत् large, पदम् footprint, अथ and then, त्रस्तायाः of the frightened lady, रामकाङ्क्षिण्याः who longed for Rama, इतस्ततः here and there, प्रधावन्त्याः who was running, राक्षसेन by the demon, अनुवृत्तायाः who followed, मैथिल्याः Maithili's, पदानि च footprints, ददर्श saw.

#### Explanation

While Rama was angry with his eyes burning, he saw on the ground as he advanced large footprints of a demon and then the footprints of Maithili which signified as if chased by the demon, she was frightened and was running hither and thither, longing to see Rama.

---

### Shloka 36

#### Shloka Text

एवं स रुषितो रामो दिधक्षन्निव चक्षुषा।।3.64.35।। ददर्श भूमौ निष्क्रान्तं राक्षसस्य पदं महत्। त्रस्ताया रामकाङ्क्षिण्याः प्रधावन्त्या इतस्ततः।।3.64.36।। राक्षसेनानुवृत्ताया मैथिल्याश्च पदान्यथ।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

एवम् in that way, रुषितः in anger, चक्षुषा with eyes, दिधक्षन्निव as if wishing to burn, रामः Rama, भूमौ ground, निष्क्रान्तम् crossed, राक्षसस्य the demon's, महत् large, पदम् footprint, अथ and then, त्रस्तायाः of the frightened lady, रामकाङ्क्षिण्याः who longed for Rama, इतस्ततः here and there, प्रधावन्त्याः who was running, राक्षसेन by the demon, अनुवृत्तायाः who followed, मैथिल्याः Maithili's, पदानि च footprints, ददर्श saw.

#### Explanation

While Rama was angry with his eyes burning, he saw on the ground as he advanced large footprints of a demon and then the footprints of Maithili which signified as if chased by the demon, she was frightened and was running hither and thither, longing to see Rama.

---

### Shloka 37

#### Shloka Text

स समीक्ष्य परिक्रान्तं सीताया राक्षसस्य च।।3.64.37।। भग्नं धनुश्च तूणी च विकीर्णं बहुधा रथम्। सम्भ्रान्तहृदयो रामश्शशंस भ्रातरंप्रियम्।।3.64.38।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः रामः that Rama, सीतायाः Sita's, राक्षसस्य च and the demon's, परिक्रान्तम् movement, भग्नम् broken, धनुश्च bow, तूणी च and quiver, बहुधा many ways, विकीर्णम् scattered, रथम् chariot, समीक्ष्य having seen, सम्भ्रान्त हृदयः bewidered mind, प्रियम् loving, भ्रातरम् brother, शशंस told.

#### Explanation

Bewildered to see the broken bow and quiver and many splinters of the chariot scattered all over, Rama said to his loving brother Lakshmana:

---

### Shloka 38

#### Shloka Text

स समीक्ष्य परिक्रान्तं सीताया राक्षसस्य च।।3.64.37।। भग्नं धनुश्च तूणी च विकीर्णं बहुधा रथम्। सम्भ्रान्तहृदयो रामश्शशंस भ्रातरंप्रियम्।।3.64.38।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः रामः that Rama, सीतायाः Sita's, राक्षसस्य च and the demon's, परिक्रान्तम् movement, भग्नम् broken, धनुश्च bow, तूणी च and quiver, बहुधा many ways, विकीर्णम् scattered, रथम् chariot, समीक्ष्य having seen, सम्भ्रान्त हृदयः bewidered mind, प्रियम् loving, भ्रातरम् brother, शशंस told.

#### Explanation

Bewildered to see the broken bow and quiver and many splinters of the chariot scattered all over, Rama said to his loving brother Lakshmana:

---

### Shloka 39

#### Shloka Text

पश्य लक्ष्मण वैदेह्याशशीर्णाः कनकबिन्दवः। भूषणानां हि सौमित्रे माल्यानि विविधानि च।।3.64.39।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सौमित्रे son of Sumitra, लक्ष्मण Lakshmana, पश्य see, वैदेह्याः Vaidehi's, भूषणानाम् of the ornaments, कनकबिन्दवः golden bits, शीर्णाः shattered, विविधानि many kinds, माल्यानि               garlands, च also.

#### Explanation

O Saumitri  see the many broken bits of gold ornaments and garlands.

---

### Shloka 40

#### Shloka Text

तप्तबिन्दुनिकाशैश्च चित्रैः क्षतजबिन्दुभिः। आवृतं पश्य सौमित्रे सर्वतो धरणीतलम्।3.64.40।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सौमित्रे Lakshmana, तप्तबिन्दुनिकाशैः shining like drops of gold, चित्रैः variegated, क्षतजबिन्दुभिः drops of blood, सर्वतः all over, आवृतम् spread, धरणीतलाम् on the ground, पश्य see.

#### Explanation

See, O Lakshmana, the patches of blood looking like drops of shining gold spread all over the ground.

---

### Shloka 41

#### Shloka Text

मन्ये लक्ष्मण वैदेही राक्षसैः कामरूपिभिः। भित्वाभित्वा विभक्ता वा भक्षिता वा भविष्यति।।3.64.41।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लक्ष्मण O Lakshmana,  वैदेही Vaidehi, कामरूपिभिः assuming any form at their will, राक्षसैः by the demons, भित्त्वाभित्त्वा breaking into pieces, विभक्ता वा she is torn, भक्षिता वा eaten or, भविष्यति would have, मन्ये I think.

#### Explanation

O Lakshmana, Vaidehi might have been cut into pieces, and eaten up by the demons who can assume any form at their free will.

---

### Shloka 42

#### Shloka Text

तस्या निमित्तं वैदेह्या द्वयोर्विवदमानयोः। बभूव युद्धं सौमित्रे घोरं राक्षसयोरिह।।3.64.42।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सौमित्रे O Lakshmana, तस्याः her, वेदेह्याः Vaidehi's, निमित्तम् on account of her, विवदमानयोः contesting with, द्वयोः of both, राक्षसयोः demons, इह here, घोरम् dreadful, युद्धम् war, बभूव was fought.

#### Explanation

O Lakshmana, two demons might have fought a dreadful duel at this place contesting with each other for the sake of Vaidehi.

---

### Shloka 43

#### Shloka Text

मुक्तामणिमयं चेदं तपनीयविभूषितम्। धरण्यां पतितं सौम्य कस्य भग्नं महद्धनुः।।3.64.43।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सौम्य O handsome Lakshmana, कस्य whose, इदम् this, मुक्तामणिमयम् embedded with pearls and gems, तपनीयविभूषितम् adorned with gold, महत् धनुः huge bow, भग्नम् broken, धरण्याम् on the ground, पतितम् fallen?

#### Explanation

O handsome Lakshmana whose is this huge bow embedded with pearls and gems and adorned with gold fallen on the ground?

---

### Shloka 44

#### Shloka Text

तरुणादित्यसङ्काशं वैडूर्यगुलिकाचितम्। विशीर्णं पतितं भूमौ कवचं कस्य काञ्चनम्।।3.64.44।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तरुणादित्यसङ्काशम् looking like the morning Sun, वैडूर्यगुलिकाचितम् studded with Vaidurya (cat's eye), काञ्चनम् gold, कस्य whose, विशीर्णम् spread all over, कवचम् shield, भूमौ on the ground, पतितम् fallen?

#### Explanation

Whose is this shield of gold studded with Vaidurya, broken and strewn all over the ground, shining like the morning Sun?

---

### Shloka 45

#### Shloka Text

छत्रं शतशलाकं च दिव्यमाल्योपशोभितम्। भग्नदण्डमिदं कस्य भूमौ सम्यङ्निपातितम्।।3.64.45।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

शतशलाकं च having a hundred spokes, दिव्यमाल्योपशोभितम् festooned with wonderful flower garlands, भग्नदण्डम् with broken staff, इदं छत्रम् this parasol, कस्य whose, सम्यक् thrown down, भूमौ on the ground, निपातितम् fallen?

#### Explanation

Whose parasol is this with a hundred spokes, festooned with wonderful flowers thrown down on the ground with broken staff ?

---

### Shloka 46

#### Shloka Text

काञ्चनोरश्छदा श्चेमे पिशाचवदनाः खराः। भीमरूपा महाकायाः कस्य वा निहता रणे।।3.64.46।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation
