काञ्चनोरश्छदाः with golden chestcoverings, पिशाचवदनाः those with devil's face, भीमरूपाः fierceful, महाकायाः those with huge body, रणे in war, निहताः slain, इमे these, खराः donkeys, कस्य वा whose?

#### Explanation

Whose are these donkeys, with golden chestcoverings, with the devil's face, fierceful and huge and slain in the encounter ?

---

### Shloka 47

#### Shloka Text

दीप्तपावकसङ्काशो द्युतिमान्समरध्वजः। अपविद्धश्च भग्नश्च कस्य साङ्ग्रामिको रथः।।3.64.47।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

दीप्तपावकसङ्काशः looking like burning fire, द्युतिमान् dazzling, समरध्वजः battle flag, कस्य वा whose, साङ्ग्रामिकः useful in battle, रथः chariot, भग्नः broken, अपविद्धश्च turned upside down and broken.

#### Explanation

Whose war chariot with a battle flag is this, shining like burning fire, turned upside down and broken?

---

### Shloka 48

#### Shloka Text

रथाक्षमात्रा विशिखास्तपनीयविभूषणाः। कस्येमेऽभिहता बाणाः प्रकीर्णा घोरकर्मणः।।3.64.48।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रथाक्षमात्राः of the size of the chariot's axle, विशिखाः pointless arrows, तपनीयविभूषणाः adorned with bright gold, अभिहताः destroyed, इमे बाणाः these arrows, घोरकर्मणः performing terrible deeds, कस्य whose, प्रकीर्णाः scattered about?

#### Explanation

Whose are these pointless arrows of the size of a chariot axle adorned with bright gold destroyed and scattered ? Who is he that has performed such terrible deeds?

---

### Shloka 49

#### Shloka Text

शरावरौ शरैः पूर्णौ विध्वस्तौ पश्य लक्ष्मण। प्रतोदाभीशुहस्तो वै कस्यायं सारथिर्हतः।।3.64.49।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लक्ष्मण Lakshmana, शरैः with arrows, पूर्णौ both filled, विध्वस्तौ shattered, शरावरौ two quivers, पश्य see, प्रतोदाभीशुहस्तः a man holding a whip and reins in the hands, हतः slain, अयं सारथिः this charioteer, कस्य whose?

#### Explanation

O Lakshmana  look at these two quivers filled with arrows and lying shattered.  The charioteer is holding a whip and reins in his hands and is lying dead. To whom do they belong?

---

### Shloka 50

#### Shloka Text

कस्येमौ पुरुषव्याघ्र शयाते निहतौ युधि। चामरग्राहिणौ सौम्य सोष्णीषमणिकुण्डलौ।।3.64.50।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पुरुषव्याघ्र O tiger among men, सौम्य O handsome one, सोष्णीषमणिकुण्डलौ with head dress and earrings made of gems, इमौ these two, चामर ग्राहिणौ carriers of fans, कस्य whose, युधि in war, निहतौ both are slain, शयाते both fallen down?

#### Explanation

O best among men, O handsome Lakshmana see these carriers of fans with head dress and earrings studded with gems lying slain in the combat. Whose are they ?

---

### Shloka 51

#### Shloka Text

पदवीपुरुषस्यैषा व्यक्तं कस्यापि रक्षसः। वैरं शतगुणं पश्य ममेदं जीवितान्तकम्।।3.64.51।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

एषा this, व्यक्तम् it is certain, कस्यापि some one's, पुरुषस्य man's, रक्षसः demon's, पदवी path, मम for me, जीवितान्तकम् till the end of my life, शतगुणम् hundred times, इदं वैरम् enmity like this, पश्य see.

#### Explanation

This indeed is the path the man or the demon has traversed. My enmity with him now has become hundredfold. This will end only with the extermination of his life.

---

### Shloka 52

#### Shloka Text

सुघोरहृदयैस्सौम्य राक्षसैः कामरूपिभिः। हृता मृता वा सीता सा भक्षिता वा तपस्विनी।।3.64.52।। नधर्मस्त्रायते सीतां ह्रियमाणां महावने।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सौम्य O handsome one, तपस्विनी a helpless woman, सा सीता that Sita, सुघोरहृदयैः of terrible heart, कामरूपिभिः those who can change their form at will, राक्षसैः by the demons, हृता वा abducted or, भक्षिता वा eaten away or, मृता वा or killed, महावने dense forest, ह्रियमाणाम् while she is being carried off, सीताम् Sita, धर्मः righteousness, न त्रायते I did not protect.

#### Explanation

O handsome one, those demons can change their form at will and are of dreadful nature. Have they abducted or devoured or killed her in this great forest. Even her righteousness could not protect the helpless Sita.

---

### Shloka 53

#### Shloka Text

भक्षितायां हि वैदेह्यां हृतायामपि लक्ष्मण।।3.64.53।। के हि लोके प्रियं कर्तुं शक्तास्सौम्य ममेश्वराः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सौम्य लक्ष्मण O handsome Lakshmana, वैदेह्याम् when Sita, भक्षितायाम् was eaten, हृतायामपि or carried off, मम to me, लोके in this world, के ईश्वराः which of the gods, प्रियम् happiness, कर्तुम् do, शक्ताः not feasible?

#### Explanation

If Sita has been devoured or kidnapped, none in this world or even gods could do anything to make me happy, O handsome Lakshmana

---

### Shloka 54

#### Shloka Text

कर्तारमपि लोकानां शूरं करुणवेदिनम्।।3.64.54।। अज्ञानादवमन्येरत्सर्वभूतानि लक्ष्मण।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लक्ष्मण Lakshmana, लोकानाम् of these worlds, कर्तारमपि one who may be even the creator, शूरम् hero, करुणवेदिनम् compassionate, सर्वभूतानि all beings, अज्ञानात् by ignorance, अवमन्येरन् will disrespect.

#### Explanation

O Lakshmana even the compassionate and heroic Creator of the worlds will be disrespected by all beings, however ignorantly (if such things are allowed to happen).

---

### Shloka 55

#### Shloka Text

मृदुं लोकहिते युक्तं दान्तं करुणवेदिनम्।।3.64.55।। निर्वीर्य इति मन्यन्ते नूनं मां त्रिदशेश्वराः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मृदुम् gentle, लोकहिते for the welfare of the world, युक्तम् one engaged, दान्तम् selfrestraint, करुणवेदिनम् a compassionate, माम् me, त्रिदशेश्वराः the gods, निर्वीर्यः इति as weak, मन्यन्ते will consider, नूनम् certainly.

#### Explanation

The gods will certainly consider me a man engaged in the welfare of the world, a man of selfrestraint and compassion, but at the same time a weak man.

---

### Shloka 56

#### Shloka Text

मां प्राप्य हि गुणो दोषस्संवृत्तः पश्य लक्ष्मण।।3.64.56।। अद्यैव सर्वभूतानां रक्षसामभवाय च। संहृत्यैव शशिज्योत्स्नां महान्सूर्य इवोदितः।।3.64.57।। संहृत्यैव गुणान्सर्वान्मम तेजः प्रकाशते।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लक्ष्मण Lakshmana, माम् me, प्राप्य after attaining, गुणः merit, दोषः demerit, संवृत्तः been  converted, पश्य see, शशिज्योत्स्नाम् light of the Moon, संहृत्यैव on effacing, उदितः risen, महान् mighty, सूर्यः Sun, इव like, तेजः glow, सर्वान् all, गुणान् qualities, संहृत्यैव effacing, अद्यैव now, सर्वभूतानाम् to all beings, रक्षसां च of demons, अभवाय for their extermination, प्रकाशते will shine forth.

#### Explanation

O Lakshmana even demerits turn into merits when they come in contact with me. Just as the Sun shines, effacing the moonlight, my brilliance will outshine my demerits of exterminating all (wicked) beings including the demons.

---

### Shloka 57

#### Shloka Text

मां प्राप्य हि गुणो दोषस्संवृत्तः पश्य लक्ष्मण।।3.64.56।। अद्यैव सर्वभूतानां रक्षसामभवाय च। संहृत्यैव शशिज्योत्स्नां महान्सूर्य इवोदितः।।3.64.57।। संहृत्यैव गुणान्सर्वान्मम तेजः प्रकाशते।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लक्ष्मण Lakshmana, माम् me, प्राप्य after attaining, गुणः merit, दोषः demerit, संवृत्तः been  converted, पश्य see, शशिज्योत्स्नाम् light of the Moon, संहृत्यैव on effacing, उदितः risen, महान् mighty, सूर्यः Sun, इव like, तेजः glow, सर्वान् all, गुणान् qualities, संहृत्यैव effacing, अद्यैव now, सर्वभूतानाम् to all beings, रक्षसां च of demons, अभवाय for their extermination, प्रकाशते will shine forth.

#### Explanation

O Lakshmana even demerits turn into merits when they come in contact with me. Just as the Sun shines, effacing the moonlight, my brilliance will outshine my demerits of exterminating all (wicked) beings including the demons.

---

### Shloka 58

#### Shloka Text

नैव यक्षा न गन्धर्वा न पिशाचा न राक्षसाः।।3.64.58।। किन्नरा वा मनुष्या वा सुखं प्राप्स्यन्ति लिक्ष्मण।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लक्ष्मण Lakshmana, यक्षाः yakshas, सुखम् peace, नैव प्राप्स्यन्ति not attain, यक्षाः yakshas, न not, पिशाचाः goblins, न not, रक्षसाः demons, न not, किन्नरा वा or kinneras, मनुष्या वा or human beings,

#### Explanation

O Lakshmana, neither yakshas, nor goblins nor demons nor kinneras nor humans  will be in peace now.

---

### Shloka 59

#### Shloka Text

ममास्त्रबाणसम्पूर्णमाकाशं पश्य लक्ष्मण।।3.64.59।। निस्सम्पातं करिष्यामि ह्यद्य त्रैलोक्यचारिणाम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लक्ष्मण Lakshman, मम my, अस्त्रबाणसम्पूर्णम् filled with all my weapons and arrows, आकाशम् sky, अद्य now, त्रैलोक्यचारिणाम् those who move in the three worlds, निस्सम्पातम् impossible to descend, करिष्यामि I will do, पश्य see.

#### Explanation

O Lakshmana, just see, I will fill the sky with all my weapons and arrows and make all movements impossible in the three worlds.

---

### Shloka 60

#### Shloka Text

सन्निरुद्धग्रहगणमावारितनिशाकरम्।।3.64.60।। विप्रणष्टानलमरुद्भास्करद्युतिसंवृतम्। विनिर्मथितशैलाग्रं शुष्यमाणजलाशयम्।।3.64.61।। ध्वस्तद्रुमलतागुल्मं विप्रणाशितसागरम्। त्रैलोक्यं तु करिष्यामि संयुक्तं कालकर्मणा।।3.64.62।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

त्रैलोक्यम् the three worlds, सन्निरुद्धग्रहगणम्  obstruct planets, आवारितनिशाकरम् preventing the movement of the Moon, विप्रणष्टानलमरुद्भास्करद्युतिसंवृतम् blocking and destroying the radiance of fire, wind and Sun, विनिर्मथितशैलाग्रम् crushing the mountain tops, शुष्यमाणजलाशयम् drying up the tanks, ध्वस्तद्रुमलतागुल्मम् destroying the trees, creepers and shrubs, विप्रणाशितसागरम् annihilating the ocean, कालकर्मणा by a fatal act, संयुक्तम् together, करिष्यामि I will do.

#### Explanation

I will obstruct the movement of planets and the Moon, destroy the radiance of the Sun, the fire and block the wind crush the mountain tops, dry up the tanks, destroy the trees shrubs and creepers and annihilate the oceans. I shall bring about this fatal change at once in the three worlds.

---

### Shloka 61

#### Shloka Text

सन्निरुद्धग्रहगणमावारितनिशाकरम्।।3.64.60।। विप्रणष्टानलमरुद्भास्करद्युतिसंवृतम्। विनिर्मथितशैलाग्रं शुष्यमाणजलाशयम्।।3.64.61।। ध्वस्तद्रुमलतागुल्मं विप्रणाशितसागरम्। त्रैलोक्यं तु करिष्यामि संयुक्तं कालकर्मणा।।3.64.62।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

त्रैलोक्यम् the three worlds, सन्निरुद्धग्रहगणम्  obstruct planets, आवारितनिशाकरम् preventing the movement of the Moon, विप्रणष्टानलमरुद्भास्करद्युतिसंवृतम् blocking and destroying the radiance of fire, wind and Sun, विनिर्मथितशैलाग्रम् crushing the mountain tops, शुष्यमाणजलाशयम् drying up the tanks, ध्वस्तद्रुमलतागुल्मम् destroying the trees, creepers and shrubs, विप्रणाशितसागरम् annihilating the ocean, कालकर्मणा by a fatal act, संयुक्तम् together, करिष्यामि I will do.

#### Explanation

I will obstruct the movement of planets and the Moon, destroy the radiance of the Sun, the fire and block the wind crush the mountain tops, dry up the tanks, destroy the trees shrubs and creepers and annihilate the oceans. I shall bring about this fatal change at once in the three worlds.

---

### Shloka 62

#### Shloka Text

सन्निरुद्धग्रहगणमावारितनिशाकरम्।।3.64.60।। विप्रणष्टानलमरुद्भास्करद्युतिसंवृतम्। विनिर्मथितशैलाग्रं शुष्यमाणजलाशयम्।।3.64.61।। ध्वस्तद्रुमलतागुल्मं विप्रणाशितसागरम्। त्रैलोक्यं तु करिष्यामि संयुक्तं कालकर्मणा।।3.64.62।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

त्रैलोक्यम् the three worlds, सन्निरुद्धग्रहगणम्  obstruct planets, आवारितनिशाकरम् preventing the movement of the Moon, विप्रणष्टानलमरुद्भास्करद्युतिसंवृतम् blocking and destroying the radiance of fire, wind and Sun, विनिर्मथितशैलाग्रम् crushing the mountain tops, शुष्यमाणजलाशयम् drying up the tanks, ध्वस्तद्रुमलतागुल्मम् destroying the trees, creepers and shrubs, विप्रणाशितसागरम् annihilating the ocean, कालकर्मणा by a fatal act, संयुक्तम् together, करिष्यामि I will do.

#### Explanation

I will obstruct the movement of planets and the Moon, destroy the radiance of the Sun, the fire and block the wind crush the mountain tops, dry up the tanks, destroy the trees shrubs and creepers and annihilate the oceans. I shall bring about this fatal change at once in the three worlds.

---

### Shloka 63

#### Shloka Text

न तां कुशलिनीं सीतां प्रदास्यन्ति ममेश्वराः। अस्मिन्मुहूर्ते सौमित्रे मम द्रक्ष्यन्ति विक्रमम्।।3.64.63।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सौमित्रे O Lakshmana, ईश्वराः gods, तां सीताम् Sita, कुशलिनीम् safely, मम to me, न प्रदास्यन्ति not give, अस्मिन् in this, मुहूर्ते this moment, मम my, विक्रमम् power, द्रक्ष्यन्ति they will witness.

#### Explanation

If the gods do not return my Sita safely to me, O Lakshmana  they will witness my power right now.

---

### Shloka 64

#### Shloka Text

नाकाशमुत्पतिष्यन्ति सर्वभूतानि लक्ष्मण। मम चापगुणोन्मुक्तैर्बाणजालैर्निरन्तरम्।।3.64.64।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लक्ष्मण O Lakshmana, सर्वभूतानि all beings, मम me, चापगुणोन्मुक्तै: arrows released from my bow string, बाणजालैः by arrows, निरन्तरम् will have no place to move, आकाशम् sky, न उत्पतिष्यन्ति not fly.

#### Explanation

Lakshmana no beings will be able to move in the sky, blocked by arrows released from my bowstring there will be no space left there.

---

### Shloka 65

#### Shloka Text

अर्दितं मम नाराचैर्ध्वस्तभ्रान्तमृगद्विजम्। समाकुलममर्यादं जगत्पश्याद्य लक्ष्मण।।3.64.65।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लक्ष्मण Lakshmana, अद्य now, मम my, नाराचैः by darts, अर्दितम् distressed, ध्वस्तभ्रान्तमृगद्विजम् समाकुलम् beasts and birds confused and destroyed, अमर्यादम् crossing all limits, जगत् world, पश्य see.

#### Explanation

O Lakshmna now beasts and birds will be confused and destroyed by my darts.You will see them crossing all limits of propriety.

---

### Shloka 66

#### Shloka Text

आकर्णपूर्णैरिषुभिर्जीवलोकं दुरावरैः। करिष्ये मैथिलीहेतोरपिशाचमराक्षसम्।।3.64.66।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मैथिलीहेतोः for the sake of Maithili, जीवलोकम् this world of mortals, आकर्णपूर्णैः fully drawing up to the ears, दुरावरैः by the irresistible, इषुभिः arrows, अपिशाचम् devoid of evil spirits, अराक्षसम् without demons, करिष्ये will do.

#### Explanation

For the sake of Maithili I will render this world devoid of goblins and demons with my irresistible arrows pulled up to my ears.

---

### Shloka 67

#### Shloka Text

मम रोषप्रयुक्तानां सायकानां बलं सुराः। द्रक्ष्यन्त्यद्य विमुक्तानामतिदूरातिगामिनाम्।।3.64.67।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

विमुक्तानाम् released, मम my, रोषप्रयुक्तानाम् released in rage, अतिदूरातिगामिनाम् of those that can reach very distant places, सायकानाम् of the arrows, बलम् power, सुराः gods, अद्य now, द्रक्ष्यन्ति will witness.

#### Explanation

Now the gods will witness the power of my arrows released in great rage which can hit distant targets.

---

### Shloka 68

#### Shloka Text

नैव देवा न दैतेया न पिशाचा न राक्षसाः। भविष्यन्ति मम क्रोधात्त्रैलोक्ये विप्रणाशिते।।3.64.68।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मम my, क्रोधात् due to anger, त्रैलोक्ये in three worlds, विप्रणाशिते destroyed, देवाः gods, नैव भविष्यन्ति not exist, दैतेयाः demons, न not, पिशाचाः evil spirits, न not , राक्षसाः demons, न not.

#### Explanation

My anger will destroy all the gods, demons and evil spirits. They will be extinghished from the three worlds.

---

### Shloka 69

#### Shloka Text

देवदानवयक्षाणां लोका ये रक्षसामपि। बहुधा न भविष्यन्ति बाणौघैश्शकलीकृताः।।3.64.69।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

देवदानवयक्षाणाम् of gods, demons and yakshas, रक्षसामपि demons too, ये those, लोकाः worlds, बाणौघैः by the touch of my arrows, बहुधा many times, शकलीकृता: broken into pieces, न भविष्यन्ति not survive.

#### Explanation

The worlds of gods, yakshas or even demons torn into shreds by my arrows will not  survive.

---

### Shloka 70

#### Shloka Text

निर्मर्यादानिमान्लोकान्करिष्याम्यद्य सायकैः। हृतां मृतां वा सौमित्रे न दास्यन्ति ममेश्वराः।।3.64.70।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सौमित्रे O Lakshmana, हृताम् a lady carried away, मृतां वा or dead,  ईश्वराः by gods, मम me, न दास्यन्ति do not give, अद्य now, सायकैः by arrows, इमान् this, लोकान् worlds, निर्मर्यादान् without any boundaries, करिष्यामि will do.

#### Explanation

If the gods do not hand over Sita to me either carried away or dead I will transgress these worlds, O Lakshmana

---

### Shloka 71

#### Shloka Text

तथारुपां हि वैदेहीं न दास्यन्ति यदि प्रियाम्। नाशयामि जगत्सर्वं त्रैलोक्यं सचराचरम्।।3.64.71।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तथारूपाम् in her true form, प्रियाम् my beloved, वैदेहीम् Vaidehi, न दास्यन्ति यदि not give me, सर्वम् all, सचराचरम् with animate and inanimate beings, त्रैलोक्यम् three worlds, जगत् world, नाशयामि I will destroy.

#### Explanation

If my beloved Vaidehi is not given back to me in her true form I will destroy the three worlds with all animate and inanimate beings.

---

### Shloka 72

#### Shloka Text

इत्युक्त्वा रोषताम्राक्षो रामो निष्पीड्य कार्मुकम्। शरमादाय सन्दीप्तं घोरमाशीविषोपमम्।।3.64.72।। सन्धाय धनुषि श्रीमान्रामः परपुरञ्जयः। युगान्ताग्निरिव क्रुद्ध इदं वचनमब्रवीत्।।3.64.73।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रामः Rama, श्रीमान् handsome, परपुरञ्जयः one who can conquer the cities of enemies, रामः Rama, इति thus, उक्त्वा having said, रोषताम्राक्षः with eyes reddened in anger, कार्मुकम् his bow, निष्पीड्य having pressed, आशीविषोपमम् comparable to a  poisonous snake, घोरम् dreadful, सन्दीप्तम् glowing, शरम् arrow, आदाय after taking out, धनुषि bow, सन्धाय after stringing, युगान्ताग्निरिव like fire at the time of the dissolution of the world, क्रुद्धः angry man, इदम् these, वचनम् words, अब्रवीत् said.

#### Explanation

After speaking these words, handsome Rama, his eyes red with anger, pressed his bow, took out the glowing arrow that was dreadful like a poisonous serpent, fixed it to his bow and said these words in a rage like the fire at the time of dissolution of the world.

---

### Shloka 73

#### Shloka Text

इत्युक्त्वा रोषताम्राक्षो रामो निष्पीड्य कार्मुकम्। शरमादाय सन्दीप्तं घोरमाशीविषोपमम्।।3.64.72।। सन्धाय धनुषि श्रीमान्रामः परपुरञ्जयः। युगान्ताग्निरिव क्रुद्ध इदं वचनमब्रवीत्।।3.64.73।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रामः Rama, श्रीमान् handsome, परपुरञ्जयः one who can conquer the cities of enemies, रामः Rama, इति thus, उक्त्वा having said, रोषताम्राक्षः with eyes reddened in anger, कार्मुकम् his bow, निष्पीड्य having pressed, आशीविषोपमम् comparable to a  poisonous snake, घोरम् dreadful, सन्दीप्तम् glowing, शरम् arrow, आदाय after taking out, धनुषि bow, सन्धाय after stringing, युगान्ताग्निरिव like fire at the time of the dissolution of the world, क्रुद्धः angry man, इदम् these, वचनम् words, अब्रवीत् said.

#### Explanation

After speaking these words, handsome Rama, his eyes red with anger, pressed his bow, took out the glowing arrow that was dreadful like a poisonous serpent, fixed it to his bow and said these words in a rage like the fire at the time of dissolution of the world.

---

### Shloka 74

#### Shloka Text

यथा जरा यथा मृत्युर्यथा कालो यथा विधिः। नित्यं न प्रतिहन्यन्ते सर्वभूतेषु लक्ष्मण।।3.64.74।। तथाहं क्रोधसंयुक्तो न निवार्योऽस्मि सर्वथा।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लक्ष्मण Lakshmana, सर्वभूतेषु in all beings, जरा old age, यथा as, मृत्युः death, यथा as, कालः time, यथा as, विधिः fate, यथा as, नित्यम् always, न प्रतिहन्यन्ते cannot be averted, तथा in the same way, क्रोधसंयुक्तः full of anger, अहम् I, सर्वथा by all means, न निवार्यः not defy, अस्मि I.

#### Explanation

Just as all beings in this world cannot avoid age and death, or defy time and fate so also none can prevent me at all when I am  angry, O Lakshmana

---

### Shloka 75

#### Shloka Text

पुरेव मे चारुदंतीमनिन्दितां दिशन्ति सीतां यदि नाद्य मैथिलीम्। सदेवगन्धर्वमनुष्यपन्नगं जगत्सशैलं परिवर्तयाम्यहम्।।3.64.75।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

चारुदंतीम् a lady with beautiful teeth, अनिन्दिताम् blemishless lady, मैथिलीम् Maithili, सीताम् Sita, पुरेव as earlier, अद्य now, मे my, न दिशन्ति यदि if they do not show me, अहम् I, सदेवगन्धर्वमनुष्यपन्नगम् gods, gandharvas, men, serpents, सशैलम् mountain, जगत् world, परिवर्तयामि I will topsyturvy.

#### Explanation

I will topsyturvy this entire world including the gods, the gandharvas, human beings, serpents and mountains if they do not show me the princess from Mithila, who is blemishless and whose teeth are beautiful इत्यार्शे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकेय आदिकाव्ये अरण्यकाण्डे चतुष्षष्टिमस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the sixtyfourth sarga of Aranyakanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 65

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

तप्यमानं तथा रामं सीताहरणकर्शितम्। लोकानामभवे युक्तं सांवर्तकमिवानलम्।।3.65.1।। वीक्षमाणं धनुस्सज्यं निश्श्वसन्तं पुनः पुनः। दग्धुकामं जगत्सर्वं युगान्ते च यथा हरम्।।3.65.2।। अदृष्टपूर्वं सङ्क्रुद्धं दृष्ट्वा रामं तु लक्ष्मणः। अब्रवीत्प्राञ्जलिर्वाक्यं मुखेन परिशुष्यता।।3.65.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तथा that way, तप्यमानम् tormented by pain, रामम् Rama, सीताहरणकर्शितम् emaciated by Sita's abduction, सांवर्तकम् pertaining to dissolution of the world, अनलमिव the fire like, लोकानाम् of the worlds, अभवे to annihilate, युक्तम् ready, सज्यम् strung, धनुः bow, वीक्षमाणम् while seeing, पुनः पुनः again and again, निश्श्वसन्तम् sighing hot breath, युगान्ते at the end of the world, हरं यथा like Lord Siva, सर्वम् all, जगत् world, दग्धुकामम् desiring to burn, अदृष्टपूर्वम् never seen before, संक्रुद्धम् enraged, रामम् Rama, दृष्ट्वा seeing, लक्ष्मणः Lakshmana, प्राञ्जलिः with folded palms, परिशुष्यता mouth dried up, मुखेन face, अब्रवीत् said.

#### Explanation

Tormented and emaciated due to Sita's abduction, resembling the fire at the time of dissolution of the creation, ready to destroy the world, Rama looked at his bow strung again and again, breathing hot sighs like Lord Siva at the end of the creation, wishing to burn down the world in a rage. Lakshmana who had never seen Rama in such anger before said to him with folded hands and parched throat.

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

तप्यमानं तथा रामं सीताहरणकर्शितम्। लोकानामभवे युक्तं सांवर्तकमिवानलम्।।3.65.1।। वीक्षमाणं धनुस्सज्यं निश्श्वसन्तं पुनः पुनः। दग्धुकामं जगत्सर्वं युगान्ते च यथा हरम्।।3.65.2।। अदृष्टपूर्वं सङ्क्रुद्धं दृष्ट्वा रामं तु लक्ष्मणः। अब्रवीत्प्राञ्जलिर्वाक्यं मुखेन परिशुष्यता।।3.65.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तथा that way, तप्यमानम् tormented by pain, रामम् Rama, सीताहरणकर्शितम् emaciated by Sita's abduction, सांवर्तकम् pertaining to dissolution of the world, अनलमिव the fire like, लोकानाम् of the worlds, अभवे to annihilate, युक्तम् ready, सज्यम् strung, धनुः bow, वीक्षमाणम् while seeing, पुनः पुनः again and again, निश्श्वसन्तम् sighing hot breath, युगान्ते at the end of the world, हरं यथा like Lord Siva, सर्वम् all, जगत् world, दग्धुकामम् desiring to burn, अदृष्टपूर्वम् never seen before, संक्रुद्धम् enraged, रामम् Rama, दृष्ट्वा seeing, लक्ष्मणः Lakshmana, प्राञ्जलिः with folded palms, परिशुष्यता mouth dried up, मुखेन face, अब्रवीत् said.

#### Explanation

Tormented and emaciated due to Sita's abduction, resembling the fire at the time of dissolution of the creation, ready to destroy the world, Rama looked at his bow strung again and again, breathing hot sighs like Lord Siva at the end of the creation, wishing to burn down the world in a rage. Lakshmana who had never seen Rama in such anger before said to him with folded hands and parched throat.

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

तप्यमानं तथा रामं सीताहरणकर्शितम्। लोकानामभवे युक्तं सांवर्तकमिवानलम्।।3.65.1।। वीक्षमाणं धनुस्सज्यं निश्श्वसन्तं पुनः पुनः। दग्धुकामं जगत्सर्वं युगान्ते च यथा हरम्।।3.65.2।। अदृष्टपूर्वं सङ्क्रुद्धं दृष्ट्वा रामं तु लक्ष्मणः। अब्रवीत्प्राञ्जलिर्वाक्यं मुखेन परिशुष्यता।।3.65.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तथा that way, तप्यमानम् tormented by pain, रामम् Rama, सीताहरणकर्शितम् emaciated by Sita's abduction, सांवर्तकम् pertaining to dissolution of the world, अनलमिव the fire like, लोकानाम् of the worlds, अभवे to annihilate, युक्तम् ready, सज्यम् strung, धनुः bow, वीक्षमाणम् while seeing, पुनः पुनः again and again, निश्श्वसन्तम् sighing hot breath, युगान्ते at the end of the world, हरं यथा like Lord Siva, सर्वम् all, जगत् world, दग्धुकामम् desiring to burn, अदृष्टपूर्वम् never seen before, संक्रुद्धम् enraged, रामम् Rama, दृष्ट्वा seeing, लक्ष्मणः Lakshmana, प्राञ्जलिः with folded palms, परिशुष्यता mouth dried up, मुखेन face, अब्रवीत् said.

#### Explanation

Tormented and emaciated due to Sita's abduction, resembling the fire at the time of dissolution of the creation, ready to destroy the world, Rama looked at his bow strung again and again, breathing hot sighs like Lord Siva at the end of the creation, wishing to burn down the world in a rage. Lakshmana who had never seen Rama in such anger before said to him with folded hands and parched throat.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

पुरा भूत्वा मृदुर्दान्तस्सर्वभूतहिते रतः। न क्रोधवशमापन्नः प्रकृतिं हातुमर्हसि।।3.65.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पुरा in the past, मृदुः gentle, दान्तः selfrestrained, सर्वभूतहिते in the welfare of all beings, रतः as engaged, भूत्वा after being, क्रोधवशम् under the spell of anger, आपन्नः attaining, प्रकृतिम् natural form, हातुम् to give up, नार्हसि do not behove.

#### Explanation

Earlier you were gentle, self restrained and engaged in the welfare of all beings. (Now) it is not proper for you to give up your natural composure in anger.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

चन्द्रे लक्ष्मीः प्रभा सूर्ये गतिर्वायौ भुवि क्षमा। एतत्तु नियतं सर्वं त्वयि चानुत्तमं यशः।।3.65.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

चन्द्रे of the Moon, लक्ष्मीः grace, सूर्ये of the Sun, प्रभा radiance, वायौ of the wind, गतिः motion, भुवि on earth, क्षमा forbearance, एतत् सर्वम् all this, नियतम् always, त्वयि in you, अनुत्तमम् the best, यशः च and fame.

#### Explanation

In you are always present the grace of the Moon, the radiance of the Sun, the motion of the wind, the forbearance of the earth and reputation.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

एकस्य नापराधेन लोकान् हन्तुं त्वमर्हसि। न तु जानामि कस्यायं भग्नस्साङ्ग्रामिको रथः।।3.65.6।। केन वा कस्य वा हेतोस्सायुधस्सपरिच्छदः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

एकस्य one's, अपराधेन by mistake, इह here, लोकान् worlds, हन्तुम् to destroy, नार्हसि it is not proper, सायुधः with weapons, सपरिच्छदः with all accesories, अयम् this, साङ्ग्रामिकः used in war, रथः chariot, कस्य whose, केन by whom, कस्य वा हेतोः for what reason, भग्नः broken, न जानामि I do not know.

#### Explanation

To destroy the world for the mistake of a single person is not proper. Why and by whom is this warchariot broken with weapons and all its accesories I do not know.

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

खुरनेमिक्षतश्चायं सिक्तो रुधिरबिन्धुभिः।।3.65.7।। देशो निर्वृत्तसङ्ग्रामस्सुघोरः पार्थिवात्मज।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पार्थिवात्मज O prince , खुरनेमिक्षतः damaged by hooves of horses and the rims of the chariot', रुधिरबिन्दुभिः by drops of blood, सिक्तः drenched, सुघोरः very dreadful, अयं देशः this place, निर्वृत्तसंग्रामः a great combat was fought here.

#### Explanation

O prince, this place is damaged by the hooves of the horses and the rims of the chariot. It is soaked with drops of blood.Very dreadful must have been the fight here.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

एकस्य तु विमर्धोऽयं न द्वयोर्वदतांवर।।3.65.8।। न हि वृत्तं हि पश्यामि बलस्य महतः पदम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वदताम् of those intelligent in speech, वर the best one, अयम् this, एकस्य by one person, विमर्धः combat, द्वयोः of two people, न not, महतः great, बलस्य army, वृत्तम् this fight, पदम् footprints, न हि पश्यामि I do not see.

#### Explanation

O, best of the eloquent  this fight involves only one and not two. I do not see the footprints of a big army.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

नैकस्य तु कृते लोकान्विनाशयितुमर्हसि।।3.65.9।। युक्तदण्डा हि मृदवः प्रशान्ता वसुधाधिपाः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

एकस्य कृते for one's sake, लोकान् the worlds, विनाशयितुम् to destroy, नार्हसि not right, हि indeed, वसुधाधिपाः lords of the earth, युक्तदण्डाः punish the deserving, मृदवः gentle, प्रशान्ताः peaceful.

#### Explanation

To destroy the entire world for one individual is not right. The lords of the earth should remain gentle, peaceful and punish only the deserving.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

सदा त्वं सर्वभूतानां शरण्यः परमा गतिः।।3.65.10।। को नु दारप्रणाशं ते साधु मन्येत राघव।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

त्वम् you, सर्वभूतानाम् of all beings, सदा always, शरण्यः a refuge, परमा गतिः ultimate resort, राघव Raghava, ते to you, दारप्रणाशम् lost track of her whereabouts, को नु who will, साधु proper, मन्येत think.

#### Explanation

You are the ultimate resort and refuge of all beings. Who would like that your wife should be abducted ?

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

सरितस्सागराश्शैला देवगन्धर्वदानवाः।।3.65.11।। नालं ते विप्रियं कर्तुं दीक्षितस्येव साधवः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सरितः rivers, सागराः seas, शैलाः mountains, देवगन्धर्वदानवाः gods, gandharvas and demons, ते to you, साधवः pious people, दीक्षितस्येव those who observe religious vows, विप्रियम् disagreeable, कर्तुम् to do, नालम् they are not.

#### Explanation

Just as pious men do not do anything disagreeable towards persons who observe religious vows, so also rivers, seas and mountains, gods, gandharvas and demons are not capable of causing unhappiness to you.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

येन राजन्हृता सीता तमन्वेषितुमर्हसि।।3.65.12।। मद्वितीयो धनुष्पाणिस्सहायैः परमर्षिभिः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राजन् king, धनुष्पाणिः wielding a bow, मद्वितीयः followed by one, सहायैः with the help of, परमर्षिभिः by great sages, सीता Sita, येन whomsoever, हृता carried off, तम् him, अन्वेषितुम् to search, अर्हसि is proper for you.

#### Explanation

O king bow in hands, followed by me and with the help of great sages you should search for the one who has abducted Sita.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

समुद्रं च विचेष्यामः पर्वतांश्च वनानि च।।3.65.13।। गुहाश्च विविधा घोरा नदी पद्मवनानि च।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

समुद्रं च in the oceans, पर्वतांश्च in the mountains, वनानि च and the forests, विविधाः several, घोराः dreadful, गुहाश्च caves, नदीः rivers, पद्मवनानि lotus ponds, विचेष्यामः we will search.

#### Explanation

We will search oceans, mountains, forests and several dreadful caves, rivers and lotusponds.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

देवगन्धर्वलोकांश्च विचेष्यामस्समाहिताः।।3.65.14।। यावन्नाधिगमिष्यामस्तव भार्यापहारिणम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

समाहिताः relentlessly, तव your, भार्यापहारिणम् the abductor of your wife, यावत् so long as, नाधिगमिष्यामःwe will not find, देवगन्धर्वलोकांश्च the world of the gods and gandharvas, विचेष्यामः we will search.

#### Explanation

We will search relentlessly for the abductor of your wife everywhere including the world of gods and gandharvas till we find her.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

न चेत्साम्ना प्रदास्यन्ति पत्नीं ते त्रिदशेश्वराः।।3.65.15।। कोसलेन्द्र ततः पश्चात्प्राप्तकालं करिष्यसि।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कोसलेन्द्र lord of Kosala, Rama, त्रिदशेश्वराः gods, ते to you, पत्नीम् your wife, साम्ना with gentle request, न प्रदास्यन्ति not restore her, चेत् if so, ततः then, पश्चात् later, प्राप्तकालम् at the right time, करिष्यसि will take action.

#### Explanation

O lord of Kosala when gods do not restore Sita on request, then you can think of taking action at the appropriate time.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

शीलेन साम्ना विनयेन सीतां नयेन न प्राप्स्यसि चेन्नरेन्द्र। ततस्समुत्सादय हेमपुङ्खै र्महेन्द्रवज्रप्रतिमैश्शरौघैः।।3.65.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

नरेन्द्र O king, शीलेन with right conduct, साम्ना by appeasing, विनयेन through humility, नयेन by diplomacy, सीताम् Sita, न प्राप्स्यसि यदि if you do not get her, ततः then, हेमपुङ्खैः with gold feathered, महेन्द्रवज्रप्रतिमैः equalling Indra's thunderbolt, शरौघैः by stream of arrows, समुत्सादय destroy.

#### Explanation

O king if you fail to get Sita through appeasement, through humility or diplomacy then you destroy (the three worlds) by your goldtipped stream of arrows comparable to Indra's thunderbolt. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये अरण्यकाण्डे पञ्चषष्टितमस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the sixtyfifth sarga of Aranyakanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 66

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

तं तथा शोकसन्तप्तं विलपन्तमनाथवत्। मोहेन महताविष्टं परिद्यूनमचेतनम्।।3.66.1।। ततस्सौमित्रिराश्वास्य मुहूर्तादिव लक्ष्मणः। रामं सम्बोधयामास चरणौ चाभिपीडयन्।।3.66.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, सौमित्रिः son of Sumitra, लक्ष्मणः Lakshmana, तथा like that, शोकसन्तप्तम् afflicted with sorrow, अनाथवत् like an orphan, विलपन्तम् while weeping, महता mighty, मोहेन by delusion, आविष्टम् engrossed, परिद्यूनम् miserable, अचेतनम् devoid of consciousness, तं रामम् Rama, मुहूर्तादिव in a moment, आश्वास्य  consoled, चरणौ च his feet, अभिपीडयन् pressing, सम्बोधयामास addressed.

#### Explanation

Lakshmana, son of Sumitra, consoling the mighty miserable Rama, who was afflicted with sorrow, and was weeping helplessly like an orphan, drowned in great delusion, devoid of consciousness, pressed his feet and addressed him:

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

तं तथा शोकसन्तप्तं विलपन्तमनाथवत्। मोहेन महताविष्टं परिद्यूनमचेतनम्।।3.66.1।। ततस्सौमित्रिराश्वास्य मुहूर्तादिव लक्ष्मणः। रामं सम्बोधयामास चरणौ चाभिपीडयन्।।3.66.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, सौमित्रिः son of Sumitra, लक्ष्मणः Lakshmana, तथा like that, शोकसन्तप्तम् afflicted with sorrow, अनाथवत् like an orphan, विलपन्तम् while weeping, महता mighty, मोहेन by delusion, आविष्टम् engrossed, परिद्यूनम् miserable, अचेतनम् devoid of consciousness, तं रामम् Rama, मुहूर्तादिव in a moment, आश्वास्य  consoled, चरणौ च his feet, अभिपीडयन् pressing, सम्बोधयामास addressed.

#### Explanation

Lakshmana, son of Sumitra, consoling the mighty miserable Rama, who was afflicted with sorrow, and was weeping helplessly like an orphan, drowned in great delusion, devoid of consciousness, pressed his feet and addressed him:

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

महता तपसा राम महता चापि कर्मणा। राज्ञा दशरथेनासि लब्धोऽमृतमिवामरैः।।3.66.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राम Rama, राज्ञा by the king, दशरथेन by Dasaratha, महता great, तपसा penance, महता great, कर्मणाचापि the action, अमरैः by the gods, अमृतमिव like nectar, लब्धः असि obtained.

#### Explanation

O Rama king Dasaratha begot you by doing a great penance and performing great deeds, just as gods begot the nectar.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

तव चैव गुणैर्बद्धस्त्वद्वियोगान्महीपतिः। राजा देवत्वमापन्नो भरतस्य यथाश्रुतम्।।3.66.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

भरतस्य Bharata's, यथा as, श्रुतम् heard, महीपतिः lord of the earth, राजा king, तव your, गुणैः          virtues, बद्धः bound, त्वद्वियोगात् by your separation, देवत्वम् godhood, आपन्नः attained.

#### Explanation

King Dasaratha, impelled by your virtues, and separation from you attained godhood as heard from Bharata.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

दुःखितो हि भवान्लोकांस्तेजसा यदि धक्ष्यते। आर्ताः प्रजा नरव्याघ्र क्व नु यास्यन्ति निर्वृतिम्।।3.66.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

नरव्याघ्र O best among men, दुःखितः in grief, भवान् you, तेजसा by your brilliance, लोकान् worlds, धक्ष्यते burn, यदि if, आर्ताः afflicted, प्रजाः people, क्व where, निर्वृतिम् relief,  यास्यन्ति will get?

#### Explanation

O best of men  if you grieve and burn the world with your brilliance, wherefrom will the (ordinary) afflicted people get relief?

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

लोकस्वभाव एवैष ययातिर्नहुषात्मजः। गतश्शक्रेण सालोक्यमनयस्तं समस्पृशत्।।3.66.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

एषः so, लोकस्वभावः एव it is natural order in the world, नहुषात्मजः Nahusha's son, ययातिः Yayati, शक्रेण to Indra, सालोक्यम् sharing his stay in the same world, गतः attached, अनयः curse, तम् him, समस्पृशत् touched .

#### Explanation

It is the go of the world. Yayati, son of Nahusa, shared heaven with Indra but because of his curse (for his bad conduct) suffered misfortune.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

महर्षिर्यो वसिष्ठस्तु यः पितुर्नः पुरोहितः। अह्ना पुत्रशतं जज्ञे तथैवास्य पुनर्हतम्।।3.66.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महर्षिः sage, यः who, वसिष्ठः Vasistha, यः that, नः पितुः our father's, पुरोहितः priest, अस्य his, अह्ना one day, पुत्रशतम् one hundred sons, जज्ञे gave birth to, पुनः again, तथैव in the same way, हतम् killed.

#### Explanation

To sage Vasistha, our father's priest, were born a hundred sons in a day.But all of them were killed in one day the same way.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

या चेयं जगतां माता देवी लोकनमस्कृता। अस्याश्च चलनं भूमेर्दृश्यते सत्यसंश्रव।।3.66.9।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सत्यसंश्रव who adheres to truth, जगताम् for the worlds, माता mother, लोकनमस्कृता is adored by the whole world, या चेयम् she being so, देवी mother, अस्याः her, भूमेः earth, चलनम् movement, दृश्यते is seen.

#### Explanation

You (rigidly) adhere to truth.(Like you) the mother earth who is adored by all the worlds, is unshakeable. Yet she is seen to be shaking at times.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

यौ धर्मौ जगतां नेत्रौ यत्र सर्वं प्रतिष्ठितम्। आदित्यचन्द्रौ ग्रहणमभ्युपेतौ महाबलौ।।3.66.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

यौ whoever, धर्मौ two righteous entities, जगताम् of  the worlds, नेत्रौ two eyes, यत्र where, सर्वम् all, प्रतिष्ठितम् is resting, महाबलौ two very powerful, आदित्यचन्द्रौ Sun and Moon, ग्रहणम् eclipse, अभ्युपेतौ endures.

#### Explanation

The powerful Sun and Moon, righteous entities, who are the two eyes of these worlds and on whom everything rests firmly, also suffer eclipses sometimes.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

सुमहान्त्यपि भूतानि देवाश्च पुरुषर्षभ। न दैवस्य प्रमुञ्चन्ति सर्वभूतानि देहिनः।।3.66.11।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पुरुषर्षभ O bull among men, सुमहन्ति भूतानि great beings, देवाश्च gods, सर्वभूतानि all creatures, देहिनः mortals, दैवस्य fate, न प्रमुञ्चन्ति cannot be released.

#### Explanation

O best among men  Fate spares none. Great beings, every creature, even the gods cannot escape fate.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

शक्रादिष्वपि देवेषु वर्तमानौ नयानयौ। श्रूयेते नरशार्दूल न त्वं शोचितुमर्हसि।।3.66.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

नरशार्दूल O tiger among men, शक्रादिषु among beings such as Indra and others, देवेषु deities, नयानयौ justice or otherwise, वर्तमानौ are present, श्रूयेते heard, त्वम् you, शोचितुम् to worry, नार्हसि not right for you.

#### Explanation

It is I heard that Indra and other deities are also governed by the principle of justice. Therefore, you should not bewail, O tiger among men.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

नष्टायामपि वैदेह्यां हृतायामपि चानघ शोचितुं नार्हसे वीर यथान्यः प्राकृतस्तथा।।3.66.13।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अनघ O sinless soul, वीर hero, वैदेह्याम् when Vaidehi, नष्टायामपि killed, हृतायामपि when  carried off too, प्राकृतः an ordinary person, अन्यः यथा other man, शोचितुम् to worry, नार्हसि not right for you.

#### Explanation

Even if Vaidehi is killed or abducted, you should not grieve like ordinary men, O sinless hero

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

त्वव्दिधा न हि शोचन्ति सततं सत्यदर्शिनः। सुमहत्स्वपि कृच्छ्रेषु रामानिर्विण्णदर्शनाः।।3.66.14।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राम Rama, सततम् always, सत्यदर्शिनः those who see the truth, अनिर्विण्णदर्शनाः men whose vision is free from depression, त्वव्दिधाः men like you, सुमहत्सु in very great, क्रुच्छ्रेष्वपि even in great calamities, न शोचन्ति हि will not lose heart.

#### Explanation

O Rama, you always see the truth. So  in the face of great difficulties men like you, whose vision is not blurred, should not lose heart.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

तत्त्वतो हि नरश्रेष्ठ बुद्ध्या समनुचिन्तय। बुध्द्या युक्ता महाप्राज्ञा विजानन्ति शुभाशुभे।।3.66.15।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

नरश्रेष्ठ O best among men, तत्त्वतः the facts, बुद्ध्या applying your intellect, समनुचिन्तय think rightly, महाप्राज्ञाः very wise, बुध्द्या with their intellect, युक्ताः endowed, शुभाशुभे right and wrong, विजानन्ति people know.

#### Explanation

O best among men  judge the facts rightly with your intellect. The wise apply their minds and know what is right and what is wrong.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

अदृष्टगुणदोषाणामध्रुवाणां तु कर्मणाम्। नान्तरेण क्रियां तेषां फलमिष्टं प्रवर्तते।।3.66.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अदृष्टगुणदोषाणाम् of those who do not know their merits and demerits, अध्रुवाणाम् unstable, तेषाम् in this, कर्मणाम् deeds, इष्टम् good, फलम् results, क्रियाम् अन्तरेण from actions, न प्रवर्तते will not be obtained.

#### Explanation

Good results are not expected from the actions of persons who cannot distinguish between their virtues and vices, from those who are unstable in their deeds.

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

त्वमेव हि पुरा राम मामेवं बहुशोऽन्वशाः। अनुशिष्याद्धि को नु त्वामपि साक्षाद्बृहस्पतिः।।3.66.17।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राम Rama, पुरा in the past, त्वमेव you yourself, माम् to me, एवम् that way, बहुशः many times, अन्वशाः you instructed, हि indeed, साक्षात् personally, बृहस्पतिरपि even Brihaspati, को नु who indeed, त्वाम् to you, अनुशिष्यात् हि can teach you.

#### Explanation

O Rama you yourself in the past have instructed me many times that way. Who can be your adviser in this matter ? You are indeed like Brihaspati.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

बुद्धिश्च ते महाप्राज्ञ देवैरपि दुरन्वया। शोकेनाभिप्रसुप्तं ते ज्ञानं सम्बोधयाम्यहम्।।3.66.18।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महाप्राज्ञ very wise, ते बुद्धि: your intellect, देवैरपि gods too, दुरन्वया difficult to fathom, शोकेन by sorrow, अभिप्रसुप्तम् put to sleep, ते ज्ञानम् your wisdom, अहम् I am, सम्बोधयामि arousing you.

#### Explanation

Even gods cannot fathom your intellect. I am only trying to rouse your wisdom your sorrow has sent to sleep.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

दिव्यं च मानुषं च त्वमात्मनश्च पराक्रमम्। इक्ष्वाकुवृषभावेक्ष्य यतस्व द्विषतां वधे।।3.66.19।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

इक्ष्वाकुवृषभ among the Ikshvakus, त्वम् you, दिव्यम् divine, मानुषं च mortal, आत्मनः yourself, पराक्रमम् powers, अवेक्ष्य after knowing, द्विषताम् of the enemies, वधे in killing, यतस्व make efforts.

#### Explanation

O bull among the Ikshvakus, weigh your divine and human prowess before you try to kill your enemies.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

किं तेन सर्वनाशेन कृतेन पुरुषर्षभ। तमेव त्वं रिपुं पापं विज्ञायोद्धर्तुमर्हसि।।3.66.20।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पुरुषर्षभ best among men, कृतेन by the act of, तेन by that, सर्वनाशेन destruction of all, किम् what for, त्वम् you, पापम् sinful, तं रिपुम् एव your enemy, विज्ञाय after knowing, उद्धर्तुम् to uproot, अर्हसि ought to.

#### Explanation

O best among men  where is the good in the destruction of all? Know your sinful enemy before you uproot him. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकेय आदिकाव्ये अरण्यकाण्डे षट्षष्टितमस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the sixtysixth sarga of Aranyakanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 67

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

पूर्वजोऽप्युक्तमात्रस्तु लक्ष्मणेन सुभाषितम्। सारग्राही महासारं प्रतिजग्राह राघवः।।3.67.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सारग्राही who can grasp the essence, राघवः Rama, पूर्वज अपि being elder, लक्ष्मणेन by Lakshmana, उक्तमात्रः तु only when told by, महासारम् the great essence, सुभाषितम् wise counsel, प्रतिजग्राह accepted.

#### Explanation

Even though Rama was elder, he could grasp the quintessence of the wise counsel only after Lakshmana had said it.

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

सन्निगृह्य महाबाहुः प्रवृत्तं कोपमात्मनः। अवष्टभ्य धनुश्चित्रं रामो लक्ष्मणमब्रवीत्।।3.67.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महाबाहुः longarmed, रामः Rama, प्रवृत्तम् risen, आत्मनः his own, कोपम् anger, सन्निगृह्य after restraining, चित्रम् wonderful, धनुः bow, अवष्टभ्य leaning over, लक्ष्मणम् to Lakshmana, अब्रवीत् said.

#### Explanation

Restraining his anger, the longarmed Rama leaned over  his wonderful bow and spoke to Lakshmana:

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

किं करिष्यावहे वत्स क्व वा गच्छाव लक्ष्मण। केनोपायेन गच्छेयं सीतामिति विचिन्तय।।3.67.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वत्स O child, लक्ष्मण Lakshmana, किम् what, करिष्यावहे shall we do, क्व वा where do we, गच्छाव will go, सीताम् Sita, केन उपायेन by what means, गच्छेयम् may I reach, इति this, विचिन्तय think.

#### Explanation

O dear Lakshmana, what shall we do? Where do we go ? Think of some means by which we can reach Sita.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

तं तथा परितापार्तं लक्ष्मणो राममब्रवीत्। इदमेव जनस्थानं त्वमन्वेषितुमर्हसि।।3.67.4।। राक्षसैर्बहुभिः कीर्णं नानाद्रुमलतायुतम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तथा so, परितापार्तम् afflicted with grief, तं रामम् that Rama, लक्ष्मणः Lakshman, अब्रवीत् said, त्वम् you, बहुभिः many, राक्षसैः by demons, कीर्णम् full of, नानाद्रुमलतायुतम् covered with different trees and creepers, इमम् this, जनस्थानमेव Janasthna alone, अन्वेषितुम् to search, अर्हसि you should.

#### Explanation

Lakshmana said to Rama, afflicted with grief, It is proper for you to (first) search in this Janasthana, covered with different kinds of trees and creepers, and teeming with demons.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

सन्तीह गिरिदुर्गाणि निर्दराः कन्दराणि च।।3.67.5।। गुहाश्च विविधा घोरा नानामृगगणाकुलाः। आवासाः किन्नराणां च गन्धर्वभवनानि च।।3.67.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

इह here, गिरिदुर्गाणि difficult mountains, निर्दराः valleys, कन्दराणि च and caves, विविधाः many, घोराः terrific, नानामृगगणाकुलाः inhabited by different animals, गुहाश्च caverns, किन्नराणाम् of kinneras, आवासाः dwellingplaces, गन्धर्वभवनानि च mansions of gandharvas, सन्ति are.

#### Explanation

Here are difficult mountains, caves, valleys, dreadful caves full of different animals, dwellingplaces of kinneras and mansions of gandharvas.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

सन्तीह गिरिदुर्गाणि निर्दराः कन्दराणि च।।3.67.5।। गुहाश्च विविधा घोरा नानामृगगणाकुलाः। आवासाः किन्नराणां च गन्धर्वभवनानि च।।3.67.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

इह here, गिरिदुर्गाणि difficult mountains, निर्दराः valleys, कन्दराणि च and caves, विविधाः many, घोराः terrific, नानामृगगणाकुलाः inhabited by different animals, गुहाश्च caverns, किन्नराणाम् of kinneras, आवासाः dwellingplaces, गन्धर्वभवनानि च mansions of gandharvas, सन्ति are.

#### Explanation

Here are difficult mountains, caves, valleys, dreadful caves full of different animals, dwellingplaces of kinneras and mansions of gandharvas.

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

तानि युक्तो मया सार्धं त्वमन्वेषितुमर्हसि। त्वद्विधा बुद्धिसम्पन्ना महात्मानो नरर्षभ।।3.67.7।। आपत्सु न प्रकम्पन्ते वायुवेगैरिवाचलाः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

त्वम् you, युक्तः accompanied, मया सार्धम् along with me, तानि those, अन्वेषितुम् to search, अर्हसि it is proper for you, नरर्षभ: great men, बुद्धिसम्पन्नाः endowed with wisdom, त्वद्धिधाः of your type, महात्मानः great people, वायुवेगैः speed of wind, अचलाः इव like mountains, न प्रकम्पन्ते are not shaken.

#### Explanation

Accompanied by me you should search for her in those spots. Great people endowed with wisdom do not destabilise in difficulties just as mountains are not shaken by the speed of the wind.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

इत्युक्तस्तद्वनं सर्वं विचचार सलक्ष्मणः।।3.67.8।। क्रुद्धो रामश्शरं घोरं सन्धाय धनुषि क्षुरम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

क्रुद्धः becoming angry, रामः Rama, इति thus, उक्तः having been told, सलक्ष्मणः along with Lakskhmana , धनुषि in bow, घोरम् terrific, क्षुरं शरम् sharp arrows, सन्धाय fixing, सर्वम् all, तत् वनम् that forest, विचचार roamed.

#### Explanation

Thus advised, the angry Rama fixed a sharp arrow to his bow and roamed in the forest accompanied by Lakshmana.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

ततः पर्वतकूटाभं महाभागं द्विजोत्तमम्।।3.67.9।। ददर्श पतितं भूमौ क्षतजार्द्रं जटायुषम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, पर्वतकूटाभम् like a mountain top, महाभागम् great, द्विजोत्तमम् a wonderful bird, भूमौ on the ground, पतितम् fallen down, क्षतजार्द्रम् drenched with blood, जटायुषम्  Jatayu, ददर्श saw.

#### Explanation

Then he saw the great Jatayu, a wonderful bird, looking like a mountain top, drenched in blood and fallen on the ground.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

तं दृष्ट्वा गिरिशृङ्गाभं रामो लक्ष्मणमब्रवीत्।।3.67.10।। अनेन सीता वैदेही भक्षिता नात्र संशयः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

गिरिशृङ्गाभम् like a peak of the mountain, तम् him, दृष्ट्वा after seeing, रामः Rama, लक्ष्मणम् to Lakshmana, अब्रवीत् said, अनेन by him, वैदेही Vaidehi, सीता Sita, भक्षिता is eaten, अत्र in this case, संशयः doubt, न no.

#### Explanation

On seeing Jatayu looking like the peak of a mountain, Rama said to Lakshmana, There is no doubt that this bird has devoured Vaidehi.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

गृध्ररूपमिदं रक्षो व्यक्तं भवति कानने।।3.67.11।। भक्षयित्वा विशालाक्षीमास्ते सीतां यथासुखम्। एनं वधिष्ये दीप्तास्यैर्घोरैर्बाणैरजिह्मगैः।।3.67.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

इदम् this, रक्षः demon, गृध्ररूपम् in the guise of a vulture, कानने in the forest, भवति is, व्यक्तम् it is clear, विशालाक्षीम् largeeyed lady, सीताम् Sita, भक्षयित्वा after eating away, यथासुखम् happily, आस्ते relaxing, दीप्तास्यैः glowing tipped, घोरैः terrific, अजिह्मगैः by those which can go straight, बाणैः by darts, एनम् him, वधिष्ये l will kill.

#### Explanation

This demon in the guise of a vulture has evidently eaten away the largeeyed Sita and is happily relaxing in the forest. With my terrific arrows with burning tips that can go straight I will kill him.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

गृध्ररूपमिदं रक्षो व्यक्तं भवति कानने।।3.67.11।। भक्षयित्वा विशालाक्षीमास्ते सीतां यथासुखम्। एनं वधिष्ये दीप्तास्यैर्घोरैर्बाणैरजिह्मगैः।।3.67.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

इदम् this, रक्षः demon, गृध्ररूपम् in the guise of a vulture, कानने in the forest, भवति is, व्यक्तम् it is clear, विशालाक्षीम् largeeyed lady, सीताम् Sita, भक्षयित्वा after eating away, यथासुखम् happily, आस्ते relaxing, दीप्तास्यैः glowing tipped, घोरैः terrific, अजिह्मगैः by those which can go straight, बाणैः by darts, एनम् him, वधिष्ये l will kill.

#### Explanation

This demon in the guise of a vulture has evidently eaten away the largeeyed Sita and is happily relaxing in the forest. With my terrific arrows with burning tips that can go straight I will kill him.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

इत्युक्त्वाभ्यपतद्गृध्रं सन्धाय धनुषि क्षुरम्। क्रुद्धो रामस्समुद्रान्तां कम्पयन्निव मेदिनीम्।।3.67.13।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रामः Rama, इति thus, उक्त्वा saying so, क्रुद्धः in anger, धनुषि on the bow, क्षुरम् sharp  arrow, सन्धाय after fixing, समुद्रान्ताम् extending up to the sea, मेदिनीम् the earth, कम्पयन्निव as though shaking, गृध्रम् vulture, अभ्यपतत् fell upon.

#### Explanation

Having siad so, Rama took his bow, fixed the sharp arrow, and aimed at the vulture. The seabound earth trembled.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

तं दीनं दीनया वाचा सफेनं रुधिरं वमन्। अभ्यभाषत पक्षी तु रामं दशरथात्मजम्।।3.67.14।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पक्षी तु but the bird, सफेनम् foamy, रुधिरम् blood, वमन् vomitting, दीनम् in a piteous way, तं him, दशरथात्मजम् Dasaratha's son, रामम् Rama, दीनया pathetic, वाचा with word, अभ्यभाषत spoke these words.

#### Explanation

But the vulture who was in a piteous state, vomitting foamy blood, spoke these words pathetically to Rama, son of Dasaratha:

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

यामोषधिमिवायुष्मन्नन्वेषसि महावने। सा देवी मम च प्राणा रावणेनोभयं हृतम्।।3.67.15।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

आयुष्मन् longlived one, याम् whom, महावने in this great forest, ओषधिमिव like herbal medicine, अन्वेषसि you are searching , सा देवी that queen, मम my, प्राणाः च life also, उभयम् both, रावणेन by Ravana, हृतम् is taken away.

#### Explanation

O Longlived one  she who you are searching in the forest like herbal medicine has been carried away by Ravana along with my life.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

त्वया विरहिता देवी लक्ष्मणेन च राघव। ह्रियमाणा मया दृष्टा रावणेन बलीयसा।।3.67.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राघव Rama, त्वया by you, लक्ष्मणेन च and by Lakshmana, विरहिता separated, देवी qeeen, बलीयसा by the powerful, रावणेन by Ravana, ह्रियमाणा taken away, मया I have, दृष्टा seen.

#### Explanation

I have seen her snatched from you and from Lakshmana and carried off by powerful Ravana.

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

सीतामभ्यवपन्नोऽहं रावणश्च रणे मया। विध्वंसितरथश्चात्र पातितो धरणीतले।।3.67.17।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अहम् I, सीताम् Sita, अभ्यवपन्नः descended down, मया by me, रावणश्च Ravana, विध्वंसितरथः with his chariot destroyed, अत्र in this place, धरणीतले on this ground, पातितः I fell down.

#### Explanation

I descended down to protect Sita, and in the battle with Ravana his chariot destroyed fell down here on the ground.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

एतदस्य धनुर्भग्नमेतदस्य शरावरम्। अयमस्य रथो राम भग्नसाङ्ग्रामिको मया।।3.67.18।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राम Rama, एतत् this, अस्य his, भग्नम् broken, धनुः bow, एतत् this, अस्य his, शरावरम् quiver, अयम् this, मया by me, भग्नः shattered, अस्य his, साङ्ग्रामिकः battle, रथः chariot.

#### Explanation

O Rama this is his broken bow, this is his quiver and this is his warchariot all devastated by me.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

अयं तु सारथिस्तस्य मत्पक्षनिहतो युधि। परिश्रान्तस्य मे पक्षौ छित्त्वा खड्गेन रावणः।।3.67.19।। सीतामादाय वैदेहीमुत्पपात विहायसम्। रक्षसा निहतं पूर्वं न मां हन्तुं त्वमर्हसि।।3.67.20।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अयं तु he is, युधि in the battle, मत्पक्षनिहतः killed by my wings, तस्य सारथिः his charioteer, रावणः Ravana, परिश्रान्तस्य an exhausted one, मे my, पक्षौ both wings, खड्गेन with a sword, छित्त्वा having cut, वैदेहीम् Vaidehi, सीताम् Sita, आदाय after taking, विहायसम् into the sky, उत्पपात flew up, रक्षसा by Ravana, पूर्वम् earlier, निहतम् slain, माम् me, त्वम् you, हन्तुम् to kill, नार्हसि not proper.

#### Explanation

He is Ravana's charioteer slain by my wings in the battle.  As I was exhausted in the encounter Ravana clipped my wings,  took Sita, and flew into the sky. You need not kill me. I am already slain by the demon.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

अयं तु सारथिस्तस्य मत्पक्षनिहतो युधि। परिश्रान्तस्य मे पक्षौ छित्त्वा खड्गेन रावणः।।3.67.19।। सीतामादाय वैदेहीमुत्पपात विहायसम्। रक्षसा निहतं पूर्वं न मां हन्तुं त्वमर्हसि।।3.67.20।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अयं तु he is, युधि in the battle, मत्पक्षनिहतः killed by my wings, तस्य सारथिः his charioteer, रावणः Ravana, परिश्रान्तस्य an exhausted one, मे my, पक्षौ both wings, खड्गेन with a sword, छित्त्वा having cut, वैदेहीम् Vaidehi, सीताम् Sita, आदाय after taking, विहायसम् into the sky, उत्पपात flew up, रक्षसा by Ravana, पूर्वम् earlier, निहतम् slain, माम् me, त्वम् you, हन्तुम् to kill, नार्हसि not proper.

#### Explanation

He is Ravana's charioteer slain by my wings in the battle.  As I was exhausted in the encounter Ravana clipped my wings,  took Sita, and flew into the sky. You need not kill me. I am already slain by the demon.

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

रामस्तस्य तु विज्ञाय बाष्पपूर्णमुखस्तदा। द्विगुणीकृततापार्तस्सीतासक्तां प्रियां कथाम्।।3.67.21।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तदा then, रामः Rama, तस्य his, सीतासक्ताम् about Sita, प्रियाम् dear, कथाम् facts, विज्ञाय having, बाष्पपूर्णमुखः face full of tears, द्विगुणीकृततापार्तः  helpless and pitiable due to redoubled sorrow.

#### Explanation

Having known from Jatayu the story of his beloved Sita, Rama was full of tears, his grief redoubled.

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

गृध्रराजं परिष्वज्य परित्यज्य महद्धनुः। निपपातावशो भूमौ रुरोद सहलक्ष्मणः।।3.67.22।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महत् great, धनुः bow, परित्यज्य giving up, गृध्रराजम् king of birds, परिष्वज्य embracing, अवशः despondent, भूमौ on the ground, निपपात fell down, सहलक्ष्मणः along with Lakshmana, रुरोद cried.

#### Explanation

Rama dropped the bow and embraced the king of birds. He fell down despondent on the ground and began crying along with Lakshmana.

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

एकमेकायने दुर्गे निश्श्वसन्तं कथञ्चन। समीक्ष्य दुःखिततरो रामस्सौमित्रिमब्रवीत्।।3.67.23।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

दुर्गे in a place difficult to reach, एकायने in a solitary place, एकम् lonely, कथञ्चन with great difficulty, निश्श्वसन्तम् sighing, समीक्ष्य on observing, रामः Rama, दुःखिततरः overcome by greater grief, सौमित्रिम् Saumitri, अब्रवीत् said.

#### Explanation

Rama saw Jatayu breathing with difficulty in that solitary, inaccessible place.Overcome by greater grief  he said to Lakshmana:

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

राज्यं भ्रष्टं वने वासस्सीता नष्टा हतो द्विजः। ईदृशीयं ममालक्ष्मीर्निर्दहेदपि पावकम्।।3.67.24।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राज्यम् kingdom, भ्रष्टम् expelled, वने forest, वासः dwelling, सीता Sita, नष्टा lost, द्विजः Jatayu, हतः is slain, मम me, ईदृशी this kind of, अलक्ष्मीः bad luck, पावकमपि even fire, निर्दहेत्  it will burn.

#### Explanation

I have been expelled from the kingdom. I am made to stay in the forest. I have lost Sita (my beloved wife). This bird (who was my wellwisher) has been slain. Such misfortune will burn even fire.

---

### Shloka 25

#### Shloka Text

सम्पूर्णमपि चेदद्य प्रविशेयं महोदधिम्। सोऽपि नूनं ममालक्ष्म्या विशुष्येत्सरितांपतिः।।3.67.25।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अद्य now, सम्पूर्णम् full, महोदधिम् अपि even the great sea also, प्रविशेयं चेत् if I enter, सः that, सरितां पतिः अपि even the lord of rivers, sea, मम me, अलक्ष्मा misfortune, विशुष्येत् will dry up.

#### Explanation

Now if I enter even the great sea, the lord of the rivers filled with water, it will also dry up due to my misfortune.

---

### Shloka 26

#### Shloka Text

नास्त्यभाग्यतरो लोके मत्तोऽस्मिन्सचराचरे। येनेयं महती प्राप्ता मया व्यसनवागुरा।।3.67.26।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

येन मया by me this way, इयम् this kind, महती great, व्यसनवागुरा snare of misfortunes, प्राप्ता attained, मत्तः more than me, अभाग्यतरः more unlucky, सचराचरे among animate and inanimate, अस्मिन् in this, लोके world, नास्ति not there.

#### Explanation

In this world of the animate and the inanimate there is no one more unfortunate than me fallen into the trap of adversities.

---

### Shloka 27

#### Shloka Text

अयं पितृवयस्यो मे गृध्रराजो जरान्वितः। शेते विनिहतो भूमौ मम भाग्यविपर्ययात्।।3.67.27।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मे my, पितृवयस्यः father's friend, जरान्वितः in old age, अयम् he is, गृध्रराजः king of birds, मम of me, भाग्यविपर्ययात् by my misfortune, विनिहतः was slain, भूमौ ground, शेते lying.

#### Explanation

This aged king of birds is my father's friend. To my illluck, he is struck down and is lying on the ground.

---

### Shloka 28

#### Shloka Text

इत्येवमुक्त्वा बहुशो राघवस्सहलक्ष्मणः। जटायुषं च पस्पर्श पितृस्नेहं विदर्शयन्।।3.67.28।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राघवः Rama, इत्येवम् in this manner, बहुशः many times, उक्त्वा after telling, सहलक्ष्मणः along with Lakshmana, पितृस्नेहम् filiat affection, विदर्शयन् showing, जटायुषम् Jatayu, पस्पर्श caressed.

#### Explanation

Saying such words and showing filial affection, he caressed Jatayu repeatedly with Lakshmana.

---

### Shloka 29

#### Shloka Text

निकृत्तपक्षं रुधिरावसिक्तं स गृध्रराजं परिरभ्य रामः। क्व मैथिली प्राणसमा ममेति विमुच्य वाचं निपपात भूमौ।।3.67.29।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः रामः that Rama, निकृत्तपक्षम् severed wings, रुधिरावसिक्तम् drenched in blood, गृध्रराजम् king of vultures, परिरभ्य hugging, मम my, प्राणसमा equal to my life, मैथिली Maithili, क्व where, इति this, वाचम् speaking, विमुच्य भूमौ leaving the ground, निपपात fell down.

#### Explanation

Rama hugged the king of vultures with servered wings, drenched in blood and asking him where Sita who was as dear to him as his life was, he dropped him and fell down on the ground. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये अरण्यकाण्डे सप्तषष्टितमस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the sixtyseventh sarga of Aranyakanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 68

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

रामस्सम्प्रेक्ष्य तं गृध्रं भुवि रौद्रेण पातितम्। सौमित्रिं मित्रसम्पन्नमिदं वचनमब्रवीत्।।3.68.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रौद्रेण by fierce, भुवि ground, पातितम्  thrown down, तं गृध्रम् that vulture, प्रेक्ष्य seeing, रामः Rama, मित्रसम्पन्नम् friendly, सौमित्रिम् Lakshmana, इदं वचनम् these words, अब्रवीत् spoke.

#### Explanation

Seeing the vulture lying on the ground thrown down by the fierce demon, Rama said to friendly Lakshmana:

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

ममायं नूनमर्थेषु यतमानो विहङ्गमः। राक्षसेन हतस्संख्ये प्राणांस्त्यक्ष्यति दुस्त्यजान्।।3.68.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मम my, अर्थेषु in my objective, यतमानः while he is making efforts, अयं विहङ्गमः this bird, सङ्ख्ये in battle, राक्षसेन by demon, हतः struck, दुस्त्यजान्  hard to leave, प्राणान् life, त्यक्ष्यति going to give up, नूनम् surely.

#### Explanation

Struck down by the demon in the combat on my account this bird is going to give up his life which is indeed difficult.

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

अयमस्य शरीरेऽस्मिन्प्राणो लक्ष्मण विद्यते। तथा हि स्वरहीनोऽयं विक्लबस्समुदीक्ष्यते।।3.68.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लक्ष्मण Lakshmana, अस्य his, अस्मिन् शरीरे in this body, प्राणः life, (अतिखिन्नः suffering from intense pain), विद्यते appears, तथा हि like that, अयम् he, स्वरहीनः faint voice, विक्लबः frightened, समुदीक्ष्यते feeling excruciating pain.

#### Explanation

O Lakshmana, it appears he is frightened and is experiencing excruciating pain in the body. His voice sounds faint, too.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

जटायो यदि शक्नोषि वाक्यं व्याहरितुं पुनः। सीतामाख्याहि भद्रं ते वधमाख्याहि चात्मनः।।3.68.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

जटायो Jatayu, पुनः again, व्याहरितुम् to speak, शक्नोषि यदि if you are able, ते भद्रम् be blessed, सीताम् about Sita, अख्याहि tell, आत्मनः yourself, वधम् striking, आख्याहि tell.

#### Explanation

O Jatayu If you are still able to speak, tell me about Sita and about how you have been struck down.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

किं निमित्तोऽहरत्सीतां रावणस्तस्य किं मया। अपराद्धं तु यं दृष्ट्वा रावणेन हृता प्रिया।।3.68.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रावणः Ravana, किं निमित्तः for what reason, सीताम् Sita, अहरत् abducted, मया by me, तस्य his, किम् what, अपराद्धम्  crime, यम् which, दृष्ट्वा seeing, प्रिया beloved, हृता taken?

#### Explanation

Why did Ravana abduct Sita? What fault did he find with me so that he kidnapped my beloved ?

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

कथं तच्चन्द्रसङ्काशं मुखमासीन्मनोहरम्। सीतया कानि चोक्तानि तस्मिन्काले द्विजोत्तम।।3.68.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

द्विजोत्तम best of birds, चन्द्रसङ्काशम् Moon like, मनोहरम् delightful, तत् मुखम् her face, कथम् how, आसीत् it was, तस्मिन् काले at the time of abduction, सीतया by Sita,  कानि what, उक्तानि did she say?

#### Explanation

O best of birds how did her delightful, Moonlike face look when she was hijacked. What did she say at that time?

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

कथं वीर्यः कथं रूपः किं कर्मा स च राक्षसः। क्व चास्य भवनं तात ब्रूहि मे परिपृच्छतः।।3.68.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः राक्षसः the demon, कथं वीर्यः how powerful, कथं रूपः how did he look, किं कर्मा what he does, अस्य his, भवनं च mansion also, क्व च where, तात dear, परिपृच्छतः I am asking you, मे to me, ब्रूहि please tell.

#### Explanation

O father  how powerful is that demon ? How does he look?What work does he do? Where does he dwell? Do answer my questions?

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

तमुद्वीक्ष्याथ दीनात्मा विलपन्तमनन्तरम्। वाचातिसन्नया रामं जटायुरिदमब्रवीत्।।3.68.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अनन्तरम् incessantly, दीनात्मा a piteous soul, जटायुः Jatayu, विलपन्तम् wailing, रामम् to Rama, उद्वीक्ष्य seeing, अथ this, अतिसन्नया in a faint voice, वाचा spoke, इदम् these, अब्रवीत् said.

#### Explanation

Thereafter Jatayu, a piteous soul, looked up at Rama who was  crying incessantly and said this in a feeble voice:

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

हृता सा राक्षसेन्द्रेण रावणेन विहायसा। मायामास्थाय विपुलां वातदुर्दिनसङ्कुलाम्।।3.68.9।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सा she, राक्षसेन्द्रेण by the lord of demons, रावणेन by Ravana, वातदुर्दिनसङ्कुलाम् violent wind, विपुलाम् huge, मायाम् magic, आस्थाय creating, विहायसा in the sky, हृता taken away.

#### Explanation

Ravana, the lord of the demons created violent winds through terrific magic and whisked her away in the sky.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

परिश्रान्तस्य मे तात पक्षौ छित्वा स राक्षसः। सीतामादाय वैदेहीं प्रयातो दक्षिणां दिशम्।।3.68.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तात O my child, सः राक्षसः that demon, परिश्रान्तस्य when I was exhausted, मे myself, पक्षौ wings, छित्त्वा after cutting, वैदेहीम् princess of Videha, सीताम् Sita, आदाय taking,दक्षिणां दिशम् southern, प्रयातः went.

#### Explanation

O my child  when I was exhausted, that demon cut off my wings and carried Sita off in the southern direction.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

उपरुध्यन्ति मे प्राणा दृष्टिर्भ्रमति राघव। पश्यामि वृक्षान्सौवर्णानुशीरकृतमूर्धजान्।।3.68.11।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राघव Rama, मे my, प्राणाः life, उपरुध्यन्ति obstructing, दृष्टिः sight, भ्रमति is reeling, अनुशीरकृतमूर्धजान् root hair grown on tops, सौवर्णान् golden , वृक्षान् trees, पश्यामि I see.

#### Explanation

O scion of the Raghu race, I am gasping for breath. My eyes are reeling. I see root hair growing on the tops of golden trees.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

येन याति मुहूर्तेन सीतामादाय रावणः। विप्रणष्टं धनं क्षिप्रं तत्स्वामी प्रतिपद्यते।।3.68.12।। विन्दो नाम मुहूर्तोऽयं स च काकुत्स्थ नाबुधत्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

काकुत्स्थ O Rama, रावणः Ravana, येन मुहूर्तेन by such moment, सीताम् Sita, आदाय taking, याति he, अयम् this, विन्द: Vinda, नाम by name, मुहूर्तः time, विनष्टम् lost, धनम् wealth, तत्स्वामी that person, क्षिप्रम् quickly, प्रतिपद्यते will obtain, सः च and he, नाबुधत् did not know.

#### Explanation

The time Ravana kidnapped Sita is known as 'Vinda'. The effect (of that time), is that her husband will regain his lost wealth. O scion of the Kakutstha race  he (Ravana )did not know it.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

त्वत्प्रियां जानकीं हृत्वा रावणो राक्षसेश्वरः।।3.68.13।। झषवद्बडिशं गृह्य क्षिप्रमेव विनश्यति।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षसेश्वरः lord of the demons, रावणः Ravana, त्वत्प्रियाम् your beloved, जानकीम् Janaki, हृत्वा after carrying away, बडिशम् fish hook, गृह्य having picked up, झषवत् like the fish, क्षिप्रमेव quickly, विनश्यति will be destroyed.

#### Explanation

Ravana, king of the demons, will perish soon for running away with your beloved Janaki just like a fish carrying away a fishhook (which causes its destruction).

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

न च त्वया व्यथा कार्या जनकस्य सुतां प्रति।।3.68.14।। वैदेह्या रंस्यसे क्षिप्रं हत्वा तं राक्षसं रणे।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

त्वया by you, जनकस्य सुतां Janaka's daughter, प्रति with regard to, व्यथा worry, न कार्या need not be, रणे in battle, तं राक्षसम् that demon, क्षिप्रम् soon, हत्वा having slain, वैदेह्या with Vaidehi, रंस्यसे will sport.

#### Explanation

You need not worry about Janaka's daughter. You will soon enjoy the company of your beloved, Vaidehi after killing that demon.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

असम्मूढस्य गृध्रस्य रामं प्रत्यनुभाषतः।।3.68.15।। आस्यात्सुस्राव रुधिरं म्रियमाणस्य सामिषम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रामं प्रति with Rama, अनुभाषतः conversing, असम्मूढस्य of the bird that was not deluded, म्रियमाणस्य while dying, गृध्रस्य vulture's, आस्यात् from his mouth, सामिषम् mixed with flesh, रुधिरम् blood, सुस्राव dripped.

#### Explanation

As he(Jatayu) was speaking to Rama with an alert mind even while dying, blood mixed with flesh started oozing from Jatayu's mouth.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

पुत्रो विश्रवसस्साक्षाद्भ्राता वैश्रवणस्य च।।3.68.16।। इत्युक्त्वा दुर्लभान्प्राणान्मुमोच पतगेश्वरः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

विश्रवसः Visrava's, पुत्रः son, वैश्रवणस्य साक्षात् Vaisravana's lawful, भ्राता brother, इति thus, उक्त्वा said, पतगेश्वरः Jatayu, the king of birds, दुर्लभान्  rare, प्राणान् life, मुमोच gave up.

#### Explanation

Ravana was Visrava's son and Vaisravana's (Kubera's) brother, said Jatayu and gave up his rare life.

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

ब्रूहि ब्रूहीति रामस्य ब्रुवाणस्य कृताञ्जलेः।।3.68.17।। त्वक्त्वा शरीरं गृध्रस्य जग्मुः प्राणा विहायसम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कृताञ्जलेः waiting with folded palms, रामस्य Rama's, ब्रुहि ब्रुहि इति saying 'tell me, tell me', ब्रुवाणस्य as he spoke so, गृध्रस्य of the vulture, प्राणाः life, शरीरम् body, त्वक्त्वा after leaving, विहायसम् to the sky, जग्मुः went.

#### Explanation

As Rama was saying to the vulture with folded palms 'Tell me, tell me'( about Ravana) the life breath of the vulture soared into the sky leaving the body.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

स निक्षिप्य शिरो भूमौ प्रसार्य चरणौ तदा।।3.68.18।। विक्षिप्य च शरीरं स्वं पपात धरणीतले।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः Jatayu, तदा then, शिरः head, भूमौ on the ground, निक्षिप्य after throwing, चरणौ feet, प्रसार्य stretching, स्वम् his, शरीरम् body, धरणीतले on the ground, विक्षिप्य scattering, पपात fallen down.

#### Explanation

Then Jatayu dropped his head down, his feet thrown about, his body outstretched on earth.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

तं गृध्रं प्रेक्ष्य ताम्राक्षं गतासुमचलोपमम्।।3.68.19।। रामस्सुबहुभिर्दुःखैर्दीनस्सौमित्रिमब्रवीत्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सुबहुभिः with much, दुःखै: with sorrow, दीनः dejected, रामः Rama, ताम्राक्षम् eyes red (with tears), गतासुम् with his life gone, अचलोपमम् looking like a mountain, तं गृध्रम् to that vulture, प्रेक्ष्य  seeing, सौमित्रिम् to Lakshmana, अब्रवीत् said.

#### Explanation

Stricken with deep grief, his eyes red (with tears) Rama saw the vulture looking like a mountain. Seeing him dead, he said to Lakshmana:

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

बहूनि रक्षसां वासे वर्षाणि वसता सुखम्।।3.68.20।। अनेन दण्डकारण्ये विशीर्णमिह पक्षिणा।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रक्षसाम् the demon's, वासे dwellingplace, इह here, दण्डकारण्ये Dandaka forest, सुखम् happily, बहूनि many, वर्षाणि years, वसता living, अनेन by this, पक्षिणा by the bird, विशीर्णम् lost life.

#### Explanation

Dwelling for many years happily in Dandaka forest, the abode of demons, the bird lost his life (on our account).

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

अनेकवार्षिको यस्तु चिरकालसमुत्थितः।।3.68.21।। सोऽयमद्य हतश्शेते कालो हि दुरतिक्रमः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अनेकवार्षिकः many years old, यः he, चिरकालसमुत्थितः lived for a long time, सः अयम् this Jatayu, अद्य now, हतः dead, शेते lying down, कालः time (fate), दुरतिक्रमः हि not possible to escape.

#### Explanation

This Jatayu, who lived for long years, is lying dead now. (The dictate of) destiny is inescapable.

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

पश्य लक्ष्मण गृध्रोऽयमुपकारी हतश्च मे।।3.68.22।। सीतामभ्यवपन्नो वै रावणेन बलीयसा।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लक्ष्मण O Lakshmana, सीताम् Sita, अभ्यवपन्नः he reached out for help, मे उपकारी my wellwisher, अयं गृध्रः this vulture, बलीयसा by the powerful, रावणेन by Ravana, हतः is killed, पश्य see.

#### Explanation

O Lakshmana, see this Jatayu, my benefactor, killed by the powerful Ravana while he was reaching out to help Sita.

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

गृध्रराज्यं परित्यज्य पितृपैतामहं महत्।।3.68.23।। मम हेतोरयं प्राणान्मुमोच पतगेश्वरः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अयं पतगेश्वरः this lord of the birds, पितृपैतामहम् ancestral, महत् great, गृध्रराज्यम् kingdom of vultures, परित्यज्य  left, मम हेतोः for my sake, प्राणान् life, मुमोच gave up.

#### Explanation

This lord of the birds left his ancestral kingdom of viltures and gave up life for my sake.

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

सर्वत्र खलु दृश्यन्ते साधवो धर्मचारिणः।।3.68.24।। शूराश्शरण्यास्सौमित्रे तिर्यग्योनिगतेष्वपि।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सौमित्रे Lakshmana, शूराः heroes, शरण्याः protectors, धर्मचारिणः rigteous people, साधवः good people, सर्वत्र all over, तिर्यग्योनिगतेष्वपि even among animals and birds, दृश्यन्ते खलु are seen.

#### Explanation

O Lakshmana valiant souls and protectors, being righteous and honest are found even among animals and birds.

---

### Shloka 25

#### Shloka Text

सीताहरणजं दुःखं न मे सौम्य तथाविधम्।।3.68.25।। यथा विनाशे गृध्रस्य मत्कृते च परन्तप।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

परन्तप O scorcher of enemies, सौम्य handsome one, गृध्रस्य bird's, विनाशे at his death, मत्कृते च for my sake, यथा as, सीताहरणजम् caused by the abduction of Sita, दुःखम् grief, तथाविधम् in that way, न not experienced.

#### Explanation

O scorcher of enemies, O handsome Lakshmana the grief I experience due to Jatayu's death on my account is more intense than Sita's abduction.

---

### Shloka 26

#### Shloka Text

राजा दशरथश्श्रीमान्यथा मम महायशाः।।3.68.26।। पूजनीयश्च मान्यश्च तथाऽयं पतगेश्वरः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महायशाः illustrious, श्रीमान् prosperous, राजा king, दशरथः Dasaratha, मम for me, यथा as, पूजनीयश्च worthy of reverence, मान्यश्च worthy of honour, अयम् this, पतगेश्वरः lord of the birds, तथा in the same way.

#### Explanation

This lord of the birds for me is as worthy of reverence and honour as the famous and prosperous king Dasaratha.

---

### Shloka 27

#### Shloka Text

सौमित्रे हर काष्ठानि निर्मथिष्यामि पावकम्।।3.68.27।। गृध्रराजं दिधक्षामि मत्कृते निधनं गतम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सौमित्रे Saumitri, काष्ठानि firewood, हर fetch, पावकम् fire, निर्मथिष्यामि I will rub, मत्कृते for my sake, निधनम् गतम् died, गृध्रराजम् king of the birds, दिधक्षामि I wish to burn.

#### Explanation

O Saumitri  fetch firewood. I shall generate fire by rubbing them and cremate the king of the birds who has laid down his life for my sake.

---

### Shloka 28

#### Shloka Text

नाथं पतगलोकस्य चितामारोप्य राघव।।3.68.28।। इमं धक्ष्यामि सौमित्रे हतं रौद्रेण रक्षसा।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राघव O scion of the Raghu race, सौमित्रे Saumitri, रौद्रेण by the fierce, रक्षसा demon, हतम् killed, इमम् this, पतगलोकस्य नाथम् lord of the world of birds, चिताम् funeral pyre, आरोप्य placing, धक्ष्यामि I will cremate with honour.

#### Explanation

O scion of the Raghus  I will place the body of the lord of the world of birds, killed by the fierce demon, on the funeral pyre and crimate him with honour.

---

### Shloka 29

#### Shloka Text

या गतिर्यज्ञशीलानामाहिताग्नेश्च या गतिः।।3.68.29।। अपरावर्तिनां या च या च भूमिप्रदायिनाम्। मया त्वं समनुज्ञातो गच्छ लोकाननुत्तमान्।।3.68.30।। गृध्रराज महासत्त्व संस्कृतश्च मया व्रज।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महासत्त्व very strong, गृध्रराज king of the birds, मया by me, समनुज्ञातः permitted, त्वम् you, यज्ञशीलानाम of those who perform sacrifices, या such, गतिः resort, आहिताग्नेश्च for him who kindles sacrificial fires, या गतिः attainment, अपरावर्तिनाम् of those who beat no retreat (from battle), या च and such state, भूमिप्रदायिनाम् for those who offer land in charity, या च that state, गच्छ go, मया by me, संस्कृतः च purified, अनुत्तमान् best of, लोकान् worlds, व्रज depart.

#### Explanation

O mighty lord of the birds  by my grace attain the state of those who perform sacrifices, who kindle and maintain sacrificial fires, who beat no retreat from the battlefield and who offer land in charity. Purified by my offering of fire, you may depart for the best of the worlds.

---

### Shloka 30

#### Shloka Text

या गतिर्यज्ञशीलानामाहिताग्नेश्च या गतिः।।3.68.29।। अपरावर्तिनां या च या च भूमिप्रदायिनाम्। मया त्वं समनुज्ञातो गच्छ लोकाननुत्तमान्।।3.68.30।। गृध्रराज महासत्त्व संस्कृतश्च मया व्रज।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महासत्त्व very strong, गृध्रराज king of the birds, मया by me, समनुज्ञातः permitted, त्वम् you, यज्ञशीलानाम of those who perform sacrifices, या such, गतिः resort, आहिताग्नेश्च for him who kindles sacrificial fires, या गतिः attainment, अपरावर्तिनाम् of those who beat no retreat (from battle), या च and such state, भूमिप्रदायिनाम् for those who offer land in charity, या च that state, गच्छ go, मया by me, संस्कृतः च purified, अनुत्तमान् best of, लोकान् worlds, व्रज depart.

#### Explanation

O mighty lord of the birds  by my grace attain the state of those who perform sacrifices, who kindle and maintain sacrificial fires, who beat no retreat from the battlefield and who offer land in charity. Purified by my offering of fire, you may depart for the best of the worlds.

---

### Shloka 31

#### Shloka Text

एवमुक्त्वा चितां दीप्तामारोप्य पतगेश्वरम्।।3.68.31।। ददाह रामो धर्मात्मा स्वबन्धुमिव दुःखितः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

धर्मात्मा righteous, रामः Rama, एवम् in that way, उक्त्वा having said, पतगेश्वरम् lord of birds, चिताम् on the pyre, आरोप्य having laid, दुःखितः sorrowful, स्वबन्धुमिव like his own relation, ददाह cremated.

#### Explanation

Thus said, righteous Rama laid him on the pyre and sadly cremated the lord of the birds as one would do to his own relative.

---

### Shloka 32

#### Shloka Text

रामोऽथ सहसौमित्रिर्वनं गत्वा स वीर्यवान्।।3.68.32।। स्थूलान्हत्वा महारोहीननुतस्तार तं द्विजम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अथ then, वीर्यवान् mighty, रामः Rama, सहसौमित्रिः with Saumitri, वनम् forest, गत्वा after going, स्थूलान् large, महारोहीन् stately deer, हत्वा killed, तं द्विजम् to that bird (Jatayu), अनुतस्तार spread it as offering.

#### Explanation

Then the mighty Rama, accompanied by Saumitri, got into the forest and killed a huge deer, brought it and spread it as an offering (to the dead Jatayu).

---

### Shloka 33

#### Shloka Text

रोहिमांसानि चोत्कृत्य पेशीकृत्य महायशाः।।3.68.33।। शकुनाय ददौ रामो रम्ये हरितशाद्वले।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महायशाः famed, रामः Rama, रोहिमांसानि flesh of the deer, उत्कृत्य cutting to pieces, पेशीकृत्य making into balls, रम्ये in a lovely, हरितशाद्वले green, grassy land, शकुनाय the bird Jatayu, ददौ offered.

#### Explanation

Tearing the flesh of the deer to pieces and making them  into balls, the celebrated Rama laid it on a lovely, green grassy land as offering to the bird.

---

### Shloka 34

#### Shloka Text

यत्तत्प्रेतस्य मर्त्यस्य कथयन्ति द्विजातयः।।3.68.34।। तत्स्वर्गगमनं तस्य पित्र्यं रामो जजाप ह।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तत् that, यत् whatever, द्विजातयः brahmins, प्रेतस्य for the dead, मर्त्यस्य for a mortal, स्वर्गगमनम् for ascending to heaven, कथयन्ति they utter, तत् all that, पित्र्यम् pertaining to father, तस्य that, जजाप muttered.

#### Explanation

Rama muttered the mantras recommended by brahmins for dead mortals as one would do for his father, to help him ascend to heaven.

---

### Shloka 35

#### Shloka Text

ततो गोदावरीं गत्वा नदीं नरवरात्मजौ।।3.68.35।। उदकं चक्रतुस्तस्मै गृध्रराजाय तावुभौ।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation
