ततः then, उभौ तौ they both, नरवरात्मजौ two princes, गोदावरीं नदीम् to river Godavari, गत्वा after reaching, तस्मै for him, गृद्रराजाय for the vultureking, उदकम् libations, चक्रतुः offered.

#### Explanation

Then both the princes went down to river Godavari and offered libations for the king of vultures.

---

### Shloka 36

#### Shloka Text

शास्त्रदृष्टेन विधिना जले गृध्राय राघवौ।।3.68.36।। स्नात्वा तौ गृध्रराजाय उदकं चक्रतुस्तदा।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तदा thereafter, (तौ) राघवौ both scions of the Raghu dynasty, शास्त्रदृष्टेन as ordained in scriptures, विधिना duly, गृध्राय for the vulture, जले in water, स्नात्वा bathing, गृध्रराजाय to the king of vultures, उदकम् water, चक्रतुः offered.

#### Explanation

Thereafter both the scions of the Raghu dynasty bathed in water, offered libations for the king of vultures as ordained in the scriptures.

---

### Shloka 37

#### Shloka Text

स गृध्रराजः कृतवान्यशस्करं सुदुष्करं कर्म रणे निपातितः। महर्षिकल्पेन च संस्कृतस्तदा जगाम पुण्यां गतिमात्मनश्शुभाम्।।3.68.37।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रणे in battle, सुदुष्करम् very difficult feat, यशस्करम् glorious, कर्म deed, कृतवान् done, निपातितः was made to fall, सः गृध्रराजः that lord of the vultures, तदा then, महर्षिकल्पेन following the prescription of sages, संस्कृतः sanctified, पुण्याम् holy, शुभाम् auspicious, आत्मनः his, गतिम् state, जगाम reached.

#### Explanation

The king of vultures had performed a very difficult and glorious feat by laying down his life fighting. Sanctified by Rama's offering as laid down in scriptures by the seers, he attained a holy, auspicious and divine state of the self.

---

### Shloka 38

#### Shloka Text

कृतोदकौ तावपि पक्षिसत्तमे स्थिरां च बुद्धिं प्रणिधाय जग्मतुः। प्रवेश्य सीताधिगमे ततो मनो वनं सुरेन्द्रविव विष्णुवासवौ।।3.68.38।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तौ अपि they both, कृतोदकौ  offering libation, पक्षिसत्तमे to the great bird, स्थिराम् settled, बुद्धिम् mind, प्रणिधाय employing their attention, ततः then, सीताधिगमे to search Sita, मनः mind, प्रवेश्य  entered , सुरेन्द्रौ like two lords of gods, विष्णुवासवाविव like Visnu and Indra, वनम् forest, जग्मतुः went.

#### Explanation

Rama and Lakshmana, like two lords of gods, Indra and Visnu, offered libations for the great bird with their settled minds and concentrated and then entered deeper into the forest turning their attention to the search for Sita. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये अरण्यकाण्डे अष्टषष्टितमस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the sixtyeighth sarga of Aranyakanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 69

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

कृत्वैवमुदकं तस्मै प्रस्थितौ रामलक्ष्मणौ। अवेक्षन्तौ वने सीतां पश्चिमां जग्मतुर्दिशम्।।3.69.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

एवम् thus, रामलक्ष्मणौ Rama and Lakshmana, तस्मै for him, उदकं कृत्वा after offering libation, प्रस्थितौ both started, पश्चिमाम् westward, दिशम् direction, वने in the forest, सीताम् Sita, अवेक्षन्तौ while both searching and looking, जग्मतुः went .

#### Explanation

Rama and Lakshmana offered libations (for Jatayu) and went westward, looking for Sita in the forest.

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

तौ दिशं दक्षिणां गत्वा शरचापासिधारिणौ। अविप्रहतमैक्ष्वाकौ पन्थानं प्रतिपेदतुः।।3.69.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

शरचापासिधारिणौ wielding bows, arrows and swords, तौ both, ऐक्ष्वाकौ two princes of the Ikshvaku race, (ताम् that), दक्षिणाम् southward, दिशम् direction, गत्वा went, अविप्रहतम् untrodden, पन्थानम् path, प्रतिपेदतुः reached.

#### Explanation

Wielding bow, arrows and sword, both the princes of the Ikshvaku race went southward and entered an untrodden (densely wooded) tract.

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

गुल्मैर्वृक्षैश्च बहुभिर्लताभिश्च प्रवेष्टितम्। आवृतं सर्वतो दुर्गं गहनं घोरदर्शनम्।।3.69.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

बहुभिः by many, गुल्मैः by shrubs, वृक्षैश्च and  by trees, लताभिश्च and by creepers, प्रवेष्टितम् overgrown, सर्वतः all over, आवृतम् covered, दुर्गम् difficult to reach, गहनम् impenetrable, घोरदर्शनम् appearing ferocious

#### Explanation

It was a dense, impenetrable and dreadful forest covered with shrubs, trees and creepers spread all over.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

व्यतिक्रम्य तु वेगेन व्यालसिंहनिषेवितम्। सुभीमं तन्महारण्यं व्यतियातौ महाबलौ।।3.69.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महाबलौ two mighty princes, व्यालसिंहनिषेवितम् where serperts and lions dwell, सुभीमम् very fierceful, तत् that, महारण्यम् great forest, वेगेन swiftly, व्यतिक्रम्य after forging through, व्यतियातौ  both crossed over.

#### Explanation

The two mighty heroes swiftly forged through the great forest, which was an abode of serpents and lions.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

ततः परं जनस्थानातत्रिक्रोशं गम्य राघवौ। क्रौञ्चारण्यं विविशतुर्गहनं तौ महौजसौ।।3.69.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, परम् later, महौजसौ mighty, तौ they both, राघवौ two scions of the Raghus, जनस्थानात् from Janasthana, त्रिक्रोशम् three krosas distance (one krosa  2 miles), गम्य after going, गहनम् impassable, क्रौञ्चारण्यम् kraunchaforest, विविशतुः entered.

#### Explanation

Both the mighty princes of the Raghu dynasty covered a distance of three krosas from Janasthana and entered the impassable kraunchaforest.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

नानामेघघनप्रख्यं प्रहृष्टमिव सर्वतः। नानापक्षिगणैर्युक्तं नानाव्यालमृगैर्युतम्।।3.69.6।। दिदृक्षमाणौ वैदेहीं तद्वनं तौ विचिक्यतुः। तत्र तत्रावतिष्ठन्तौ सीताहरणकर्शितौ।।3.69.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सीताहरणकर्शितौ both tormented  by the abduction of Sita, तौ they two, वैदेहीम् Vaidehi, दिदृक्षमाणौ eager to see, तत्र there, अवतिष्ठन्तौ both stopping a while, नानामेघघनप्रख्यम्  looking like dense clouds, सर्वतः all over, प्रहृष्टमिव as if it was over joyed, नानापक्षिगणैः with different birds, युक्तम्  filled, नानाव्यालमृगैः by various snakes and animals, युतम् filled with, तत् that, वनम् forest, विचिक्यतुः they both searched.

#### Explanation

Tormented due to abduction of Sita, the two princes anxious to see her searched all over the dense forest stopping here and there on the way. The thick forest looked like a conglomeration of big clouds. It was filled with different kinds of birds, snakes and animals.

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

नानामेघघनप्रख्यं प्रहृष्टमिव सर्वतः। नानापक्षिगणैर्युक्तं नानाव्यालमृगैर्युतम्।।3.69.6।। दिदृक्षमाणौ वैदेहीं तद्वनं तौ विचिक्यतुः। तत्र तत्रावतिष्ठन्तौ सीताहरणकर्शितौ।।3.69.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सीताहरणकर्शितौ both tormented  by the abduction of Sita, तौ they two, वैदेहीम् Vaidehi, दिदृक्षमाणौ eager to see, तत्र there, अवतिष्ठन्तौ both stopping a while, नानामेघघनप्रख्यम्  looking like dense clouds, सर्वतः all over, प्रहृष्टमिव as if it was over joyed, नानापक्षिगणैः with different birds, युक्तम्  filled, नानाव्यालमृगैः by various snakes and animals, युतम् filled with, तत् that, वनम् forest, विचिक्यतुः they both searched.

#### Explanation

Tormented due to abduction of Sita, the two princes anxious to see her searched all over the dense forest stopping here and there on the way. The thick forest looked like a conglomeration of big clouds. It was filled with different kinds of birds, snakes and animals.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

ततः पूर्वेण तौ गत्वा त्रिक्रोशं भ्रातरौ तदा। क्रौञ्चारण्यमतिक्रम्य मतङ्गाश्रममन्तरा।।3.69.8।। दृष्ट्वा तु तद्वनं घोरं बहुभीममृगद्विजम्। ननासत्त्वसमाकीर्णं सर्वं गहनपादपम्।।3.69.9।। ददृशाते तु तौ तत्र दरीं दशरथात्मजौ। पातालसमगम्भीरां तमसा नित्यसंवृताम्।।3.69.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तदा then, भ्रातरौ the two brothers, तौ दशरथात्मजौ the two sons of Dasaratha, ततः then, पूर्वेण by eastern direction, त्रिक्रोशम् three krosas distance, गत्वा went, क्रौञ्चारण्यम्  kraunchaforest, अतिक्रम्य  crossed, मतङ्गाश्रमम् अन्तरा near the hermitage of sage Matanga, घोरम् terrific, बहुभीममृगद्विजम् filled with fearful beasts and birds, नानासत्त्वसमाकीर्णम् teeming with different kinds of creatures, गहनपादपम् dense trees, सर्वम् all, तत् that, वनम् forest, दृष्ट्वा seeing, तत्र there, (गिरौ  on the hill), पातालसमगम्भीराम् deep like the nether world, तमसा darkness, नित्यसंवृताम् always filled with, दरीम् cave, ददृशाते both saw.

#### Explanation

Then both the sons of Dasaratha, went further east, crossed the kraunchaforest after three krosas. They saw a dreadful forest which was full of fierce animals and birds near the hermitage of sage Matanga. It was densely covered with trees. They saw there a cave perpetually enveloped in darkness which was as deep as the netherworld.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

ततः पूर्वेण तौ गत्वा त्रिक्रोशं भ्रातरौ तदा। क्रौञ्चारण्यमतिक्रम्य मतङ्गाश्रममन्तरा।।3.69.8।। दृष्ट्वा तु तद्वनं घोरं बहुभीममृगद्विजम्। ननासत्त्वसमाकीर्णं सर्वं गहनपादपम्।।3.69.9।। ददृशाते तु तौ तत्र दरीं दशरथात्मजौ। पातालसमगम्भीरां तमसा नित्यसंवृताम्।।3.69.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तदा then, भ्रातरौ the two brothers, तौ दशरथात्मजौ the two sons of Dasaratha, ततः then, पूर्वेण by eastern direction, त्रिक्रोशम् three krosas distance, गत्वा went, क्रौञ्चारण्यम्  kraunchaforest, अतिक्रम्य  crossed, मतङ्गाश्रमम् अन्तरा near the hermitage of sage Matanga, घोरम् terrific, बहुभीममृगद्विजम् filled with fearful beasts and birds, नानासत्त्वसमाकीर्णम् teeming with different kinds of creatures, गहनपादपम् dense trees, सर्वम् all, तत् that, वनम् forest, दृष्ट्वा seeing, तत्र there, (गिरौ  on the hill), पातालसमगम्भीराम् deep like the nether world, तमसा darkness, नित्यसंवृताम् always filled with, दरीम् cave, ददृशाते both saw.

#### Explanation

Then both the sons of Dasaratha, went further east, crossed the kraunchaforest after three krosas. They saw a dreadful forest which was full of fierce animals and birds near the hermitage of sage Matanga. It was densely covered with trees. They saw there a cave perpetually enveloped in darkness which was as deep as the netherworld.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

ततः पूर्वेण तौ गत्वा त्रिक्रोशं भ्रातरौ तदा। क्रौञ्चारण्यमतिक्रम्य मतङ्गाश्रममन्तरा।।3.69.8।। दृष्ट्वा तु तद्वनं घोरं बहुभीममृगद्विजम्। ननासत्त्वसमाकीर्णं सर्वं गहनपादपम्।।3.69.9।। ददृशाते तु तौ तत्र दरीं दशरथात्मजौ। पातालसमगम्भीरां तमसा नित्यसंवृताम्।।3.69.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तदा then, भ्रातरौ the two brothers, तौ दशरथात्मजौ the two sons of Dasaratha, ततः then, पूर्वेण by eastern direction, त्रिक्रोशम् three krosas distance, गत्वा went, क्रौञ्चारण्यम्  kraunchaforest, अतिक्रम्य  crossed, मतङ्गाश्रमम् अन्तरा near the hermitage of sage Matanga, घोरम् terrific, बहुभीममृगद्विजम् filled with fearful beasts and birds, नानासत्त्वसमाकीर्णम् teeming with different kinds of creatures, गहनपादपम् dense trees, सर्वम् all, तत् that, वनम् forest, दृष्ट्वा seeing, तत्र there, (गिरौ  on the hill), पातालसमगम्भीराम् deep like the nether world, तमसा darkness, नित्यसंवृताम् always filled with, दरीम् cave, ददृशाते both saw.

#### Explanation

Then both the sons of Dasaratha, went further east, crossed the kraunchaforest after three krosas. They saw a dreadful forest which was full of fierce animals and birds near the hermitage of sage Matanga. It was densely covered with trees. They saw there a cave perpetually enveloped in darkness which was as deep as the netherworld.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

आसाद्य तौ नरव्याघ्रौ दर्यास्तस्याविदूरतः। ददृशाते महारूपां राक्षसीं विकृताननाम्।।3.69.11।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तौ  both of them, नरव्याघ्रौ best among men, आसाद्य  reached, तस्याः from that, दर्याः from the cave, विदूरतः not very far from that place, महारूपाम् a woman of huge size, विकृताननाम् a woman of disfigured face, राक्षसीम् demoness, ददृशाते both saw.

#### Explanation

Both the princes,the best among men, reached the cave and saw a huge demoness with a disfigured face not far from the cave.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

भयदामल्पसत्त्वानां बीभत्सां रौद्रदर्शनाम्। लम्बोदरीं तीक्ष्णदंष्ट्रां करालीं परुषत्वचम्।।3.69.12।। भक्षयन्तीं मृगान्भीमान्विकटां मुक्तमूर्धजाम्। प्रैक्षेतां तौ ततस्तत्र भ्रातरौ रामलक्ष्मणौ।।3.69.13।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, भ्रातरौ both brothers, तौ रामलक्ष्मणौ both Rama and Lakshmana, अल्पसत्त्वानाम् for the timid, भयदाम् one creating fear, बीभत्साम् a woman of uncouth appearance, रौद्रदर्शनाम्    a woman of terrific appearance, लम्बोदरीम् a woman of big stomach, तीक्ष्णदंष्ट्राम् a woman with sharp teeth, करालीम् terrific woman, भीमान् fearful, मृगान् animals, भक्षयन्तीम् was eating, विकटाम् who had huge curved skins, मुक्तमूर्धजाम् a woman with hair spread out, तत्र there, प्रैक्षेताम् saw.

#### Explanation

Then the two brothers saw a fearful demoness who could strike terror in the mind of the timid, an uncouth figure with huge curved skins, with a big stomach, dishevelled hair, sharp and long teeth, devouring wild animals.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

भयदामल्पसत्त्वानां बीभत्सां रौद्रदर्शनाम्। लम्बोदरीं तीक्ष्णदंष्ट्रां करालीं परुषत्वचम्।।3.69.12।। भक्षयन्तीं मृगान्भीमान्विकटां मुक्तमूर्धजाम्। प्रैक्षेतां तौ ततस्तत्र भ्रातरौ रामलक्ष्मणौ।।3.69.13।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, भ्रातरौ both brothers, तौ रामलक्ष्मणौ both Rama and Lakshmana, अल्पसत्त्वानाम् for the timid, भयदाम् one creating fear, बीभत्साम् a woman of uncouth appearance, रौद्रदर्शनाम्    a woman of terrific appearance, लम्बोदरीम् a woman of big stomach, तीक्ष्णदंष्ट्राम् a woman with sharp teeth, करालीम् terrific woman, भीमान् fearful, मृगान् animals, भक्षयन्तीम् was eating, विकटाम् who had huge curved skins, मुक्तमूर्धजाम् a woman with hair spread out, तत्र there, प्रैक्षेताम् saw.

#### Explanation

Then the two brothers saw a fearful demoness who could strike terror in the mind of the timid, an uncouth figure with huge curved skins, with a big stomach, dishevelled hair, sharp and long teeth, devouring wild animals.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

सा समासाद्य तौ वीरौ व्रजन्तं भ्रातुरग्रतः। एहि रंस्यावहेत्युक्त्वा समालम्बत लक्ष्मणम्।।3.69.14।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सा she, वीरौ both heroes, तौ both, समासाद्य  coming close, एहि come on, रंस्यावहे we will both sport and enjoy sex, इति thus, उक्त्वा said, भ्रातुः brother's, अग्रतः in front of, व्रजन्तम् moving, लक्ष्मणम् Lakshmana, समालम्बत held.

#### Explanation

She came close to them, held Lakshmana who was walking ahead of his brother and said Come, let us enjoy.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

उवाच चैनं वचनं सौमित्रिमुपगूह्य सा। अहं त्वयोमुखी नाम लब्धा ते त्वमसि प्रियः।।3.69.15।। नाथ पर्वतकूटेषु नदीनां पुलिनेषु च। आयुश्शेषमिमं वीर त्वं मया सह रंस्यसे।।3.69.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सा she, सौमित्रिम् to Saumitri, उपगुह्य took hold of, एनम् him, वचनम् words, उवाच च spoke, अहं तु I am, अयोमुखी नाम Ayomukhi by name, ते to you, लब्धा obtained by you, त्वम् him, प्रियः dear, वीर hero, नाथ lord, इमम् this, आयुःशेषम् rest of your  life, त्वम् you, मया सह with me, पर्वतकूटेषु on the lofty hills, नदीनाम् पुलिनेषु  च on the river banks, रंस्यसे you will enjoy.

#### Explanation

Taking hold of Saumitri the demoness said 'I am Ayomukhi and I have been obtained by you. You got me, O hero, O lord  For the rest of your life you will sport with me on the lofty hills and river banks.'

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

उवाच चैनं वचनं सौमित्रिमुपगूह्य सा। अहं त्वयोमुखी नाम लब्धा ते त्वमसि प्रियः।।3.69.15।। नाथ पर्वतकूटेषु नदीनां पुलिनेषु च। आयुश्शेषमिमं वीर त्वं मया सह रंस्यसे।।3.69.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सा she, सौमित्रिम् to Saumitri, उपगुह्य took hold of, एनम् him, वचनम् words, उवाच च spoke, अहं तु I am, अयोमुखी नाम Ayomukhi by name, ते to you, लब्धा obtained by you, त्वम् him, प्रियः dear, वीर hero, नाथ lord, इमम् this, आयुःशेषम् rest of your  life, त्वम् you, मया सह with me, पर्वतकूटेषु on the lofty hills, नदीनाम् पुलिनेषु  च on the river banks, रंस्यसे you will enjoy.

#### Explanation

Taking hold of Saumitri the demoness said 'I am Ayomukhi and I have been obtained by you. You got me, O hero, O lord  For the rest of your life you will sport with me on the lofty hills and river banks.'

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

एवमुक्तस्तु कुपितः खड्गमुद्धृत्य लक्ष्मणः। कर्णनासास्तनं तस्या निचकर्तारिसूदनः।।3.69.17।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

एवम् thus, उक्तः having been told, अरिसूदनः subduer of enemies, लक्ष्मणः Lakshmana, कुपितः became angry, खड्गम् sword, उद्धृत्य after lifting, तस्याः her, कर्णनासास्तनम् ears, nose and breasts, चकर्त cut off.

#### Explanation

Thus addressed, Lakshmana, subduer of enemies, became angry, took out the sword and cut off her ears, nose and breasts.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

कर्णनासे निकृत्ते तु विस्वरं सा विनद्य च। यथागतं प्रदुद्राव राक्षसी भीमदर्शना।।3.69.18।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

भीमदर्शना a woman of frightening appearance, सा राक्षसी that demoness, कर्णनासे ears and nose, निकृत्ते when they were cut, विस्वरम् with cracked voice, विनद्य after shouting, यथागतम् in the same way as she came, प्रदुद्राव ran away.

#### Explanation

When the ears and nose of the frightening demoness were cut off she shouted in a fierce tone and ran away in the direction she had come from.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

तस्यां गतायां गहनं व्रजन्तौ वनमोजसा। आसेदतुरमित्रघ्नौ भ्रातरौ रामलक्ष्मणौ।।3.69.19।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तस्याम् when she, गतायाम् went away, व्रजन्तौ as both were advancing, भ्रातरौ two brothers, अमित्रघ्नौ both being destroyers of enemies, रामलक्ष्मणौ Rama and Lakshmana, ओजसा with their might, गहनम् dense, वनम् woods, आसेदतुः reached.

#### Explanation

The destroyers of enemies, Rama and Lakshmana of great valour went through the dense forest after she ran away.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

लक्ष्मणस्तु महातेजास्सत्त्ववान् शीलवान् शुचिः। अब्रवीत्प्राञ्जलिर्वाक्यं भ्रातरं दीप्ततेजसम्।।3.69.20।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महातेजाः of great brilliance, सत्त्ववान् a powerful one, शीलवान् a man of good conduct, शुचिः pure, लक्ष्मणस्तु Lakshmna too, प्राञ्जलिः with folded hands, दीप्ततेजसम् glowing in splendour, भ्रातरम् his brother, वाक्यम् words, अब्रवीत् said.

#### Explanation

Lakshmana of great brilliance and power, of good conduct and pure character spoke to his highly effulgent brother with folded hands:

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

स्पन्दते च दृढं बाहुरुद्विग्नमिव मे मनः। प्रायशश्चाप्यनिष्टानि निमित्तान्युपलक्षये।।3.69.21।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मे my, बाहुः arm, दृढम् strongly, स्पन्दते is throbbing, मनः mind, उद्विग्नमिव is as if agitated, प्रायशः probably, अनिष्टानि undesirable ones, निमित्तानि omens, उपलक्षये I am observing.

#### Explanation

My arm is throbbing heavily. My mind is agitated. The omens I see foretell some undesirable events.

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

तस्मात्सज्जीभवार्य त्वं कुरुष्व वचनं हितम्। ममैव हि निमित्तानि सद्यश्शंसन्ति सम्भ्रमम्।।3.69.22।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

आर्य O noble one, तस्मात् therefore, त्वम् you, सज्जीभव be ready, हितम् good, वचनम् words, कुरुष्व you may act upon, निमित्तानि omens, सद्यः presently, सम्भ्रमम् fear, मम I am, शंसन्ति इव हि as though they are forecasting.

#### Explanation

O noble brother be alert. Heed my words of good advice. The bad omens I see presage perils.

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

एष वञ्चुलको नाम पक्षी परमदारुणः। आवयोर्विजयं युद्धे शंसन्निव विनर्दति।।3.69.23।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

परमदारुणः extremely dreadful, वञ्चुलको नाम  by name Vanchulaka, एषः पक्षी this bird, युद्धे in strife, आवयोः both of us, विजयम् triumph, शंसन्निव as though telling, विनर्दति cries loudly.

#### Explanation

This vanchulaka bird's loud and dreadful screams suggest our triumph in war.

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

तयोरन्वेषतोरेवं सर्वं तद्वनमोजसा। संजज्ञे विपुलः शब्दो प्रभञ्जन्निव तद्वनम्।।3.69.24।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तयोः both, एवम् in that manner, ओजसा with strength, सर्वम् all over, तत् वनम् that forest, अन्वेषतोः while both were searching, तत् वनम् that forest, प्रभञ्जन्निव as if shattering, विपुलः very big, शब्दः sound, संजज्ञे broke out.

#### Explanation

As both the brothers with great prowess were searching for Sita all over, a huge sound exploded as if shattering the forest.

---

### Shloka 25

#### Shloka Text

संवेष्टितमिवात्यर्थं गगनं मातरिश्वना। वनस्य तस्य शब्दोऽभूद्दिवमापूरयन्निव।।3.69.25।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

गगनम् sky, अत्यर्थम् very much, मातरिश्वना by fierce wind, संवेष्टितमिव was as if covered, तस्य of that, वनस्य of the forest, शब्दः sound, दिवम् sky, आपूरयन्निव was filling, अभूत् rose.

#### Explanation

It appeared as though a fierce wind rose from the forest and filled the sky.

---

### Shloka 26

#### Shloka Text

तं शब्दं काङ्क्षमाणस्तु रामः कक्षे सहानुजः। ददर्श सुमहाकायं राक्षसं विपुलोदरम्।।3.69.26।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तं शब्दम् that sound, काङ्क्षमाणः a man looking for it, सहानुजः along with his brother, रामः Rama, कक्षे on the side, सुमहाकायम् one with a huge body, विपुलोदरम् and a big belly, राक्षसम् demon, ददर्श saw.

#### Explanation

While looking for the sound, Rama with his brother saw a demon with a huge body and a big belly.

---

### Shloka 27

#### Shloka Text

आसेदतुस्ततस्तत्र तावुभौ प्रमुखे स्थितम्। विवृद्धमशिरोग्रीवं कबन्धमुदरे मुखम्।।3.69.27।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, तौ उभौ both of them, तत्र there, प्रमुखे in front, स्थितम् stood, विवृद्धम् very well grown, अशिरोग्रीवम् without head and neck, उदरे मुखम् with his face in the stomach, कबन्धम् trunk, आसेदतुः reached.

#### Explanation

Then both the brothers saw a very tall figure with a trunk that stood facing them. He had neither neck nor head. His face was in the stomach.

---

### Shloka 28

#### Shloka Text

रोमभिर्निचितैस्तीक्ष्णैर्महागिरिमिवोछ्रितम्। नीलमेघनिभं रौद्रं मेघस्तनितनिस्वनम्।।3.69.28।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

निचितैः by those spread all over the body, तीक्ष्णैः with sharp bristles, रोमभिः hair, उच्छ्रिचतम् mammoth figure, महागिरिमिव like a big mountain, नीलमेघनिभम् resembling a dark cloud, रौद्रम् a fierce one, मेघस्तनितनिस्वनम् voice like the thunder of the cloud.

#### Explanation

With sharp bristled hair all over the body, he was a mammoth figure who looked like a mountain  resembling a dark cloud with his voice like the thunder.

---

### Shloka 29

#### Shloka Text

अग्निज्वालानिकाशेन ललाटस्थेन दीप्यता। महापक्ष्मेण पिङ्गेन विपुलेनायतेन च।।3.69.29।। एकेनोरसि घोरेण नयनेनाशुदर्शिना। महादंष्ट्रोपपन्नं तल्लेलिहानं महामुखम्।।3.69.30।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अग्निज्वालानिकाशेन resembling the flaming fire, ललाटस्थेन on the forehead, दीप्यता by the glowing, महापक्ष्मेण with huge eyelashes, पिङ्गेन with reddishbrown colour, विपुलेन huge, आयतेन च stretched , आशुदर्शिना with instant vision, घोरेण terrific one, उरसि on the chest, एकेन one, नयनेन eye, महादंष्ट्रोपपन्नम् having massive fangs, लेलिहानम् while licking his lips, महामुखम् his huge mouth, तम् him

#### Explanation

He had a single eye that was reddishbrown in colour resembling flaming fire. It was fixed in the chest. His eyelashes glowed. He had instant vision with massive fangs in his huge mouth. He was licking his lips.

---

### Shloka 30

#### Shloka Text

अग्निज्वालानिकाशेन ललाटस्थेन दीप्यता। महापक्ष्मेण पिङ्गेन विपुलेनायतेन च।।3.69.29।। एकेनोरसि घोरेण नयनेनाशुदर्शिना। महादंष्ट्रोपपन्नं तल्लेलिहानं महामुखम्।।3.69.30।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अग्निज्वालानिकाशेन resembling the flaming fire, ललाटस्थेन on the forehead, दीप्यता by the glowing, महापक्ष्मेण with huge eyelashes, पिङ्गेन with reddishbrown colour, विपुलेन huge, आयतेन च stretched , आशुदर्शिना with instant vision, घोरेण terrific one, उरसि on the chest, एकेन one, नयनेन eye, महादंष्ट्रोपपन्नम् having massive fangs, लेलिहानम् while licking his lips, महामुखम् his huge mouth, तम् him

#### Explanation

He had a single eye that was reddishbrown in colour resembling flaming fire. It was fixed in the chest. His eyelashes glowed. He had instant vision with massive fangs in his huge mouth. He was licking his lips.

---

### Shloka 31

#### Shloka Text

भक्षयन्तं महाघोरानृक्षसिंहमृगद्विपान्। घोरौ भुजौ विकुर्वाणमुभौ योजनमायतौ।।3.69.31।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महाघोरान् terrific, ऋक्षसिंहमृगद्विपान् bears, lions, deer and elephants, भक्षयन्तम् while eating, योजनम् a Yojana (eight miles), आयतौ long ones, घोरौ both frightening, उभौ both, भूजौ two arms, विकुर्वाणम् as he was stretching.

#### Explanation

The terrific demon kept eating bears, lions, deer and elephants, catching with both his arms stretching up to one yojana.

---

### Shloka 32

#### Shloka Text

कराभ्यां विविधान्गृह्यऋक्षान्पक्षिगणान्मृगान्। आकर्षन्तं विकर्षन्तमनेकान्मृगयूथपान्।।3.69.32।। स्थितमावृत्य पन्थानं तयोर्भ्रात्रोः प्रपन्नयोः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ऋक्षान् bears, विविधान् various, पक्षिगणान् birds, मृगान् deer, अनेकान् many, मृगयूथपान् herds of deer, कराभ्याम्  by both his hands, गृह्य taking, आकर्षन्तम् seizing, विकर्षन्तम् throwing  away, प्रपन्नयोः reached there, तयोः both the, भ्रात्रोः of brothers, पन्थानम् path, आवृत्य after blocking, स्थितम् stood.

#### Explanation

He stood rooted there, catching bears, flocks of birds and deer with both his hands stretching up to one yojana, pulling and pushing them. (Now) he obstructed the path of the two brothers.

---

### Shloka 33

#### Shloka Text

अथ तौ समभिक्रम्य क्रोशमात्रे ददर्शतुः।।3.69.33।। महान्तं दारुणं भीमं कबन्धं भुजसंवृम्। कबन्धमिव संस्थानादतिघोरप्रदर्शनम्।।3.69.34।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अथ then, तौ both, समभिक्रम्य after covering, क्रोशमात्रे at a distance of one krosa (2 miles), महान्तम् mighty, दारुणम् cruel, भीमम् frightening, भुजसंवृतम् his arms encircling, संस्थानात् from his form, कबन्धमिव like a trunk, घोरप्रदर्शनम् putting is a monstrous appearance, कबन्धम् one demon, Kabandha, ददर्शतुः they both saw.

#### Explanation

Then the brothers after covering one krosa (2 miles) saw a cruel, fierce, mighty monster named Kabandha looking like a trunk. He had a dreadful appearance and his arms encircled the area.

---

### Shloka 34

#### Shloka Text

अथ तौ समभिक्रम्य क्रोशमात्रे ददर्शतुः।।3.69.33।। महान्तं दारुणं भीमं कबन्धं भुजसंवृम्। कबन्धमिव संस्थानादतिघोरप्रदर्शनम्।।3.69.34।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अथ then, तौ both, समभिक्रम्य after covering, क्रोशमात्रे at a distance of one krosa (2 miles), महान्तम् mighty, दारुणम् cruel, भीमम् frightening, भुजसंवृतम् his arms encircling, संस्थानात् from his form, कबन्धमिव like a trunk, घोरप्रदर्शनम् putting is a monstrous appearance, कबन्धम् one demon, Kabandha, ददर्शतुः they both saw.

#### Explanation

Then the brothers after covering one krosa (2 miles) saw a cruel, fierce, mighty monster named Kabandha looking like a trunk. He had a dreadful appearance and his arms encircled the area.

---

### Shloka 35

#### Shloka Text

स महाबाहुरत्यर्थं प्रसार्य विपुलौ भुजौ। जग्राह सहितावेव राघवौ पीडयन्बलात्।।3.69.35।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महाबाहुः long armed one, सः he, विपुलौ very big, भुजौ both arms, अत्यर्थम् greatly, प्रसार्य  spreading, सहितावेव राघवौ brothers together, बलात् with strength, पीडयन्  pressing, जग्राह held.

#### Explanation

The longarmed demon spread his hands and caught hold of Rama and Lakshmana together by pressing them with all his force.

---

### Shloka 36

#### Shloka Text

खड्गिनौ दृढधन्वानौ तिग्मतेजोवपुर्धरौ। भ्रातरौ विवशं प्राप्तौ कृष्यमाणौ महाबलौ।।3.69.36।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

खड्गिनौ  wielders of swords, दृढधन्वानौ  with strong bows, तिग्मतेजोवपुर्धरौ  with bodies of fiery splendour, महाबलौ mighty, भ्रातरौ both brothers, कृष्यमाणौ both being seized, विवशम् प्राप्तौ both became helpless.

#### Explanation

The two mighty brothers who wielded swords and strong bows and whose bodies were of fiery splendour became helpless, seized by the hands of Kabandha.

---

### Shloka 37

#### Shloka Text

तत्र धैर्येण शूरस्तु राघवो नैव विव्यथे। बाल्यादनाश्रयत्वाच्च लक्ष्मणस्त्वतिविव्यथे।।3.69.37।। उवाच स विषण्णस्सन्राघवं राघवानुजः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तत्र there, शूरः heroic, राघवः Raghava, धैर्येण with courage, नैव विव्यथे not pained, लक्ष्मणस्तु but Lakshmana, बाल्यात् being younger, आनाश्रयत्वाच्च not having much courage, अतिविव्यथे very worried, सः that, राघवानुजः brother of Rama, विषण्णस्सन् depressed, राघवम् to Rama, उवाच said,

#### Explanation

Of the two heroes, Rama being courageous was not worried but Lakshmana being younger and not having as much courage, was very much concerned and depressed. He said to Rama:

---

### Shloka 38

#### Shloka Text

पश्य मां वीर विवशं राक्षसस्य वशं गतम्।।3.69.38।। मयैकेन विनिर्युक्तः परिमुञ्चस्व राघव।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वीर heroic Rama, विवशम् helpless, राक्षसस्य वशम् under the control of the demon, गतम् gone, माम् me, पश्य see, राघव Rama, एकेन by one alone, मया by me, विनिर्युक्तः offering me, परिमुञ्चस्व set yourself free.

#### Explanation

O heroic Rama see how helpless I am under the control of the demon. Set yourself free, leaving me alone and using me as an offering.

---

### Shloka 39

#### Shloka Text

मां हि भूतबलिं दत्वा पलायस्व यथासुखम्।।3.69.39।। अधिगन्तासि वैदेहीमचिरेणेति मे मतिः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

माम् me, भूतबलिम् as an offering, दत्वा after giving, यथासुखम् happily, पलायस्व you may run away, वैदेहीम् Vaidehi, अचिरेण soon, अधिगन्तासि will attain her, इति this is, मे my, मतिः opinion.

#### Explanation

Giving me to this monstor as an offering, you may run away happily. I think you will soon obtain Vaidehi.

---

### Shloka 40

#### Shloka Text

प्रतिलभ्य च काकुत्स्थ पितृपैतामहीं महीम्।।3.69.40।। तत्र मां राम राज्यस्थस्स्मर्तुमर्हसि सर्वदा।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

काकुत्थ्स O scion of Kakutstha dynasty, राम Rama, पितृपैतामहीम् heriditary reign, महीम् land, प्रतिलभ्य च on getting, तत्र there, राज्यस्थः as one ruling the country, सर्वदा always, माम् me, स्मर्तुम् remember, अर्हसि should.

#### Explanation

O scion of the Kakutstha dynasty, on getting the hereditary kingdom and ruling over it you should always remember me.

---

### Shloka 41

#### Shloka Text

लक्ष्मणेनैवमुक्तस्तु रामस्सौमित्रिमब्रवीत्।।3.69.41।। मा स्म त्रासं कृथा वीर न हि त्वादृग्विषीदति।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लक्ष्मणेन by Lakshmana, एवम् in that way, उक्तः having been said, रामः Rama, सौमित्रिम् to Soumitri, अब्रवीत् said, वीर heroic Lakshmana, त्रासम् fear, मा स्म कृथाः do not entertain, त्वादृक् a hero like you, न विषीदति हि  should not get desperate.

#### Explanation

O  valiant Lakshmana, a hero like you should not get desparate. said Rama to Saumitri when he was thus entreated by Lakshmana.

---

### Shloka 42

#### Shloka Text

एतस्मिन्नन्तरे क्रूरो भ्रातरौ रामलक्ष्मणौ।।3.69.42।। पप्रच्छ घननिर्घोषः कबन्धो दानवोत्तमः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

एतस्मिन् अन्तरे in the meanwhile, क्रूरः cruel one, दानवोत्तमः foremost of the demons, कबन्धः Kabandha, घननिर्घोषः rumbling like thunder, भ्रातरौ to the brothers, रामलक्ष्मणौ Rama and Lakshmana, पप्रच्छ questioned.

#### Explanation

In the meanwhile the foremost of the demons, Kabandha, rumbling like thunder questioned the brothers, Rama and Lakshmana:

---

### Shloka 43

#### Shloka Text

कौ युवां वृषभस्कन्धौ महाखड्गधनुर्धरौ।।3.69.43।। घोरं देशमिमं प्राप्तौ मम भक्षावुपस्थितौ।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वृषभस्कन्धौ whose shoulders are comparable to a bull, महाखड्गधनुर्धरौ both holding great sword and bow, घोरम् dreadful, इमं देशम् this place, प्राप्तौ reached here, युवाम् you both, कौ who are you, मम for me, भक्षौ both, उपस्थितौ have reached.

#### Explanation

Who are you with bulllike shoulders, wielding big swords, bows and arrows? By coming to this place you have become my food.

---

### Shloka 44

#### Shloka Text

वदतं कार्यमिह वां किमर्थं चागतौ युवाम्।।3.69.44।। इमं देशमनुप्राप्तौ क्षुधार्तस्येह तिष्ठतः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

इह here, वाम् to you both, कार्यम् work, वदतम् speak, युवाम् both, किमर्थम् for what purpose?, आगतौ have come, क्षुधार्तस्य for the hungry, इह here, तिष्ठतः while staying here, इमम् this, देशम् place, अनुप्राप्तौ came at the right time.

#### Explanation

For what purpose have you come? What work have you here ? You have come at the right time as my food, since I am hungry.

---

### Shloka 45

#### Shloka Text

सबाणचापखड्गौ च तीक्ष्णशृङ्गाविवर्षभौ।।3.69.45।। ममास्यमनुसम्प्राप्तौ दुर्लभं जीवितं पुनः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सबाणचापखड्गौ holding bows and arrows and swords, तीक्ष्णशृङ्गौ two sharp horns, ऋषभौ इव like bulls, मम my, आस्यम् mouth, अनुसम्प्राप्तौ you both have reached, पुनः again, जीवितम् lives, दुर्लभम् impossible.

#### Explanation

Holding bows, arrows and swords, looking like bulls with pointed horns you have entered my mouth. It is impossible for you to live any more.

---

### Shloka 46

#### Shloka Text

तस्य तद्वचनं श्रुत्वा कबन्धस्य दुरात्मनः।।3.69.46।। उवाच लक्ष्मणं रामो मुखेन परिशुष्यता।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

दुरात्मनः of the evilminded, तस्य कबन्धस्य that Kabandha's, तत् that, वचनम् word, श्रुत्वा on hearing, रामः Rama, परिशुष्यता by dried up, मुखेन by mouth, लक्ष्मणम् to Lakshmna, अब्रवीत् said:

#### Explanation

On hearing the words of the evilminded Kabandha, Rama said to Lakshmana with dried up mouth:

---

### Shloka 47

#### Shloka Text

कृच्छ्रात् कृच्छ्रतरं प्राप्य दारुणं सत्यविक्रमः।।3.69.47।। व्यसनं जीवितान्ताय प्राप्तमप्राप्य तां प्रियाम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सत्यविक्रम  one whose valour is truth, कृच्छ्रात् from one calamity, कृच्छ्रतरम् to a greater calamity, प्राप्य having reached, प्रियाम् beloved, ताम् her, अप्राप्य having lost, जीवितान्ताय for ending life, दारुणम् unbearable, व्यसनम् crisis, प्राप्तम् experienced.

#### Explanation

O Lakshmana, your valour is truth.We have gone through from one calamity to a greater one without finding the beloved Sita. Now we have landed into a terrible crisis which will put an end to our own life.

---

### Shloka 48

#### Shloka Text

कालस्य सुमहद्वीर्यं सर्वभूतेषु लक्ष्मण।।3.69.48।। त्वां च मां च नरव्याघ्र व्यसनैः पश्य मोहितौ।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

नरव्याघ्र tiger among men, लक्ष्मण Lakshmana, सर्वभूतेषु for all beings, कालस्य of the time, वीर्यम् power, सुमहत् great, व्यसनैः by calamity, मोहितौ afflicted, त्वां च yourself, मां me, च and, पश्य see.

#### Explanation

O tiger among men see how powerful the might of time is for all beings Just see  yourself and myself afflicted wth this great calamity.

---

### Shloka 49

#### Shloka Text

नातिभारोस्ति कालस्य सर्वभूतेषु लक्ष्मण।।3.69.49।। शूराश्च बलवन्तश्च कृतास्त्राश्च रणाजिरे। कालाभिपन्नास्सीदन्ति यथा वालुकसेतवः।।3.69.50।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लक्ष्मण Lakshmana, कालस्य of the time, सर्वभूतेषु over all creatures, अतिभारः great weight, नास्ति not there, शूराश्च brave people, बलवन्तश्च or strong men, रणाजिरे कृतास्त्राश्च those who know the use of arms in the war, कालाभिपन्नाः by the force of time, वालुकसेतवः यथा like barriers built with sand, सीदतन्ति make way.

#### Explanation

O Lakshmana look at the power of time over all creatures There is nothing too heavy for time. Even those who know the use of arms in war, or are brave or strong enough, cannot  withstand the force of time like the barriers built with sand.

---

### Shloka 50

#### Shloka Text

नातिभारोस्ति कालस्य सर्वभूतेषु लक्ष्मण।।3.69.49।। शूराश्च बलवन्तश्च कृतास्त्राश्च रणाजिरे। कालाभिपन्नास्सीदन्ति यथा वालुकसेतवः।।3.69.50।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लक्ष्मण Lakshmana, कालस्य of the time, सर्वभूतेषु over all creatures, अतिभारः great weight, नास्ति not there, शूराश्च brave people, बलवन्तश्च or strong men, रणाजिरे कृतास्त्राश्च those who know the use of arms in the war, कालाभिपन्नाः by the force of time, वालुकसेतवः यथा like barriers built with sand, सीदतन्ति make way.

#### Explanation

O Lakshmana look at the power of time over all creatures There is nothing too heavy for time. Even those who know the use of arms in war, or are brave or strong enough, cannot  withstand the force of time like the barriers built with sand.

---

### Shloka 51

#### Shloka Text

इति ब्रुवाणो दृढसत्यविक्रमो महायशा दाशरथिः प्रतापवान्। अवेक्ष्य सौमित्रिमुदग्रपौरुषं स्थिरां तदा स्वां मतिमात्मनाऽकरोत्।।3.69.51।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

दृढसत्यविक्रमः Firm in the strength of truth, महायशाः illustrious, प्रतापवान्  powerful, दाशरथिः Dasaratha's son (Rama), इति thus, ब्रुवाणः spoke, उदग्रपौरुषम् of mighty manliness, सौमित्रिम् Saumitri, अवेक्ष्य on seeing, तदा then, स्वाम् his own, स्थिराम् determined, मतिम् mind, आत्मना himself, अकरोत् fixed.

#### Explanation

Firm in the strength of truth, famous and powerful Rama, son of Dasaratha,  determined to act with a steadfast mind, spoke to Lakshmana of mighty manliness on seeing him. इत्यार्ष श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये अरण्यकाण्डे एकोनसप्ततितमस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the sixtyninth sarga of Aranyakanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 70

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

तौ तु तत्र स्थितौ दृष्ट्वा भ्रातरौ रामलक्ष्मणौ। बाहुपाशपरिक्षिप्तौ कबन्धो वाक्यमब्रवीत्।।3.70.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

बाहुपाशपरिक्षिप्तौ bound by the shackles of his arms, तत्र स्थितौ both stood there, भ्रातरौ brothers, तौ both, रामलक्ष्मणौ Rama and Lakshmana, दृष्ट्वा after seeing, कबन्धः Kabandha, वाक्यम् these words, अब्रवीत् said.

#### Explanation

Kabandha saw the brothers, Rama and Lakshmana, stand there bound by the shackles of his arms and said:

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

तिष्ठतः किं नु मां दृष्ट्वा क्षुधार्तं क्षत्रियर्षभौ। आहारार्थं तु सन्दिष्टौ दैवेन गतचेतसौ।।3.70.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

क्षत्रियर्षभौ two bulls among the kshatriyas, क्षुधार्तम् suffering from hunger, माम् me, दृष्ट्वा after seeing, किं नु why do you, तिष्ठतः both stand here, गतचेतसौ with the senses lost, दैवेन by god, आहारार्थम् for my food, सन्दिष्टौ  are sent.

#### Explanation

O bulls among the kshatriyas You are sent by destiny as food to appease my hunger. Seeing me hungry, why do you stand there with your senses switched off ?

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

तच्छ्रुत्वा लक्ष्मणो वाक्यं प्राप्तकालं हितं तदा। उवाचाऽर्तिं समापन्नो विक्रमे कृतलक्षणः।।3.70.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लक्ष्मणः Lakshmana, तत् that, श्रुत्वा having heard, तदा then, आर्तिम् suffering, समापन्नः he underwent, विक्रमे  to exhibit courage, कृतलक्षणः he has resorted to, प्राप्तकालम् appropriate time, हितम् good, वाक्यम् words, उवाच said.

#### Explanation

Having heard this, Lakshmana, who was greatly suffering gathered courage at appropriate time and said these salutary words:

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

त्वां च मां च पुरा तूर्णमादत्ते राक्षसाधमः। तस्मादसिभ्यामस्याशु बाहू छिन्दावहै गुरू।।3.70.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षसाधमः this vile demon, तूर्णम् quickly, त्वां च your, मां च and me, पुरा आदत्ते before he gulps, तस्मात् therefore, अस्य his, गुरू long, बाहू shoulders, असिभ्याम् by our swords, छिन्दावहै cut off.

#### Explanation

Before this vile demon gulps you and me let us cut off his long arms with our swords.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

भीषणोऽयं महाकायो राक्षसो भुजविक्रमः। लोकं ह्यतिजितं कृत्वा ह्यावां हन्तुमिहेच्छति।।3.70.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

भीषणः horrible, महाकायः huge, भुजविक्रमः having the strength of his arms, राक्षसः demon, लोकम् world, अतिजितम्  having conquered, कृत्वा after doing so, इह here, आवाम् us, हन्तुम् to kill, इच्छति intends.

#### Explanation

This huge, horrible demon with the strength of his arms has conquered the world, and now intends to kill us here.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

निश्चेष्टानां वधो राजन्कुत्सितो जगतीपतेः। क्रतुमध्योपनीतानां पशूनामिव राघव।।3.70.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राजन् king, राघव of the Raghu race, निश्चेष्टानाम् of harmless creatures, वधः killing, क्रतुमध्योपनीतानाम् brought in the midst of a sacrifice, पशूनामिव like animals, जगतीपतेः lord of the world, कुत्सितः censurable.

#### Explanation

O prince of the Raghu race  just as the animal brought in the midst of a sacrifice should not be killed, so also for a king to kill any harmless creature is despicable.

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

एतत्सञ्जल्पितं श्रुत्वा तयोः क्रुद्धस्तु राक्षसः। विदार्याऽस्यं तदा रौद्रस्तौ भक्षयितुमारभत्।।3.70.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

क्रूरः cruel, रौद्रः ferocious, राक्षसस्तु the demon, तदा then, तयोः them, सञ्जल्पितम् conversing, श्रुत्वा having heard, आस्यम् mouth, विदार्य widely opening, तौ them, भक्षयितुम् to eat, आरभत् made an effort.

#### Explanation

The cruel, ferocious demon heard them talk. He opened his mouth and attempted to eat both of them.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

ततस्तौ देशकालज्ञौ खड्गाभ्यामेव राघवौ। अच्छिन्दतां सुसंविग्नौ बाहू तस्यांसदेशतः।।3.70.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, सुसंविग्नौ became agitated, देशकालज्ञौ knowers of time and space, तौ those two, राघवौ Raghavas, खड्गाभ्याम् with their swords, तस्य his, बाहू two arms, अंसदेशतः from  the shoulders, अच्छिन्दताम् cut off.

#### Explanation

Then the agitated brothers, aware of time and space, quickly pulled out their swords and cut off both his arms from the shoulders.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

दक्षिणो दक्षिणं बाहुमसक्तमसिना ततः। चिच्छेद रामो वेगेन सव्यं वीरस्तु लक्ष्मणः।।3.70.9।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, दक्षिणः  standing on the right, रामः Rama, असक्तम् severed, वेगेन quickly, दक्षिणं बाहुम् right arm, असिना with a sword, चिच्छेद cut off, वीरः hero, लक्ष्मणस्तु Lakshmana, सव्यम् left arm.

#### Explanation

While standing on the right, Rama cut off his right arm and the brave Lakshmana cut off the left.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

स पपात महाबाहुश्छिन्नबाहुर्महास्वनः। खं च गां च दिशश्चैव नादयञ्जलदो यथा।।3.70.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महाबाहुः with strong arms, सः that, छिन्नबाहुः with his arms severed, महास्वनः roaring, जलदः यथा like a stormy cloud, खं च sky, गां च earth, दिशश्चैव quarters, नादयन् while resounding, पपात he fell down.

#### Explanation

The strongarmed demon, his arms severed, rumbling like a raincloud filling the sky, the earth and the quarters, fell down.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

स निकृत्तौ भुजौ दृष्ट्वा शोणितौघपरिप्लुतः। दीनः पप्रच्छ तौ वीरौ कौ युवामिति दानवः।।3.70.11।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः that, दानवः demon, निकृत्तौ with amputated, भूजौ arms, दृष्ट्वा after seeing, शोणितौघपरिप्लुतः drenched in the flood of blood, दीनः a pathetic one, युवाम् you both, कौ who are you, इति thus, तौ वीरौ those two heroes, पप्रच्छ asked.

#### Explanation

The demon with his arms amputated, drenched in a pool of blood  looked pathetic and asked them, Who are you?

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

इति तस्य ब्रुवाणस्य लक्ष्मणश्शुभलक्षणः। शशंस राघवं तस्य कबन्धस्य महात्मनः।।3.70.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तस्य his, इति thus, ब्रुवाणस्य when he was speaking, शुभलक्षणः a man of auspicious traits, सः लक्ष्मणः Lakshmana, महात्मनः of the great self, कबन्धस्य of Kabandha, राघवम् Raghava, शशंस revealed.

#### Explanation

Imbued with auspicious signs, Lakshmana revealed the identity of Rama to great Kabandha.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

अयमिक्ष्वाकुदायादो रामो नाम जनैश्श्रुतः। अस्यैवावरजं विद्दि भ्रातरं मां च लक्ष्मणम्।।3.70.13।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अयम् he, रामो नाम by name Rama, जनैः by people, श्रुतः famous, इक्ष्वाकुदायादः an heir of the Ikshvaku family, माम् me, अस्य his, अवरजं भ्रातरम् as his younger brother, लक्ष्मणम् Lakshmana, विद्दि you may know me,

#### Explanation

Here is Rama, an heir of the Ikshvaku family, known to the people and I am his younger brother Lakshmana for your information.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

अस्य देवप्रभावस्य वसतो विजने वने। रक्षसाऽपहृता पत्नी यामिच्छन्ताविहागतौ।।3.70.14।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

विजने desolate, वने in the forest, वसतः living, देवप्रभावस्य as powerful as a god, अस्य his, पत्नी wife, रक्षसा by a demon, अपहृता is carried off, याम् whom, इच्छन्तौ both wishing, इह here, आगतौ came.

#### Explanation

He is equal to a god in prowess. While living in this desolate forest his wife has been carried off by a demon. We both came here searching for her.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

त्वं तु को वा किमर्थं वा कबन्धसदृशो वने। आस्येनोरसि दीप्तेन भग्नजङ्घो विचेष्टसे।।3.70.15।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

त्वं तु you, कः वा who are you, कबन्धसदृशः appearing like a trunk, उरसि in the chest, दीप्तेन by a glittering, आस्येन with a mouth, भग्नजङ्घः with calf muscles broken, वने in the forest, किमर्थं वा for what purpose, विचेष्टसे you are rolling down?

#### Explanation

Who are you with only the trunk of the body, and a glittering mouth in the chest with your calf muscles broken rolling down in the forest?

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

एवमुक्तः कबन्धस्तु लक्ष्मणेनोत्तरं वचः। उवाच परमप्रीत स्तदिन्द्रवचनं स्मरन्।।3.70.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लक्ष्मणेन by Lakshmana, एवम् in that way, उक्तः having been told, कबन्धस्तु the Kabandha, तत् that, इन्द्रवचनम् Indra's words, स्मरन् remembering, परमप्रीतः very pleased, उत्तरम् reply, वचः words, उवाच said.

#### Explanation

While hearing Lakshmana, Kabandha, reminded of Indra's words, replied to him in great joy :

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

स्वागतं वां नरव्याघ्रौ दिष्ट्या पश्यामि चाप्यहम्। दिष्ट्या चेमौ निकृत्तौ मे युवाभ्यां बाहुबन्धनौ।।3.70.17।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

नरव्याघ्रौ two tigers among men, वाम् to you, स्वागतम् welcome, अहम् I, दिष्ट्या luckily, पश्यामि I see, दिष्ट्या by my luck, युवाभ्याम् by both of you, मे my, इमौ these, बाहुबन्धनौ that arms which bound you, निकृत्तौ are cut off.

#### Explanation

O tigers among men you are welcome. It is my good luck that I am able to see you. It is my good luck also that the arms that bound you have been amputated.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

विरूपं यच्च मे रूपं प्राप्तं ह्यविनयाद्यथा। तन्मे शृणु नरव्याघ्र तत्त्वतश्शंसतस्तव।।3.70.18।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

नरव्याघ्र O best among men (Rama), मे my, विरूपम् deformity, यत् such, रूपम् form, यथा as, अविनयात् due to haughtiness, प्राप्तम् is obtained, तव to your, तत्त्वतः exactly, शंसतः as I narrate, मे from me, शृणु you may listen.

#### Explanation

O best among men hear how I have been deformed due to my haughtiness. इत्यार्ष श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये अरण्यकाण्डे सप्ततितमस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the seventieth sarga of Aranyakanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 71

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

पुरा राम महाबाहो महाबलपराक्रम। रूपमासीन्ममा चिन्त्यं त्रिषु लोकेषु विश्रुतम्।।3.71.1।। यथा सोमस्य शक्रस्य सूर्यस्य च यथा वपुः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महाबाहो longarmed, महाबलपराक्रम strong and powerful, राम O Rama, पुरा in the past, सोमस्य the Moon's, शक्रस्य Indra's, यथा as, सूर्यस्य च the Sun's, वपुः body, यथा like that, मम my,रूपम् form, अचिन्त्यम् inconceivable, त्रिषु लोकेषु in the three worlds, विश्रुतम् renowned, आसीत् it was.

#### Explanation

O longarmed, mighty and powerful Rama in the past I was handsome like the Moon or Indra or the Sun inconceivable to all the three worlds.

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

सोऽहं रूपमिदं कृत्वा लोकवित्रासनं महत्।।3.71.2।। ऋषीन्वनगतान्राम त्रासयामि ततस्ततः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राम Rama, सः that, अहम् I, लोकवित्रासनम् a great terror in the world, महत् great, इदं रूपम् this form, कृत्वा after creating, ततस्ततः eventually, वनगतान् those men in the forests, ऋषीन् seers, त्रासयामि used to terrorise.

#### Explanation

With my appearance, I was a great nuisance to the world and used to terrorise the sages living in the forest.

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

ततस्स्थूलशिरा नाम महर्षिः कोपितो मया।।3.71.3।। सञ्चिन्वन्विविधं वन्यं रूपेणानेन धर्षितः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, विविधम् different, वन्यम् forest material, सञ्चिन्वन् while collecting, स्थूलशिराः नाम by name Sthulasira, महर्षिः seer, अनेन by this, रूपेण form, धर्षितः was assaulted, मया by me, कोपितः was enraged.

#### Explanation

With my appearance I invited the wrath of Sthulasira who I assaulted among the many seers, while he was collecting food in the forest.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

तेनाहमुक्तः प्रेक्ष्यैवं घोरशापाभिधायिना।।3.71.4।। एतदेव नृशंसं ते रूपमस्तु विगर्हितम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

प्रेक्ष्य seeing me, घोरशापाभिधायिना  pronounced a dreadful curse, तेन by him, अहम् I, एवम् in that way, उक्तः said, ते you, नृशंसम् mean, विगर्हितम् dispising, एतत् this, रूपमेव form only, अस्तु it may be.

#### Explanation

Seeing me he pronounced a dreadful curse on me saying, 'You will get a cruel, despicable form.'

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

स मया याचितः क्रुद्धश्शापस्योन्तो भवेदिति।।3.71.5।। अभिशापकृतस्येति तेनेदं भाषितं वचः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

क्रुद्धः angry, सः he, अभिशापकृतस्य pronounced on, शापस्य curse's, अन्तः end of it, भवेत् may be, इति thus, मया by me, प्रार्थितः begged, तेन by him, इदम् this, वचः words, भाषितम् spoke.

#### Explanation

When I sought to know how the curse angrily pronounced on me would come to an end, he said:

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

यदा छित्त्वा भुजौ रामस्त्वां दहेद्विजने वने।।3.71.6।। तदा त्वं प्राप्स्यसे रूपं स्वमेव विपुलं शुभम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रामः Rama, भुजौ two arms, छित्त्वा cutting, त्वाम् you, यदा when, विजने in a desolate, वने in forest, दहेत् cremate, तदा then, स्वमेव your own, विपुलम् शुभम् greatly auspicious, रूपम् form, प्राप्स्यसे you will obtain.

#### Explanation

'When Rama gets your arms in a desolate forest amputated and your body cremated, you would get back your gloriously auspicious form.'

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

श्रिया विराजितं पुत्रं दनोस्त्वं विद्धि लक्ष्मण।।3.71.7।। इन्द्रकोपादिदं रूपं प्राप्तमेवं रणाजिरे।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लक्ष्मण Lakshmana, दनोः Danu's family, श्रिया by prosperity, विराजितम् shining, पुत्रम् son, त्वम् you, विद्धि know, इन्द्रकोपात् by the wrath of Indra, रणाजिरे in the battlefield, एवम् this, इदं रूपम् this form, प्राप्तम् is obtained.

#### Explanation

O Lakshmana know that I was the prosperous son of demon Danu. By the wrath of Indra in the battlefeild I obtained this form.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

अहं हि तपसोग्रेण पितामहमतोषयम्।।3.71.8।। दीर्घमायुस्स मे प्रादात्ततो मां विभ्रमोऽस्पृशत्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अहम् I, उग्रेण by intense, तपसा by penance, पितामहम् the creator Brahma, आतोषयम् I made him happy, सः he, मे my, दीर्घम् long, आयुः life, प्रादात् graced, ततः then, माम् me, विभ्रमः confusion, अस्पृशत्  overtook me.

#### Explanation

I pleased the creator Brahma with great penance. He blessed me with long life. Then this pride and confusion overtook me.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

दीर्घमायुर्मया प्राप्तं किं मे शक्रः करिष्यति।।3.71.9।। इत्येवं बुद्धिमास्थाय रणे शक्रमधर्षयम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मया by me, दीर्घम् long, आयुः life, प्राप्तम् has been attained, शक्रः Indra, मे to me, किं करिष्यति  what can he do, इत्येवम् in that manner, बुद्धिम् in mind, आस्थाय considering, रणे in battle, शक्रम् Indra, अधर्षयम् I attacked .

#### Explanation

Having been blessed with long life and thinking, what can Indra do to me?, I attacked him.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

तस्य बाहुप्रयुक्तेन वज्रेण शतपर्वणा।।3.71.10।। सक्थिनी चैव मूर्धा च शरीरे सम्प्रवेशितम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तस्य his, बाहुप्रयुक्तेन deployed  from his arm, शतपर्वणा by a weapon of hundred nodes, वज्रेण with the thunderbolt, सक्थिनी चैव both my lower limbs, मूर्धा च head also, शरीरे into the body, सम्प्रवेशितम् entered.

#### Explanation

The thunderbolt having a hundred nodes deployed by Indra pierced my thighs and my head.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

स मया याच्यमानस्सन्नानयद्यमसादनम्।।3.71.11।। पितामहवचस्सत्यं तदस्त्विति ममाब्रवीत्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः he, मया by me, याच्यमानः सन् being begged of him, यमसादनम् to the abode of Yama, न आनयत् did not take me, तत् that, पितामहवचः word of the creator, सत्यम् true, अस्तु इति should become true, मम to me, अब्रवीत् said.

#### Explanation

Entreated, he did not send me to the abode of Yama ( lord of death) and said, 'Let the words of the creator be true' .

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

अनाहारः कथं शक्तो भग्नसक्थिशिरोमुखः।।3.71.12।। वज्रेणाभिहतः कालं सुदीर्घमपि जीवितुम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वज्रेण by the thunderbolt, अभिहतः struck by, भग्नसक्थिशिरोमुखः with thigh bones, head and face broken, अनाहारः starving, सुदीर्घं कालम् for a long time, जीवितुमपि to live, कथम् somehow, शक्तः was possible.

#### Explanation

Hit by the thunderbolt, my thighbones, head and face were broken. Somehow I could manage to live for a long time without food.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

एवमुक्तस्तु मे शक्रो बाहू योजनमायतौ।।3.71.13।। प्रादादास्यं च मे कुक्षौ तीक्ष्णदंष्ट्रमकल्पयत्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

एवम् in that way, उक्तः having been entreated, शक्रः Indra, मे to me, योजनम् one yojana, आयतौ long, बाहू two arms, प्रादात् he gave, मे to me, कुक्षौ in the stomach, तीक्ष्णदंष्ट्रम् with sharp teeth, आस्यं च mouth also, अकल्पयत् created.

#### Explanation

Having been entreated, Indra created in me two long arms extending upto a yojana and set my mouth with sharp teeth in the stomach.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

सोऽहं भुजाभ्यां दीर्घाभ्यां संकृष्यास्मिन्वने चरान्।।3.71.14।। सिंहव्दिपमृगव्याघ्रान् भक्षयामि समन्ततः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः अहम् with that I, अस्मिन् in this, वने in the forest, दीर्घाभ्याम् with long, भुजाभ्याम् with arms, अस्मिन् in this, वने in the forest, समन्ततः all over, चरान् moving creatures, सिंहव्दिपमृगव्याघ्रान् lions, tigers, elephants and deer, संकृष्य after pulling, भक्षयामि I eat.

#### Explanation

Stretching the long arms in the forest, I dragged creatures like lions, tigers, elephants and deer moving in the forest and ate them.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

स तु मामब्रवीदिन्द्रो यदा रामस्सलक्ष्मणः।।3.71.15।। छेत्स्यते समरे बाहू तदा स्वर्गं गमिष्यसि।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः that, इन्द्रः तु Indra too, माम् to me, अब्रवीत् said, यदा when, सलक्ष्मणः along with Lakshmana, रामः Rama, समरे in the fight, बाहू छेत्स्यते cuts off the arms, तदा then, स्वर्गम् heaven, गमिष्यसि you will go.

#### Explanation

Indra too had said, 'When Rama along with Lakshmana cuts off your arms in a fight,  you would reach heaven.'

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

अनेन वपुषा राम वनेऽस्मिन्राजसत्तम।।3.71.16।। यद्यत्पश्यामि सर्वस्य ग्रहणं साधु रोचये।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राजसत्तम best of kings, राम Rama, अस्मिन् वने in this forest, अनेन वपुषा with this body, यद्यत् whatever, पश्यामि I see, सर्वस्य of all, ग्रहणम् catch, साधु proper, रोचये I like.

#### Explanation

O Rama, the best of kings I thought it was only proper in this forest and with this body to catch whatever creature I saw.

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

अवश्यं ग्रहणं रामो मन्येऽहं समुपैष्यति।।3.71.17।। इमां बुद्धिं पुरस्कृत्य देहन्यासकृतश्रमः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रामः Rama, अवश्यम् certainly, ग्रहणम् into the hold, समुपैष्यति will come, अहम् I, मन्ये  thinking, इमाम् this, बुद्धिम् thought, पुरस्कृत्य depending on, देहन्यासकृतश्रमः I am struggling to preserve my body.

#### Explanation

O Rama, I was struggling to preserve my body all the time thinking that some day you would certainly come into my arms.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

स त्वं रामोऽसि भद्रं ते नाहमन्येन राघव।।3.71.18।। शक्यो हन्तुं यथातत्त्वमेवमुक्तं महर्षिणा।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राघव Rama, त्वम् you, सः रामः that Rama, असि you are, ते भद्रम् be blessed, महर्षिणा by the seer, तत्त्वम् truly, एवम् in that way, यथा as, उक्तम् stated, अहम् I, अन्येन by others, हन्तुम् be attacked, शक्यः possible, न not.

#### Explanation

You are that Rama. Be blessed. As stated by the seer I cannot be really killed by any one except you.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

अहं हि मतिसाचिव्यं करिष्यामि नरर्षभ।।3.71.19।। मित्रं चैवोपदेक्ष्यामि युवाभ्यां संस्कृतोऽग्निना।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

नरर्षभ O best of men, अहम् I, अग्निना by fire, संस्कृतः purified man, युवाभ्याम् by you both, मतिसाचिव्यम् help you with my wisdom, करिष्यामि I will do, मित्रं चैव as a friend also, उपदेक्ष्यामि I will advise you.

#### Explanation

O best among men I will help you with my wisdom and give you friendly advice when I am purified by you both through cremation on the funeral pyre.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

एवमुक्तस्तु धर्मात्मा दनुना तेन राघवः।।3.71.20।। इदं जगाद वचनं लक्ष्मणस्योपशृण्वतः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तेन by him, दनुना by Danu, एवम् in that way, उक्तः having been told, धर्मात्मा righteous, राघवः Rama, लक्ष्मणस्य to Lakshmana, उपशृण्वतः listening, इदम् these, वचनम् words, जगाद spoke.

#### Explanation

Rigteous Rama spoke the following words in response to the story of Danu within Lakshmana's earshot:

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

रावणेन हृता भार्या मम सीता यशस्स्विनी।।3.71.21।। निष्क्रान्तस्य जनस्थानात्सहभ्रात्रा यथासुखम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

जनस्थानात् from Janasthana, सह भ्रात्रा along with my brother, निष्क्रान्तस्य when I had gone out, मम my, भार्या wife, यशस्स्विनी famed lady, सीता Sita, रावणेन by Ravana, यथासुखम्  happily, हृता carried off.

#### Explanation

My illlustrious wife Sita was carried away by Ravana comfortably when I was away from Janasthana along with my brother.

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

नाममात्रं तु जानामि न रूपं तस्य रक्षसः।।3.71.22।। निवासं वा प्रभावं वा वयं तस्य न विद्महे।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तस्य his, नाममात्रम् only the name, जानामि I know, रूपम् form, न do not know, तस्य his, निवासं वा residence not known, प्रभावं वा or his power, वयम् we, न विद्महे we do not know.

#### Explanation

I only know his name. We do not know how he looks, where he lives and what influence he has.

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

शोकार्तानामनाथानामेवं विपरिधावताम्।।3.71.23।। कारुण्यं सदृशं कर्तुमुपकारे च वर्तताम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

शोकार्तानाम् striken by sorrow, अनाथानाम् of orphans, एवम् that way, विपरिधावताम् of those running all over, उपकारे help, वर्ततां च do to others, कारुण्यम् kindness, कर्तुम् do show, सदृशम् worthy of your compasssion.

#### Explanation

Creatures like you may help those who, like orphans, run here and there, stricken with sorrow.

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

काष्ठान्यादाय शुष्काणि काले भग्नानि कुञ्जरैः।।3.71.24।। धक्ष्यामस्त्वां वयं वीर श्वभ्रे महति कल्पिते।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वीर hero, काले in the proper season, कुञ्जरैः by elephants, भग्नानि broken, शुष्काणि dried, काष्ठानि logs of wood, आदाय after getting, कल्पिते in a place set, महति in a large one, श्वभ्रे in a pit, त्वाम् you, धक्ष्यामः we will cremate you.

#### Explanation

O hero we will cremate you in a huge pit to be prepared for this occasion, collecting dry logs of wood broken by elephants.

---

### Shloka 25

#### Shloka Text

स त्वं सीतां समाचक्ष्व येन वा यत्र वा हृता।।3.71.25।। कुरु कल्याणमत्यर्थं यदि जानासि तत्त्वतः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः he, त्वम् you, तत्त्वतः truly, जानासि यदि if you know, सीताम् about Sita, येन वा by whom, यत्र वा of wherever, हृता carried, समाचक्ष्व tell me, अत्यर्थम् very much, कल्याणम् wellbeing, कुरु you may do.

#### Explanation

If you really know where SIta has been kidnapped and by whom, tell me. You will be doing a great service.

---

### Shloka 26

#### Shloka Text

एवमुक्तस्तु रामेण वाक्यं दनुरनुत्तमम्।।3.71.26।। प्रोवाच कुशलो वक्तुं वक्तारमपि राघवम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रामेण by Rama, एवम् in that way, उक्तः  having been asked, वक्तुम् telling, कुशलः one who can speak well, दनुः Danu, वक्तारम् addressing one who can speak, राघवम् to Rama, अनुत्तमम् best, वाक्यम् word, प्रोवाच spoke.

#### Explanation

Danu who was proficient in speech spoke with chosen words to Rama who was a great speaker himself:

---

### Shloka 27

#### Shloka Text

दिव्यमस्ति न मे ज्ञानं नाभिजानामि मैथिलीम्।।3.71.27।। यस्तां ज्ञास्यति तं वक्ष्ये दग्धस्स्वं रूपमास्थितः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मे to me, दिव्यम् divine, ज्ञानम् wisdom, नास्ति I do not have, मैथिलीम् Maithili, नाभिजानामि I do not know, दग्धः burnt one, स्वं रूम् my original form, आस्थितः getting, ताम् her, यः whoever, ज्ञास्यति know of her, तम् him, वक्ष्ये I will reveal.

#### Explanation

I do not have divine wisdom (now). Nor do I know Sita. When you cremate me, I shall assume my original form and will tell you the name of the person who knows it.

---

### Shloka 28

#### Shloka Text

अदग्धस्य तु विज्ञातुं शक्तिरस्ति न मे प्रभो।।3.71.28।। राक्षसं तं महावीर्यं सीता येन हृता तव।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

प्रभो O lord, येन by whom, तव your, सीता Sita, हृता is taken away, तम् him, महावीर्यम् very powerful, राक्षसम् rakshasa, विज्ञातुम् to know, अदग्धस्य without being burnt, मे to me, शक्तिः ability, नास्ति is not there.

#### Explanation

O lord before I am cremated, I do not have the ability to know  about that powerful demon by whom Sita has been abducted.

---

### Shloka 29

#### Shloka Text

विज्ञानं हि मम भ्रष्टं शापदोषेण राघव।।3.71.29।। स्वकृतेन मया प्राप्तं रूपं लोकविगर्हितम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राघव Rama, शापदोषेण due to the curse, मम my, विज्ञानम् wisdom, भ्रष्टं हि is indeed lost, मया by me, स्वकृतेन by my own deed, लोकविगर्हितम् a form despised by the world, रूपम् this form, प्राप्तम् is attained.

#### Explanation

O Rama due to the curse I have lost my wisdom. By my own deed, I have attained this form despicable to the world.

---

### Shloka 30

#### Shloka Text

किं तु यावन्न यात्यस्तं सविता श्रान्तवाहनः।।3.71.30।। तावन्मामवटे क्षिप्त्वा दह राम यथाविधि।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राम Rama, किं तु but, सविता Sun, श्रान्तवाहनः with his vehicle (horses) tired, अस्तम् (याति) setting, यावत् before, तावत् by then, माम् me, अवटे in a pit, क्षिप्त्वा dropping, यथाविधि in accordance with tradition, दह you may cremate.

#### Explanation

Before the Sun  disappears with his tired horses drop me into a pit and cremate me in accordance with tradition, O Rama

---

### Shloka 31

#### Shloka Text

दग्धस्त्वयाहमवटे न्यायेन रघुनन्धन।।3.71.31।। वक्ष्यामि तमहं वीर यस्तं ज्ञास्यति राक्षसम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वीर O hero, रघुनन्धन O delight of the Raghus, अहम् I, त्वया by you, न्यायेन as per custom, अवटे in a pit, दग्धः cremated, यः whoever, तं राक्षसम् to that demon, ज्ञास्यति knows, तम् about him, वक्ष्यामि I will let you know.

#### Explanation

O heroic Rama, delight of the Raghus, if you drop me in a pit and cremate me as per custom, I will tell you about him who knows the demon.

---

### Shloka 32

#### Shloka Text

तेन सख्यं च कर्तव्यं न्याय्यवृत्तेन राघव।।3.71.32।। कल्पयिष्यति ते प्रीतस्साहाय्यं लघुविक्रमः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राघव Rama, न्याय्यवृत्तेन rightfully conducting, तेन with him, सख्यं च friendship, कर्तव्यम् you should establish, लघुविक्रमः a warrior of swift action, प्रीतः a pleased one, ते to you, साहाय्यम् help, कल्पयिष्यति he will do.

#### Explanation

O Rama you should establish friendship with him in a just manner. That hero of swift action will help you when he is pleased.

---

### Shloka 33

#### Shloka Text

न हि तस्यास्त्यविज्ञातं त्रिषु लेकेषु राघव।।3.71.33।। सर्वान्परिसृतो लोकान्पुरासौ कारणान्तरे।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राघव Rama, त्रिषु in the three, लोकेषु worlds, तस्य for him, अविज्ञातम् unknown to him, नास्ति is not there, हि do, असौ he, पुरा in the past, कारणान्तरे for a different cause, सर्वान् all, लोकान् worlds, परिसृतः has roamed about.

#### Explanation

O Rama, there is nothing that he does not know in the three worlds. In the past for a different reason he roamed all the (three) worlds. इत्यार्ष श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये अरण्यकाण्डे एकसप्ततितमस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the seventyfirst sarga of Aranyakanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 72

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

एवमुक्तौतु तौ वीरौ कबन्धेन नरेश्वरौ। गिरिप्रदरमासाद्य पावकं विससर्जतुः।।3.72.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कबन्धेन by Kabandha, एवम् in that manner, उक्तौ having been advised, नरेश्वरौ two kings, तौ वीरौ the two heroes, गिरिप्रदरम्  mountain cave, आसाद्य reached, पावकम् fire, विससर्जतुः lighted.

#### Explanation

Thus said by Kabandha, the two lords of men, the heroes reached a cave in the mountain and lighted the fire.

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

लक्ष्मणस्तु महोल्काभिर्ज्वलिताभिस्समन्ततः। चितामादीपयामास सा प्रजज्वाल सर्वतः।।3.72.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लक्ष्मणस्तु Lakshmana himself, ज्वलिताभिः with burning, महोल्काभिः with big meteorlike logs, समन्ततः all round, चिताम् pyre, आदीपयामास set fire, सर्वतः on all sides, प्रजज्वाल lighted.

#### Explanation

Lakshmana lit the pyre with big meteorlike logs on all sides and it burned.

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

तच्छरीरं कबन्धस्य घृतपिण्डोपमं महत्। मेदसा पच्यमानस्य मन्दं दहति पावकः।।3.72.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मेदसा with flesh, पच्यमानस्य as the corpse was burning, कबन्धस्य Kabandha's, महत् great, घृतपिण्डोपमम् like a huge ball of ghee, तत् that, शरीरम्  body, पावकः the fire, मन्दम् slowly, दहति burnt.

#### Explanation

As the flesh of Kabandha's body decomposed, if burnt slowly in the fire although it was like a huge mass of fat.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

स विधूय चितामाशु विधूमोऽग्निरिवोत्थितः। अरजे वाससी बिभ्रन्मालां दिव्यां महाबलः।।3.72.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महाबलः very strong , सः he, अरजे clean, वाससी pair of clothes, दिव्याम् divine, मालाम् garland, बिभ्रत् wearing, चिताम् from the pyre, विधूय  shaking, आशु immediately, विधूमः smokeless, अग्निरिव like fire, उत्थितः he rose up.

#### Explanation

Mighty Kabandha shook himself off the funeral pyre and rose like smokeless fire, wearing a pair of clean clothes and a divine garland.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

ततश्चिताया वेगेन भास्वरो विमलाम्बरः। उत्पपाताशु संहृष्टस्सर्वप्रत्यङ्गभूषणः।।3.72.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः thereafter, भास्वरः shining brightly, विमलाम्बरः wearing clean clothes, संहृष्टः happily, सर्वप्रत्यङ्गभूषणः limbs adorned with ornaments, आशु instantaneously, वेगेन speedily, चितायाः from the pyre, उत्पपात came out.

#### Explanation

Thereafter shining brightly in pure clothes and wearing ornaments all over the body, Kabandha hurtled out happily and instantaneously from the pyre.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

विमाने भास्वरे तिष्ठन्हंसयुक्ते यशस्करे। प्रभया च महातेजा दिशो दश विराजयन्।।3.72.6।। सोऽन्तरिक्षगतो रामं कबन्धो वाक्यमब्रवीत्। शृणु राघव तत्त्वेन यथा सीतामवाप्स्यसि।।3.72.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः कबन्धः he, Kabandha, अन्तरिक्षगतः reached the sky, हंसयुक्ते harnessed by swans, यशस्करे famous, भास्वरे shining, विमाने in an aerial chariot, तिष्ठन् seated, महातेजाः a brilliant one, प्रभया with glow, दश दिशः all the ten directions, विराजयन् illumining, रामम् Rama, वाक्यम् words, अब्रवीत् said, राघव Raghava, सीताम् Sita, यथा as, अवाप्स्यसि you will be getting her, तत्त्वेन truly, शृणु listen.

#### Explanation

The effulgent Kabandha rose into the sky on a famous aerial chariot harnessed to swans. Seated on it, he illuminated all ten directions and spoke from the sky,'O Raghava I will truly tell you how you will get Sita, listen to me'.

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

विमाने भास्वरे तिष्ठन्हंसयुक्ते यशस्करे। प्रभया च महातेजा दिशो दश विराजयन्।।3.72.6।। सोऽन्तरिक्षगतो रामं कबन्धो वाक्यमब्रवीत्। शृणु राघव तत्त्वेन यथा सीतामवाप्स्यसि।।3.72.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः कबन्धः he, Kabandha, अन्तरिक्षगतः reached the sky, हंसयुक्ते harnessed by swans, यशस्करे famous, भास्वरे shining, विमाने in an aerial chariot, तिष्ठन् seated, महातेजाः a brilliant one, प्रभया with glow, दश दिशः all the ten directions, विराजयन् illumining, रामम् Rama, वाक्यम् words, अब्रवीत् said, राघव Raghava, सीताम् Sita, यथा as, अवाप्स्यसि you will be getting her, तत्त्वेन truly, शृणु listen.

#### Explanation

The effulgent Kabandha rose into the sky on a famous aerial chariot harnessed to swans. Seated on it, he illuminated all ten directions and spoke from the sky,'O Raghava I will truly tell you how you will get Sita, listen to me'.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

राम षड्युक्तयो लोके याभिस्सर्वं विमृश्यते। परिमृष्टो दशान्तेन दशाभागेन सेव्यते।।3.72.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राम Rama, याभिः by those, सर्वम् all, विमृश्यते is tested, षट् युक्तयः six expedients, लोके in the world, दशान्तेन when one crosses that period, परिमृष्टः a man's test, दशाभागेन met with bad luck, सेव्यते he will be served.

#### Explanation

O Rama there are six expedients (peace,war,marching,neutrality, alliance, making peace with one and waging war with another, for attaining your goal in the world. Only the unlucky can help the unfortunate.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

दशाभागगतो हीनस्त्वं हि राम सलक्ष्मणः। यत्कृते व्यसनं प्राप्तं त्वया दारप्रधर्षणम्।।।3.72.9।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राम Rama, सलक्ष्मणः along with Lakshmana, त्वम् you, दशाभागगतः going through a part of such bad period, हीनः low ebb, यत्कृते by that , त्वया by you, दारप्रधर्षणम् abduction of wife, व्यसनम् sorrow, प्राप्तम् obtained.

#### Explanation

Along with Lakshmna you are passing through a bad period on account of which your wife has been kidnapped and you, lying low, are experiencing great grief, O Rama

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

तदवश्यं त्वया कार्यस्ससुहृत्सुहृदां वर। अकृत्वा हि न ते सिद्धिमहं पश्यामि चिन्तयन्।।3.72.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सुहृदाम् of friends, वर best one, तत् so, सः that, त्वया by you, अवश्यम् certainly, सुहृत् friend, कार्यः should be done, अकृत्वा without doing so, ते to you, सिद्धिम् achievement, अहम् I, चिन्तयन् while I think over, न पश्यामि I do not see.

#### Explanation

O Rama, the best of friends  you should certainly make friendship with him. As I think over the matter, I do not see any success if you do not adopt this method.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

श्रूयतां राम वक्ष्यामि सुग्रीवो नाम वानरः। भ्रात्रा निरस्तः क्रुद्धेन वालिना शक्रसूनुना।।3.72.11।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राम Rama, वक्ष्यामि l tell you, श्रूयताम् listen , शक्रसूनुना by Indra's son, भ्रात्रा by his brother, क्रुद्धेन in a fit of anger, वालिना by Vali, निरस्तः banished from the kingdom, सुग्रीवो नाम named Sugriva, वानरः monkey.

#### Explanation

O Rama, listen. I shall tell you. A monkey named Sugriva has been banished from the kingdom in a fit of anger by his brother Vali, the son of Indra.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

ऋश्यमूके गिरिवरे पम्पापर्यन्तशोभिते। निवसत्यात्मवान्वीरश्चतुर्भिस्सह वानरैः।।3.72.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

आत्मवान् he is a man of great selfrespect, वीरः a hero, पम्पापर्यन्तशोभिते extending up to lake Pampa, ऋश्यमूके on Rishyamuka, गिरिवरे the best of the mountain, चतुर्भिः with four, वानरैः सह along with monkeys, निवसति residing.

#### Explanation

A hero of great selfrespect, he lives with four monkeys on a beautiful mountain Rishyamuka extending up to lake Pampa.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

वानरेन्द्रो महावीर्यस्तेजोवानमितप्रभः। सत्यसन्धो विनीतश्च धृतिमान्मतिमान्महान्।।3.72.13।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वानरेन्द्रः king of monkeys, महावीर्यः endowed with great strength, तेजोवान् full of valour, अमितप्रभः of limitless lustre, सत्यसन्धः truthful, विनीतः humble, धृतिमान् patient, मतिमान् intelligent, महान् great.

#### Explanation

This king of monkeys is energetic with great strength and limitless lustre. He is humble and truthful, valiant, patient and intelligent. He is great.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

दक्षः प्रगल्भो द्युतिमान्महाबलपराक्रमः। भ्रात्रा विवासितो राम राज्यहेतोर्महाबलः।।3.72.14।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राम Rama, दक्षः efficient, प्रगल्भः eloquent, द्युतिमान् brilliant, महाबलपराक्रमः mighty and brave, महाबलः very strong, राज्यहेतोः for the sake of kingdom, भ्रात्रा by brother, विवासितः is banished.

#### Explanation

He is efficient, eloquent, brilliant. He is mighty and strong. (But) he is banished by his brother from the kingdom, O Rama

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

स ते सहायो मित्रं च सीतायाः परिमार्गणे। भविष्यति हिते राम मा च शोके मनः कृथाः।।3.72.15।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राम O Rama, सः he, ते your, मित्रम् friend, सीतायाः Sita's, परिमार्गणे in searching, सहायः  helpful, हिते in the welfare, भविष्यति he will be, शोके in sorrow, मनः your mind, मा कृथाः not indulge.

#### Explanation

O Rama he will help you as a benevolent friend in searching out Sita. Do not indulge in sorrow.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

भवितव्यं हि यच्चापि न तच्छक्यमिहान्यथा। कर्तुमिक्ष्वाकुशार्दूल कालो हि दुरतिक्रमः।।3.72.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

इक्ष्वाकुशार्दूल best of the Ikshvaku dynasty, यच्चापि whatever, भवितव्यम् has to happen, तत् that, अन्यथा otherwise, कर्तुम् to do, न शक्यम् is not possible, कालः time, दुरतिक्रमः हि cannot be transgressed.

#### Explanation

O best of the Ikshvaku race whatever is due to happen cannot be otherwise.  It is not possible to transgress time (fate).

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

गच्छ श्रीघ्रमितो राम सुग्रीवं तं महाबलम्। वयस्यं तं कुरु क्षिप्रमितो गत्वाऽद्य राघव।।3.72.17।। अद्रोहाय समागम्य दीप्यमाने विभावसौ।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राम Rama, इतः thus, श्रीघ्रम् swiftly, महाबलम् mighty, तं सुग्रीवम् to that Sugriva, गच्छ reach, राघव Rama, अद्य today, इतः from here, क्षिप्रम् quickly, गत्वा having gone, अद्रोहाय make alliance without any malice, विभावसौ  the fire, दीप्यमाने  is burning, तम् with him, वयस्यम् friend, कुरु you do.

#### Explanation

O Rama, immediately proceed today from here to mighty Sugriva. Having reached him, make him your friend without malice in the presence of burning fire.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

स च ते नावमन्तव्यस्सुग्रीवो वानराधिपः।।3.72.18।। कृतज्ञः कामरूपी च सहायार्थी च वीर्यवान्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वानराधिपः (अपि सन्) king of the monkeys, सः सुग्रीवः that Sugriva, ते by you, नावमन्तव्यः not to be insulted, कृतज्ञः a grateful one, कामरूपी च he can take any form at will, सहायार्थी च he seeks your help, वीर्यवान् strong.

#### Explanation

Thinking that Sugriva is only a vanara king, he should not be disrespected. He has a sense of gratitude. He can take any form at will. He is in need of your help although he is very strong.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

शक्तौह्यद्य युवां कर्तुं कार्यं तस्य चिकीर्षितम्।।3.72.19।। कृतार्थो वाऽकृतार्थो वा कृत्यं तव करिष्यति।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

युवाम् you both, अद्य now, तस्य his, चिकीर्षितम् desired, कार्यम् his work, कर्तुम् to do, शक्तौ हि you both have the ability, कृतार्थो वा whether his work is done, अकृतार्थो वा or not done, तव your, कृत्यम् work, करिष्यति he will do.

#### Explanation

You are both young and able to do the work he wants. Whether his work is done or not, he will fulfil your task.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

स ऋक्षरजसः पुत्रः पम्पामटति शङ्कितः।।.3.72.20।। भास्करस्यौरसः पुत्रो वालिना कृतकिल्बिषः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ऋक्षरजसः Riksharaja's, पुत्रः son, भास्करस्य Sungod's, औरसःपुत्रः lawful son, सः he, वालिना Valin, कृतकिल्बिषः developing enmity, शङ्कितः apprehending danger, पम्पाम् on the banks of Pampa, अटति roaming.

#### Explanation

Born of Riksharaja, he is the offspring of the Sungod. He has developed enmity with Vali, and is roaming about the banks of Pampa, suspecting danger from Vali.

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

सन्निधायायुधं क्षिप्रमृष्यमूकालयं कपिम्।।3.72.21।। कुरु राघव सत्येन वयस्यं वनचारिणम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राघव Rama, आयुधम् weapon, सन्निधाय after keeping ready, वनचारिणम् a wanderer in the forest, ऋष्यमूकालयम् dwelling on Rishyamuka, कपिम् monkey, सत्येन truthfully, क्षिप्रम् at once, वयस्यम् friend, कुरु make.

#### Explanation

O Rama, with your weapons kept ready, go to Sugriva, king of the monkeys, a resident of Rishyamuka. He is wandering in the forest. Establish friendship with him.

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

स हि स्थानानि सर्वाणि कार्त्स्न्येन कपिकुञ्जरः।।3.72.22।। नरमांसाशिनां लोके नैपुण्यादधिगच्छति।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कपिकुञ्जरः foremost of monkeys, सः he, लोके in the world, नरमांसाशिनाम् of cannibals, सर्वाणि all, स्थानानि dwellings, कार्त्स्न्येन entirely, नैपुण्यात् with tact, अधिगच्छति हि will find and reach.

#### Explanation

He is the foremost of the monkeys. He knows well by his wisdom all the dwellingplaces of the carnivorous demons in the world.

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

न तस्याविदितं लोकेकिञ्चिदस्ति हि राघव।।3.72.23।। यावत्सूर्यः प्रतपति सहस्रांशुररिन्दम।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अरिन्दम O subduer of enemies, राघव Rama, सहस्रांशुः the god of a thousand rays, सूर्यः Sun, यावत् so far, प्रतपति radiates, लोके in the world, तस्य his, अविदितम् unknown, किञ्चित् hardly anything, नास्ति हि is not.

#### Explanation

There is nothing unknown to Sugriva in this world as far as the Sun shines, O subduer of enemies

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

स नदीर्विपुलान्शैलान् गिरिदुर्गाणि कन्दरान्।।3.72.24।। अन्वीक्ष्य वानरैस्सार्धं पत्नीं तेऽधिगमिष्यति।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः he, वानरैः सार्धम् along with monkeys, नदीः rivers, विपुलान् big, शैलान् mountains, गिरिदुर्गाणि mountain caves, कन्दरान् caverns, अन्वीक्ष्य searching, ते your, पत्नीम् consort, अधिगमिष्यति will find out.

#### Explanation

Along with his monkeys, he will search all rivers, mountains and caves and will find out your consort.

---

### Shloka 25

#### Shloka Text

वानरांश्च महाकायान्प्रेषयिष्यति राघव।।3.72.25।। दिशो विचेतुं तां सीतां त्वद्वियोगेन शोचतीम्। स ज्ञास्यति वरारोहां निर्मलां रावणालये।।3.72.26।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राघव Rama, त्वद्वियोगेन by separation from you, शोचतीम् brooding, तां सीताम् that Sita, विचेतुम् to search, महाकायान् gigantic, वानरांश्च monkeys, दिशः in all directions, प्रेषयिष्यति he will send, सः he, रावणालये in Ravana's home, निर्मलाम् chaste, वरारोहाम् beautiful, ज्ञास्यति will find out.

#### Explanation

Sugriva will find out the lady of purity and chastity brooding over you in separation in the abode of Ravana. He can send gigantic monkeys in different directions in order to search for her.

---

### Shloka 26

#### Shloka Text

वानरांश्च महाकायान्प्रेषयिष्यति राघव।।3.72.25।। दिशो विचेतुं तां सीतां त्वद्वियोगेन शोचतीम्। स ज्ञास्यति वरारोहां निर्मलां रावणालये।।3.72.26।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation
